EDCS-00380429.text_edition: \n Deo Laribero Breo aram posuit voto\n
EDCS-00380429.translation: Breo set up this altar to the god Liber in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-00380429.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar by Breo to the god Liber, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380430.text_edition: \n Deo Laribero Breoroni aram posuit VI \n
EDCS-00380430.translation: Breoroni set up this altar to the god Liber, [perhaps in the] sixth [year or month or as the sixth person].
EDCS-00380430.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar by Breoroni to the god Liber, possibly indicating a quantitative or temporal aspect with the number VI, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380432.translation: Breo set up this altar to the god Liber.
EDCS-00380432.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar by Breo to the god Liber, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380434.translation: To the god Liber, [dedicated by] Reo.
EDCS-00380434.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Liber by someone named Reo, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380435.summary: This inscription records a partial dedication to the god Liber, with the dedicant's name lost, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380436.summary: This inscription records a partial dedication to the god Liber, with the dedicant's name lost, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380438.summary: This inscription, likely part of a larger monument, records a dedication by Coemia.

EDCS-00380487.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucio Domitio Hirsuto Iuniori Filius matri pientissimae Valerianae Domitiae sit tibi annorum LXXV sit terra levis\n
EDCS-00380487.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Domitius Hirsutus the Younger, son, for his most dutiful mother Valeriana Domitia, may the earth be light for you. Aged 75.
EDCS-00380487.summary: This funerary inscription records the memorial for Valeriana Domitia, set up by her son Lucius Domitius Hirsutus the Younger, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380491.text_edition: \n Sempronia Marcella Truti de suo fecit\n
EDCS-00380491.translation: Sempronia Marcella made this from her own resources.
EDCS-00380491.summary: This inscription records a private undertaking by Sempronia Marcella, likely for a building or monument, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380492.text_edition: \n Gaius Cautenico Acconis filius annorum XX hic situs est faciendum curavit Carisia mater\n
EDCS-00380492.translation: Gaius Cautenicus, son of Accon, aged 20, lies here. His mother Carisia saw to its construction.
EDCS-00380492.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Gaius Cautenicus, aged 20, and notes that his mother Carisia supervised the construction of his tomb, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380493.text_edition: \n Aleonis uxor Aleonis filia Manli uxor\n
EDCS-00380493.translation: Aleonis, wife of Aleonis, daughter of Aleonis, wife of Manlius.
EDCS-00380493.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying a woman by her paternal lineage, husband, and her own father. It is likely a tombstone or a funerary marker.

EDCS-00380494.translation: To the departed gods below, to the spirits of the departed, to Pacatus.
EDCS-00380494.summary: This is a dedication to the Manes, the spirits of the dead, likely marking a tomb, with the name Pacatus as the recipient of the dedication.

EDCS-00380536.text_edition: \n Cornelia Luci filia Sirasteiun hic sita est Sodalis amor rapuisti me nunc sumus una dum vixsimus sic semper concordes nunc sumus certe pares tres et viginti annos aetas s nostra teneret fui semper RAER subito R AC ME \n
EDCS-00380536.translation: Cornelia, daughter of Lucius, wife of Sirasteiun, rests here. Love seized me, now we are one. As long as we lived, so always in harmony; now we are certainly equal. For three and twenty years our age held us. I was always... suddenly... me.
EDCS-00380536.summary: This is a poignant funerary epitaph for Cornelia, daughter of Lucius and wife of Sirasteiun, lamenting her death and celebrating their marital harmony over 23 years.

EDCS-00380537.text_edition: \n Apodemus cinedus Becerrus cinedus et quis \n
EDCS-00380537.translation: Apodemus the eunuch, Becerrus the eunuch, and whoever...
EDCS-00380537.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or a statement involving two individuals identified as eunuchs, Apodemus and Becerrus, with an incomplete continuation.

EDCS-00380548.text_edition: \n Quis res tunicam tolid sic e Livia odi eam vel ium sic item is sic quis sic quaestom sic hhabeat sic tracta\n
EDCS-00380548.translation: Whoever has the tunic, thus he has from Livia; I hate her, or... thus, likewise, he, thus, whoever, thus, the profit, thus, it is handled.
EDCS-00380548.summary: This inscription appears to be a peculiar statement, possibly a curse or a decree, related to a tunic and its ownership or transfer involving Livia.

EDCS-03000462.text_edition: \n Sunuae filiae annorum XXIII Doquirus Doci Celtico Flaviensis de suo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-03000462.translation: Doquirus, son of Doci, of the Flavian tribe of Celtici, arranged for this to be made at his own expense for his daughter Sunua, aged 23.
EDCS-03000462.summary: This is a funerary dedication commissioned by Doquirus for his daughter Sunua, specifying her age and his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-03000466.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Reu Doiderus Nostronis annorum LXX SCCCXX VISA\n
EDCS-03000466.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Reu, son of Nostron, aged 70. SCCCXX VISA.
EDCS-03000466.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Manes for Reu, son of Nostron, who lived to be 70 years old, with some unclear additional notation.

EDCS-03000489.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aelia Locris annorum XXII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-03000489.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aelia Locris, aged 22, rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-03000489.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aelia Locris, aged 22, with the common wish for a light burial.

EDCS-03000490.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Chrestus annorum V hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-03000490.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Chrestus, aged 5, rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-03000490.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Chrestus, a child aged 5, with the common wish for a light burial.

EDCS-03000491.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Poletice annorum XXX pia in suis natis et contubernio hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-03000491.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Poletice, aged 30, rests here, pious towards her children and companion, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-03000491.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Poletice, aged 30, described as pious towards her children and companion, with the standard burial wish.

EDCS-03000495.text_edition: \n Lucio Caesari Augusti filio Divi nepoti\n
EDCS-03000495.translation: To Lucius Caesar, son of Augustus, grandson of the Divine [Julius Caesar].
EDCS-03000495.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Lucius Caesar, a prominent figure in the early Roman Empire.

EDCS-03300497.translation: Sacred to the goddess Canduedia by the castellani (garrison soldiers).
EDCS-03300497.summary: This is a religious offering to the goddess Canduedia, made by soldiers stationed at a fort.

EDCS-03300503.text_edition: \n Sedato Arreni filio annorum XXIII Valerius Elaesus Fusci filius Uxsamensis sic et Elguius Modestinus Emeritensis amici\n
EDCS-03300503.translation: To Sedatus, son of Arrenus, aged 23; Valerius Elaesus, son of Fuscus, from Uxama, and Elguis Modestinus, from Emerita, his friends, have erected this.
EDCS-03300503.summary: This is a funerary monument for Sedatus, erected by his friends Valerius Elaesus and Elguis Modestinus.

EDCS-03300509.translation: N. placed this in gratitude for the favor of the holy Fortuna.
EDCS-03300509.summary: This is a votive offering to the goddess Fortuna, placed by an individual identified only by the initial N.

EDCS-03300510.text_edition: \n Deo Dubunecisao Diocus Cuntani votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-03300510.translation: Diocus Cuntani fulfilled his vow to the god Dubunecisao willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-03300510.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Diocus Cuntani fulfilling a vow to the god Dubunecisao.

EDCS-03300511.text_edition: \n Atto Caebaliqum Elaesi filio Drusunae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-03300511.translation: Atto fulfilled his vow to Elaes, son of Elaes, to Drusuna willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-03300511.summary: This is a votive inscription by Atto, fulfilling a vow to Elaes, son of Elaes, in honor of Drusuna.

EDCS-03300514.translation: To Lucius Antonius, son of Elaesus.
EDCS-03300514.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Lucius Antonius, son of Elaesus.

EDCS-03300515.text_edition: \n Arcarus faciendum curavit Caernio Afrani filio annorum L\n
EDCS-03300515.translation: Arcarus saw to its construction, for Caernius, son of Afranius, aged 50.
EDCS-03300515.summary: This is a funerary monument for Caernius, son of Afranius, overseen by Arcarus.

EDCS-03300517.text_edition: \n Aemiliae Paternae Paenanici filiae annorum XLV Valeria Severina matri faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-03300517.translation: Valeria Severina had this made for her mother, Aemilia Patern, daughter of Paenanicus, aged 45.
EDCS-03300517.summary: This is a funerary monument for Aemilia Patern, daughter of Paenanicus, commissioned by her daughter Valeria Severina.

EDCS-03300519.text_edition: \n Lugunis deabus Aurelius Celer votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-03300519.translation: Aurelius Celer fulfilled his vow to the goddesses Lugunis willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-03300519.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Aurelius Celer fulfilling a vow to the goddesses Lugunis.

EDCS-03300520.text_edition: \n Munimentum sic Doviteri etoni lti filii Vadiniensis annorum \n
EDCS-03300520.translation: Monument of Doviterus, son of Eton, from Vadena, aged [---].
EDCS-03300520.summary: This inscription records the monument of Doviterus, likely a funerary monument given the mention of age, from the town of Vadena.

EDCS-03300529.text_edition: \n Marco Clodio Marci filio Galeria Flacco IIviro bis flamini adlecto in quinque decurias ab Imperatore Hadriano Caesare Augusto tribuno militum legionis IIII Flaviae in Moesia superiore ex testamento Corneliae Neillae heredes eius\n
EDCS-03300529.translation: To Marcus Clodius Flaccus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, twice duovir, twice priest, appointed to the five decuries by the Emperor Hadrian Augustus Caesar, military tribune of the Fourth Legion Flavia in Upper Moesia, from the will of Cornelia Neilla, by his heirs.
EDCS-03300529.summary: This is a dedication to Marcus Clodius Flaccus, honoring his various public and military offices, erected by his heirs based on a will.

EDCS-03300530.text_edition: \n Genio municipii Labitulosani Marcus Clodius Flaccus\n
EDCS-03300530.translation: To the Genius of the municipium of Labitulosus, Marcus Clodius Flaccus.
EDCS-03300530.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the local deity (Genius) of Labitulosus by Marcus Clodius Flaccus.

EDCS-03300531.text_edition: \n Sexto Iunio Silvino ex testamento Corneliae Neillae heredes eius\n
EDCS-03300531.translation: To Sextus Junius Silvinus, from the will of Cornelia Neilla, by his heirs.
EDCS-03300531.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Sextus Junius Silvinus, established by his heirs according to the will of Cornelia Neilla.

EDCS-03300532.text_edition: \n Lucio Aemilio Attaesoni ex testamento Corneliae Neillae heredes eius\n
EDCS-03300532.translation: To Lucius Aemilius Attaeson, from the will of Cornelia Neilla, by his heirs.
EDCS-03300532.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Lucius Aemilius Attaeson, established by his heirs according to the will of Cornelia Neilla.

EDCS-03300533.text_edition: \n Corneliae Neillae Cornelius Philemon et Clodia \n
EDCS-03300533.translation: To Cornelia Neilla, by Cornelius Philemon and Clodia.
EDCS-03300533.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Cornelia Neilla, made by Cornelius Philemon and Clodia.

EDCS-03300534.translation: To Lucius Aemilius, son of Lucius.
EDCS-03300534.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Lucius Aemilius, son of Lucius.

EDCS-03300539.text_edition: \n Titus Martius Vibius Fulvius Quietus Caius Grattius Grattianus IIviri hoc opus faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-03300539.translation: Titus Martius Vibius, Fulvius Quietus, and Caius Grattius Grattianus, the two duovirs, saw to the construction of this work.
EDCS-03300539.summary: This inscription records that the two duovirs, Titus Martius Vibius, Fulvius Quietus, and Caius Grattius Grattianus, supervised the construction of a public work.

EDCS-03300573.text_edition: \n Fortunata Si filia annorum IIII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-03300573.translation: Fortunata, daughter of Si, aged four, lies here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-03300573.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Fortunata, indicating her age and a common wish for the afterlife.

EDCS-03300594.translation: To Lucius Planus Russinus, son of Lucius.
EDCS-03300594.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Lucius Planus Russinus, son of Lucius.

EDCS-03300595.text_edition: \n Nice liberta annorum XV Nympheros servus annorum X Aemilia Zosime\n
EDCS-03300595.translation: Nice, a freedwoman of fifteen years, and Nympheros, a slave of ten years.
EDCS-03300595.summary: This inscription likely marks the ages of two individuals, Nice and Nympheros, possibly on a tombstone.

EDCS-03300596.text_edition: \n Aemilio Germano patri Aemiliae Aestivae matri Aemilius \n
EDCS-03300596.translation: To Aemilius Germanus, father, and Aemilia Aestiva, mother.
EDCS-03300596.summary: This text is a dedication to parents, Aemilius Germanus and Aemilia Aestiva, likely on a tomb or memorial.

EDCS-03300597.translation: Marcus Clodius Fortunatus, of fourteen years.
EDCS-03300597.summary: This inscription records the name and age of a young boy, Marcus Clodius Fortunatus, probably on his tombstone.

EDCS-03300598.translation: To Gaius Licinius, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-03300598.summary: This is a dedication to Gaius Licinius, enacted by a local council's decision, likely commemorating a public figure.

EDCS-03400076.text_edition: \n Marco Clodio Marci filio Galeria Martiali IIviro quaestori flamini Augustorum praefecto fabrum praefecto insularum [Baliarum ------\n
EDCS-03400076.translation: To Marcus Clodius Martialis, son of Marcus, who held the office of IIvir, quaestor, flamen of the Augusti, prefect of the Fabri, prefect of the Balearic Islands [and...].
EDCS-03400076.summary: This inscription lists the extensive public career of Marcus Clodius Martialis, a prominent local official, likely on a public monument.

EDCS-03400146.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Sulpicio Euclidi VIviro Augustali magistro coloniae Tarraconensium Sulpicius Syntropus patrono optimo\n
EDCS-03400146.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Sulpicius Syntropus made this for Gaius Sulpicius Euclides, a most excellent patron, VIvir Augustalis and magistrate of the colony of Tarraco.
EDCS-03400146.summary: This funerary inscription honors Gaius Sulpicius Euclides, a patron and local magistrate, set up by his client Sulpicius Syntropus.

EDCS-03400225.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Flavia Cleopatra and her daughter Fonteia Melitine made this for Flavius Onesimus, aged fifty, their most indulgent and incomparable husband and father. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-03400225.summary: This funerary inscription honors Flavius Onesimus, the husband and father, erected by his wife and daughter, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03400275.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Terentius Nicomedes made this for Luccia Optata, whether freedwoman or wife. I did what I could; now my son fears you. You have lived abroad and many have congratulated you on your prosperity. Prantius Alienus has been with me throughout. A good man, well-deserving.
EDCS-03400275.summary: This funerary inscription for Luccia Optata, possibly a freedwoman or wife, was made by Terentius Nicomedes, with a mention of Prantius Alienus.

EDCS-03400467.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aureliae Paulinae filiae o crudele funus qui nunc attigit mihii renovatus o dolor dolueram fratri simulque sorori nunc lugeo filiam pariterque sanguinem esto iam placide posita Lethes in morte quiesce iunctaque tumulo fratri simulque sorori vixit annos XX mensem dies XVII Aurelia Purpuris mater filiae dulcissimae et pientissimae fecit Qu\n
EDCS-03400467.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. O cruel fate that has now befallen me! My grief for my brother and sister is renewed; now I mourn my daughter equally. Rest peacefully in death and lie joined in the tomb with my brother and sister. She lived twenty years, one month, and seventeen days. Aurelia Purpuris, mother, made this for her dearest and most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-03400467.summary: This funerary inscription laments the death of Aurelia Paulina, who died at twenty years of age, by her mother Aurelia Purpuris, also mourning the loss of her son and daughter.

EDCS-03400469.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to record the names of a freedman and Elpis, possibly on a tomb or monument.

EDCS-03400470.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Sulpicio Sabino beneficario civi Bracaro militi legionis VII geminae piae felicis nnorum XLV stipendiorum XXV posuerunt Sulpicius Fuscinus alumnus et Sulpicia Celeriana marito bene merenti fecerunt\n
EDCS-03400470.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Sulpicius Sabinus, a high-ranking soldier of the Legio VII Gemina Pia Felix, aged 45 with 25 years of service, and Sulpicia Celeriana, his adopted son Sulpicius Fuscinus and his wife, erected this monument to their deserving husband.
EDCS-03400470.summary: A funerary monument for a soldier, Sulpicius Sabinus, erected by his adopted son and wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03400475.translation: For Pol(lia) Ingenua, freedwoman of St(atius), Amoena; this monument will not follow an heir.
EDCS-03400475.summary: A funerary epitaph for Amoena, a freedwoman, indicating that she owned the property and it would not pass to her heirs, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03400476.translation: Quintus Clodius Euangelus had this monument made for his grandson, aged 12 years and 7 months, his dearest nephew.
EDCS-03400476.summary: A funerary inscription for a young boy, commissioned by his grandfather, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03400478.text_edition: \n Lucio Aemilio filio Palatina Sempronio Clementi Silvaniano aedili quaestori IIviro flamini curatori Capitoli iudici decuria IIII Caius Apuleius Lupus Complutensis amico\n
EDCS-03400478.translation: To Lucius Aemilius, son of Palatina, Sempronius Clemens Silvanianus, his friend, who served as aedile, quaestor, duovir, flamen, curator of the Capitol, and judge of the fourth decuria.
EDCS-03400478.summary: An epitaph honoring Lucius Aemilius and listing his civic and religious offices, likely from a Roman provincial city.

EDCS-03400479.translation: Of Caius Valerius Avitus, from Augus(tobri)ga.
EDCS-03400479.summary: A fragmentary inscription likely indicating ownership or dedication by Caius Valerius Avitus from Augus(tobri)ga.

EDCS-03400504.translation: Clymene, born of the ether... you will not lose your soul... light, divine... mother Annia Fideme to her dearest daughter Clymene, who lived 3 years, 3 months, and 12 days.
EDCS-03400504.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl named Clymene, erected by her mother, Annia Fideme, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03400507.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Aeliodorus sic natione Graeca civis Tarsus Cilicia commorans Hispali qui vixit annos LXXX plus minus recessit fidelis in pace\n
EDCS-03400507.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Aurelius Aeliodorus, of Greek origin, a citizen of Tarsus in Cilicia, living in Hispalis, who lived 80 years more or less, departed in peace.
EDCS-03400507.summary: A funerary inscription for Aurelius Aeliodorus, a Greek from Tarsus who settled in Hispalis (modern Seville), likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03400509.translation: Aur(elia) Euthemia, without blemish, faithful, dear to her husband, devoted in mind to God, who lived 17 years, 10 months, and 22 days, rests in peace in Christ. Flavius Zoticus erected this for his sweetest and incomparable wife.
EDCS-03400509.summary: A Christian funerary epitaph for Aurelia Euthemia, erected by her husband Flavius Zoticus, likely dating to the later Roman Empire or Early Byzantine period.

EDCS-03400513.translation: In memory of Cornelius Iulianus(?), who lived 31 years, 5 months, and 29 days. Lucia Valentina(?) had this made for her dearest husband, who lived with her 10 years, 4 months, and 29 days.
EDCS-03400513.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cornelius Iulianus(?), erected by his wife Lucia Valentina(?), detailing their married life, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03400522.text_edition: \n Lupa infans quae recessit X Kalendas Martias quae vissit sic annus sic XVII menses III dies\n
EDCS-03400522.translation: Lupa, an infant, who departed on the 10th day before the Kalends of March, who lived 17 years and 3 months.
EDCS-03400522.summary: A funerary inscription for an infant named Lupa, providing her age at death and date of departure, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03400542.translation: To the sweetest daughter Sirica, who lived 2 years, her father Acleus made this memorial. May she rest here.
EDCS-03400542.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Sirica, erected by her father Acleus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03400550.translation: To the spirits of the departed. A holy spirit rests in the name of God. Valeria Pompeia made this for Asellus, well-deserving, who lived 8 years, 8 months, and 9 days, dying at 36 years.
EDCS-03400550.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Asellus, set up by Valeria Pompeia, recording his age and likely dating to the Christian Roman period.

EDCS-03400552.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-03400552.translation: The epitaph of Victoes, who died at 9 years old.
EDCS-03400552.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a child named Victoes, recording his age at death.

EDCS-03400555.text_edition: \n MEN cara liminITPA digno viro SCTASEDLI verum amorem CAC late et patientiam nullas vestes concupieram nec gemmas nec pupuras solum computans obire eminenti gloria officium meum secuta unanima sententia unicam natam reliqui parvolis tum mensibus mihi memoriae post futuram et patri solacio spiritu durante emensa annos triginta et novem sic vixi ut merito possem vita in aeternum frui\n
EDCS-03400555.translation: I, MEN, have followed a worthy man, believing in true love, and patiently desired no fine clothes, nor jewels, nor purple dye; considering only how to fulfill my duty with outstanding glory, I followed a unified decision and left behind my only child. For the future, she would be a memory for me and a comfort to her father. While my spirit endured, I lived for thirty-nine years in such a way that I could deservedly enjoy life forever.
EDCS-03400555.summary: This is a personal testament and epitaph by a woman named MEN, reflecting on her life, her love for her husband, and her devotion to her child, dating likely to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03400581.translation: To the spirits of the departed. May you rest in peace, [3]lla, mother (?).
EDCS-03400581.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph, likely for a mother, invoking the 'spirits of the departed' and wishing peace for the deceased.

EDCS-03400598.translation: She lived 40 years, [3] months. Quietus [lies here?].
EDCS-03400598.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph, possibly mentioning a person named Quietus, and recording the age of the deceased.

EDCS-03400627.translation: Here is a memorial of good remembrance. Isidora, daughter of the well-remembering Jonah and Axiaes. May her soul rest in peace with all Israel. Amen, amen, amen.
EDCS-03400627.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Isidora, daughter of Jonah and Axiaes, with a clear Christian invocation for peace and inclusion of 'Israel', likely dating to the later Roman or early Byzantine period.

EDCS-03700450.text_edition: \n Urbis conditori Augusto monumentum Caesari Paullus Fabius Maxumus legatus Caesaris\n
EDCS-03700450.translation: Paullus Fabius Maximus, legate of Caesar, [erected] this monument to Augustus, founder of the city.
EDCS-03700450.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription erected by Paullus Fabius Maximus, a legate, honoring Emperor Augustus as the founder of the city, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-03700457.text_edition: \n Medditus Stennicon Lo--- filius annorum LXXV hic situs est\n
EDCS-03700457.translation: Medditus, son of Stennicon, L---, aged 75 years, is buried here.
EDCS-03700457.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Medditus, son of Stennicon, recording his age at death, likely from a Roman provincial area.

EDCS-03700462.text_edition: \n Superatae nnorum L dulcissimae coniugi Asterius monumentum fecit\n
EDCS-03700462.translation: Asterius erected this monument to his sweetest wife Superata, aged 50 years.
EDCS-03700462.summary: This is a funerary epitaph erected by Asterius for his wife Superata, recording her age at death, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03700463.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Domitia - f(ilia) Carica Marci Carisi Limici uxor annorum XVII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-03700463.translation: To the Divine Manes. Domitia Carica, daughter of Marcus Carisius Limicus, his wife, aged 17, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-03700463.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of Domitia Carica, wife of Marcus Carisius Limicus, with a standard funerary dedication and wish for the deceased.

EDCS-03700464.text_edition: \n Licinius Andronicus Bassi libertus hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-03700464.translation: Licinius Andronicus, freedman of Bassus, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-03700464.summary: This is a tombstone for Licinius Andronicus, a freedman, with a standard funerary dedication and wish for the deceased.

EDCS-03700465.text_edition: \n Albanus Clousocom Cai et Luci Manli libertus \n
EDCS-03700465.translation: Albanus Clousocom, freedman of Caius and Lucius Manlius.
EDCS-03700465.summary: This inscription records Albanus Clousocom as a freedman of Caius and Lucius Manlius.

EDCS-03700475.text_edition: \n Lucius Fabius Fabullus annorum LXX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-03700475.translation: Lucius Fabius Fabullus, aged 70, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-03700475.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of Lucius Fabius Fabullus, aged 70, with a standard funerary dedication and wish for the deceased.

EDCS-03700476.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Faventinae nnm XI\n
EDCS-03700476.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Faventina, 11 years old.
EDCS-03700476.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Divine Manes for Faventina, who died at the age of 11.

EDCS-03700478.summary: This fragmented inscription likely records the burial of an individual named Va]/[risidi[3], though the exact details are incomplete.

EDCS-03700479.text_edition: \n Quintus Fotidius Quinti filius I Pla-to hic situs est fecit pater quot sic filius debuit\n
EDCS-03700479.translation: Quintus Fotidius Plato, son of Quintus, is laid here. His father made it, as much as a son owed.
EDCS-03700479.summary: This tombstone records Quintus Fotidius Plato, with a dedication from his father who states he fulfilled his filial obligation.

EDCS-03700482.text_edition: \n Caius Hostilius Marullus annorum LXXV hiq sic situs sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-03700482.translation: Caius Hostilius Marullus, aged 75, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-03700482.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of Caius Hostilius Marullus, aged 75, with a standard funerary dedication and wish for the deceased.

EDCS-03700483.translation: Cornelius Firmus, aged [X], is laid here.
EDCS-03700483.summary: This incomplete tombstone records Cornelius Firmus, with a partial indication of his age.

EDCS-04900525.text_edition: \n propro Minuci Prisci primi pili legionis VII geminae piae felicis Verna actor posuit\n
EDCS-04900525.translation: Verna, servant of Minucius Priscus, primipilus of the loyal and fortunate Legio VII Gemina, erected this.
EDCS-04900525.summary: This inscription records Verna, a servant, setting up a monument for Minucius Priscus, a primipilus (centurion of the first cohort) of Legio VII Gemina.

EDCS-04900531.translation: To Asturio, son of Tritus, aged 50.
EDCS-04900531.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Asturio, son of Tritus, dated by his age of 50.

EDCS-04900561.text_edition: \n Deanae mulieres Albo(enses) Umenses libentes solverunt votum\n
EDCS-04900561.translation: The women of Alba and Ume willingly paid their vow to Deana.
EDCS-04900561.summary: This inscription records women from the communities of Alba and Ume fulfilling a vow to the goddess Deana.

EDCS-04900569.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum no Luci gum hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-04900569.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Lucius Gum is buried here; may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-04900569.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Gum, with a common epitaph wishing the earth lie lightly upon the deceased.

EDCS-04900570.text_edition: \n celli filio Crasiciano nurus faciendum curavit vixit nnos LIV sit tibi terra levis hic situs est\n
EDCS-04900570.translation: His daughter-in-law had this made for Cellus Crasicianus. He lived 54 years; may the earth rest lightly upon you. He is buried here.
EDCS-04900570.summary: This funerary inscription records Cellus Crasicianus, who lived 54 years, and states that his daughter-in-law commissioned the monument.

EDCS-04900572.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum C C Pilonicorum hic situs est annorum XXV pater faciendum quravit sic\n
EDCS-04900572.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. C. C. Pilonicus is buried here, aged 25. His father had this made. Sic (completes the inscription).
EDCS-04900572.summary: This funerary inscription for C. C. Pilonicus, aged 25, states that his father commissioned the monument.

EDCS-04900574.summary: This fragmentary funerary inscription identifies Quintianus and his age, with significant portions missing.

EDCS-04900576.text_edition: \n D(is)? M(anibus)? s(acrum) Surisus an(norum) V carus suis mater faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-04900576.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead? Surisus, aged 5, dear to his own family. His mother had this made.
EDCS-04900576.summary: This funerary inscription records Surisus, aged 5, and states that his mother commissioned the monument.

EDCS-04900589.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio Felici Augusto Parthico maximo Britannico maximo Germanico maximo pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XVII imperatori III consuli IIII patri patriae proconsuli via Augusta CCLXXXIII\n
EDCS-04900589.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, conqueror of Parthia, conqueror of Britain, conqueror of Germany, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 17th time, imperator for the 3rd time, consul for the 4th time, father of the country, proconsul, at mile 283 of the Via Augusta.
EDCS-04900589.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix, listing his many titles and honors, and marks a location on the Via Augusta.

EDCS-04900590.text_edition: \n Cornelius Simplex sit terra levis Manlia Trita Manlia Serana hic siti sunt\n
EDCS-04900590.translation: Cornelius Simplex; may the earth rest lightly upon you. Manlia Trita, Manlia Serana are buried here.
EDCS-04900590.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Cornelius Simplex, Manlia Trita, and Manlia Serana, with a common epitaph wishing the earth lie lightly upon them.

EDCS-04900594.text_edition: \n Kauti votum solverunt Successus Elaine Caesaris\n
EDCS-04900594.translation: Successus and Elaine Caesaris paid their vow.
EDCS-04900594.summary: This inscription records Successus and Elaine Caesaris fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-04900601.summary: This very fragmentary inscription likely records a measurement, possibly for a boundary or a construction, and dates to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-04900603.text_edition: \n Lucius Avillius Primulus sibi et Domitiae Optatae uxori\n
EDCS-04900603.translation: Lucius Avillius Primulus for himself and his wife Domitia Optata.
EDCS-04900603.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Lucius Avillius Primulus for himself and his wife, likely for a funerary monument or a religious offering, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05000462.text_edition: \n Bandu Vordeaeco sacrum Sulpicius Paternus votum solvit\n
EDCS-05000462.translation: Sacred to Bandu Vordeaeco; Sulpicius Paternus fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05000462.summary: This votive inscription records Sulpicius Paternus fulfilling a vow to the deity Bandu Vordeaeco, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05000468.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Aplonus et Saturninus votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-05000468.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest; Aplonus and Saturninus willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-05000468.summary: This votive inscription records Aplonus and Saturninus fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05000477.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Flavio Valerio Constantino Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo C\n
EDCS-05000477.translation: To our lord Flavius Valerius Constantinus, Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered, Augustus, pontifex maximus...
EDCS-05000477.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Constantine I, likely part of a public building or monument, dating to his reign (306-337 AD).

EDCS-05000480.translation: To Silvinus, Gaius Grattius Senilis, his friend.
EDCS-05000480.summary: This is a dedication, likely funerary, from Gaius Grattius Senilis to his friend Silvinus, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05000483.text_edition: \n Avianus exs sic voto solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-05000483.translation: Avianus fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-05000483.summary: This votive inscription records Avianus fulfilling a vow, likely to a deity or for a deceased person, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05000484.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum FESTV MA S A V P N A T P P III C\n
EDCS-05000484.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed...
EDCS-05000484.summary: This very fragmentary inscription is dedicated to the 'Dis Manibus' (Spirits of the Departed), likely a funerary monument, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05000485.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Fortunatus annorum XX \n
EDCS-05000485.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed; Fortunatus, aged 20.
EDCS-05000485.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Fortunatus, aged 20, dedicated to the 'Dis Manibus', dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05000486.text_edition: \n ame uxsori sic Ammonis V A P Ammoni an(n)orum L sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-05000486.translation: To the wife, also of Ammonis... aged 50. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05000486.summary: This funerary inscription, possibly for the wife of Ammonis, includes the common wish 'sit tibi terra levis' (may the earth lie lightly upon you), dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05200366.text_edition: \n Marci Corneli Marci fili Aniensis Foro Iulii militis legionis X geminae centuria Terebrae annorum XXII I hic situs est\n
EDCS-05200366.translation: Marcus Cornelius, son of Marcus, of the Aniensis tribe, a soldier of the Tenth Legion Gemina, of Terebra's century, aged 22, lies here.
EDCS-05200366.summary: This is a tombstone for a soldier named Marcus Cornelius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05200384.text_edition: \n Monis Bocouriqum Allonis filii annorum XXX hic situs \n
EDCS-05200384.translation: Monis, son of Bocouriqus, aged 30, lies here.
EDCS-05200384.summary: This is a tombstone for an individual named Monis, likely of non-Roman origin, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05200386.text_edition: \n Domitia Vicci Malugeniqum filia Luraezi Aucaliqum uxsor sic hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-05200386.translation: Domitia, daughter of Viccus Malugenis, wife of Luraezus Aucalis, lies here; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05200386.summary: This is a tombstone for a woman named Domitia, the wife of Luraezus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05200387.text_edition: \n Ammisa Benilti Aeturiqum filia Clouti Maureicum uxor hic sita est\n
EDCS-05200387.translation: Ammisa, daughter of Beniltus, wife of Cloutus Maureicus, lies here.
EDCS-05200387.summary: This is a tombstone for a woman named Ammisa, wife of Cloutus Maureicus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05200390.summary: This is a dedication to an unconquered god, likely Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05200392.summary: This is a tombstone for an individual named Severinus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05200395.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription mentioning Quintus Sertorius, who held the office of proconsul, likely dating to the Roman Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-05200397.text_edition: \n Lucius Cornelius Luci libertus Sorex hic situs est\n
EDCS-05200397.translation: Lucius Cornelius Sorex, freedman of Lucius, lies here.
EDCS-05200397.summary: This is a tombstone for a freedman named Lucius Cornelius Sorex, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05200398.text_edition: \n Firmana pia in suos annorum XXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-05200398.translation: Firmana the dutiful, aged 25, lies here among her own people; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05200398.summary: This is a tombstone for a woman named Firmana, indicating her pietas towards her family, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05200400.text_edition: \n Lasciva nutrix indulgentissuma sic annorum LVIIII pro meritis suis Lucius Aelius Celer Ingenuus posuit maestitia luctuque impensam funeris locum sepulturae monumentum ordoordo hic sita est \n
EDCS-05200400.translation: Lasciva, a most indulgent nurse, aged 59, lies here. Lucius Aelius Celer Ingenuus erected this monument for her, for her merits, with great sadness and grief, paying for the funeral expenses and the burial place.
EDCS-05200400.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Lucius Aelius Celer Ingenuus for his nurse Lasciva, detailing her age and his affection, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05200401.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Laodamiae annorum XXX Hedistus uxori dulcissimae hic sita est\n
EDCS-05200401.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Laodamia, aged 30, lies here, the dearest wife of Hedistus.
EDCS-05200401.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Laodamia, dedicated by her husband Hedistus.

EDCS-05200402.text_edition: \n Cania Maxsumi sic liberta hic sita est\n
EDCS-05200402.translation: Cania, freedwoman of Maxsumus, lies here.
EDCS-05200402.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cania, a freedwoman.

EDCS-05200404.text_edition: \n Tur Maxim-- Olari hic sit-- est\n
EDCS-05200404.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for Tur Maximus Olarius.

EDCS-05200405.text_edition: \n Caius Granius Luci filius Africanus annorum XIIX hic stetit\n
EDCS-05200405.translation: Gaius Granius Africanus, son of Lucius, aged 18, stood here.
EDCS-05200405.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Gaius Granius Africanus, son of Lucius, at the age of 18.

EDCS-05200406.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely records the name Lucius Serranius.

EDCS-05200407.text_edition: \n Serranius er annorum XXV hic situs est\n
EDCS-05200407.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Serranius, aged 25.

EDCS-05200408.text_edition: \n Lucius Cornelius Cai filius Serg(ia) hic situs est Varisdia Cai filia Maxuma rapta crudelibus fatis Cornelia mater se viva f(ecit) vos sic venio\n
EDCS-05200408.translation: Lucius Cornelius, son of Caius, of the Sergia tribe, lies here. Varisdia, daughter of Caius, Maxuma, snatched away by cruel fate, and Cornelia, mother, made this tomb for herself while alive. I come to you thus.
EDCS-05200408.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Cornelius and Varisdia, dedicated by Cornelia, the mother, and expresses grief over their untimely deaths.

EDCS-05200410.text_edition: \n Rogius Titi libertus Amandus annorum V hic situs est\n
EDCS-05200410.translation: Amandus, freedman of Rogius Titius, aged 5, lies here.
EDCS-05200410.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Amandus, a freedman, aged 5.

EDCS-05200413.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iulius Paternus sibi et Corneliae Maae bene merenti titulum (ex) testamento de commune posuit Cornelia annorum XXXV Paternus annorum LXV hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis\n
EDCS-05200413.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Paternus set up this monument according to his will for himself and for Cornelia Maae, who deserved well. Cornelia aged 35, Paternus aged 65, lie here. May the earth be light for you both.
EDCS-05200413.summary: This is a funerary epitaph by Iulius Paternus for himself and his wife Cornelia Maae, detailing their ages and erected by will.

EDCS-05200414.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. [---]ucio.
EDCS-05200414.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph, likely beginning with a dedication to the Spirits of the Departed and a name fragment.

EDCS-05200415.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Plotius Rhodanus ex voto\n
EDCS-05200415.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest. Plotius Rhodanus, fulfilling his vow.
EDCS-05200415.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter by Plotius Rhodanus, likely on a shrine or altar, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-05200416.text_edition: \n Rubria Calitice hic sita est sit terra levis\n
EDCS-05200416.translation: Rubria Calitice lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-05200416.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of Rubria Calitice, with a common wish for her repose.

EDCS-05200420.text_edition: \n Fusca Luci Postumi annorum XXX sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-05200420.translation: Fusca, daughter of Lucius Postumius, aged 30. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-05200420.summary: This tombstone records the death of Fusca, daughter of Lucius Postumius, aged 30.

EDCS-05500757.text_edition: \n Aemilio Severino Calpurniani filio Aemilia Severina Aemilius Severus\n
EDCS-05500757.translation: Aemilia Severina and Aemilius Severus [made this] for their son Aemilius Severinus, son of Calpurnianus.
EDCS-05500757.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Aemilia Severina and Aemilius Severus for their son, Aemilius Severinus.

EDCS-05500760.translation: His parents arranged for this to be made for Popillius Flavus, son of Campanus, aged 28.
EDCS-05500760.summary: This funerary inscription indicates that parents commissioned the monument for their son, Popillius Flavus, who died at age 28.

EDCS-05501709.translation: Lucius Allius Gloutius, son of Sextus, lies here.
EDCS-05501709.summary: This is a simple tombstone indicating the burial of Lucius Allius Gloutius.

EDCS-05501727.text_edition: \n Lucius Valerius Silvanus miles legionis VI victricis Turiaco solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-05501727.translation: Lucius Valerius Silvanus, a soldier of the VI Victrix legion, willingly paid his dues at Turiaco.
EDCS-05501727.summary: This inscription records a soldier, Lucius Valerius Silvanus of the VI Victrix legion, fulfilling an obligation at Turiaco.

EDCS-05501741.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Valerius Vilianus ex voto\n
EDCS-05501741.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest. Valerius Vilianus, fulfilling his vow.
EDCS-05501741.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter by Valerius Vilianus, likely on a shrine or altar, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-05501749.text_edition: \n Diis Severis in hoc templo locatis aedem Gaius C3 Calpurnius Rufinus vir clarissimus\n
EDCS-05501749.translation: For the Severi Gods, established in this temple. Gaius Calpurnius Rufinus, a man of the highest distinction, [built] the temple.
EDCS-05501749.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a temple by Gaius Calpurnius Rufinus for the 'Severi Gods'.

EDCS-05501752.text_edition: \n Diis cum hoc et lacum quo voto miscetur Gaius C Calpurnius Rufinus vir clarissimus\n
EDCS-05501752.translation: For the Gods, along with this pool in which it is mixed by vow. Gaius Calpurnius Rufinus, a man of the highest distinction.
EDCS-05501752.summary: This inscription, likely related to the previous one, mentions Gaius Calpurnius Rufinus dedicating a pool for the Gods.

EDCS-05501786.translation: To Gaius Caesar, son of Augustus, pontiff, augur. Callaecia.
EDCS-05501786.summary: This inscription honors Gaius Caesar, son of Augustus, a prominent figure, possibly commemorating a local dedication or his connection to the region of Callaecia.

EDCS-05501787.text_edition: \n Caio Caleronio Cai filio Camilia Miccioni tribuno plebis praetori legato Augusti Hispaniae citerioris legato Augusti legionis II Augustae proconsuli provinciae Baeticae praefecto aerarii militaris praefecto reliquorum exigendorum populi Romani cives Romani qui negotiantur Bracaraugustae\n
EDCS-05501787.translation: To Gaius Calerionius, son of Gaius, of the Camilia tribe, former tribune of the plebs, praetor, imperial legate of Hispania Citerior, legate of the II Augusta legion, proconsul of the province Baetica, prefect of the military treasury, prefect for the collection of outstanding debts from Roman citizens trading in Bracara Augusta.
EDCS-05501787.summary: This lengthy inscription lists the extensive career of Gaius Calerionius, detailing his military and administrative posts, likely set up by the Roman citizens trading in Bracara Augusta to honor him.

EDCS-05501831.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Gaius Valens, a veteran of the VI Victrix Pia Fidelis legion, had this made for Macro, son of Alluquius, and Clutimonus, son of Alluquius, and for Aetura, daughter of Arquius, and Alluquius, son of Andergius.
EDCS-05501831.summary: This funerary inscription, dedicated by a veteran, records the death and burial of Macro, Clutimonus, Aetura, and Alluquius, likely family members.

EDCS-05501894.translation: To the Nymphs. Calpurnia Abana willingly fulfilled her vow to Aeboso, after a vision.
EDCS-05501894.summary: This votive inscription records Calpurnia Abana fulfilling a vow to the Nymphs, inspired by a vision.

EDCS-05501921.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Marci Aureli Antonini et Luci Aureli Veri Augustorum ob natalem aquilae vexillatio legionis VII geminae felicis sub cura Licini Paterni |(centurionis) legionis eiusdem et Hermetis Augustorum liberti procuratoris et Lucreti Paterni decurionis cohortis I Celtiberorum et Fabi Marciani beneficiarii procuratoris Augustorum et Iuli Iuliani signiferi legionis eiusdem IIII Idus Iunias Laeliano et Pastore consulibus\n
EDCS-05501921.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, for the health of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus and Lucius Aurelius Verus Augusti, on the anniversary of the eagle. The standard-bearer detachment of the VII Gemina Felix legion, under the supervision of Licinius Paternus, centurion of the same legion, and Hermes, freedman of the Augusti, procurator, and Lucretius Paternus, decurion of the I Cohort of Celtiberi, and Fabius Marcianus, beneficiarius of the procurator Augusti, and Julius Iulianus, standard-bearer of the same legion. The fourth day before the Ides of June, during the consulship of Laelianus and Pastor.
EDCS-05501921.summary: This military inscription records a dedication to Jupiter by a detachment of the VII Gemina Felix legion for the health of the emperors Marcus Aurelius and Lucius Verus, dated to June 10th, 161 AD.

EDCS-05501923.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Marci Aureli Commodi Antonini Augusti ob natalem aquilae vexillatio legionis VII geminae fidelis sub cura Aureli Eutychetis Augusti liberti procuratoris et Valeri Semproniani decurionis alae II Flaviae IIII Idus Iunias Marullio sic et Aeliano consulibus\n
EDCS-05501923.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, for the health of Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Augustus, on the anniversary of the eagle. The standard-bearer detachment of the VII Gemina Fidelis legion, under the supervision of Aurelius Eutyches, freedman of Augustus, procurator, and Valerius Sempronianus, decurion of the II Flavia wing. The fourth day before the Ides of June, during the consulship of Marullinus and Aelianus.
EDCS-05501923.summary: This military dedication to Jupiter by a detachment of the VII Gemina Fidelis legion, made for the health of Emperor Commodus, is dated to June 10th, 180 AD.

EDCS-05501978.text_edition: \n Deo Aerno Marcus Placidius Placidianus votum libens reddidit\n
EDCS-05501978.translation: To the god Aerno. Marcus Placidius Placidianus willingly offered his vow.
EDCS-05501978.summary: This votive inscription records Marcus Placidius Placidianus fulfilling a vow to the deity Aerno.

EDCS-05501985.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Calpurnia Severinus and his very dear daughter Calpurnia, aged 18.
EDCS-05501985.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Calpurnia, daughter of Severinus, who died at the age of 18, and possibly her father Severinus as well.

EDCS-05501987.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For our dearest son Aurelius, aged 23. To Maximina.
EDCS-05501987.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Aurelius, who died at the age of 23, and also includes a separate, brief memorial to Maximina.

EDCS-05501988.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. For Clotina, wife of Magilo, aged 35.
EDCS-05501988.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Clotina, wife of Magilo, at the age of 35.

EDCS-05501989.translation: To the departed spirits. For Cuneirus, freedman of Aleucia, aged 57.
EDCS-05501989.summary: A tombstone for Cuneirus, a freedman, who died at age 57, recorded on a funerary monument.

EDCS-05501995.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Messi(a), aged 15.
EDCS-05501995.summary: A funerary inscription for Messi(a), who died at age 15.

EDCS-05501998.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Veteulenus, aged 32. Sacred to the departed spirits. For Felicula, aged 7.
EDCS-05501998.summary: A tombstone recording the deaths of two individuals, Veteulenus (aged 32) and Felicula (aged 7).

EDCS-05502007.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Fabius Aco Catullinus, a man of consular rank, governor of the province of Gallaecia, set this up for his own health and that of all his family.
EDCS-05502007.summary: An inscription dedicating an offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Fabius Aco Catullinus, a former consul and governor of Gallaecia, for the well-being of himself and his family.

EDCS-05502010.translation: ] Memmius Aniensis Barbarus, priest of Rome and the Emperors, at the Grove of Augustus, Flamen of the province of Hispania Citerior, military tribune of the I Italica legion, aged 59, lies here.
EDCS-05502010.summary: A funerary inscription for Memmius Aniensis Barbarus, a prominent religious and military official, who died at age 59.

EDCS-05502011.translation: Quintus Cumelius Rufinus, son of Quintus Cumelius Celer Bracarensis, a veteran of the II Adjutrix legion, aged 75, lies here. Quintus Cumelius Rufinus, soldier of the II Adjutrix legion, his heir, and Cumelius Mascellio, his freedman, set this up.
EDCS-05502011.summary: A funerary monument erected by a freedman and son for Quintus Cumelius Celer Bracarensis, a veteran of the II Adjutrix legion, and for his son, Quintus Cumelius Rufinus, a soldier in the same legion.

EDCS-05502012.translation: To the underworld spirits. To Placidus, a veteran of the VII Gemina Pia Felix legion, lived 57 years. Papia Maximina set up this memorial to her incomparable husband.
EDCS-05502012.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated by Papia Maximina to her husband Placidus, a veteran of the VII Gemina Pia Felix legion, who lived 57 years.

EDCS-05502015.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marrinia Procula, daughter of Marcus, most holy and most chaste wife. Truttedius Clemens, procurator of Asturia and Gallaecia, Dalmatia and Histria.
EDCS-05502015.summary: A funerary dedication for Marrinia Procula, wife of Truttedius Clemens, a procurator responsible for several Roman provinces.

EDCS-05502018.translation: To the departed spirits. For Julia Propidi. Publius Aelius Eustomus, freedman of the Emperor, to his wife.
EDCS-05502018.summary: A funerary inscription for Julia Propidi, dedicated by her husband Publius Aelius Eustomus, an imperial freedman.

EDCS-05502024.translation: To the departed spirits. For Aelia Rhodina, aged 25, most devoted wife. Calpurnius Calvus.
EDCS-05502024.summary: A tombstone for Aelia Rhodina, who died at age 25, dedicated by her husband Calpurnius Calvus.

EDCS-05502031.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Pompeia Musa, aged 18. Pompeia Epictesis [dedicated this] to her most devoted and most holy sister.
EDCS-05502031.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a sister to Pompeia Musa, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502038.translation: Tullius Maximus, rector of the Aeneadae, dedicates this. Who is the seventh legion, a twin? He himself, honored with the highest praise, snatched it from a bear.
EDCS-05502038.summary: A fragmented inscription, likely an epigram or dedication, possibly relating to military achievement by a commander named Tullius Maximus, referencing the seventh legion and a victory over a bear.

EDCS-05502039.translation: For the health and long duration of the empire of the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus and Julia Pia Felix Augusta, mother of Antoninus Augustus, governor and consul [legate] of the province of Hispania Nova Citerior, first sent by him after the province was divided, Gaius Julius Cerealis, consul [legate] of the Emperor, proconsul of the province of Hispania Nova Citerior Antoniniana.
EDCS-05502039.summary: A dedication to the imperial family for the health of the empire, commemorating Gaius Julius Cerealis, a governor of Hispania Nova Citerior, likely from the Antonine period.

EDCS-05502041.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, conqueror of Parthia, conqueror of Britain, conqueror of Germany, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the 19th time, consul for the 4th time, general for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, proconsul. The mounted troops of the legion VII Antoniniana Pia Felix, devoted to his divine majesty and authority. Dedicated on the seventh day before the kalends of October, when Catius Sabinus and Cornelius Anullinus were consuls.
EDCS-05502041.summary: An official dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, listing his titles and honors, made by the mounted troops of the 7th Gemina legion, dated to 191 AD.

EDCS-05502066.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Alloni annorum XXV Mercurius et taVitalis filio sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-05502066.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Allonus, aged 25. Mercurius and Vitalis to their son. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05502066.summary: A funerary epitaph for Allonus, erected by his parents Mercurius and Vitalis, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502070.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Aurelius Fraternus [of the tribe Quirina?], aged 20.
EDCS-05502070.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Aurelius Fraternus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502075.translation: To the spirits of the departed. His wife erected this monument out of devotion. To Assatus, who lived 25 years. Pious in his affections. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05502075.summary: A funerary epitaph for Assatus, erected by his devoted wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502083.text_edition: \n Fonti sacrum Brocci libertus Vipstanus Alexis aquilegus votum solvit libens merito Genio \n
EDCS-05502083.translation: Sacred to the Spring. Broccus' freedman Vipstanus Alexis, a water-carrier, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Genius.
EDCS-05502083.summary: A dedication to a water deity (Fonti) and a Genius, made by Vipstanus Alexis, a freedman and water-carrier, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502085.translation: Monument to Andotus Flavius, son of Arenus, aged 60. He is buried here.
EDCS-05502085.summary: A funerary epitaph for Andotus Flavius, son of Arenus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502092.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Augusto Divi filio consuli XIII imperatori XX pontifici maximo patri patriae tribunicia potestate XXXII [] sacrum\n
EDCS-05502092.translation: Sacred to the Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the Divine [Julius Caesar], consul for the 13th time, general for the 20th time, chief priest, father of the fatherland, holding tribunician power for the 32nd time.
EDCS-05502092.summary: An inscription recording titles and honors for Emperor Augustus, likely related to a public monument or dedication, dating to 11 AD.

EDCS-05502094.translation: Pleveius, with the father of fathers Leone Mithrae presiding, set up this altar to the unconquered god Augustus.
EDCS-05502094.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar to the god Augustus by Pleveius, dated by the presence of the patron of patrons Leone Mithrae.

EDCS-05502098.translation: The monument of Fuscus Cabedi, son of Ambatus, of Vadinis, aged 25, is placed here.
EDCS-05502098.summary: This is a tombstone for Fuscus Cabedi, a 25-year-old man from Vadinis.

EDCS-05502101.translation: His father erected this monument for Penti Flavius Victor, son of Vadinius, aged 30, because of his merits.
EDCS-05502101.summary: This inscription records a father setting up a tombstone for his son, Penti Flavius Victor, aged 30.

EDCS-05502102.translation: To the departed gods, Antonius Flaccus of Vadinis erected this monument to his wife Terentia, daughter of Aronaecivorus, aged 41, in the 338th year of the consulship. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502102.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Antonius Flaccus for his wife Terentia, dated by a consulship year and including the common epitaph 'sit tibi terra levis'.

EDCS-05502103.translation: To the departed gods, Terentius Boddus of Vadinis erected this monument for his dear mother Voccareca, aged 28, in the 328th year of the consulship. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502103.summary: This inscription records the erection of a tombstone by Terentius Boddus for his mother Voccareca, dated by a consulship year and including the common epitaph 'sit tibi terra levis'.

EDCS-05502118.translation: Valeria Annula, his wife, made this according to the will for Flavius Comenescius, son of Flavius, from Cauca. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502118.summary: This inscription records a wife, Valeria Annula, fulfilling her husband Flavius Comenescius's will by erecting this monument, which includes the epitaph 'sit tibi terra levis'.

EDCS-05502119.translation: The monument of Fuscus, son of Papirius Interamnicus, aged [3?] is placed here.
EDCS-05502119.summary: This is a tombstone for Fuscus, son of Papirius Interamnicus, with an incomplete age recorded.

EDCS-05502120.translation: The associates arranged for the construction of this monument for Gaius Pompeius Mucro of Uxame, aged 90.
EDCS-05502120.summary: This inscription records that associates commissioned a monument for Gaius Pompeius Mucro of Uxame, aged 90.

EDCS-05502121.translation: The associates arranged for the construction of this monument for Valerius Annonus, son of Luguadicos, of Uxame, aged 25.
EDCS-05502121.summary: This inscription records that associates commissioned a monument for Valerius Annonus, son of Luguadicos, of Uxame, aged 25.

EDCS-05502124.translation: Sacred to the departed gods. To Arronis Camalicus, [his most beloved father?], his son erected [this]. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502124.summary: This is a funerary inscription to Arronis Camalicus, likely a father, erected by his son and including the epitaph 'sit tibi terra levis'.

EDCS-05502131.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Domitia Quinta, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502132.translation: To Licinius Caenon, aged 40. Licinia Aprilia, his wife, had this made for herself and her husband. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502132.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a wife for herself and her deceased husband, Licinius Caenon, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502138.text_edition: \n Sempronio Campano annorum Aemilia Alla faciendum curavit sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-05502138.translation: Aemilia Alla had this made for Sempronius Campanus, aged [unknown]. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502138.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Aemilia Alla for Sempronius Campanus, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502152.translation: Sacred to Fortuna Balnearis. Quintus Valerius Bucco, a soldier of the Second Adjutrix Pia Fidelis Legion, [serving] under the century of Aemilius Secundus, [dedicates this].
EDCS-05502152.summary: This is a religious dedication to the goddess Fortuna Balnearis by a soldier serving in the Roman army, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502161.translation: Madicenus Vailico, son of Accon, lies here.
EDCS-05502161.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Madicenus Vailico, son of Accon, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502162.translation: To Aiioragatus. Lucius Aemilius Quartio, a stonemason, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05502162.summary: This is a votive inscription where a stonemason, Lucius Aemilius Quartio, fulfilled a vow to Aiioragatus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502163.translation: To Fortuna Redux. Gnaeus Tautius Moschas, freedman of Semnius, [dedicated this] in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-05502163.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Fortuna Redux by a freedman named Moschas, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502164.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Titus Valerius Flaccus, [dedicates this] for the safety of his son, Titus Valerius Flavinius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow. Aligenus [also associated].
EDCS-05502164.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the health of the dedicator's son, by Titus Valerius Flaccus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502166.translation: Terentius (?) Fraternus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Gallic Mothers.
EDCS-05502166.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Matres Gallicae by Terentius Fraternus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502170.translation: For the safety of the Emperor Caesar Hadrianus Augustus [and] of the colony of Clunium. Publius Aelius, freedman of Augustus, [dedicates this].
EDCS-05502170.summary: This is a public inscription recording a dedication for the safety of Emperor Hadrian and the colony of Clunium, by a freedman of Augustus, likely from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-05502172.translation: To Gaius Calvisius Sabinus, son of Aiionis, of the Galeria tribe, magistrate, flamen of Rome and of the deified Augustus, because he gave grain to the people when the food supply was dear, his friends.
EDCS-05502172.summary: This is a dedication by friends to Gaius Calvisius Sabinus, a magistrate and flamen, for providing grain to the people, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-05502175.translation: To Lucius Valerius Crescens, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, Bundalicon, his father, Valeria Paterna, to her father.
EDCS-05502175.summary: This is a dedication by Valeria Paterna to her father, Lucius Valerius Crescens Bundalicon, likely a funerary monument from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502176.text_edition: \n Attuae Boutae Bouti filiae Intercatiensi annorum XXX Aius Antonius uxori suae faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-05502176.translation: Aius Antonius had this made for his wife Attua Bota, daughter of Boutis, from Intercatius, aged thirty.
EDCS-05502176.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Aius Antonius for his wife Attua Bota, who died at the age of thirty, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502177.translation: Marcus Aemilius Murrianus, son of Carbilius, from Uxama, aged sixty-three. [His freedman?] ...
EDCS-05502177.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Marcus Aemilius Murrianus, who died at the age of sixty-three, with an incomplete record of the dedicator, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502178.translation: To Coemea Betunia, daughter of Citi, aged seventy-one. Tulia had this made for her mother.
EDCS-05502178.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Tulia for her mother, Coemea Betunia, who died at the age of seventy-one, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502179.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Cornelius Avianus and Aemilia Atia had this made for their most devoted son, Gaius Cornelius Maternus, aged twenty.
EDCS-05502179.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Cornelius Avianus and Aemilia Atia for their son Gaius Cornelius Maternus, who died at the age of twenty, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502180.translation: Abatus had this made for his son Fabio Silvino, aged thirty-one.
EDCS-05502180.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Abatus for his son Fabio Silvino, who died at the age of thirty-one, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502181.text_edition: \n Firmo annorum LX Mascellioni annorum XV\n
EDCS-05502181.translation: To Firminus, aged sixty. To Mascellio, aged fifteen.
EDCS-05502181.summary: This inscription records the ages of two individuals, Firminus (sixty) and Mascellio (fifteen), likely on a shared funerary monument from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502182.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Iulia Paterna for her daughter Memmia Materna, aged three.
EDCS-05502182.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Iulia Paterna for her daughter Memmia Materna, who died at the age of three, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502183.translation: To the Divine Spirits. The mistress placed this and had it made for her slave girl Lupia, aged thirty.
EDCS-05502183.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a mistress for her slave girl Lupia, who died at the age of thirty, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502186.translation: To the departed spirits. To Patricia, my excellent comrade-in-arms, Valerius Quintus had this made.
EDCS-05502186.summary: A tombstone dedicated by Valerius Quintus to his partner Patricia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502187.translation: Lucius Pompeius Gustumus, son of Ammon, is buried here.
EDCS-05502187.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Lucius Pompeius Gustumus, son of Ammon, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502188.translation: To Lucius Pompeius Paternus, son of Paternus, aged 19. Pompeia Paterna, to her brother, had this made.
EDCS-05502188.summary: A tombstone erected by Pompeia Paterna for her brother Lucius Pompeius Paternus, who died at age 19, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502189.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Pompeio Serano annorum XXXXV et Lucio Caelio Paterno annorum XXXV Pompeia Iustilla fratri piissimo et filio\n
EDCS-05502189.translation: To the departed spirits. To Gaius Pompeius Seranus, aged 45, and Lucius Caelian Paternus, aged 35. Pompeia Iustilla, to her most dutiful brother and son, had this made.
EDCS-05502189.summary: A tombstone commemorating two individuals, Gaius Pompeius Seranus and Lucius Caelian Paternus, erected by Pompeia Iustilla, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502190.translation: To Titus Pompeius Urcalocus, of the Galeria tribe. Pompeia Pusinna had this made for her husband.
EDCS-05502190.summary: A tombstone erected by Pompeia Pusinna for her husband Titus Pompeius Urcalocus, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502191.translation: To Acutia Proculina. Proculinus, for his daughter, out of dutiful affection, had this made. To the departed spirits and their memory.
EDCS-05502191.summary: A tombstone dedicated by Proculinus to his daughter Acutia Proculina, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502192.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gaio Pullio Quirina Mercuriali Dionisi sic filio IIviro annorum XXXII Lucius Pullius Cani patri faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-05502192.translation: To the departed spirits. To Gaius Pullius Mercurialis, son of Dionisus, of the Quirina tribe, a magistrate, aged 32. Lucius Pullius Cani, to his father, had this made.
EDCS-05502192.summary: A tombstone erected by Lucius Pullius Cani for his father Gaius Pullius Mercurialis, a magistrate who died at 32, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502193.translation: Reburrus Pingancon, son of Melman, is buried here. Pupilius, his brother and heir, had this made at his own expense.
EDCS-05502193.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Reburrus Pingancon, with the monument erected by his brother and heir Pupilius, likely from Roman Britain.

EDCS-05502194.translation: To Sempronius Seranus. For his parents, N.
EDCS-05502194.summary: A fragmentary tombstone mentioning Sempronius Seranus and an indication of parental dedication, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502195.translation: To the departed spirits. To Terentia Quieta, aged 60. Gaius Trebius Lupus and Gaius Trebius Quietus, her sons, to their most dutiful mother. Prosperus made this.
EDCS-05502195.summary: A tombstone erected by Gaius Trebius Lupus and Gaius Trebius Quietus for their mother Terentia Quieta, also mentioning Prosperus, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502196.translation: To Gaius Valerius [?], of the Anicia family; Lucius Valerius [?]; Gaius Valerius M[?]
EDCS-05502196.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription likely commemorating members of the Valerius family, possibly from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05502197.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Valerio Galeria Marciano annorum XXIX Marcus Valerius Paternus vatricus sic et Valeria Titulla filio pientissumo sic\n
EDCS-05502197.translation: To the Divine Shades. Marcus Valerius Paternus, his father, and Valeria Titulla, his mother, had this made for their most dutiful son, Lucius Valerius Galerianus, aged 29.
EDCS-05502197.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Valerius Galerianus, erected by his parents, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502198.translation: Gaius Vatricus Naevos, aged 26. Acca, his mother, had this made for her son and herself.
EDCS-05502198.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Vatricus Naevos, erected by his mother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502199.translation: [?] Viscunos, aged 20. Arria Paterna, his mother, and Sempronia, his sister, had this made.
EDCS-05502199.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Viscunos, erected by his mother and sister, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502200.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary epitaph for a boy named Viscunos, indicating his age.

EDCS-05502202.translation: In these lands of Aurelia Juventiana Pegasus [?], good [?], [?] the good [?].
EDCS-05502202.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription concerning land ownership or management, mentioning Aurelia Juventiana, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502227.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Eudemus [?] and Modestus [?], freedmen, had this made for their patron.
EDCS-05502227.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the erection of a monument by freedmen for their patron, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502229.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Urbanus and Primitiva [had this made] for their dearest daughter, aged 9.
EDCS-05502229.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl, erected by her parents, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502237.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Pompeia Nitliata Candide sic Cossouqum filia sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-05502237.translation: To the Divine Shades. Pompeia Nitliata, her daughter Candide, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502237.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pompeia Nitliata, mentioning her daughter Candide, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502239.translation: Hospitality. To Matubos, Fronto [son?] of [?], fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-05502239.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfillment, possibly related to hospitality or a guest-friendship, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502241.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Caius Pomponius Dionysius, in fulfillment of a vow, for the arrival of our lords and emperors.
EDCS-05502241.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter, likely made in the 2nd or 3rd century CE, to mark the arrival of the emperors.

EDCS-05502244.translation: To Gaius Dellius Flavius, son of Marcus, of the Pomptina tribe, a veteran of the VII Gemina Felix legion, aged 61. Dellius Flavus [erected this].
EDCS-05502244.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Dellius Flavius, a veteran of the VII Gemina Felix legion, erected by his relative Dellius Flavus, probably dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05502246.translation: I ask you passing by, say: may the earth be light to Hilario, son of Flavius, a servant from Carpetani, from Uxama, aged 20.
EDCS-05502246.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Hilario, a servant from Uxama, asking for a light burial, probably from the late 1st to early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05502247.translation: Ambata Albeauca, daughter of Segovetis, aged 20.
EDCS-05502247.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age of Ambata Albeauca, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-05502248.translation: Ambata Paesica, wife of ArgaMonica, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-05502248.summary: This inscription records Ambata Paesica, wife of ArgaMonica, who commissioned a monument, probably from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-05502249.translation: To Ambata Terentia, daughter of Severus, aged 60. Sempronia Candiadiana [erected this] to her mother.
EDCS-05502249.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Sempronia Candiadiana for her mother, Ambata Terentia, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05502250.translation: Antonius arranged for this to be made for his son.
EDCS-05502250.summary: This is a funerary commission by Antonius for his son, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-05502251.translation: Apolonius Turinus, son of Aipon, aged 22 and a half, lies here.
EDCS-05502251.summary: This is a tombstone for Apolonius Turinus, son of Aipon, who died at age 22 and a half, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-05502252.translation: Arcea Plandica, daughter of Matricula, aged 30.
EDCS-05502252.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age of Arcea Plandica, daughter of Matricula, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-05502253.translation: Marcus Secundus made this at his own expense for Atilia Betoona, freedwoman of Mecanus, aged 60.
EDCS-05502253.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Marcus Secundus at his own expense for his freedwoman Atilia Betoona, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-05502254.translation: To Boso, for his sister of 30 years, daughter of Marcus. A brother saw to the making of this cenotaph.
EDCS-05502254.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Boso, commemorating his sister, likely erected by her brother.

EDCS-05502255.summary: This is a funerary inscription or a name marker for Cabedus, son of Seggues Ambatus.

EDCS-05502256.translation: To Campanus Paterno, son of Flavius. Publius Valerius Agathopus and Materna, his sister, saw to its making. He was 55 years old.
EDCS-05502256.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Campanus Paterno, erected by his sister and another individual, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05502257.translation: To Carilla, for her son Flavius, 20 years old.
EDCS-05502257.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavius, erected by his mother Carilla.

EDCS-05502258.translation: To Coemea Desica, daughter of Aquinus. Gaius Valerius Turancicus saw to the making of this for his mother. She was 60 years old.
EDCS-05502258.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Coemea Desica, erected by her son Gaius Valerius Turancicus.

EDCS-05502261.translation: Madicenus Calaetus, son of Ambatus, 55 years old.
EDCS-05502261.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Madicenus Calaetus, son of Ambatus.

EDCS-05502262.summary: This is a dedication or funerary inscription to Marcellus Aurelius.

EDCS-05502263.translation: Secoveso, son of Lougei, 20 years old. He lies here.
EDCS-05502263.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Secoveso, son of Lougei, stating he is buried here.

EDCS-05502264.text_edition: \n Tito Sempronio Valentis filio <Q=C>uirina Reservato annorum XL \n
EDCS-05502264.translation: To Titus Sempronius Valentis, son of Quirina. Reservatus, 40 years old.
EDCS-05502264.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Sempronius Valentis, possibly identified as Reservatus, who was 40 years old.

EDCS-05502266.translation: To Titulla Ticconia, 20 years old. Barc() saw to the making of this monument.
EDCS-05502266.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titulla Ticconia, erected by Barc( ).

EDCS-05502267.translation: To Valerius Seranus, son of Opidanus, aged 25.
EDCS-05502267.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Valerius Seranus, son of Opidanus, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-05502269.translation: To Titus Valerius Severus, freedman of Severina, aged 49. Severinus (made this).
EDCS-05502269.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Severinus to his freedman, Titus Valerius Severus, who died at 49.

EDCS-05502271.translation: To Gaius Caecilius Constans, son of Proculus. Proculus Caecilius made this for his father.
EDCS-05502271.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Proculus Caecilius to his father, Gaius Caecilius Constans.

EDCS-05502273.translation: To Briton, aged 14. May the earth lie lightly on you here.
EDCS-05502273.summary: This is a tombstone for Briton, aged 14, with a common epitaph.

EDCS-05502274.translation: To Ambata Veniaena, daughter of Valerius Crescens, aged 40.
EDCS-05502274.summary: This inscription commemorates Ambata Veniaena, daughter of Valerius Crescens, who died at 40.

EDCS-05502275.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Aurelius, who lived 50 years, is placed here. XVvir of the Senate made this in an assigned plot for the patron.
EDCS-05502275.summary: This tomb inscription records Marcus Aurelius, a *quindecimvir* of the Senate, who lived 50 years and was buried in an assigned plot.
