EDCS-05502947.translation: Sicceia Donata, freedwoman of Publius, placed this monument for her son Piero, who died aged 14.
EDCS-05502947.summary: This tombstone records Sicceia Donata setting up a monument for her young son Piero, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05502948.text_edition: \n Varro nnorum XVIII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-05502948.translation: Varro, aged 18, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05502948.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Varro, aged 18, and includes a common epitaph wishing him peace, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05502950.text_edition: \n Furia Tyche nnm XXVIII hic sita est\n
EDCS-05502950.translation: Furia Tyche, aged 28, is buried here.
EDCS-05502950.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Furia Tyche, aged 28, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05502951.text_edition: \n Marcus Sempronius Hymnus suo et Marci Semproni Reburri fili sui nomine macellum vetustate conlabsum sic sua pecunia restituerunt itemque et mensas lapideas posuerunt\n
EDCS-05502951.translation: Marcus Sempronius Hymnus and his son Marcus Sempronius Reburrus restored the market, which had collapsed from age, at their own expense, and also placed stone tables.
EDCS-05502951.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a market and the placement of stone tables by Marcus Sempronius Hymnus and his son, likely during the Imperial period.

EDCS-05502952.text_edition: \n Quinto Manlio Quinti filio Quirina Celsino IIviro flamini III Manlia Chrysis uxor\n
EDCS-05502952.translation: To Quintus Manlius Celsinus, son of Quintus, of the tribe Quirina, twice a priest, priest for three years. His wife Manlia Chrysis.
EDCS-05502952.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Quintus Manlius Celsinus by his wife, Manlia Chrysis, likely on a tomb or monument.

EDCS-05502953.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus - Alfius Zosimus annorum LV hic situs est homo optime sit tibi terra levis Alfia Rhode uxor annorum XLV hic sita est mulier optima sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-05502953.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Alfius Zosimus, aged 55, rests here, an excellent man; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Alfia Rhode, his wife, aged 45, rests here, an excellent woman; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502953.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Alfius Zosimus and his wife Alfia Rhode, recording their names, ages, and expressing wishes for their afterlife.

EDCS-05502955.translation: Lucius Terentius Mancinus, son of Marcus.
EDCS-05502955.summary: This inscription is a simple dedication or identification of Lucius Terentius Mancinus, son of Marcus.

EDCS-05502956.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Volcinaeo Callisto nnum XVIIII Volcinaea Calliste mater\n
EDCS-05502956.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Volcinaea Calliste, mother, [erected this for] Marcus Volcinaeus Callistus, aged 19.
EDCS-05502956.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication of Volcinaea Calliste, mother, for her deceased son, Marcus Volcinaeus Callistus, aged 19.

EDCS-05502957.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed.
EDCS-05502957.summary: This is a brief dedication, likely part of a larger funerary inscription, to the 'Spirits of the Departed'.

EDCS-05502958.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Fabius ianus annorum XXXV hic situs est pater filio pientissimo fecit\n
EDCS-05502958.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Fabius Ianus, aged 35, rests here. His father made this for his most devoted son.
EDCS-05502958.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Fabius Ianus, aged 35, is buried here, and that the monument was erected by his father.

EDCS-05502959.text_edition: \n Pro salute Augusti Veneri sacrum Cnaeus Octavius Florus IIIIIIvir Augustalis municipio dono dat dedicat loco dato ex decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-05502959.translation: For the health of Augustus. Gnaeus Octavius Florus, a sextumvir Augustalis, gives and dedicates this to Venus, in the town, on land granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05502959.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Venus by Gnaeus Octavius Florus, a local official, for the health of Augustus, on land granted by local authorities.

EDCS-05502961.text_edition: \n Cornelio Quinti filio Galeria Placido omnibus honoribus in republica sua functo Aemilia Luci filia Severina marito\n
EDCS-05502961.translation: Aemilia Severina, daughter of Lucius, [erected this] to her husband Cornelius Placidas, son of Quintus, of the tribe Galeria, who had held all offices in his republic.
EDCS-05502961.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Aemilia Severina dedicates a monument to her husband, Cornelius Placidas, acknowledging his public career.

EDCS-05502962.text_edition: \n Tito Iunio Titi filio Galeria Severo Dianensi omnibus honoribus in republica sua functo praefecto cohortis IIII Dalmatarum tribuno legionis XX Valeriae Victricis Lucius Sempronius Enipeus amico optimo\n
EDCS-05502962.translation: Lucius Sempronius Enipeus [erected this] to his best friend Titus Junius Severus, son of Titus, of the tribe Galeria, Dianensis, who had held all offices in his republic, was prefect of the 4th cohort of Dalmatians, and tribune of the 20th Legion Valeria Victrix.
EDCS-05502962.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Lucius Sempronius Enipeus dedicates a monument to his friend Titus Junius Severus, detailing his distinguished military and civil career.

EDCS-05502963.text_edition: \n Lucio Valerio Luci filio Galeria Propinquo omnibus honoribus in re publica sua functo adlecto in V decurias flamini provinciae Hispaniae citerioris Gamus et Trophime liberti patrono optimo et indulgentissimo\n
EDCS-05502963.translation: Gamus and Trophime, freedmen, [erected this] to their best and most indulgent patron, Lucius Valerius Propinquus, son of Lucius, of the tribe Galeria, who had held all offices in his republic, was admitted into the five decuries, and was flamen of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05502963.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication by freedmen Gamus and Trophime to their patron, Lucius Valerius Propinquus, listing his significant public offices.

EDCS-05502964.text_edition: \n Lucio Valerio Luci filio Galeria Propinquo flamini provinciae Hispaniae citerioris Baebi Iustus et Calpurnianus amico optimo\n
EDCS-05502964.translation: To Lucius Valerius Propinquus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, flamen of the province of Hispania Citerior, from his friends Baebius Iustus and Calpurnianus, his best friend.
EDCS-05502964.summary: This is a dedication from friends to Lucius Valerius Propinquus, a provincial flamen in Hispania Citerior, likely dating to the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05502967.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Urbanus veteranus princeps vexillationis legionis VII geminae piae felicis Maximinianae missus cum suis a Decio Valeriano consulari Lucius Alfius Donatus optio faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-05502967.translation: Gaius Julius Urbanus, a veteran, principal of a detachment of the VII Gemina Pia Felix Maximiana legion, discharged with his men by the consular Decius Valerianus. Lucius Alfius Donatus, optio, saw to its construction.
EDCS-05502967.summary: This inscription records the commissioning of a monument by Lucius Alfius Donatus, an optio, to honor Gaius Julius Urbanus, a veteran and commander of a legionary detachment, likely from the late 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05502968.text_edition: \n Aufidia Publi liberta Saturia et Aufidia Publi liberta Hediste sorores pientissimae\n
EDCS-05502968.translation: From Aufidia Saturia and Aufidia Hediste, freedwomen of Publius, sisters, most devoted.
EDCS-05502968.summary: This is a funerary dedication from two sisters, Aufidia Saturia and Aufidia Hediste, who were freedwomen of Publius, likely dating to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-05502969.text_edition: \n Calpurniae Marcellae Marcus Sempronius Fidus uxori optimae et dignissimae\n
EDCS-05502969.translation: To Calpurnia Marcella, from Marcus Sempronius Fidus, to his best and most worthy wife.
EDCS-05502969.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a husband, Marcus Sempronius Fidus, to his wife, Calpurnia Marcella, likely dating to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-05502971.text_edition: \n Quinto Sempronio Quinti filio Galeria Tauro decurioni municipii \n
EDCS-05502971.translation: To Quintus Sempronius Taurus, son of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe, decurion of the municipium.
EDCS-05502971.summary: This is a dedication to Quintus Sempronius Taurus, a decurion in a municipium, likely dating to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-05502972.text_edition: \n Publius Sicinius Publi libertus Eutychus medicus hic situs est\n
EDCS-05502972.translation: Publius Sicinius Eutychus, freedman of Publius, a doctor, is buried here.
EDCS-05502972.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Publius Sicinius Eutychus, a freedman and doctor, likely dating to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-05502975.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Titi Iuni Galeria Achillei decurialis scribae librarii quaestorii trium decuriarum marito pio et incomparabili Pacideia Hedone uxor commune monumentum fecit ut ab eo cum quo per annos quinque et triginta socia iucundam vitam aequabili concordia vixisset nec sepulcro separaretur\n
EDCS-05502975.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Pacideia Hedone, his wife, made this joint monument for Titus Junius Achilleus, of the Galeria tribe, a decurial scribe of the quaestor's office for three decuriae, a devoted and incomparable husband, so that she might not be separated in death from him with whom she had lived a joyful life in equal harmony for thirty-five years.
EDCS-05502975.summary: This is a joint funerary monument erected by Pacideia Hedone for her devoted husband Titus Junius Achilleus, a scribe in the quaestor's office, commemorating their 35 years of marriage and likely dating to the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-05502976.text_edition: \n Sexto Terentio Lemnaeo honore functo seviratus Sextus Terentius Lemnaeus filius et Aemilia Scintilla marito dignissimo\n
EDCS-05502976.translation: To Sextus Terentius Lemnaeus, who held the office of sevir. From Sextus Terentius Lemnaeus, his son, and Aemilia Scintilla, to their most worthy husband.
EDCS-05502976.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a son and his mother to their husband and father, Sextus Terentius Lemnaeus, who had held the position of sevir, likely dating to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-05502977.text_edition: \n Quinto Sempronio Quinti filio Galeria Valeriano Sempronii Valerianus et Marina filio dulcissimo ex decreto ordinis honore contenti impensam statuae remiserunt\n
EDCS-05502977.translation: Quintus Sempronius Valerianus and Marina, to their son Quintus Sempronius Taurus, of the Galeria tribe. Content with the honor granted to their dearest son by decree of the council, they waived the expense of a statue.
EDCS-05502977.summary: This inscription records the decision of the town council to grant an honor to Quintus Sempronius Taurus and the parents' subsequent waiver of the cost of a statue in his honor, likely dating to the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-05502978.text_edition: \n Beryllus venator annorum XXXV hic situs est Pausilipus sic conturbernali\n
EDCS-05502978.translation: Beryllus, hunter, aged 35, is buried here. Pausilipus, to his tent-mate.
EDCS-05502978.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Beryllus, a 35-year-old hunter, dedicated by his tent-mate, likely dating to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-05502980.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Appius Quinti filius Fulvianus annorum XIII Appia Saturnina filio piissimo fecit\n
EDCS-05502980.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Appius Fulvianus, son of Quintus, 13 years old, Appia Saturnina made this for her most devoted son.
EDCS-05502980.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Appia Saturnina for her 13-year-old son, Quintus Appius Fulvianus.

EDCS-05502981.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quintus Appius Marcellus civis Carthaginensis nnorum XVI hic situs est Quintus Appius Bithinias sic et Pompeia Gnato filio carissimo\n
EDCS-05502981.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Appius Marcellus, a citizen of Carthage, 16 years old, is buried here. Quintus Appius Bithinias and Pompeia Gnato made this for their dearest son.
EDCS-05502981.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Appius Marcellus, a 16-year-old Carthaginian citizen, erected by his parents.

EDCS-05502982.text_edition: \n Publio Atellio Publi filio Galeria Verecundo annorum XXX Homullus filio et sibi\n
EDCS-05502982.translation: For Publius Atellius Verecundus, son of Publius, of the Galeria tribe, 30 years old; Homullus made this for his son and himself.
EDCS-05502982.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Atellius Verecundus, aged 30, erected by Homullus for his son and himself.

EDCS-05502983.text_edition: \n Caius Sempronius Cai filius Galeria Severinus hic situs est Minicia Eleutheris piissima mater\n
EDCS-05502983.translation: Caius Sempronius Severinus, son of Caius, of the Galeria tribe, is buried here; Minicia Eleutheris, his most devoted mother, [made this].
EDCS-05502983.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Sempronius Severinus, erected by his devoted mother Minicia Eleutheris.

EDCS-05502984.text_edition: \n Voconia Pancarpe Proculi et Clementis liberta nnorum LX hic sita est\n
EDCS-05502984.translation: Voconia Pancarpe, freedwoman of Proculus and Clementis, 60 years old, is buried here.
EDCS-05502984.summary: A funerary epitaph for Voconia Pancarpe, a freedwoman, aged 60.

EDCS-05502985.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Minicius Marci filius Galeria Marcellus decurio omnibus honoribus in republica sua functus annorum X hic situs est Pompeia Luci filia mater indulgentissimo filio sibi et suis\n
EDCS-05502985.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Minicius Marcellus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, a decurion who held all offices in his community, 10 years old, is buried here. Pompeia, daughter of Lucius, [made this] for her most indulgent son, for herself and her family.
EDCS-05502985.summary: A funerary epitaph for Minicius Marcellus, a 10-year-old decurion, erected by his mother Pompeia, also for herself and family.

EDCS-05502986.text_edition: \n Didius Probus et Didius Alcidis sorori bene merenti sua p(ecunia) f(ecerunt)\n
EDCS-05502986.translation: Didius Probus and Didius made this with their own money for their well-deserving sister Alcidis.
EDCS-05502986.summary: A funerary epitaph for Alcidis, erected by her brothers Didius Probus and Didius.

EDCS-05502988.text_edition: \n Marcus Acilius Pilignus annorum LXXV hic situs est\n
EDCS-05502988.translation: Marcus Acilius Pilignus, 75 years old, is buried here.
EDCS-05502988.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Acilius Pilignus, aged 75.

EDCS-05502989.text_edition: \n Festus Quinti Munati Iusti servus annorum XX hic situs est Munatia Damalis filio karissimo\n
EDCS-05502989.translation: Festus, slave of Quintus Munatius Justus, 20 years old, is buried here. Munatia Damalis [made this] for her dearest son.
EDCS-05502989.summary: A funerary epitaph for Festus, a 20-year-old slave, erected by Munatia Damalis for her son.

EDCS-05502990.text_edition: \n Munatia Quinti liberta Fausta hic sita est\n
EDCS-05502990.translation: Munatia Fausta, freedwoman of Quintus, is buried here.
EDCS-05502990.summary: A funerary epitaph for Munatia Fausta, a freedwoman.

EDCS-05502993.text_edition: \n Strobilus Cnei Saufei |(servus) annorum XV hic situs est\n
EDCS-05502993.translation: Strobilus, the slave of Cnaeus Saufeius, aged 15, is buried here.
EDCS-05502993.summary: This is a tombstone for Strobilus, a 15-year-old slave, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502994.summary: This epitaph records the name and age of Quintus Grattius Constitutus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502995.translation: To Baebia Quieta, in accordance with her will.
EDCS-05502995.summary: This is a dedication or memorial to Baebia Quieta, established according to her testament, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502996.text_edition: \n Caius Pompeius Claudius annorum LXXXX hic situs est Pompeia Asterope patrono bene merenti et sibi\n
EDCS-05502996.translation: Gaius Pompeius Claudius, aged 90, is buried here. Pompeia Asterope for her well-deserving patron and for herself.
EDCS-05502996.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Gaius Pompeius Claudius and a dedication by Pompeia Asterope for her patron, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502997.text_edition: \n Lucius Fabius Tropus Marti Domino votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-05502997.translation: Lucius Fabius Tropus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Mars the Lord.
EDCS-05502997.summary: This inscription records Lucius Fabius Tropus fulfilling a vow to the god Mars, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502998.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Claudio Pio felici invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate consuli II patri patriae proconsuli decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-05502998.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Claudius Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power, consul for the second time, Father of the Fatherland, Proconsul, by decree of the town council.
EDCS-05502998.summary: This is a formal dedication to Emperor Claudius Gothicus, passed by the local council, dating to his reign (268-270 CE).

EDCS-05502999.text_edition: \n Quinto Iunio Quinti filio Galeria Iusto IIviro flamini Divi Augusti\n
EDCS-05502999.translation: To Quintus Junius Iustus, son of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe, twice a magistrate, priest of the deified Augustus.
EDCS-05502999.summary: This is an inscription honoring Quintus Junius Iustus, a local magistrate and priest, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503000.text_edition: \n Quinto Iunio Quinti filio Galeria Aenibeli\n
EDCS-05503000.translation: To Quintus Junius, son of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe, from Aenibelus.
EDCS-05503000.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Quintus Junius, son of Quintus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503001.text_edition: \n Corneliae Cai filiae Flaccellae Quintus Iunius Pupillus matri\n
EDCS-05503001.translation: To Cornelia Flaccella, daughter of Caius, Quintus Junius Pupillus for his mother.
EDCS-05503001.summary: This inscription records Quintus Junius Pupillus setting up a memorial for his mother, Cornelia Flaccella, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503002.text_edition: \n Marco Fulvio Luci filio Galeria Marciano IIviro flamini Romae et Augusti Fulvia Marci filia Marcella mater\n
EDCS-05503002.translation: To Marcus Fulvius Marcianus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, twice a magistrate, priest of Rome and Augustus; by Fulvia Marcella, daughter of Marcus, his mother.
EDCS-05503002.summary: This inscription honors Marcus Fulvius Marcianus, a local magistrate and priest, dedicated by his mother Fulvia Marcella, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503003.text_edition: \n Marco Granio Marci filio Galeria Superstiti cui omnes honores ob merita vitae a municipibus suis oblati sunt Publius Cornelius Iunianus fratri ex decreto decurionum honore usus ex testamento\n
EDCS-05503003.translation: To Marcus Granius Superstes, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, to whom all honors were offered by his fellow townsmen for his life's merits, Publius Cornelius Iunianus, his brother, used these honors by decree of the town council and by will.
EDCS-05503003.summary: A funerary monument honoring Marcus Granius Superstes, erected by his brother Publius Cornelius Iunianus, likely from the late Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-05503005.text_edition: \n Caio Clodio Cai filio Flavo Segontinensi fecerunt ex testamento\n
EDCS-05503005.translation: Made for Gaius Clodius Flavus, son of Gaius, of Seguntum, by will.
EDCS-05503005.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Clodius Flavus of Seguntum, erected according to his will, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-05503006.summary: A tombstone recording the name and age of Gaius Arruntius Paternus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503007.text_edition: \n annorum XXX hic sita est Baebius Bareta uxori\n
EDCS-05503007.translation: Aged 30, lies here. Baebius Bareta for his wife.
EDCS-05503007.summary: A funerary inscription for an unnamed woman, aged 30, erected by Baebius Bareta, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503008.text_edition: \n Lucius Caecilius Galeria Murrus Grannia Cai filia Avita\n
EDCS-05503008.translation: Lucius Caecilius Murrus, Galeria tribe, and Grannia Avita, daughter of Gaius.
EDCS-05503008.summary: An epitaph or dedication for Lucius Caecilius Murrus and Grannia Avita, possibly a married couple, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503009.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Clodia Patricia annorum XX hic sita est Clodia Primitiva mater\n
EDCS-05503009.translation: To the Divine Shades. Clodia Patricia, aged 20, lies here. Her mother, Clodia Primitiva.
EDCS-05503009.summary: A funerary inscription for Clodia Patricia, aged 20, erected by her mother Clodia Primitiva, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503010.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Coelio Quintiano patri Coeliae Patronae parenti Marco Antonio Crispo piissimo f(ilio)\n
EDCS-05503010.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Marcus Coelius Quintianus, father, and Coelia Patrona, parent, [and] for Marcus Antonius Crispus, most devoted son.
EDCS-05503010.summary: A funerary dedication for members of a family, Marcus Coelius Quintianus, Coelia Patrona, and their son Marcus Antonius Crispus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503011.text_edition: \n Gaio Cornelio Gai filio Galeria Festo Fulvia Venula Festo et sibi\n
EDCS-05503011.translation: For Gaius Cornelius Festus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, and for himself, Fulvia Venula Festo.
EDCS-05503011.summary: A funerary dedication by Fulvia Venula for her husband Gaius Cornelius Festus and for herself, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503012.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Cornelio Flaccino nnorum XXXV morumque bonorum pleno Iulia Maura marito pientissimo fecit\n
EDCS-05503012.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Marcus Cornelius Flaccinus, aged 35, and full of good qualities. Iulia Maura made this for her most devoted husband.
EDCS-05503012.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcus Cornelius Flaccinus, aged 35, erected by his wife Iulia Maura, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503013.text_edition: \n Corneliae Publi filiae Probae Publius Cornelius Rufus filiae\n
EDCS-05503013.translation: For Cornelia Proba, daughter of Publius. Publius Cornelius Rufus, [her father], [for his] daughter.
EDCS-05503013.summary: A funerary dedication for Cornelia Proba, daughter of Publius, erected by her father Publius Cornelius Rufus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503014.text_edition: \n Corneliae Publi filiae Propinquae Chaldaea Quinti filia Verecunda mater\n
EDCS-05503014.translation: To Cornelia Propinqua, daughter of Publius, from Chaldaea, Verenda daughter of Quintus, her mother.
EDCS-05503014.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication to Cornelia Propinqua by her mother, Verenda.

EDCS-05503016.text_edition: \n Marcus Iunius ] Phractus annorum XLV hic situs est\n
EDCS-05503016.translation: Marcus Junius Phractus, aged 45, is buried here.
EDCS-05503016.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of Marcus Junius Phractus, who died at the age of 45.

EDCS-05503017.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lupus nnorum XXXIIII mensium II hic situs est Velata uxsor marito dulcissimo sit Lupo terra levis\n
EDCS-05503017.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lupus, aged 34 years and 2 months, is buried here. Velata, his wife, to her dearest husband Lupus; may the earth rest lightly on you.
EDCS-05503017.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Lupus, aged 34 years and 2 months, with a dedication from his wife Velata.

EDCS-05503018.text_edition: \n Manliae Marci filiae Proculae Probus libertus\n
EDCS-05503018.translation: To Manlia Procula, daughter of Marcus, from Probus, her freedman.
EDCS-05503018.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Manlia Procula by her freedman, Probus.

EDCS-05503019.translation: To Pompeia [---], daughter of [---], from Murtius [---].
EDCS-05503019.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription likely dedicating a monument to Pompeia by Murtius.

EDCS-05503021.text_edition: \n Marcus Publicius Pannicus sibi et Corneliae Publi libertae Melete\n
EDCS-05503021.translation: Marcus Publicius Pannicus for himself and for Cornelia Melete, freedwoman of Publius.
EDCS-05503021.summary: This inscription records a burial arrangement made by Marcus Publicius Pannicus for himself and for his freedwoman, Cornelia Melete.

EDCS-05503022.text_edition: \n Valerio Marci filio Galeria Mariano Terentia Terentulla mater\n
EDCS-05503022.translation: To Valerius Mariano, son of Marcus, from Galeria. Terentia Terentulla, his mother.
EDCS-05503022.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication to Valerius Mariano by his mother, Terentia Terentulla.

EDCS-05503023.text_edition: \n Valeria mulieris lib(erta) Crocine Valeria Successa mater fecit\n
EDCS-05503023.translation: Valeria Crocina, freedwoman of a woman, Valeria Successa, her mother, made this.
EDCS-05503023.summary: This funerary inscription records the construction of a monument by Valeria Successa for her freedwoman, Valeria Crocina.

EDCS-05503024.text_edition: \n Valeriae Marci filiae Maxsimae Terentia Marci filia Terentulla filiae\n
EDCS-05503024.translation: To Valeria Maxsima, daughter of Marcus, from Terentia Terentulla, daughter of Marcus, her daughter.
EDCS-05503024.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Valeria Maxsima by her daughter, Terentia Terentulla.

EDCS-05503025.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to record the name and filiation of a man named Fabius.

EDCS-05503029.text_edition: \n Lucius Furius Murrus Furia Luci filia Murrana vixit annos XIV\n
EDCS-05503029.translation: Lucius Furius Murrus [and] Furia Murrana, daughter of Lucius, lived for 14 years.
EDCS-05503029.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Furia Murrana, daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-05503030.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publius Manlius Probillio annorum XLIII hic situs est\n
EDCS-05503030.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius Manlius, aged 43, is buried here.
EDCS-05503030.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Publius Manlius.

EDCS-05503031.text_edition: \n Fabiae Luci filiae Fabullae Publius Licinius Licinianus matri piissimae\n
EDCS-05503031.translation: Publius Licinius Licinianus [erected this] to his most devoted mother, Fabia Fabulla, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-05503031.summary: This funerary inscription records the erection of a monument by Publius Licinius Licinianus to his mother, Fabia Fabulla.

EDCS-05503032.text_edition: \n Titus Licinius filius Galeria Priscus Licinia Marci filia Marcella uxor\n
EDCS-05503032.translation: Titus Licinius Priscus, son of Titus, [and] Licinia Marcella, wife of Marcus, [erected this].
EDCS-05503032.summary: This funerary inscription appears to record the names of Titus Licinius Priscus and his wife Licinia Marcella.

EDCS-05503033.text_edition: \n Valerius Rucius vixit annos LXXX milit(avit) ex vita annos XX hic situs est\n
EDCS-05503033.translation: Valerius Rucius lived for 80 years; he served in the military for 20 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-05503033.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan and military service of Valerius Rucius.

EDCS-05503034.text_edition: \n M(arco) Clodio Marci filio Galeria Celeri fecit ex decreto decurionum Saetabi Augustanorum\n
EDCS-05503034.translation: Galeria Celer erected [this] for Marcus Clodius, son of Marcus, by decree of the town council of Saetabi Augustanorum.
EDCS-05503034.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by Galeria Celer for Marcus Clodius, as decreed by the town council of Saetabi.

EDCS-05503036.text_edition: \n Valeria Secunda nnorum XXV HC FACERE\n
EDCS-05503036.summary: This incomplete inscription records the name and age of Valeria Secunda.

EDCS-05503037.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Lucius Oculatius Quirina Rectus et Aemilia Caii filia Restituta uxor et Lucius Oculatius Quirina Rectus filius cum suis de suo posuerunt\n
EDCS-05503037.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, to Juno Regina. Lucius Oculatius Rectus of the Quirina tribe and his wife Aemilia Restituta, daughter of Gaius, and their son Lucius Oculatius Rectus of the Quirina tribe, placed [this] with their own property.
EDCS-05503037.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument to Jupiter and Juno by Lucius Oculatius Rectus, his wife Aemilia Restituta, and their son.

EDCS-05503038.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Caro Pio Felici invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate II patri patriae consuli proconsuli ordo Ebusitanorum devotus numini maiestati eius\n
EDCS-05503038.translation: The order of the Ebusitani, devoted to the divine majesty of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power for the second time, father of the country, consul, proconsul, [erected this].
EDCS-05503038.summary: This inscription records the devotion of the municipal council of Ebusus to Emperor Carus.

EDCS-05503039.text_edition: \n Caio Iulio Caii filio Galeria Tironi Gaetulico quaestori urbano tribuno plebis praetori amico optumo Lucius Sempronius Lucii filius Quirina Senecio\n
EDCS-05503039.translation: Lucius Sempronius Senecio, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, [erected this] to Gaius Julius Tiro Gaetulicus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, quaestor of the city, tribune of the plebs, praetor, his excellent friend.
EDCS-05503039.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by Lucius Sempronius Senecio to his friend Gaius Julius Tiro Gaetulicus, a prominent magistrate.

EDCS-05503040.text_edition: \n Lucio Oculatio Luci filio Quirina Recto aedili IIviro flamini Lucius Oculatius Lucii filius Quirina tribu Rectus filius patri indulgentissimo posuit\n
EDCS-05503040.translation: To Lucius Oculatius Rectus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, twice aedile, twice duovir, priest, his son Lucius Oculatius Rectus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, erected this to his most indulgent father.
EDCS-05503040.summary: A dedication by a son to his deceased father, Lucius Oculatius Rectus, who held several local magistracies and priesthoods, likely erected in the late Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-05503041.text_edition: \n Lucius Cornelius Longus et Marcus Cornelius Avitus filius et Lucius Cornelius Longus et Caius Cornelius Servinus et Marcus Cornelius Avitus et Publius Cornelius Cornelianus nepotes ex Lucio et Marci filii aquam in municipium Flavium Ebusum sua pecunia perduxerunt\n
EDCS-05503041.translation: Lucius Cornelius Longus, Marcus Cornelius Avitus his son, and Lucius Cornelius Longus, Caius Cornelius Servinus, Marcus Cornelius Avitus, and Publius Cornelius Cornelianus his grandsons, sons of Lucius and Marcus, brought water to the municipality of Flavium Ebusum at their own expense.
EDCS-05503041.summary: A public works inscription recording the construction of a water supply to the town of Flavium Ebusum by members of the Cornelius family, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05503042.text_edition: \n PX et Caius Cornelius Servinus heredes et curatores operis eius posuerunt hic rei publicae Ebusitanae XC milia nummorum legavit ut ex eis quodannis tributum Romanis penderetur et ne cives iniquo tempore tributa pendere cogerentur reliqua VI milia fenerarentur et ex usuris ludi ederentur quodannis cum vasis luminum natali eius V \n
EDCS-05503042.translation: PX and Caius Cornelius Servinus, his heirs and overseers of his work, erected this; he bequeathed 90,000 sesterces to the Republic of Ebusus, from which the annual tribute to the Romans should be paid, and so that citizens would not be forced to pay taxes at an inconvenient time, the remainder of 6,000 [sesterces] was to be loaned out, and from the interest, games were to be presented annually with lamps on his birthday.
EDCS-05503042.summary: This inscription records a substantial bequest by a deceased individual (PX) to the municipality of Ebusus for paying taxes and funding games, managed by his heirs and overseers, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05503043.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Apollonio patri Apollonius filius\n
EDCS-05503043.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Apollonius to his father Apollonius.
EDCS-05503043.summary: A simple funerary dedication from a son, Apollonius, to his deceased father, Apollonius.

EDCS-05503044.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Sempronio Apollonio medico Lucius Sempronius Philoxenus patri pio fecit\n
EDCS-05503044.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Sempronius Philoxenus made this for his dutiful father, Lucius Sempronius Apollonius, the physician.
EDCS-05503044.summary: A funerary dedication by a son, Lucius Sempronius Philoxenus, to his father, Lucius Sempronius Apollonius, a physician.

EDCS-05503045.text_edition: \n Marco Valerio Luci filio Pudenti Publius Valerius Modestus filio piissimo\n
EDCS-05503045.translation: To Marcus Valerius Pudentus, son of Lucius. Publius Valerius Modestus to his most dutiful son.
EDCS-05503045.summary: A dedication, likely funerary, from Publius Valerius Modestus to his son, Marcus Valerius Pudentus.

EDCS-05503047.text_edition: \n Caio Aburio filio Velina Montano Iulio Grato pontifici Lucius Manlius Montanus piissimo nepoti\n
EDCS-05503047.translation: To Caius Aburius Montanus, son of Velina, to Julius Gratus, the priest. Lucius Manlius Montanus to his most dutiful grandson.
EDCS-05503047.summary: A dedication by Lucius Manlius Montanus to his grandson, Caius Aburius Montanus, who is also noted as a priest.

EDCS-05503049.text_edition: \n Annia Asitio anno XIIII Ocratia Sisenia annis XVI dulcissumae fuistis vale\n
EDCS-05503049.translation: Annia Asitio, aged 14, and Ocratia Sisenia, aged 16, you were most sweet; farewell.
EDCS-05503049.summary: A brief epitaph for two young girls, Annia Asitio and Ocratia Sisenia, lamenting their early death.

EDCS-05503052.summary: A short epitaph from Antestia to Asitio, likely a grandchild, calling her grandmother and bidding farewell.

EDCS-05503053.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Asini vixit nnis LXV Vatro contubernali et collegae\n
EDCS-05503053.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Asinius lived 65 years with Vatro, his comrade and colleague.
EDCS-05503053.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Asinius at age 65, who is commemorated by Vatro, identified as his comrade and colleague.

EDCS-05503054.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph for Quintus Caecilius Aetara, dated to the Roman period.

EDCS-05503055.text_edition: \n Quintus Caecilius Isaptu ave et vale\n
EDCS-05503055.translation: Quintus Caecilius Isaptu, greetings and farewell.
EDCS-05503055.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph for Quintus Caecilius Isaptu, dated to the Roman period.

EDCS-05503056.summary: This is a very brief funerary epitaph mentioning Caecilius Isapto, dated to the Roman period.

EDCS-05503058.text_edition: \n Quinta Caecilia porisi ave et vale annorum XX\n
EDCS-05503058.translation: Quinta Caecilia, greetings and farewell, aged 20.
EDCS-05503058.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Quinta Caecilia at the age of 20, dated to the Roman period.

EDCS-05503061.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph for Marcus Hernius Rufus, dated to the Roman period.

EDCS-05503062.summary: This is a funerary epitaph indicating that Manila Hilera rests here, dated to the Roman period.

EDCS-05503064.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Pompeia Asitio Pompei Aspri filia vixit nnos XX have et vale\n
EDCS-05503064.translation: To the departed spirits. Pompeia Asitio, daughter of Pompeius Asper, lived 20 years, greetings and farewell.
EDCS-05503064.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the memory of Pompeia Asitio, who lived for 20 years, dated to the Roman period.

EDCS-05503067.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph for Quintus Sergius Lucius, dated to the Roman period.

EDCS-05503071.text_edition: \n Quintus Sergius Quinti libertus Demetrius salve\n
EDCS-05503071.translation: Greetings to Quintus Sergius Demetrius, freedman of Quintus.
EDCS-05503071.summary: This is a greeting addressed to Quintus Sergius Demetrius, a freedman, dated to the Roman period.

EDCS-05503073.text_edition: \n Marco Aemilio Lepido Lucio Arruntio consulibus Kalendis Maiis ex insula Baliarum Maiore senatus populusque Bocchoritanus Marcum Atilium Marci filium Galeria Vernum patronum cooptaverunt Marcus Atilius Marci filius Galeria Vernus senatum popolumque Bocchoritanum in fidem clientelamque suam suorumque recepit egerunt Quintus Caecilius Quinctus Caius Valerius Icesta praetores\n
EDCS-05503073.translation: In the consulship of Marcus Aemilius Lepidus and Lucius Arruntius, on the Kalends of May, the Senate and People of Bocchoris chose Marcus Atilius Vernus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, as patron; Marcus Atilius Vernus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, received the Senate and People of Bocchoris under his protection and patronage, and that of his own family. The praetors were Quintus Caecilius Quinctus and Caius Valerius Icesta.
EDCS-05503073.summary: This is an official decree from the Roman period, recording the adoption of Marcus Atilius Vernus as patron by the Senate and People of Bocchoris, with specific praetors named for the occasion.

EDCS-05503074.text_edition: \n Quinto Caecilio Quinti filio Velina Catullo aedili IIviro II fllamini Romae et Augusti Lucius Flavius Luci filius Damas Caecilia Zosima liberta egregio viro amico carissimo et sanctissimo locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-05503074.translation: To Quintus Caecilius Catullus, son of Quintus, of the Velina tribe, former aedile, duovir, and flamen of Rome and of Augustus; Lucius Flavius Damas, son of Lucius, of the Velina tribe, and his freedwoman Caecilia Zosima, to their distinguished husband, their dearest and most sacred friend, this place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05503074.summary: This inscription records the granting of a burial place to Lucius Flavius Damas and his freedwoman Caecilia Zosima, in honor of their friend Quintus Caecilius Catullus, a local magistrate and priest, by decree of the town council.

EDCS-05503075.text_edition: \n Lucio Dentilio Lucio filio Velina Modesto aedili IIviro flamini Lucius Favonius tuo avonculo pientissimo locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-05503075.translation: To Lucius Dentilius Modestus, son of Lucius, of the Velina tribe, former aedile, duovir, and flamen; Lucius Favonius to his most devoted uncle, this place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05503075.summary: This inscription records the granting of a burial place to Lucius Dentilius Modestus, a local magistrate and priest, by his nephew Lucius Favonius, as decreed by the town council.

EDCS-05503076.text_edition: \n Lucio Vibio Luci filio Velina Nigellioni aedili IIviro bis Manlia Fabiana uxor marito optimo suo et Vibi Manliani fili nomine sua pecunia posuit locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-05503076.translation: Manlia Fabiana, his wife, and on behalf of their son Vibi Manlianus, has erected this at her own expense to Lucius Vibius Nigellio, son of Lucius, of the Velina tribe, former aedile and twice duovir; to her most excellent husband; this place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05503076.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument and the granting of a burial place by Manlia Fabiana and her son to her husband, Lucius Vibius Nigellio, a local official, by decree of the town council.

EDCS-05503077.text_edition: \n Quinto Flavio Pontico ex testamento ipsius\n
EDCS-05503077.translation: To Quintus Flavius Ponticus, in accordance with his will.
EDCS-05503077.summary: This very brief inscription records a grant or dedication made in accordance with the will of Quintus Flavius Ponticus.

EDCS-05503078.text_edition: \n Flaviae Paulinae Flaviae Mammae filiae Lucius Flavius Paulinus pater et Flavii Paulinus et Macrinus filii matri karissimae locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-05503078.translation: To Flavia Paulina, daughter of Flavia Mammae; Lucius Flavius Paulinus, father, and his sons Flavius Paulinus and Macrinus, to their dearest mother, this place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05503078.summary: This inscription records the granting of a burial place by Lucius Flavius Paulinus and his sons to his wife and their mother, Flavia Paulina, by decree of the town council.

EDCS-05503079.text_edition: \n Sulpicia Gallieni vixit annis XXV mensibus V ave\n
EDCS-05503079.translation: Sulpicia Gallieni lived 25 years and 5 months. Hail!
EDCS-05503079.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name, age at death, and a valediction for Sulpicia Gallieni.

EDCS-05503083.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tabernari vixit annos XXIII Modesta filia fecit\n
EDCS-05503083.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Tabernarius lived 23 years. Modesta, his daughter, made this.
EDCS-05503083.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name and age at death of Tabernarius, erected by his daughter Modesta to his spirit.

EDCS-05503086.text_edition: \n Quinto Caecilio Quinti filio Labeoni aedili IIvir municipi Flavi Magontani Quintus Caecilius Philistio et Iulia Severa filio piissimo\n
EDCS-05503086.translation: To Quintus Caecilius Labeo, son of Quintus, former aedile and duovir of the municipality of Magontanus; Quintus Caecilius Philistio and Iulia Severa, to their most devoted son.
EDCS-05503086.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by Quintus Caecilius Philistio and Iulia Severa to their son, Quintus Caecilius Labeo, who held local magistracies.

EDCS-05503087.text_edition: \n Quinto Cornelio Quinti? filio Quirina Verecundo aedili IIviro municipii Magontani flamini divorum et Augustali Lucius Cornelius Satur socero Quintus Cornelius Satur avo optimo ob plurima merita\n
EDCS-05503087.translation: To Quintus Cornelius Verecundus, son of Quintus(?), of the Quirina tribe, former aedile, duovir of the municipality of Magontani, and flamen of the gods and of the Augustales; Lucius Cornelius Satur, to his father-in-law; Quintus Cornelius Satur, to his excellent grandfather, for his many services.
EDCS-05503087.summary: This inscription records honors bestowed by Lucius Cornelius Satur and Quintus Cornelius Satur upon Quintus Cornelius Verecundus, a local magistrate and priest, acknowledging his services to the family and community.

EDCS-05503088.text_edition: \n Lucio Luci filio Quirina Fabullo aedili IIviro III flamini divorum Augustali res publica Magontana ob multa eius merita\n
EDCS-05503088.translation: To Lucius Fabullus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, former aedile, duovir, and thrice flamen of the Augustales; the Republic of Magontana, for his many services.
EDCS-05503088.summary: This inscription records the republic of Magontana honoring Lucius Fabullus, a prominent local official who served multiple terms as aedile, duovir, and flamen, for his many services.

EDCS-05503089.text_edition: \n ? Maecius Maecianus Quirina Montanus aedilicius ter IIviratu in insula functus etiam flaminatu provinciae Hispaniae Citerioris ob aeternitatem honorum suorum memoriae conlocavit\n
EDCS-05503089.translation: ? Maecius Maecianus Montanus, belonging to the Quirina tribe, after serving as aedile and twice as duovir, and also as flamen of the province of Hispania Citerior, set this up in memory of the eternity of his honors.
EDCS-05503089.summary: This is a funerary or memorial inscription set up by Maecius Maecianus, who held various magistracies and priesthoods in Hispania Citerior, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05503093.text_edition: \n Caeciliae Quinti filiae Severae Quintus Caecilius Philistio patronae optimae fecit\n
EDCS-05503093.translation: Quintus Caecilius Philistio made this for Caecilia Severa, daughter of Quintus, his excellent patroness.
EDCS-05503093.summary: A dedication by Quintus Caecilius Philistio to his patroness Caecilia Severa, likely a funerary monument from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503094.text_edition: \n Lunae Caius IUlius Flavius miles votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-05503094.translation: Caius Julius Flavius, a soldier, willingly paid his vow, as was deserved.
EDCS-05503094.summary: A votive inscription by a soldier named Caius Julius Flavius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503095.text_edition: \n Caeciliae Quinti filiae Quintiae Quintus Caecilius Philistio et Iulia Severa filiae piissimae\n
EDCS-05503095.translation: Quintus Caecilius Philistio and his daughter Julia Severa made this for Caecilia Quintia, daughter of Quintus, their most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-05503095.summary: A funerary dedication by Quintus Caecilius Philistio and his daughter Julia Severa to another daughter/relative named Caecilia Quintia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503101.translation: To Numerius Iovius Victor, from Sextus.
EDCS-05503101.summary: A short dedication or memorial inscription from Sextus to Numerius Iovius Victor, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503108.translation: Titus Flavius Titianus, imperial legate pro praetore, and his wife Postumia Varia dedicated this to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Juno, Minerva, the Genius of the governor's residence, and the consuls, with the gods assenting.
EDCS-05503108.summary: A dedication to the Capitoline Triad and other deities by Titus Flavius Titianus, a high-ranking imperial official in Hispania, and his wife, likely from the late 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05503138.translation: Badius Macrinus, vir perfectissimus, governor of the province of Hispania Tarraconensis, most devoted to his numen and majesty, to his most pious, most brave, most fortunate lord, Constantine the Great Victor, always Augustus.
EDCS-05503138.summary: A dedication to Emperor Constantine the Great by Badius Macrinus, governor of Hispania Tarraconensis, expressing devotion to the emperor's divine presence, likely dating to the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-05503140.translation: Badius Macrinus, vir perfectissimus, governor of the province of Hispania Tarraconensis, most devoted to his numen and majesty, to our most pious and illustrious lord Constantius, the most noble, most brave, and most fortunate Caesar.
EDCS-05503140.summary: A dedication to Caesar Constantius by Badius Macrinus, governor of Hispania Tarraconensis, expressing devotion to the Caesar's divine presence, likely dating to the mid-4th century CE.

EDCS-05503149.translation: Marcus Fabius Priscus, quaestor of the province of Achaia, military tribune of the I Legion, and duovir for the care of roads, made this for Gaius Apronius Secundus.
EDCS-05503149.summary: An honorific inscription for Gaius Apronius Secundus, listing his career as a local magistrate and military officer, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503201.translation: To the spirits of the departed, to Valerius Rufus, a soldier of the VII Gemina Legion, who lived 35 years and 8 days; Pompeia Bassilla, his wife, with whom he lived 5 years and 2 months, made this for him, a deserving man.
EDCS-05503201.summary: A funerary inscription for a soldier named Valerius Rufus, set up by his wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503228.text_edition: \n Lucio Aufidio Quinti filio Velina Secundo Palmensi omnibus honoribus in re publica sua functo flamini provinciae Hispaniae citerioris provincia Hispania citerior\n
EDCS-05503228.translation: To Lucius Aufidius Secundus, son of Quintus, of the Velina tribe, from Palmensis, who held all offices in his republic, priest of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503228.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Lucius Aufidius Secundus, a former official and priest of Hispania Citerior, likely set up by himself or on his behalf.

EDCS-05503229.text_edition: \n Aureliae Marcellinae ex (conventu) Cluniensi flaminicae uxori L(uci) Licini Sparsi flaminis cives Tarraconenses\n
EDCS-05503229.translation: To Aurelia Marcellina, from the district of Clunia, wife of Lucius Licinius Sparsus, priest, honored by the citizens of Tarraco.
EDCS-05503229.summary: This inscription honors Aurelia Marcellina, wife of Lucius Licinius Sparsus, a priest, and mentions her origin and the citizens of Tarraco as dedicants.

EDCS-05503236.text_edition: \n Lucio Clodio Marci filio Velina Ingenuo Palmensi omnibus honoribus in re publica sua perfuncto praefecto fabrum flamini Romae divorum et Augustorum provinciae Hispaniae citerioris provincia Hispaniae citerioris\n
EDCS-05503236.translation: To Lucius Clodius Ingenuus, son of Marcus, of the Velina tribe, from Palmensis, who held all offices in his republic, prefect of the engineers, priest of Rome and of the divine emperors of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503236.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Lucius Clodius Ingenuus, a prominent figure who served as prefect of engineers and priest of the imperial cult in Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503262.translation: To Lucius Numisius Montanus, son of Lucius, of the Palatina tribe, from Tarraco, who held all offices in his republic, priest of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503262.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Numisius Montanus, a man who served in various civic offices and as a priest of Hispania Citerior in Tarraco.

EDCS-05503274.translation: To Lucius Saenius Iustus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, priest of Rome and of the divine emperors of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503274.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Lucius Saenius Iustus, who served as a priest of the imperial cult in Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503304.translation: To Lucius Minicius Apronianus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, aedile, quaestor, twice chief magistrate and quinquennalis of the colony of Tarraco, and priest of the divine Trajan Parthicus; from his heirs by his will.
EDCS-05503304.summary: This is a posthumous dedication to Lucius Minicius Apronianus, a local magistrate and priest of the divine Trajan Parthicus in Tarraco, funded by his heirs.

EDCS-05503307.translation: Valeria Firmina to her son, Gaius Valerius Avitus, duovir, who was transferred from the municipality of Augustobriga by the divine Pius to the colony of Tarraco.
EDCS-05503307.summary: This is a dedication by Valeria Firmina to her son, Gaius Valerius Avitus, who was a duovir and transferred from Augustobriga to Tarraco.

EDCS-05503312.text_edition: \n Ex testamento Luci Licini Luci fili Sergia Surae consanguineinguinei\n
EDCS-05503312.translation: From the will of Lucius Licinius Sura, son of Lucius, of the Sergia tribe, his relative.
EDCS-05503312.summary: This inscription indicates a dedication or bequest made from the will of Lucius Licinius Sura by a relative.

EDCS-05503314.translation: May you live prosperously; behold, the house is adorned; if I endure filth... but I am ashamed of hospitality.
EDCS-05503314.summary: This is a short, somewhat enigmatic inscription that appears to be a private reflection or comment on a dwelling and its upkeep, possibly related to hospitality.

EDCS-05503328.translation: To Marcus Fulvius Museus, Augustalis sevir; from his wife, perhaps Sutor(ia) Surilla, to her excellent husband.
EDCS-05503328.summary: This is a dedication to Marcus Fulvius Museus, an Augustalis sevir, likely by his wife.

EDCS-05503330.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Lucretius Nicephorus, Augustorum's *sevirus*.
EDCS-05503330.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Lucius Lucretius Nicephorus, identified as a *sevirus augustalis*, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503357.translation: To Sulpicia, of Calagurris, an most honorable matron, his most chaste wife, his most dutiful mother. Marcus Vetilius Aelianus, her husband, and Sulpicia Aeliana, their daughter.
EDCS-05503357.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a husband and daughter for Sulpicia, honoring her virtues as a wife and mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503370.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Baebia Helice, his excellent wife. Cornelius Theseus made this.
EDCS-05503370.summary: A funerary dedication by Cornelius Theseus for his wife Baebia Helice, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503442.translation: To Calaria Liciniana, daughter of Lucius Calarius, his best friend. Gaius Lutatius Carus, to his wife.
EDCS-05503442.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication by Gaius Lutatius Carus to his wife Calaria Liciniana, daughter of Lucius Calarius, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503477.translation: In fulfillment of a vow, Gaius Clodius Aemilianus.
EDCS-05503477.summary: This is a votive inscription by Gaius Clodius Aemilianus, indicating a dedication made in fulfillment of a promise, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503478.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aphrodite Varia Iucunda.
EDCS-05503478.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aphrodite Varia Iucunda, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503479.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Selledius Achilleus, possibly funerary or votive, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503480.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Sempronia Charis, likely funerary, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503483.translation: Lucius Horatius, son of Marcus, from Viseradin, is buried here.
EDCS-05503483.summary: This is a tomb inscription stating that Lucius Horatius, son of Marcus, from Viseradin, is buried here, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503484.text_edition: \n Iunia Cupita hic sita est annorum LV Mercurialis et Licinia Limphidia sua pecunia sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-05503484.translation: Iunia Cupita is buried here, aged 55. Mercurialis and Licinia Limphidia, with their own money. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05503484.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Iunia Cupita, aged 55, with funds provided by Mercurialis and Licinia Limphidia, and includes a wish for a peaceful afterlife, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503485.text_edition: \n Marco Aurelio Numeriano nobilissimo Caesari filio domini nostri Imperatoris Marci Aureli Cari Augusti ordo Iessonensium \n
EDCS-05503485.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Numerianus, most noble Caesar, son of our lord Emperor Marcus Aurelius Carus Augustus, the council of the people of Iessos.
EDCS-05503485.summary: A dedication by the council of Iessos to Caesar Marcus Aurelius Numerianus, son of Emperor Carus, likely from the late 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05503489.text_edition: \n Lucio Caecilio Agidillo Lucius Caecilius Agilio patri pientissimo et Severiano filio karissimo annorum XVI\n
EDCS-05503489.translation: To Lucius Caecilius Agidillus, from Lucius Caecilius Agilio, for his most dutiful father and dearest son Severianus, aged sixteen.
EDCS-05503489.summary: A dedication to a deceased father and son, Lucius Caecilius Agidillus and Severianus, by Lucius Caecilius Agilio, probably a tombstone inscription from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05503491.text_edition: \n Lunae Augustae sacrum in honorem et memoriam Aemiliae Luci filiae Maternae Lucius Aemilius Maternus IIvir et Fabia Fusca parentes sua pecunia faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-05503491.translation: Sacred to Luna Augusta, in honor and memory of Aemilia Materna, daughter of Lucius. Lucius Aemilius Maternus, duumvir, and Fabia Fusca, parents, had this made at their own expense.
EDCS-05503491.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Aemilia Materna by her parents, Lucius Aemilius Maternus and Fabia Fusca, who served as duumvir, likely from the late Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-05503492.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Lucio Domitio Aureliano Pio Felici Invicto Augusto Parthico maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae consuli II proconsuli II\n
EDCS-05503492.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Domitius Aurelianus, Pious, Fortunate, Invincible, Augustus, greatest conqueror of Parthia, invested with tribunician power, father of the fatherland, consul for the second time, proconsul for the second time.
EDCS-05503492.summary: A titulature inscription for Emperor Aurelian, listing his full imperial titles, likely erected during his reign (270-275 AD).

EDCS-05503493.text_edition: \n Marco Aemilio Luci filio Galeria Fraterno praefecto fabrum tribuno militum fratri Aemilius Paternus primipilaris\n
EDCS-05503493.translation: To Marcus Aemilius Fraterno, son of Lucius, of the Galerian tribe, prefect of the craftsmen, tribune of the soldiers, from his brother Aemilius Paternus, a primipilaris.
EDCS-05503493.summary: A dedication to Marcus Aemilius Fraterno, a military officer and prefect of craftsmen, by his brother Aemilius Paternus, a senior centurion, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05503494.text_edition: \n Lucio Aemilio Luci filio Galeria Paterno primipilari praefecto fabrum |(centurioni) legionis VII geminae |(centurioni) legionis I Minerviae |(centurioni) legionis VII Claudiae |(centurioni) legionis XIIII geminae |(centurioni) cohortis X urbanae |(centurioni) cohortis IIII praetoriae CCCtrecenario |(centurioni) legionis II Augustae et primo pilo ter donis donato ab Imperatore Traiano torquibus armillis phaleris corona vallari bis in Dacia semel in Parthia Atilia Luci filia Vera bene de se merito\n
EDCS-05503494.translation: To Lucius Aemilius Paterno, son of Lucius, of the Galerian tribe, primipilaris, prefect of the craftsmen, centurion of the VII Geminae legion, centurion of the I Minerviae legion, centurion of the VII Claudiae legion, centurion of the XIIII Geminae legion, centurion of the X Urban cohort, centurion of the IIII Praetorian cohort, a 300-man officer, centurion of the II Augustae legion, and first spearman thrice honored by Emperor Trajan with necklaces, armbands, and phalerae, with a rampart crown twice in Dacia and once in Parthia, from Atilia Vera, daughter of Lucius, who deserved well of him.
EDCS-05503494.summary: A detailed honorific inscription for Lucius Aemilius Paterno, a distinguished soldier and officer, highlighting his career and decorations awarded by Emperor Trajan, likely from the early 2nd century AD.

EDCS-05503497.text_edition: \n Marco Licinio Luci filio Quirina Celtibero aedili IIviro Lucius Terentius Terentianus tutori\n
EDCS-05503497.translation: To Marcus Licinius Celtiber, son of Lucius, of the Quirine tribe, aedile, duumvir, from his guardian Lucius Terentius Terentianus.
EDCS-05503497.summary: A dedication by a guardian, Lucius Terentius Terentianus, to his ward Marcus Licinius Celtiber, who held civic offices, likely a funerary or honorific inscription from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05503498.text_edition: \n Publio Mario Mariani filio Calpurniano Orretano defuncto annorum XVIII Marius Marianus communi adfectione Mariae Calpurniae uxoris et matris recepto in clientelam civium Aesonensium et liberalibus studiis erudito impetrato loco ex decreto ordinis statua equestri memoriam piaetatis sic honoravit datisque sportulis dedicavit\n
EDCS-05503498.translation: To Publius Marius Orretanus, son of Marius, of the Calpurnian tribe, who died at eighteen years of age. Marius Marianus, with shared affection from Maria Calpurnia, his wife and mother, having received him into the patronage of the citizens of Aeson, and educated him in liberal studies, obtained a site by decree of the council, and honored him with an equestrian statue in memory of his piety, and dedicated it after giving feasts.
EDCS-05503498.summary: A posthumous equestrian statue erected by Publius Marius' father and mother, honoring his piety and education, likely a funerary monument from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05503499.text_edition: \n Lucio Porcio Luci filio Quirina Sereno IIIIviro IIviro Porcia Procula filia fecit ex testamento\n
EDCS-05503499.translation: To Lucius Porcius Sereno, son of Lucius, of the Quirine tribe, holder of the office of quattuorvir and duumvir, his daughter Porcia Procula made this according to his will.
EDCS-05503499.summary: A funerary dedication made by a daughter, Porcia Procula, for her father Lucius Porcius Sereno, who had held civic offices, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05503500.text_edition: \n Lucio Porcio Luci filio Quirina Prisco Porcia Procula soror ex testamento Porci Sereni\n
EDCS-05503500.translation: To Lucius Porcius Priscus, son of Lucius, of the Quirine tribe, from his sister Porcia Procula, according to the will of Porcius Sereno.
EDCS-05503500.summary: A funerary dedication made by a sister, Porcia Procula, for her brother Lucius Porcius Priscus, based on the will of Porcius Sereno, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05503502.text_edition: \n Caio Aemilio Cai? filio Quirina o Fabia Celera mater Aemilia Pressa soror Lucius Aemilius Cai filius Crescentinus patruus heredes ex testamento\n
EDCS-05503502.translation: To Gaius Aemilius, son of Gaius, of the tribe Quirina. Fabia Celera, mother, Aemilia Pressa, sister, and Lucius Aemilius Crescentinus, paternal uncle, by will, the heirs.
EDCS-05503502.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to Gaius Aemilius by his mother, sister, and uncle.

EDCS-05503503.text_edition: \n Antonia Saturnina Luci Antoni Silvani liberta\n
EDCS-05503503.translation: Antonia Saturnina, freedwoman of Lucius Antonius Silvanus.
EDCS-05503503.summary: This inscription identifies Antonia Saturnina as the freedwoman of Lucius Antonius Silvanus.

EDCS-05503505.text_edition: \n Memoriae Luci Fulvi Luci fili Quirina Saturnini Lucius Fulvius Luci filius Quirina Celtiber patri\n
EDCS-05503505.translation: To the memory of Lucius Fulvius Saturninus, son of Lucius, of the tribe Quirina. Lucius Fulvius Celtiber, son of Lucius, of the tribe Quirina, dedicated this to his father.
EDCS-05503505.summary: This is a memorial inscription set up by Lucius Fulvius Celtiber for his father, Lucius Fulvius Saturninus.

EDCS-05503506.text_edition: \n Fulviae Luci Fulvi Restuti filiae Catullae Aesonensi nnorum XL Fulvius Homullus libertus patronae de se bene meritae faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-05503506.translation: Fulvia Catulla, daughter of Lucius Fulvius Restutus, of Aeson, aged 40. Fulvius Homullus, freedman, had this made for his patroness who deserved well of him.
EDCS-05503506.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Fulvia Catulla, daughter of Lucius Fulvius Restutus, and notes that her freedman Fulvius Homullus erected the monument.

EDCS-05503507.summary: This is a very brief inscription indicating that Catulla commissioned the monument.

EDCS-05503512.text_edition: \n Caio Vibio Luperco IIIIviro municipi Sigarrensis Caius Vibius Latro filius\n
EDCS-05503512.translation: To Gaius Vibius Lupercus, one of the duoviri of the municipium of Sigarra. Gaius Vibius Latro, his son.
EDCS-05503512.summary: This inscription dedicates a monument to Gaius Vibius Lupercus, a magistrate of Sigarra, by his son Gaius Vibius Latro.

EDCS-05503513.text_edition: \n Iuniae Severinae Caius Vibius Latro matri\n
EDCS-05503513.translation: To Junia Severina, his mother. Gaius Vibius Latro.
EDCS-05503513.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Junia Severina, mother of Gaius Vibius Latro.

EDCS-05503514.text_edition: \n Caius Annius Cai filius Galeria Proculus sibi et Porciae Restitutae uxori optimae et suis hoc monumentum heredem non sequeretur\n
EDCS-05503514.translation: Gaius Annius Proculus, son of Gaius, of the tribe Galeria, for himself and for his excellent wife Porcia Restituta and for his own family, (stipulating that) this monument should not pass to an heir.
EDCS-05503514.summary: This is a self-dedicatory funerary monument for Gaius Annius Proculus, his wife Porcia Restituta, and their family, specifying it is not for inheritance.

EDCS-05503515.text_edition: \n Caio Cornelio Mansueto patri Cornelia Cupita\n
EDCS-05503515.translation: To Gaius Cornelius Mansuetus, his father. Cornelia Cupita.
EDCS-05503515.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Gaius Cornelius Mansuetus by his daughter Cornelia Cupita.

EDCS-05503516.text_edition: \n Caio Licinio Cai filio Galeria Siloni Caius Licinius Paternus patri ex testamento Imperatori Caesari Marco Aure[lio] Valerio Maximiano Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate pater patriae proconsuli ordo Segarrensis\n
EDCS-05503516.translation: To Gaius Licinius Silo, son of Gaius, of the tribe Galeria. Gaius Licinius Paternus, for his father, by will. To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Valerius Maximianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power, father of the fatherland, proconsul. The town council of Segarra.
EDCS-05503516.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Gaius Licinius Paternus, by will, to his father Gaius Licinius Silo, and also includes a dedication to Emperor Maximian by the town council of Segarra.

EDCS-05503517.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Heuresidi Melodiae <li=E><b=D>(ertae) opsequentissimae sic \n
EDCS-05503517.translation: To the Divine Shades, for Heuresis Melodia, a most dutiful freedwoman.
EDCS-05503517.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring Heuresis Melodia, a freedwoman, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-05503520.translation: To Apollo, Marcus Fonteius Novatianus, by decree.
EDCS-05503520.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Apollo by Marcus Fonteius Novatianus, likely a local magistrate, possibly from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05503521.text_edition: \n Apollini Lucius Minicius Apronianus Galeria Tarraconensis testamento poni iussit\n
EDCS-05503521.translation: To Apollo, Lucius Minicius Apronianus of the tribe Galeria from Tarraco ordered to set this up by his will.
EDCS-05503521.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Apollo by Lucius Minicius Apronianus of Tarraco, ordered by his will, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05503522.translation: To holy Apollo, Lucius Vibius Alcinous.
EDCS-05503522.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Apollo by Lucius Vibius Alcinous, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503523.text_edition: \n Quintus Cassius Garonicus animo votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-05503523.translation: Quintus Cassius Garonicus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow with good spirit.
EDCS-05503523.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Quintus Cassius Garonicus fulfilling a vow, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503524.text_edition: \n Publius Licinus Philetus et Licinia Crassi liberta Peregrina Isidi votum solverunt libentes merito loco accepto publice a re publica\n
EDCS-05503524.translation: Publius Licinus Philetus and Licinia Peregrina, freedwoman of Crassus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow to Isis, having received the place publicly from the state.
EDCS-05503524.summary: This is a votive inscription to Isis by Publius Licinus Philetus and Licinia Peregrina, who received the site publicly from the state, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503525.text_edition: \n Cornelia Flora pro Philippo Minervae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-05503525.translation: Cornelia Flora for Philippus willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Minerva.
EDCS-05503525.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Cornelia Flora fulfilling a vow to Minerva on behalf of Philippus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503526.text_edition: \n Saluti sacrum Caius Trocina Zoticus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-05503526.translation: Sacred to Salus, Caius Trocina Zoticus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05503526.summary: This is a votive inscription to Salus (Health) by Caius Trocina Zoticus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503527.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Hadriani filio divi Traiani Parthici nepoti divi Nervae pronepoti Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio pontifici maximo tribunicia potestati sic consuli II designato III patri patriae decreto decurionum municipium Flavium Egara\n
EDCS-05503527.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Trajan the Parthian, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, Pontifex Maximus, holder of tribunician power, consul for the second time, designated for the third, father of the country, by decree of the decurions of the municipium Flavium Egara.
EDCS-05503527.summary: This is a formal dedication to Emperor Antoninus Pius by the town council of Egara, dated to his consulship and tribunician power, likely between 140-161 CE.

EDCS-05503528.text_edition: \n Quinto Granio Quinti filio Galeria Optato IIviro Egara tribuno militum Grania Anthusa marito optimo locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-05503528.translation: To Quintus Granius Optatus, son of Quintus, of the tribe Galeria, IIvir, from Egara, and tribune of the soldiers, Grania Anthusa to her most excellent husband, the place was given by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05503528.summary: This inscription records the allocation of a plot of land by the decurions of Egara to Grania Anthusa for her husband Quintus Granius Optatus, a local magistrate and military tribune, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05503529.text_edition: \n Dibus deabus Titus Iulius Placidus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-05503529.translation: Titus Iulius Placidus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the gods and goddesses.
EDCS-05503529.summary: This is a votive inscription by Titus Iulius Placidus fulfilling a vow to the gods.

EDCS-05503530.text_edition: \n Dibus deabus Titus Iulius Placidus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-05503530.translation: Titus Iulius Placidus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the gods and goddesses.
EDCS-05503530.summary: This is a votive inscription by Titus Iulius Placidus fulfilling a vow to the gods.

EDCS-05503531.text_edition: \n Dibus deabus Titus Iulius Placidus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-05503531.translation: Titus Iulius Placidus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the gods and goddesses.
EDCS-05503531.summary: This is a votive inscription by Titus Iulius Placidus fulfilling a vow to the gods.

EDCS-05503532.text_edition: \n Dibus deabus Titus Iulius Placidus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-05503532.translation: Titus Iulius Placidus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the gods and goddesses.
EDCS-05503532.summary: This is a votive inscription by Titus Iulius Placidus fulfilling a vow to the gods.

EDCS-05503533.text_edition: \n Dibus deabus Titus Iulius Placidus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-05503533.translation: Titus Iulius Placidus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the gods and goddesses.
EDCS-05503533.summary: This is a votive inscription by Titus Iulius Placidus fulfilling a vow to the gods.

EDCS-05503534.text_edition: \n Dibus deabus Titus Iulius Placidus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-05503534.translation: Titus Iulius Placidus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the gods and goddesses.
EDCS-05503534.summary: This is a votive inscription by Titus Iulius Placidus fulfilling a vow to the gods.

EDCS-05503535.text_edition: \n Dibus deabus Titus Iulius Placidus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-05503535.translation: Titus Iulius Placidus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the gods and goddesses.
EDCS-05503535.summary: This is a votive inscription by Titus Iulius Placidus fulfilling a vow to the gods.

EDCS-05503536.text_edition: \n Dibus deabus Titus Iulius Placidus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-05503536.translation: Titus Iulius Placidus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the gods and goddesses.
EDCS-05503536.summary: This is a votive inscription by Titus Iulius Placidus fulfilling a vow to the gods.

EDCS-05503556.text_edition: \n Quinto Flavio Cai filio Festivo |(centurioni) legionis VII geminae felicis et legionis IVIVlaviae amico optimo locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-05503556.translation: A place was granted by decree of the decurions to Quintus Flavius Festivus, son of Caius, a centurion of the loyal VII Gemina legion and an excellent friend of the IV Flavia legion.
EDCS-05503556.summary: This inscription records the granting of a burial place by the town council (decurions) to Quintus Flavius Festivus, a centurion serving in two legions.

EDCS-05503560.text_edition: \n Caio Aemilio Cai filio Galeria Antoniano aedili IIviro flamini Aemiliae Cai filiae Optatae annorum XVI\n
EDCS-05503560.translation: To Gaius Aemilius Antonianus, son of Gaius, of the Galerian tribe, twice aedile, twice duovir, and flamen, and to Aemilia Optata, daughter of Gaius, of the Galerian tribe, aged 16.
EDCS-05503560.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring Gaius Aemilius Antonianus and Aemilia Optata, likely his daughter or wife, based on their civic and religious titles and age.

EDCS-05503561.text_edition: \n Gaio Iulio Gai filio Galeria Paulino Barcinonensi omnibus honoribus in re publica sua perfuncto marito optimo benignissimo rarissimo Sergia Fulvianilla locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-05503561.translation: To Gaius Julius Paulinus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, from Barcinona, who held all public offices in his town, a most excellent, kind, and rare husband, a plot was granted by decree of the decurions to Sergia Fulvianilla.
EDCS-05503561.summary: This epitaph records the grant of burial space to Sergia Fulvianilla for her exceptional husband, Gaius Julius Paulinus, a Roman official in Barcinona.

EDCS-05503562.text_edition: \n Quinto Calpurnio Quinti filio Galeria Flavo nnorum XXIX aedili IIviro cui post mortem ordo Barcinonensium honores flaminales decrevit Cominia Nereis mater locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-05503562.translation: To Quintus Calpurnius Flablus, son of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe, aged 29, who served as aedile and duovir; to whom the town council of Barcinona decreed flaminal honors after his death, a plot was granted by decree of the decurions to his mother, Cominia Nereis.
EDCS-05503562.summary: This epitaph commemorates Quintus Calpurnius Flablus of Barcinona, detailing his civic offices and posthumous honors, with a burial plot granted to his mother.

EDCS-05503563.text_edition: \n Lucio Calpurnio Luci filio Galeria Iunco aedili IIviro flamini Valeriae Luci libertae uxori Lucio Calpurnio Luci filio Galeria Iunco filio aedili IIviro II flamini Calpurniae Luci filiae Severae nepti\n
EDCS-05503563.translation: To Lucius Calpurnius Iuncus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, aedile and duovir and flaminal priest; to his wife Valeria Iuncus, freedwoman of Lucius; to his son Lucius Calpurnius Iuncus, aedile, duovir, and twice flaminal priest; and to his granddaughter Calpurnia Severa, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-05503563.summary: This funerary inscription lists multiple family members, including Lucius Calpurnius Iuncus, who held prominent civic and religious offices in Barcinona.

EDCS-05503564.text_edition: \n Marco Herennio Cai filio Galeria Severo aedili IIviro flamini Augusti Herennia Cai filia Optata fratri optimo\n
EDCS-05503564.translation: To Marcus Herennius Severus, son of Caius, of the Galeria tribe, aedile, duovir, and priest of Augustus, a most excellent brother, from Herennia Optata, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-05503564.summary: This epitaph is a dedication from Herennia Optata to her brother, Marcus Herennius Severus, a civic and religious official in Barcinona.

EDCS-05503565.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Iulio Cai filio Palatina Silvano nnorum XVIII mensium IIII aedili Barcinonensi Caius Publicius Melissus pater filio karissimo et Caio Publicio Hermeti IIIIIIviro Augustali Aurelia Nigella marito karissimo et sibi hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-05503565.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Gaius Julius Silvanus, son of Gaius, of the Palatina tribe, aged 18 years and 4 months, aedile of Barcinona; from his father Caius Publicius Melissus to his dearest son, and to Caius Publicius Hermetus, Augustalis duovir; from Aurelia Nigella to her dearest husband and for herself; this monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-05503565.summary: This funerary monument, erected by Caius Publicius Melissus and Aurelia Nigella, honors their son Gaius Julius Silvanus, a young aedile of Barcinona, and also serves for Aurelia Nigella and her husband.

EDCS-05503566.text_edition: \n Publio Manlio Gnaei filio Galeria aedili IIviro Cnaeo Manlio Publi filio Galeria Secundo aedili\n
EDCS-05503566.translation: To Publius Manlius Secundus, son of Gnaeus, of the Galeria tribe, aedile and duovir; to Gnaeus Manlius, son of Publius, of the Galeria tribe, aedile.
EDCS-05503566.summary: This epitaph records the honors granted to Publius Manlius Secundus and his son Gnaeus Manlius, both holding the office of aedile in Barcinona.

EDCS-05503567.text_edition: \n Lucio Pedanio Luci filio Atiliano aedili cui Lucius Pedanius Paternus pater statuam aeneam ponendam destinaverat heredes Iulius Eutychianus Pedanii Irenicus et Irene Pomponia Philete uxor voluntati eius satisfecerunt locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-05503567.translation: To Lucius Pedanius Atilanus, son of Lucius, aedile; his father Lucius Pedanius Paternus had intended to set up a bronze statue; his heirs, Julius Eutychianus, Pedanii Irenicus and Irene, and his wife Pomponia Philete, fulfilled his wish. A plot was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05503567.summary: This inscription details the posthumous honoring of Lucius Pedanius Atilanus, an aedile, by his father's wish and his heirs' fulfillment, including the grant of a burial plot.

EDCS-05503568.text_edition: \n Quinto Salvio Luci filio Galeria aedili duoi sic viro et duoi sic viro quinquennali heredes ex testamento\n
EDCS-05503568.translation: To Quintus Salvius, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, aedile, twice quinquennial duovir; from his heirs, by his will.
EDCS-05503568.summary: This epitaph records a bequest from the heirs of Quintus Salvius of Barcinona, who served as aedile and quinquennial duovir.

EDCS-05503569.text_edition: \n Lucio Valerio Luci filio Galeria Terentiano nnum XXIIII dierum XL decurioni Barcinonensi Lucius Valerius Luci filius Cornelianus fratri pientissimo locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-05503569.translation: To Lucius Valerius Terentianus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, aged 24 years and 40 days, decurion of Barcinona; from his brother Lucius Valerius Cornelianus, most dutiful. A plot was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05503569.summary: This epitaph records the grant of burial space to Lucius Valerius Terentianus, a young decurion of Barcinona, by his devoted brother.

EDCS-05503572.text_edition: \n Marcus Cornelius Marci libertus Euvenus IIIIIIvir Augustalis sibi et Coeliae Luci filiae Severae uxori et Phaenusae libertae et Iphiclo liberto hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur nec locus sepulturae\n
