EDCS-05503572.translation: Marcus Cornelius Euvenus, freedman of Marcus, Augustalis duovir, for himself and for his wife Coelia Severa, daughter of Lucius, and for his freedwoman Phaenusa, and for his freedman Iphiclus; this monument will not pass to an heir, nor a burial plot.
EDCS-05503572.summary: This funerary monument, erected by Marcus Cornelius Euvenus, an Augustalis duovir, excludes heirs from inheritance and burial rights for himself and his household.

EDCS-05503574.text_edition: \n Lucio Licinio Secundo accenso patrono suo Lucio Licinio Surae primo secundo tertio consulatu eius IIIIIIviro Augustali coloniae Iuliae Urbis Triumphalis Tarraconis et coloniae Faventiae Iuliae Augustae Paternae Barcinonis ex decreto decurionum Barcinonensium\n
EDCS-05503574.translation: To Lucius Licinius Secundus, as lictor, for his patron Lucius Licinius Sura, during his first, second, and third consulships, and as a Six-man official of the Augustales of the colony Iulia Concordia Triumphalis Tarraconensis and the colony Faventia Iulia Augusta Paterna Barcinona, by decree of the decurions of Barcino.
EDCS-05503574.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Licinius Secundus, who held multiple public offices, likely on a monument erected by the town council of Barcino.

EDCS-05503575.text_edition: \n Lucio Licinio Secundo accenso patrono suo Lucio Licinio Surae primo secundo tertio consulatu eius IIIIIIviro Augustali coloniae Iuliae Urbis Triumphalis Tarraconis et coloniae Faventiae Iuliae Augustae Paternae Barcinonis ex decreto decurionum Barcinonensium\n
EDCS-05503575.translation: To Lucius Licinius Secundus, as lictor, for his patron Lucius Licinius Sura, during his first, second, and third consulships, and as a Six-man official of the Augustales of the colony Iulia Concordia Triumphalis Tarraconensis and the colony Faventia Iulia Augusta Paterna Barcinona, by decree of the decurions of Barcino.
EDCS-05503575.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Licinius Secundus, who held multiple public offices, likely on a monument erected by the town council of Barcino.

EDCS-05503576.text_edition: \n Lucio Licinio Secundo accenso patrono suo Lucio Licinio Surae primo secundo tertio consulatu eius IIIIIIviro Augustali coloniae Iuliae Urbis Triumphalis Tarraconis et coloniae Faventiae Iuliae Augustae Paternae Barcinonis ex decreto decurionum Ausetanorum\n
EDCS-05503576.translation: To Lucius Licinius Secundus, as lictor, for his patron Lucius Licinius Sura, during his first, second, and third consulships, and as a Six-man official of the Augustales of the colony Iulia Concordia Triumphalis Tarraconensis and the colony Faventia Iulia Augusta Paterna Barcinona, by decree of the decurions of the Ausetani.
EDCS-05503576.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Licinius Secundus, who held multiple public offices, likely on a monument erected by the decurions of the Ausetani.

EDCS-05503577.text_edition: \n Lucio Licinio Secundo accenso patrono suo Lucio Licinio Surae primo secundo tertio consulatu eius IIIIII viro Augustali coloniae Iuliae Urbis Triumphalis Tarraconis et coloniae Faventiae Iuliae Augustae Paternae Barcinonis ordo municipi Flavi Iamontani ex insula minore\n
EDCS-05503577.translation: To Lucius Licinius Secundus, as lictor, for his patron Lucius Licinius Sura, during his first, second, and third consulships, and as a Six-man official of the Augustales of the colony Iulia Concordia Triumphalis Tarraconensis and the colony Faventia Iulia Augusta Paterna Barcinona, the council of the municipium Flavi Iamontani, from the lesser island.
EDCS-05503577.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Licinius Secundus, who held multiple public offices, erected by the council of the municipium Flavi Iamontani.

EDCS-05503578.text_edition: \n Lucio Licinio Secundo accenso patrono suo Lucio Licinio Surae primo secundo tertio consulatu eius IIIIIIviro Augustali coloniae Iuliae Urbis Triumphalis Tarraconis et coloniae Faventiae Iuliae Augustae Paternae Barcinonis IIIIIIviri Augustales Barcinonis\n
EDCS-05503578.translation: To Lucius Licinius Secundus, as lictor, for his patron Lucius Licinius Sura, during his first, second, and third consulships, and as a Six-man official of the Augustales of the colony Iulia Concordia Triumphalis Tarraconensis and the colony Faventia Iulia Augusta Paterna Barcinona, by the Six-man officials of the Augustales of Barcino.
EDCS-05503578.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Licinius Secundus, who held multiple public offices, erected by the Six-man officials of the Augustales of Barcino.

EDCS-05503580.text_edition: \n Lucio Licinio Secundo accenso patrono suo Lucio Licinio Surae primo secundo tertio consulatu eius IIIIIIviro Augustali coloniae Iuliae Urbis Triumphalis Tarraconis et coloniae Faventiae Iuliae Augustae Paternae Barcinonis Lucius Flavius Chrysogonus IIIIIIvir Augustalis \n
EDCS-05503580.translation: To Lucius Licinius Secundus, as lictor, for his patron Lucius Licinius Sura, during his first, second, and third consulships, and as a Six-man official of the Augustales of the colony Iulia Concordia Triumphalis Tarraconensis and the colony Faventia Iulia Augusta Paterna Barcinona, Lucius Flavius Chrysogonus, a Six-man official of the Augustales.
EDCS-05503580.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Licinius Secundus, honoring him as a patron, by Lucius Flavius Chrysogonus, a local official.

EDCS-05503581.text_edition: \n Lucio Licinio Secundo accenso patrono suo Lucio Licinio Surae primo secundo tertio consulatu eius IIIIIIviro Augustali coloniae Iuliae Urbis Triumphalis Tarraconis et coloniae Faventiae Iuliae Augustae Paternae Barcinonis Marci Galerii Syrus Gratus IIIIIIviri Augustales coloniae Iuliae Urbis Triumphalis Tarraconis amico optimo\n
EDCS-05503581.translation: To Lucius Licinius Secundus, as lictor, for his patron Lucius Licinius Sura, during his first, second, and third consulships, and as a Six-man official of the Augustales of the colony Iulia Concordia Triumphalis Tarraconensis and the colony Faventia Iulia Augusta Paterna Barcinona, Marcus Galerius Syrus Gratus, a Six-man official of the Augustales of the colony Iulia Concordia Triumphalis Tarraconensis, for an excellent friend.
EDCS-05503581.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Licinius Secundus, honoring him as an excellent friend and patron, by Marcus Galerius Syrus Gratus, a local official.

EDCS-05503582.text_edition: \n Lucio Licinio Secundo accenso patrono suo Lucio Licinio Surae primo secundo tertio consulatu eius IIIIIIviro Augustali coloniae Iuliae Urbis Triumphalis Tarraconis et coloniae Faventiae Iuliae Augustae Paternae Barcinonis Marcus Quintius IIIIIIvir Aug(ustalis) --- amico\n
EDCS-05503582.translation: To Lucius Licinius Secundus, as lictor, for his patron Lucius Licinius Sura, during his first, second, and third consulships, and as a Six-man official of the Augustales of the colony Iulia Concordia Triumphalis Tarraconensis and the colony Faventia Iulia Augusta Paterna Barcinona, Marcus Quintius, Six-man official of the Augustales, for a friend.
EDCS-05503582.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Licinius Secundus, honoring him as a friend and patron, by Marcus Quintius, a local official.

EDCS-05503583.text_edition: \n Lucio Licinio Secundo accenso patrono suo Lucio Licinio Surae primo secundo tertio consulatu eius IIIIIIviro Augustali coloniae Iuliae Urbis Triumphalis Tarraconis et coloniae Faventiae Iuliae Augustae Paternae Barcinonis Caius Granius Felix amico\n
EDCS-05503583.translation: To Lucius Licinius Secundus, as lictor, for his patron Lucius Licinius Sura, during his first, second, and third consulships, and as a Six-man official of the Augustales of the colony Iulia Concordia Triumphalis Tarraconensis and the colony Faventia Iulia Augusta Paterna Barcinona, Gaius Granius Felix, for a friend.
EDCS-05503583.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Licinius Secundus, honoring him as a friend and patron, by Gaius Granius Felix.

EDCS-05503584.text_edition: \n Lucio Licinio Secundo accenso patrono suo Lucio Licinio Surae primo secundo tertio consulatu eius IIIIIIviro Augustali coloniae Iuliae Urbis Triumphalis Tarraconis et coloniae Faventiae Iuliae Augustae Paternae Barcinonis Caius Herennius Optatus amico\n
EDCS-05503584.translation: To Lucius Licinius Secundus, as lictor, for his patron Lucius Licinius Sura, during his first, second, and third consulships, and as a Six-man official of the Augustales of the colony Iulia Concordia Triumphalis Tarraconensis and the colony Faventia Iulia Augusta Paterna Barcinona, Gaius Herennius Optatus, for a friend.
EDCS-05503584.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Licinius Secundus, honoring him as a friend and patron, by Gaius Herennius Optatus.

EDCS-05503585.text_edition: \n Lucio Licinio Secundo accenso patrono suo Lucio Licinio Surae primo secundo tertio consulatu eius IIIIIIviro Augustali coloniae Iuliae Urbis Triumphalis Tarraconis et coloniae Faventiae Iuliae Augustae Paternae Barcinonis Marcus Paullius Paullinus amico\n
EDCS-05503585.translation: To Lucius Licinius Secundus, his attendant patron, Lucius Licinius Sura, in his first, second, and third consulships, a quinquennial priest of the Augustales of the colony Julia Urbs Triumphalis Tarraconensis and of the colony Faventia Julia Augusta Paterna Barcinona, Marcus Paullius Paullinus, his friend.
EDCS-05503585.summary: This funerary inscription honors Marcus Paullius Paullinus, dedicating it to his friend and patron Lucius Licinius Secundus and mentioning the patron's prestigious civic offices in Tarraconensis and Barcinona, likely dating to the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05503586.text_edition: \n Lucio Licinio Secundo accenso patrono suo Lucio Licinio Surae primo secundo tertio consulatu eius IIIIIIviro Augustali coloniae Iuliae Urbis Triumphalis Tarraconis et coloniae Faventiae Iuliae Augustae Paternae Barcinonis Perperna Numisianus \n
EDCS-05503586.translation: To Lucius Licinius Secundus, his attendant patron, Lucius Licinius Sura, in his first, second, and third consulships, a quinquennial priest of the Augustales of the colony Julia Urbs Triumphalis Tarraconensis and of the colony Faventia Julia Augusta Paterna Barcinona, Perperna Numisianus.
EDCS-05503586.summary: This funerary inscription honors Perperna Numisianus, dedicating it to his patron Lucius Licinius Secundus and mentioning the patron's high civic offices in Tarraconensis and Barcinona, likely dating to the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05503587.text_edition: \n Lucio Licinio Secundo accenso patrono suo Lucio Licinio Surae primo secundo tertio consulatu eius IIIIIIviro Augustali coloniae Iuliae Urbis Triumphalis Tarraconis et coloniae Faventiae Iuliae Augustae Paternae Barcinonis Montanus libertus \n
EDCS-05503587.translation: To Lucius Licinius Secundus, his attendant patron, Lucius Licinius Sura, in his first, second, and third consulships, a quinquennial priest of the Augustales of the colony Julia Urbs Triumphalis Tarraconensis and of the colony Faventia Julia Augusta Paterna Barcinona, Montanus, his freedman.
EDCS-05503587.summary: This funerary inscription honors Montanus, his freedman, dedicating it to his patron Lucius Licinius Secundus and detailing the patron's distinguished civic roles in Tarraconensis and Barcinona, likely dating to the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05503588.text_edition: \n Lucio Pedanio Clementini liberto Clementi IIIIIIviro Augustali Maximinus libertus patrono optimo locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-05503588.translation: To Lucius Pedanius Clementinus, his freedman Clementus, a quinquennial priest of the Augustales, Maximinus, his freedman, to his most excellent patron, a plot of land granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05503588.summary: This inscription records the granting of a burial plot by the decurions (local councillors) to Maximinus, a freedman, for his excellent patron Lucius Pedanius Clementinus, who was also a quinquennial priest of the Augustales.

EDCS-05503589.text_edition: \n Lucio Pedanio Luci liberto Euphroni IIIIIIviro Augustali Primus et Agathopus liberti Lucius Pedanius Clemens in memoriam Luci Pedani Euphronis cuius basis lapidea aere clusa vetustate erat corrupta statuam eius marmoreae superposuit permittente ordine Barcinonensium\n
EDCS-05503589.translation: To Lucius Pedanius Euphron, son of Lucius, Primus and Agathopus, freedmen of Lucius Pedanius Clemens, in memory of Lucius Pedanius Euphron, whose stone base, enclosed by bronze, had been corrupted by age; he placed his marble statue on top, with the permission of the magistrates of Barcino.
EDCS-05503589.summary: This inscription commemorates Lucius Pedanius Euphron, a freedman of Lucius Pedanius Clemens, detailing the restoration of his damaged tomb and the erection of a new statue, granted by the magistrates of Barcino.

EDCS-05503590.text_edition: \n Lucio Valerio Luci liberto Hedisto IIIIIIviro Augustali Valeria Thallusa marito indulgentissimo locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-05503590.translation: To Lucius Valerius Hedistus, son of Lucius, his most indulgent husband, a quinquennial priest of the Augustales, Valeria Thallusa, a plot of land granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05503590.summary: This inscription records the granting of a burial plot by the decurions to Valeria Thallusa for her indulgent husband, Lucius Valerius Hedistus, a quinquennial priest of the Augustales.

EDCS-05503592.text_edition: \n Clodiae Pephilemene Lucius Clodius Hyginus uxori locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-05503592.translation: To Clodia Pephilemene, Lucius Clodius Hyginus, his wife, a plot of land granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05503592.summary: This inscription records the granting of a burial plot by the decurions to Lucius Clodius Hyginus for his wife, Clodia Pephilemene.

EDCS-05503593.text_edition: \n Corneliae Luci filiae Secundinae Lucius Valerius Rufus maritae optimae et bene de se meritae et Cornelia Secunda mater filiae pientissimae locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-05503593.translation: To Cornelia Secundina, daughter of Lucius, his most excellent wife, who deserved well of him, and to Cornelia Secunda, her most dutiful mother, a plot of land granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05503593.summary: This inscription records the granting of a burial plot by the decurions to Lucius Valerius Rufus for his wife, Cornelia Secundina, and her mother, Cornelia Secunda.

EDCS-05503594.text_edition: \n Numisiae Luci? Numisi [At]tici filiae Perperniae Lucius Fulvius Luci filius Quirina Numisianus matri pientissimae locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-05503594.translation: To Numisia, daughter of Lucius [At]ticus Numisius, his most dutiful mother, Perpernia, Lucius Fulvius son of Lucius Quirinus Numisianus, a plot of land granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05503594.summary: This inscription records the granting of a burial plot by the decurions to Lucius Fulvius Quirinus Numisianus for his mother, Numisia Perpernia.

EDCS-05503595.text_edition: \n Valeriae Silvanae Caius Iulius Hispanus ob merita eius in se locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-05503595.translation: To Valeria Silvana, for her merits towards him, Caius Julius Hispanus, a plot of land granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05503595.summary: This inscription records the granting of a burial plot by the decurions to Caius Julius Hispanus for Valeria Silvana, acknowledging her merits towards him.

EDCS-05503596.text_edition: \n C(aio) Iulio C(ai) f(ilio) Rufo iario domo Tolosa Iulia Cai liberta Fausta Iulia Cai filia Primula Caio Iulio Cai filio Rufino\n
EDCS-05503596.translation: To Gaius Julius Rufus, son of Gaius, from Tolosa; to Julia Fausta, freedwoman of Gaius, daughter of Gaius; to Gaius Julius Rufinus, son of Gaius.
EDCS-05503596.summary: This funerary inscription lists three individuals: Gaius Julius Rufus, Julia Fausta, and Gaius Julius Rufinus, with Rufus and Fausta being from Tolosa.

EDCS-05503597.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelio Primiano Caecilia Daphinis marito karissimo et Caecilio Primiano filio dulcissimo\n
EDCS-05503597.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. To Aelius Primianus, dearest husband of Caecilia Daphnis, and to Caecilius Primianus, most beloved son.
EDCS-05503597.summary: This is a dedication to the Manes (spirits of the dead) for Aelius Primianus, husband of Caecilia Daphnis, and their son Caecilius Primianus.

EDCS-05503598.text_edition: \n Caius Annius Bassus sibi et Numeniae Quinti filiae Germanae\n
EDCS-05503598.translation: Gaius Annius Bassus for himself and for Numenia Germana, daughter of Quintus.
EDCS-05503598.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Gaius Annius Bassus for himself and Numenia Germana.

EDCS-05503600.text_edition: \n Marco Aufidio Fructo annorum XXII et Antoniae Synpherusae uxori annorum XXIII Marco Flaminio Fructino Antonia Peregrina de suo fecit hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-05503600.translation: Marcus Aufidius Fructus, aged 22, and his wife Antonia Synpherusa, aged 23. Marcus Flaminus Fructinus and Antonia Peregrina had this monument made at their own expense; it will not follow an heir.
EDCS-05503600.summary: This monument was erected by Marcus Flaminus Fructinus and Antonia Peregrina for Marcus Aufidius Fructus and his wife Antonia Synpherusa, specifying it is not hereditary.

EDCS-05503601.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cal<l=I>ityches annorum XXVII Gaius Trocina Agathio coniugi bene merenti et sibi \n
EDCS-05503601.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. To Callityches, aged 27. Gaius Trocina Agathio [dedicated this] to his deserving spouse and to himself.
EDCS-05503601.summary: This is a dedication to the Manes for Callityches, aged 27, made by Gaius Trocina Agathio for his spouse and himself.

EDCS-05503603.text_edition: \n Lucius Domitius Luci libertus Corinthus sibi et Domitiae Primulae uxori et Domitis Primulo Florentino Pyramo Phoebo Picentino libertis isdem filiis\n
EDCS-05503603.translation: Lucius Domitius Corinthus, freedman of Lucius, for himself, and for his wife Domitia Primula, and for his freedmen and likewise sons, Domitis Primulus, Florentinus, Pyramus, Phoebus, and Picentinus.
EDCS-05503603.summary: Lucius Domitius Corinthus records a dedication for himself, his wife Domitia Primula, and his freedmen who are also considered his sons.

EDCS-05503604.text_edition: \n Publio Fabio Publi liberto Aucto Victor libertus hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur nec locus sepulturae\n
EDCS-05503604.translation: To Publius Fabius Auctus, freedman of Publius. Victor, a freedman, made this monument; it will not follow an heir, nor a burial place.
EDCS-05503604.summary: This inscription states that Victor, a freedman, erected a monument for Publius Fabius Auctus, a freedman, and that the site is not hereditary.

EDCS-05503606.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Fabiae Psyche Telesphorus contubernali fidelissimae\n
EDCS-05503606.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. To Fabia Psyche, most faithful companion. Telesphorus took care to have this made.
EDCS-05503606.summary: Telesphorus commissioned this tomb for his most faithful companion, Fabia Psyche, dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-05503607.text_edition: \n Lucius Fabius Firmani libertus Quietus sibi et Miniciae Iucundae\n
EDCS-05503607.translation: Lucius Fabius Quietus, freedman of Firmanus, for himself and for Minicia Iucunda.
EDCS-05503607.summary: Lucius Fabius Quietus, a freedman, records a dedication for himself and Minicia Iucunda.

EDCS-05503608.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Faustinulae conservae bene merenti Secundinus faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-05503608.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. To Faustinula, a deserving fellow slave. Secundinus arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-05503608.summary: Secundinus commissioned this dedication to the Manes for his deserving fellow slave, Faustinula.

EDCS-05503609.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld: Fidela for her companion Iucunda.
EDCS-05503609.summary: A funerary dedication for Iucunda, set up by Fidela, likely a couple or close companions, datable to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503610.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gaio Aemilio Luci filio Nigro Trophime coniugi optimo\n
EDCS-05503610.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld: Trophime for her excellent husband, Gaius Aemilius Niger, son of Lucius.
EDCS-05503610.summary: A funerary dedication for Gaius Aemilius Niger, set up by his wife Trophime, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503611.text_edition: \n Decimus Herennuleius Dorotheus Porcia Marci liberta Buccia\n
EDCS-05503611.translation: Decimus Herennuleius Dorotheus and Buccia, freedwoman of Porcia, daughter of Marcus.
EDCS-05503611.summary: This is a funerary dedication possibly for Dorotheus, mentioning his status as a freedman and the name of his patroness Buccia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503612.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ianuariae bene merenti fecit Fortis\n
EDCS-05503612.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld: Fortis made this for the well-deserving Ianuaria.
EDCS-05503612.summary: A funerary dedication for Ianuaria, set up by Fortis, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503613.text_edition: \n Decimo Iulio Docilonis liberto Fausto et Decimo Iulio Decimi filio Verno\n
EDCS-05503613.translation: For Decimus Iulius Faustus, freedman of Docilo, and for Decimus Iulius Vernus, son of Decimus.
EDCS-05503613.summary: A funerary dedication for two individuals, Decimus Iulius Faustus and Decimus Iulius Vernus, likely related and possibly freedmen, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503614.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Luci Iuli Primuli Iulia Fortunata marito bene merenti\n
EDCS-05503614.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld: Iulia Fortunata for her well-deserving husband, Lucius Iulius Primulus.
EDCS-05503614.summary: A funerary dedication for Lucius Iulius Primulus, set up by his wife Iulia Fortunata, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503615.text_edition: \n Iuliae Amianthidi nnorum XXI Iulius Amianthus et Iulia Italia filiae pientissimae\n
EDCS-05503615.translation: For Iulia Amianthis, aged 21, Iulius Amianthus and Iulia Italia for their most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-05503615.summary: A funerary dedication for Iulia Amianthis, set up by her parents Iulius Amianthus and Iulia Italia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503616.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iuliae Chrysidi Iulia Eugenia matri karissimae\n
EDCS-05503616.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld: Iulia Eugenia for her dearest mother, Iulia Chrysida.
EDCS-05503616.summary: A funerary dedication for Iulia Chrysida, set up by her daughter Iulia Eugenia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503617.text_edition: \n Lucius Iunius Luci filius Iucundinus Iunia Luci filia Iucunda Titinia Urbana Calpurnia Luci filia Fida Aemilia Cai lib(erta) Avita\n
EDCS-05503617.translation: Lucius Iunius Iucundinus, son of Lucius; Iunia Iucunda, daughter of Lucius; Titinia Urbana; Calpurnia Fida, daughter of Lucius; Aemilia Avita, freedwoman of Caius.
EDCS-05503617.summary: This inscription lists the names of five women, possibly all related or part of a household, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503618.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucius Marius Rusticus et Lucius Marius Pisonianus Mariae Cetegianae sorori suae bene merenti de suo fecerunt\n
EDCS-05503618.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld: Lucius Marius Rusticus and Lucius Marius Pisonianus made this at their own expense for their well-deserving sister, Maria Cetegiana.
EDCS-05503618.summary: A funerary dedication for Maria Cetegiana, set up by her brothers Lucius Marius Rusticus and Lucius Marius Pisonianus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503621.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Porcio Ceriali Trocina Philumene marito bene merenti\n
EDCS-05503621.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Philumene for her husband Porcius Cerialis, who deserved well.
EDCS-05503621.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a wife to her deserving husband, Porcius Cerialis, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503624.text_edition: \n Lucio Rufidio Onesimo et Rufidiae Euche uxori et Rufidiae Fortunatae libertae et Rufidiae Festivae libertae et Lucio Rufidio Fortunato liberto\n
EDCS-05503624.translation: For Lucius Rufidius Onesimus and his wife Rufidia Euche, and their freedwoman Rufidia Fortunata, and their freedman Lucius Rufidius Fortunatus.
EDCS-05503624.summary: This epitaph lists several individuals, likely family members and freedmen, for whom a memorial was erected, probably in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503625.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Stabilius Caelestinus fecit se vivum\n
EDCS-05503625.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Marcus Stabilius Caelestinus made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-05503625.summary: This is a self-funded funerary monument erected by Marcus Stabilius Caelestinus, indicating he planned for his own burial, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503626.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Sulpicio Ursiano annorum XVI mensium XI dierum II Porcia Octavia mater et Decenber sic pater infelices filio dulcissimo et inconparabili! cuiius relicuiae sic in Helviano positae sunt\n
EDCS-05503626.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Sulpicius Ursianus, aged 16 years, 11 months, and 2 days. His mother Porcia Octavia and father Decenber [?] sic [made this] for their most sweet and incomparable son, whose remains are placed here in Helviana.
EDCS-05503626.summary: This epitaph records the death of a young boy, Sulpicius Ursianus, and is dedicated by his grieving parents, providing his age and the location of his burial, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503627.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cai Terenti Romani Valeria Crescentina marito bene merenti VII\n
EDCS-05503627.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Caius Terentius Romanus, [dedicated by] Valeria Crescentina, his wife, who deserved well. [He lived] 7 [years/months/days - number is incomplete].
EDCS-05503627.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Terentius Romanus, dedicated by his wife Valeria Crescentina, and includes a partial age, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503628.text_edition: \n filius Teretina Lenta Teretina Quintus Teretina Lentinus\n
EDCS-05503628.translation: Son of Teretina Lenta. Teretina Quintus. Son of Teretina Lentina.
EDCS-05503628.summary: This inscription lists individuals, likely a son and parents, identified by patronymic or matronymic, possibly part of a larger funerary monument, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503629.text_edition: \n Lucius Valerius Luci filius Aniensis Montanus \n
EDCS-05503629.translation: Lucius Valerius Montanus, son of Lucius, of the Aniensis tribe.
EDCS-05503629.summary: This is a formal declaration of Roman citizenship and tribal affiliation for Lucius Valerius Montanus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503630.text_edition: \n Valeriae Luci filiae Faventinai sic et filio Avito Hestricata sic mater fecit\n
EDCS-05503630.translation: For Valeria Faventina, daughter of Lucius, and [her] son Avitus. Hestricata, the mother, made this.
EDCS-05503630.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by a mother, Hestricata, for her daughter Valeria Faventina and grandson Avitus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503631.translation: Privata gave [this] to Galla Valeria Copa.
EDCS-05503631.summary: This inscription records a dedication or gift from Privata to Galla Valeria Copa, possibly a funerary context, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503633.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Usuleniae Valentinae Lucius Sempronius Flegon uxori optimae\n
EDCS-05503633.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Usulenian Valentina, his most excellent wife. Lucius Sempronius Flegon [made this].
EDCS-05503633.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Lucius Sempronius Flegon to his excellent wife, Usulenian Valentina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503634.text_edition: \n us Luci filius Quintio Corneliae Luci filiae uxsori sic\n
EDCS-05503634.translation: To Lucius, son of Lucius, for Quintia, daughter of Lucius, his wife.
EDCS-05503634.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintia, wife of Lucius, son of Lucius.

EDCS-05503637.text_edition: \n Platanus filiae pientissimae hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-05503637.translation: This monument to Platanus, my most devoted daughter, will not be inherited by an heir.
EDCS-05503637.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Platanus, a devoted daughter, indicating the inheritance status of the monument.

EDCS-05503641.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iunio Eutycheti patri pientissimo qui vixit nnos LXXV Iunia Euthychia filia pientissima faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-05503641.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iunia Euthychia, her most devoted daughter, saw to the construction of this monument for Eutychetes, her most devoted father, who lived 75 years.
EDCS-05503641.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Eutychetes by his devoted daughter Iunia Euthychia, who also records his age.

EDCS-05503642.text_edition: \n Lupae Augustae Lucius Visellius Evangeli libertus Tertius IIIIIIvir Augustalis\n
EDCS-05503642.translation: To Lupa Augusta. Lucius Visellius Evangelus, freedman Tertius, Augustalis priest.
EDCS-05503642.summary: This is an honorary inscription for Lupa Augusta, dedicated by Lucius Visellius Evangelus, a freedman and priest.

EDCS-05503643.text_edition: \n Soli deo sacrum Aulus P Abascantus\n
EDCS-05503643.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the god Sol by Aulus P. Abascantus.

EDCS-05503644.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Hadriani filio divi Traiani Parthici nepoti divi Nervae pronepoti Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate consuli III decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-05503644.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, Pontifex Maximus, holder of tribunician power, consul for the third time, son of the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Parthicus, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05503644.summary: This is an official dedication to Emperor Antoninus Pius, likely commemorating a public work funded by the local senate.

EDCS-05503645.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Antonio Gordiano Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate consuli proconsuli patri patriae optimo maximoque principi nostro ordo Baetulonensium devotus numini maiestatique eius\n
EDCS-05503645.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holder of tribunician power, consul, proconsul, father of the country, our best and greatest prince, the order of the Baetulonenses is devoted to his divine majesty.
EDCS-05503645.summary: This is an official dedication to Emperor Gordian III by the local senate of Baetulo, affirming their loyalty.

EDCS-05503646.text_edition: \n Sabiniae Tranquillinae sanctissimae Augustae coniugi domini nostri Marci Antoni Gordiani Pii Felicis Augusti ordo Baetulonensium devotissimus numini maiestatique eorum\n
EDCS-05503646.translation: To Sabinia Tranquillina, most holy Augusta, wife of our lord Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus, the most devoted order of Baetulonenses is devoted to their divine majesty.
EDCS-05503646.summary: This is an official dedication to Empress Sabinia Tranquillina by the local senate of Baetulo, affirming their loyalty.

EDCS-05503647.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Iulio Philippo Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae consuli proconsuli ordo decurionum Baetulonensium devotus numini maiestatique eius\n
EDCS-05503647.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holder of tribunician power, father of the country, consul, proconsul, the order of the decurions of Baetulo is devoted to his divine majesty.
EDCS-05503647.summary: This is an official dedication to Emperor Philip the Arab by the local senate of Baetulo, affirming their loyalty.

EDCS-05503648.text_edition: \n Quinto Licinio Quinti filio Silvano Graniano Quadronio Proculo IIIviro ad monetam tribuno militum legionis VI victricis piae fidelis decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-05503648.translation: To Quintus Licinius Gratianus Quadronius Proculus Silvanus, IIIvir for the mint, tribune of the soldiers of the VI legion Pia Fidelis, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05503648.summary: This is an honorary inscription for Quintus Licinius Proculus, a magistrate and military officer, by decree of the local senate.

EDCS-05503649.text_edition: \n Marco Fabio Galeria Nepoti Iessonensi aedili IIviro II flamini Romae et Augustorum curatori balinei novi ob curam et innocentiam ex decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-05503649.translation: To Marcus Fabius Nepos of Iessona, aedile twice, duovir twice, and flamen of Rome and of the emperors, curator of the new baths, in recognition of his care and integrity, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05503649.summary: This inscription records a public honor conferred upon Marcus Fabius Nepos by the local council for his service as aedile, duovir, flamen, and curator of baths.

EDCS-05503651.text_edition: \n Bono Eventui Augusto sacrum Publius Aemilius Gemellus VIvir Augustalis\n
EDCS-05503651.translation: Sacred to Bonus Eventus Augustus: Publius Aemilius Gemellus, six-member Augustalis.
EDCS-05503651.summary: This is a dedication to the deified Bonus Eventus by Publius Aemilius Gemellus, who held the rank of VIvir Augustalis.

EDCS-05503652.text_edition: \n Iunoni Augustae sacrum Caius Quintius Quintiae Severae libertus Myronus IIIIIIvir Augustalis\n
EDCS-05503652.translation: Sacred to Juno Augusta: Myron, freedman of Gaius Quintius and Quintia Severa, four-member Augustalis.
EDCS-05503652.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Juno Augusta made by Myron, a freedman who held the rank of VIvir Augustalis.

EDCS-05503653.text_edition: \n Mercurio Augusto sacrum Caius Baebius Corinthus VIvir Augustalis\n
EDCS-05503653.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus: Gaius Baebius Corinthus, six-member Augustalis.
EDCS-05503653.summary: This is a dedication to Mercury Augustus by Gaius Baebius Corinthus, a VIvir Augustalis.

EDCS-05503654.text_edition: \n Silvano Augusto sacrum Publius Cornelius Florus VIvir Augustalis\n
EDCS-05503654.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus: Publius Cornelius Florus, six-member Augustalis.
EDCS-05503654.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Silvanus Augustus by Publius Cornelius Florus, who was a VIvir Augustalis.

EDCS-05503655.text_edition: \n Lucius Marcius Quinti filius Galeria Optatus aedilis Tarracone IIvir Ilurone et IIvir quinquennalis primus praefectus Asturiae tribunus militum legionis secundae Augustae nnum XXXVI in Phrygia decessit\n
EDCS-05503655.translation: Lucius Marcius Optatus, son of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe, aedile at Tarraco, duovir of Iluro and quinquennial duovir, first prefect of Asturia, military tribune of the Second Legion Augusta, died at 36 years of age in Phrygia.
EDCS-05503655.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the career and death of Lucius Marcius Optatus, a prominent local official and military officer.

EDCS-05503657.text_edition: \n Dianae ob honorem seviratus Caius Cornelius Grati libertus Magnio IIIIIIvir idem aram et sedilia\n
EDCS-05503657.translation: To Diana, for the honor of the office of six-member Augustalis: Gaius Cornelius Magnio, freedman of Gratus, also dedicated the altar and the seats.
EDCS-05503657.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Diana by Gaius Cornelius Magnio, a freedman who held the office of VIvir Augustalis, who also provided the altar and seats.

EDCS-05503658.text_edition: \n Antonius Galeria Verus Cornelia filia Nigrina uxor\n
EDCS-05503658.translation: Antonius Verus and Cornelia Nigrina, his wife, daughter of Galeria.
EDCS-05503658.summary: This is a funerary epitaph commemorating Antonius Verus and his wife Cornelia Nigrina.

EDCS-05503659.text_edition: \n Sabiniae Tranquillinae Augustae res publica Gerundensium\n
EDCS-05503659.translation: To Sabinia Tranquillina Augusta, the state of Gerundenses.
EDCS-05503659.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Sabinia Tranquillina Augusta by the municipality of Gerundenses.

EDCS-05503660.text_edition: \n Marco Iulio Philippo nobilissimo Caesari res publica Gerundensium\n
EDCS-05503660.translation: To Marcus Julius Philippus, most noble Caesar, the state of Gerundenses.
EDCS-05503660.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Marcus Julius Philippus, a highly esteemed Caesar, by the municipality of Gerundenses.

EDCS-05503661.text_edition: \n Lucio Plotio Luci filio Galeria Asprenati aedili IIviro flamini tribuno legionis III Gallicae Iulia Cai filia Marcia marito indulgentissimo\n
EDCS-05503661.translation: To Lucius Plotus Asprenas, son of Lucius, of the Galerian tribe, aedile, duovir, flamen, and tribune of the Third Gallic Legion, and to Julia Marcia, daughter of Gaius, for her most indulgent husband.
EDCS-05503661.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Julia Marcia to her husband, Lucius Plotus Asprenas, highlighting his public and military career.

EDCS-05503663.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Valerio Lavino beneficiario consulari Marcus Valerius Geminus fratri optimo\n
EDCS-05503663.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Valerius Geminus to his most excellent brother, Marcus Valerius Lavinus, a consular associate.
EDCS-05503663.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Marcus Valerius Geminus for his brother, Marcus Valerius Lavinus, who served as a consular associate.

EDCS-05503664.text_edition: \n Lucio Aemilio Montano Bacasitano Lacerilis filio hic situs est\n
EDCS-05503664.translation: Lucius Aemilius Montanus Bacasitanus lies here, son of Lacerilis.
EDCS-05503664.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription stating that Lucius Aemilius Montanus Bacasitanus is buried here.

EDCS-05503666.text_edition: \n Publio Fabrinio Primo et Corneliae Atacinae Publius Fabrinius Modestus libertus LI patrono et sibi hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-05503666.translation: To Publius Fabrinius Primus and Cornelia Atacina. Publius Fabrinius Modestus, a freedman, [dedicated this monument] to his patron and to himself; he will not be succeeded by an heir.
EDCS-05503666.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Publius Fabrinius Modestus for his patron, Publius Fabrinius Primus, and for himself, with the stipulation that no heir would inherit it.

EDCS-05503667.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription stating that Sergia Montana is buried here.

EDCS-05600117.translation: To Emperor Nerva Germanicus Augustus, holding tribunician power, consul for the third time, father of the country.
EDCS-05600117.summary: This fragmentary inscription honors Emperor Nerva, listing his titles and powers, likely from the period of his reign (96-98 AD).

EDCS-05600155.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Felix Invictus and Magnus Augustus, and to Marcus Opellius Antoninus Diadumenianus, most noble Caesar, prince of youth.
EDCS-05600155.summary: This inscription honors the emperors Macrinus and his son Diadumenianus, with Macrinus also holding the title Magnus Augustus.

EDCS-05600165.translation: To Gaius Calpetanus Rantius Quirinalis Valerius Festus, legate of the emperor with proconsular power, [from] Bracara, 13 miles.
EDCS-05600165.summary: This inscription records the construction or length of a road segment of 13 miles from Bracara, built under the authority of the imperial legate Gaius Calpetanus Rantius Quirinalis Valerius Festus.

EDCS-05600168.translation: To Domitian, consul for the seventh time; to Gaius Calpetanus Rantius Quirinalis Valerius Festus, legate of the emperor with proconsular power, a new road, 18 miles.
EDCS-05600168.summary: This inscription records the construction of an 18-mile new road, dedicated under the consulship of Domitian and under the authority of the imperial legate Gaius Calpetanus Rantius Quirinalis Valerius Festus.

EDCS-05600169.translation: Under the emperor Titus Caesar Vespasianus Augustus, son of the deified Vespasian, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 9th time, imperator for the 15th time, father of the country, consul for the 8th time; Caesar Domitianus, son of the deified Vespasian, consul for the 7th time. For Caius Calpetanus Rantius Quirinalis Festus, legate of the emperor, pro praetore, from Bracara, 19 miles. The Via Nova was built.
EDCS-05600169.summary: This inscription records the construction of the Via Nova road under Emperors Vespasian and Domitian, dedicating it to the legate Caius Calpetanus Rantius Quirinalis Festus.

EDCS-05600175.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Traianus Decius Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, proconsul twice, consul twice, father of the fatherland. 25 miles from Bracara.
EDCS-05600175.summary: This inscription is a milestone marking a distance of 25 miles from Bracara, dedicated to Emperor Trajan Decius.

EDCS-05600178.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Augustus Gaius Messius Traianus Decius Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, proconsul four times, consul twice, father of the fatherland. 26 miles from Bracara Augusta.
EDCS-05600178.summary: This inscription is a milestone marking a distance of 26 miles from Bracara Augusta, dedicated to Emperor Trajan Decius.

EDCS-05600180.translation: Emperor Caesar Vespasian Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power for the 9th time, invoked as imperator 19 times, father of the fatherland, consul 8 times, gave and dedicated the work of widening the road. 27 miles from Bracara Augusta.
EDCS-05600180.summary: This milestone records the widening of a road, dedicated by Emperor Vespasian, dating to his 9th tribunician power.

EDCS-05600201.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Traianus Decius Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, proconsul four times, consul three times. 34 miles from Bracara Augusta.
EDCS-05600201.summary: This milestone marks a distance of 34 miles from Bracara Augusta, dedicated to Emperor Trajan Decius.

EDCS-05600204.translation: To the Emperor Titus Caesar Vespasian Augustus, son of the divine Vespasian, chief priest, holder of tribunician power for the 9th time, invoked as imperator 15 times, father of the fatherland, consul 8 times; [and to Domitian], son of the divine Vespasian, consul 7 times. The new road from Bracara was constructed under the charge of Gaius Calpetanus Rantius Quirinalis Valerius Festus, imperial legate pro praetore. 34 miles.
EDCS-05600204.summary: This milestone records the construction of the Via Nova from Bracara, dedicated to Emperors Titus and Domitian, and overseen by legate Gaius Calpetanus Rantius Quirinalis Valerius Festus.

EDCS-05600207.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power for the 18th time, consul three times, father of the fatherland. 35 miles from Bracara.
EDCS-05600207.summary: This milestone marks a distance of 35 miles from Bracara, dedicated to Emperor Hadrian.

EDCS-05600222.text_edition: \n Imperatore Tito Caesare divi Vespasiani filio Vespasiano Augusto pontifice maximo tribunicia potestate VIII imperatore XV patri patriae consule VIII Caesare divi Vespasiani filio Domitiano consule VII via nova facta ab Asturica Bracaram Caio Calpetano Rantio Quirinale Valerio milia passuum XXIII\n
EDCS-05600222.translation: Under Emperor Titus Caesar Vespasian Augustus, son of the divine Vespasian, chief priest, holder of tribunician power for the 8th time, invoked as imperator 15 times, father of the fatherland, consul 8 times; and Caesar Domitian, son of the divine Vespasian, consul 7 times. A new road was made from Asturica to Bracara under the charge of Gaius Calpetanus Rantius Quirinalis Valerius. 23 miles.
EDCS-05600222.summary: This inscription records the construction of a new road from Asturica to Bracara, completed under Emperors Titus and Domitian and overseen by Gaius Calpetanus Rantius Quirinalis Valerius.

EDCS-05600226.translation: Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, Germanicus Maximus, Dacicus Maximus, Sarmaticus Maximus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power for the 5th time, invoked as imperator 7 times, father of the fatherland, proconsul; and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, Germanicus Maximus, Dacicus Maximus, Sarmaticus Maximus, prince of the youth, son of our lord Emperor Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, ordered the roads and bridges, collapsed by age, to be restored. Under the charge of Quintus Decius, legate of the emperors pro praetore. 66 miles from Bracara.
EDCS-05600226.summary: This inscription records the restoration of roads and bridges, ordered by Emperors Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus Caesar, and overseen by legate Quintus Decius, marking a distance of 66 miles from Bracara.

EDCS-05600235.translation: To the Emperor Caesar [Trajan] Hadrianus Augustus, chief priest, [...]
EDCS-05600235.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription dedicated to Emperor Hadrianus, likely a milestone, but incomplete.

EDCS-05600246.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Constantino perpetuo semper Augusto\n
EDCS-05600246.translation: To our lord Constantine, forever Augustus.
EDCS-05600246.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Constantine.

EDCS-05600249.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Probo Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae pro consuli\n
EDCS-05600249.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Probus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, father of the country, proconsul.
EDCS-05600249.summary: This is a titulary inscription honoring Emperor Probus, likely from a public monument or milestone.

EDCS-05600250.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Carino Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae consuli proconsuli\n
EDCS-05600250.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carinus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, father of the country, consul, proconsul.
EDCS-05600250.summary: This is a titulary inscription honoring Emperor Carinus, likely from a public monument or milestone.

EDCS-05600251.text_edition: \n Tiberius Caesar divi Augusti filius Divi Iuli nepos Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XXXV imperator IIX consul V a Pisoraca milia passuum I\n
EDCS-05600251.translation: Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of the deified Augustus, grandson of the deified Julius, chief priest, holder of tribunician power for the 35th time, emperor for the 8th time, consul for the 5th time, one mile from Pisoraca.
EDCS-05600251.summary: This is a milestone inscription recording the distance from Pisoraca and listing the imperial titles of Tiberius during his 35th tribunician year.

EDCS-05600252.text_edition: \n Nero Claudius divi Claudi Augusti filius Germanici Caesaris nepos Tiberi Caesaris Augusti pronepos divi Augusti abnepos Caesar Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate imperator consul a Pisoraca milia passuum I\n
EDCS-05600252.translation: Caesar Augustus Germanicus, Nero Claudius, son of the deified Claudius Augustus, grandson of Germanicus Caesar, great-grandson of Tiberius Caesar Augustus, great-great-grandson of the deified Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, emperor, consul, one mile from Pisoraca.
EDCS-05600252.summary: This is a milestone inscription recording the distance from Pisoraca and listing the imperial titles of Nero.

EDCS-05600254.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Gaio Iulio Vero Maximino Pio Felici Augusto Germanico maximo Dacico maximo Sarmatico maximo pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate V imperatori VII patri patriae consuli proconsuli Gaio Iulio Vero Maximo nobilissimo Caesari Germanico maximo Dacico maximo Sarmatico maximo principi iuventutis filio domini nostri Imperatoris Gaii Iuli Veri Maximini Pii Felicis Augusti vias et pontes tempore vetustatis conlapsos restituerunt curante Quinto Decio legato Augustorum pro praetore clarissimo viro\n
EDCS-05600254.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, conqueror of Germany, conqueror of Dacia, conqueror of Sarmatia, chief priest, holder of tribunician power for the 5th time, emperor for the 7th time, father of the country, consul, proconsul; and to Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, conqueror of Germany, prince of youth, son of our lord Emperor Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus. They restored roads and bridges collapsed by age, under the supervision of Quintus Decius, clarissimus vir, legate of the emperors pro praetore.
EDCS-05600254.summary: This inscription records the restoration of roads and bridges under the authority of Emperor Maximinus Thrax and his son Caesar Maximinus, overseen by the legate Quintus Decius.

EDCS-05600255.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Caius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus Germanicus maximus Dacicus maximus Sarmaticus maximus pontifex maximus tribuniciae potestatis V imperator VII pater patriae consul proconsul et Caius Iulius Verus Maximus nobilissimus Caesar Germanicus maximus princeps iuventutis filius domini nostri Imperatoris Cai Iuli Veri Maximini Pii Felicis Augusti vias et pontes tempore vetustatis conlapsos restituerunt curante <Q=O>(uinto) Decio legato Augusti pro praetore clarissimo viro via Augusta milia passuum C \n
EDCS-05600255.translation: Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, conqueror of Germany, conqueror of Dacia, conqueror of Sarmatia, chief priest, holder of tribunician power for the 5th time, emperor for the 7th time, father of the country, consul, proconsul; and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, conqueror of Germany, prince of youth, son of our lord Emperor Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus. They restored roads and bridges collapsed by age, under the supervision of Quintus Decius, clarissimus vir, legate of the emperor pro praetore; the Via Augusta, 100 miles.
EDCS-05600255.summary: This inscription records the restoration of the Via Augusta over 100 miles, under the authority of Emperor Maximinus Thrax and his son Caesar Maximinus, overseen by the legate Quintus Decius.

EDCS-05600256.text_edition: \n Nero Claudius divi Claudi filius Caesar Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VIII imperator IX consul IIII a Pisoraca milia passum CLXXX\n
EDCS-05600256.translation: Nero Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, son of the deified Claudius, chief priest, holder of tribunician power for the 8th time, emperor for the 9th time, consul for the 4th time, 180 miles from Pisoraca.
EDCS-05600256.summary: This is a milestone inscription recording the distance from Pisoraca and listing the imperial titles of Nero.

EDCS-05600257.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Lucii Septimii Perthinacis Augusti Arabici Parthici maximi filio divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici nepoti divi Antonini Pii pronepoti divi Hadriani abnepoti divi Traiani\n
EDCS-05600257.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Lucius Septimius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Parthicus Maximus, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan.
EDCS-05600257.summary: This is a titulary inscription listing the extensive imperial lineage of an unnamed emperor, likely from the Severan period.

EDCS-05600258.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate pater patriae consul II fecit ab Augustobriga milia passuum II\n
EDCS-05600258.translation: Emperor Caesar Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, father of the country, consul for the 2nd time, made it, two miles from Augustobriga.
EDCS-05600258.summary: This is a milestone inscription recording the construction and distance from Augustobriga under Emperor Trajan.

EDCS-05600260.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Traiani Parthici filius divi Nervae nepos Traianus Hadrianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XV consul III pater patriae refecit ab Augustobriga milia passuum III\n
EDCS-05600260.translation: Emperor Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, chief priest, holder of tribunician power for the 15th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the country, repaired it, three miles from Augustobriga.
EDCS-05600260.summary: This is a milestone inscription recording the repair of a road and its distance from Augustobriga under Emperor Hadrian.

EDCS-05600266.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate pater patriae consul III fecit ab Augustobriga milia passuum X\n
EDCS-05600266.translation: Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus, chief priest, with tribunician power, father of the fatherland, consul for the third time, made [this road] ten miles from Augustobriga.
EDCS-05600266.summary: This milestone records the construction of a road section by Emperor Trajan, dated to his consulship.

EDCS-05600268.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate pater patriae consul III fecit ab Augustobriga milia passuum X\n
EDCS-05600268.translation: Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus, chief priest, with tribunician power, father of the fatherland, consul for the third time, made [this road] ten miles from Augustobriga.
EDCS-05600268.summary: This milestone records the construction of a road section by Emperor Trajan, dated to his consulship.

EDCS-05600269.text_edition: \n Tiberius Claudius Nero Imperator Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate IIII consul XIII imperator VIII pater patriae Augustobrigam milia passuum XXVI\n
EDCS-05600269.translation: Emperor Tiberius Claudius Nero Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the fourth time, consul for the thirteenth time, imperator for the eighth time, father of the fatherland, [made this road] twenty-six miles from Augustobriga.
EDCS-05600269.summary: This milestone records the construction of a road section by Emperor Tiberius, likely dating to his eighth acclamation as imperator.

EDCS-05600270.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Flavio Valerio Constantino Pio Felici Invicto Augusto\n
EDCS-05600270.translation: To Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantine, Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered, Augustus.
EDCS-05600270.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Constantine I, likely on a public monument.

EDCS-05600271.text_edition: \n Imperatori Flavio Valerio Constantio Pio Felici invicto Augusto\n
EDCS-05600271.translation: To Emperor Flavius Valerius Constantius, Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered, Augustus.
EDCS-05600271.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Constantius I Chlorus.

EDCS-05600272.translation: Tiberius Caesar, son of the divine Augustus, descendant of the divine Julius, chief priest, consul for the fifth time, imperator for the eighth time, with tribunician power for the thirty-fourth time, [this road] one mile [long].
EDCS-05600272.summary: This fragmentary milestone records road construction under Tiberius Caesar, dating to his 34th tribunician power.

EDCS-05600273.text_edition: \n Tiberius Caesar divi Augusti filius Augustus consul II tribunicia potestate XVI imperator VII pontifex maximus milia passuum II\n
EDCS-05600273.translation: Tiberius Caesar, son of the divine Augustus, Augustus, consul for the second time, with tribunician power for the sixteenth time, imperator for the seventh time, chief priest, [this road] two miles [long].
EDCS-05600273.summary: This milestone records road construction under Tiberius Caesar, dated to his seventh acclamation as imperator and sixteenth tribunician power.

EDCS-05600274.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Nervae nepos Traianus Hadrianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XIIX consul III pater patriae a Cara milia passuum III\n
EDCS-05600274.translation: Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, descendant of the divine Nerva, chief priest, with tribunician power for the eighteenth time, consul for the third time, father of the fatherland, [made this road] three miles from Cara.
EDCS-05600274.summary: This milestone records road construction by Emperor Hadrian, dated to his third consulship and eighteenth tribunician power.

EDCS-05600277.text_edition: \n Domino indulgentissimo Marco Numerio Numeriano Nobilissimo et Piissimo Caesari\n
EDCS-05600277.translation: To the most indulgent lord, Marcus Numerius Numerianus, most noble and most pious Caesar.
EDCS-05600277.summary: This is a dedication to Caesar Marcus Numerius Numerianus, likely a member of the imperial family, possibly during the late 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05600278.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Imperatori Caesari Flavio Valerio Constantino Pio Felici Invicto semper maximo Augusto duvu Constantini Augusti Pii filio \n
EDCS-05600278.translation: To our lord Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantine, Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered, ever-greatest Augustus, son of the divine Constantine Augustus, Pious.
EDCS-05600278.summary: This dedication honors Emperor Constantine I, emphasizing his divine lineage and titles.

EDCS-05600280.text_edition: \n Imperator Nerva Caesar Augustus Traianus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate IIII pater patriae consul II restituit a Compluto \n
EDCS-05600280.translation: Emperor Nerva Caesar Augustus Traianus Germanicus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 4th time, father of the country, consul for the 2nd time, restored this from Complutum.
EDCS-05600280.summary: This inscription records an imperial restoration by Emperor Trajan, dated to his 4th tribunician power and 2nd consulship.

EDCS-05600281.text_edition: \n Imperator Nerva Caesar Augustus Traianus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate IIII pater patriae consul II restituit a Compluto \n
EDCS-05600281.translation: Emperor Nerva Caesar Augustus Traianus Germanicus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 4th time, father of the country, consul for the 2nd time, restored this from Complutum.
EDCS-05600281.summary: This inscription records an imperial restoration by Emperor Trajan, dated to his 4th tribunician power and 2nd consulship.

EDCS-05600282.text_edition: \n Imperator Nerva Caesar Augustus Traianus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate IIII pater patriae cons(ul) II restituit a Compluto XIIII\n
EDCS-05600282.translation: Emperor Nerva Caesar Augustus Traianus Germanicus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 4th time, father of the country, consul for the 2nd time, restored this from Complutum, 14 miles away.
EDCS-05600282.summary: This inscription records an imperial restoration by Emperor Trajan, specifying a distance of 14 miles from Complutum, dated to his 4th tribunician power and 2nd consulship.

EDCS-05600296.text_edition: \n Tiberius Claudius Drusi filius Caesar Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate IIII imperator VIII consul III pater patriae CCXVIII\n
EDCS-05600296.translation: Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, son of Drusus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 4th time, emperor for the 8th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the country. 218.
EDCS-05600296.summary: This inscription lists the titles and honors of Emperor Claudius, including his 4th tribunician power, 8th acclamation as emperor, and 3rd consulship.

EDCS-05600297.text_edition: \n Imperator Nerva Caesar Augustus Germanicus inferior pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate \n
EDCS-05600297.translation: Emperor Nerva Caesar Augustus Germanicus the Younger, chief priest, with tribunician power.
EDCS-05600297.summary: This inscription records the titles of Emperor Trajan, identifying him as 'Germanicus the Younger' and noting his tribunician power.

EDCS-05600300.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Nervae filius Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate II cos II pater patriae fecit et restituit\n
EDCS-05600300.translation: Emperor Caesar Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus, son of the deified Nerva, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 2nd time, consul for the 2nd time, father of the country, made and restored this.
EDCS-05600300.summary: This inscription records imperial construction and restoration work by Emperor Trajan, dated to his 2nd tribunician power and 2nd consulship.

EDCS-05600301.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Nervae filius Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate II cos II pater patriae fecit et restituit\n
EDCS-05600301.translation: Emperor Caesar Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus, son of the deified Nerva, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 2nd time, consul for the 2nd time, father of the country, made and restored this.
EDCS-05600301.summary: This inscription records imperial construction and restoration work by Emperor Trajan, dated to his 2nd tribunician power and 2nd consulship.

EDCS-05600302.text_edition: \n Tiberius Caesar divi Augusti filius Divi nepos Augustus pontifex maxumus consul V imperator VIII tribunicia potestate XXXIIII aGON\n
EDCS-05600302.translation: Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of the deified Augustus, grandson of the deified [Caesar], chief priest, consul for the 5th time, emperor for the 8th time, with tribunician power for the 34th time. AGON.
EDCS-05600302.summary: This inscription details the extensive titles and powers of Emperor Tiberius, including his 5th consulship, 8th acclamation as emperor, and 34th tribunician power.

EDCS-05600303.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Divi filius Augustus consul XI tribunicia potestate XVI imperator XIIII pontifex maximus XXII\n
EDCS-05600303.translation: Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the deified [Caesar], consul for the 11th time, with tribunician power for the 16th time, emperor for the 14th time, chief priest for the 22nd time.
EDCS-05600303.summary: This inscription lists the accumulated titles and honors of an emperor, likely Hadrian or Antoninus Pius, showing his 11th consulship, 16th tribunician power, 14th acclamation as emperor, and 22nd time as chief priest.

EDCS-05600309.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Numerianus, Most Noble Caesar.
EDCS-05600309.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Marcus Aurelius Numerianus, a Caesar (junior emperor), likely dating to the period of his co-rule.

EDCS-05600310.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Marcus Cassianius Latinius Postumus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus Germanicus maximus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate consul III pater patriae proconsul restituit\n
EDCS-05600310.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Cassianius Latinius Postumus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, greatest German, chief priest, with tribunician power, consul for the third time, father of the country, proconsul, restored this.
EDCS-05600310.summary: An inscription recording the restoration of a public work by Emperor Postumus, dated to his reign (260-269 AD).

EDCS-05600316.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Gaio Messio Quinto Traiano Decio Invicto Pio Felici Augusto Dacico maximo pontifici maximo tribuniciae potestates II consuli II patri patriae pro consuli et Quinto Herennio Etruscio Messio nobilissimo Caesari via Augusta milia passuum CLIX\n
EDCS-05600316.translation: For Emperor Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Traianus Decius Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest Dacian, chief priest, with tribunician power for the second time, consul for the second time, father of the country, proconsul, and for Quintus Herennius Etruscus Messius Decius, most noble Caesar: the Augusta road, 159 miles.
EDCS-05600316.summary: An inscription marking a section of the Augusta road, dedicated to Emperors Decius and his son Herennius Etruscus, likely dating to their joint reign (250-251 AD).

EDCS-05600320.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Caio Messio Quinto Traiano Decio Invicto Pio Felici Augusto Dacico maximo et Quinto Herennio Etrusco Messio Decio Nobilissimo Caesari pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate III consuli II patri patriae procons(uli) via Augusta milia passuum CLXXX\n
EDCS-05600320.translation: For Emperor Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Traianus Decius Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest Dacian, and for Quintus Herennius Etruscus Messius Decius, most noble Caesar, chief priest, with tribunician power for the third time, consul for the second time, father of the country, proconsul: the Augusta road, 180 miles.
EDCS-05600320.summary: An inscription indicating a segment of the Augusta road, dedicated to Emperors Decius and Herennius Etruscus, with dating inferable from the tribunician power and consulship (likely around 251 AD).

EDCS-05600324.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari <C=Q>(aio) Messio Quinto Traiano Decio Pio Felici Invicto Augusto Dacico maximo pontifici maximo O tribunicia potestate IIII patri patriae consuli III proconsuli et Quinto Herennio Etrusco Messio Decio consuli et Caio Valerio Hostiliano \n
EDCS-05600324.translation: For Emperor Caesar (Gaius) Messius Quintus Traianus Decius Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, greatest Dacian, chief priest, with tribunician power for the fourth time, consul for the third time, father of the country, proconsul, and for Quintus Herennius Etruscus Messius Decius, consul, and for Gaius Valerius Hostilianus.
EDCS-05600324.summary: An inscription honoring Emperors Decius and Herennius Etruscus, and Gaius Valerius Hostilianus, with dating indicated by their offices, likely around 251 AD.

EDCS-05600328.translation: Valerius Tiro Dialco willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05600328.summary: A votive inscription by Valerius Tiro Dialco, fulfilling a vow, likely from the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05600334.text_edition: \n Septumia sic Cai liberta Secunda viva sibi fecit hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-05600334.translation: Septumia Secunda, freedwoman of Caius, made this monument for herself while alive; she will not be succeeded by an heir.
EDCS-05600334.summary: A tombstone inscription for Septumia Secunda, who commissioned her own burial monument, likely from the 1st-3rd centuries AD.

EDCS-05600405.translation: Proculus, son of Lucius, from Ux{s}ama, aged 3 years, is buried here.
EDCS-05600405.summary: A grave marker for a young child named Proculus, indicating his parentage and origin, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600407.translation: [...], a citizen, a teacher of grammar, aged 70 years, is buried here; his sister took care to have this made.
EDCS-05600407.summary: A tombstone inscription for a grammarian who died at the age of 70, with the monument arranged by his sister, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600408.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Modia Victoria Sofia lived for 35 years.
EDCS-05600408.summary: A tombstone inscription for Modia Victoria Sofia, stating her name and age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600411.text_edition: \n Nymphis fontis Amevi Cnaeus Lucius Terentius Luci filius Homullus Iunior legatus legionis VII geminae felicis\n
EDCS-05600411.translation: To the Nymphs of the Amevi spring, Gnaeus Lucius Terentius Homullus Junior, son of Lucius, legate of the victorious Seventh Twin Legion, [dedicated this].
EDCS-05600411.summary: A dedication to the Nymphs of a spring by Gnaeus Lucius Terentius Homullus Junior, a legionary legate, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600446.text_edition: \n Lucius Vergilius Luci libertus Hilarus sutor hic situs est faciendum curaverunt uxor et liberti\n
EDCS-05600446.translation: Lucius Vergilius Hilarus, freedman of Lucius, a shoemaker, lies here. His wife and freedmen arranged for his burial.
EDCS-05600446.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Vergilius Hilarus, a shoemaker, erected by his wife and freedmen.

EDCS-05600447.text_edition: \n Publius Didius Publi l(ibertus) Felix hic situs est\n
EDCS-05600447.translation: Publius Didius Felix, freedman of Publius, lies here.
EDCS-05600447.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Didius Felix, a freedman.

EDCS-05600630.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelius Cosconianus erected this for his dearest sister, Julia Vitalis, aged 65.
EDCS-05600630.summary: A funerary dedication to Julia Vitalis by her brother Aurelius Cosconianus.

EDCS-05600837.text_edition: \n Caio Annio Silvano annorum L Annius Rufinus patri\n
EDCS-05600837.translation: To Gaius Annianus Silvanus, aged 50. Annius Rufinus [erected this for his] father.
EDCS-05600837.summary: A funerary dedication to Gaius Annianus Silvanus by his son Annius Rufinus.

EDCS-05600840.summary: A dedication to Silvanus, son of Apilicus.

EDCS-05600844.translation: To the gods. Cassius Arcesilaus [erected this for] his most sweet son, Cassius Dion.
EDCS-05600844.summary: A funerary dedication to Cassius Dion by his father Cassius Arcesilaus.

EDCS-05600847.translation: Lucius Pompeius Paternus fulfilled his vow to Mandica, deservedly.
EDCS-05600847.summary: A votive inscription by Lucius Pompeius Paternus to Mandica.

EDCS-05600853.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus. Flaccus Aelianus, procurator of the emperors, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05600853.summary: A votive inscription to Mercury Augustus by Flaccus Aelianus, a procurator.

EDCS-05600864.translation: To the departed spirits. Hermodorus and Sextilia, parents, and Martialis, husband, [erected this for their] most dutiful wife/daughter, Helena, aged 23 years, 8 months, and 19 days.
EDCS-05600864.summary: A funerary dedication to Helena by her parents and husband.

EDCS-05600876.translation: To Vadinensis, son of Bovecus, aged 25.
EDCS-05600876.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to a man named Vadinensis.

EDCS-05600879.translation: To the departed spirits. Aliomus the parent set up this monument to his most dutiful daughter Maisontina, aged about 19 years.
EDCS-05600879.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by a parent for his deceased daughter, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-05600882.translation: This monument is here for Ablonus, son of Taurinus, of Vadini, aged 30 years; Placidus set it up from his own means, [honoring his] great-uncle.
EDCS-05600882.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a great-nephew for his deceased great-uncle Ablonus, a native of Vadini, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-05600883.translation: Ambadus, son of Palarus, aged 20 years, set up this monument.
EDCS-05600883.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Ambaddus for himself (or a relative), dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-05600885.translation: To the departed spirits. Bodero, son of Bodives, son of Doider, aged 25 years, his mother (?) set up this monument for her son; he is here.
EDCS-05600885.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a mother for her deceased son, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-05600886.translation: Monument. Caesaronius, son of Iulibagus, aged 40 years, Butuda Ammia set up [this monument] for her father, of Vadini; he is here.
EDCS-05600886.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a daughter for her deceased father, a native of Vadini, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-05600887.translation: To the departed spirits. Cangilus set up this monument for his great-uncle Vironus, son of Segisamus, of Vadini, aged 35 years; he is here.
EDCS-05600887.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a great-nephew for his deceased great-uncle Vironus, a native of Vadini, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-05600888.translation: Monument to the departed spirits. Dovideus, son of Aravis, set up [this monument] for Negalus, son of Veronigo, aged 25 years.
EDCS-05600888.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Dovideus for his deceased relative Negalus, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-05600889.translation: Monument. Elanius Veliagus, son of Paternus, aged 30 years; Tridallus set up this monument for his friend, at his own expense.
EDCS-05600889.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Tridallus for his deceased friend Elanius Veliagus, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-05600890.translation: Manilius, son of Arava, son of Elanus, of Vadini, aged 30 years; Cadus set up [this monument] for his great-uncle; he is here.
EDCS-05600890.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a great-nephew for his deceased great-uncle Manilius, a native of Vadini, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-05600891.translation: Monument. Munigalius, son of Abanus Bouti, of Vadini, aged 25 years; the heir (?) set up [this monument]; he is here.
EDCS-05600891.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by the heir for the deceased Munigalius, a native of Vadini, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-05600892.translation: A monument for Necon, son of Boddegus, from the Vadinian people, aged 21. Aurelius Pro(?) erected this monument for his friend.
EDCS-05600892.summary: A funerary monument erected by Aurelius Pro(?) for his friend Necon, a young man of Vadinian origin, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600894.translation: Vironus, son of Doider, from Vadini, aged 40, lies here. Placidus erected this for his uncle.
EDCS-05600894.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Vironus, son of Doider, from Vadini, aged 40, erected by his nephew Placidus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600895.translation: Turantus, son of Nautius(?), aged [...]. Sempronius(?) erected this for his brother.
EDCS-05600895.summary: A funerary inscription recording the erection of a monument for Turantus, son of Nautius(?), by his brother Sempronius(?), with missing age and a lacuna.

EDCS-05600898.translation: Cillius, son of Vironigus, son of Arenus, aged [...
EDCS-05600898.summary: A fragment of a funerary inscription recording the name and parentage of Cillius, with a lacuna for his age and the rest of the text.

EDCS-05600902.translation: To the shades of the underworld. A monument. Aelius(?) erected this for his brother Septimius Silo, from Vadini, aged 35. May the earth lie lightly upon you. To Lucius Septimius Silo, the hunter.
EDCS-05600902.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Septimius Silo, from Vadini, by his brother, a hunter, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600903.translation: A monument set up to the divine shades. For Scorcia, daughter of Onnacaus, of the gens Peniorum, aged 15. Her father erected this for his daughter. In the consulship of our lords Postumus and Victorinus (AD 259).
EDCS-05600903.summary: A funerary monument erected by a father for his 15-year-old daughter Scorcia, of the gens Peniorum, dated by the consulship of Postumus and Victorinus to AD 259.

EDCS-05600905.translation: To the shades of the underworld. A monument erected for Dova(?), his dearest daughter, Flavia, aged 20. Flavia, may you be victorious(?). In the consulship 422 (equivalent to AD 240 or 253).
EDCS-05600905.summary: A funerary inscription for the 20-year-old Flavia, erected by her father Dova(?), with a possible acclamation and dating by consulship to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-05600909.translation: To the shades of the underworld. A monument erected by Terentius for his daughter Cantia(?), aged 15. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600909.summary: A funerary inscription erected by Terentius for his 15-year-old daughter Cantia(?), with a standard epitaph, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600913.translation: Lucius Valerius Postumus of Uxama, aged 50, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600913.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Valerius Postumus of Uxama, aged 50, with a common epitaph, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600919.translation: To the shades of the underworld. A monument erected by Anna D(?) for her husband Domitius Flavo, aged 50. In the consulship 316 (equivalent to AD 266). May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600919.summary: A funerary inscription for Domitius Flavo, aged 50, erected by his wife Anna D(?), dated by consulship to AD 266.

EDCS-05600929.translation: To Lucius Attius Proculus of Caesaraugusta, aged 40. Attia Saturnina [dedicated this].
EDCS-05600929.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Attius Proculus, likely dedicated by Attia Saturnina, and dates to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600934.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Cassius Reburrus, son, had this made for his father, Claudius Reburrus.
EDCS-05600934.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a son for his deceased father, Claudius Reburrus, during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600937.translation: To Marcus Iurius Cantaber, for himself.
EDCS-05600937.summary: This is a funerary inscription mentioning Marcus Iurius Cantaber, likely a self-funded burial, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600938.translation: To the departed spirits. For Lucius Aemilius Liberalis, aged 70. A[---] [dedicated this].
EDCS-05600938.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Aemilius Liberalis, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600940.translation: Ambatus, [dedicated this] for his son, aged [---] years. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600940.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an unnamed son, set up by his father Ambatus, and includes a common epitaph formula, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600948.text_edition: \n Eponae sacrum Secundus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-05600948.translation: Secundus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the sacred Epona.
EDCS-05600948.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Secundus fulfilling a vow to the goddess Epona, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600949.translation: Valerius Bedaciqum Candidus lies here. Valeria Venniqum Successa, wife of Candidus, had this made for herself and her husband while alive. Titus Licinius Cornutanulus, son of Titus Licinius Quirinus, lies here. Successa, his sister, had this made at her own expense.
EDCS-05600949.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription commemorating at least three individuals, Valerius Candidus, Valeria Successa, and Titus Licinius Cornutanulus, with familial relationships and self-funded burials noted, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600950.text_edition: \n Letondo Segossoqum Melmandi filius situs est hic\n
EDCS-05600950.translation: Letondus, son of Segossus Melmandus, lies here.
EDCS-05600950.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Letondus, son of Segossus Melmandus, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600951.translation: In the consulship of Gaius Laecanius Bassus and Quintus Terentius Culleo, the Clunienses from Further Spain established a guest-friendship with Gaius Terentius Bassus, son of Gaius of the Fabia tribe, Mephanae, prefect of the Augustan cavalry wing, for himself, his children, and descendants; and they established it for themselves, their children, and descendants. The envoys were Gaius Magius Silo, son of Lucius of the Galeria tribe, and Titus Aemilius Fuscus.
