EDCS-09100843.text_edition: \n Publio Valerio Dionysio VI viro Augustali cui ordo Dertosanorum ob merita eius aedilicios honores decrevit Publius Valerius Pardus libertus VI vir Augustalis patrono optimo\n
EDCS-09100843.translation: To Publius Valerius Dionysius, VI vir Augustalis, to whom the council of Dertosanorum decreed the aedilician honors for his services; erected by Publius Valerius Pardus, freedman, VI vir Augustalis, to his most excellent patron.
EDCS-09100843.summary: Publius Valerius Pardus, a freedman and Augustalis, erected this monument to his patron Publius Valerius Dionysius, honoring his achievements and aedilician office, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09100844.text_edition: \n Pomponiae Gai filiae S PRO Lucius Munnius \n
EDCS-09100844.translation: To Pomponia, daughter of Gaius, daughter of S. PRO; Lucius Munnius.
EDCS-09100844.summary: This fragmented inscription records a dedication to Pomponia, daughter of Gaius, with Lucius Munnius mentioned, the purpose and date are unclear.

EDCS-09100845.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucius Numisius Liberalis miles classis praetoriae nnatis III Marte stipendiorum XIII natione Cursicanus Marcus Didius Polio heres et commanipularis et Gellia Excitata \n
EDCS-09100845.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Numisius Liberalis, soldier of the praetorian fleet, completed 3 years of service, of Cursican nationality. Marcus Didius Polio, his comrade and heir, and Gellia Excitata [erected this].
EDCS-09100845.summary: This funerary inscription records Lucius Numisius Liberalis, a soldier in the praetorian fleet with 13 years of service and Cursican origin, honored by his comrade and heir, Marcus Didius Polio, and Gellia Excitata, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09100846.text_edition: \n Marco Sallustio Felici peregre defuncto sodales Herculanei\n
EDCS-09100846.translation: To Marcus Sallustius Felix, who died abroad, the associates of Herculaneum [erected this].
EDCS-09100846.summary: A funerary monument erected by fellow associates for Marcus Sallustius Felix, who died while away from home.

EDCS-09100847.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Auli Caecili Cubiculari peregre defuncti Porcia Euphrosyne marito optimo posuit\n
EDCS-09100847.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Porcia Euphrosyne placed this for her most excellent husband, Aulus Caecilius Cubicularius, who died abroad.
EDCS-09100847.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Porcia Euphrosyne for her deceased husband, Aulus Caecilius Cubicularius, who died abroad.

EDCS-09100848.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quinto Iulio Flaviano patrono et marito pientissimo et Quinto Iulio Gaetulico patri Flaviani Iulia Marcia impensa sua fecit\n
EDCS-09100848.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Marcia, at her own expense, made this for Quintus Iulius Flavianus, most dutiful patron and husband, and for Quintus Iulius Gaetulicus, Flavianus' father.
EDCS-09100848.summary: A funerary monument erected by Iulia Marcia for her husband Quintus Iulius Flavianus and his father Quintus Iulius Gaetulicus.

EDCS-09100849.text_edition: \n Lucio Sempronio Luci filio Quaesito Lucius Sempronius Hiberus et Caecilia Quinti filia Quieta \n
EDCS-09100849.translation: To Lucius Sempronius Quaestus, son of Lucius. Lucius Sempronius Hiberus and Caecilia Quieta, daughter of Quintus, [erected this].
EDCS-09100849.summary: A monument set up by Lucius Sempronius Hiberus and Caecilia Quieta for Lucius Sempronius Quaestus, son of Lucius.

EDCS-09100851.text_edition: \n io Marci filio Serano annorum XIIII us Paludinus meria Cai filia Serena mater\n
EDCS-09100851.translation: To Marcius Seranus, son of Marcius, 14 years old. His mother, Paludinus Maria, daughter of Caius, [set this up].
EDCS-09100851.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcius Seranus, aged 14, set up by his mother.

EDCS-09100856.text_edition: \n Caius Porcius Seranus Edebensis nnorum XXXVI hic situs est\n
EDCS-09100856.translation: Gaius Porcius Seranus of Edeba, 36 years old, lies here.
EDCS-09100856.summary: A tombstone marking the burial place of Gaius Porcius Seranus of Edeba, who died at the age of 36.

EDCS-09100857.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quinto Lo Syntucheti et Furio Menomacho Lucio Veranio Furia Nectaris Cn mater IEO--YS MSDMA-- ET\n
EDCS-09100857.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. [Erected by] Lucius Veranius [and] Furia Nectaris, mother of Quintus Lo Syntucheti and Furio Menomachus... [text is damaged].
EDCS-09100857.summary: A damaged funerary inscription to the Spirits of the Departed, mentioning several individuals including Quintus Lo Syntucheti and Furio Menomachus.

EDCS-09100861.text_edition: \n Caius Aebutius Tardus Caius Aebutius Verecundus filius miles legionis VI in Suria Iamque XVIII aetas mea nunc conpleverat annos ruperunt misero fata repente viam lectus miles eras legio quae sexta tenebat qui pius in Suria fortiter arma tulit et fortuna nimis saevas exercuit eiras sic quam sic ut torrens ante diem rapuit nemo ne votisque meis adeo usque parentes militiae referat splendida facta suae o miseros quei sic me talem genuere parentes non licuit patrios continuisse sinus te miseram mater quae sic deserta quereris nam patris interitu lapsa ruina iacet nec te praetereor sic cor et solacia nostri indignor miseras non licuisse frui dulces anplexus morientis et oscula data nec tenuit moriens deficiente manu in supero it si qua viast aditusque sepulcro instituto casus pareat usque meus inritus infelix peregrinas possidet auras neu quisquam pressit lumina nemo manus hospes praeteriens titulum ne desere nostrum et precor ut deicas sit tibi terra levis Valeria Laura dilargitur\n
EDCS-09100861.translation: Gaius Aebutius Tardus, son of Gaius Aebutius Verecundus, a soldier of the Sixth Legion in Syria. My eighteenth year I had now completed when cruel fate suddenly broke my path; I was a soldier in the Sixth Legion which bravely bore arms in Syria, and fortune exercised too harsh a wrath. Thus it happened that the torrent snatched me away before my day. Did no one heed my wishes, or bring back my glorious deeds of military service to my parents? Alas, wretched parents who bore me such! I was not permitted to remain in my father's embrace, nor you, wretched mother, who weep so abandoned, for she lies ruined by the death of her father. Nor do I pass you by, my dear one, sharing our solace. I am indignant that we were not allowed to enjoy the sweet embrace and kisses of a dying man. Holding me with a weakening hand, he gave kisses. If there is a path and access to the tomb, may my fate submit to the established order. Unhappy, I possess foreign airs, and no stranger pressed my eyes or hands. Passing stranger, do not abandon our inscription, and I beg you, say 'May the earth lie lightly upon you.' Valeria Laura bestows.
EDCS-09100861.summary: A lengthy epitaph, partly in verse, from a soldier of the Sixth Legion in Syria named Gaius Aebutius Tardus, lamenting his premature death and dedicated by Valeria Laura.

EDCS-09200319.translation: To Hercules, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-09200319.summary: A dedication to the god Hercules, made in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-09200321.text_edition: \n Iuniae Luci filiae Antiquae Publius Valerius Antiquos sic matri piissumae sic annorum XXXXV\n
EDCS-09200321.translation: To Iunia Antiqua, daughter of Lucius. Publius Valerius Antiquos [set this up] for his most dutiful mother, 45 years old.
EDCS-09200321.summary: A funerary monument erected by Publius Valerius Antiquos for his 45-year-old mother, Iunia Antiqua.

EDCS-09300278.text_edition: \n Vurovio Lucius Cassius Flaccus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09300278.translation: Lucius Cassius Flaccus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09300278.summary: A dedication recording the fulfillment of a vow by Lucius Cassius Flaccus, likely to a local deity.

EDCS-09300279.text_edition: \n Vurovio Turaius Eburenius Calaeti filius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09300279.translation: Turaius Eburenius, son of Calaetus, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09300279.summary: A dedication recording the fulfillment of a vow by Turaius Eburenius, son of Calaetus, likely to a local deity.

EDCS-09300282.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed: Accannius.
EDCS-09300282.summary: A funerary dedication to the Manes, honoring Accannius.

EDCS-09300283.translation: To Ammia, daughter of denae Ele, wife of Caerri...
EDCS-09300283.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription for Ammia, daughter of denae Ele and wife of Caerri.

EDCS-09300293.text_edition: \n Pompeiae Flavinae Flavi filiae annorum XX parentes faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-09300293.translation: Her parents arranged for this to be set up for Pompeia Flavia, daughter of Flavius, aged 20.
EDCS-09300293.summary: A funerary monument for Pompeia Flavia, erected by her parents.

EDCS-09300294.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Coeliae Maternae Quoeli sic filiae annorum XXX liberti Coelius Sextanus et Coelia Hospita et Coeli\n
EDCS-09300294.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: For Coelia Materna, daughter of Quoelius, aged 30. Her freedmen Coelius Sextanus and Coelia Hospita and Coelius [arranged for this].
EDCS-09300294.summary: A funerary inscription for Coelia Materna, set up by her freedmen, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09300295.text_edition: \n Genio loci Ati Fr votum posuit libens merito\n
EDCS-09300295.translation: Ati Fr gladly and deservedly placed this vow to the genius of the place.
EDCS-09300295.summary: A dedication to the local genius by Ati Fr, recording the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-09300298.text_edition: \n Pompeio annorum XXIIII Pompeius Caccus et Clodia Elena parentes ponendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-09300298.translation: Pompeius Caccus and Clodia Elena, parents, arranged for this to be set up for Pompeius, aged 24.
EDCS-09300298.summary: A funerary monument for Pompeius, aged 24, erected by his parents.

EDCS-09300301.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Divi filius Augustus pontifex maxsumus sic consul XI designatus XII tribunicia potestate XVIII imperator XIIII a Segisamone milia passuum II\n
EDCS-09300301.translation: Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the Divine [Augustus], chief priest, consul designate for the 11th time, consul for the 12th time, with tribunician power for the 18th time, Imperator for the 14th time, 2 miles from Segisamon.
EDCS-09300301.summary: A milestone inscription from the reign of Augustus, likely dating to between 8 BCE and 5 CE, indicating distance.

EDCS-09300305.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Severi Pertinacis filio divi Marci Aureli nepoti divi Antonini Pii pronepoti divi Hadriani abnepoti divi Traiani Parthici et divi Nervae abnepoti Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio Felici Augusto Parthico maximo Brittannico maximo Germanico maximo pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XVIIII imperatori III consuli IIII patri patriae proconsuli\n
EDCS-09300305.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the Divine Severus Pertinax, grandson of the Divine Marcus Aurelius, great-grandson of the Divine Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the Divine Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the Divine Trajan Parthicus and the Divine Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest conqueror of Parthia, greatest conqueror of Britain, greatest conqueror of Germany, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 19th time, Imperator for the 3rd time, Consul for the 4th time, Father of the Fatherland, Proconsul.
EDCS-09300305.summary: A lengthy imperial titulature inscription for Caracalla, dating to his reign (c. 212-213 CE) and listing his numerous titles and honors.

EDCS-09300306.text_edition: \n Cornelia mulieris liberta Bucca hic sita est nnorum XXVII Memmius Clodus contubernalis\n
EDCS-09300306.translation: Cornelia Bucca, freedwoman of Cornelia, is buried here, aged 27. Memmius Clodus, her partner, [erected this].
EDCS-09300306.summary: A tombstone for Cornelia Bucca, a freedwoman, erected by her partner Memmius Clodus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09301030.text_edition: \n Flavo filio Flavia filio posuit sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09301030.translation: Flavia placed this for her son Flavo; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09301030.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavo, set up by his mother Flavia, with a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-09301031.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Antonio Paterno Paterni filio nnorum LIII\n
EDCS-09301031.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Antonio Paterno, son of Paterni, aged 53.
EDCS-09301031.summary: A tombstone for Antonio Paterno, son of Paterni, aged 53, dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-09301032.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aemilio Clementino Publi filio Publius Aemilius Severini posuit annorum XXXV\n
EDCS-09301032.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Publius Aemilius Severini set up [this] for Aemilius Clementinus, son of Publius, aged 35.
EDCS-09301032.summary: A funerary inscription for Aemilius Clementinus, son of Publius, aged 35, erected by Publius Aemilius Severini.

EDCS-09301033.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valerius Ursus pater Valerio Fortunatiano annorum XIII posuit\n
EDCS-09301033.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Valerius Ursus set up [this] for Valerius Fortunatiano, aged 13.
EDCS-09301033.summary: A tombstone for Valerius Fortunatiano, aged 13, erected by his father Valerius Ursus.

EDCS-09301034.text_edition: \n Caius Terentius Maternus Eturissensis annorum XVII hoc loco sepultus est\n
EDCS-09301034.translation: Gaius Terentius Maternus from Eturissis, aged 17, is buried in this place.
EDCS-09301034.summary: An epitaph for Gaius Terentius Maternus, a youth aged 17 from Eturissis, recording his burial.

EDCS-09301038.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Chresime Sempronius amthius uxori pientissimae faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09301038.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sempronius Amthius took care to have [this monument] made for his most devoted wife Chresime.
EDCS-09301038.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Chresime, a devoted wife, by her husband Sempronius Amthius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09301039.text_edition: \n Bucco Eusadansis filius Arsitanus hic situs est\n
EDCS-09301039.translation: Bucco, son of Eusadansis, of Arsita, is buried here.
EDCS-09301039.summary: An epitaph for Bucco, son of Eusadansis, from Arsita, marking his burial place.

EDCS-09301041.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Flavio Claudi<o=S> Constantinou sic Iuniori nubelesmo sic Caesari bono rei publicae nato \n
EDCS-09301041.translation: To our lord Flavius Claudius Constantinus, the noble and good Caesar, born for the state.
EDCS-09301041.summary: A formal dedication to Emperor Constantius II (Flavius Claudius Constantinus Iunior), praising him as a noble Caesar born for the state.

EDCS-09301042.text_edition: \n Flava Flavi filia nnorum XX hic sita est\n
EDCS-09301042.translation: Flava, daughter of Flavi, aged 20, is buried here.
EDCS-09301042.summary: A tombstone for Flava, daughter of Flavi, aged 20, recording her burial.

EDCS-09301043.summary: This is a tombstone for Proculus, who died at the age of 13.

EDCS-09301044.summary: This is a funerary inscription stating that Altus Dusanharis is buried here.

EDCS-09301046.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caeciliae Themidi filiae et Iunius Aeucarpus sic maritus et Valerius Peculiaris gener et Titulla filia matri piae fecerunt\n
EDCS-09301046.translation: To the Divine Shades: Caecilia Themis, daughter, and Iunius Aeucarpus, her husband, and Valerius Peculiaris, her son-in-law, and Titulla, her daughter, made this for their pious mother.
EDCS-09301046.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication of a monument by a family to Caecilia Themis.

EDCS-09301047.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valerius Mar Corneliae uxsori sic titulum posuit\n
EDCS-09301047.translation: To the Divine Shades: Valerius Mar erected this monument for Cornelia, his wife.
EDCS-09301047.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Valerius Mar erected a monument for his deceased wife, Cornelia.

EDCS-09301051.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Liciniae Licini filiae annorum XX\n
EDCS-09301051.translation: To the Divine Shades, sacred to Licinia, daughter of Licinius, aged 20.
EDCS-09301051.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication of a sacred space to Licinia, daughter of Licinius, who died at age 20.

EDCS-09301057.text_edition: \n Claudia Anna pro salute Claudi Licerici viri sui Nymphis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09301057.translation: Claudia Anna, for the safety of her husband Claudius Licericus, freely and deservedly paid her vow to the Nymphs.
EDCS-09301057.summary: This is a votive inscription by Claudia Anna to the Nymphs for the well-being of her husband Claudius Licericus.

EDCS-09400460.text_edition: \n Fortunae Licinius Capito pecunia sua solvit\n
EDCS-09400460.translation: Licinius Capito paid this to Fortuna with his own money.
EDCS-09400460.summary: This is a votive inscription by Licinius Capito to the goddess Fortuna.

EDCS-09400461.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus monumentum Terentius Imetus posuit Annae filiae nnorum III\n
EDCS-09400461.translation: To the Divine Shades: Terentius Imetus erected this monument to his daughter Anna, aged 3.
EDCS-09400461.summary: This funerary inscription records the erection of a monument by Terentius Imetus for his deceased daughter Anna.

EDCS-09400463.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cloutina, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-09401098.text_edition: \n Imperatore Tito Caesare divi Vespasiani filio Vespasiano Augusto pontifice maximo tribunicia potestate VIIII imperatore XV patre patriae consule VIII Caesare divi Vespasiani filio Domitiano consule VII via nova facta Caio Calpurnio Rantio Valerio Festo legato Augusti pro praetore ab Asturica milia passuum XCIV\n
EDCS-09401098.translation: Under Emperor Titus Caesar Vespasianus Augustus, son of the deified Vespasian, chief priest, in his ninth tribunician power, proclaimed emperor for the 15th time, father of the country, in his eighth consulship; and Caesar Domitian, son of the deified Vespasian, in his seventh consulship; the new road was made by Gaius Calpurnius Piso Crassus Frugi Licinianus, imperial legate with praetorian authority, 94 miles from Asturica.
EDCS-09401098.summary: This is a milestone inscription recording the construction of a road under the emperors Vespasian and Domitian, and its distance from Asturica, with details of the governor.

EDCS-09401110.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Traiani Parthici filius divi Nervae nepos Traianus Hadrianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XVII consul III pater patriae ab Andelone milia passuum III\n
EDCS-09401110.translation: Emperor Caesar Hadrian Augustus, son of the deified Trajan the Parthian, grandson of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 17th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, from Andelone, 3 miles.
EDCS-09401110.summary: An imperial dedication by Emperor Hadrian recording a road construction project, likely from the mid-2nd century CE.

EDCS-09401444.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, from the townspeople.
EDCS-09401444.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a municipal community, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700174.text_edition: \n Caio Iulio Serio Augurino Caio Trebio Sergiano consulibus Coelerni ex Hispania citeriore conventus Bracari cum Caio Antonio Aquilo Novaugustano praefecto cohortis I Celtiberorum liberis posterisque eius hospitium fecerunt Caius Antonius Aquilus cum Coelernis liberis posterisque eorum hospitium fecit legatus egit Publius Campanius Geminus\n
EDCS-09700174.translation: During the consulship of Gaius Julius Serius Augurinus and Gaius Trebius Sergianus, the Coelerni from Hispania Citerior, of the conventus Bracari, established a client-patron relationship with Gaius Antonius Aquilus, prefect of the 1st Cohort of Celtiberi, and his children and descendants; Gaius Antonius Aquilus established a client-patron relationship with the Coelerni and their children and descendants. Publius Campanius Geminus acted as legate.
EDCS-09700174.summary: A record of a formal client-patron relationship (hospitium) established between a Roman military officer and a tribal community from Hispania, dating to the early to mid-Imperial period.

EDCS-09700620.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Caius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus Germanicus maximus Dacicus maximus Sarmaticus maximus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate V imperator VII pater patriae consul proconsul et Caius Iulius Verus Maxiimus sic nobilissimus Caesar Germanicus maximus Dacicus maximus Sarmaticus maximus princeps iuventutis filius domini nostri Imperatoris Caii Iulii Veri Maximini Pii Felicis Augusti vias et pontes tempore conlapsos restituerunt agente Quinto Decio legato Augustorum pro praetore a Cara milia passuum \n
EDCS-09700620.translation: Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, Germanicus Maximus, Dacicus Maximus, Sarmaticus Maximus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 5th time, imperator for the 7th time, father of the fatherland, consul, proconsul, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus, most noble Caesar, Germanicus Maximus, Dacicus Maximus, Sarmaticus Maximus, prince of youth, son of our lord Emperor Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, restored roads and bridges that had collapsed at the time. Quintus Decius acted as legate of the Augusti pro praetore, from Cara, ... miles.
EDCS-09700620.summary: An imperial dedication by Emperors Maximinus Thrax and his son Caesar Maximinus recording the restoration of roads and bridges, dating to Maximinus Thrax's reign (235-238 CE).

EDCS-09800183.text_edition: \n Publius Cosconius Publi Galeria Arsensis miles legionis X geminae centuria Etrili annorum XXXX aerorum XI hic situs est\n
EDCS-09800183.translation: Publius Cosconius of the Arsensis tribe, soldier of the 10th Gemina Legion, of Etrilius's century, 40 years old, 11 years in service, is laid here.
EDCS-09800183.summary: A tombstone inscription for a soldier of the Legio X Gemina, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10200014.text_edition: \n Sacrum Minervae Lucius Postumius Postumiae Luci filiae Blandinae libertus Zosimus ob honorem seviratus dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-10200014.translation: Sacred to Minerva. Zosimus, freedman of Lucius Postumius, freedman of Lucius, son of Postumia Blandina, gave and dedicated this in honor of holding the office of sevir.
EDCS-10200014.summary: A dedication to Minerva by a freedman who had served as a sevir, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10200015.text_edition: \n Deae Nemesi Decimus Fonteius Apolaustus cum suis votum libens animo solvit\n
EDCS-10200015.translation: Decimus Fonteius Apolaustus, with his family, willingly paid his vow to the goddess Nemesis.
EDCS-10200015.summary: A votive offering to the goddess Nemesis, made by Decimus Fonteius Apolaustus and his household, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10200020.text_edition: \n Marco Iunio Cai filio Galeria Paterno IIviro flamini Romae et Augusti statuam decretam ab decurionibus Caesariorium Iuvenalium Castulonensium Cornelia Publi filia Severa uxor honore usa inpensa sua posuit\n
EDCS-10200020.translation: Cornelia Severa, wife of Publius, daughter of Publius, dedicated this statue, decreed by the town council of the Caesarian youth of Castulo, to Marcus Junius Paterno, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, who was IIvir, flamen of Rome and Augustus, using her own funds.
EDCS-10200020.summary: A funerary monument honoring Marcus Junius Paterno, a local magistrate and priest, erected by his wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period in Castulo.

EDCS-10200031.text_edition: \n Lucius Cornelius Flaccus et Valeria Corneliae Fabatillae filiae\n
EDCS-10200031.translation: Lucius Cornelius Flaccus and Valeria, daughter of Cornelius Fabatilla.
EDCS-10200031.summary: A fragmentary inscription that appears to be a funerary dedication or a record of family members, Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10200081.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Alexander, 25 years old.
EDCS-10200081.summary: A tombstone inscription for a man named Alexander, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10200082.translation: To the departed spirits. Phoebe, aged 2 years and 5 months. Actius and Hagne set up this for their daughter, who lies here.
EDCS-10200082.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl named Phoebe, dedicated by her parents Actius and Hagne.

EDCS-10200084.translation: Valeria Proba fulfilled her vow for herself, her freedmen, and their descendants.
EDCS-10200084.summary: This is a votive inscription by Valeria Proba fulfilling a vow for herself and her dependents.

EDCS-10200085.translation: For Valerius, aged 75. His wife Valeria Severa and son erected this. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10200085.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valerius, erected by his wife and son.

EDCS-10200095.text_edition: \n Ataecinae Dis Manibus Caius Valerius Proculinus situs est tibi terra levis annorum VI Cai Valerii Proculi filius\n
EDCS-10200095.translation: To the departed spirits. Caius Valerius Proculinus lies here, may the earth lie lightly upon you. Aged 6 years, son of Caius Valerius Proculus.
EDCS-10200095.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young boy named Caius Valerius Proculinus, noting his age and parentage.

EDCS-10200096.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Achoristus annorum XXXII pius in hic situs <e=H>(st) sit tibi terra levis pater posuit \n
EDCS-10200096.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Achoristus, aged 32, dutifully lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His father erected this.
EDCS-10200096.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Achoristus, aged 32, erected by his father.

EDCS-10200113.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gaius Catius Gai filius Eppianus annorum L Sallia Marci filia Laeta uxor annorum XXX hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis \n
EDCS-10200113.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Catius Eppianus, son of Gaius, aged 50, and Sallia Laeta, daughter of Marcus, his wife, aged 30, lie here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10200113.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a couple, Gaius Catius Eppianus and Sallia Laeta, noting their ages and parentage.

EDCS-10200114.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Cornelius Apolla annorum LXV pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Afra uxor et Apollonius filius posuerunt\n
EDCS-10200114.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cornelius Apollonius, aged 65, dutifully lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His wife Afra and son Apollonius erected this.
EDCS-10200114.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cornelius Apollonius, aged 65, erected by his wife and son.

EDCS-10200150.translation: Aemilia Antiope fulfilled her vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-10200150.summary: This is a votive inscription by Aemilia Antiope fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-10200166.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Batalia annorum LXV hic situs hic sit tibi \n
EDCS-10200166.translation: To the departed spirits. Batalia, aged 65, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10200166.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a woman named Batalia, aged 65.

EDCS-10301561.text_edition: \n Caio Caesari Augusti filio pontifici principi iuventutis\n
EDCS-10301561.translation: To Gaius Caesar, son of Augustus, pontifex, prince of the youth.
EDCS-10301561.summary: This is a dedication or honorific inscription for Gaius Caesar, son of Augustus.

EDCS-10301563.text_edition: \n Marco Baebio Marci filio Galeria Maximo\n
EDCS-10301563.translation: To Marcus Baebius Maximus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe.
EDCS-10301563.summary: This is a dedication to Marcus Baebius Maximus, an individual identified by his father's name and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-10301564.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld for Cornelia.
EDCS-10301564.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the underworld spirits for Cornelia, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-10301566.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Grattiae Philoninae nnum LXX hic sita est\n
EDCS-10301566.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld; Grattia Philonea, aged 70, is buried here.
EDCS-10301566.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Grattia Philonea, aged 70, under the invocation of the underworld spirits.

EDCS-10301567.translation: To Iunia Leda, [from] her dearest husband.
EDCS-10301567.summary: This is a dedication from a husband to his wife, Iunia Leda, likely on a tomb.

EDCS-10301568.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Manlius Crescens nnorum XXXX hic situs est\n
EDCS-10301568.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld; Marcus Manlius Crescens, aged 40, is buried here.
EDCS-10301568.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Marcus Manlius Crescens, aged 40, dedicated to the underworld spirits.

EDCS-10301569.text_edition: \n Lucius Marius Luci filius Galeria Severus Pompeia Gnaei liberta Faustina Lucius Marius Luci filius Severus annorum XVI\n
EDCS-10301569.translation: Lucius Marius Severus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe; Faustina, freedwoman of Pompeius Gnaeus; Lucius Marius Severus, son of Lucius, aged 16.
EDCS-10301569.summary: This complex inscription likely records two individuals: Lucius Marius Severus, an adult from the Galeria tribe, and Lucius Marius Severus, a 16-year-old, possibly the son of Faustina.

EDCS-10301570.text_edition: \n matri dulcissimae Valerius Paternus filius beneficiarius consularis\n
EDCS-10301570.translation: To his dearest mother, Valerius Paternus, son and beneficiary of a consular.
EDCS-10301570.summary: This is a dedication from Valerius Paternus to his mother, identifying himself as a 'beneficiarius consularis', suggesting a military or administrative connection.

EDCS-10301576.text_edition: \n Hilarus annorum LX hic situs est \n
EDCS-10301576.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription recording the burial of Hilarus, who was 60 years old.

EDCS-10301582.text_edition: \n Gaius Cornelius Gai filius Galeria Murranus hic situs est\n
EDCS-10301582.translation: Gaius Cornelius Murranus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, is buried here.
EDCS-10301582.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Gaius Cornelius Murranus, identified by his father's name and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-10301583.summary: This is a dedication to Clodia Perpetua, likely on a tomb or monument.

EDCS-10301585.text_edition: \n Aemilia Publi filia hic sita est\n
EDCS-10301585.translation: Aemilia, daughter of Publius, is laid here.
EDCS-10301585.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aemilia, daughter of Publius, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10301586.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ursa annorum XVIIII hic sita est Aeras mater fecit\n
EDCS-10301586.translation: To the Divine Shades. Ursa, aged 19, is laid here. Her mother, Aeras, made this.
EDCS-10301586.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ursa, aged 19, erected by her mother Aeras, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10301587.text_edition: \n Luci Fulvi Silvani Luci fili annorum Fonteia Marci filia T Severa\n
EDCS-10301587.translation: Lucius Fulvius Silvanus, son of Lucius; Fonteia, daughter of Marcus Severus, T. [daughter?]...
EDCS-10301587.summary: This inscription appears to be an incomplete funerary epitaph or a list of names, possibly for Lucius Fulvius Silvanus and Fonteia T. Severa, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10301588.text_edition: \n Herculi sacrum in honorem Publi Ma Herennia Luci filia Avita fecit\n
EDCS-10301588.translation: Sacred to Hercules, in honor of Publius Ma[---]. Herennia Avita, daughter of Lucius, made this.
EDCS-10301588.summary: This is a dedication to the god Hercules, made by Herennia Avita in honor of Publius Ma[---], dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10301589.text_edition: \n Leonas Publi Corneli Iuniani servus an(norum)? ---? h(ic)? s(itus)? e(st)\n
EDCS-10301589.translation: Leonas, slave of Publius Cornelius Iunianus, aged [---], is laid here.
EDCS-10301589.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Leonas, a slave of Publius Cornelius Iunianus, with an illegible age, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10301590.text_edition: \n Natalis Publi Corneli Iuniani servus annorum LX hic situs est\n
EDCS-10301590.translation: Natalis, slave of Publius Cornelius Iunianus, aged 60, is laid here.
EDCS-10301590.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Natalis, a slave of Publius Cornelius Iunianus, aged 60, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10301591.summary: This is a dedication or commission by Lucius Lucilius, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10301600.translation: Marcella, daughter of Mari Caledus, is laid here. Maria [her mother?] Stenna [niece?].
EDCS-10301600.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcella, daughter of Mari Caledus, possibly mentioning other family members, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10301603.translation: Valeria Severa, daughter of Marcus, wife of Sempronius Capito, is laid here.
EDCS-10301603.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Severa, daughter of Marcus and wife of Sempronius Capito, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10301604.translation: [---] Proculus [---]elguanus, [---] Proculus the son [---]dirtanus, are laid here.
EDCS-10301604.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph for multiple individuals named Proculus and others, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10301606.translation: Aquilus and Nigrinus gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vow to Hercules.
EDCS-10301606.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow to Hercules by Aquilus and Nigrinus.

EDCS-10301607.translation: Sergia Severa, 40 years of age, is buried here.
EDCS-10301607.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Sergia Severa, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-10301608.translation: Lucius Sempronius Propinquos, son of Lucius, aged 17.
EDCS-10301608.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Lucius Sempronius Propinquos at the age of 17.

EDCS-10301609.translation: Sulpicia, daughter of Sextus, from Edeba, aged 23, is buried here.
EDCS-10301609.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Sulpicia, from Edeba, who died at the age of 23.

EDCS-10301634.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Caius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus Germanicus maximus Dacicus maximus Sarmaticus maximus pontifex maximus tribunicie potestatis V imperator VII pater patriae consul proconsul et Caius Iulius Verus Maximus Nobilissimus Caesar Germanicus maximus Dacicus maximus Sarmaticus maximus princeps iuventutis filius domini nostri Imperatoris Caii Iuli Veri Maximini Pii Felicis Augusti\n
EDCS-10301634.translation: Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest conqueror of Germans, greatest conqueror of Dacians, greatest conqueror of Sarmatians, chief priest, holder of tribunician power for the fifth time, imperator for the seventh time, father of the country, consul, proconsul, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus Nobilissimus Caesar, greatest conqueror of Germans, greatest conqueror of Dacians, greatest conqueror of Sarmatians, prince of the youth, son of our lord Emperor Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-10301634.summary: This is an imperial titulature inscription, likely from the reign of Maximinus Thrax and his son, listing their full imperial titles and honors, dating to the 310s AD.

EDCS-10301638.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Probo Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae consuli proconsuli\n
EDCS-10301638.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Probus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, father of the country, consul, proconsul.
EDCS-10301638.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription honoring Emperor Probus with his imperial titles, likely from the late 3rd century AD.

EDCS-10301639.translation: To the most noble Caesar Marcus Numerius Numerianus.
EDCS-10301639.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription honoring Caesar Numerius Numerianus, likely from the late 3rd century AD.

EDCS-10301640.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Karino Pio Felici Augusto filio domini nostri Kari Augusti\n
EDCS-10301640.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carinus Pius Felix Augustus, son of our lord Emperor Carus Augustus.
EDCS-10301640.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription honoring Emperor Carinus with his imperial titles, dating to the late 3rd century AD.

EDCS-10301643.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Imperatori Caesari Caio Valerio Liciniano Licinio Pio Felici Invicto semper Augusto\n
EDCS-10301643.translation: To our lord Emperor Caesar Gaius Valerius Licinianus Licinius Pius Felix Invictus Always Augustus.
EDCS-10301643.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription honoring Emperor Licinius with his imperial titles, dating to the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-10301644.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Sempronius Geminus libens posuit\n
EDCS-10301644.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Lucius Sempronius Geminus gladly erected this.
EDCS-10301644.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Lucius Sempronius Geminus dedicating a monument to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-10301649.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Cnaeus Flavius votum libens perecit\n
EDCS-10301649.translation: Gnaeus Flavius willingly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-10301649.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter, likely from the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-10301654.translation: Velon Satta; Calpurnia Paterna, daughter of Severus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-10301654.summary: This is a votive inscription with a likely local dedication, indicating a woman named Calpurnia Paterna fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-10301658.translation: Valerius, son of Porconis, and Primigenia Satta willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vows.
EDCS-10301658.summary: This votive inscription records two individuals, Valerius and Primigenia Satta, fulfilling their vows.

EDCS-10301665.translation: To the Divine Shades. Cornelia Flavina made this for her husband Valerius Ursinus.
EDCS-10301665.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a wife for her deceased husband, Valerius Ursinus.

EDCS-10301680.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph for Caecilia Geseladion.

EDCS-10301683.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph for Marcus Antonius Felix.

EDCS-10301684.translation: To the Divine Shades. Severina erected this monument for her most devoted husband, Valerius E().
EDCS-10301684.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a wife for her devoted husband, Valerius E(---).

EDCS-10400174.text_edition: \n Nymphis Lucius Pompeius Paulinianus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10400174.translation: Lucius Pompeius Paulinianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Nymphs.
EDCS-10400174.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Nymphs by Lucius Pompeius Paulinianus.

EDCS-10400177.summary: This is a dedication to Ilurberrixo, son of Anderexo, likely on a public monument or funerary context.

EDCS-10900743.text_edition: \n Marcus Iulius Acceptus filiae sua<e=I> Iuliae Marci filia<e=I> Quietae annorum tres IINO sita hic est \n
EDCS-10900743.translation: Marcus Iulius Acceptus placed this monument for his daughter Iulia Quieta, daughter of Marcus, aged three.
EDCS-10900743.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of a young girl, Iulia Quieta, and was set up by her father.

EDCS-10900744.text_edition: \n Hic positus est in pace Iulianus servus Christi qui vixit annos tres menses duos dies XVII\n
EDCS-10900744.translation: Here lies in peace Julianus, servant of Christ, who lived three years, two months, and seventeen days.
EDCS-10900744.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the burial of Julianus, a servant of Christ, along with his lifespan.

EDCS-10900745.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely the beginning of a funerary epitaph or a dedication, identifying an individual named Mani.

EDCS-10900751.text_edition: \n ius Paetinianus hic situs est eia Paetiniana filia fecit Dis Manibus Iasonti sic fici sic Valeriana colliberto bene merenti\n
EDCS-10900751.translation: Ius Paetinianus is buried here; his daughter, Paetinianus, made this for her well-deserving co-libertus Iason. So it is, so it was for Valeriana, his co-libertus.
EDCS-10900751.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Ius Paetinianus, for whom his daughter had a monument made, and also mentions Valeriana.

EDCS-10900756.text_edition: \n Calpurnius Navus votum fecit votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10900756.translation: Calpurnius Navus made a vow and willingly paid it, as deserved.
EDCS-10900756.summary: This inscription records Calpurnius Navus fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-10900757.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucius Atilius Atilianus Porciae Catullae uxori obsequentissimae faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-10900757.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Atilius Atilianus arranged for this to be made for Porcia Catulla, his most obedient wife.
EDCS-10900757.summary: This is a funerary epitaph commissioned by Lucius Atilius Atilianus for his wife Porcia Catulla.

EDCS-10900760.translation: Sempronius Hermippus made this for Diana.
EDCS-10900760.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Diana made by Sempronius Hermippus.

EDCS-10900764.text_edition: \n Lucius Fius Armiger libertus et heres\n
EDCS-10900764.translation: Lucius Fius Armiger, freedman and heir.
EDCS-10900764.summary: This inscription identifies Lucius Fius Armiger as a freedman and heir, likely associated with a tomb or property.

EDCS-10900765.text_edition: \n Lucius Porcius Parthenius avus faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-10900765.translation: Lucius Porcius Parthenius, the grandfather, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-10900765.summary: This funerary epitaph indicates that Lucius Porcius Parthenius, the grandfather, commissioned the monument.

EDCS-10900769.text_edition: \n Deo Herotorago Marcia Pietas de animo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-10900769.translation: Marcia Pietas arranged for this to be made for the god Herotoragus from her own will.
EDCS-10900769.summary: This is a dedication to the god Herotoragus, commissioned willingly by Marcia Pietas.

EDCS-10900770.text_edition: \n B Deo votum solverunt libentes merito Chares et Irinaeus sic Iulius II \n
EDCS-10900770.translation: To the god B. Chares and Irinaeos willingly paid their vow, as deserved. Thus Julius II.
EDCS-10900770.summary: This inscription records Chares and Irineaus fulfilling a vow to the god B., with Julius II also mentioned.

EDCS-10900775.translation: To Domitius Macr, son of Macr.
EDCS-10900775.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or epitaph, possibly for a freedman named Domitius Macr, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10900778.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Clodio Urso liberto bene merenti Marcus Clodius Hyginus et Clodia Gratilla et sibi et suis fecerunt\n
EDCS-10900778.translation: To the Divine Shades. Marcus Clodius Hyginus and Clodia Gratilla, for their well-deserving freedman Urso and for themselves and their kin, made this.
EDCS-10900778.summary: This funerary epitaph records a dedication made by Marcus Clodius Hyginus and Clodia Gratilla to their freedman Urso, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10900784.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Publius Obstorius Publi filius? vixit nnos VI menses dies Obstoria filio piissimo vixit nnos XXVIII post mortem fili sui diebus VI\n
EDCS-10900784.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Publius Obstorius lived 6 years, X months, X days. Obstoria, for her most devoted son, lived 28 years. After the death of her son, for X days.
EDCS-10900784.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages at death of Obstoria and her son Publius Obstorius, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10900787.translation: To Emperor Augustus, by his will.
EDCS-10900787.summary: This brief inscription indicates a posthumous dedication or memorial made in accordance with a will to Emperor Augustus, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-10900800.text_edition: \n Caius SIII A Porciae ae Porciae |(mulieris) libertae Quietae\n
EDCS-10900800.translation: To Caius, son of SIII A. To Porcia, freedwoman of Porcia.
EDCS-10900800.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or epitaph, possibly mentioning two individuals, one named Caius and a freedwoman named Quieta, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10900803.text_edition: \n Kauti deo Lucius Petreius Victor aliarius deo Kauti Mithrae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10900803.translation: To the god Kautis. Lucius Petreius Victor, a dealer in animal hides, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the god Kautis Mithras.
EDCS-10900803.summary: This votive inscription records Lucius Petreius Victor fulfilling a vow to the god Kautis Mithras, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10900806.text_edition: \n Marcus M filius Celer IIvir I stipe\n
EDCS-10900806.translation: Marcus Celer, son of Marcus, twice appointed magistrate, from the collection.
EDCS-10900806.summary: This inscription mentions Marcus Celer, who held the office of IIvir (duumvir), likely related to civic administration or a religious collection, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10900819.text_edition: \n Publio Sicinio Publi liberto Germano pistori Caeciliae Marci libertae C Sicinia Publi filia Germana matri et patri\n
EDCS-10900819.translation: To Publius Sicinius Germanus, freedman of Publius, a baker, and to Caecilia Germanus, freedwoman of Marcus. From Sicinius, son of Publius.
EDCS-10900819.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication made by Sicinius, son of Publius, to his parents, Publius Sicinius Germanus and Caecilia Germanus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10900820.summary: This is a simple epitaph or dedication naming Statia Luculla, daughter of Lucius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10900821.summary: This fragmentary inscription is a dedication or epitaph, possibly referring to a mother and someone named Stator, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10900840.text_edition: \n Lucio Licinio Secundo accenso patrono suo Lucio Licinio Surae primo secundo tertio consulatu eius IIIIIIviro Augustali coloniae Iuliae Urbis Triumphalis Tarraconis coloniae Faventiae Iuliae Augustae Paternae Barcinonis Caius Trocina Onesimus amico\n
EDCS-10900840.translation: To Lucius Licinius Secundus, his accensus and patron, Lucius Licinius Surus, in his first, second, and third consulships, and as one of the six Augustales of the colony Iulia Urbs Triumphalis Tarraconensis, the colony Faventia Iulia Augusta Paterna Barcino, Caius Trocina Onesimus, as a friend.
EDCS-10900840.summary: This dedication honors Lucius Licinius Secundus by Caius Trocina Onesimus, listing Secundus's multiple public offices and patronage roles in several colonies, likely dating to the early imperial period.

EDCS-10900846.text_edition: \n Imperatore Nerva Caesare Traiano Augusto Germanico II Caio Pomponio Pio consulibus VI Idus Iunias Baetulonenses ex Hispania citeriore hospitium fecerunt cum Quinto Licinio Silvano Graniano eumque liberos posterosque eius sibi liberis posterisque suis patronum cooptaverunt Quintus Licinius Silvanus Granianus Baetulonenses ex Hispania citeriore ipsos liberos posterosque eorum in fidem clientelamque suam liberorum posterorumque suorum recepit egit legatus Quintus Caecilius Tertullus\n
EDCS-10900846.translation: During the consulships of Emperor Nerva Caesar Trajan Augustus Germanicus II and Gaius Pomponius Pius, on the 8th day before the Ides of June, the people of Baetulo from Hispania Citerior established a guest-friendship with Quintus Licinius Silvanus Granianus, and they co-opted him, his children, and their descendants as their patron, along with their children and descendants. Quintus Licinius Silvanus Granianus received the people of Baetulo from Hispania Citerior, and their children and descendants, into his protection and patronage, along with his children and descendants. Quintus Caecilius Tertullus acted as legatus.
EDCS-10900846.summary: This inscription records the formal establishment of guest-friendship and patronage between the people of Baetulo and Quintus Licinius Silvanus Granianus, likely dating to the late 1st century CE, during the joint consulship of Nerva and Trajan.

EDCS-10900852.translation: To Lucius Mallius Ilarus and Valeria Urbana.
EDCS-10900852.summary: This is a dedication or tombstone for Lucius Mallius Ilarus and Valeria Urbana, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10900879.text_edition: \n nobilissimo Caesari Domino Nostro Imperatori Caesari Valerio Maximiano ENTO \n
EDCS-10900879.translation: To our most noble Caesar, our Lord, Emperor Caesar Valerius Maximianus ENTO
EDCS-10900879.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Caesar Valerius Maximianus, a title used by Galerius, likely dating to the Tetrarchic period (late 3rd to early 4th century CE).

EDCS-10900880.text_edition: \n Tiberius Claudius Drusi filius Caesar Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate IIII consul III imperator VIII pater patriae \n
EDCS-10900880.translation: Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 4th time, consul for the 3rd time, imperator for the 8th time, father of the country.
EDCS-10900880.summary: This is a standard imperial titulature for Emperor Claudius, likely part of a larger inscription recording his achievements or dedications, dating to his reign (41-54 CE).

EDCS-11600326.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Euphrosyne Laleto, his faithful companion, who lived 35 years.
EDCS-11600326.summary: This is a tombstone for Euphrosyne Laleto, dedicated by her companion (contubernalis), likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11600338.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Quintus Cornelius Trophimus, his most dutiful son, aged [approx. 3] years. He is buried here.
EDCS-11600338.summary: This is a tombstone for Quintus Cornelius Trophimus, a young son, dedicated by his father, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11600339.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Graecae posuerunt Graecus filius et Ilarus conservus optimus Masclinus conservus bonus\n
EDCS-11600339.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Graeca, Graecus the son, and Ilarus the fellow slave, and Masclinus the good fellow slave, placed this.
EDCS-11600339.summary: This tombstone was erected by slaves (Graecus, Ilarus, and Masclinus) for Graeca, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11600342.text_edition: \n iussu Dominae Caelestis aram libens posuit\n
EDCS-11600342.translation: By order of Lady Caelestis, [he/she] gladly erected an altar.
EDCS-11600342.summary: This records the erection of an altar by an unnamed person, at the command of 'Lady Caelestis', likely a local deity, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11600343.text_edition: \n io Quinti filio aedili IIviro et quinqennali\n
EDCS-11600343.translation: To [name], son of Quintus, aedile and duovir and quinquennalis.
EDCS-11600343.summary: This inscription records the public offices held by an unnamed individual, son of Quintus, including aedile, duovir, and quinquennalis, likely dating to the Roman imperial period, particularly in a municipal context.

EDCS-11600348.summary: This inscription records that Publius Sil was responsible for some task, likely related to a public work or building, based on its fragmentary nature.

EDCS-11600349.text_edition: \n Sicci iugera CXXX et traiectus ex limite V |(cardine) III iugera VI semis et ex limite IIII |(cardine) III iugera VI semis homninibus X super<e=I>st iugera XI in singulos iugera XIII Caius Annius Cai filius Galeria Seneca Icosi Caius Aufustius Cai filius Galeria Icosi Caius Tettius Cai filius Scaptia Praeneste Marcus Marius Marci filius Galeria Vibone Lucius Aemilius Luci filius Horatia Ulia Publius Horatius Publi filius Quirina Malaca Caius Marius Cai filius Veturia Corduba Lucius Valerius Luci filius Falerna Aurelia Carissa Lucius Fabius Luci filius Galeria Icosi Quintus Fufius Quinti filius Maecia Ba<l=C>iaricus \n
EDCS-11600349.translation: 130 jugera and a crossing from boundary 5 on the 3rd cardo, 6 and a half jugera, and from boundary 4 on the 3rd cardo, 6 and a half jugera, and for 10 men 11 jugera, for each jugerum 13. Caius Annius Seneca, son of Caius, of the Galeria tribe, from Icosi; Caius Aufustius, son of Caius, of the Galeria tribe, from Icosi; Caius Tettius, son of Caius, of the Scaptia tribe, from Praeneste; Marcus Marius, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, from Vibone; Lucius Aemilius, son of Lucius, of the Horatia tribe, from Ulia; Publius Horatius, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, from Malaca; Caius Marius, son of Caius, of the Veturia tribe, from Corduba; Lucius Valerius, son of Lucius, of the Falerna tribe, from Aurelia Carissa; Lucius Fabius, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, from Icosi; Quintus Fufius, son of Quintus, of the Maecia tribe, from Baiaricus.
EDCS-11600349.summary: This inscription details land divisions and records the names of ten individuals, likely landholders or beneficiaries, associated with specific tribes and places, possibly related to a land distribution or survey.

EDCS-11600352.translation: Gnaeus Aufidius Lacinianus, son of Gnaeus.
EDCS-11600352.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of Gnaeus Aufidius Lacinianus.

EDCS-11600375.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucio Cornelio Luci filio Quirinali annorum \n
EDCS-11600375.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Cornelius, son of Lucius, of the Quirinal tribe, aged...
EDCS-11600375.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name, filiation, tribe, and age of Lucius Cornelius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11600376.text_edition: \n Terentiae Priscae libertae Tethini annorum XXVII Terentia Luci liberta Arbuscula mater Tethini filiae monumentum fecit\n
EDCS-11600376.translation: Terentia Prisca, freedwoman of Tethinus, aged 27. Her mother, Terentia, freedwoman of Lucius, made this monument for her daughter Tethini.
EDCS-11600376.summary: This is a funerary epitaph where a mother commissioned a monument for her deceased daughter, Terentia Prisca, a freedwoman, indicating a familial bond and social status.

EDCS-11600378.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caius Licinius Licinianus annorum XXXVIII hic situs est\n
EDCS-11600378.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caius Licinius Licinianus, aged 38, is buried here.
EDCS-11600378.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Caius Licinianus and his age.

EDCS-11600385.text_edition: \n Saturninus Luci Fundani servus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-11600385.translation: Saturninus, slave of Lucius Fundanius, willingly paid his vow, deservedly.
EDCS-11600385.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Saturninus, a slave, to his master Lucius Fundanius.

EDCS-11600387.translation: Marcus Popilius Onyx, at his own expense.
EDCS-11600387.summary: This inscription indicates that Marcus Popilius Onyx funded something at his own expense, likely a public or religious offering.

EDCS-11600388.text_edition: \n Publius Astranius Venustus IIIIIIvir Augustalis Lucentis nnum XXIII te rogo praeteriens dicas sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-11600388.translation: Publius Astranius Venustus, Augustalis and member of the IIIIvir Augustalis college, aged 23, from Lucentis. I ask you passing by, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11600388.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Astranius Venustus, an Augustalis official, including his age, origin, and a common epitaph plea.

EDCS-11600389.text_edition: \n Publius Fulvius Asclas Pompeianus nnorum XXXII hic situs est\n
EDCS-11600389.translation: Publius Fulvius Asclas Pompeianus, aged 32, is buried here.
EDCS-11600389.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Publius Fulvius Asclas Pompeianus and his age.

EDCS-11600391.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Hermeros Pyrallidi contubernali pientissimae annorum XXVII\n
EDCS-11600391.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Hermeros, most devoted companion, aged 27.
EDCS-11600391.summary: A funerary epitaph for Hermeros, a companion, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11600406.summary: A fragmentary inscription that likely records the names of two individuals, Sennia T. and Cassius S., possibly a dedication or a tombstone.

EDCS-11600411.translation: Of Saturi, of Sallustius, [and] of Atius.
EDCS-11600411.summary: A short inscription listing three personal names, possibly indicating ownership or dedication.

EDCS-11600412.text_edition: \n Aurelius puer recessit nnorum XII fidelis |(Christi) Sarra sic puella recessit nnorum fidelis |(Christi)\n
EDCS-11600412.translation: Aurelius, a faithful boy, departed at the age of 12, by the grace of Christ. Sarah, a faithful girl, likewise departed at the age of [?] by the grace of Christ.
EDCS-11600412.summary: A funerary inscription for two Christian children, Aurelius and Sarah, mentioning their faithfulness and age at death.

EDCS-11600415.summary: A highly fragmentary inscription that preserves only the end of a woman's name, Aemilia, likely from a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-11600416.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Voconia Macedonia vixit nnos XXVI menses VII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-11600416.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Voconia Macedonia lived 26 years, 7 months; she is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11600416.summary: A funerary epitaph for Voconia Macedonia, detailing her lifespan and including a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-11600418.text_edition: \n Luci Lucretio Servilii Galli Semproniani clarissimi viri Felicio (fecit)\n
EDCS-11600418.translation: Lucius Lucretius, son of Servilius Gallus Sempronianus, a most distinguished man, Felicio made [it].
EDCS-11600418.summary: An inscription recording the maker (Felicio) of a monument for Lucius Lucretius Servilius Gallus Sempronianus, a prominent figure.

EDCS-11600424.text_edition: \n us Iustus hic situs est annorum XXIIII\n
EDCS-11600424.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Iustus and his age at death.

EDCS-11600425.text_edition: \n Silvano Augusto posuit Bassus Luci V Scriboniani verna pro salutem sic Hadriani\n
EDCS-11600425.translation: Bassus, a household slave of Lucius V. Scribonianus, dedicated [this] to Silvanus Augustus for the health of Hadrian.
EDCS-11600425.summary: A dedication to the god Silvanus Augustus by a slave named Bassus, for the well-being of Emperor Hadrian.

EDCS-11600426.summary: A very brief inscription likely intended as a dedication or memorial for Quintus Aemilius.

EDCS-11600428.text_edition: \n Caius Aemilius Mnasillus hic situs est annorum XXXII hic situs est\n
EDCS-11600428.translation: Gaius Aemilius Mnasillus lies here, aged 32.
EDCS-11600428.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death of Gaius Aemilius Mnasillus, aged 32.

EDCS-11600437.text_edition: \n Quintus Cornelius Clemens hic situs est\n
EDCS-11600437.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death of Quintus Cornelius Clemens.

EDCS-11600444.translation: Saturninus willingly set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-11600444.summary: This is a votive inscription by Saturninus.

EDCS-11600450.text_edition: \n Pompeia Cai liberta Sintyche Caio Pompeio Polydeuci patrono bene merenti annorum LX\n
EDCS-11600450.translation: Sintyche, freedwoman of Gaius, to her patron Gaius Pompeius Polydeuces, deserving of much, aged 60.
EDCS-11600450.summary: This tombstone is for Sintyche, aged 60, and dedicated to her patron Gaius Pompeius Polydeuces.

EDCS-11600451.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Pompeiae Venustae annorum LXX us uxori carissimae aram posuit et Gaio Pompeio Nicostrato consobrino et Quinto Pompeio Philippo sororis viro adfinibus piissimis\n
EDCS-11600451.translation: To the Divine Manes. Pompeia Venusta, aged 70, to her dearest wife, erected this altar, and to Gaius Pompeius Nicostratus, her cousin, and to Quintus Pompeius Philippus, her sister's husband, most devoted relatives.
EDCS-11600451.summary: This is a funerary monument by Gaius Pompeius Nicostratus and Quintus Pompeius Philippus to their deceased relatives, including Pompeia Venusta.

EDCS-11600452.text_edition: \n Mystes nnum X hic situs est\n
EDCS-11600452.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death of Mystes, aged 10.

EDCS-11600454.text_edition: \n Acilia Luci liberta Modesta annorum XVIII hic sita est p p\n
EDCS-11600454.translation: Acilia Modesta, freedwoman of Lucius, aged 18, lies here. She paid for it herself.
EDCS-11600454.summary: This tombstone records the death of Acilia Modesta, aged 18, who paid for her own burial.

EDCS-11600455.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Pompeio filio Iusto \n
EDCS-11600455.translation: To the Divine Manes, sacred. To Pompeius Iustus, my son.
EDCS-11600455.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Pompeius Iustus, son of the dedicator.

EDCS-11600456.text_edition: \n Sempronia Iusta annorum XXXI merenti Marcus Baebius Potens titulum faciendum quravit de suo\n
EDCS-11600456.translation: Sempronia Iusta, aged 31, deserving. Marcus Baebius Potens arranged for this inscription to be made at his own expense.
EDCS-11600456.summary: This tombstone records Sempronia Iusta, aged 31, and notes that Marcus Baebius Potens paid for the inscription.

EDCS-11600457.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Terentius Ianuarius annorum LXXXX hic situs est Terentius Thaumastus patrono bene merenti\n
EDCS-11600457.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Terentius Ianuarius lies here, aged 90. Terentius Thaumastus [erected this] for his deserving patron.
EDCS-11600457.summary: This tombstone records Marcus Terentius Ianuarius, aged 90, and was erected by Terentius Thaumastus for his patron.

EDCS-11600459.text_edition: \n Quintus Appius Claudius annorum XXV hic situs est Asmenus contubernali\n
EDCS-11600459.translation: Quintus Appius Claudius, aged 25, is buried here, [along with] Asmenus, his comrade.
EDCS-11600459.summary: This is a tombstone for Quintus Appius Claudius, noting his age and companion, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11600460.translation: Gnaeus Numisius Numisianus, son of Gnaeus.
EDCS-11600460.summary: This inscription records the name and parentage of Gnaeus Numisius Numisianus, likely a funerary monument.

EDCS-11600461.text_edition: \n Anius Marinus hic situs est Marcus Anius des \n
EDCS-11600461.translation: Anius Marinus is buried here. Marcus Anius [set this up].
EDCS-11600461.summary: This is a tombstone for Anius Marinus, erected by Marcus Anius, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11600462.text_edition: \n Lucius Baebius Trupo hic situs est\n
EDCS-11600462.translation: Lucius Baebius Trupo is buried here.
EDCS-11600462.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Baebius Trupo, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11600464.summary: This inscription records the name and age of Baebia Fausta, likely a funerary monument from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11600465.text_edition: \n Hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Baebia Fausta ae libertae bene merenti annorum XXXX\n
EDCS-11600465.translation: Baebia Fausta, a freedwoman, is buried here; may the earth rest lightly upon you, deserving well. Aged 40.
EDCS-11600465.summary: This is a tombstone for Baebia Fausta, a freedwoman, noting her age and a wish for her repose, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11600468.text_edition: \n Caius Baebius Florus annorum L hic situs est Iulia Cai filia Placida uxor posuit\n
EDCS-11600468.translation: Caius Baebius Florus, aged 50, is buried here. Iulia Placida, daughter of Caius, his wife, set this up.
EDCS-11600468.summary: This is a tombstone for Caius Baebius Florus, erected by his wife Iulia Placida, noting his age and dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11600469.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Callityche uxor Pardo bene merenti fecit vixit annis XXXXIIII et menses V dies VIIII\n
EDCS-11600469.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Callityche, his wife, set this up for Pardo, who deserved well. She lived 42 years, 5 months, and 9 days.
EDCS-11600469.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Callityche, wife of Pardo, and the length of her life, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11700912.translation: To Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 18th year, consul for the 3rd time, father of the country, from Tu(de) [for] 17 miles.
EDCS-11700912.summary: This milestone records the road length of 17 miles from Tu(de) and attributes its maintenance to Emperor Hadrian, dating to his reign (c. 117-138 AD).

EDCS-11700913.translation: To Emperor Caesar, son of Severus Pius, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified [Nerva], Marcus Aurelius Antoninus [Pius Felix] Augustus, Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Germanicus Maximus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 17th year, imperator for the 3rd time, consul for the 4th time, father of the country, [proconsul], from Bracara [for] 66 miles.
EDCS-11700913.summary: This milestone records a road length of 66 miles from Bracara and honors Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla), dating to the 17th year of his tribunician power (c. 213 AD).

EDCS-11700915.translation: To Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, Germanicus Maximus, Dacicus Maximus, Sarmaticus Maximus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power for the 4th time, imperator for the 7th time, father of the fatherland, consul, proconsul, and to Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, Germanicus Maximus, Dacicus Maximus, Sarmaticus Maximus, prince of youth, son of our lord Emperor Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, [they] restored the roads and bridges collapsed by age, under the supervision of Quintus Decius, governor with praetorian power.
EDCS-11700915.summary: This inscription records the restoration of roads and bridges by Emperors Maximinus Thrax and his son Caesar Maximus, overseen by the legate Quintus Decius, likely in the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-11700916.translation: To Emperor Caesar Pius Felix Numerianus, Invictus, Augustus.
EDCS-11700916.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription honouring Emperor Numerian, presented as a title, likely from the late 3rd century AD.

EDCS-11700922.translation: To our lord Magnus Decentius Caesar, the victorious.
EDCS-11700922.summary: This inscription honours Magnus Decentius Caesar, likely a usurper or a junior emperor, presented with epithets of victory, probably from the 4th century AD.

EDCS-11700940.text_edition: \n L(ucio) Atilio Commodo omnibus honoribus in re publica sua functo \n
EDCS-11700940.translation: To Lucius Atilius Commodus, who held all the offices in his state.
EDCS-11700940.summary: This is an epitaph honouring Lucius Atilius Commodus for his extensive public service, dating uncertain but likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-11700942.text_edition: \n nti Cerinthi et Semproni Aviti fili us Fibularensis et Corinthia sua pecunia fecerunt\n
EDCS-11700942.translation: Cerinthus and his son, son of Sempronius Avitus, and his wife Corinthia made [this] with their own money.
EDCS-11700942.summary: This inscription records a private commission of a monument by Cerinthus, his son, and his wife Corinthia, likely a family tomb or dedication, date uncertain.

EDCS-11700943.translation: To Sempronia Tempestiva, from her mother Marcia Tempestiva.
EDCS-11700943.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a mother, Marcia Tempestiva, to her daughter, Sempronia Tempestiva, dating uncertain.

EDCS-11700945.text_edition: \n Theodorae bene merenti quae vixit cum marito annos XLIIII mensis III vidua vixit annos XXII mensis VIIII recessit in pace annorum LXVII\n
EDCS-11700945.translation: To Theodora, well-deserving, who lived with her husband for 44 years and 3 months, lived as a widow for 22 years and 9 months, passed away in peace at the age of 67.
EDCS-11700945.summary: This is a funerary epitaph detailing the life and age of Theodora, including her marital status and years of widowhood, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11700948.text_edition: \n Lucio Licinio Luci liberto Argo Licinius Felix patrono optimo\n
EDCS-11700948.translation: To Lucius Licinius Felix, freedman of Lucius Licinius, his most excellent patron.
EDCS-11700948.summary: This inscription records a freedman, Lucius Licinius Felix, honouring his patron Lucius Licinius, dating uncertain.

EDCS-11700953.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori pro salute Marci Caecili M Prepon servus vovit\n
EDCS-11700953.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Preserver, Marcus Caecilius M. Prepon, a slave, vowed for the safety [of himself or another].
EDCS-11700953.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a slave named Marcus Caecilius M. Prepon, seeking divine protection, date uncertain.

EDCS-11700954.text_edition: \n Mercurio Compitali Viali Lucius Musius posuit ex voto\n
EDCS-11700954.translation: To Mercurius Compitalis Vialis, Lucius Musius erected [this] in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-11700954.summary: This is a votive inscription to Mercurius Compitalis Vialis by Lucius Musius, indicating a private religious offering, date uncertain.

EDCS-11700957.text_edition: \n Gaio Antonio Gai filio Galeria Verecundo bis IIviro tribuno militum legionis III Augustae bis Porcia Serana mater filio piissimo\n
EDCS-11700957.translation: To Gaius Antonius Verecundus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, twice duovir, twice tribune of the soldiers of the III Augusta legion, from Porcia Serana, his mother, for her most dutiful son.
EDCS-11700957.summary: This is a dedication from a mother to her son, Gaius Antonius Verecundus, who held several public and military offices.

EDCS-11700958.text_edition: \n Caio Iulio Galeria Lepido Iessonensi primipilari |(centurioni) legionis XIII geminae piae fidelis |(centurioni) legionis I adiutricis |(centurioni) legionis X geminae piae fidelis |(centurioni) legionis XXX Ulpiae victricis |(centurioni) legionis VII geminae piae felicis adlecto in numerum decurionum ab ordine Barcinonensium heredes ex testamento cura Agathopi Trophimi Policarpi libertorum locus datus ex decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-11700958.translation: To Gaius Julius Lepidus, of the Galeria tribe, from Iesson, a primipilar centurion of the XIII Gemina legion, a centurion of the I Adiutrix legion, a centurion of the X Gemina legion, a centurion of the XXX Ulpia Victrix legion, a centurion of the VII Gemina legion, appointed to the ranks of decurions by the council of Barcino; his heirs, by his will, through the care of Agathopus, Trophimus, and Policarpius, his freedmen, a place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-11700958.summary: This is a dedication honoring Gaius Julius Lepidus, a distinguished centurion who served in multiple legions and was later honored with a public space by his heirs.

EDCS-11700959.text_edition: \n Quinto Fabio Quinti filio Galeria Materno aedili Licinia Marci filia Numantina mater\n
EDCS-11700959.translation: To Quintus Fabius Maternus, son of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe, aedile; from Licinia Numantina, daughter of Marcus, his mother.
EDCS-11700959.summary: This is a dedication from a mother to her son, Quintus Fabius Maternus, who served as an aedile.

EDCS-11700960.text_edition: \n Marco Licinio Luci filio Quirina Celtibero aedili IIviro Quintus Fabius Licinianus avo\n
EDCS-11700960.translation: To Marcus Licinius Celtiber, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, aedile, duovir; from Quintus Fabius Licinianus, his grandfather.
EDCS-11700960.summary: This is a dedication from a grandfather to his grandson, Marcus Licinius Celtiber, who held the offices of aedile and duovir.

EDCS-11700961.text_edition: \n Marco Licinio Celtibero Fabio Liciniano IIviro Licinia Marci filia Numantina mater\n
EDCS-11700961.translation: To Marcus Licinius Celtiber, duovir; from Licinia Numantina, daughter of Marcus, his mother.
EDCS-11700961.summary: This is a dedication from a mother to her son, Marcus Licinius Celtiber, who served as duovir.

EDCS-11700962.text_edition: \n Marco Porcio Marci filio Galeria Catullo IIviro bis Porcia Marci filia Catulla fratri\n
EDCS-11700962.translation: To Marcus Porcius Catullus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, twice duovir; from Porcia Catulla, daughter of Marcus, his sister.
EDCS-11700962.summary: This is a dedication from a sister to her brother, Marcus Porcius Catullus, who held the office of duovir twice.

EDCS-11700963.text_edition: \n Lucio Valerio Luci filio Galeria Faventino IIvirali qui annona frumentaria empta plebem adiuvit et ob alia merita eius collegia Kalendarium et Iduaria duo civi gratissimo posuerunt\n
EDCS-11700963.translation: To Lucius Valerius Faventinus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, who aided the people with the purchase of grain during his duovirate, and for his other merits, the collegia of the Calendar and the Ides erected this for their most grateful fellow citizen.
EDCS-11700963.summary: This dedication honors Lucius Valerius Faventinus for his public service, specifically his efforts in supplying grain, and for other contributions recognized by the collegia.

EDCS-11700964.text_edition: \n Atiliae Paternae Aesaonensis sic Luci Atili Materni filiae Lucius Licinius Oppidanus socrae obsequentissimae bene meritae quae sic egit mecum et filia sua quae vixit nnos LXXV\n
EDCS-11700964.translation: To Atilia Paternana, daughter of Lucius Atilius Maternus, from Aeson, for her most obedient mother-in-law, who acted so well towards me, and for his daughter, who lived 75 years.
EDCS-11700964.summary: This inscription is a dedication from a son-in-law to his mother-in-law, praising her character and also noting his deceased wife.

EDCS-11700965.text_edition: \n Valeriae Publi Corneli ValeNtis filiae ValentiNae Aesonens(i) A IIE I TI maritus uxori obsequentissimae\n
EDCS-11700965.translation: To Valeria Valentina, daughter of Publius Cornelius Valentis, from Aeson, wife of A., for his most obedient wife.
EDCS-11700965.summary: This is a dedication from a husband to his wife, Valeria Valentina, praising her obedience.

EDCS-11700966.translation: To Lucius Fabius Maternus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-11700966.summary: This is a simple dedication to Lucius Fabius Maternus.

EDCS-11700967.text_edition: \n Porciae Marci filiae Catullae ex voluntate Licini Silvani mariti optimi\n
EDCS-11700967.translation: To Porcia Catulla, daughter of Marcus, by the will of her most excellent husband Licinius Silvanus.
EDCS-11700967.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Porcia Catulla, likely on a tombstone, erected by her husband, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11700969.translation: Marciana, to her most excellent brother.
EDCS-11700969.summary: This inscription is a dedication, likely on a tombstone, from a sister to her brother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11700973.text_edition: \n Vivus viva Lucius Valerius Eupo et Valeria Lau Valeriae Suriscae \n
EDCS-11700973.translation: While he lived, she lived: Lucius Valerius Eupo and Valeria Surisca for their Valeria Lau.
EDCS-11700973.summary: This is a funerary epitaph indicating a joint dedication by Lucius Valerius Eupo and Valeria Surisca for Valeria Lau, likely a relative, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11700975.text_edition: \n Quinto Caecilio Luci filio Quirina Campano Caius Annius Annianus et Lucius Caecilius Maturus\n
EDCS-11700975.translation: To Quintus Caecilius Campanus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe; Caius Annius Annianus and Lucius Caecilius Maturus [dedicated this].
EDCS-11700975.summary: This inscription records a dedication, likely on a tombstone, to Quintus Caecilius Campanus by Caius Annius Annianus and Lucius Caecilius Maturus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11700981.translation: To the Divine Manes of Gaius Antonius Verecundus.
EDCS-11700981.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Gaius Antonius Verecundus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11700989.text_edition: \n Flavos sic Flavi f(ilius) Limicus S \n
EDCS-11700989.summary: This inscription appears to be a simple statement of identity, possibly on a tombstone, naming Flavus, son of Flavius Limicus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11700990.text_edition: \n Marco Caecilio Marci Caecili Arguti filio Galeria Probo Tyche liberta de suo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-11700990.translation: Probo Tyche, freedwoman of Marcus Caecilius Argutus, son of Marcus Caecilius, took care to have this made at her own expense.
EDCS-11700990.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument erected by a freedwoman, Probo Tyche, for her patron Marcus Caecilius Argutus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11700991.text_edition: \n Servilla Praepusa filiae suae Lesbiae annorum XI mensium X hic sepultae sit levis terra quid sibi fata velint bellissima quaeque creari edita laetitiae commoda si rapiuunt sed quae fatorum legi servare necesse est perverso lacrimas fundimus officio haec bis sex annos vix bene transierat ill<a=E> suas lacrimas nondum emiserat omnes et poterat semper flebilis esse suis parcite enim vobis tristes sine fine parentes parcius et manes sollicitare meos ponimus hunc titulum luctus solacia nostri qui legit ut dicat sit tibi terra levis \n
EDCS-11700991.translation: Servilla Praepusa to her daughter Lesbia, aged eleven years and ten months, buried here. 'May the earth lie lightly on you, sweetest one, for what reason have the Fates decreed that the fairest things must be created? If they snatch away life's joys, it is necessary to keep the laws of the Fates. We pour forth tears in the performance of our duty. She had barely completed twelve years, nor had she yet shed all her tears, and she could always be sorrowful for her own. Spare yourselves, sorrowful parents without end, and disturb my shades less. We place this inscription, a solace for our grief. Whoever reads, let him say: "May the earth lie lightly on you."'
EDCS-11700991.summary: This is a lengthy and poignant epitaph commemorating a young girl named Lesbia, who died at age eleven, with laments from her mother Servilla Praepusa and a plea for gentle treatment of her remains, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11700993.text_edition: \n SIL turio Min servo annorum XX\n
EDCS-11700993.translation: To Turio Min, slave, aged twenty years.
EDCS-11700993.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph for a slave named Turio Min, who died at the age of twenty, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11700997.summary: This inscription, likely a funerary marker, expresses a wish for peace for Carthage, possibly referring to a person or the city itself, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11700998.text_edition: \n Lucius Iunius Laurbeles Publius Iunius Silo Iunia Luci filia hic siti sunt\n
EDCS-11700998.translation: Lucius Iunius Laurbeles, Publius Iunius Silo, and Iunia, daughter of Lucius, are buried here.
