EDCS-16200953.summary: A funerary inscription for three individuals from the Medutici tribe, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-16201544.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Scribonius Fuscinus Suestatiensis annorum LXXV hic situs est Aemilia Materna Suestatiensis annorum XXX hic sita est Scribonia Frontina annorum V hic sita est Scribonius Fronto patri uxsori sic vivus faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-16201544.translation: To the Divine Manes. Scribonius Fuscinus of Suestatium, aged 75, is buried here. Aemilia Materna of Suestatium, aged 30, is buried here. Scribonia Frontina, aged 5, is buried here. Scribonius Fronto had this made alive for his father, wife, and [daughter?].
EDCS-16201544.summary: A family tomb inscription for Scribonius Fuscinus, his daughter Aemilia Materna, granddaughter Scribonia Frontina, and the father of Fuscinus, Scribonius Fronto, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16201631.text_edition: \n Tutelae sacrum Marcus Caecilius Montanus pro salute Fusci fili sui posuit QVNO fecit\n
EDCS-16201631.translation: Sacred to Tutela. Marcus Caecilius Montanus set this up for the health of his son Fuscus. QVNO made it.
EDCS-16201631.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Tutela by Marcus Caecilius Montanus for his son's well-being.

EDCS-16300116.text_edition: \n Dobiterus Caburoniqum Equaesi filius sit tibi terra levis Arena Mentoviequm Aergui filia sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-16300116.translation: May the earth lie lightly on you, Dobiterus, son of Caburo, and on you, Arena, daughter of Aergui.
EDCS-16300116.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for two individuals, Dobiterus and Arena, invoking the wish for a light earth upon them.

EDCS-16400425.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely commemorating or dedicating something to Nero Claudius.

EDCS-16700168.text_edition: \n Persius Marci filius Pollia esus domo Hasta miles legionis X geminae centuria Sils nnorum L aerorum sic XXVI hic situs est\n
EDCS-16700168.translation: Persius, son of Marcius, of the Pollia tribe, from Hasta, a soldier of the Tenth Gemina Legion, under centurion Sils, aged 50, serving 26 years, lies here.
EDCS-16700168.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Persius, a soldier in the Tenth Gemina Legion, detailing his age and years of service.

EDCS-16700299.text_edition: \n Baebius Latro Nigri filius Uxama Ibarcensis annorum XIII hic situs est\n
EDCS-16700299.translation: Baebius Latro, son of Nigrus, from Uxama, aged 13, lies here.
EDCS-16700299.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Baebius Latro, a young boy from Uxama.

EDCS-16900244.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely the beginning of a name or dedication.

EDCS-16900276.text_edition: \n Valeriae Cai filiae Faustae Caio Marcio Cai filio Senecae hoc monumentum heredi non cedit Marcus Aemilius Avitus Cornelia Publi filia Primula mater Cornelia Quieta\n
EDCS-16900276.translation: This monument does not pass to an heir for Valeria Faustus, daughter of Caius, to Gaius Marcius Senecus, son of Caius. Marcus Aemilius Avitus and Cornelia Primula, daughter of Publius, mother Cornelia Quieta.
EDCS-16900276.summary: This funerary monument is for Valeria Faustus and Gaius Marcius Senecus, with clauses regarding inheritance and erected by their mother(s).

EDCS-16900285.text_edition: \n filius Festus Turpilla Publi filius filius \n
EDCS-16900285.translation: Festus, son of Turpilla, son of Publius.
EDCS-16900285.summary: This inscription names Festus, son of Turpilla, and grandson of Publius, likely a funerary monument.

EDCS-16900287.translation: Crescens and Piganco[n?], son of [3]ia, and his wife [3]ri. [Here lies]... had this made.
EDCS-16900287.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription recording the burial of Crescens and his wife, with an indication that someone paid for the monument.

EDCS-16900288.translation: Severus of the VIIth Legion Gemina Felix, Crescens, set this up.
EDCS-16900288.summary: This inscription records Severus, a soldier of the VII Legion Gemina Felix, and indicates that Crescens erected the monument.

EDCS-16900289.translation: To Rustica, daughter of Meduttus, Avia.
EDCS-16900289.summary: This is a dedication or memorial inscription to Rustica, daughter of Meduttus, by Avia.

EDCS-16900332.text_edition: \n Atta Turocom Gai uxsor sic hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Gaius Turocom Pacati filius vivus sibi monumentum faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-16900332.translation: Atta Turocom, wife of Gaius, lies here. May the earth be light on you. Gaius Turocom, son of Pacatus, arranged for this monument to be made during his lifetime for himself.
EDCS-16900332.summary: This is a self-funded funerary inscription set up by Gaius Turocom for himself and his wife Atta, with a wish for her soul.

EDCS-16900408.summary: This brief inscription likely records the name of Marcus Statorius Amphio, possibly on a tombstone.

EDCS-17100189.text_edition: \n Marti Caius Petronius Maternus Quinti filius missicius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-17100189.translation: To Mars, Caius Petronius Maternus, son of Quintus, a discharged soldier, willingly paid his vow as deserved.
EDCS-17100189.summary: This is a votive inscription by Caius Petronius Maternus, a retired soldier, to the god Mars.

EDCS-17800381.translation: To the holy god Larius, Breo Bro.
EDCS-17800381.summary: This is a dedication to the god Larius, possibly by Breo Bro.

EDCS-18100083.text_edition: \n Gaio Voconio Venusto annorum X Gaio Voconio Primigenio patri annorum LV\n
EDCS-18100083.translation: To Gaius Voconius Venustus, aged 10, and to Gaius Voconius Primigenius, his father, aged 55.
EDCS-18100083.summary: This inscription records the names and ages of Gaius Voconius Venustus and his father Gaius Voconius Primigenius, likely a funerary monument.

EDCS-18100089.translation: You break knees in the rough joint with your shins. As much as my old age indicates in this way, as it is the height of war. Give me a little kiss, lord. Playful girls always laugh with pleasure. If only... Caius Valerius Verdullus paints.
EDCS-18100089.summary: This fragmented inscription contains a mix of themes, possibly a witty epitaph or poem, and ends with the name of the painter Caius Valerius Verdullus.

EDCS-18100245.translation: By order of our lords Avitus and Faustina, a cistern was built, 13 feet wide, 17 feet long, 10 feet high, with a capacity of 125 (cubic) moduli.
EDCS-18100245.summary: This inscription records the construction of a public cistern, likely during the reign of emperors Avitus and Faustina.

EDCS-18100251.translation: By the favor of Hadrian, I have recorded here that three streams of water are brought across the valley, which will be recovered as private property.
EDCS-18100251.summary: This inscription details the management of water resources, referencing a benefit from Emperor Hadrian.

EDCS-18100256.translation: Nemadora, wife of Lucius Lepidus, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-18100256.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Nemadora, wife of Lucius Lepidus, with a common wish for a light burial.

EDCS-18100258.translation: Contaeca, servant of Moeniccus, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-18100258.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Contaeca, a servant of Moeniccus.

EDCS-18100261.translation: Turamus, son of Coerobrius, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-18100261.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Turamus, son of Coerobrius.

EDCS-18100268.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Petracio Flavo, aged 50. To the spirits of the underworld. Flavius Cloutus, aged [?].
EDCS-18100268.summary: This inscription appears to be a fragment containing two funerary dedications to the De Manibus for individuals named Petracio Flavo and Flavius Cloutus.

EDCS-18100280.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of Macilo, a freedman.

EDCS-18100327.text_edition: \n Accae Metelli servae annorum XXV Epaphroditus \n
EDCS-18100327.summary: This inscription commemorates Acca Metella, a servant aged 25, and possibly records the name of the dedicator, Epaphroditus.

EDCS-19000353.text_edition: \n Caio Publicio Melisso IIIIIIviro ob causas utilitatesque publicas fideliter et constanter defensas ordo Barcinonensium pecunia publica\n
EDCS-19000353.translation: The order of the people of Barcino, with public funds, for Gaius Publicius Melissus, as a IIIIvir for the public causes and advantages faithfully and steadfastly defended.
EDCS-19000353.summary: This inscription records a public dedication by the city of Barcino to Gaius Publicius Melissus, likely for his service as a magistrate.

EDCS-19100805.text_edition: \n ------ Sempr]onia Arganta Marcus Iulius Cephalo de sua pecunia\n
EDCS-19100805.translation: Sempronia Arganta, Marcus Julius Cephalo, from their own money.
EDCS-19100805.summary: This inscription records a dedication made at private expense by Sempronia Arganta and Marcus Julius Cephalo.

EDCS-19100841.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Valerius Diocletianus, the Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered Augustus.
EDCS-19100841.summary: This is a dedication to the Roman Emperor Diocletian, likely on a public monument, dating to his reign (284-305 AD).

EDCS-19100923.text_edition: \n Thaumastus Cai S C servus annorum XIII hic situs est Acceptus posuit\n
EDCS-19100923.translation: Thaumastus, a slave of Caius S.C., aged thirteen years, lies here. Acceptus set this up.
EDCS-19100923.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Thaumastus, a slave boy thirteen years old, who was commemorated by Acceptus.

EDCS-20300494.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely refers to a primicius, a chief servant or official, possibly on a tombstone.

EDCS-20300495.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely refers to a primicius, a chief servant or official, possibly on a tombstone.

EDCS-20300982.summary: This inscription records ownership, identifying Quintus Atilius as a slave.

EDCS-20301052.summary: This inscription likely marks a tomb or property belonging to Gaius Titedius Moderatus.

EDCS-20301091.summary: This inscription indicates the relationship of a freedman named Trigetus to his former master, Lucius.

EDCS-20301770.summary: This inscription identifies a freedman named Caelius and his former master, Marcius.

EDCS-20301794.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription beginning with the name of Aulus Vibius, possibly for a tomb or dedication.

EDCS-20400039.text_edition: \n Diis Manibus QVSCOIECIVS annorum L hic situs est EVTNRA uxor marito bene merenti faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-20400039.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Quscoiejcius, aged fifty years, lies here. Eutnra, his wife, had this made for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-20400039.summary: This epitaph records the death of Quscoiejcius, aged fifty, and commemorates his wife Eutnra's initiative in erecting the tombstone.

EDCS-20400097.summary: This very fragmentary inscription, likely a boundary marker or a simple direction, dates to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400118.text_edition: \n Caio Trocinae Cai liberto Synecdemo IIIIIIviro Augustali Valeria Haline marito optimo\n
EDCS-20400118.translation: To Gaius Synecdemus, freedman of Gaius, a municipal priest, husband of Valeria Haline, the best of husbands.
EDCS-20400118.summary: This epitaph honors Gaius Synecdemus, a freedman and municipal priest, by his wife Valeria Haline, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400120.text_edition: \n Semproniae Modestae Luci filiae annorum XL Modestus\n
EDCS-20400120.translation: To Sempronia Modesta, daughter of Lucius, aged 40. Modestus [erected this].
EDCS-20400120.summary: This epitaph records the death of Sempronia Modesta, daughter of Lucius, aged 40, and was likely set up by Modestus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400140.translation: To the departed spirits. To Quintia, mother of Segges(?), most dutiful, aged 35. Terentia [erected this].
EDCS-20400140.summary: This funerary epitaph honors Quintia, the most dutiful mother of Segges(?), who died at age 35, erected by Terentia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400142.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Traiani Parthici filius divi Nervae nepos Traianus Hadrianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate consul III refecit a Clunia milia passuum IIII\n
EDCS-20400142.translation: Emperor Caesar Hadrian Augustus, son of the deified Trajan the Parthian, grandson of the deified Nerva, supreme pontiff, holding tribunician power, consul for the third time, rebuilt [this road] from Clunia for four miles.
EDCS-20400142.summary: This inscription records Emperor Hadrian's rebuilding of a road segment near Clunia, dating to his third consulship, likely around 119-121 AD.

EDCS-20400144.translation: To the departed spirits. To Lucius Sertorius Paternus, aged 60. Sertoria Septimina [erected this] for her most dutiful husband, and for herself, aged 40.
EDCS-20400144.summary: This funerary epitaph records Sertoria Septimina dedicating a tomb to her husband Lucius Sertorius Paternus and reserving a space for herself, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400206.summary: This is a simple dedication naming Aurolus, son of Veniatioq., likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400210.text_edition: \n Euodus Marcelli Parthenis Marcelli Rustica Marcelli hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis Euodus sibi uxori socrae curavit\n
EDCS-20400210.translation: Euodus, Marcellus, Parthenis, Marcellus, Rustica, Marcellus are buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Euodus arranged [this] for himself, his wife, and his mother-in-law.
EDCS-20400210.summary: This epitaph records the burial of several individuals, including Euodus who arranged the burial for himself, his wife, and mother-in-law, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400211.translation: Valeria Afra [dedicated this] to Hercules, according to her vow.
EDCS-20400211.summary: This is a votive dedication by Valeria Afra to the god Hercules, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400216.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription recording the burial of Retucenus Telassicus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400220.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius Annius, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, priest of Juno, aged 60. Iulia [his wife/daughter], aged 50. Gaius Annius, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe. Marcus Annius Rufus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, priest of Hercules, aged 24. Lucius Annius [son of...], Sextus Annius [son of...]. Here they lie. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-20400220.summary: This is a funerary inscription for several members of the Annius family, likely a father and his sons, and possibly a wife or daughter, who served as priests, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400221.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Saturna made this for her Flaccus.
EDCS-20400221.summary: A funerary epitaph, likely from the Roman Imperial period, set up by Saturna for her deceased husband, Flaccus.

EDCS-20400223.translation: [text illegible] aged 19, he lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-20400223.summary: A fragment of a funerary epitaph from the Roman Imperial period, recording the age of the deceased and a standard closing sentiment.

EDCS-20400224.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Divi filius Augustus consul XI imperator XIIII tribunicia potestate XVI pontifex maximus \n
EDCS-20400224.translation: Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the Divine, consul for the 11th time, imperator for the 14th time, holding tribunician power for the 16th time, high priest.
EDCS-20400224.summary: This text is an official titulature of Emperor Augustus, listing his numerous honors and magistracies, likely from a public inscription during his reign (27 BCE - 14 CE).

EDCS-20400225.text_edition: \n Marco Clodio Marci filio Galeria Flacco IIviro bis flamini tribuno militum legionis IIII Flaviae viro praestantissimo et civi optimo ob plurima erga rem publicam suam merita decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-20400225.translation: To Marcus Clodius Flaccus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, twice chief magistrate, priest, military tribune of the IIII Flavia legion, a most outstanding man and an excellent citizen, for his very many services to his state, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-20400225.summary: A civic honorific inscription honoring Marcus Clodius Flaccus for his public service and dignities, likely from a Roman provincial town during the Imperial period.

EDCS-20400238.translation: Caius Publicius [name illegible]ianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-20400238.summary: A votive inscription from the Roman Imperial period, recording the fulfillment of a vow by Caius Publicius [name illegible]ianus.

EDCS-20400239.translation: Gaius C. Senecianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-20400239.summary: A votive inscription from the Roman Imperial period, recording the fulfillment of a vow by Gaius C. Senecianus.

EDCS-20400274.text_edition: \n Sei quaeris nomen consiste et percipe pauca dum vixei et potuei Pontiliena et Pontilieni liberta et filia eadem moriens et fui et sum epistolium vale\n
EDCS-20400274.translation: If you ask my name, stand still and perceive a few things: while I lived and could, I was Pontiliena, freedwoman and daughter of Pontilienus; dying, I was and am both. Farewell.
EDCS-20400274.summary: A self-penned epitaph from the Roman Imperial period, attributed to Pontiliena, who describes her identity as a freedwoman and daughter, and bids farewell.

EDCS-20400286.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Flavio Valerio Severo Pio F(elici) Invicto Nobilissimo Caesari\n
EDCS-20400286.translation: To our Lord Flavius Valerius Severus Pius Felix Invictus Nobilissimus Caesar.
EDCS-20400286.summary: An acclamatory inscription from the late Roman Empire, addressing a Caesar, likely honoring the emperor or a prominent member of the imperial family.

EDCS-20400296.text_edition: \n Dis Inferis Manibus Iunia Materna Cai filia Aemiliano marito annorum LXX et Aemilio Materno filio annorum XXV\n
EDCS-20400296.translation: To the Infernal Divine Shades. Iunia Materna, daughter of Caius, [sets this up] for her husband Aemilianus, aged 70, and for her son Aemilius Maternus, aged 25.
EDCS-20400296.summary: A funerary inscription from the Roman Imperial period, set up by Iunia Materna for her deceased husband Aemilianus and son Aemilius Maternus.

EDCS-20400299.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Flavio Valerio Constantino Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate consuli patri patriae proconsuli \n
EDCS-20400299.translation: To our lord Flavius Valerius Constantinus, pious, fortunate, unconquered Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power, consul, father of the fatherland, proconsul.
EDCS-20400299.summary: This is a formal imperial titulature for Emperor Constantine I, likely part of a larger inscription on a public monument.

EDCS-20400369.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Galerio Valerio Maxsimiano Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae consuli V proconsuli\n
EDCS-20400369.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Galerius Valerius Maxsimianus, pious, fortunate, unconquered Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power, father of the fatherland, consul five times, proconsul.
EDCS-20400369.summary: This is a formal imperial titulature for Emperor Galerius Valerius Maxsimianus, likely part of a larger inscription on a public monument.

EDCS-20400374.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Galerio Valerio Maxsimiano Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae consuli V proconsuli\n
EDCS-20400374.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Galerius Valerius Maxsimianus, pious, fortunate, unconquered Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power, father of the fatherland, consul five times, proconsul.
EDCS-20400374.summary: This is a formal imperial titulature for Emperor Galerius Valerius Maxsimianus, likely part of a larger inscription on a public monument.

EDCS-20400376.translation: To the departed spirits. Antestia Titulla Sempronia, 20 years old, who is buried here, erected this monument to Titulla Sempronia. Minicia Felicisima placed [it].
EDCS-20400376.summary: This is a funerary epitaph stating that Minicia Felicisima erected a tomb for the 20-year-old Antestia Titulla Sempronia.

EDCS-20400377.translation: Pergamis, slave of Lucius O() C(), is buried here, 30 years old. Helius and his daughter Musice made [this monument] for you, companion.
EDCS-20400377.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pergamis, a slave, erected by her companion Helius and daughter Musice.

EDCS-20400413.summary: This is a very abbreviated funerary inscription recording the name Iulius and his age of 18.

EDCS-20400419.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Imperatori Caesari Valerio][3 [] [] [] [] [] [] bono rei publicae nato\n
EDCS-20400419.translation: To our lord the Emperor Caesar Valerius [---], born for the good of the state.
EDCS-20400419.summary: This is an incomplete imperial dedication, likely to Emperor Valerius, celebrating his birth for the state's benefit.

EDCS-20400423.summary: This is a very fragmented inscription, possibly a dedication or a tombstone, mentioning the names Baebia and Claudia.

EDCS-20400443.summary: This is a concise funerary inscription recording the name Efrem and his age of 50.

EDCS-20400463.translation: To Virnon, son of Turonus, 60 years old.
EDCS-20400463.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Virnon, son of Turonus, at the age of 60.

EDCS-20400467.translation: Vibius Turinus set this up by Jupiter's command.
EDCS-20400467.summary: A private votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by Vibius Turinus, likely a small shrine or offering.

EDCS-20400469.translation: To Gaius Caesar, son of Augustus.
EDCS-20400469.summary: A dedication to Gaius Caesar, son of Augustus, likely erected during his lifetime or in his memory.

EDCS-20400472.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesari Gaio Vibio Treboniano Gallo Invicto Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate III patri patriae consuli II proconsuli et Imperatori Caesari Gaio Vibio Afenio Gallo Veldumiano Volusiano Invicto Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate III consuli \n
EDCS-20400472.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Vibius Trebonianus Gallus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, consul for the 2nd time, proconsul, and to the Emperor Caesar Gaius Vibius Afenius Gallus Veldumianus Volusianus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, consul.
EDCS-20400472.summary: A dedication to co-emperors Trebonianus Gallus and Volusianus, recording their titles and offices, likely from the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-20400478.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Petronia Lupina, his wife, had this made for Leopardo, who lived 25 years.
EDCS-20400478.summary: A funerary epitaph for Leopardo, who died at age 25, erected by his wife Petronia Lupina.

EDCS-20400479.translation: To the departed spirits. Nardenus set up this monument for his wife Serenus and himself.
EDCS-20400479.summary: A funerary monument set up by Nardenus for his wife Serenus and himself.

EDCS-20400495.translation: Under Emperor Titus Caesar Vespasianus Augustus, son of the deified Vespasian, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 9th time, acclaimed imperator for the 15th time, father of the fatherland, consul for the 8th time, and Caesar Domitian, son of the deified Vespasian, consul for the 7th time. The Via Nova was built, 14 miles from Bracara, by Gaius Calpetanus Rantius Quirinalis Valerius Festus, legate of the emperor with proconsular power.
EDCS-20400495.summary: An official milestone or boundary stone recording the construction of the Via Nova under Emperors Vespasian and Domitian, with specific measurements and the name of the legate responsible.

EDCS-20400532.translation: To the departed spirits. Publius Clodius [---] pious to his own, lived 63 years. He lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-20400532.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Clodius, recording his age and a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-20401612.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavius Marcellus, recording his name and age.

EDCS-20401618.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Fabius pater mater Flavia Urso filio annorum XII pientissimo sibi posuerunt\n
EDCS-20401618.translation: Fabius and his mother Flavia Urso set up this for their most devoted son, aged 12.
EDCS-20401618.summary: A funerary epitaph erected by parents for their deceased son, aged 12.

EDCS-20401625.translation: Muria, aged 2 years, [daughter] of Licinius Saf[---]
EDCS-20401625.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for a child named Muria, aged 2, identifying her father.

EDCS-20401628.text_edition: \n Acatemeris Luci Corneli Maturi serva annorum X hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-20401628.translation: Acatemeris, the slave girl of Lucius Cornelius Maturus, aged 10, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-20401628.summary: This is a tombstone for Acatemeris, a slave girl, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401629.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Primitiva pia in suis annoru<m=N> XXXV hic sita sit terra levis \n
EDCS-20401629.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Underworld. Primitiva, dutiful, aged 35, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-20401629.summary: This is a tombstone for Primitiva, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401630.text_edition: \n Operis Titi Pasidi Publi filii Sergia Sabinei\n
EDCS-20401630.translation: Of the work of Titus Pasidius, son of Publius, of the Sergia tribe.
EDCS-20401630.summary: This inscription records a public work commissioned by Titus Pasidius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401631.text_edition: \n Lucio Aemilio Propinquo Accitano veteri ex decreto ordinis Accitanorum veterum Lucius Aemilius Anus pater honore accepto impensa remissa ponendum c(uravit)\n
EDCS-20401631.translation: Lucius Aemilius Anus, father, had this erected in honor of Lucius Aemilius Propinquus Accitanus, a veteran, by decree of the elders of Accitani Veteres, with the expense remitted.
EDCS-20401631.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Lucius Aemilius Anus for Lucius Aemilius Propinquus Accitanus, a veteran, likely from Roman provincial Gaul.

EDCS-20401639.text_edition: \n Silvano sacrum ob dedicationem thermarum vovit Publius Marcius \n
EDCS-20401639.translation: Dedicated to Silvanus. Publius Marcius vowed this upon the dedication of the baths.
EDCS-20401639.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Silvanus, related to the dedication of baths, likely from Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401641.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Vesonia Florida nnorum LIII pia in suis hic sita est tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-20401641.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Underworld. Vesonia Florida, aged 53, dutiful, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-20401641.summary: This is a tombstone for Vesonia Florida, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401642.text_edition: \n Cryse ligo sic auri pondoII rogat et ad Iau dat pequnia sic quae a me accepit Heracla conservus meus ut insttetur sic huius senus sic ocelus sic et vires quicumqui sint sic aride m do pequniam sic honori sacricola\n
EDCS-20401642.translation: Cryseus, gold weights: 2 pounds. And he gives money here for the service. He received money from Heracla, my fellow slave, so that they may be established, like his sight, like his strength. Whoever they may be, I give money for their honor, as a votary.
EDCS-20401642.summary: This inscription seems to be a will or a statement concerning the disposition of property and honoring fellow slaves, possibly dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20900140.text_edition: \n Heraclida habet catinum et neminem hoc dat et ab aliis gratis non rogat\n
EDCS-20900140.translation: Heraclida has a dish, and she gives it to no one, and she does not ask for it from others for free.
EDCS-20900140.summary: This inscription appears to be a personal note or declaration regarding ownership and generosity, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20900142.translation: Of Faustus, son of Caius Marius Statius.
EDCS-20900142.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely recording a personal name and parentage, possibly for a tombstone or a public dedication, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20900289.text_edition: \n Caius Coelius Atisi filius IIvir quinquennalis murum turres portas faciendas coeravit\n
EDCS-20900289.translation: Caius Coelius, son of Atisius, duumvir quinquennalis, oversaw the construction of the wall, towers, and gates.
EDCS-20900289.summary: This inscription records the public works of Caius Coelius, a duumvir quinquennalis, likely from a Roman colonial city in the provinces.

EDCS-21000117.summary: This inscription likely records a number, possibly related to a count or a group, followed by the name Fortunatus, possibly of a freedman or owner.

EDCS-21200004.text_edition: \n Caius Farronius Cai filius Quietus annorum XVIII na mater filio\n
EDCS-21200004.translation: Gaius Farronius Quietus, son of Gaius, aged 18, to his mother.
EDCS-21200004.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Farronius Quietus, who died at age 18, likely dedicated by his mother.

EDCS-21200005.translation: To Aulus Oppius, son of Aulus.
EDCS-21200005.summary: This is a dedication or funerary inscription for Aulus Oppius, son of Aulus.

EDCS-21200009.summary: This inscription is a fragment containing only the name Anicetus, likely a freedman or a personal name.

EDCS-21200020.text_edition: \n Licinius Faventinus sibi et Maeciae Libadi uxori carissimae et libertis hoc monumentum heredem meum non sequetur\n
EDCS-21200020.translation: Licinius Faventinus for himself, and for his dearest wife Maecia Libas, and their freedmen, erected this monument; my heir shall not inherit it.
EDCS-21200020.summary: This is a funerary monument inscription by Licinius Faventinus for himself, his wife, and freedmen, stipulating that the monument is not to be inherited.

EDCS-21500407.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to be a religious dedication or funerary text for Servanus, described as a slave of 'the Lord', possibly a Christian context.

EDCS-21500414.summary: This is a simple inscription bearing the full name Gaius Antonius Varus, likely a personal name on a monument or object.

EDCS-21500415.summary: This is a severely fragmented inscription with illegible text, offering no specific information.

EDCS-21500421.text_edition: \n Fortunae Lucius Valerius Flav(inus) miles legionis VIII Augustae votum sovit libens merito\n
EDCS-21500421.translation: Lucius Valerius Flav(inus), soldier of the Legio VIII Augusta, gladly paid his vow, deservedly.
EDCS-21500421.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius Valerius Flav(inus), a soldier of Legio VIII Augusta, fulfilling a vow made to Fortuna.

EDCS-21500424.text_edition: \n iae Titi filiae idae sacerdoti Dianae Minicia Mummia Quinti filia \n
EDCS-21500424.translation: Minicia Mummia, daughter of Quintus, priestess of Diana, [daughter] of Titus Idae.
EDCS-21500424.summary: This inscription records Minicia Mummia, daughter of Quintus and Titus Idae, as a priestess of Diana.

EDCS-21500447.summary: This is a dedication by Licinus, Nius, and Hippolytus, though the exact nature of the monument and the relationship between the dedicator and recipient are unclear.

EDCS-21500449.text_edition: \n Maximus infans vixit annum I menses VI hic requiescit in pace Idus Iunias\n
EDCS-21500449.translation: Maximus, an infant, lived for 1 year and 6 months; he rests here in peace on the Ides of June.
EDCS-21500449.summary: This epitaph records the death of an infant named Maximus, who died on June 13th, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21500453.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Censorino filio et Asello contubernali Eucharis\n
EDCS-21500453.translation: To the Divine Shades: Eucharis [erected this] for Censorius, her son, and for Asellus, her companion.
EDCS-21500453.summary: This funerary inscription by Eucharis is dedicated to her son Censorius and her companion Asellus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21700129.summary: This is a very abbreviated inscription, possibly recording a name and a formula, its meaning is uncertain.

EDCS-21700133.text_edition: \n iglo nnorum LXXX Marcus S dius \n
EDCS-21700133.translation: [He lived] eighty years. Marcus S. dius [erected this].
EDCS-21700133.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription recording the age of the deceased and the name of the dedicator, Marcus S. dius.

EDCS-21700135.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Luci Iuli Brininli sic annorum LXXX sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-21700135.translation: To the Divine Shades: For Lucius Iulius Brininlus, eighty years old. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-21700135.summary: This funerary inscription honors Lucius Iulius Brininlus, who was eighty years old, and includes the traditional wish for a light earth.

EDCS-21700147.text_edition: \n Medugenus Caesaronis sibi et filio Rutilio annorum VX ex gentte Cilurnigorum\n
EDCS-21700147.translation: Medugenus for himself and for his son Rutilius, aged [15?] years, from the tribe of the Cilurnigores.
EDCS-21700147.summary: This inscription records Medugenus dedicating something for himself and his son Rutilius, who was likely fifteen years old, and identifies his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-21700176.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Anna, daughter of Tucius, arranged for this to be made for her sons Anaeus and Accaeus, and for her son Paternu[s].
EDCS-21700176.summary: This funerary monument was arranged by Anna for her sons Anaeus, Accaeus, and Paternu[s].

EDCS-21700188.text_edition: \n Matugeno Matugeniqum Quinti Tancini filio Tibirae uxori et Cuttirae filiae mater filiis faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-21700188.translation: Matugena arranged for this to be made for Matugenicus, son of Quintus Tancinus, and for Tibira, his wife, and for Cuttira, his daughter.
EDCS-21700188.summary: This funerary inscription indicates that Matugena commissioned the monument for her son Matugenicus, her husband, and her daughter.

EDCS-21700348.text_edition: \n Valentina annorum XXII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-21700348.translation: Valentina, twenty-two years old, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-21700348.summary: This epitaph records the death of Valentina, who was twenty-two years old, and includes a standard funerary wish.

EDCS-21700349.text_edition: \n Acrecte Luci liberta nnorum L hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-21700349.translation: Acrecte, freedwoman of Lucius, forty years old, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-21700349.summary: A funerary epitaph for Acrecte, a freedwoman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21700350.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum IA Gai filia NIC OS annorum XXXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-21700350.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. IA, daughter of Gaius, thirty-five years old, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-21700350.summary: A funerary epitaph for IA, daughter of Gaius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21700351.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Pacatus pius in suis annorum mensium VI hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-21700351.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Pacatus, devout, in his own plot, six months old, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-21700351.summary: A funerary epitaph for Pacatus, an infant, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21700352.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Fidentinae annorum XVII bene merenti hic sita tibi hic terra levis Dis Manibus sacrum Nectareo annorum XXXX bene merenti hic situs terra levis\n
EDCS-21700352.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Fidentina, seventeen years old, deserving of good treatment, buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Nectareus, forty years old, deserving of good treatment, buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-21700352.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of both Fidentina and Nectareus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21700356.text_edition: \n Manibus Dis sic Cassia Faustina annorum XXXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-21700356.translation: To the Divine Shades. Thus Cassia Faustina, thirty-five years old, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-21700356.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cassia Faustina, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21700371.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Priscus had this made for his most pious Mucius Mamilianus.
EDCS-21700371.summary: A funerary dedication erected by Priscus for Mucius Mamilianus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21700392.text_edition: \n Sacris Manibus XXX Mercator Primigeniae matri curavit\n
EDCS-21700392.translation: For the Divine Shades. Mercator had this made for his mother Primigenia, aged thirty.
EDCS-21700392.summary: A funerary dedication erected by Mercator for his mother Primigenia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21700410.text_edition: \n filius Tertius veteranus legionis IIII Macedonicae Aniensis Caesaraugustanus annorum LXX hic situs est heres ex testamento\n
EDCS-21700410.translation: Tertius, a veteran of the Fourth Macedonian Legion, from Aniensis Caesaraugusta, seventy years old, is buried here, his heir by will.
EDCS-21700410.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tertius, a veteran soldier, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21700428.translation: For Aisa Vian... [of the] Caucesii... years old.
EDCS-21700428.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for an individual named Aisa, possibly of the Caucesii tribe, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21700437.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Valeria Rustica, twenty years old, buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-21700437.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Rustica, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21700570.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iuli Lupi VI viri Augustalis Iulia Nymphidia patrono indulgentissimo bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-21700570.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Iulia Nymphidia set this up for her most indulgent and well-deserving patron, Iulius Lupus, a VIvir Augustalis.
EDCS-21700570.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Iulia Nymphidia to her patron, Iulius Lupus, a member of the college of VIviri Augustales, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-21700573.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caecilia Parata Caecili Severini filia annorum XV hic sita eest sit tibi terra levis Dis Manibus Caecilius Severinus annorum XXXV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-21700573.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Caecilia Parata, daughter of Caecilius Severinus, aged 15, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. To the Divine Spirits. Caecilius Severinus, aged 35, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-21700573.summary: This is a double funerary inscription recording the burial of Caecilia Parata and Caecilius Severinus, likely father and daughter, with standard epitaph phrases, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-21700577.summary: This fragmentary inscription contains names and possibly a word related to snakes ('venae') or veins, making its precise purpose unclear, but it likely relates to a person or people from the Roman period.

EDCS-21700580.text_edition: \n Domitia Luci filia Iusta sibi et \n
EDCS-21700580.translation: Domitia Iusta, daughter of Lucius, for herself and...
EDCS-21700580.summary: This is a funerary inscription that begins by recording Domitia Iusta, daughter of Lucius, for herself, but it is incomplete, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21700625.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Attius Aquilus annorum XXXV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-21700625.translation: To the Divine Spirits, sacred. Attius Aquilus, aged 35, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-21700625.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Attius Aquilus, aged 35, who is buried here, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21900139.translation: Aurelia Tertiola fulfilled her vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-21900139.summary: This is a votive inscription by Aurelia Tertiola, indicating she willingly fulfilled a vow, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21900141.translation: Here lies (?) Ellico(n), son of Stenion.
EDCS-21900141.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription that names Ellico(n), son of Stenion, as being buried here, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21900173.translation: Valeria Lucia, wife, Ursus and [...] his...
EDCS-21900173.summary: This incomplete inscription mentions Valeria Lucia and Ursus, possibly recording family members or a dedication, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21900238.text_edition: \n Marcus Aemilius Lupianus hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-21900238.translation: Marcus Aemilius Lupianus is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-21900238.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Aemilius Lupianus, who is buried here, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21900245.text_edition: \n ------ Co]sconius Ovinianus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-21900245.translation: [...] Cosconius Ovinianus fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-21900245.summary: This fragmentary inscription records Cosconius Ovinianus fulfilling a vow, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21900260.summary: This is a funerary epigram for Leander Tityrus, likely from a tombstone.

EDCS-21900264.translation: To the departed spirits of Cornelia Placid(a), aged 31. Cornelia Tertiola, her most devoted mother, had this made.
EDCS-21900264.summary: This funerary dedication honors Cornelia Placidia, commissioned by her mother, Cornelia Tertiola, indicating a Roman burial.

EDCS-21900266.summary: This is a simple inscription recording the name of Municeps, son of Elaisio, possibly from a tombstone or a dedication.

EDCS-21900278.summary: This inscription records the name and age of Aemilius Flavus, likely from a tombstone or a funerary monument.

EDCS-22200006.text_edition: \n Turesica Turesami filia annorum L hic sita est\n
EDCS-22200006.translation: Turesica, daughter of Turesamus, aged 50, lies here.
EDCS-22200006.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Turesica, daughter of Turesamus, indicating her burial at this location.

EDCS-22200008.translation: To the departed spirits of H...
EDCS-22200008.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary dedication to the 'Dis Manibus' (departed spirits), but the name of the deceased is lost.

EDCS-22200034.text_edition: \n Segontiecus Abloni filius annorum LXX hic situs est\n
EDCS-22200034.translation: Segontiecus, son of Ablonius, aged 70, lies here.
EDCS-22200034.summary: This epitaph records that Segontiecus, son of Ablonius, aged 70, is buried here.

EDCS-22200155.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Fuscinianus annorum XXV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis \n
EDCS-22200155.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fuscinianus, aged 25, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22200155.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fuscinianus, aged 25, with the common wish 'sit tibi terra levis' (may the earth lie lightly upon you).

EDCS-22200167.translation: Lucretia Parthenope had this made for Caellia Matern(a).
EDCS-22200167.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned by Lucretia Parthenope for Caellia Materna, likely indicating a familial relationship and a burial.

EDCS-22200168.text_edition: \n Caletyche annorum IIII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-22200168.translation: Caletyche, aged 4, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22200168.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young child named Caletyche, aged 4, with a wish for peace in the afterlife.

EDCS-22200169.translation: To Primitivus, aged 20. Rhodope and Rusticanus had this made for their brother. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22200169.summary: This is a tombstone for Primitivus, erected by his sister Rhodope and brother Rusticanus.

EDCS-22200173.translation: Lucius? Sempronius? Auspic[...], aged 29. Lucius? Sempron[...]
EDCS-22200173.summary: This fragmentary tombstone records the name and age of a Roman individual, likely with repeating names.

EDCS-22200174.translation: Famulus, for Famula, his daughter, and Martialis, his uncle. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22200174.summary: This tombstone is dedicated by Martialis to his daughter Famula, with Famulus also mentioned.

EDCS-22200175.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Sothera gave this to Crescentina Magidia, my beloved, in memory of her, aged 35.
EDCS-22200175.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Sothera to Crescentina Magidia, aged 35.

EDCS-22200176.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Licinia Vicaria had this made for her most devoted son Aurelius Pyrrhus, who died in Rome, aged 35. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22200176.summary: This tombstone records the death of Aurelius Pyrrhus at age 35 in Rome, erected by his devoted mother Licinia Vicaria.

EDCS-22200179.summary: This fragmentary tombstone records the name, occupation, and age of a young man named Endymion.

EDCS-22200182.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription that seems to mention a forum.

EDCS-22200183.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary dedication to an individual named Octavius.

EDCS-22200184.translation: Rectugnus?, son of Arg() and Capreius [...]
EDCS-22200184.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that seems to record the parentage of an individual named Rectugnus.

EDCS-22200218.translation: Tombstone of Turromus, son of Vadiniensis Comius, aged 25. Pudens placed this for his brother. He lies here.
EDCS-22200218.summary: This tombstone records Turromus, aged 25, erected by his brother Pudens.

EDCS-22200219.translation: To the Gods of the place, Julius Reburrus gladly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-22200219.summary: This is a votive inscription by Julius Reburrus, likely a dedication to local deities, to fulfill a vow.

EDCS-22200226.text_edition: \n lerius Luci f(ilius) Valerianus Segontinus nnum XXIX hic situs est\n
EDCS-22200226.translation: Ierius Valerianus, son of Lucius, from Segontia, aged 29, is buried here.
EDCS-22200226.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ierius Valerianus, a 29-year-old man from Segontia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22200241.summary: This is a simple greeting inscription addressed to Lucius Argentarius Nicander.

EDCS-22200328.translation: Lucius Pompeius Fuscis filius Fuscimus, aged 62, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22200328.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Pompeius Fuscis filius Fuscimus, aged 62, from the Roman Imperial period, expressing a wish for a light burial.

EDCS-22200330.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Florinus defunctus est annorum XXXXV Musturus frater faciendum curavit hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-22200330.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Florinus has died, aged 45. His brother Musturus arranged for this to be made. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22200330.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Florinus, aged 45, arranged by his brother Musturus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22200332.translation: Primu[l]a, aged 10, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon her.
EDCS-22200332.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl named Primula, aged 10, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22200333.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Pompeius arranged for this to be made for Pompeia Paulina, his freedwoman, aged [around 70?].
EDCS-22200333.summary: This funerary inscription records that Gaius Pompeius arranged for the burial of his freedwoman, Pompeia Paulina, whose age is partially reconstructed.

EDCS-22200335.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Popeia Vereqda annorum XXIII sit terra levis VALE LAIVS ARQISV faciendum quravit sic titulum\n
EDCS-22200335.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Popeia Vereqda has died, aged 23. Farewell. Valerius Arquisus arranged for this monument to be made with this inscription.
EDCS-22200335.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Popeia Vereqda, aged 23, and credits Valerius Arquisus with arranging the monument and inscription.

EDCS-22200337.text_edition: \n VSOR or IIIIIIviratus circensibus editis decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-22200337.translation: He held the office of magistrate of the games, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-22200337.summary: This fragmented inscription likely refers to a magistrate who organized games, as mandated by local council (decurions), possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22200347.translation: [...] Hilarus the pedagogue placed [this] for [...] Vareq[...], whose honor was greatest even in life.
EDCS-22200347.summary: This funerary inscription, set up by a pedagogue named Hilarus, commemorates an individual of high honor, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22200365.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Traiani Parthici filius divi Nervae nepos Traianus Hadrianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate V consul III fecit\n
EDCS-22200365.translation: Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, son of the deified Trajan the Parthian, grandson of the deified Nerva, chief priest, in his fifth tribunician power, consul for the third time, built this.
EDCS-22200365.summary: This inscription records a building project by Emperor Hadrian, likely from his reign (117-138 AD).

EDCS-22200368.translation: To the Lares Anaei, Priscus and Hieron, freedmen stewards of Emperor Domitian Augustus, paid their vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-22200368.summary: This inscription is a dedication to household gods (Lares) by freedmen administrators, likely from the late 1st century AD during the reign of Domitian.

EDCS-22400206.translation: To Arquius, from Anulla, for Ambatus.
EDCS-22400206.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely a dedication by Anulla to Arquius for Ambatus, perhaps a relative or friend.

EDCS-22400330.translation: Augustan boundary marker of the months.
EDCS-22400330.summary: This inscription marks a boundary stone associated with Augustan measurements or divisions, possibly related to land or administrative districts.

EDCS-22400365.translation: Titus Sempronius Pullus, son of Marcus, designated as a magistrate for the second time, dedicates.
EDCS-22400365.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Titus Sempronius Pullus, who held or was designated for a local magistracy (IIIIvir), likely from the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-22400385.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Titi Aeli Hadriani Antonini Augusti Pii domuique eius Quintus Iunius Rusticus consul legatus Augusti pro praetore provinciae Hisp(aniae) citerioris\n
EDCS-22400385.translation: For Jupiter Best and Greatest, for the safety of the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius and his family, Quintus Junius Rusticus, consul and imperial legate with praetorian power of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-22400385.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter by Quintus Junius Rusticus, a high-ranking imperial governor, for the well-being of Emperor Antoninus Pius, likely in the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-22400392.text_edition: \n Invicto Mithrae Gaius Victorius Victorinus |(centurio) legionis VII geminae Antoninianae piae felicis in honorem stationis Lucensis et Victoriorum Secundi et Victoris libertorum suorum aram posuit libenti animo\n
EDCS-22400392.translation: To the Unconquered Mithras, Gaius Victorius Victorinus, centurion of the loyal and fortunate Legio VII Gemina Antoniniana, erected this altar willingly in honor of the Lucensian garrison and of his freedmen, Victorinus Secundus and Victor.
EDCS-22400392.summary: This is a dedication of an altar to the god Mithras by a centurion of Legio VII Gemina, likely from the 3rd century AD, honoring his garrison and freedmen.

EDCS-22400406.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Her mother erected this for Aemilia Vafra, aged fifteen.
EDCS-22400406.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a mother for her deceased daughter, Aemilia Vafra, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22400434.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Allaugana filio suo pientissimo Sempronio Paterno annorum XI consulatu CCCLXI\n
EDCS-22400434.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Sempronius Paternus, aged eleven, to his most dutiful son, in the 361st year.
EDCS-22400434.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Sempronius Paternus to his deceased son, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period and referring to a local era.

EDCS-22400440.translation: To the Genius of the place, Paternus Sapecus paid willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-22400440.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the local protective spirit (genius loci) by Paternus Sapecus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22400442.translation: To Manius Julius, son of a magistrate, [a woman freed by whom] freedman, aged [number] years.
EDCS-22400442.summary: This funerary inscription records a deceased man, likely a son and a freedman, with partial details about his age and family.

EDCS-22400483.summary: This votive inscription records a fulfillment of a vow by an individual named Sterc, dated from its style to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22400486.translation: Mamilia Caesias fulfilled her vow to Tutela.
EDCS-22400486.summary: This votive inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Mamilia Caesias to the goddess Tutela.

EDCS-22400497.translation: To the spirits of the departed, in memory of Datius V[...]
EDCS-22400497.summary: This funerary inscription begins a dedication to the Manes (spirits of the dead) and records the name Datius, with the rest of the text lost.

EDCS-22400498.translation: To the spirits of the departed. His wife Octava erected [this monument?] to Manius Reso Herophilus, son of Phil[...], sister of Philate. Aged 50 years.
EDCS-22400498.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication of a tomb by Octava to her husband Manius Reso Herophilus, also noting his age.

EDCS-22400499.summary: This fragment of a funerary inscription records the age of a deceased individual named [...nianus] and mentions his nephew and another person.

EDCS-22600033.text_edition: \n Marcus Valerius Marci filius Galeria Reburrus Segobrigensis aedilis IIIIvir \n
EDCS-22600033.translation: Marcus Valerius Reburrus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, from Segobriga, aedile and member of the IIIIviri.
EDCS-22600033.summary: This public inscription records the name, tribal affiliation, origin, and political offices (aedile and IIIIvir) of Marcus Valerius Reburrus.

EDCS-22800021.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Flavius Callinus, here lies Crispinus, to his brother, aged 30 years. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-22800021.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Crispinus by his brother Flavius Callinus, recording his age and including a common epitaph wishing him a light burial.

EDCS-22800022.text_edition: \n Festivos sic Laribus Vialibus ex somnio votum solvit\n
EDCS-22800022.translation: Festivus fulfilled his vow to the household gods and the gods of Vialibus from a dream.
EDCS-22800022.summary: This votive inscription records Festivus fulfilling a vow made to domestic deities, prompted by a dream.

EDCS-22800047.text_edition: \n Caius Anneius memoriam restituit coniugi merenti Vettiae Claudiae\n
EDCS-22800047.translation: Caius Anneius restored the memorial to his deserving wife, Vettia Claudia.
EDCS-22800047.summary: This inscription records Caius Anneius restoring a memorial for his wife, Vettia Claudia, emphasizing her merit.

EDCS-22800065.translation: To the Mothers Endeiteris, Felix Prisca gladly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-22800065.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Felix Prisca to the Matres Endeiteris, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800066.translation: L.I. Proculina had this made for her mother.
EDCS-22800066.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument commissioned by L.I. Proculina for her mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800068.translation: Bergius Seranus, aedile, declares: Whoever comes here, wine [costs] 507 [denarii/units].
EDCS-22800068.summary: This inscription, set up by the aedile Bergius Seranus, likely records the price of wine, possibly in a public market context from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800166.text_edition: \n Mercurio Decimus Cornelius Britto votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-22800166.translation: Decimus Cornelius Britto gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Mercury.
EDCS-22800166.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Decimus Cornelius Britto to the god Mercury, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800167.text_edition: \n Anteros Flavae Cili servas sic vernae atque libertae annorum XV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-22800167.translation: Anteros, slave of Flava Cilia, and a household slave and freedwoman, aged 15, is laid here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-22800167.summary: This inscription records the burial of Anteros, a 15-year-old slave and freedwoman belonging to Flava Cilia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800168.text_edition: \n Marcus C Galeria Flavus Marcus Valerius Proculus magistri II\n
EDCS-22800168.translation: Marcus C. Galeria Flavus and Marcus Valerius Proculus, magistrates.
EDCS-22800168.summary: This inscription records the names of two magistrates, Marcus C. Galeria Flavus and Marcus Valerius Proculus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800169.text_edition: \n Viva Calpurnia Clara Deutero filio annorum X \n
EDCS-22800169.translation: Viva Calpurnia Clara [erected this for] her son Deuteros, aged 10.
EDCS-22800169.summary: This inscription records a memorial erected by Viva Calpurnia Clara for her son Deuteros, who died at the age of 10, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800170.text_edition: \n Quintus Cosconius Cousanus et Vettia et Cosconia \n
EDCS-22800170.translation: Quintus Cosconius Cousanus, and Vettia, and Cosconia.
EDCS-22800170.summary: This inscription lists three names: Quintus Cosconius Cousanus, Vettia, and Cosconia, possibly family members or individuals associated with a monument, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800171.text_edition: \n Triumphalis Atia et Rusticus fili Callisto patri pio nnorum LXX hic sito sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-22800171.translation: Triumphalis [erected this for] Callisto, her pious father, aged 70, who is laid here; may the earth lie lightly on you. Atia and Rusticus, his son.
EDCS-22800171.summary: This inscription records the burial of Callisto, aged 70, set up by his daughter Triumphalis, with mention of Atia and his son Rusticus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800172.text_edition: \n Turpa Rusticae Dequmiae sic serva annorum XX Castelatus verna ]\n
EDCS-22800172.translation: Turpa, slave of Dequmia Rustica, household slave, aged 20. Castelatus, a household slave.
EDCS-22800172.summary: This inscription records the death of Turpa, a 20-year-old slave of Dequmia Rustica, and mentions Castelatus, also a household slave, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800173.text_edition: \n -aco Acara Materni serva libens animo votum solvit\n
EDCS-22800173.translation: Acara, servant of Acus Maternius, gladly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-22800173.summary: A funerary dedication by a freedwoman, possibly from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-22800175.text_edition: \n S V V V E annorum XXX VIAN\n
EDCS-22800175.translation: This is a funerary monument for [---] V. V. V. E., aged thirty-six.
EDCS-22800175.summary: A funerary inscription recording the name and age of the deceased, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800178.text_edition: \n Valeria Pacula annorum XXX hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-22800178.translation: Valeria Pacula, aged thirty, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22800178.summary: A tombstone for Valeria Pacula, aged thirty, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800179.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Vibiae Callistae matri Vibia Threpte faciendum curavit hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-22800179.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Vibia Threpte took care to have this made for her mother, Vibiae Callistae. She lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22800179.summary: A funerary monument erected by Vibia Threpte for her mother, Vibiae Callistae, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800180.text_edition: \n I magno consiliis et d ui genitor \n
EDCS-22800180.translation: To the great counsel and the divine father.
EDCS-22800180.summary: A fragment of a dedication to divine figures, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800181.text_edition: \n Censorina Ursae sorori pientissimae annorum hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-22800181.translation: To Censorina, for her most devoted sister Ursae, aged [---]. She lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22800181.summary: A tombstone for Ursae, a sister, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800187.translation: Porcia Lucilla, daughter of Lucius, to [her father and mother(?)] // To Lucius Porcius, son of Lucius, [---] / To Porcia, daughter of Lucius, Pla[---]
EDCS-22800187.summary: A fragmented funerary monument likely dedicating multiple family members, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800192.translation: [---], son(?) of Aur[---], aged twenty-one, lies here.
EDCS-22800192.summary: A funerary inscription recording the name and age of the deceased, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800193.translation: ] to Quietae, [his?] wife // ] to Gaius, son of Gaius, Tranquillus of the Galeria tribe
EDCS-22800193.summary: A fragmented funerary monument likely for a wife named Quietae and a son named Tranquillus, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800201.translation: Turaesius Letondicum, son of Marsi, aged thirty, lies here.
EDCS-22800201.summary: A tombstone for Turaesius Letondicum, son of Marsi, aged thirty, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800206.text_edition: \n Loucanus Criciqum Anonis filius hic situs annorum XXX\n
EDCS-22800206.translation: Loucanus, son of Criciqus and Anonis, lies here, aged 30.
EDCS-22800206.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Loucanus, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-22800211.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Pusinca nnorum XX hic sita est Caellacus contubernali faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-22800211.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Pusinca, aged 20, lies here. Caellacus had this made for his fellow soldier.
EDCS-22800211.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Pusinca, aged 20, and was commissioned by Caellacus, a fellow soldier.

EDCS-22800212.text_edition: \n Laricius omn annorum XXXX hic iacet\n
EDCS-22800212.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Laricius, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-22800230.text_edition: \n Lucius Acilius Maxsumi sic filius Quirina Maxsuminus sic annorum XXXIII\n
EDCS-22800230.translation: Lucius Acilius, son of Maxsumus, and Maxsuminus, son of Lucius Acilius, aged 33.
EDCS-22800230.summary: This epitaph records Lucius Acilius and Maxsuminus, likely father and son, who died at the age of 33.

EDCS-22800256.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Maltrepsis, aged 18.

EDCS-22800323.translation: To Acceti, for his son Cariqon, son of Ambatus.
EDCS-22800323.summary: This is a dedication from Acceti to his son Cariqon, son of Ambatus.

EDCS-22800354.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest. Scribonius Probus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-22800354.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter, fulfilled by Scribonius Probus.

EDCS-22800355.text_edition: \n Quintio Neii Aviti servus hic situs tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-22800355.translation: Quintius, slave of Neius Avitus, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22800355.summary: This funerary epitaph records Quintius, a slave of Neius Avitus.

EDCS-22800357.translation: Castio, son of Castio, aged 50. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22800357.summary: This funerary epitaph records Castio, son of Castio, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-22800358.translation: Turpio, aged 25, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Caizita had this made.
EDCS-22800358.summary: This funerary epitaph records Turpio, aged 25, and was commissioned by Caizita.

EDCS-22800359.translation: Marcus Turius Murtilus is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you. Marcus Magilio saw to its construction.
EDCS-22800359.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Marcus Turius Murtilus, with the monument commissioned by Marcus Magilio, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-22800362.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Arcelaus annorum XXXII hic situs <e>(st) Maxumilla sic patri pientissimo fecit sit <t>(ibi) terra levis\n
EDCS-22800362.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Arcelaus, aged 32, is buried here. Maxumilla made this for her most devoted father. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-22800362.summary: A funerary epitaph for Arcelaus, aged 32, erected by his daughter Maxumilla, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-22800364.text_edition: \n Martia Q P P serva annorum XL hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-22800364.translation: Martia, freedwoman of Q. P. P., aged 40, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-22800364.summary: A funerary epitaph for Martia, a freedwoman aged 40, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-22800365.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Zoticus Martiae uxori pientissimae annorum faciendum curavit hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-22800365.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Zoticus saw to the construction for his most devoted wife Martia, aged [..]. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-22800365.summary: A funerary epitaph for Martia, erected by her husband Zoticus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23001909.text_edition: \n Mascutio et filiae Nireniae Didi Maeni Mamamarius frater faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-23001909.translation: Mascutius and his daughter Nirenia. Didi, her brother, saw to its construction.
EDCS-23001909.summary: A funerary epitaph commemorating Mascutius and his daughter Nirenia, with the monument commissioned by Didi, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23001910.text_edition: \n Archio Antoni servus hic situs est Antonia Felco de sua inpensa faciendum curavit M H\n
EDCS-23001910.translation: Archio, slave of Antonius, is buried here. Antonia Felco provided for its construction at her own expense. M. H.
EDCS-23001910.summary: A funerary epitaph for Archio, slave of Antonius, erected by Antonia Felco, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23001911.text_edition: \n Dis Manicus sacrum Fabricius nnorum XX hic situs est pater et mater memoriam posuerunt\n
EDCS-23001911.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Fabricius, aged 20, is buried here. His father and mother set up this memorial.
EDCS-23001911.summary: A funerary epitaph for Fabricius, aged 20, erected by his parents, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23001913.text_edition: \n Antoniae annorum X Marcus Antonius urculus matri \n
EDCS-23001913.translation: To Antonia, aged 10. Marcus Antonius Urculus for his mother.
EDCS-23001913.summary: A funerary epitaph for Antonia, aged 10, likely erected by her relative Marcus Antonius Urculus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23001915.summary: A funerary epitaph simply stating the burial of Sergius Gracilis, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23001916.summary: A funerary epitaph fragment mentioning 'Helena' and 'Graeca', likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23002314.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Vergilia Philomusia.

EDCS-23002353.summary: A tombstone fragment that likely records the name of Gaius Valerius Verdullus.

EDCS-23002355.summary: An inscription that mentions Gaius Valerius Verdullus as a painter, possibly part of a funerary monument.

EDCS-23002418.text_edition: \n ------ A]nnio Quirina ano annorum XII Quinti Anni Firmani filio Aemiliae Avianae uxori Aemilius mulieris libertus \n
EDCS-23002418.translation: [---] of the Annii tribe, aged 12 years, son of Quintus Annius Firmanus and Aemilia Aviana, freedman of Aemilius...
EDCS-23002418.summary: A fragmented funerary inscription for a boy named [---]nius, providing his age and family connections.

EDCS-23002421.text_edition: \n Gaio Cornelio Gaius Porcius Baebia CA\n
EDCS-23002421.translation: Gaius Porcius for Gaius Cornelius, Baebia...
EDCS-23002421.summary: A fragmentary inscription likely commemorating Gaius Cornelius, with Gaius Porcius and Baebia involved.

EDCS-23300240.summary: A very fragmentary tombstone recording the burial of someone whose name is mostly lost.

EDCS-23900078.text_edition: \n Valerio Tyrmo Valeria Eraste marito fecit\n
EDCS-23900078.translation: Valeria Eraste made [this] for her husband Valerius Tyrmo.
EDCS-23900078.summary: A tombstone erected by Valeria Eraste in honor of her husband Valerius Tyrmo.

EDCS-23900089.text_edition: \n ------ Proc]ulus Spantamicus Laus forum sternundum de sua pecunia curavit\n
EDCS-23900089.translation: [---] Proculus Spantamicus took care to pave the forum at his own expense.
EDCS-23900089.summary: A public works inscription recording Proculus Spantamicus's paving of a forum at his own expense.

EDCS-23900250.text_edition: \n ------ sa]crum Talus Medami filius solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-23900250.translation: [---] Sacrum, son of Talus Medamus, paid freely and deservedly.
EDCS-23900250.summary: A dedication or payment record where Sacrum, son of Talus Medamus, fulfills an obligation.

EDCS-23900260.text_edition: \n Bassio Restituto Diogenes Castus Liberalis Flavianus Dionysus\n
EDCS-23900260.translation: Bassius Restitutus, Diogenes, Castus, Liberalis, Dionysus...
EDCS-23900260.summary: A list of names, possibly of members of a collegium or beneficiaries of a monument, on a fragmentary inscription.

EDCS-23900263.text_edition: \n Antestius Sesenco Paterni filius annorum XX hic situs est\n
EDCS-23900263.translation: Antestius Sesenco, son of Paternus, aged 20, is buried here.
EDCS-23900263.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Antestius Sesenco, aged 20, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24400053.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Marcus Cornelius Laetinus, aged 27. From his dearest wife, Cornelia Hispana.
EDCS-24400053.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Marcus Cornelius Laetinus by his wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24400079.summary: A funerary inscription that names Segilo, son of Elarcus, with an incomplete record, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24400080.translation: Boutia Uquilonca, daughter of Segilius, aged 50.
EDCS-24400080.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Boutia Uquilonca, daughter of Segilius, aged 50, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24400081.translation: To Alionius, son of Areiecus, son of Camalus.
EDCS-24400081.summary: An inscription that names Alionius, son of Areiecus, son of Camalus, possibly a funerary dedication or honorary, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24400082.translation: To Ambata Medica, daughter of Veratus, aged 60.
EDCS-24400082.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Ambata Medica, daughter of Veratus, aged 60, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24400083.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Lucretia Primitiva, aged 40, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24400084.translation: To Segilo Aespanco, son of Mambata, aged 80.
EDCS-24400084.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Segilo Aespanco, son of Mambata, aged 80, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24400085.translation: To Ambato Burgae, son of Segilius, aged 30.
EDCS-24400085.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Ambato Burgae, son of Segilius, aged 30, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24400086.translation: To Quemia Iacometa, daughter of Ligirius, aged 70.
EDCS-24400086.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Quemia Iacometa, daughter of Ligirius, aged 70, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24400087.translation: To Gaius Caelalion Bassicus, freedman, aged 75.
EDCS-24400087.summary: A funerary inscription for the freedman Gaius Caelalion Bassicus, who lived to be 75 years old.

EDCS-24400088.translation: To Ambata Peditaga, daughter of Ambatus, aged 52.
EDCS-24400088.summary: A funerary inscription for Ambata Peditaga, daughter of Ambatus, who died at the age of 52.

EDCS-24400089.translation: To Fabio Petaco of Ambata, aged 71.
EDCS-24400089.summary: A funerary inscription for Fabio Petaco of Ambata, who died at the age of 71.

EDCS-24400090.translation: To Muccius Torivianus, son of Vivius.
EDCS-24400090.summary: A funerary inscription for Muccius Torivianus, son of Vivius.

EDCS-24400091.translation: To the Divine Manes. Titus, his father, had this made for his most dutiful son, Aurelius Maternus, aged 20. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-24400091.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelius Maternus, aged 20, dedicated by his grieving father Titus, indicating a high degree of filial piety.

EDCS-24400092.translation: Quemia Magiaena, freedwoman of Quietus, lies here, aged 80.
EDCS-24400092.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Quemia Magiaena, a freedwoman of Quietus, at the age of 80.

EDCS-24400093.translation: To Lucius Sempronius Pupillus, veteran, aged 80.
EDCS-24400093.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Sempronius Pupillus, a veteran, who lived to be 80.

EDCS-24400094.summary: A funerary inscription for Secontia, daughter of Aelius, who died at the age of 55.

EDCS-24400096.translation: To Metellius Reburrus, son of Ambatus, aged 15.
EDCS-24400096.summary: A funerary inscription for Metellius Reburrus, son of Ambatus, who died at the young age of 15.

EDCS-24400097.translation: To Horatius Alion, son of Ligirus, the deserving, aged 50.
EDCS-24400097.summary: A funerary inscription for Horatius Alion, son of Ligirus, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-24500023.translation: To the shades below. [Name]nis, aged 27 years, [X months and] 20 days. Lucifer [named her] most dutiful. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-24500023.summary: This is a tombstone for a young woman named [Name]nis, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period, with an epitaph expressing a wish for her peace in the afterlife.

EDCS-24500074.translation: From the workshop of Cirinus and Victorinus.
EDCS-24500074.summary: This inscription records the origin of a product, likely pottery or metalwork, from the workshop of Cirinus and Victorinus, probably dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24500864.text_edition: \n Caio Calvisio Sabino consuli VII viro epulonum legato pro praetore patrono ex decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-24500864.translation: To Gaius Calvisius Sabinus, consul for the seventh time, member of the college of epulones, legatus pro praetore, patron, by decree of the town councillors.
EDCS-24500864.summary: This is an honorary inscription dedicated to Gaius Calvisius Sabinus, a prominent Roman official and patron, likely dating to the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-24500865.text_edition: \n Marco Licinio Crasso Frugi pontifici sodali Augustali consuli patrono decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-24500865.translation: To Marcus Licinius Crassus Frugi, pontifex, sodalis Augustalis, consul, patron, by decree of the town councillors.
EDCS-24500865.summary: This honorary inscription honors Marcus Licinius Crassus Frugi, a distinguished Roman citizen holding multiple prestigious religious and political offices, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24500869.text_edition: \n Marco Porcio Marci filio Pupinia Caesaris Augusti scribae Segobrigenses patrono\n
EDCS-24500869.translation: To Marcus Porcius, son of Marcus, of the Pupinia tribe, scribe of the town of Segobriga, patron.
EDCS-24500869.summary: This is an honorary inscription for Marcus Porcius, identified by his tribal affiliation and his role as a scribe in the Spanish town of Segobriga, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24500870.text_edition: \n C(aio) Iulio Cai filio Galeria Montano IIIIviro bis flamini divi Augusti decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-24500870.translation: To Gaius Julius Montanus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, IIIIvir, twice flamen of the deified Augustus, by decree of the town councillors.
EDCS-24500870.summary: This honorary inscription recognizes Gaius Julius Montanus, a local magistrate and priest from a Roman town, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24500874.summary: This inscription seems to record the names of two individuals, Galeria Callionis and Lucius Iulius, potentially as donors or beneficiaries of a monument, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24500876.translation: To Titus Mollicus Severus, from his father.
EDCS-24500876.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by a father to his son, Titus Mollicus Severus, likely a tombstone or dedication of a monument, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24500879.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription likely recording a dedication or a name, with a partial patronymic, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24500888.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of an individual named Retugena, likely from a Roman province, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24500895.translation: To Fortuna Natalis, Lucius fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-24500895.summary: A votive inscription to Fortuna Natalis, dedicated by Lucius.

EDCS-24500896.translation: Gaius H. E. fulfilled his vow willingly.
EDCS-24500896.summary: A dedication by Gaius H. E., who fulfilled his vow.

EDCS-24500897.translation: Pusinna, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-24500897.summary: A fragmentary inscription indicating Pusinna fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-24500911.summary: A funerary epitaph for Amoena, placed by Avelicum.

EDCS-24500912.summary: A fragmentary inscription that likely records the name and parentage of Evandius.

EDCS-24500913.summary: A fragmentary dedication, likely from Porcius Secundus to Porcia Urbana.

EDCS-24500915.summary: A very brief inscription that likely identifies an owner or dedicator named Privatus, possibly from Litica.

EDCS-24500929.text_edition: \n Fortunae pro salute Manius Cassius Caecilianus votum solvit libens\n
EDCS-24500929.translation: For the safety of Manius Cassius Caecilianus, he willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-24500929.summary: An inscription recording Manius Cassius Caecilianus fulfilling a vow for his own well-being.

EDCS-24500948.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Placidae Satur atavus eiusdem et Thallusae contubernali\n
EDCS-24500948.translation: To the Divine Manes. Placida. Her great-grandfather and Thallusa, her fellow soldier.
