EDCS-46400787.summary: This is a complex epitaph written in elegiac verse, mourning the deceased Nigella, a servant, who expresses her acceptance of death and desire for peace, and addresses her surviving family and a poet.

EDCS-46400848.translation: Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of the deified Augustus, grandson of the deified Julius, pontifex maximus, consul for the fifth time, imperator for the eighth time, holding tribunician power for the 34th year, [from] Carthage [distance in] miles 230(?).
EDCS-46400848.summary: This is a milestone inscription recording Tiberius Caesar's titles, consulships, military commands, and tribunician power, along with a distance marker from Carthage.

EDCS-46600210.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph recording the name of Avinta, described as a dutiful wife.

EDCS-46600216.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely indicating a workshop owner or location, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-49300100.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or epitaph, naming Publius Iulius Eroti, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-49300112.translation: To Quintus Calpurnius Flavus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, as aedile, duovir, and flamen of Rome and of the Augusti.
EDCS-49300112.summary: This inscription records the public offices held by Quintus Calpurnius Flavus, including aedile and duovir, likely from the Roman Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-49300178.translation: Crise, slave of Marcus O. P. [---]
EDCS-49300178.summary: This inscription records the name of a slave, Crise, belonging to Marcus O. P.

EDCS-49300428.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Aelius Atticiano, a good son, aged 20 [---], Aelius Attia[n]o set up (this) at request. [---]
EDCS-49300428.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aelius Atticiano, a young son, by his father Aelius Attiano.

EDCS-49700018.translation: To Lucius Aelius Polycletus, decurion, dearest son, from his mother.
EDCS-49700018.summary: This is a fragmentary epitaph for Lucius Aelius Polycletus, a decurion, dedicated by his mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-49700019.translation: To Lucius Bennius Primigenius, a most excellent husband, from Quintia Procula and for herself.
EDCS-49700019.summary: This is an epitaph dedicated by Quintia Procula to her husband Lucius Bennius Primigenius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-49700061.translation: Son of Lucius, grandson of Marcus.
EDCS-49700061.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription providing a man's filiation and agnatic descent, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-49700095.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely identifying an individual named Paris(s)ius Minicius, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-49700102.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly identifying an individual or a relationship, naming Tettius Princeps, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-49700103.summary: This inscription records the name Tettius Princeps, possibly associated with Secundi.

EDCS-49700118.summary: This inscription contains the name Mara() and a fragmented phrase, possibly part of a dedication or title.

EDCS-49700128.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Calia Sabata.

EDCS-49900078.translation: To our pious and renowned lord Constantius, most noble, most brave, and most fortunate Caesar, Badius Macrinus, a most perfect man, praeses of the province of Hispania Tarraconensis, devoted always to his numen and majesty.
EDCS-49900078.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Caesar Constantius by Badius Macrinus, governor of Hispania Tarraconensis, likely dating to his period of governance.

EDCS-49900095.text_edition: \n Tito Flavio Titiano legato Auggustorum nnostrorum pro praetore clarissimo viro proconsuli provinciae Africae praesidi provinciae Hispaniae citerioris Marcus Aurelius Modestinus \n
EDCS-49900095.translation: Marcus Aurelius Modestinus, to Titus Flavius Titianus, legatus of our Augusti pro praetore, a most distinguished man, proconsul of the province of Africa, praeses of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-49900095.summary: This is an inscription honoring Titus Flavius Titianus, who held multiple high-ranking provincial administrative positions, including proconsul of Africa and praeses of Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-50702315.translation: To the Eternal God, Lucretius Valens, according to his vow.
EDCS-50702315.summary: This is a votive dedication to the god 'Aeternus' by Lucretius Valens.

EDCS-51400136.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Julius Stolico, [a] well-deserving husband.
EDCS-51400136.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Julius Stolico, identified as a husband, by his wife.

EDCS-51400886.translation: Retugeni, son of Moricus, is buried here.
EDCS-51400886.summary: This is a funerary inscription stating that Retugenus, son of Moricus, is buried here.

EDCS-51400887.translation: Marcus Calpurnius Auctus, father, to his most dutiful son, Marcus Calpurnius Hermetus.
EDCS-51400887.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a father, Marcus Calpurnius Auctus, to his son, Marcus Calpurnius Hermetus.

EDCS-51400888.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Fonteius Bubalus, to his chaste wife, Fonteia Iucunda.
EDCS-51400888.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Fonteius Bubalus to his wife, Fonteia Iucunda.

EDCS-51400889.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Servenia Paulina, his most beloved wife, who deserved well of him, Lucius Publilius Glycerus.
EDCS-51400889.summary: A funerary dedication by Lucius Publilius Glycerus to his wife Servenia Paulina, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-51400890.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Publicia Attice, his chaste wife, Publicius Corinthus.
EDCS-51400890.summary: A funerary dedication by Publicius Corinthus to his wife Publicia Attice, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-51900511.translation: Lucius Antonius is buried here. His freedmen made (this).
EDCS-51900511.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Antonius, erected by his freedmen, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-51900520.text_edition: \n Lucretia Crispini filia annorum XI Lucretius Crispinus hic siti sunt\n
EDCS-51900520.translation: Lucretia, daughter of Crispinus, aged 11. Lucretius Crispinus are buried here.
EDCS-51900520.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Lucretia and Lucretius Crispinus, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-51900522.summary: A fragmentary inscription that likely records the name of Plotia, possibly a funerary monument, from the imperial period.

EDCS-52300943.translation: Monument. To the Divine Spirits. He placed this monument to Reburinia, his most beloved wife, aged 55. In the consulship of 377 (years since founding of Rome?); may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-52300943.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by an unknown person to his wife Reburinia, dated to the Roman Republic or early Empire by the consulship notation.

EDCS-53000089.translation: Aelia Ma[...], daughter of Montanus(?), is buried here.
EDCS-53000089.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription recording the burial of Aelia Ma[...], likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-53000092.translation: Aemilius(?), to his wife [...], a title for memory.
EDCS-53000092.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription possibly dedicating a memorial to a wife, from the imperial period.

EDCS-53000095.translation: The fellow members of the Atrium Mistico contributed 200 sesterces.
EDCS-53000095.summary: A record of a contribution made by members of a collegium or association named Atrium Mistico, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-53000099.translation: To Caecilius Victor, freedman of Caecilius Isargyrus; Caecilia Contaiza for her husband and herself and her family and her daughter Eutichia.
EDCS-53000099.summary: A funerary dedication by Caecilia Contaiza for her husband Caecilius Victor and other family members, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-53000103.translation: Here lie Clodia Marcella, daughter of Festus, aged 20, and Manius Aurelius Serenus, aged 2 years and 9 days. Aurelius Turranus had them made for his wife and son.
EDCS-53000103.summary: This is a tombstone for Clodia Marcella and Aurelius Serenus, set up by Aurelius Turranus, likely her husband and father of Serenus, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-53000104.translation: Titus Cornelius Amphio, freedman of Hyacinthus.
EDCS-53000104.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Cornelius Amphio, a freedman, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-53000105.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Dusquena Accia, possibly with formulaic phrases for the deceased, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-53000107.translation: Exorata, slave of Iulia Phoebilla, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Stephanus [made this] for his companion.
EDCS-53000107.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Exorata, a slave, set up by her partner Stephanus, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-53000110.translation: Parthenius, freedman of Primulus, lies here, aged 14 years. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-53000110.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Parthenius, a freedman, who died at a young age, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-53000112.translation: [Epitaph for] Iulia Antiochis, daughter of Marcus Iulius [---].
EDCS-53000112.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for a woman named Iulia Antiochis, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-53000114.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iullus, a freedman, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-53000117.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph recording the burial of Magonia Restituta, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-53000118.translation: Matira, freedwoman of Lucius and Nepos Valerius, lies here. Caecilius Victor and Contaita [set this up] for their mother.
EDCS-53000118.summary: This is a tombstone for Matira, a freedwoman, erected by Caecilius Victor and Contaita, likely her children, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-53000122.translation: Mustelia made [this] for Pulicio, son of Domitius Mieritus and Statutus, aged 20.
EDCS-53000122.summary: This is a funerary monument for Pulicio, erected by Mustelia, possibly his wife or relative, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-53000124.translation: Restitutus, slave of Gaius Grattius Glaucus, aged 30, is buried here. Amabilis made this and saw to its construction for himself. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-53000124.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Restitutus, a slave, erected by Amabilis, who also arranged for his own burial, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-53000126.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph possibly for a woman named Sempronia Corinthilla, with the beginning of a dedicator's name, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-53000131.translation: Turnia, slave of Buccon, is buried here. Surilioni Primula made this.
EDCS-53000131.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Turnia, a slave, erected by Surilioni Primula, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-53000133.translation: [Epitaph] for Valeria [name illegible], daughter of <f=E>ilia<e=I> (likely Aelia), and for himself. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-53000133.summary: This is a fragmentary epitaph, possibly funerary, mentioning Valeria and a dedicator, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-53000134.translation: Lucius Valerius [erected this] for Lampas' daughter, Valeria Prepus[a] Herma, and for himself. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-53000134.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Prepus[a] Herma, erected by Lucius Valerius for her and himself, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-53000135.translation: The fellow members of the association contributed 300 sesterces to Valerius Rufinus.
EDCS-53000135.summary: This is a record of a financial contribution made by a collegium (association) to Valerius Rufinus, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-53000140.summary: This is a fragmentary epitaph for a young man named Victor, likely funerary, with part of a dedicator's name, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-53000278.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph, possibly for someone named Seranus, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-53000282.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly a funerary epitaph, with partial names, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-53000283.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph with parts of two names, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-53000299.summary: This fragment appears to be the beginning of a dedication or epitaph, possibly mentioning the name Gaius Orestes, and is likely Roman in date.

EDCS-53000303.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records the burial of a slave belonging to someone named Marcus, with a standard funerary formula, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-53000313.summary: This very fragmented inscription may contain names like Auguste and Torquatus, and its context remains unclear, likely Roman.

EDCS-53000356.translation: To Sextilia M[ater?], Manioquum, Valerius, son of Marcus […], Latro, to [his] mother...
EDCS-53000356.summary: This inscription seems to be a dedication from Valerius Latro, son of Marcus, to his mother Sextilia, likely Roman.

EDCS-53000369.translation: To Zeus the Most High God, Gaius Iulius Silvanus founded this sacred precinct according to his vow.
EDCS-53000369.summary: This Greek inscription records the dedication of a sacred precinct by Gaius Iulius Silvanus to Zeus Hypsistos, indicating a votive offering, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-53200182.summary: This is the beginning of a dedication to Titus Sempronius, likely from the Roman Republican or Imperial period.

EDCS-53200187.summary: This fragment begins a dedication to Dis Pater, the Roman god of the underworld, likely part of a funerary monument.

EDCS-53200203.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Memmia Potita lived 56 years. For her most devoted mother, her children made this.
EDCS-53200203.summary: This epitaph records the death of Memmia Potita after 56 years, dedicated by her children to their devoted mother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-53200208.summary: This inscription likely records the name Epictetus, son of Lucius Pedanius Clementis, possibly a dedication or part of a funerary monument from the Roman period.

EDCS-53200222.translation: To Tocuon, son of Caeno, aged 30, he lies here. May the earth be light for you. His father and brother placed this marker at their own expense.
EDCS-53200222.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Tocuon, son of Caeno, aged 30, with a wish for his peaceful rest and a note that his father and brother erected the marker, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-53300294.summary: This inscription appears to be a fragmentary funerary text for slaves or freedmen, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-53300295.translation: Laureolus, son of Severus, aged 20, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-53300295.summary: This is a tombstone for Laureolus, who died at the age of 20, dating to the Roman Imperial period, and includes a common funerary wish.

EDCS-53300296.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription for Apicula, daughter of Valerius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-53300297.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription indicating the presence of Valerianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-54900653.text_edition: \n Sexto Pompeio Sedatino Aquensi ex provincia Aquitanica seviro Augustali Apu[leius ------\n
EDCS-54900653.translation: To Sextus Pompeius Sedatinus of Aquis, from the province of Aquitania, Augustal Sevire, Apuleius [restored name]...
EDCS-54900653.summary: This is a dedication from a colleague, Apuleius, to Sextus Pompeius Sedatinus, an Augustal Sevire from Aquitania, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-55200061.translation: ...ranus fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-55200061.summary: This is a votive inscription fragment recording the fulfillment of a vow by an individual named [-]ranus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-55701562.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan of Quintus B() Propinquus, who lived for 20 years, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-55701709.summary: This is a very brief funerary marker, possibly indicating the burial place of Eutyches, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-55701710.inscription_type: carmina, tituli fabricationis, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-55701710.translation: Of Aries, of Taurus, to Gemini, of Leo, of Cancer, of Capricorn, of Scorpio, of Virgo, of Aquarius, of Sagittarius, of Libra, of Pisces. Human beings are born under these signs and stars. Gaius Valerius Verdullus paints.
EDCS-55701710.summary: This inscription lists the twelve signs of the zodiac, states that humans are born under these celestial influences, and is signed by the artist Gaius Valerius Verdullus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-55701732.translation: To Marcus Allius Avitus, son of Marcus, of the Collina tribe, a veteran, and the veterans, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-55701732.summary: This is a dedication by veterans, likely on behalf of the decurions, for Marcus Allius Avitus, a veteran of the Collina tribe, probably dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-55701736.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Cypris, aged 30.
EDCS-55701736.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Cypris, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-55701739.translation: Of Quintus Vireius, son of Caius.
EDCS-55701739.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely recording the name and parentage of an individual, Quintus Vireius.

EDCS-56500225.summary: This is a short inscription dedicating a monument or offering to the goddess Venus.

EDCS-56500228.translation: Procula, aged 13, is buried here. Clemen(s) made this.
EDCS-56500228.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Procula, aged 13, with the maker identified as Clemen(s).

EDCS-56800022.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Caius Fabius Felix, decurion of Tarraco. Numisia Ursa for her excellent husband and Didius Honoratus for his deserving uncle. He lived 50 years.
EDCS-56800022.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Fabius Felix, a decurion from Tarraco, set up by his wife Numisia Ursa and his uncle Didius Honoratus, who died at age 50.

EDCS-56800024.translation: For Lucius Valerius Tempstivus, father of Valerius Gallus. Valeria Silvana made this for her husband. Quintius Flaccus, her uncle, and Valeria Verana, her mother-in-law, her heirs, having bought back a portion of Valerius Avitus, whose price Valeria Silvana paid, placed this in the forum, found in the house.
EDCS-56800024.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription for Lucius Valerius Tempstivus, set up by his wife Valeria Silvana and her relatives, detailing the repurchase of a portion of the property and its placement in the forum.

EDCS-56800083.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, sacred. For Lucius Caecilius Laitanus, his most dutiful son. He lived 18 years, 5 months, 21 days. His father Caecilius Apuleius [made this].
EDCS-56800083.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Caecilius Laitanus, who died at age 18 years, 5 months, and 21 days, dedicated by his father.

EDCS-56800084.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Calpurnius...
EDCS-56800084.summary: This is a very fragmented funerary inscription beginning with 'To the spirits of the underworld' and mentioning the name Calpurnius.

EDCS-56800088.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Claudia Hygeia, their deserving mother. Her sons made this.
EDCS-56800088.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Claudia Hygeia, dedicated by her sons.

EDCS-56800117.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Ulpius Oliaster. His wife Aemilia Salviana made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-56800117.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ulpius Oliaster, dedicated by his wife Aemilia Salviana.

EDCS-56800150.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records the construction of a monument by an unknown dedicator.

EDCS-57200140.text_edition: \n ioniqum et Amiae coniugi sit terra levis IIose faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-57200140.translation: May the earth lie lightly on Ionicus and his wife Amia. Iose had this made.
EDCS-57200140.summary: This epitaph records a funerary monument for Ionicus and Amia, commissioned by Iose, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-57900004.translation: Publicius Verecundus, in accordance with his vow.
EDCS-57900004.summary: This inscription records a dedication made in fulfillment of a vow by Publicius Verecundus, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-57900006.translation: Sacred to Amma. Amata, in accordance with her vow.
EDCS-57900006.summary: This votive inscription dedicates something to Amma by Amata, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-57900027.translation: ___, son of Fortunatus, aged 20, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-57900027.summary: This epitaph records the burial of a young man named Fortunatus, from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-57900028.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Annius Troilus to his most dutiful father, Gaius Annius Politicus, aged 85, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-57900028.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Gaius Annius Politicus, commemorated by his son Publius Annius Troilus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-57900029.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly a name or part of a dedication, from the Roman period.

EDCS-57900050.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, potentially a name or part of a text, from the Roman period.

EDCS-57900051.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely recording one or two individuals named Lucius Sempronius, from the Roman period.

EDCS-57900063.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly recording names or titles, from the Roman period.

EDCS-57900073.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription likely recording the name of a deceased individual.

EDCS-57900103.summary: This is a fragmented inscription that likely records the name of an individual and their father.

EDCS-58800009.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Tyche, aged 60, lies here. Porcius made this for his most beloved tent-mate and wife. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-58800009.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Tyche, dedicating it to his wife and tent-mate, Porcius, likely from the Roman military.

EDCS-58800012.translation: Fuscus, son of Anicaius, willingly paid his vows to the household gods of Caeceae and Ienae.
EDCS-58800012.summary: This inscription records a vow paid by Fuscus, son of Anicaius, to local household deities.

EDCS-59500200.summary: This is a highly fragmented inscription that may refer to a mother named Alla Cam[---].

EDCS-59500201.summary: This fragmented inscription possibly names an individual, Utia[tus?], and includes other unrecoverable text.

EDCS-59500204.summary: This fragment likely refers to a person of significance, Marcus Aemilius Lepidus.

EDCS-60300112.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Arcia Quartina made [this] for her most indulgent husband, P. [Arcio?], who lived 42 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-60300112.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a husband, to whom his wife Arcia Quartina dedicated a monument.

EDCS-60300259.translation: To the god Bero Briecoco[---]. Vale. M[---]
EDCS-60300259.summary: This is a fragmented inscription dedicating something to the god Bero Briecoco, likely a local deity.

EDCS-60300261.translation: To the god Bero. I set up this altar for [my] health.
EDCS-60300261.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating an altar to the god Bero for the dedicator's health.

EDCS-60300267.translation: Lucius Cassius [name] was victorious as a retiarius.
EDCS-60300267.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a gladiator named Lucius Cassius, who was a retiarius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60300294.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Antonio Sollemne, aged 70 [years], is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-60300294.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonio Sollemne, recording his age and a wish for his peaceful rest, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60300305.summary: This is a dedication or memorial inscription to the divine Drusilla, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60300307.translation: To Aelia Tranquilla, daughter of Gaius, in accordance with her will.
EDCS-60300307.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelia Tranquilla, established by her will, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60300310.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Eugenia, aged 20 years, Primulus and Metrodora, an incomparable slave, [restored text]...
EDCS-60300310.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Eugenia, recording her age and a dedication by Primulus and Metrodora for their 'incomparable' slave, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60300311.translation: Statius, aged 30 years, is buried here.
EDCS-60300311.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph for Statius, recording his age and burial place, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60300312.translation: Lucius Valerius Val[erius/ius] is buried here.
EDCS-60300312.summary: This is a partial funerary epitaph for Lucius Valerius, indicating his burial, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60300323.translation: Sempronia, daughter of Gaius, [name/title], Marcus Junius, son of Marcus, Ga[...], [name/title]...
EDCS-60300323.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to record the names and parentage of at least two individuals, possibly related to a funerary monument, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60400038.translation: Calaetus, son of Serenus, aged 50 years.
EDCS-60400038.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Calaetus, son of Serenus, recording his age, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60600001.translation: Nigrinus, son of Beisunis, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Fabianus, son of Nigrinus, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-60600001.summary: This is a double funerary epitaph recording the burial of Nigrinus and his son Fabianus, with standard wishes for their repose, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60600002.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Primus, aged [3] years, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-60600002.summary: A funerary epitaph for Primus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60600003.translation: Flavina placed this for her son, Pompeius Candidus, in his own plot.
EDCS-60600003.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Flavina for her son, Pompeius Candidus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60600005.translation: Licinia Rufa, freedwoman of Marcus. Oceanus, freedman of Marcus. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-60600005.summary: A funerary epitaph mentioning two freedpersons of Marcus, Licinia Rufa and Oceanus, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60700129.translation: Flavinus, son of Flavius, aged 25 years. His uncle Cornelius and his mother Sunua had this made. He is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-60700129.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavinus, erected by his uncle and mother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60700132.translation: A monument for Cado Cor(nelius) Rib(o?) Vad(iniensis), aged 25 years. Alio/mus dedicated this for his friend, laid here.
EDCS-60700132.summary: A funerary monument for Cado Cornel(ius) Rib(o?) Vad(iniensis) erected by his friend Alio/mus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-61200033.text_edition: \n Tito Aemilio Quinti filio Severo annorum LXXV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Aemiliae Chresime\n
EDCS-61200033.translation: Titus Aemilius Severus, son of Quintus, aged 75 years, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. (Erected by) Aemilia Chresime.
EDCS-61200033.summary: A funerary epitaph for Titus Aemilius Severus, erected by Aemilia Chresime, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-62800009.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sempronius Fabianus, son of [3], aged 81 years, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-62800009.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sempronius Fabianus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64000110.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sacred to the departed spirits. Aelius Attianus, aged 20 [3] years. Aelius Attianus erected (this) by request.
EDCS-64000110.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aelius Attianus, erected by request by someone named Aelius Attianus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64000112.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Valerio Luppiano. Valeria [3]a, mother? [
EDCS-64000112.summary: An incomplete funerary epitaph mentioning Valerio Luppiano and possibly his mother Valeria, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64000115.translation: Of Lucius Cornelius Celsus. Of Lucius Cornelius Celsus.
EDCS-64000115.summary: An incomplete inscription indicating ownership or dedication to Lucius Cornelius Celsus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64000121.translation: To Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Valerianus Gallienus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, father of the fatherland, consul.
EDCS-64000121.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Gallienus, likely from the 3rd century CE, honoring his imperial titles.

EDCS-64100266.translation: Hyacintus the warehouse keeper rests here.
EDCS-64100266.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Hyacintus, a warehouse keeper, from the Roman period.

EDCS-64500435.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Valerianus, 18 years old, rests here.
EDCS-64500435.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valerianus, who died at the age of 18, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64500436.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Verrius [died] at 80 years.
EDCS-64500436.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, likely for Verrius, noting his age at death, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64600288.summary: This is a short inscription likely indicating a patron-client relationship or familial tie, possibly from the Roman period.

EDCS-64600374.translation: Greetings, Aurunculeia Quarta, daughter of Spurius.
EDCS-64600374.summary: This is a personal greeting or dedication to Aurunculeia Quarta, daughter of Spurius, from the Roman period.

EDCS-64600386.translation: Of Lucius Cornelius Celsus. Of Lucius Cornelius Celsus.
EDCS-64600386.summary: This inscription is a repetitive mention of Lucius Cornelius Celsus, possibly marking ownership or dedication of a monument.

EDCS-64600427.translation: To the departed spirits. Aemilius Valentinus made this for Julia Afrania, his most beloved wife, aged 40.
EDCS-64600427.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Aemilius Valentinus for his wife Julia Afrania, who died at 40, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64600436.summary: This inscription records the freedman Napenis, possibly identifying his former master and his own name, from the Roman period.

EDCS-64600487.translation: To the departed spirits. Sempronia Alla for her most dutiful son, Marcus Aemilius Reburrus, 18 years old, and for herself, aged 50.
EDCS-64600487.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Sempronia Alla for her son Marcus Aemilius Reburrus (aged 18) and for herself (aged 50), from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64600500.summary: This inscription likely records the name of a person, possibly Calpurnius, on an unspecified monument.

EDCS-64700296.translation: Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus Germanicus Maximus Dacicus Maximus Sarmaticus Maximus, Pontifex Maximus, holder of tribunician power for the 5th time, Imperator for the 7th time, Father of the Fatherland, Consul, Proconsul, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, the most noble Caesar, Germanicus Maximus, Dacicus Maximus, Sarmaticus Maximus, Prince of the Youth, son of our lord Emperor Julius Verus Maximinus.
EDCS-64700296.summary: This inscription details the full titulature and titles of Emperors Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus Caesar, likely dating to their joint rule around 236-238 AD.

EDCS-64700461.summary: This inscription records the name of Gaius Marius Melissus, possibly as the owner or maker of an object or monument.

EDCS-64700493.summary: This inscription begins with the name Seranus, followed by an incompletely preserved word, likely a placename or tribal affiliation.

EDCS-64800362.translation: [Son of] Seranus, aged 15. May the earth lie light upon you.
EDCS-64800362.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a 15-year-old boy, son of Seranus, indicating his burial and expressing a wish for the deceased.

EDCS-64800367.translation: From the workshop of Gaius Fulvius Paternus. [3 letters]
EDCS-64800367.summary: This inscription indicates the maker or owner of an object, identifying it as coming from the workshop of Gaius Fulvius Paternus.

EDCS-64900636.translation: Aburnus(?) the husband placed this monument for his wife.
EDCS-64900636.summary: This inscription records a husband, possibly named Aburnus, placing a monument for his wife.

EDCS-64900653.translation: To the Infernal Deities, the Manes. Patern(a) placed the statue as a memorial, aged 60. May she rest here.
EDCS-64900653.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Patern(a), who died at the age of 60, recording the placement of a statue in her memory.

EDCS-64900657.translation: Sacred to the Manes. Agrippa placed this for his deserving wife Felicula, who lived 76 years.
EDCS-64900657.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Agrippa erected a monument for his wife Felicula, who lived to be 76 years old.

EDCS-65000079.summary: This inscription likely records the name of an individual, possibly Gaius T. Gra[...], holding the office of aedile.

EDCS-65000080.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Pomponius Ianuarius, son of Abiutel, lived 21 years. Octavius Quintinus, his brother, erected this.
EDCS-65000080.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pomponius Ianuarius, erected by his brother, dating likely to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-65600126.translation: Acca Ambatica, aged 93 years. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-65600126.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Acca Ambatica, dating likely to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-66000068.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly funerary, with the name Lucceius Salvianus.

EDCS-66000071.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Fortunata lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-66000071.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fortunata, dating likely to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-66000072.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, recording the age of a person whose name is mostly lost.

EDCS-66000076.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Sacred to Dugidio...
EDCS-66000076.summary: This is a funerary inscription, sacred to Dugidio, dating likely to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-66000087.translation: [...]dura, daughter of Virotus, erected this for her husband.
EDCS-66000087.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a woman named Dura for her husband, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-66000089.translation: Tertulla lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-66000089.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tertulla, dating likely to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-66000091.translation: Lucius Cornelius Hispanius Cluniensis, aged 45 years, lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-66000091.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Cornelius Hispanius Cluniensis, dating likely to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-66000104.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Sacred. [Son?] of Lucius... aged 55 years. Iul[...][...] lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-66000104.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a man named Iulius, son of Lucius, aged 55, dating likely to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-66000107.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Caecilia Musis, for her most devoted husband Geminius Maternus, aged [3] years. May the earth lie light upon him.
EDCS-66000107.summary: This is a tombstone inscription dedicated by a wife to her deceased husband, providing his name and age.

EDCS-66000109.summary: This is a fragmentary tombstone inscription, likely recording the name and age of the deceased.

EDCS-66000118.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, possibly a funerary monument, commemorating Gaius Aelius Propinquus.

EDCS-66000124.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely a funerary monument, commemorating Quintus Appidius Clarus.

EDCS-66700082.translation: Lucretius Hermes fulfills his vow to Mars willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-66700082.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Lucretius Hermes fulfilling a vow to the god Mars.

EDCS-67100012.translation: To the unconquered, most indulgent, most devout Emperor Caesar Lucius Domitius Aurelianus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power.
EDCS-67100012.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Aurelian, listing his titles and acknowledging his divine virtues.

EDCS-67100057.translation: Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of the deified Augustus, grandson of the deified Julius, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power for the 35th time, imperator for the 9th time, consul for the 5th time, [from Augustobriga] 13 [miles].
EDCS-67100057.summary: This is an imperial dedication by Tiberius Caesar Augustus, detailing his tribunician power, military titles, consulships, and a geographical reference.

EDCS-67100070.translation: To the most noble Caesar, born for the good of the state.
EDCS-67100070.summary: This is a dedication to a noble Caesar, praising him for his suitability to govern.

EDCS-67100097.translation: To Emperor Caesar Lucius Domitius Aurelianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, holder of tribunician power, consul for the 3rd time.
EDCS-67100097.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Aurelian, listing his titles and consulships.

EDCS-67100098.translation: To the most noble Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-67100098.summary: This is a dedication to Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus (Constantine the Great), praising his noble status and imperial virtues.

EDCS-67200285.translation: Terentius Maximos and Kastos, from Heliopolis, sons of Quintus Aithibilos, made it.
EDCS-67200285.summary: This is a Greek inscription likely recording the makers of a monument or work, identified as Terentius Maximos and Kastos, originating from Heliopolis.

EDCS-67400292.translation: Anchar(i)us, a slave of the Republic of Veleia, dedicated this to the Mother Goddess.
EDCS-67400292.summary: This Latin inscription records the dedication of a monument by Anchar(i)us, a slave associated with the public affairs of Veleia, to the Mother Goddess.

EDCS-67400293.translation: Sacred to Apollo(?). Pompeia Paulla, daughter of Pullatis, and her heirs, by her will.
EDCS-67400293.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Apollo(?) by Pompeia Paulla and her heirs, fulfilling a testamentary bequest.

EDCS-67400294.translation: To Nymphis(?). Pompeia Paulla, daughter of Pullatis, and her heirs, by her will.
EDCS-67400294.summary: This inscription records a dedication, possibly to Nymphis(?), by Pompeia Paulla and her heirs, according to her will.

EDCS-67400295.translation: To the Mother Goddesses(?), the Tribes(?). Pompeia(?), daughter of Pullatis(?), and her heirs, by her will.
EDCS-67400295.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Mother Goddesses and the Tribes by Pompeia and her heirs, as stipulated in her will.

EDCS-67400296.translation: Sacred(?) to Jupiter(?). Paulla and her heirs, by her will.
EDCS-67400296.summary: This inscription records a sacred dedication, likely to Jupiter, made by Paulla and her heirs according to her will.

EDCS-67400298.translation: An altar to the divine power of Silvanus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-67400298.summary: This inscription records an altar dedicated to Silvanus, fulfilling a vow, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-67900815.translation: To Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of the deified [Augustus], grandson of the deified [Augustus], Pontifex Maximus, Imperator for the 8th time, holding tribunician power for the 33rd time, consul for the 5th time. Quintus Sempronius Vitulus, son of Lucius, decurion of the cavalry, ordered this to be made by his will.
EDCS-67900815.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Tiberius by Quintus Sempronius Vitulus, a decurion of the cavalry, detailing Tiberius's extensive titles and honors, likely dating to the late 1st century BCE or early 1st century CE.

EDCS-67900816.translation: To Quintus Sempronius Vitulus, decurion of the cavalry of the Taurian ala, sub-prefect of the cohort of Germans. Aesopus, his freedman, erected [this], by his will.
EDCS-67900816.summary: This inscription records the funerary monument for Quintus Sempronius Vitulus, detailing his military career, erected by his freedman Aesopus as per his will, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-68300038.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription beginning with a dedication or ownership statement for Aulus Valerius.

EDCS-68300043.summary: This is a posthumous dedication or ownership mark belonging to Marcus Antonius Adventus.

EDCS-68300097.translation: Quintus Sacar, son of Aulus Vibius Scrofula.
EDCS-68300097.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary or ownership marker for Quintus Sacar, son of Aulus Vibius Scrofula.

EDCS-68400032.translation: To the departed spirits. Elanus, son of Aravus, a native slave of Vadinius, aged twenty-four. Aravus placed [this] for his devoted son, [with] earth leveled over him.
EDCS-68400032.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Elanus, a native slave from Vadinius, aged 24, set up by his father Aravus.

EDCS-68700009.translation: [Belonging to] Priscus, of the two Domitii. [Belonging to] Priscus, [of the two] Domitii.
EDCS-68700009.summary: This inscription seems to indicate ownership or a funerary dedication to someone named Priscus, possibly associated with two individuals named Domitius.

EDCS-68700031.translation: Monument for Tridius Quietus, son of Bedunigus Corai, of Vadiniensis, aged 50. Origenus placed [this] for his uncle. He is buried here.
EDCS-68700031.summary: This is a funerary monument for Tridius Quietus, aged 50, from Vadiniensis, erected by his uncle Origenus.

EDCS-69000050.translation: For Antonia Flavia, daughter of Antonius, aged 30.
EDCS-69000050.summary: This is likely a funerary dedication for Antonia Flavia, daughter of Antonius, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-69000052.summary: This inscription is a dedication, possibly funerary, to two individuals named Elgio and Alebia.

EDCS-69100253.summary: This inscription likely marks the burial or belongs to a person named Ganus who died at the age of 12.

EDCS-69200323.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Marcus Apronius Rufus Nemorius, aged 18. His father Apronius Rufus placed [this] for his son.
EDCS-69200323.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Apronius Rufus Nemorius, aged 18, set up by his father, Apronius Rufus.

EDCS-69200324.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Apronia Rufa, aged 3. Apronius Rufus, her father, placed [this] for his daughter.
EDCS-69200324.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Apronia Rufa, aged 3, set up by her father, Apronius Rufus.

EDCS-69200325.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Rufonia Rufina, aged 25. Her husband, Apronius Rufus, erected this.
EDCS-69200325.summary: A funerary epitaph for Rufonia Rufina, erected by her husband, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-69200326.translation: Soldier of the Legio VII Gemina Felix, century of Favonius, aged 31, lies here. His heir had this made. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-69200326.summary: A funerary epitaph for a soldier of Legio VII Gemina Felix, indicating his unit and age, with a standard hopeful closing phrase, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-69200327.translation: To Gaius Domitius, son of Gaius, aged 11.
EDCS-69200327.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young boy named Gaius Domitius, indicating his age, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-69200328.summary: A funerary epitaph for Arrenus, son of Arcon, indicating his age, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-69200329.translation: To Lentio, son of Tritus, aged [...]
EDCS-69200329.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for Lentio, son of Tritus, indicating his age, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-69200330.translation: To Tritius, son of Asturus, aged 50.
EDCS-69200330.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tritius, son of Asturus, indicating his age, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-69200331.summary: A dedication to the god Mars, likely by a person named Cemennilus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-69200336.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Lucius Sulpicius Ianuarius, a most devoted patron, Lucius Sulpicius Calopudius.
EDCS-69200336.summary: A funerary epitaph honoring Lucius Sulpicius Ianuarius as a patron, erected by Lucius Sulpicius Calopudius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-69200337.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To [name unclear], wife of Sulpicius Ursinus, for his incomparable wife who lived 35 years.
EDCS-69200337.summary: A funerary epitaph for the wife of Sulpicius Ursinus, noting her age and Sulpicius's deep affection, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-69200338.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Valeria Harmoges, wife of Chryseros, accountant for our Emperor Augustus, to his dearest wife.
EDCS-69200338.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Harmoges, wife of Chryseros, an imperial accountant, erected by her husband, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-69200339.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aemilius Largus, aged seventy years, has died.
EDCS-69200339.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aemilius Largus, who died at seventy years old, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-70300254.summary: A dedication to the Nymphs, possibly for a sanctuary or spring, with the rest of the text lost.

EDCS-70300257.summary: A fragment of an inscription, likely a funerary epitaph, mentioning an age and a name, with most of the text illegible.

EDCS-70300259.translation: Cornelia Ebovinna, freedwoman of Marcus, is buried here, aged seventy-five.
EDCS-70300259.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cornelia Ebovinna, a freedwoman, who died at the age of seventy-five, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-70300260.translation: [She is] buried here, aged thirty. To Lupulus, [her husband, for his] well-deserving wife.
EDCS-70300260.summary: A funerary epitaph for a woman, buried at thirty years old, erected by her husband Lupulus for his well-deserving wife.

EDCS-70300263.translation: Felix bought [this] on the Kalends of July.
EDCS-70300263.summary: A record of a purchase by Felix on July 1st, with the object of the purchase and context lost.

EDCS-70300266.translation: To Grattiae Maximillae, daughter of Caius. Lucius Annius Vitulus and Licinia Tranquilla, daughter of Lucius, [her] relatives.
EDCS-70300266.summary: A funerary dedication to Grattiae Maximillae by her relatives Lucius Annius Vitulus and Licinia Tranquilla.

EDCS-70300268.translation: Caius Cornelius Festus is buried here.
EDCS-70300268.summary: A funerary epitaph stating that Caius Cornelius Festus is buried in this location.

EDCS-70300270.translation: Grattia Mauruca, freedwoman of Caius, aged forty years, is buried here. To her sister, by Atimetus.
EDCS-70300270.summary: A funerary epitaph for Grattia Mauruca, a freedwoman who died at forty, erected by her sister Atimetus.

EDCS-70300301.translation: Caius Fulvius Hibericus is buried here.
EDCS-70300301.summary: A funerary epitaph stating that Caius Fulvius Hibericus is buried in this location, with further details lost.

EDCS-70300312.translation: To Quintus Sempronius Vitulus, son of Lucius, decurion of the cavalry of the Taurian ala, subprefect of the cohort of Germans, Aesopus his freedman [erected this].
EDCS-70300312.summary: This is a funerary inscription honouring Quintus Sempronius Vitulus, a Roman military officer, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-70600017.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Sempronius Granus, possibly the recipient of a monument.

EDCS-70600088.summary: This is a name inscription, likely for a tombstone or a dedicatory plaque.

EDCS-70600089.summary: This is a name inscription, likely for a tombstone or a dedicatory plaque.

EDCS-70600090.translation: Marcus Fabius Novus and Porcia Faventina made this at their own expense to the divine Victoria Augusta.
EDCS-70600090.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Victoria Augusta by Marcus Fabius Novus and Porcia Faventina.

EDCS-70600091.translation: Porcia Faventina took care to have this made at her own expense for Porcia Germulla.
EDCS-70600091.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Porcia Faventina commissioned a monument for Porcia Germulla.

EDCS-70600092.translation: Marcus Fabius Novus took care to have this made at his own expense for his uncle Lucius Fabius Placidus.
EDCS-70600092.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Marcus Fabius Novus commissioned a monument for his uncle Lucius Fabius Placidus.

EDCS-70700152.translation: On the day before the Ides of December, with Gaius Sempronius Avitus and Lucius Aemilius Paetinus as the two magistrates. Gaius Valerius Verduillus paints in the circus of the municipality of Calagurris Iulia.
EDCS-70700152.summary: This is a record of a painting commissioned for the circus of Calagurris Iulia during the tenure of two magistrates, likely dating to the late Roman period.

EDCS-71100125.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Decia Acca, mother of Tautus Flaccus.
EDCS-71100125.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Decia Acca, mother of Tautus Flaccus.

EDCS-71100127.translation: With Senecio and Publius Apollinaris as consuls. Gaius Tautius Semnus, as aedile, made this new [building/structure] at his own expense.
EDCS-71100127.summary: This is a public works inscription recording the construction of a new building by the aedile Gaius Tautius Semnus during the consulship of Senecio and Publius Apollinaris.

EDCS-71200295.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Anna, aged 35.

EDCS-71200296.summary: A fragment of a tombstone dedicated to Quintilius, intended for the departed spirits.

EDCS-71200309.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Paelina, aged 26 and a half, slave of Porcius Laetinus, is laid here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-71200309.summary: A tombstone for Paelina, a slave, aged 26 and a half, and dedicated to the departed spirits, with the common wish for light earth.

EDCS-71200310.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucifero, a freedman, lived 11 years. His parents made this; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-71200310.summary: A tombstone for Lucifero, a freedman, who died at 11 years old, erected by his parents.

EDCS-71200312.summary: A fragmentary inscription possibly related to Ata Vitula.

EDCS-71200316.translation: Arquius of Emerita, son of Camalus...
EDCS-71200316.summary: A funerary inscription for Arquius of Emerita, son of Camalus.

EDCS-71200317.summary: A funerary inscription naming Celsutius Orconius and Reburrus.

EDCS-71200464.translation: To Lucius Caesar, son of Augustus.
EDCS-71200464.summary: An dedication to Lucius Caesar, son of Augustus.

EDCS-71300084.translation: To the departed spirits. For Garbilus, son of Mel[...], from Doiderus, his friend from Tamaricum, aged 31, most pious, erected this.
EDCS-71300084.summary: A funerary inscription by Doiderus for his pious friend Garbilus, aged 31, who was from Tamaricum.

EDCS-71300092.summary: A funerary inscription for Julius Theophilus, who lived to be 65.

EDCS-71300094.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Cornelius Musaevus. Lyde, his wife.
EDCS-71300094.summary: A funerary dedication to Cornelius Musaevus by his wife Lyde, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-71300099.summary: A fragmentary inscription with illegible names and a possible indication of status or family. Dating is uncertain.

EDCS-71300101.translation: Aemilia Atesta and Aemilius Iuventus, freedpersons, have arranged for this to be set up for their most excellent patron Lucius Aemilius Terentianus, aged 56.
EDCS-71300101.summary: A funerary monument set up by freedpersons for their patron, Lucius Aemilius Terentianus, who died at the age of 56, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-71300103.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Octavia Fructuosa, aged 35, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-71300103.summary: A funerary inscription for Octavia Fructuosa, aged 35, with a common wish for a light burial, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-71300105.translation: Gaius Cornelius, son of Gaius, Sedbal.
EDCS-71300105.summary: A personal name inscription, likely identifying an individual and possibly serving as a tomb marker, dating from the Roman period.

EDCS-71300106.summary: A personal name inscription, possibly identifying an individual or serving as a tomb marker, dating from the Roman period.

EDCS-72300296.summary: A personal name inscription that likely identifies Quintus C. C. as being from Tolosa, dating from the Roman period.

EDCS-72400202.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Valerius Quirinus, aged 38. His wife Valeria Aemiliana, freedwoman, has set this up for her freedman. For Aemilius Quietus, aged 102.
EDCS-72400202.summary: A complex funerary monument commemorating Valerius Quirinus, set up by his wife, and also mentioning Aemilius Quietus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-72500117.translation: Monument to the spirits of the departed. Flaus placed this for his dear wife Licinia Amb[---], aged [---].
EDCS-72500117.summary: A funerary monument erected by Flaus for his wife Licinia, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-72600005.translation: For Lucius Grattius Rufinus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe. Fabia Flavina, mother.
EDCS-72600005.summary: A funerary dedication for Lucius Grattius Rufinus, set up by his mother Fabia Flavina, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-72600092.translation: Eutyches, for the boys. Place the fur.
EDCS-72600092.summary: This inscription is a brief funerary marker, likely for a slave or freedman named Eutyches, intended for boys, with an unclear secondary instruction. Its date is uncertain.

EDCS-72600093.translation: Eutyches, for the boys. Place the fur.
EDCS-72600093.summary: This inscription is a brief funerary marker, likely for a slave or freedman named Eutyches, intended for boys, with an unclear secondary instruction. Its date is uncertain.

EDCS-72600094.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary marker for Eutyches, intended for boys, with a partially preserved verb indicating management or carrying. Its date is uncertain.

EDCS-72600412.translation: Annius Cupitus, son of Annibalis, served for 31 years.
EDCS-72600412.summary: This is a military service record for Annius Cupitus, son of Annibalis, who served for 31 years, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-72600413.translation: Sempronius Dorus and Cornelius Pistus made [it].
EDCS-72600413.summary: This inscription records the makers of a monument or object as Sempronius Dorus and Cornelius Pistus, dating from the Roman period.

EDCS-72700054.summary: This is a fragmented funerary or dedication inscription for a slave girl named [...]ocrati(a), dedicated by her parents, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-72700055.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription that begins with a personal name, T. Potitus, with the rest of the text missing, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-72700071.summary: This fragmentary inscription mentions the name Vesp(asianus) or a similar form, possibly related to the Flavian dynasty, with the rest of the text lost, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-72800083.translation: [...] son? of Q. Fra[...], IIvir, fla[men]...
EDCS-72800083.summary: This is a very fragmented inscription referencing a person holding magisterial and priestly offices (IIvir, flamen), likely from a Roman town or colony, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-72800084.translation: Sacred to Mars. Ligurius Primitivus, according to his vow.
EDCS-72800084.summary: This is a dedication to the god Mars made by Ligurius Primitivus in fulfillment of a vow, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72800086.translation: To Minerva, Italicus erected this shrine in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-72800086.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Minerva by Italicus, likely a personal dedication.

EDCS-72800094.translation: To Marcus Aemilius Niger, from the will of Marcus Aemilius.
EDCS-72800094.summary: This inscription records a dedication made from a will, likely to Marcus Aemilius Niger, by Marcus Aemilius.

EDCS-72800103.translation: Let Lovesius, son of Toutonius, aged 70, lie here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-72800103.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lovesius, son of Toutonius, who died at the age of 70.

EDCS-72800104.translation: Procula, daughter of Arcinicus, aged 20, lies here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-72800104.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Procula, daughter of Arcinicus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-73700313.translation: Gaius Septimius Aper, legate of the VII Gemina legion, and Aper Iustinus and Iusta [and someone else] donated this altar to the springs of Actium and to Apollo the most powerful and most holy.
EDCS-73700313.summary: This inscription records the donation of an altar to Apollo and the Actian springs by Gaius Septimius Aper, a legionary legate, and others.

EDCS-73900027.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication to an individual named Gaius, made publicly.

EDCS-73900030.translation: Sabinus, son of Albinus, fulfilled his vow to Sol Invictus.
EDCS-73900030.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Sabinus, son of Albinus, fulfilling a vow to the sun god Sol Invictus.

EDCS-73900039.translation: To Aebarus, son of Aius [and] Segi.
EDCS-73900039.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or memorial to Aebarus, son of Aius and Segi.

EDCS-73900044.translation: To Vironus, son of Tauros, aged 12.
EDCS-73900044.summary: This inscription is a memorial for Vironus, son of Tauros, who died at the age of 12.

EDCS-73900045.translation: To Segontius, son of Aloncus Ambati, soldier, aged 29.
EDCS-73900045.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Segontius, son of Aloncus Ambati, a soldier who died at the age of 29.

EDCS-73900046.translation: Octavia Gemella, daughter, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-73900046.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a daughter, Octavia Gemella, for a deceased individual, likely her father.

EDCS-73900051.translation: To Marcus Baebius Glyco, Marcus Baebius Crassus Opsequens arranged for this [monument] for his parent.
EDCS-73900051.summary: A funerary dedication by Marcus Baebius Crassus Opsequens for his parent, Marcus Baebius Glyco.

EDCS-73900052.translation: Most unfortunate. He made for himself and for his wife Aphro... deceased... 22 years.
EDCS-73900052.summary: A funerary epitaph likely for a man who died and dedicated the tomb to himself and his deceased wife, Aphro, mentioning an age of 22.

EDCS-73900053.translation: May you place the Apri with us.
EDCS-73900053.summary: A very fragmentary inscription that appears to be a plea or request related to placing an object or name.

EDCS-73900438.translation: Sacred to Edovius. Lucius Caburius Florus willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-73900438.summary: This is a votive dedication to the deity Edovius by Lucius Caburius Florus.

EDCS-74000100.translation: To the divine shades of Comes.
EDCS-74000100.summary: A funerary dedication to the 'divine shades' of an individual named Comes.

EDCS-74100262.translation: His daughter arranged for this to be made for Marcus Vireius, her father. May the earth be light upon you. The wife placed [this monument], aged 70.
EDCS-74100262.summary: A funerary monument erected by a daughter and her mother for Marcus Vireius, her father, and including his wife's age at death.

EDCS-74100263.summary: A fragmentary dedication to the 'dis manibus' (divine shades) of an unknown individual.

EDCS-74200144.translation: Lucius Cornelius Clarinu, aged 20 years, lies here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-74200144.summary: A standard Roman tombstone for Lucius Cornelius Clarinu, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-74200162.translation: Blastus of Veneti... On the day before the Ides of December, during the tenure of Gaius Sempronius Avitus and Lucius Aemilius Paetinus as IIvirs for the circuses of the municipality of Calagurris Julia... Gaius Valerius Ver[dul]lus paints.
EDCS-74200162.summary: This inscription appears to be a record of a contract or commission, possibly for decorative painting, mentioning local magistrates and artists in Calagurris Julia around the time of the Ides of December.

EDCS-74200230.summary: This is a dedication, likely to a Christian saint, possibly from the 4th century or later.

EDCS-74200322.summary: This inscription records the name of Marcus Cornelius Reburrus, likely a tombstone or a name tag from the Imperial period.

EDCS-74200323.summary: This inscription records the name of Marcus Cornelius Reburrus, likely a tombstone or a name tag from the Imperial period.

EDCS-74200347.summary: This inscription records the name of Pharnaces and his father Rasinius, possibly indicating a familial relationship or a freedman status, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-74200368.summary: This inscription records the names Apollo and Avilius, possibly a dedication to the god Apollo or a tombstone for a person named Apollo, son of Avilius, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-74200387.translation: [...] to the utmost [...]. Vibius Martialis and Vibius Comes [arranged for] their fellow soldier Chresimus, servant; may the earth lie light upon you.
EDCS-74200387.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a freedman named Chresimus, arranged by his fellow soldiers Vibius Martialis and Vibius Comes, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-74200388.translation: Superstes, servant of our Publius, aged 3 years, lies here; may the earth lie light upon you. Amatustus [dedicated this] to his son.
EDCS-74200388.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a young slave named Superstes, dedicated by his father Amatustus, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-74200391.translation: Lucius Gramnus Superstes lies here; may the earth lie light upon you.
EDCS-74200391.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Lucius Gramnus Superstes, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-74200394.translation: Natalis, freedman of Mani; Gorge, of Mani; [dedicated this to] Mal...; Laeta.
EDCS-74200394.summary: This inscription appears to be a tombstone or funerary dedication listing several individuals and their patron, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-74200395.translation: Arruntia Caria arranged [this] for her sister Apileite.
EDCS-74200395.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Arruntia Caria for her sister Apileite, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-74200426.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Velua, daughter of Tritus, indicating her age.

EDCS-74200436.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely funerary, mentioning Sempronius Flaccus.

EDCS-74200449.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Fabius Fuscinus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-74200449.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by Fabius Fuscinus.

EDCS-74200452.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Sempronia Pusinca, aged 62; also for her son Sempronius Maternus, aged 22.
EDCS-74200452.summary: This is a double funerary inscription commemorating Sempronia Pusinca and her son Sempronius Maternus.

EDCS-74200470.summary: This is likely a funerary epitaph or identification marker for Paternus Marcus.

EDCS-74200471.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary epitaph, possibly recording the deceased Verdula and the dedicator Caius Valerius Verdula.

EDCS-74200490.summary: This is likely a funerary epitaph or identification marker for Valerius Reburrinus.

EDCS-74200507.summary: This inscription refers to a workshop, possibly identifying its owner or location.

EDCS-74200508.summary: This inscription refers to a workshop, possibly identifying its owner or location.

EDCS-74200509.summary: This inscription likely identifies a workshop belonging to Segi.

EDCS-74200510.translation: From the workshop of Segi of Tritium.
EDCS-74200510.summary: This inscription identifies an item as originating from the workshop of Segi in Tritium, likely a potter's mark.

EDCS-74200511.summary: This inscription likely refers to Segi, a person from Tritium, possibly related to a workshop or production.

EDCS-74200516.translation: From the workshop of Segi BPR.
EDCS-74200516.summary: This inscription indicates an item is from the workshop of Segi, with 'BPR' possibly being a further descriptor or abbreviation, functioning as a maker's mark.

EDCS-74200517.summary: This inscription marks an item as coming from the workshop of Segi, suggesting it's a potter's or maker's mark.

EDCS-74200518.summary: This inscription marks an item as coming from the workshop of Segi, suggesting it's a potter's or maker's mark.

EDCS-74200519.summary: This inscription marks an item as coming from the workshop of Segi, suggesting it's a potter's or maker's mark.

EDCS-74200541.translation: Of Lucius Annius, son of Ar[...].
EDCS-74200541.summary: This inscription records the name Lucius Annius and the beginning of his father's name, likely identifying an owner or maker.

EDCS-74200549.summary: This inscription identifies 'Quartus' and his affiliation with 'Publius Cornelius', possibly indicating ownership or a maker's mark.

EDCS-74200554.summary: This inscription simply states the name Marcus Perennius, likely identifying the owner or maker of an object.

EDCS-74200555.summary: This inscription identifies 'Hospes' and his affiliation with 'Lucius Umbricius', possibly indicating ownership or a maker's mark.

EDCS-74200556.summary: This inscription records the name of Gaius Sentius, likely a personal name.

EDCS-74200558.summary: This inscription records the name of Chrecus, son of Publius Cornelius, indicating a patron-client relationship or filiation.

EDCS-74200559.summary: This inscription records the full name Sextus Villius Natalis, likely a personal name.

EDCS-74200572.summary: This inscription indicates that the object comes from the workshop of Felix, referring to a craftsman or business owner.

EDCS-74200573.summary: This inscription indicates that the object comes from a workshop, with the owner's name lost.

EDCS-74200619.translation: To the departed spirits. Primulus and Lasciva set up this tomb for their daughter Gemellina, aged 5.
EDCS-74200619.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Primulus and Lasciva for their daughter Gemellina, who died at the age of 5.

EDCS-74200620.translation: To the departed spirits. Nice, aged 7 and a half, her sister Luperca and Dulcitius saw to the erection of the sepulchre for their dearest daughter.
EDCS-74200620.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Luperca and Dulcitius arranged the burial of their daughter Nice, aged 7.5.

EDCS-74200645.translation: Lucius Pompeius Saturninus, of the tribe Quirina, aged 50, lies here.
EDCS-74200645.summary: This is a funerary inscription stating that Lucius Pompeius Saturninus, aged 50, is buried here.

EDCS-74200646.translation: To the departed spirits. For Lucretia [---], his most devoted wife [---]
EDCS-74200646.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Lucretia, described as a devoted wife, with details lost.

EDCS-74200648.summary: This inscription records the name Afrunus Matigenus, son of Aleo, likely a personal name with patronymic.

EDCS-74200649.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Antonio Maternus, son. Veni had (this made) for her son, aged 40 years.
EDCS-74200649.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Antonio Maternus, set up by his mother Veni, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-74200650.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Sulpicius Paternus. His mother took care to have (this) made.
EDCS-74200650.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sulpicius Paternus, whose mother arranged for his monument, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-74200651.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Unlucky wife. Florentina placed this monument for her husband Ursinus, a small stone.
EDCS-74200651.summary: A funerary epitaph from a wife, Florentina, to her husband Ursinus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-74200652.translation: For Terentius Severianus, aged 25 years. Terentia Doidina took care to have (this) made for her brother.
EDCS-74200652.summary: A funerary epitaph for Terentius Severianus, erected by his sister Terentia Doidina, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-74200653.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titus Clodius (his) son placed (this) for Sextus Maganus, his father, and re-erected this title for this memorial.
EDCS-74200653.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sextus Maganus, set up by his son Titus Clodius, indicating a re-establishment of the memorial, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-74200654.translation: Crispina, slave of Sextus Marius, is buried here. Pious to her family, may the earth lie lightly upon you. Corinthus made (this) for his wife.
EDCS-74200654.summary: A funerary epitaph for Crispina, a slave of Sextus Marius, erected by her husband Corinthus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-74300002.translation: [---]lio Elaeso, son of [---]i, soldier aged 25 years, of the Ninth Legion Hispana. His mother Aia took care to have (this) made.
EDCS-74300002.summary: A funerary epitaph for a soldier of the Ninth Legion Hispana, erected by his mother Aia, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-74300052.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Octavia Fructuosa, aged 35 years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-74300052.summary: A funerary epitaph for Octavia Fructuosa, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-74300215.translation: Lucius Valerius Reburrus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, aged 20 years, is buried here. Titus Burdalicus, son of Rufus, because of [---]
EDCS-74300215.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Valerius Reburrus, with a dedication by Titus Burdalicus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-74300387.translation: Nero Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, son of the deified Claudius, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 8th time, imperator for the 9th time, consul for the 4th time, from Pisauraca, 180 miles.
EDCS-74300387.summary: An inscription recording the mileage from Pisauraca, attributed to Emperor Nero in his 8th year of tribunician power and 4th consulship, dating to the mid-1st century AD.

EDCS-74300392.translation: To Helion, son of Publius, aged 60 years. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-74300392.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Helion, son of Publius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74400001.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a label or ownership mark, identifying Caius Vibius Faustus.

EDCS-74400002.translation: Belonging to Philocalus, son of Caius Volusenus.
EDCS-74400002.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a label or ownership mark, identifying Philocalus, son of Caius Volusenus.

EDCS-74400003.translation: Belonging to Silo, son of Caius Volusenus.
EDCS-74400003.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a label or ownership mark, identifying Silo, son of Caius Volusenus.

EDCS-74400004.translation: Belonging to Silo, son of Caius Volusenus.
EDCS-74400004.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a label or ownership mark, identifying Silo, son of Caius Volusenus.

EDCS-74400016.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary epitaph or name label, recording Titus Rufrenius Rufio.

EDCS-74400017.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary epitaph or name label, recording Crito, son of Lucius Tettius.

EDCS-74400018.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary epitaph or name label, recording Samia, daughter of Lucius Tettius.

EDCS-74400019.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary epitaph or name label, recording Hilarius as belonging to Lucius Tettius.

EDCS-74400022.translation: Belonging to Titus, son of Titus, the innkeeper.
EDCS-74400022.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a label or ownership mark, identifying Titus, son of Titus, the innkeeper.

EDCS-74400024.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Christianus, son of Titus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74400031.summary: This inscription records the name of Umbricius Acratus, likely a tombstone or dedication from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74400032.translation: Son of Umbricius, Hospes the descendant of Auctus
EDCS-74400032.summary: This is a funerary inscription potentially for Hospes, son of Umbricius and descendant of Auctus, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74400035.summary: This inscription likely indicates ownership or dedication by Gaius Vibienus, possibly on a tomb or building, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74400039.summary: This inscription records the name of Gaius Gavius, likely appearing on a tombstone or as a dedicator, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74400050.summary: This inscription records the name Primus, son of Naevius, likely for a tomb or dedication, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74400051.summary: This inscription refers to a 'Spearman' belonging to Naevius, possibly a soldier or a craftsman's designation, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74400052.summary: This inscription records the name of Marcus Perennius Tigranus, likely a tombstone or a dedication, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74400053.summary: This inscription records the name of Marcus Perennius, likely a tombstone or a dedication, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74400057.summary: This inscription records the name of Quintus Petillius, likely a tombstone or a dedication, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74400061.summary: This is a maker's mark indicating the workshop of someone named Ateius.

EDCS-74400065.summary: This inscription likely records ownership or a dedication by Gnaeus Ateius Euhodus, possibly a freedman.

EDCS-74400069.summary: This is a maker's mark indicating the workshop of someone named Xanthi.

EDCS-74400075.summary: This inscription likely records ownership or a dedication by Avilius Stabilis.

EDCS-74400077.summary: This inscription likely records ownership or a dedication by two individuals, Mama and Calidus.

EDCS-74400081.summary: This inscription likely records ownership or a dedication by someone named Publius Cornelius.

EDCS-74400082.summary: This inscription likely records ownership or a dedication by Publius Cornelius Didalus, possibly a freedman.

EDCS-74400095.summary: This is a maker's mark indicating the workshop of someone named Canti.

EDCS-74400107.summary: This is a maker's mark indicating the workshop of someone named Primus.

EDCS-74400124.summary: This is a maker's mark indicating the workshop of someone named Ateius.

EDCS-74400157.translation: Aelius, son of Elius and Paterna, and Aemilianus Atisuenatonus.
EDCS-74400157.summary: This inscription seems to be a dedication by Aelius and Aemilianus Atisuenatonus, possibly to deities or ancestors, with the names suggesting a family or collegium.

EDCS-74400162.translation: [Named] Arocnius set this up in his [---] year.
EDCS-74400162.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Arocnius set up the monument in an unspecified year of his life.

EDCS-74400164.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quemiana Boudica, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-74400174.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Aemilianus and Valerius, aged 25, and Reurena, aged 20.
EDCS-74400174.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aemilianus and Valerius, who died at age 25, and Reurena, who died at age 20.

EDCS-74400175.translation: Parents took care to have this made for Cornelius Flavus, aged 28.
EDCS-74400175.summary: This is a funerary monument for Cornelius Flavus, aged 28, for whom his parents arranged its construction.

EDCS-74400176.translation: To the spirits of the departed: for Atilia Cantabrequa, daughter of Atilius.
EDCS-74400176.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Atilia Cantabrequa, daughter of Atilius.

EDCS-74400177.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Tredius and Donatus for their son Donatianus, [aged ---], [son of ---], and Donata, aged 30.
EDCS-74400177.summary: This funerary inscription records the deaths of Donatianus (aged unknown) and Donata (aged 30), with Tredius and Donatus as dedicants.

EDCS-74400178.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed: his wife took care to have this made for Octavius (?).
EDCS-74400178.summary: This funerary inscription records that a wife arranged for the construction of a monument for her deceased husband, Octavius (?).

EDCS-74400183.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Vivatia Ambada made this for her husband, Gaius Atilius Maternus, son of Atilius, most devoted.
EDCS-74400183.summary: This funerary inscription records that Vivatia Ambada dedicated this monument to her devoted husband, Gaius Atilius Maternus.

EDCS-74400188.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Valeria Reburrina took care to have this made for her father, Valerius Reburrinus, aged 75. To the spirits of the departed: Valeria Reburrina took care to have this made for her mother, Valeria Proculina, aged 70.
EDCS-74400188.summary: This is a double funerary inscription where Valeria Reburrina arranged for monuments for both her father, Valerius Reburrinus (75), and her mother, Valeria Proculina (70).

EDCS-74400192.translation: To the gods of the underworld. [Name?] to her son [Name?], her most dutiful son.
EDCS-74400192.summary: A funerary dedication, likely for a mother to her son, with illegible parts.

EDCS-74400194.translation: Marina, aged six years, [this is] her monument.
EDCS-74400194.summary: A funerary monument for a young girl named Marina.

EDCS-74400196.summary: An incomplete funerary inscription for a person named Doidena Pesica.

EDCS-74400197.translation: Here lies Terentia, daughter of Terentius, aged eighty years.
EDCS-74400197.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial and age of Terentia.

EDCS-74400204.translation: [Name?], aged fifty years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-74400204.summary: A funerary inscription with a common epitaph, recording a burial and the age of the deceased.

EDCS-74400390.translation: Gaius Roscius Lucretius, son of Gaius, freedman.
