EDCS-74400390.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the name and status of the deceased.

EDCS-74500181.translation: [Flainus?], presbyter of our lord King Egicanus.
EDCS-74500181.summary: A dedication or title for Flainus, a presbyter serving under King Egicanus, likely from a late Roman or post-Roman period.

EDCS-74500368.translation: Look upon our entrance, Clement. May [he] have a son, and there he obtained what he sought.
EDCS-74500368.summary: A religious inscription, possibly an appeal for divine favor or a record of answered prayer related to having a son.

EDCS-74600067.translation: Licinia, daughter of Gaius, aged fifteen years, is buried here.
EDCS-74600067.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial and age of Licinia.

EDCS-74600179.translation: [Buried here] a son of Sextus Amminus, from Clunium, aged fifty-one years and [some] months.
EDCS-74600179.summary: A funerary inscription for a man identified by his father, origin, age, and months of life.

EDCS-74600209.summary: This is a Latin text indicating the workshop of a person named Ianvarius, likely for an inscribed object or building.

EDCS-74600218.translation: To the spirits of the departed. L. Avitis saw to the making of this [monument] from his own resources for A[---]A and his family.
EDCS-74600218.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by L. Avitis to A[---]A and family, indicating he paid for its construction.

EDCS-74800074.translation: [---]S[---] elected aedile for 26 years, Pompeius and Proculus [erected this] for their nephew.
EDCS-74800074.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records the election and age of an aedile, with Pompeius and Proculus erecting it for their nephew.

EDCS-75200083.translation: Monument for the most devoted Amp[a]ramus, son of [---]ni, aged 60 years.
EDCS-75200083.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Amp[a]ramus, son of [---]ni, who died at the age of 60, indicating it is a monument dedicated to him.

EDCS-75300104.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Attia Prima. Claudia Spathale, her maternal aunt, [erected this].
EDCS-75300104.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Claudia Spathale, the maternal aunt, for Attia Prima, indicating she set up the monument.

EDCS-75300105.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Episteminus, dearest husband. Adiutor, her husband, saw to the making of this. He lived 32 years.
EDCS-75300105.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Adiutor for his most beloved wife Episteminus, who lived 32 years, stating he commissioned the monument.

EDCS-75300106.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Candidianus Filet[---], his most devoted wife, who lived more or less 27 years.
EDCS-75300106.summary: This funerary inscription is for the devoted wife of Candidianus, identified as Filet[--], who lived approximately 27 years.

EDCS-75300107.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Tychenus, aged 40 years. Attius placed this for his most deserving wife.
EDCS-75300107.summary: This funerary inscription by Attius for his deserving wife Tychenus, aged 40, indicates he placed the monument.

EDCS-75300108.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Trofimenus, mother, most devoted, aged 110 years; and for Martialis, brother, aged 50 years. Fortunatus placed [this] for the most devoted.
EDCS-75300108.summary: This funerary inscription by Fortunatus honors his mother Trofimenus (110 years old) and brother Martialis (50 years old).

EDCS-75300109.translation: [To the spirits] of the departed, sacred. For Trofimenus, aged 19 years. Bur[--]ia[--] his brother saw to the making of this.
EDCS-75300109.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Trofimenus at age 19, with his brother Bur[--]ia[--] commissioning the monument.

EDCS-75300119.translation: Of Cornelius [name illegible], freedman Cin[name illegible]. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-75300119.summary: A tombstone inscription for a freedman, likely from the Roman Imperial period, ending with a common funerary formula.

EDCS-75300120.translation: To the departed spirits. Publius Furius Succesus, aged 40, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Furia Pusinna, his wife, out of piety, took care to have this made at her own expense. // Furia Pusinna, freedwoman of Publius, Fortu[name illegible], placed this monument for her son, aged [age illegible]. [He] lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-75300120.summary: A funerary inscription for Publius Furius Succesus, erected by his wife, which also includes a second, partially restored inscription for their son.

EDCS-75300122.summary: A very brief inscription likely indicating ownership or dedication by Publius Servilius Primus, possibly for a tomb or property.

EDCS-75600023.summary: A fragmentary inscription consisting only of initials, possibly identifying a person or a deity, date uncertain.

EDCS-75600031.summary: A fragmentary inscription naming Numerius Sabia[lius?], date uncertain.

EDCS-75600032.summary: A fragmentary inscription indicating the name Urinius Maternus, likely part of a longer funerary or dedicatory text.

EDCS-75600195.translation: [Name illegible]ius Clemens. [He] lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-75600195.summary: A funerary inscription for a person named Clemens, with much of the text missing, ending with a common epitaph formula.

EDCS-75700016.translation: To the Lares of the Road, Caius [name illegible] Aro from a vow.
EDCS-75700016.summary: A dedication to the Lares Viales (deities of the road) by Caius Aro, likely indicating a votive offering made for safe travel.

EDCS-75800039.translation: Quintus Minicius Navos, son of Quintus, aged 25, lies here. Quintus Minicius Urbanus and Domitia Buccula, his wife, for themselves and for their sons: Lucius Minicius Maritumus, aged 30.
EDCS-75800039.summary: A tombstone inscription for Quintus Minicius Navos, with his father Quintus Minicius Urbanus and mother Domitia Buccula erecting it for themselves and their sons, including Lucius Minicius Maritumus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-75800160.translation: [Name illegible]us, freedman of Quintus. [Name illegible]mus. [Name illegible]ia, daughter of Quintus. [Name illegible]tana.
EDCS-75800160.summary: A fragmentary inscription likely commemorating multiple individuals, possibly family members or freedpersons, with much of the text illegible.

EDCS-75900045.translation: Licius, aged 55. Prot[---]. Perpetu[---]. On the Kalends of December.
EDCS-75900045.summary: This funerary inscription records the name, age, and possibly other details of a person named Licius, with the date likely indicated by the Kalends of December.

EDCS-75900073.translation: To Aurelius Marcus, her most beloved husband, Quintia Marcella, well deserving.
EDCS-75900073.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated by Quintia Marcella to her husband Aurelius Marcus.

EDCS-75900074.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Hostilius Geminus, aged 47.
EDCS-75900074.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name and age of Hostilius Geminus.

EDCS-75900075.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph stating that Caecilia Quieta is buried here.

EDCS-75900151.translation: Legio VII Gemina Philippiana, Pious, Fortunate.
EDCS-75900151.summary: This is an honorific inscription for the Seventh Gemina Legion, specifically its 'Philippiana' designation, likely indicating loyalty to Emperor Philip.

EDCS-75900152.translation: Legio VII Gemina Philippiana, Pious, Fortunate.
EDCS-75900152.summary: This is an honorific inscription for the Seventh Gemina Legion, specifically its 'Philippiana' designation, likely indicating loyalty to Emperor Philip.

EDCS-75900153.translation: Legio VII Gemina Philippiana, Pious, Fortunate.
EDCS-75900153.summary: This is an honorific inscription for the Seventh Gemina Legion, specifically its 'Philippiana' designation, likely indicating loyalty to Emperor Philip.

EDCS-75900154.translation: Legio VII Gemina Maximiana, Pious, Fortunate.
EDCS-75900154.summary: This is an honorific inscription for the Seventh Gemina Legion, specifically its 'Maximiana' designation, likely indicating loyalty to Emperor Maximinus.

EDCS-75900155.translation: Legio VII Gemina Maximiana, Pious, Fortunate.
EDCS-75900155.summary: This is an honorific inscription for the Seventh Gemina Legion, specifically its 'Maximiana' designation, likely indicating loyalty to Emperor Maximinus.

EDCS-75900238.summary: This very fragmentary inscription likely records the birth of a person named Aprius, possibly on a tombstone.

EDCS-75900245.translation: To Lucius Pennius, freedman of Lucius; [---], freedwoman of Lucius; [---]ice.
EDCS-75900245.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication for several individuals, likely members of a family or household, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-75900249.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records the name of a person or persons belonging to the Aemilia family, possibly in a funerary context, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-75900284.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph recording the name Gnaeus Magnus, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-75900398.translation: [---] to Atia; [---]le, aged 15 years; Flavia Anna, aged 31 years.
EDCS-75900398.summary: This fragmentary inscription is a funerary monument for Flavia Anna, aged 31, and another individual aged 15, likely related to Atia, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-75900399.translation: Capito, son of Cornelius, aged 50 years.
EDCS-75900399.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name Capito, son of Cornelius, and his age, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-75900408.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Scevus of Turonis, aged 2 years.
EDCS-75900408.summary: This is a funerary epitaph (Dis Manibus) for Scevus of Turonis, a child aged 2, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-75900411.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to be a funerary dedication to Aunias Cassia, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-75900412.summary: This fragmentary inscription records the name Balaeso, son of Ablis, possibly in a funerary context, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-75900413.translation: Pintovus, son of Arron, aged 20 years.
EDCS-75900413.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name Pintovus, son of Arron, and his age, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-76100183.translation: Voconia Sincera provided and dedicated at her own expense this for her devoted son Lucius Caecilius Sincerus, aged 25 years, and for her husband Caecilius Epaphroditus, and for her father Aelius Natalis.
EDCS-76100183.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Voconia Sincera for her son, husband, and father, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-76100214.translation: To the departed spirits. Aelius, aged 5 years, is placed here.
EDCS-76100214.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a child named Aelius, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-76100220.translation: To Cincius Ursus, his father. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-76100220.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, likely a fragment of a larger monument, for Cincius Ursus.

EDCS-76200028.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to record a personal name and patronymic, with illegible content.

EDCS-76300032.translation: To the departed spirits. Rubbius Maximus, son of Anius, and brother, saw to it that this was made for Rubbia Nycite, aged 40.
EDCS-76300032.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by a son and brother for Rubbia Nycite, dated by the style of the text and name to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-76300097.translation: Mogontius and Decumina had this made for [their] son, the slave [named] Iani. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-76300097.summary: This funerary epitaph, likely from the Roman Imperial period, records the names of the parents and the deceased son, a slave.

EDCS-76300098.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely from the Roman Imperial period, that mentions a child aged 11 and their father.

EDCS-77700190.translation: Lucius Aurelius Ursinus ordered this to be set up for himself by his will.
EDCS-77700190.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Lucius Aurelius Ursinus's arrangement for his own posthumous monument.

EDCS-78200095.translation: To the departed spirits. For Calpurnius Sula, aged 70 years. To the departed spirits. For Sceva Sula, aged 16 years.
EDCS-78200095.summary: This inscription records two individuals, Calpurnius Sula and Sceva Sula, likely related, with their ages.

EDCS-78200096.translation: To the departed spirits. For Claudia Florentina, aged 39 years, 11 months, and 17 days. Claudius Union for his most remarkable wife. [---]
EDCS-78200096.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates Claudia Florentina, detailing her age precisely and noting her husband Claudius Union as the commissioner.

EDCS-78200097.translation: To the departed spirits. For Claudia Palla, aged 50 years. Ayus Moschis, her son, saw to it that this was done for his most devoted mother.
EDCS-78200097.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Claudia Palla, aged 50, and was commissioned by her son Ayus Moschis.

EDCS-78200099.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Aia, aged 35. For Metallica, her most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-78200099.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aia, likely set up by her mother Metallica, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-78200100.translation: To the Divine Shades. To All(a), aged 10. Acathus, her father, placed this.
EDCS-78200100.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Alla, aged 10, dedicated by her father Acathus, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-78200101.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Anna, aged 25. Fusc[...]
EDCS-78200101.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Anna, aged 25, with the dedicator's name partially preserved as Fusc[...], dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-78200102.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Artimidorus, aged 20, his most dutiful son. His father Man[---] and mother Primitiva [---] placed this.
EDCS-78200102.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Artimidorus, aged 20, dedicated by his parents Man[---] and Primitiva, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-78200103.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Atta, aged 20 years and 3 months. Aliomus Munychius placed this for his most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-78200103.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Atta, aged 20 years and 3 months, dedicated by her father Aliomus Munychius, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-78200104.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Amanda, his dearest wife, who lived 35 years. Atius Notori[...]
EDCS-78200104.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Amanda, wife of Atius Notori, who lived 35 years, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-78200105.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Camalica, daughter of Agilodus, aged 30 years. Valerius Flavus(?) placed this for his wife.
EDCS-78200105.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Camalica, daughter of Agilodus, aged 30, dedicated by her husband Valerius Flavus(?), dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-78200106.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Eutychus, aged 25. Primus placed this.
EDCS-78200106.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Eutychus, aged 25, dedicated by Primus, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-78200107.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Fort[---], father, aged 30. Primula, daughter, aged 8[---]. Severus, son, aged 3. Placed [here?].
EDCS-78200107.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the deaths of Fort[---] (father, aged 30), Primula (daughter, aged 8[---]), and Severus (son, aged 3), dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-78200108.translation: [---] for Laturus, his son, aged 35, placed this.
EDCS-78200108.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Laturus, aged 35, dedicated by his parent, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-78200109.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Minutia(?) made this for her most sweet husband Mansueto, who lived 50 years, well-deserving.
EDCS-78200109.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a wife to her deceased husband, with an approximate date inferred from common burial practices.

EDCS-78200110.translation: To the gods of the underworld (?). Urbicus made this memorial for his most dutiful son Mercurio, who lived 27 years more or less.
EDCS-78200110.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a father for his young son, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-78200111.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Modesta for her father Vicario, aged 55, and her father (?), aged 50. May the earth lie lightly on you; she saw to it at her own expense.
EDCS-78200111.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages of two fathers, likely brothers or father and stepfather, remembered by Modesta, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-78200112.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Rufina, most dutiful daughter, placed this for her mother, who lived 50 years.
EDCS-78200112.summary: This epitaph records a daughter's dedication to her mother, specifying the mother's age and her filial piety, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-78200113.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Septiminus lived 43 years, 5 months, and 23 days. Iulia Restuta for her most excellent husband.
EDCS-78200113.summary: This epitaph records the lifespan of Septiminus and is a dedication from his wife Iulia Restuta, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-78200114.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Aliomus arranged for the erection of this monument for his dearest father Thallo, who lived 60 years.
EDCS-78200114.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication from a son to his father, providing the father's age and the son's care in arranging the memorial, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-78200115.translation: To the gods of the underworld. The father Tritius arranged for this monument for Venustus, his son, well-deserving, who lived 14 years, 2 months, and 25 days.
EDCS-78200115.summary: This epitaph is a dedication by a father for his son, specifying the son's age at death and that the monument was erected at the father's expense, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-78200116.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Eurus the father placed this for Apolline, daughter of Atillus, his most sweet [daughter?], aged 1 year [and something].
EDCS-78200116.summary: This funerary epitaph records a father's dedication to his young daughter, providing her name and partial age, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-78200117.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Eutica for her most dutiful husband Eutanus, who lived beyond 90 years.
EDCS-78200117.summary: This epitaph records a wife's dedication to her husband, highlighting his longevity, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-78200118.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Galena [made this] for [her son/husband/etc.], aged 45 years.
EDCS-78200118.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph indicating the deceased's name and age, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-78200119.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Heraclida set up this monument for her most dutiful daughter Abinnesia, who lived for 11 years, and for herself.
EDCS-78200119.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Heraclida to her daughter Abinnesia and herself, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-78200120.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Evantus for his wife Lasciva, aged 22.
EDCS-78200120.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Evantus for his wife Lasciva, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-78200121.translation: To the spirits of the departed. His sister set up [this monument] for Onesimus, her most dutiful brother, who lived for 26 years.
EDCS-78200121.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a sister for her brother Onesimus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-78200122.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucca set up [this monument] for her most dutiful husband Patroclus, who lived for approximately 20 years.
EDCS-78200122.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Lucca for her husband Patroclus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-78200125.translation: Ambatus, son of Tritanocon, son of Sec[---] Pandi.
EDCS-78200125.summary: This inscription records the name and parentage of Ambatus, possibly a tombstone or ownership mark, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-78200140.summary: This incomplete inscription likely records a name, Antiopa, and possibly her family connections or a dedication, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-78300011.translation: Of Lucius Pompeius, freedman of Lucius.
EDCS-78300011.summary: This is a legal or property marker identifying ownership or association with Lucius Pompeius, a freedman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-78300014.summary: This inscription simply records the name Publius Licinius, likely a property marker or part of a larger inscription, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-78300015.translation: Workshop of [---]. Herennius, freedman of Lucius, restored [it].
EDCS-78300015.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a workshop by Herennius, a freedman of Lucius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-78300018.summary: This inscription identifies a workshop belonging to Gaius Candidus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-78300019.summary: This inscription likely records ownership or dedication by Gaius Iulius Laetus, possibly on a tomb or monument.

EDCS-78300584.summary: This inscription records the maker of an object or monument as Hermes, son of Favor.

EDCS-78700056.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Nicephorus.

EDCS-80000094.translation: Adulescens, Marcus Aemilius, aged 17. Isipallus, 10. Written by the board of four officials. Bella, daughter of Cassius. [3]ella, daughter of Bassus. ADIN[3]DETERE. Cornelius ERDOILD[3].
EDCS-80000094.summary: This complex inscription appears to record the ages of individuals and official responsibilities, possibly related to a funerary monument or a civic record, with some lines being difficult to decipher.

EDCS-80900097.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely recording a dedication or mention of a son of Titus.

EDCS-80900272.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Valerius Diocletianus Augustus.
EDCS-80900272.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Roman Emperor Diocletian, dating from his reign.

EDCS-80900277.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication to a deity identified as Coso, with the rest of the text illegible.

EDCS-80900313.summary: This inscription indicates that the object or work was made by Germanus.

EDCS-81200174.summary: This is a partial dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-81200176.translation: Primia, daughter of Maia of Lunensis, willingly dedicated an altar to Jupiter Optimus.
EDCS-81200176.summary: This inscription records the voluntary dedication of an altar to Jupiter Optimus by Primia, daughter of Maia, from Lunensis.

EDCS-81200200.summary: This is a dedication to the gods Alandicus and Cacuortus, likely from an altar or shrine.

EDCS-81200207.summary: This inscription, likely from a tombstone, indicates that the deceased is from the place or estate of Fortunatio.

EDCS-81200208.summary: This inscription, likely from a tombstone, indicates that the deceased is from the place or estate of Fortunatio.

EDCS-81200210.summary: This is a dedication to the god Mars by someone named Crispinus, likely from a military context.

EDCS-81500014.translation: To our Lord Galerius Valerius Maximinus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-81500014.summary: This is an acclamation or dedication to Caesar Maximinus Daia, likely from the period of his rule (early 4th century AD).

EDCS-81500015.translation: To Emperor Nerva Caesar Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power, consul for the third time, father of the country, [dedicated this].
EDCS-81500015.summary: This is likely a dedication to Emperor Nerva, based on the titulature, dating to his reign (96-98 AD).

EDCS-81500016.translation: To our Lord(?) Galerius Valerius Maximinus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-81500016.summary: This is an acclamation or dedication to Caesar Maximinus Daia, likely from the period of his rule (early 4th century AD).

EDCS-81500039.translation: To Emperor Nerva Caesar Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power, consul for the third time, father of the country, [dedicated this].
EDCS-81500039.summary: This is likely a dedication to Emperor Nerva, based on the titulature, dating to his reign (96-98 AD).

EDCS-81500040.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, father of the country, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the 18th time, consul for the third time. [Built] 96 miles of roads by order of Augustus.
EDCS-81500040.summary: This inscription records the construction of roads under Emperor Hadrian, dating to his reign (117-138 AD).

EDCS-81500067.translation: Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest conqueror of Germans, greatest conqueror of Dacians, greatest conqueror of Sarmatians, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the 5th time, emperor for the 7th time, father of the country, proconsul, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, greatest conqueror of Germans, greatest conqueror of Dacians, greatest conqueror of Sarmatians, prince of the youth, son of our lord Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, restored roads and bridges collapsed by age, under the supervision of Quintus Decius, legate of the emperor, proconsul.
EDCS-81500067.summary: This inscription records the restoration of roads and bridges by Emperors Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus, under the supervision of the legate Decius, likely from the period 236-238 AD.

EDCS-82000138.translation: Sacred to Lady Minerva, from Climenus, fulfilling a vow.
EDCS-82000138.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Minerva by Climenus.

EDCS-82000147.summary: A tomb inscription recording the burial of Novella.

EDCS-82000149.translation: To the departed spirits. C(aius) C(a)ecilius, aged 26, was cremated here. Vern[---] made this, [---] aged 30 years [---].
EDCS-82000149.summary: A funerary inscription for C(aius) C(a)ecilius, who died at 26, with further text and names of the deceased or maker, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-82000158.translation: Licinia Helena willingly and deservedly fulfills her vow to Fortunatus.
EDCS-82000158.summary: A votive inscription by Licinia Helena to Fortunatus, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-82000159.translation: For the health of ++ARIS+[---]. Titus Flaccinius Maximus(?) to Mars, from a vow.
EDCS-82000159.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Mars by Titus Flaccinius Maximus(?) for health, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-82000163.summary: A tomb inscription recording the burial of Marcus Aelius Nigrinus.

EDCS-82000164.summary: A tomb inscription recording the burial of Titus Aemilius Li[---].

EDCS-82000165.summary: A tomb inscription recording the burial of Antoni[---] Prima[---].

EDCS-82000166.summary: A tomb inscription recording the burial of Antonia Pyrallis, who died at 40.

EDCS-82000167.translation: Marcus Caecilius, son of Marcus, Aviola, aged 20, lies here.
EDCS-82000167.summary: A tomb inscription recording the burial of Marcus Caecilius Aviola, son of Marcus, who died at 20.

EDCS-82000168.summary: This inscription appears to record the names Paetus and Guandi, possibly related to a funerary monument, with significant lacunae.

EDCS-82000169.summary: This fragmented inscription mentions the name Porcius, likely part of a personal name or gentilician, with the remaining text illegible.

EDCS-82000202.summary: This fragment appears to be part of a dedication or epitaph to a daughter named A[--]cae.

EDCS-82000205.summary: This inscription begins with the name Lucius Fabius Ianvarius, likely the subject of the monument with further details lost.

EDCS-82000206.translation: [---] Cancilus, having fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-82000206.summary: This inscription records that T. Canci[l]us willingly fulfilled a vow, with the preceding text lost.

EDCS-82100008.translation: Agria, daughter of Decimus, lived 10 years. She rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-82100008.summary: This is a tombstone for Agria, daughter of Decimus, who died at the age of 10, with a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-82100022.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Pompeius Atticus made this for his wife, for his wife, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-82100022.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Pompeius Atticus for his wife, recording her marital status and expressing a wish for her repose.

EDCS-82100056.summary: This fragmented epitaph records the name of a woman ending in '-nati', her age at death as 40, and mentions her lifespan.

EDCS-82100057.translation: Aurelius Vincentius was a deacon for seventy years and passed away in peace.
EDCS-82100057.summary: This inscription records the death of Aurelius Vincentius, who served as a deacon for seventy years, likely from a church context.

EDCS-82100064.translation: Crispin[---], servant of Marcus. He rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Marcus Iulius [---]
EDCS-82100064.summary: This epitaph for Crispinus, identified as a servant of Marcus, includes a wish for his repose and the beginning of another name, Marcus Iulius.

EDCS-82100067.translation: Pyle is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-82100067.summary: A tombstone for Pyle, likely from the Roman Imperial period, containing a common funerary formula.

EDCS-82100074.translation: Ursio, aged 40 years, [caused this to be set up?] and placed it? Pud[...].
EDCS-82100074.summary: A funerary inscription for Ursio, a 40-year-old person, with part of the dedicant's name lost, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-82100075.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Fortunatus, aged 20 years. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-82100075.summary: A funerary inscription for Fortunatus, aged 20, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-82100078.translation: Lucius Valenti[nus] and Marcus A[...], having paid their vow freely, made this.
EDCS-82100078.summary: A votive inscription by Lucius Valentinus and Marcus A[...], likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-82100079.translation: Gaius Septimius Severianus freely paid his vow.
EDCS-82100079.summary: A votive inscription by Gaius Septimius Severianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-82100086.summary: An inscription mentioning Marcus Apronius, possibly a dedication or a memorial, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-82100090.summary: The beginning of a name, Gaius Grattius, likely part of a larger inscription from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-82100092.summary: An inscription mentioning Grattius, possibly a dedication or a memorial, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-82100095.translation: Gaius Mini[...?] having paid his vow, made this willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-82100095.summary: A votive inscription by Gaius Mini[...], likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-82100096.summary: The beginning of an inscription mentioning Lucius Aemilius Alchimus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-82100097.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription naming Gaius Grattius, likely a tombstone or memorial marker.

EDCS-82100098.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription naming Publius Aemilius, potentially a dedication or funerary marker.

EDCS-82100100.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Aemilius, likely part of a larger text such as a funerary monument or dedication.

EDCS-82100108.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Chrestus, aged 5 years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-82100108.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Chrestus, a child aged 5, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-82400147.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription that appears to record a person's name and age, likely from a funerary context.

EDCS-82500160.translation: Valeria Silvana made (this) for her father, Lucius Valerius Tempstivus; (and for) Quintius Flaccus, the uncle; (and for) Valeria Verana, the mother-in-law, her heirs. Having redeemed the share, Valeria Silvana contributed (the money) for Valerius Avitus. They placed (it) in the forum, having found (it) in the house.
EDCS-82500160.summary: This inscription records the construction of a monument or tomb, funded by Valeria Silvana, for her father, father-in-law, and paternal uncle, placed in the forum.

EDCS-82800034.translation: To Cosus Maelobrigo, son of Valerius...
EDCS-82800034.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or funerary monument, beginning with a dedication to Cosus Maelobrigo.

EDCS-82800035.translation: To the Lares Thuringi, Rufus, son of Publius Claudius, willingly fulfilled his vow with a willing heart.
EDCS-82800035.summary: This inscription records Rufus, son of Publius Claudius, fulfilling a vow to the Lares Thuringi.

EDCS-82800036.translation: To the god Mundonus, Faus() willingly fulfilled his vow with a willing heart.
EDCS-82800036.summary: This inscription records Faus() fulfilling a vow to the god Mundonus.

EDCS-00380425.translation: Sacred to Diana. Quintus Valerius Victor, by the instruction of Juno.
EDCS-00380425.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Diana, made by Quintus Valerius Victor, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380443.translation: Lucius Terentius of the Fourth Macedonian Legion.
EDCS-00380443.summary: This inscription records the name of Lucius Terentius, a soldier in the Legio IIII Macedonica, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380497.summary: This is a simple inscription recording the name and filiation of Marcus Crastuniqum, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380504.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Anna, aged 35, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380505.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Otila, aged 10, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380511.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that records the name and filiation of an individual named O[---], likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380517.translation: To the departed spirits. Sintilla, wife, for her most devoted husband, Lutbelscottius, aged 71, is buried here.
EDCS-00380517.summary: This funerary monument, likely from the Roman Imperial period, was erected by Sintilla for her husband Lutbelscottius.

EDCS-00380518.translation: To the spirits of the departed; Luntbelsar, aged 71.
EDCS-00380518.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Luntbelsar, who died at the age of 71.

EDCS-00380519.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying Elasus as the son of Nelandus.

EDCS-00380521.translation: Aemilius Longinus, soldier, aged 60. H. S. E. (here lies).
EDCS-00380521.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aemilius Longinus, a soldier who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-00380522.translation: Sempronia Cosia, daughter of Cosus, is buried here.
EDCS-00380522.summary: This is a funerary inscription stating that Sempronia Cosia, daughter of Cosus, is buried here.

EDCS-00380525.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fortunio, aged 35.

EDCS-00380526.translation: To the spirits of the departed; Paesina, daughter of Ota, aged [X].
EDCS-00380526.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Paesina, daughter of Ota, with her age partially preserved.

EDCS-00380549.translation: Neitirke, daughter of Serbak, from Tako.
EDCS-00380549.summary: This funerary inscription identifies Neitirke, daughter of Serbak, and her place of origin.

EDCS-02900413.translation: Lucius Terentius, the potter, of the Fourth Macedonian Legion.
EDCS-02900413.summary: This inscription identifies Lucius Terentius as a potter belonging to the Fourth Macedonian Legion.

EDCS-03000463.translation: Bodocena, daughter of Aravis, from the fortress of Agubrigia, aged 12, is buried here.
EDCS-03000463.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Bodocena, daughter of Aravis, who died at the age of 12 and was buried at the fortress of Agubrigia.

EDCS-03000464.translation: To Minerva, Rusus willingly paid his vow for the merit he received. To Minerva, he willingly paid his vow for the merit he received.
EDCS-03000464.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Minerva by Rusus, who willingly paid his vow.

EDCS-03000465.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Lupa, my dearest wife, aged 45. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-03000465.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lupa, dedicated by her husband, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03000493.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Vibia Lavinia, aged 27, dutiful to her family, her husband Valerius Verecundus and her mother Vibia Modestina placed this for her, a well-deserving woman. She lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Sacred to the Divine Shades.
EDCS-03000493.summary: A funerary epitaph for Vibia Lavinia, dedicated by her husband and mother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03000496.translation: Lucius Terentius Censorinus, aged 60, lies here.
EDCS-03000496.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Terentius Censorinus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300498.translation: Reburrus fulfilled his vow to Nemesis.
EDCS-03300498.summary: A votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow to the goddess Nemesis, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300500.translation: Arrius Constans Speratianus, prefect of cavalry, willingly placed this statue of a hunter for Diana Augusta, according to his vow.
EDCS-03300500.summary: A votive inscription by Arrius Constans Speratianus, a prefect of cavalry, dedicating a statue to Diana Augusta, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300501.translation: La Flavo, aged 35, set up this memorial to the Divine Shades.
EDCS-03300501.summary: A funerary epitaph for La Flavo, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300513.translation: Calus, son of Aemilia Litanionis, aged 20. Cantabra, sister, Ovina, sister. Florus and Tegula Rustica arranged for this to be made with their own money. They lie here.
EDCS-03300513.summary: A funerary epitaph for Calus, his sisters Cantabra and Ovina, and Florus and Tegula Rustica, who funded the monument, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300524.translation: Pro established a monument to the Divine Spirits for her daughter, aged 25, in the year 391 AUC.
EDCS-03300524.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Pro for her daughter.

EDCS-03300525.translation: Antestius Aemilius set up a memorial for his son Antestius Patruinus, aged 25, in the consular year 392 AUC.
EDCS-03300525.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Antestius Aemilius for his son.

EDCS-03300542.summary: A short epitaph for Acilius, likely from a tombstone.

EDCS-03300543.translation: May Eulali live in God. Sidonia, his daughter, in God, we embrace you.
EDCS-03300543.summary: A funerary epitaph with Christian elements, possibly for Eulali.

EDCS-03300546.summary: A very brief inscription, possibly identifying Aurelius as the paterfamilias.

EDCS-03300551.translation: Martiales may he live in God. Amen. He was mindful of my Lord and spoke well of him.
EDCS-03300551.summary: A Christian inscription for Martiales, expressing faith and remembrance.

EDCS-03300556.translation: Serpensius will live in Christ in Christ. Amen.
EDCS-03300556.summary: A short, clear Christian inscription for Serpensius.

EDCS-03300559.translation: Asturius, may you live in God, may you remain in Christ.
EDCS-03300559.summary: A Christian epitaph wishing eternal life in God and Christ for Asturius.

EDCS-03300562.translation: Ida, help your servant Alasius, the cleric, in the son, he died, may he help [him].
EDCS-03300562.summary: A prayer for help addressed to Ida for Alasius, a cleric, possibly related to his death and afterlife.

EDCS-03300563.translation: Ioannes may he live in God. My soul will be devastated, God has risen, amen. He who made [this] may he live and let him remember God. Amen, he lives. Amen.
EDCS-03300563.summary: A Christian inscription for Ioannes, expressing faith in the resurrection and a plea for divine remembrance.

EDCS-03300565.translation: Marturius made this on Thursday; he went in healthy, he returned healthy, he departed.
EDCS-03300565.summary: This is a personal inscription likely from a tomb, recording the fate of Marturius.

EDCS-03300570.translation: Into this holy place will enter a man of God, by name Cila.
EDCS-03300570.summary: This inscription likely marks a burial or a sacred space, identifying a person named Cila who will enter.

EDCS-03300574.translation: Marcus Aquinius, freedman of Marcus, Andro, gladly and deservedly took care of Jupiter Stator with his own money.
EDCS-03300574.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Stator by Marcus Aquinius, a freedman, who funded the work.

EDCS-03300575.summary: This inscription likely refers to a dedication or a funerary monument commissioned by Lucius Caecilius.

EDCS-03300585.translation: Thesæus, aged 40, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-03300585.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Thesæus, providing his age and a common funerary wish.

EDCS-03300586.summary: This is a simple name inscription, possibly on a tombstone or a building owned by Lucius Afranius.

EDCS-03300587.summary: This is an abbreviation likely referring to a bishop, possibly on a religious artifact or building.

EDCS-03400002.summary: This is an inscription dedicating a monument or object to Publius Mucius Scaevola.

EDCS-03400005.translation: Lucius Nonius Hilarus, freedman of Lucius, and his freedman Lucius Nonius Philoxsinus, live on.
EDCS-03400005.summary: This is a funerary inscription for two freedmen, Lucius Nonius Hilarus and Lucius Nonius Philoxsinus, possibly indicating a family tomb.

EDCS-03400006.translation: Aulus Varaeus, freedman of Aulus, and his freedwoman Danais, Philonicus, made this while alive.
EDCS-03400006.summary: This is a funerary inscription or dedication made by Aulus Varaeus and his freedwoman Danais, and Philonicus, indicating it was done during their lifetime.

EDCS-03400008.translation: Lucius Valerius, a soldier of the Seventh Gemina Felix legion, gladly and deservedly paid his vow to Apollo.
EDCS-03400008.summary: This is a votive inscription by a soldier of the Legio VII Gemina dedicated to Apollo.

EDCS-03400009.translation: Numitorius, son of Lucius, and Marita and Munnian, from Expediensis, to the good goddess Com... [illegible]
EDCS-03400009.summary: This is a dedication to a goddess, likely of local importance, possibly by members of a family or group.

EDCS-03400010.translation: To the divine spirit of the conventus of Clunium.
EDCS-03400010.summary: This is an inscription dedicating something to the genius (divine spirit) of the administrative district (conventus) of Clunium.

EDCS-03400012.translation: Lucius S. Agaton gladly and deservedly paid his vow to Jupiter.
EDCS-03400012.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius S. Agaton to Jupiter.

EDCS-03400013.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Publius Aelius Victor, freedman of Augustus, in charge of records for the XXth Inheritance [office] of the province of Hispania Citerior, [fulfilled his] vow.
EDCS-03400013.summary: This is a votive inscription by a Roman official in Hispania Citerior to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-03400014.translation: Marius Gamicus, the master, gave and dedicated this gift to Holy Minerva.
EDCS-03400014.summary: This is a dedication to Minerva by Marius Gamicus, identified as a 'magister' (master or president), possibly of a collegium.

EDCS-03400015.translation: Lucius Valerius Flavus B, a soldier of the Seventh Gemina Felix legion, for the health of the college of stratores, gladly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-03400015.summary: This is a votive inscription by a soldier of the Legio VII Gemina for the health of the collegium stratorum.

EDCS-03400016.translation: Cornelia Seneciana and Valeria Pompeia set this up for the health of Nummius Didymus, with a vow.
EDCS-03400016.summary: This is an inscription by two women dedicating something to Nemesis for the health of Nummius Didymus.

EDCS-03400019.translation: Aemilius Adelphus gladly and deservedly paid his vow to the Silvan gods.
EDCS-03400019.summary: This is a votive inscription by Aemilius Adelphus to the Silvan gods.

EDCS-03400020.translation: Baba, wife of Lucius Numisius Sticus, paid her vow to Tutela gladly and deservedly, because she successfully and properly completed the construction of two workshops and the temple...
EDCS-03400020.summary: This is a votive inscription by Baba, wife of Lucius Numisius Sticus, to the goddess Tutela, commemorating the successful completion of a building project.

EDCS-03400022.translation: Grattius Crescens, *sevir Augustalis*, and his wife Fabia Beronice raise this monument where I vow sacred rites to the divine Augusta.
EDCS-03400022.summary: This is a private dedication by Grattius Crescens and his wife to the divine Augusta, likely marking the erection of a religious monument.

EDCS-03400023.translation: Iulius Vegetus gladly and deservedly paid his vow to Jupiter, Best and Greatest.
EDCS-03400023.summary: A private votive offering by Iulius Vegetus to Jupiter, expressing gratitude for divine favor.

EDCS-03400024.translation: Philetus, from the Mummii, by vow.
EDCS-03400024.summary: A small dedication by Philetus, likely a freedman or client, fulfilling a vow to his patrons.

EDCS-03400028.translation: To the deified Vespasian, Marcus Acilius Nymphodotus.
EDCS-03400028.summary: A dedication by Marcus Acilius Nymphodotus to the deified emperor Vespasian, likely commemorating a public building or honor.

EDCS-03400039.translation: Our most holy and eternal Emperors Gaius Valerius Diocletian and Marcus Aurelius Valerius Maximian, the Pious, Fortunate, Invincible Augusti, ordered the portico of Iovia in the basilica to be built; Iulius Valens, *vir perfectissimus*, governor of the province of Hispania Citerior, most devoted to their divine will, saw to its construction and dedicated it.
EDCS-03400039.summary: This inscription records the construction of a portico in the basilica of Iovia, ordered by Emperors Diocletian and Maximian and executed by the provincial governor Iulius Valens, likely dating to the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-03400044.summary: An incomplete dedication to an Emperor Caesar, likely part of a larger inscription.

EDCS-03400070.translation: To Raecius Tauri filius Galeria Gallus, tribune of the soldiers of Emperor Galba, flamen of Emperor Vespasian Caesar Augustus for life, and by decree of the town council, flamen of the province of Hispania Citerior, quaestor of the province of Baetica, tribune of the plebs, praetor, and member of the Augustal college, [this monument is erected by] Marcus Minatius [to] his most excellent and outstanding friend.
EDCS-03400070.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Marcus Minatius to his friend Raecius Gallus, a distinguished official who held various military, religious, and administrative posts, likely dating to the Flavian or Antonine period.

EDCS-03400073.translation: To Lucius Caecina Severus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, *duovir*, quaestor, *praefectus fabrum*, prefect of the 1st cohort and of the maritime coast, Lucius Bennius Hermes [erected this monument] for his many services to him.
EDCS-03400073.summary: A dedication by Lucius Bennius Hermes to Lucius Caecina Severus, likely a funerary monument honoring Severus for his public service and patronage, probably dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-03400074.translation: The town councillors of [the town of] Larum [erected this monument] to Lucius Caecina Severus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, *duovir*, quaestor, flamen, *praefectus fabrum*, prefect of the 1st cohort and of the maritime coast.
EDCS-03400074.summary: The town councillors of Larum dedicated this monument to Lucius Caecina Severus, recognizing his significant public career and service to the community, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-03400075.translation: To Tiberius Claudius Paullinus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, *duovir*, quaestor, prefect of the Balearic Islands and the maritime coast.
EDCS-03400075.summary: This is likely a funerary or honorific inscription for Tiberius Claudius Paullinus, who held important administrative and military positions, possibly dating to the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-03400077.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Marcus Lucretius Peregrinus, a centurion of the First Legion Minervia Pia Felix, also of the Third Legion Cyrenaica, prefect of the Fourth Cohort of Lingones, a decurion of Tarraco, admitted into the rank of senator; Lucretia Eucarpia, to her most indulgent patron.
EDCS-03400077.summary: This funerary epitaph honors Marcus Lucretius Peregrinus, a centurion and prefect, and was dedicated by his patron Lucretia Eucarpia.

EDCS-03400078.translation: To Lucius Rufidius Iullus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, prefect of the Fourth Cohort of Vindelici, tribune of soldiers of the Third Augustan Legion; Lucius Rufidius Pollentinus, to his son.
EDCS-03400078.summary: This epitaph records the career of Lucius Rufidius Iullus, a prefect and tribune, and was dedicated by his father.

EDCS-03400079.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Marcus Aurelius Justus, of the Palatina tribe, formerly a Roman knight, centurion of the Tenth Fretensis, Sixth Victrix, Third Ulpia Traiana, Fourth Flavia, Third Cyrenaica, chief prior spearman, aged 42 years, with 20 years of service. Aurelia Iusta, his wife, and his sons Alexander and Julian, to their most devoted father, had this made.
EDCS-03400079.summary: This funerary epitaph honors Marcus Aurelius Justus, a centurion with extensive military service, and was dedicated by his wife and sons.

EDCS-03400080.translation: To Marcus Valerius Secundus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, a centurion of the Seventh Gemina Felix, also of the Third Augustan Legion, also of the Second Traiana, also of the Fourteenth Gemina; to their well-deserving patron, by his freedmen and heirs Valerius Euvanthes, Callistus, Privatus, Saturninus, and Glaucilla.
EDCS-03400080.summary: This epitaph honors Marcus Valerius Secundus, a centurion who served in multiple legions, and was dedicated by his freedmen and heirs.

EDCS-03400081.translation: To Gaius Caecilius Quartus, a veteran of the Seventh Gemina Felix, a staff-officer to a consular governor, originally from Sicca Veneria; Sempronia Fabiana, his wife, to her most indulgent and excellent husband, who deserved well of her.
EDCS-03400081.summary: This funerary epitaph honors Gaius Caecilius Quartus, a veteran and staff-officer, and was dedicated by his wife.

EDCS-03400082.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Flavius Aurelianus, a staff-officer; Licinia Cupita, to her dearest husband.
EDCS-03400082.summary: This funerary epitaph honors Flavius Aurelianus, a staff-officer, and was dedicated by his wife.

EDCS-03400083.translation: To Caecilius Julianus, a staff-officer of the Seventh Gemina Pia Felix; Iulia Severa, his wife, saw to the making of this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-03400083.summary: This funerary epitaph honors Caecilius Julianus, a staff-officer, and was dedicated by his wife.

EDCS-03400084.translation: To Lucius Valerius Barbarus, a soldier of the Seventh Gemina Felix, aged 37 years, a staff-officer to a consular governor; Hiberia Materna, to her dearest spouse.
EDCS-03400084.summary: This funerary epitaph honors Lucius Valerius Barbarus, a soldier and staff-officer, and was dedicated by his wife.

EDCS-03400085.summary: This inscription records the name Gaius Valerius, identifying him as a scout.

EDCS-03400087.translation: Rufonius [---] of the Galeria tribe, a soldier of [---]
EDCS-03400087.summary: This fragmentary inscription begins to record the name Rufonius, identifying him as a soldier of the Galeria tribe.

EDCS-03400089.translation: Lupercus, a soldier of the legion VII Gemina, of the century of X, died in the year IX of the era; he rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon him.
EDCS-03400089.summary: This is a tombstone for Lupercus, a soldier of the legion VII Gemina, dating to the era of IX.

EDCS-03400090.translation: To the departed spirits. Betutius Satto, son of Caius, a veteran of the legion VII Gemina, rests here. Marcus Iulius Fidelis made this for a devoted friend.
EDCS-03400090.summary: This is a tombstone for Betutius Satto, a veteran of the legion VII Gemina, made by his friend Marcus Iulius Fidelis.

EDCS-03400092.translation: Fuficia Germana, freedwoman and heir, made this for Lucius Fuficius Priscus, a veteran of the legion VII Gemina Felix, and for his wife Flaminia Melete, and for her relatives Domitia Saturnina.
EDCS-03400092.summary: This tombstone was erected by Fuficia Germana, freedwoman and heir, for Lucius Fuficius Priscus, a veteran of legion VII Gemina Felix, and his wife and relatives.

EDCS-03400093.translation: To the departed spirits. The mother to her son FI, a veteran, who lived PO years.
EDCS-03400093.summary: This is a fragment of a tombstone for a veteran, erected by his mother.

EDCS-03400094.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. Ulpius Macedo took care to have this made for his comrade Gaius Iulius Proclus, a veteran of the legion VII Gemina Felix.
EDCS-03400094.summary: This tombstone was erected by Ulpius Macedo for his comrade Gaius Iulius Proclus, a veteran of legion VII Gemina Felix.

EDCS-03400095.translation: to Publius, son of Publius, a veteran.
EDCS-03400095.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely a tombstone, for a veteran named Publius.

EDCS-03400097.translation: To Ceionia Maxima, also called Achorista, freedwoman of Plautia, sister of the deified Verus, most holy, most sweet, most innocent beloved wife. I lived with her for 7 years and 1 month. She herself lived for 30 years, 11 months, and 8 days. Lucius Septimius Polybius, freedman of our Emperors, a commentator, procurator of the centenarius of the province of Hispania Citerior, for his most faithful, most chaste wife.
EDCS-03400097.summary: This tombstone commemorates Ceionia Maxima, freedwoman of Plautia and wife of Lucius Septimius Polybius, a freedman and official in Hispania Citerior, who died at age 30.

EDCS-03400098.translation: To the memory of Martialis, a holy soul. He lived 6 years and 67 days. Martialis and Flavia Trophime, parents, made this for their most devoted son.
EDCS-03400098.summary: This tombstone was erected by parents for their son Martialis, who died at the age of 6 years and 67 days.

EDCS-03400100.translation: To the departed spirits. To Publius Septimius, freedman of Augustus, assistant to the procurator of the province of Hispania Citerior, who lived 29 years, 3 months, and 10 days. Cosconia Leda lived with him for 7 years, 2 months, and 10 days. For her most devoted, most sweet, incomparable husband.
EDCS-03400100.summary: This tombstone was erected by Cosconia Leda for her husband Publius Septimius, a freedman and assistant procurator in Hispania Citerior, who died at age 29.

EDCS-03400102.translation: Geminus, dispensator of the province of Hispania Citerior, [lived] I [year?].
EDCS-03400102.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription recording Geminus, a dispensator (financial officer) in the province of Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-03400103.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Caius Iulius Aquilinus and Iulia Favorina, to their most dutiful father, Favoris, freedman of Augustus, a clerk of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-03400103.summary: A funerary dedication from Caius Iulius Aquilinus and Iulia Favorina to their father, Favoris, who was a freedman and clerk in Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-03400104.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Iulius Narcissus, freedman of Augustus, a messenger.
EDCS-03400104.summary: A funerary dedication to Iulius Narcissus, a freedman and messenger of Augustus.

EDCS-03400105.translation: To Iuventia Paula, their deserving mother. Iuvencus, freedman of Augustus, her son.
EDCS-03400105.summary: A dedication from Iuvencus, a freedman of Augustus, to his mother, Iuventia Paula.

EDCS-03400111.translation: To Baebia Galla, daughter of Titus, priestess of Silvanus Granianus of the province of Hispania Citerior. Caius Terentius Philetus, from Rome.
EDCS-03400111.summary: A dedication to Baebia Galla, priestess of Silvanus Granianus in Hispania Citerior, by Caius Terentius Philetus of Rome.

EDCS-03400112.translation: To Fulvia Celer, daughter of Marcus, perpetual priestess of the colony of Tarraconenses and priestess of the province of Hispania Citerior. Fulvius Musaeus and Fulvius Moschus, freedmen, by her will.
EDCS-03400112.summary: A funerary dedication to Fulvia Celer, a perpetual priestess in Tarraco and priestess of Hispania Citerior, made by her freedmen according to her will.

EDCS-03400113.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Gavia Athenaïs, my dearest and most loving companion. Lucius Fabius Victor, freedman of the province, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-03400113.summary: A funerary dedication from Lucius Fabius Victor, a freedman of the province, to his companion Gavia Athenaïs.

EDCS-03400117.translation: To Munnia Severa, daughter of Lucius, perpetual priestess of Concordia Augusta of Tarraconenses. Novatianus, her son, remitted the expense by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-03400117.summary: A dedication to Munnia Severa, perpetual priestess in Tarraco, whose son Novatianus reduced the cost of the monument by order of the town council.

EDCS-03400119.translation: To Marcus Valerius Vindicus of Tarraco, who held all the offices in his state. His heirs.
EDCS-03400119.summary: A dedication by the heirs of Marcus Valerius Vindicus of Tarraco, acknowledging his public service.

EDCS-03400125.translation: To Galeria Atticus, by his first kindness.
EDCS-03400125.summary: A dedication to Galeria Atticus, likely a benefactor.

EDCS-03400128.translation: A plea for favour. Cacabulus, a native servant, junior messenger, to the Senate and People of Rome, and to the Roman People, a happy and good age, with the Augusti safe. Happy Tarraco.
EDCS-03400128.summary: An inscription from Cacabulus, a junior messenger, expressing good wishes for the Roman Empire and its people, dedicated in Tarraco.

EDCS-03400129.translation: To Atilia Valeria, daughter of Lucius, from Atilius Onesimus and Alcyone, her most affectionate patrons.
EDCS-03400129.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Atilia Valeria by her patrons.

EDCS-03400130.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Sulpicius Primitivus, VI vir of Tarraco, made this for his most beloved and deserving wife, Baebia Ursinna, of Ilerda.
EDCS-03400130.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Baebia Ursinna, made by her husband.

EDCS-03400131.translation: Marcus Fabius Asiaticus of Tarraco made this at his own expense for Marcus Fabius Paullinus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, who was honored with the public equestrian status by the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, as a token of the most devoted paternal friendships.
EDCS-03400131.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Marcus Fabius Asiaticus for Marcus Fabius Paullinus, who received equestrian status from Emperor Hadrian.

EDCS-03400132.translation: Lucius Minicius Pudens made this for Marcus Fabius Paullinus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, who was honored with the public equestrian status by the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, as his best student and friend.
EDCS-03400132.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Lucius Minicius Pudens for Marcus Fabius Paullinus, who received equestrian status from Emperor Hadrian.

EDCS-03400133.translation: Geminia Paterna, his granddaughter, made this for Titus Geminius Philippus, from the district of Bracara.
EDCS-03400133.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Geminia Paterna for her grandfather, Titus Geminius Philippus.

EDCS-03400134.translation: Quintus Voconius Rufinus Callacus was buried here, aged 35. Quieta Voconia made this for her well-deserving patron.
EDCS-03400134.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Quintus Voconius Rufinus Callacus, erected by his patron.

EDCS-03400135.translation: Raecia Irene made this for her son, Gaius Caecilius Potitus, of the Galeria tribe.
EDCS-03400135.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Raecia Irene for her son, Gaius Caecilius Potitus.

EDCS-03400136.translation: To Lucius Rufidius, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe.
EDCS-03400136.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Lucius Rufidius.

EDCS-03400137.translation: Afranius Dioscurides made this for his wife, Hortia Maxima Sardiana, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-03400137.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Afranius Dioscurides for his wife, Hortia Maxima Sardiana.

EDCS-03400138.translation: Made by Cornelia Marullina for her husband, Iulius Marcius Artemidorus, a native Dalmatian who lived 25 years, 4 months, and 2 days, with whom she lived [some years]; and by Marcia Marciana for the deserving Dalmatian.
EDCS-03400138.summary: This is a double funerary dedication for Iulius Marcius Artemidorus and another individual, erected by his wife and another relative.

EDCS-03400140.translation: To Liciniana, daughter of Publius Satrius Felix Secundus of the Arnensis tribe, his most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-03400140.summary: A funerary dedication from a son to his dutiful daughter, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-03400141.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Saturninus, from Mauretania Caesariensis, erected this monument for his most dutiful wife, Valeria Meletina; she will not be followed by an heir.
EDCS-03400141.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Meletina, set up by her husband Saturninus, stating that no heir will inherit this monument, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03400142.translation: Lucius Cornelius, a *seviri* (member of the *seviri Augustales*), and Cornelia, daughter of Lucius, and Cornelia.
EDCS-03400142.summary: A fragmented inscription mentioning Lucius Cornelius, a *seviri*, and two women named Cornelia, possibly a family group.

EDCS-03400143.translation: To Marcus Junius Celsus, who held the rank of *VI viri* and was an innkeeper from the *picaria* at the fountain; he rests here in his wine-cask.
EDCS-03400143.summary: A tomb inscription for Marcus Junius Celsus, a *VI viri* and an innkeeper, suggesting a date in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03400144.translation: Marcus Raecius Privatus, *VI vir* and magistrate of the Lares Augustales, ordered this monument to be made by his heirs according to his will for his daughter Raecia Liciniana, daughter of Marcus.
EDCS-03400144.summary: A funerary dedication by Marcus Raecius Privatus, a *VI vir* and magistrate, for his daughter Raecia Liciniana, with instructions for its construction.

EDCS-03400145.translation: To Gaius Sextius Valerius Firmus, son of Gaius, patron of the body of *sevir Augustales* of Tarraco, for the many kindnesses which Sextius Callistus [did for him]...
EDCS-03400145.summary: A dedication to Gaius Sextius Valerius Firmus, patron of the *sevir Augustales* of Tarraco, mentioning a benefactor named Sextius Callistus.

EDCS-03400148.summary: A very fragmentary inscription possibly referring to a group of *VI viri*.

EDCS-03400150.translation: Here lies buried the young man Aper, a metalworker, whose youthful integrity was well-regarded; you lived poorly, you were devoted to friends. You lived 32 years and 8 months. Oh sorrow! Oh tears! Where am I, wretched father, pouring these tears for you, my son, while I look for you? Behold, the light has fled and my limbs fail with grief. It would have been better if you had prepared this funeral for me. Underworld, if you have any sense, take away this wretched father from here, for I have already lost the light of my life by losing you, my son. If any passerby is going on this journey, stop for a little and re-read this inscription carved in marble with iron. What I, a father, did for my dearest son, this tomb will remain, and the bones will rest quietly. Farewell, my dearest son, forever.
EDCS-03400150.summary: An emotional epitaph for Aper, a young metalworker who died at 32, written by his grieving father, lamenting his son's early death and his own loss.

EDCS-03400152.translation: Julius Statutus was here, renowned for his great skill; he worked gold for men, women, and girls, full of all good things, happy in his morals and life and discipline. He was not content with one [source of income], he rejoiced in many friends; this was always his life. He left his descendants to wash themselves every morning and evening, those who would be his successors in his station, through whom the station and name of Statutus will live on. I, his first disciple, Secundinius Felicissimus, have written this, almost equal in age to three administrators, but only in name. Master, I have made these contrary gifts for you. I add this inscription to your tomb, so that you may keep your senses and your friends with me, wherever you may be, and so that whenever we speak the annual vows to you, we may also speak with pious voice: May the earth be light upon you in Carnuntum.
EDCS-03400152.summary: An epitaph for the goldsmith Julius Statutus, written by his disciple Secundinius Felicissimus, praising his skill, generosity, and good character, and dedicating the inscription to his memory at Carnuntum.

EDCS-03400153.translation: To Aurelius Silicius, who deserved well, from his associates.
EDCS-03400153.summary: A dedication from associates to Aurelius Silicius, likely a memorial or testimonial.

EDCS-03400154.translation: To Gaius Lutatius Marinus, Publius Aelius [---] his best friend.
EDCS-03400154.summary: This is a dedication to a friend, likely on a tombstone, from the Roman Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-03400156.translation: To Lucius Gavius Servatus, Gaius Clodius Charito his best friend.
EDCS-03400156.summary: This is a dedication to a friend, likely on a tombstone, from the Roman Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-03400159.translation: To Lucius Vibius Alcinous, Vibius Silvanus his freedman, his best patron.
EDCS-03400159.summary: This is a dedication to a patron by his freedman, likely on a tombstone, from the Roman Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-03400160.translation: To Vibius Felicula, Lucius Vibius Alcinous, his freedwoman and wife, his best.
EDCS-03400160.summary: This is a dedication to a wife and freedwoman by her husband, likely on a tombstone, from the Roman Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-03400166.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely records a person's name and parentage, possibly from a tombstone, date uncertain.

EDCS-03400171.translation: [---]us, Quintus Egnatius Lucius, Egnatius Caius, Sempronius [---]lus, Marinius II, Caius Terentius [---]tu, Lucius Porcius [---]
EDCS-03400171.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription listing several names, possibly a list of officials or members of a family, date uncertain.

EDCS-03400173.translation: To Achaïs, aged 30. Iulius Primus for his most excellent wife, from the burial place of Caius Satti Mercator.
EDCS-03400173.summary: This tombstone inscription records the death of Achaïs, wife of Iulius Primus, and mentions the burial plot of Caius Satti Mercator, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-03400174.translation: Freedman of Quintus. [---] Freedman of Quintus. [---] Cra[---] Freedman of Quintus.
EDCS-03400174.summary: This fragmentary inscription records freedmen of a Quintus, likely from a tombstone or funerary context, date uncertain.

EDCS-03400175.translation: To Publius Aelius E[---]. Publius Aelius V[---].
EDCS-03400175.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication to a Publius Aelius, possibly on a tombstone or dedication to a deity, date uncertain.

EDCS-03400176.translation: To the memory of Publius Aelius Philometor. Publius Aelius Philumenus and Valeria Iulia, to their most unhappy son, aged 27.
EDCS-03400176.summary: This tombstone inscription records the death of a young man named Publius Aelius Philometor, mourned by his parents, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-03400182.translation: To the departed spirits. Antonia Vernacla, a most unhappy mother, made this for her most dutiful and obedient son, Lucius Aemilius Valentis, who lived 22 years and 2 months, a deserving man.
EDCS-03400182.summary: This is a tombstone inscription from an unhappy mother dedicating a monument to her deceased son, Lucius Aemilius Valentis.

EDCS-03400183.translation: To the departed spirits. For Aemilia Tryphosa, aged 14. Caius Aemilius Chresimus and Crescentina made this for their dearest daughter.
EDCS-03400183.summary: This is a tombstone inscription from parents, Caius Aemilius Chresimus and Crescentina, dedicating a monument to their deceased daughter, Aemilia Tryphosa.

EDCS-03400184.translation: To the departed spirits. For Afra, his sweetest wife.
EDCS-03400184.summary: This is a tombstone inscription from a husband dedicating a monument to his deceased wife, Afra.

EDCS-03400186.summary: This is a tombstone inscription from Antistia dedicating a monument to Lucius Antistius.

EDCS-03400190.translation: Terentia Zenonilla made this for her husband, Aurelius Onesimus.
EDCS-03400190.summary: This is a tombstone inscription from Terentia Zenonilla dedicating a monument to her deceased husband, Aurelius Onesimus.

EDCS-03400191.translation: To the departed spirits. For Aurelia Pia, his most holy and incomparable wife, who lived with me more or less 14 years. May the earth be light for you, my sweet Luxuri, daughter of Lucius Pistus. Caius Lutatius Pistus made this.
EDCS-03400191.summary: This is a tombstone inscription from Caius Lutatius Pistus dedicating a monument to his deceased wife, Aurelia Pia, and expressing sorrow for their daughter, Luxuri.

EDCS-03400192.translation: Axsilia, freedwoman of Cnaeus. Axsilia, freedwoman of Cnaeus.
EDCS-03400192.summary: This appears to be a funerary marker or inscription for Axsilia, a freedwoman of Cnaeus, possibly indicating a shared burial plot or a repeated dedication.

EDCS-03400199.translation: To the departed spirits. For Gaius Caecilius Heraclidus.
EDCS-03400199.summary: This is a tombstone inscription dedicating a monument to Gaius Caecilius Heraclidus.

EDCS-03400200.translation: Callistus [made this] for Phoebas, his excellent freedwoman.
EDCS-03400200.summary: This is a tombstone inscription from Callistus dedicating a monument to his freedwoman, Phoebas.

EDCS-03400206.translation: To Gaius Clodius Amaranto, freedman of Gaius, and Saturnina, freedwoman of Lucius. Gaius Clodius Privatus, freedman of Caius, for his patrons and for himself.
EDCS-03400206.summary: This inscription records Gaius Clodius Privatus, a freedman, dedicating a monument for his patrons and himself, and also honoring fellow freedpersons Gaius Clodius Amaranto and Saturnina.

EDCS-03400208.summary: A fragmentary inscription that likely records a dedication or a familial relationship between two individuals named Cornelius.

EDCS-03400209.translation: Publius Cornelius Arabus lies here. P
EDCS-03400209.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Cornelius Arabus, indicating his burial location.

EDCS-03400211.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Cornelia Decimina made this for her well-deserving husband, Papirius Successus.
EDCS-03400211.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Cornelia Decimina to her deserving husband, Papirius Successus.

EDCS-03400212.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Titus Marius Aurelianus Tarracius arranged for this to be made for his dearest and most obedient wife, Cornelia Tiberina, as she deserved.
EDCS-03400212.summary: A funerary epitaph commissioned by Titus Marius Aurelianus Tarracius for his deserving wife, Cornelia Tiberina.

EDCS-03400215.translation: Domitius Crescentinus made this for himself and his descendants while alive.
EDCS-03400215.summary: A funerary monument established by Domitius Crescentinus for himself and his descendants during his lifetime.

EDCS-03400218.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Fabius Augendus made this for his well-deserving patron, Fabius Festus, who lived 60 years.
EDCS-03400218.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Fabius Augendus to his deserving patron, Fabius Festus, who lived to be 60.

EDCS-03400219.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Fabia Festa, farewell. Fabius Festus greets you. Farewell.
EDCS-03400219.summary: A funerary inscription addressed to the deceased Fabia Festa by Fabius Festus.

EDCS-03400220.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Fabia Protis made this for her excellent and deserving husband, Marcus Porcius Thallus.
EDCS-03400220.summary: A funerary epitaph created by Fabia Protis for her deserving husband, Marcus Porcius Thallus.

EDCS-03400221.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Porcia Flaminina made this for her most dutiful mother, Fabia Protis.
EDCS-03400221.summary: A funerary epitaph erected by Porcia Flaminina for her devoted mother, Fabia Protis.

EDCS-03400223.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Felicianus, who lived 17 years, 8 months, and 10 days. His most unhappy mother, Iulia Felicitas, had this made.
EDCS-03400223.summary: A funerary epitaph for Felicianus, detailing his age at death, and commissioned by his grieving mother, Iulia Felicitas.

EDCS-03400224.summary: This is a dedication to Flavius, likely a person of high standing given the epithet 'tripiissimo', possibly a family member or patron.

EDCS-03400228.translation: Fortunatiana, an unhappy mother, [dedicated this] to her most dutiful son, Lucius Concordius Fortunatianus, who lived 25 years and 19 days.
EDCS-03400228.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Lucius Concordius Fortunatianus, erected by his mother, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-03400230.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Lucius Gavius Restitutus, an unhappy father, [dedicated this] to his most beloved and most missed daughter, Gavia Marciana, who lived 37 years, 3 months, and 13 days.
EDCS-03400230.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Gavia Marciana, erected by her grieving father, Lucius Gavius Restitutus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-03400231.translation: Genialis and Sutoria Restituta made [this] for her husband and his most dutiful father, from their own resources.
EDCS-03400231.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Genialis and Sutoria Restituta for her husband and his father, implying a familial relationship and possibly a shared tomb, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-03400233.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Ippodanya had this made for her deserving husband, Gellius Licinianus.
EDCS-03400233.summary: This is a funerary dedication commissioned by Ippodanya for her husband, Gellius Licinianus, indicating a loving marriage, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-03400237.translation: Marcus Iulius Maxumus, soldier of the VI Legion, [belonging to the] century of Valerius.
EDCS-03400237.summary: This is a military inscription identifying Marcus Iulius Maxumus as a soldier in the VI Legion, serving in the century commanded by Valerius, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-03400238.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Lucius Iunius Dionysius, who lived 3 years, 5 months, and 6 days. Lucius Iunius Dionysius and Naevia Tryphera [dedicated this] to their most sweet son.
EDCS-03400238.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a young child named Lucius Iunius Dionysius, erected by his parents, Lucius Iunius Dionysius and Naevia Tryphera, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-03400240.translation: To the spirits of the departed: For Symphorus, to his deserving wife Dionysia.
EDCS-03400240.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Dionysia, a deserving wife, by her husband Symphorus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-03400241.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Lucius Mamilius Fortunatus, freedman of Mamilia Prisca, made [this] for himself and his descendants while alive.
EDCS-03400241.summary: This is a funerary inscription commissioned by Lucius Mamilius Fortunatus, a freedman, for himself and his family, indicating his status and foresight, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-03400244.translation: Melpomene, slave of Lucius Minicius Rufus, aged 18 years, is buried here.
EDCS-03400244.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Melpomene, a slave of Lucius Minicius Rufus, who died at the age of 18, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-03400245.translation: To the spirits of the departed: For Minicia, aged 20. Lucius Minicius Niloticus for his freedwoman and wife, most cherished and most beloved, and for himself.
EDCS-03400245.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Minicia, set up by her husband and former owner, Lucius Minicius Niloticus.

EDCS-03400247.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Callistus made this for his most beloved companion Nymphidia, who deserved well of him.
EDCS-03400247.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Nymphidia, dedicated by her companion Callistus.

EDCS-03400249.translation: To Aelia Severa for her excellent husband.
EDCS-03400249.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an unnamed husband, dedicated by his wife Aelia Severa.

EDCS-03400250.translation: To the spirits of the departed: For Sextus P. Ursicinus, who lived 19 years, 5 months, 29 days. His mother P. Lauriola made this for her most dutiful son, who deserved well.
EDCS-03400250.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sextus P. Ursicinus, erected by his mother.

EDCS-03400251.translation: For Titus Paccio Quartinus, son of Titus, aged 18. Primilla, his slave.
EDCS-03400251.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Paccio Quartinus, likely set up by his slave Primilla.

EDCS-03400255.translation: Hail, Governor! To the spirits of the departed: For Plotia Saturnina, aged 28 years, 9 months, 13 days. Aelius Melpon for his most excellent and most longed-for wife, with whom he lived 12 years, 5 months, 8 days, and Plotia Iucunda, his daughter, for their sweetest mother, who deserved well, made this.
EDCS-03400255.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Plotia Saturnina, erected by her husband Aelius Melpon and daughter Plotia Iucunda.

EDCS-03400256.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely the beginning of a name on a tomb or monument.

EDCS-03400260.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Lucius Popilius Prunicus, aged 25, is buried here. Gaius Cornelius Primigenius, his father.
EDCS-03400260.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Popilius Prunicus, erected by his father Gaius Cornelius Primigenius.

EDCS-03400261.translation: To the spirits of the departed: For Porcia Corinthis. Iulius Statutus for his most cherished wife.
