EDCS-03400261.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Porcia Corinthis, dedicated by her husband Iulius Statutus.

EDCS-03400263.translation: Praecilia Ianuaria made this for her most dutiful mother, to the spirits of the departed.
EDCS-03400263.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an unnamed mother, dedicated by her daughter Praecilia Ianuaria.

EDCS-03400265.translation: To the memory of Publicia Alpina.
EDCS-03400265.summary: This is a memorial inscription for Publicia Alpina.

EDCS-03400266.translation: Sempronia Ianuaria made this for her most dutiful son Rutilianus, aged 18.
EDCS-03400266.summary: Sempronia Ianuaria dedicated this monument to her son Rutilianus, aged 18.

EDCS-03400268.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Lucius Sempronius Paternus.

EDCS-03400269.summary: This inscription identifies Sempronia Lychnis as a grandmother.

EDCS-03400270.translation: To Sempronia Meroe, aged 22, and to Sempronia Albina, most dutiful; Cornelius Rhetoricus [dedicated this].
EDCS-03400270.summary: Cornelius Rhetoricus dedicated this inscription to Sempronia Meroe (aged 22) and Sempronia Albina.

EDCS-03400271.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Sempronia Ursa, who lived with me for 25 years. Fulvius Fronto to his most virtuous and deserving wife.
EDCS-03400271.summary: Fulvius Fronto dedicated this funerary monument to his wife Sempronia Ursa, who lived with him for 25 years.

EDCS-03400273.translation: Lucius Septimius Celer to Atilius Fuscus.
EDCS-03400273.summary: Lucius Septimius Celer dedicated this inscription to Atilius Fuscus.

EDCS-03400274.translation: Made this for the most dutiful and deserving [person]. Terentius Hermippus.
EDCS-03400274.summary: Terentius Hermippus dedicated this monument to a deserving individual.

EDCS-03400276.summary: This is a partial inscription, likely a dedication to a woman named Tertia.

EDCS-03400277.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Domitia Themis. Felicla, freedwoman.
EDCS-03400277.summary: Felicla, a freedwoman, dedicated this funerary inscription to Domitia Themis.

EDCS-03400278.summary: This is a tombstone marking the death of Trophimus at the age of 10.

EDCS-03400281.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Fannius Saturninus, a relative, took care of decorating the tomb for Valerius Rogatus, who lived 48 years.
EDCS-03400281.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Fannius Saturninus for Valerius Rogatus, who died at age 48.

EDCS-03400282.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Valerius Victor, the best son, and Cornelia Flora, who lived 9 years, some months, some hours.
EDCS-03400282.summary: This is a tombstone for Valerius Victor and Cornelia Flora, who died at the ages of 9 years, some months, and some hours.

EDCS-03400283.translation: For the memory of Decimus Fabius, husband and most beloved, aged 22 years, some months. Valeria Saturnina.
EDCS-03400283.summary: This is a tombstone for Decimus Fabius, who died at the age of 22 years and some months, erected by his wife Valeria Saturnina.

EDCS-03400306.translation: Libonius lived 2 months and 9 days. He made this for one deserving well.
EDCS-03400306.summary: This is a tombstone for Libonius, who died at 2 months and 9 days old.

EDCS-03400307.translation: Here lies S. Magia Mar, who lived...
EDCS-03400307.summary: This is a tombstone marking the death of S. Magia Mar.

EDCS-03400321.summary: This is a fragment of a tombstone, likely referring to the age of a child.

EDCS-03400322.summary: This is a fragment of a tombstone, likely referring to the age of a son.

EDCS-03400332.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication by or for a father and mother, possibly on a tomb.

EDCS-03400347.summary: This inscription likely refers to Publicus Symmachus, perhaps as the subject of a dedication or as a tombstone.

EDCS-03400376.translation: Severus, about to die, may Nemesis, victor at last, more cautiously guard us and our shields.
EDCS-03400376.summary: This is a funerary inscription, possibly a curse or a plea for protection after death, from an unknown individual addressing Nemesis.

EDCS-03400387.summary: A very fragmentary inscription, likely the end of a name and possibly a cognomen, possibly identifying an owner or deceased person.

EDCS-03400392.summary: A fragmentary inscription that appears to record the name of a freedwoman, Nna Lena.

EDCS-03400457.summary: A very fragmentary inscription that seems to start with a reference to Vespasian, possibly indicating a date or dedication.

EDCS-03400483.summary: This inscription records a small jug belonging to someone named Donatus.

EDCS-03400487.translation: He cultivated the wondrous poet John in Tarraco, and laid his limbs to rest in this place in peace; in you, Libra, he held a balance of character, in you, modesty reigned, shining with eloquence, most gentle you were; bearing in your heart care for the poor, endowed with ample piety, holy indeed, and in faith you appeared more magnificently to all, proceeding to the rewards of Christ. Your name indeed, and your most sweet mind, future generations will praise; never to be abolished through the ages, they extol deeds with praise, diminishing in the equal balance of decades of years. You presided as ruler and teacher over monks and peoples, happily spending eighty years of life.
EDCS-03400487.summary: This is a funerary inscription celebrating the life and virtues of a holy man, possibly a poet and monastic leader, who died after eighty years of life, and was buried in Tarraco.

EDCS-03400488.translation: Wise, magnanimous, pious, with a pure disposition, here rests in the tomb Sergius, a holy pontiff, who, restoring the sacred, crumbling roofs of the temple, built a monastery not far from the city. The poor had this father as their protector, the orphans as their guardian, he found solace for widows, a ransom for captives, nourishment for the hungry. He was generous with tears, repelled the contagions of the flesh, most beloved by all, abounding in abundant grace, sparing in abundance, rich to the needy. He lived seventy years of this present age, and completed seventeen years of his priestly and religious life.
EDCS-03400488.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sergius, a holy pontiff and monastery founder, who was known for his piety, charity, and restoration of a temple, and who lived to be at least 87 years old (70 + 17).

EDCS-03400489.translation: Stephanus of Alexandria, in honor of God and all the saints, on the 8th day before the Ides of April, in the third year of his ordination, with his own, under the pontificate of Bishop George, let the seal be here.
EDCS-03400489.summary: This is a funerary inscription or dedication, dated to the third year of Stephanus of Alexandria's ordination, during the pontificate of Bishop George.

EDCS-03400490.translation: Here rests the most holy man Cyprian, bishop of the first see of the city of Tarragona, laid to rest in this tomb on the eighth day before the Kalends of May, in peace.
EDCS-03400490.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cyprian, a bishop of Tarragona, who died on April 24th and was buried in peace.

EDCS-03400492.translation: He gave back his soul; may he rest in peace, under the consulship of Decentius Caesar and Paulus.
EDCS-03400492.summary: This is a short funerary inscription, likely from the late Roman period, stating that someone has died and requesting peace for their soul.

EDCS-03400493.translation: Marturia lived 4 years and departed in the first consulate of Eugenius Augustus; may Marturia live among the blessed.
EDCS-03400493.summary: This epitaph records the short life and hopeful afterlife of a person named Marturia, dating to the first consulate of Eugenius Augustus.

EDCS-03400494.translation: Euplentia rested here in peace on the Ides of January, after the consulate of Aetius and Studius, most distinguished men, in peace.
EDCS-03400494.summary: This epitaph marks the burial place of Euplentia, who died after the consulate of Aetius and Studius.

EDCS-03400495.translation: The honorable Aventinus lies here, 40 years old, born in the 13th year of Honorius and 10th of Theodosius, and was laid to rest on the fifth day before the Kalends of January, during the coming consulate of Magnus; he rests in peace.
EDCS-03400495.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Aventinus, detailing his age, birth relative to the reigns of Emperors Honorius and Theodosius, and the date of his interment, which occurred before the consulate of Magnus.

EDCS-03400496.translation: Rusticus lies here, awaiting resurrection in eternal life; he lived ninety years and died on the first day of the Kalends of July, after the consulate of Severus and Iordanis, most distinguished men, as consuls.
EDCS-03400496.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Rusticus, who lived for ninety years and died on the Kalends of July, with a profession of belief in the resurrection, dating after the consulate of Severus and Iordanis.

EDCS-03400498.translation: Aemilius rests in peace; he lived 2 years.
EDCS-03400498.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Aemilius, who died at the age of two.

EDCS-03400499.summary: This epitaph is a prayer for the peaceful repose of Aemilia.

EDCS-03400508.translation: Victorinus made this for his dearest wife, Aurelia Aemilia, who lived 17 years, 9 months, and 17 days, to a well-deserving person.
EDCS-03400508.summary: This epitaph is a dedication by Victorinus to his wife Aurelia Aemilia, commemorating her life of 17 years, 9 months, and 17 days.

EDCS-03400510.translation: To the memory of Aurelia Tonneia, most holy and faithful woman.
EDCS-03400510.summary: This is a memorial inscription dedicated to Aurelia Tonneia, described as a most holy and faithful woman.

EDCS-03400514.translation: The name Crispines lives on; may he dwell in eternity. Amen. Peace in God.
EDCS-03400514.summary: This inscription expresses a wish for Crispines to live eternally, reflecting a belief in peace in God.

EDCS-03400518.translation: What your wife Festela Quommia gave to you, O [---], this offering ought rather to have been yours.
EDCS-03400518.summary: This epitaph is a lament by Festela Quommia, suggesting she provided a burial or memorial for someone who should have reciprocated the gesture.

EDCS-03400520.translation: To the good memory of Leucadius, chief of the domestic guard, who lived with his wife Nonnita for 25 years, buried the day before the Kalends of January; he lived, moreover, for more or less sixty years in total.
EDCS-03400520.summary: This epitaph records the death and lifespan of Leucadius, a chief of the domestic guard, and his wife Nonnita.

EDCS-03400524.summary: This is a short personal wish for a person named Macarius, possibly inscribed on a tomb.

EDCS-03400525.translation: Here is light, here is peace, the spirit of Marturi rested in peace.
EDCS-03400525.summary: This epitaph marks the resting place of Marturi, emphasizing peace and spiritual presence.

EDCS-03400526.translation: Messor rested here in peace, aged 3 years and 14 days, on the day before the Kalends of December.
EDCS-03400526.summary: This epitaph records the death of Messor, who died at the age of 3 years and 14 days.

EDCS-03400527.summary: This is a simple epitaph identifying the burial place of Metopius.

EDCS-03400529.translation: The memory of Paulinus departed on the 8th day before the Ides of August; he lived 26 years.
EDCS-03400529.summary: This epitaph commemorates Paulinus, recording his death date and age at death.

EDCS-03400530.translation: Here rests Peter, who lived [---] years, rests.
EDCS-03400530.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Peter, but the lifespan is incomplete.

EDCS-03400531.summary: This is a simple epitaph stating that Pipelles rests in peace.

EDCS-03400532.translation: Pompeia lived in peace for five years.
EDCS-03400532.summary: This epitaph records that Pompeia lived for five years.

EDCS-03400534.summary: This is an incomplete inscription likely commemorating Chris, son of Priscus.

EDCS-03400536.translation: Prorcaria lived in peace for twenty-five years.
EDCS-03400536.summary: This is a tombstone epitaph for Prorcaria, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-03400538.translation: Ringilius, may you rest in peace. Lived 5 years and 8 months.
EDCS-03400538.summary: This is a tombstone epitaph for Ringilius, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-03400540.translation: Serapia, may you live joyfully in God. Martorius.
EDCS-03400540.summary: This is a Christian epitaph likely from a tomb, invoking blessings for Serapia.

EDCS-03400548.translation: Titzanus Karpitanus lived thirty-five years in peace.
EDCS-03400548.summary: This is a tombstone epitaph for Titzanus Karpitanus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-03400549.translation: To Valeria, my wife, with whom I lived.
EDCS-03400549.summary: This is a tombstone epitaph dedicated to Valeria by her husband.

EDCS-03400551.summary: This is a tombstone epitaph for Urbica, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-03400553.translation: Here rests Vigilia, handmaid of God, in peace.
EDCS-03400553.summary: This is a Christian tombstone epitaph for Vigilia, identifying her as a servant of God.

EDCS-03400567.translation: Sweet and dear, you rest in the seat of the saints, for two years and four months; thus you have earned your reward.
EDCS-03400567.summary: This is a Christian epitaph, likely from a tomb, for a deceased person who is described as resting with the saints.

EDCS-03400587.summary: This is a tombstone epitaph for Iulias MI, indicating rest.

EDCS-03400608.translation: To the departed spirits. To Ru, the deserving.
EDCS-03400608.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the 'Dis Manibus' (departed spirits), likely for someone named Ru.

EDCS-03400629.translation: May peace and faithfulness be upon Israel and upon his children and upon his descendants. Amen.
EDCS-03400629.summary: This is a Hebrew inscription invoking peace and faithfulness, likely a blessing or a dedication.

EDCS-03700447.summary: This is a simple epitaph or identification marker for Secundus Caetus, a wool-merchant.

EDCS-03700452.translation: Masculus of the Legio VII Gemina.
EDCS-03700452.summary: This inscription refers to a soldier named Masculus from the Legio VII Gemina.

EDCS-03700453.translation: Severus of the Legio VII Gemina.
EDCS-03700453.summary: This inscription refers to a soldier named Severus from the Legio VII Gemina.

EDCS-03700454.translation: Lucius of the Legio VII Gemina.
EDCS-03700454.summary: This inscription refers to a soldier named Lucius from the Legio VII Gemina.

EDCS-04900520.summary: This is an epitaph recording the death of Victorinus, who is stated to have lived to the remarkable age of 120 years.

EDCS-04900522.translation: Ausuruena placed this altar for the god Anduresonus.
EDCS-04900522.summary: This is a dedication of an altar by Ausuruena to the god Anduresonus.

EDCS-04900526.translation: To the departed spirits below. Fusia placed this for her husband Stanerus for his deserts, after more or less 30 years.
EDCS-04900526.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Fusia for her husband Stanerus, commemorating his merits after approximately 30 years of marriage.

EDCS-04900528.translation: Tritia, daughter of Pinton, aged 60 years.
EDCS-04900528.summary: This is an epitaph recording the death of Tritia, daughter of Pinton, at the age of 60.

EDCS-04900529.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Annia Reburra, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-04900530.translation: To Flaccinus, son of Flaccus, aged [---].
EDCS-04900530.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, likely for a person named Flaccinus, son of Flaccus, with the age missing.

EDCS-04900532.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Docina, daughter of Rufus, who died at the age of 65.

EDCS-04900555.summary: This inscription likely records ownership or a dedication related to a horse named Amor.

EDCS-04900597.translation: Urn of Metreta, son of Marcus.
EDCS-04900597.summary: This inscription identifies an urn belonging to Metreta, son of Marcus.

EDCS-04900599.summary: This is a dedication by Gaius Aticus, likely for a building or monument.

EDCS-04900600.translation: Mari, son of Laro, son of Eronis, son of Pharion, son of Eronis.
EDCS-04900600.summary: This inscription records a lengthy patronymic lineage for an individual named Mari.

EDCS-04900602.translation: Lucius Lucilius Numerius, freedman of Lucius.
EDCS-04900602.summary: This is an identification of an individual, Lucius Lucilius Numerius, who was the freedman of a Lucius.

EDCS-04900606.translation: To the spirits of the departed. His most unhappy mother made this for her son Nepotianus, who lived for 8 years, 8 months, and 19 days, well-deserving.
EDCS-04900606.summary: This is a deeply personal funerary epitaph from a grieving mother for her young son Nepotianus, who died after 8 years, 8 months, and 19 days.

EDCS-05000463.translation: Quintus Perennius Reve, son of Vaecius, willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Tebiecus.
EDCS-05000463.summary: This is a votive inscription where Quintus Perennius Reve pays a vow to Tebiecus.

EDCS-05000467.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Titiana, aged 45, is placed here. May the earth rest lightly upon you, Quintia, most devoted sister, and Vindemitor, most indulgent husband.
EDCS-05000467.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a relative for Titiana, her sister Quintia, and her husband Vindemitor.

EDCS-05000471.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Quintilius.
EDCS-05000471.summary: A brief funerary dedication to Quintilius.

EDCS-05000482.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurinus, son of Primulus.

EDCS-05000494.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Saturnina, aged 40, is placed here. May the earth rest lightly upon you, daughter. Aurelius, aged 11, is placed here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Maritima, aged 50, dutiful in her own home, is placed here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-05000494.summary: A funerary epitaph for Saturnina, her son Aurelius, and possibly Maritima, with wishes for peace in the afterlife.

EDCS-05200353.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Ate.
EDCS-05200353.summary: A short funerary dedication to Ate.

EDCS-05200355.translation: Caius Narcisius Rufus fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-05200355.summary: An inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by Caius Narcisius Rufus.

EDCS-05200359.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Fulvius Ferronius, aged 72, is placed here.
EDCS-05200359.summary: A funerary epitaph for Fulvius Ferronius.

EDCS-05200360.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Claudia Accula Zoela, aged 40, is placed here. Claudius Sergius set this up for his wife.
EDCS-05200360.summary: A funerary epitaph for Claudia Accula Zoela, set up by her husband Claudius Sergius.

EDCS-05200365.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Creseme, mother, set this up for her most devoted daughter, aged 40, and for her most devoted grandson.
EDCS-05200365.summary: A funerary monument set up by a mother for her daughter and grandson.

EDCS-05200373.translation: Antestia Oandissena, daughter of Lucius, aged 11, is buried here.
EDCS-05200373.summary: A funerary epitaph for Antestia Oandissena, daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-05200383.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus Crocius Proculinus had this made for his most dutiful wife, Fabia Materna.
EDCS-05200383.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a husband for his wife, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05200388.translation: To Africania Maxima, daughter of Marci, from the workshop of F. Melybus.
EDCS-05200388.summary: This inscription appears to record a dedication or memorial to Africania Maxima, possibly by a craftsman or workshop.

EDCS-05200391.translation: Sextus gladly paid his vow, as was deserved.
EDCS-05200391.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by Sextus.

EDCS-05200419.summary: This is a simple gentilial inscription, likely identifying an individual and his lineage.

EDCS-05200426.translation: Modestus, aged 85, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05200426.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Modestus, providing his age and a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-05500903.translation: Lucius Vibius Priscus, from Togoti, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-05500903.summary: This inscription records Lucius Vibius Priscus fulfilling a vow, possibly related to a military or public service context.

EDCS-05500956.translation: To Sempronia Fusca, from Sextus Nymphodotus, his wife. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500956.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Sextus Nymphodotus for his wife, Sempronia Fusca.

EDCS-05501249.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Valeria Saturnia, aged 40, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501249.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Saturnia, providing her age and a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-05501710.translation: Bomartthus of the Retugeni is buried here.
EDCS-05501710.summary: This inscription records the burial of Bomartthus, identifying his ethnic origin.

EDCS-05501723.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication to Durius by Caius Iulius Pylades.

EDCS-05501724.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cassia Miduttia, likely a private tombstone from the Roman period.

EDCS-05501725.translation: Iulia Avita, daughter of Marcus, is laid here.
EDCS-05501725.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Avita, indicating her father's name, likely a private tombstone from the Roman period.

EDCS-05501726.translation: Saurus, freedman of Sextus Arquus Cimbrius, willingly paid his vow deservedly.
EDCS-05501726.summary: This is a votive inscription recording a freedman, Saurus, fulfilling a vow to a deity, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501729.translation: Flavus, son of Corolleas, willingly paid his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest deservedly.
EDCS-05501729.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Flavus fulfilling a vow to Jupiter, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501730.translation: Potitus of Tameobriga, son of Cumelus, willingly paid his father's vow deservedly.
EDCS-05501730.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Potitus fulfilling his father's vow, possibly indicating a family religious obligation from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501732.translation: Sacred to the Underworld spirits. Vir dedicated this to his deserving spouse Severus, aged 23.
EDCS-05501732.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Severus, noting his age and relationship to the dedicator, Vir, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501735.translation: Dedicated to the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Augustus Pius Felix, by Marcus Valerius Carus and Marcus Valerius Proculinus, Prefects.
EDCS-05501735.summary: This is an official dedication to Emperor Gordian III, likely from a public monument, overseen by two prefects during his reign in the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-05501736.translation: [---] to the S aitrini gods [---] years.
EDCS-05501736.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely recording a dedication to local deities, with missing context about the dedicator and date.

EDCS-05501737.translation: Niger, son of Proculus, willingly paid his vow to the Lar es of Cerenae.
EDCS-05501737.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Niger fulfilling a vow to the household gods of Cerenae, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501738.translation: Flavus willingly paid his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest deservedly.
EDCS-05501738.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Flavus fulfilling a vow to Jupiter, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501740.translation: Cornelius, son of Reburrus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05501740.summary: This is a votive inscription by Cornelius, son of Reburrus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-05501742.translation: The soldiers of the Seventh Gemina Pious Legion fulfilled their vow; Catullinus erected it.
EDCS-05501742.summary: This inscription records soldiers of the Seventh Gemina Pious Legion fulfilling a vow, with Catullinus as the dedicator.

EDCS-05501744.translation: Poanto, son of Iadiise, Urrus, of the Campi OEIC, aged 47, [dedicates this].
EDCS-05501744.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely records the age and origin of an individual named Urrus, possibly related to Poanto and Iadiise.

EDCS-05501745.summary: This is a simple dedication or identification of Silvanus, son of Severus.

EDCS-05501746.translation: To the spirits of the dead; to Reburrus, son of Lucius Cornelius Flacillus and Morinis.
EDCS-05501746.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Reburrus, son of Lucius Cornelius Flacillus and Morinis.

EDCS-05501747.translation: Alius Reburrus fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-05501747.summary: This is a votive inscription by Alius Reburrus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-05501750.translation: Gaius C. Calpurnius Rufinus, most distinguished man, dedicated for all time this lake, the temple, and all the Deities and spirits, in which sacrifices are consumed by vow.
EDCS-05501750.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a lake, temple, and deities by Gaius C. Calpurnius Rufinus, indicating its use for sacrificial rites.

EDCS-05501751.translation: Gaius C. Calpurnius Rufinus, most distinguished man, erected [this] to the Most High Serapis, together with the inner sanctuary and the mysteries.
EDCS-05501751.summary: This inscription records Gaius C. Calpurnius Rufinus dedicating a sanctuary and mysteries to the god Serapis.

EDCS-05501757.translation: To the spirits of the dead; to Iulius Hispanus.
EDCS-05501757.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Iulius Hispanus.

EDCS-05501758.translation: Aelius Albinus rests here; may the earth be light upon him.
EDCS-05501758.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aelius Albinus, expressing a wish for a light burial.

EDCS-05501762.translation: Medamus Camali Bormanicus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05501762.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Medamus Camali Bormanicus, who gladly fulfilled his vow.

EDCS-05501763.translation: Caius Pompeius Motugenus, son of Galeria Caturonis, from Uxama, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the god Bormanicus; whoever holds this honor, may your glory preserve you and command your boy not to leave this stone.
EDCS-05501763.summary: This is a votive inscription by Caius Pompeius Motugenus to the god Bormanicus, including a curse against anyone who might disturb the monument.

EDCS-05501764.translation: Reburrinus the stonecutter gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05501764.summary: This is a votive inscription by Reburrinus, a stonecutter, who fulfilled his vow.

EDCS-05501765.translation: Flavus, son of Flavius, the fuller, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Genius of Laquiniesium.
EDCS-05501765.summary: This is a votive inscription by Flavus to the Genius of Laquiniesium.

EDCS-05501766.translation: Flavius Aventinus fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest for his wife ENCRATI.
EDCS-05501766.summary: This is a votive inscription by Flavius Aventinus to Jupiter for his wife.

EDCS-05501767.translation: To Juno Regina, Minerva, Sol, Luna, all-powerful gods, Fortune, Mercury, the Genius of Jupiter, the Genius of Mars, Aesculapius, Luci, Somnus, Venus, Cupid, Caelus, HI OIBVS, Ceres, the Genius of Victory, my Genius, the perpetual gods of this place, and of death, IAII C C C R C O S CINNS GL.
EDCS-05501767.summary: This is a long dedication to a multitude of gods and spirits.

EDCS-05501768.translation: Titus Flavius Archelaus Claudianus, legate of the emperor, dedicated this.
EDCS-05501768.summary: This is a dedication made by Titus Flavius Archelaus Claudianus, an imperial legate.

EDCS-05501769.translation: Juno of the MEIRVRNARUM, in the consulship of Quintillus and Priscus.
EDCS-05501769.summary: This is an inscription mentioning Juno of the MEIRVRNARUM during the consulship of Quintillus and Priscus.

EDCS-05501774.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Provincialis Nereus, freedman of the province, to his dearest wife, Provinciali Protidias, aged 26.
EDCS-05501774.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Provincialis Nereus to his wife, Provinciali Protidias.

EDCS-05501775.translation: Marcus, according to his vow, to Asclepius and Hygieia.
EDCS-05501775.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus to Asclepius and Hygieia.

EDCS-05501776.translation: To the holy god Eventus, Flavius Fronto dedicated [this] by command.
EDCS-05501776.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Eventus by Flavius Fronto, likely a private offering based on a directive.

EDCS-05501777.translation: Flavius Urbicius erected this sacred offering to the Genius of the Market, according to his vow.
EDCS-05501777.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the Genius of the Market by Flavius Urbicius.

EDCS-05501778.translation: Durmia Pusinna erected this by vow to Jupiter the Repeller.
EDCS-05501778.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Depulsor by Durmia Pusinna.

EDCS-05501779.translation: Aemilius Crescens, companion of the most illustrious man Triarius Maternus, the legal official, and his wife Procula, freely and deservedly paid his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest for their health.
EDCS-05501779.summary: This inscription records a vow paid by Aemilius Crescens for the health of Triarius Maternus and his wife Procula, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05501780.translation: Sacred to Augusta Isis. Lucretia Fida, her perpetual priestess, and the conventus of the Augusti of Bracara Augusta dedicated [this].
EDCS-05501780.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Isis Augusta by Lucretia Fida, her priestess, and a collegial body from Bracara Augusta.

EDCS-05501781.translation: Flavius Albinus freely and deservedly paid his vow to the household gods of the street.
EDCS-05501781.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the Lares Vicinales (household gods of the street) by Flavius Albinus.

EDCS-05501784.translation: Titus Caelicus Sipipes Fronto and Marcus and Lucius, sons of Titus, great-grandsons of Caelicus Fronto, restored [this].
EDCS-05501784.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a monument by descendants of Caelicus Fronto, likely a family tomb or building.

EDCS-05501788.translation: To Lucius Terentius Rufus, son of Marcus, of the tribe Quirina, prefect of the VI cohort of Britons, centurion of the I Minervia Pia Fidelis legion, decorated with gifts by Emperor Trajan in the first Dacian War, chief centurion of the XV Apollinaris legion, tribune of the II cohort of vigiles, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05501788.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Terentius Rufus, detailing his distinguished military career from prefect to tribune, including service under Emperor Trajan and decorations for the Dacian Wars.

EDCS-05501789.translation: Marcus Antonius Augustanus, son of Marcus, of the tribe Galeria, soldier of the VII Gemina Felix legion, of the century of Mamilius Lucanus, aged 45, is buried here with his 18 years of service; Sempronius Graecinus, his heir, saw to it that it was made.
EDCS-05501789.summary: This inscription is a tombstone for Marcus Antonius Augustanus, a soldier of the VII Gemina Felix legion, detailing his age, service length, and that his heir arranged the burial.

EDCS-05501791.translation: Sacred offering for Nigrina, aged 50, flaminica of the province Hispania Citerior, who served well; her heir erected [this].
EDCS-05501791.summary: This inscription is a votive offering for Nigrina, a priestess (flaminica) of Hispania Citerior, erected by her heir.

EDCS-05501794.translation: Tarquinus, aged eleven, is buried here.
EDCS-05501794.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tarquinus, an eleven-year-old boy.

EDCS-05501795.translation: Agathopus, fellow slave of Titus Satrius Zethus.
EDCS-05501795.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Agathopus, a fellow slave of Titus Satrius Zethus.

EDCS-05501796.translation: Amaranthus, son of Senecio, is buried here.
EDCS-05501796.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Amaranthus, son of Senecio.

EDCS-05501797.translation: Apilus, son of Arquius, immunis (exempt), of PERIFV [cohort?] for nineteen years, of the century of Agrippa, is buried here.
EDCS-05501797.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Apilus, an immunis (exempt soldier) of Agrippa's century, who served for nineteen years.

EDCS-05501799.translation: Melcaecus, son of Pelistus, erected this monument for Arquius Viriati son, who is buried in the fortress of Acripia.
EDCS-05501799.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Melcaecus for Arquius Viriati, who served at the fortress of Acripia.

EDCS-05501800.translation: To his daughter Materna, the best and dearest mother, Caius Caecilius Sempiternus, son of an incomparable pietas (duty/piety).
EDCS-05501800.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Caius Caecilius Sempiternus to his daughter Materna, praising her pietas.

EDCS-05501801.translation: To Aulus Caelus, son of Titus, of the tribe Quirina, called Flaccus.
EDCS-05501801.summary: This is an inscription dedicating something to Aulus Caelus Flaccus, son of Titus, from the tribe Quirina.

EDCS-05501802.translation: To Titus Caelus, son of Titus, of the tribe Quirina, called Flaccus.
EDCS-05501802.summary: This is an inscription dedicating something to Titus Caelus Flaccus, son of Titus, from the tribe Quirina.

EDCS-05501803.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. [His daughter?] gonus Encrati erected [this].
EDCS-05501803.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the departed spirits, likely erected by a daughter for her mother.

EDCS-05501804.translation: Faustus, slave of Julia Severa, aged nineteen, is buried here.
EDCS-05501804.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Faustus, a nineteen-year-old slave of Julia Severa.

EDCS-05501806.translation: Helenus, slave of Talavus, aged 30, is buried here.
EDCS-05501806.summary: This is a tombstone for Helenus, a slave who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-05501807.translation: Iulius Pilides Orestes is buried here.
EDCS-05501807.summary: This is a tombstone for Iulius Pilides Orestes.

EDCS-05501808.translation: Lucretius Saturninus, son of Lucius, of the tribe Quirina, and Quintus [---]
EDCS-05501808.summary: This inscription seems to be part of a funerary monument naming Lucretius Saturninus and Quintus.

EDCS-05501809.translation: Pinarea Camala, aged 45, arranged for this to be made for her mother Tritei. Paugenda, aged 30, and Vegetus and Flaccus arranged for this to be made for their mother.
EDCS-05501809.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned by Pinarea Camala and her siblings Paugenda, Vegetus, and Flaccus for their mother Tritei.

EDCS-05501810.translation: Quartus, son of Lucius Tuscus Valentinus, is buried here. His father arranged for this to be made for his son.
EDCS-05501810.summary: This is a tombstone for Quartus, commissioned by his father.

EDCS-05501811.summary: This inscription likely marks the burial of Reburrus Camali Valerinus, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-05501812.translation: Decimus Salvius Athictus, aged 19, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501812.summary: This is a tombstone for Decimus Salvius Athictus, who died at the age of 19.

EDCS-05501813.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Tacanius Dorus for his wife Claudia Aecilena, aged 31, and for his son Claudius Theodorus, aged 2 years, 11 months, 20 days, and aged 2 years, 11 months, 12 days.
EDCS-05501813.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Claudia Aecilena and her son Claudius Theodorus by Tacanius Dorus, likely her husband and father.

EDCS-05501814.translation: Valerius Rufus, son of Lucius Valerius Quirinus Rufinus, heir for [a sum] of one thousand nummi.
EDCS-05501814.summary: This inscription records an inheritance settlement by Valerius Rufus, heir to Lucius Valerius Quirinus Rufinus.

EDCS-05501815.translation: Valerius Siber, aged 70, [arranged this] for his most devoted husband Prontus.
EDCS-05501815.summary: This funerary monument was arranged by Valerius Siber for her husband Prontus.

EDCS-05501818.translation: Gomunius, aged 75, is buried here. Ricius Procu-
EDCS-05501818.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Gomunius, aged 75.

EDCS-05501819.translation: Papiria, from the tribe Aniensis, had this prepared with great care.
EDCS-05501819.summary: This inscription records the preparation of a monument by Papiria, belonging to the Aniensis tribe.

EDCS-05501822.translation: To the Nymphs, from a vow.
EDCS-05501822.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Nymphs.

EDCS-05501823.translation: To the goddesses Nymphs, from a vow.
EDCS-05501823.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Nymphs.

EDCS-05501826.summary: This inscription records a fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-05501827.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Marcus Pronius, son of Marcus Canimius, aged 20, is buried here.
EDCS-05501827.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Pronius, son of Marcus Canimius, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-05501829.translation: Sacred to the god Mars. OSI VIF SIIONI IAFPSIIDI C SVSDVS I IVVS VDIIVV willingly paid his vow deservedly.
EDCS-05501829.summary: This is a dedication to the god Mars, recording the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-05501830.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits of the underworld. Allius Verinus, son of Allius, gives this to his most dutiful mother.
EDCS-05501830.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Allius Verinus to his mother.

EDCS-05501832.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Publius Aelius Flaccinus willingly paid his vow deservedly.
EDCS-05501832.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter, Best and Greatest, by Publius Aelius Flaccinus.

EDCS-05501834.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Septimius Argilicus willingly paid his vow deservedly.
EDCS-05501834.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter, Best and Greatest, by Septimius Argilicus.

EDCS-05501835.translation: Quintus Nivius Envinus, son of Placidi, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the household gods of Cusiciolen.
EDCS-05501835.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the household gods of Cusiciolen by Quintus Nivius Envinus.

EDCS-05501838.translation: Rarius Ravu willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the household gods of Tarmucubacae.
EDCS-05501838.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the household gods of Tarmucubacae by Rarius Ravu.

EDCS-05501839.translation: Gaius Cexaeus Fuscus, in fulfillment of a vow, for the good outcome of a gladiatorial show.
EDCS-05501839.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Hermes (Eidevoros) by Gaius Cexaeus Fuscus for a successful gladiatorial show.

EDCS-05501840.translation: To the Nymphs, Aurelius Dionysius, freedman of Augustus.
EDCS-05501840.summary: This is a dedication to the Nymphs by Aurelius Dionysius, a freedman of Augustus.

EDCS-05501842.translation: Allius asks the god Reburrus, helper in preserving these rented properties, that if anyone steals these rented properties from me or mine, or any of my property, [he will fulfill a vow].
EDCS-05501842.summary: This is a bilingual inscription requesting divine aid from the god Reburrus to protect the donor's rented properties from theft.

EDCS-05501846.translation: Lucius Aelius Flaccus, standard-bearer of the Legio II Augusta, arranged for this to be built while his most sacred father was alive and present, in Iuliobriga.
EDCS-05501846.summary: This funerary monument in Iuliobriga was commissioned by Lucius Aelius Flaccus for his father.

EDCS-05501847.translation: Sulpicia, from [the town of] I, [daughter of] Sempronius.
EDCS-05501847.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription identifying Sulpicia, possibly indicating her origin or parentage.

EDCS-05501848.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Visala, daughter of Reburrus Sambrucolensis, made this for her most dutiful grandchildren from her own property.
EDCS-05501848.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Visala for her grandchildren.

EDCS-05501849.translation: Gaius, son of Sempronius, is buried here, aged 55. Plancia arranged for this tomb to be built for his wife. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501849.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius, son of Sempronius, commissioned by his wife Plancia.

EDCS-05501850.translation: Camalus, son of Bornus, is buried here, aged 30. His brother Aelius arranged for this tomb to be built, from the curia of Tardum.
EDCS-05501850.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Camalus, son of Bornus, commissioned by his brother Aelius.

EDCS-05501851.translation: Condisa, daughter of Bovalus, aged 35, may the earth lie lightly upon her.
EDCS-05501851.summary: A funerary epitaph for Condisa, daughter of Bovalus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501852.translation: Daphnus, freedman of the heirs of Claudius Flavius, aged 60, may the earth lie lightly upon you; he and Synethe, his fellow freedman.
EDCS-05501852.summary: A funerary epitaph for Daphnus, a freedman, and his fellow freedman Synethe, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501853.translation: Loucius Maturus, aged 50, son of La Laduma, from the curia of Saqua, may the earth lie lightly upon you; his son Maxuminus took care to have this made.
EDCS-05501853.summary: A funerary epitaph for Loucius Maturus, commissioned by his son Maxuminus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501854.translation: Pictelancea, daughter of Pictelancus, aged 40, may the earth lie lightly upon her; Cemelus, her brother, and Modestus, her father.
EDCS-05501854.summary: A funerary epitaph for Pictelancea, daughter of Pictelancus, commissioned by her brother Cemelus and father Modestus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501860.translation: The fortress of Sesmacelense. B. F. D. (built, foundation, donated?)
EDCS-05501860.summary: An inscription likely referring to a local fortress or fortification, potentially with an abbreviated dedication, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501861.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Appius Sabinus, son of Probus.
EDCS-05501861.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Appius Sabinus, son of Probus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501863.translation: Mearus, son of Contarus, aged 60; Fidus, son of Pultarus, took care to have this made at his own expense.
EDCS-05501863.summary: A funerary epitaph for Mearus, commissioned by Fidus at his own expense, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501864.translation: To Bandua, the Celtic goddess, Erata Rufina Montanus dedicated this, fulfilling a vow.
EDCS-05501864.summary: A votive dedication to the Celtic goddess Bandua by Erata Rufina Montanus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501865.summary: A personal votive inscription by Flaccus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501866.translation: To Buana, freedwoman of Aebutus, aged 75; Caburene took care to have this made for her mother.
EDCS-05501866.summary: A funerary epitaph for Buana, a freedwoman, commissioned by her daughter Caburene, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501867.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the age of Caburn.

EDCS-05501868.translation: Marcus Sulpicius Flavo to his most virtuous wife, aged 50 years.
EDCS-05501868.summary: This is a tombstone inscription dedicated by Marcus Sulpicius Flavo to his wife.

EDCS-05501869.summary: This is a tombstone inscription indicating that Capito erected the monument for his wife.

EDCS-05501870.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the age of Clodius Turonus.

EDCS-05501871.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the age of Cornelius Clacilius.

EDCS-05501872.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the age of Domitianus Elaesus.

EDCS-05501873.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the age of Flavius Bedunus.

EDCS-05501874.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the age of Frontonis.

EDCS-05501875.translation: Albutius Capitolinus, aged 75 years; this was arranged to be made at public expense.
EDCS-05501875.summary: This tombstone inscription records the age of Albutius Capitolinus and indicates that the monument was funded by public money.

EDCS-05501876.translation: Proculus, son of Silo, aged 58 years.
EDCS-05501876.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the age of Proculus, son of Silo.

EDCS-05501878.summary: This inscription records the age of Probianus Sabinus.

EDCS-05501879.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Claudius Horatius.

EDCS-05501881.translation: Gaius Maecius, son of Coutus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Aetiobriga Bandua.
EDCS-05501881.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to the deity Bandua of Aetiobriga by Gaius Maecius.

EDCS-05501885.translation: Maxumus, son of Lovessus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Vial Lares.
EDCS-05501885.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to the household gods (Lares) by Maxumus.

EDCS-05501886.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Ladro, son of Verotus.

EDCS-05501887.translation: Medamus, son of Arcisius, lies here in the fort of Meidunium; Ancondei made this monument for a dear friend.
EDCS-05501887.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by Ancondei for his friend Medamus, located in the fort of Meidunium.

EDCS-05501888.translation: To Juno Mater deum, Aemilia Flavina [dedicated this].
EDCS-05501888.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the goddess Juno Mater deum by Aemilia Flavina.

EDCS-05501889.translation: To the Genius of the Roman People, Gaius Marcius Maximus, centurion of the VII Gemina Pia Felix legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05501889.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Gaius Marcius Maximus, a centurion of the VII Gemina legion, to the Genius of the Roman People.

EDCS-05501890.translation: To Aegiamunnia, Cilius Alterniaicinus, son of Cilius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05501890.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to the deity Aegiamunnia by Cilius Alterniaicinus.

EDCS-05501891.translation: Sacred to Nabia Elaesurranega, set up through the care of Viccus Silonis.
EDCS-05501891.summary: This inscription records a sacred dedication to the goddess Nabia Elaesurranega, arranged by Viccus Silonis.

EDCS-05501892.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Ladicus, Iullius Gracilis, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-05501892.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by Iullius Gracilis.

EDCS-05501893.translation: To Tellus, Gaius Sulpicius Flavus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-05501893.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Tellus by Gaius Sulpicius Flavus.

EDCS-05501895.summary: This inscription records the age of Iulianus, son of Iulius.

EDCS-05501896.translation: For Mars, for the safety of Iulia Augusta, mother of the camps, and of the Augusti, Marcus Didius Marinus ROO dedicated this.
EDCS-05501896.summary: This is a dedication to Mars by Marcus Didius Marinus for the safety of Iulia Augusta and the Augusti, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-05501897.translation: To the Nymphs, Boelius Rufus fulfilled a vow for his own safety.
EDCS-05501897.summary: This is a votive inscription to the Nymphs by Boelius Rufus.

EDCS-05501898.translation: To the god Cario, Flaccus son of Secundus fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-05501898.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Cario by Flaccus.

EDCS-05501901.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Buried here is Longinia Longini, daughter of Secundinus, to her most dutiful husband, aged 60.
EDCS-05501901.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a husband, with his wife Longinia Longini identified as the daughter of Secundinus.

EDCS-05501902.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. For Valeriae Florinae, aged 23, Pompeius Lupulus, to his most dutiful wife.
EDCS-05501902.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Pompeius Lupulus for his wife Valeria Florina.

EDCS-05501904.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Quintus Pu Dionysius, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-05501904.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Quintus Pu Dionysius.

EDCS-05501905.translation: To Tutela, Lucius Antonius Avitius with his sons and Zozima his freedwoman, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-05501905.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Tutela by Lucius Antonius Avitius and his family.

EDCS-05501906.translation: To the goddess Ataecina Inferica Ilferica, Sulpicius Severus willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-05501906.summary: This is a votive inscription to the goddess Ataecina Inferica Ilferica, dedicated by Sulpicius Severus.

EDCS-05501908.translation: Flavia Tertulla [made this for] her husband.
EDCS-05501908.summary: This is a dedication for a husband, made by Flavia Tertulla.

EDCS-05501910.translation: To Edovius Adalus Cloutus, [someone] willingly and deservedly paid [their] vow.
EDCS-05501910.summary: This is a votive inscription to Edovius Adalus Cloutus.

EDCS-05501911.translation: To the god Mercury, Fuscus son of Fuscus willingly and with a good heart paid his vow.
EDCS-05501911.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Mercury, dedicated by Fuscus son of Fuscus.

EDCS-05501912.translation: Gaius Julius Victor, a soldier of the Tenth Gemina Legion, from the century of Fabius Celtiber, 42 years old, 18 years in service, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501912.summary: This is a soldier's tombstone for Gaius Julius Victor of the Tenth Gemina Legion, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501913.translation: To the Nymphs, Gaius Antonius Florus [dedicated this].
EDCS-05501913.summary: This is a dedication to the Nymphs by Gaius Antonius Florus.

EDCS-05501914.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Atia Modesta and Atius had this memorial erected for their most devoted granddaughter Seviria Modesta, 16 years old, and for themselves, and took care that it be made.
EDCS-05501914.summary: This is a tomb inscription set up by Atia Modesta and Atius for their granddaughter Seviria Modesta and themselves.

EDCS-05501915.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Acilia Modesta, daughter of Modestus, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501915.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Acilia Modesta, daughter of Modestus.

EDCS-05501916.translation: Senecio Modestus made this for his most devoted mother Aquilia Modesta, 70 years old; may she lie here, may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501916.summary: This is a tomb inscription set up by Senecio Modestus for his mother Aquilia Modesta.

EDCS-05501917.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Numerius Vitalionis made this for his well-deserving wife Numeria Vitalia; may she lie here, may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501917.summary: This is a tomb inscription set up by Numerius Vitalionis for his wife Numeria Vitalia.

EDCS-05501918.translation: Procula, daughter of Camalus, of the Crovia tribe, aged 30, is buried here. Secundus and D...
EDCS-05501918.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Procula, dating to the Roman period, which provides her name, tribal affiliation, age, and burial location.

EDCS-05501919.translation: Sacred to the Chthonic Deities. V. Fl. dedicated this, for his own health and that of his family.
EDCS-05501919.summary: This is a dedication to the Chthonic Deities, likely a funerary or protective offering, made by an individual identified by the initials V. Fl.

EDCS-05501922.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, for the health of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus and Lucius Aurelius Verus Augusti, on the birthday of the standards, the detachment of the Ist Cohort of Celtiberians, under the supervision of Zoilus, freedman of the Augusti, procurator, and Valerius Flavius, centurion of the Ist Cohort of Gauls, and Aelius Flavius, beneficiarius of the procurator of the Augusti, and Lucretius Maternus, imaginifer of the Legio VII Gemina Felix, and Julius Sedulus, tessellarius of the Ist Cohort of Celtiberians, dedicated this on the Ides of October, when Lucius Aurelius Verus III and Quadratus were consuls.
EDCS-05501922.summary: This is a military dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made by soldiers of various units for the health of the emperors on a specific occasion, dated to the consulship of Lucius Aurelius Verus III and Quadratus.

EDCS-05501924.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, for the health of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus and Lucius Aurelius Verus Augusti, on the birthday of the sunshades, the soldiers of the Ist Cohort of Gauls, under the supervision of Hermes, freedman of the Augusti, procurator, and Lucretius Paternus, decurion of the Ist Cohort of Celtiberians, and Ful...
EDCS-05501924.summary: This is a military dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made by soldiers of the Ist Cohort of Gauls for the health of the emperors on a specific occasion, with supervision by military officials.

EDCS-05501926.translation: To Quintus L. S., aged 55. Vibia Fida placed this for her husband.
EDCS-05501926.summary: This is a tombstone inscription set up by Vibia Fida for her husband Quintus, providing his age and likely indicating his social status.

EDCS-05501927.translation: To Fortuna, from Julius Plato, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-05501927.summary: This is a votive dedication to the goddess Fortuna, made by Julius Plato.

EDCS-05501928.translation: Sacred to Mars Augustus, from Gaius Sevius Lupus, architect from Alfellanum, from Uxellodunum, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-05501928.summary: This is a votive dedication to Mars Augustus made by Gaius Sevius Lupus, an architect from two specified locations.

EDCS-05501929.summary: This is a very short inscription, likely a name scratched or painted, possibly belonging to Licinius Gallienus, who was Roman Emperor.

EDCS-05501930.translation: To the Spirits of the departed. Flaccinia Severa for her most dutiful husband, Gaius Arruntius Serenus, aged 60.
EDCS-05501930.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Gaius Arruntius Serenus, erected by his wife Flaccinia Severa, providing his age and expressing her affection.

EDCS-05501931.translation: Caius Attius Attianaus Rufinus of Seili, aged 22, is buried here. Attius Rufus and his mother Sabinula made this.
EDCS-05501931.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Caius Attius Attianaus Rufinus, stating his age and that it was made by his father Attius Rufus and mother Sabinula.

EDCS-05501933.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Gaius Julius Severus, aged 47. Iulia Severa [dedicated this] to her most devoted husband.
EDCS-05501933.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Gaius Julius Severus at age 47 and his wife Iulia Severa's devotion.

EDCS-05501934.summary: This inscription, possibly a dedication to a deity or person with a foreign name, is attributed to Rufonia Severa.

EDCS-05501935.translation: To Marcus Junius Robustus of the Quirina tribe. Junius Montanus and Rutilia Perueda, heirs, undertook to have this made for their father and husband.
EDCS-05501935.summary: This epitaph records that Junius Montanus and Rutilia Perueda, acting as heirs, commissioned a monument for Marcus Junius Robustus.

EDCS-05501936.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Maternus set up this inscription for his most devoted wife Flavia, aged 34. I beg you, passerby, may the earth be light for you.
EDCS-05501936.summary: This funerary epitaph records Flavia's death at age 34 and is dedicated by her husband Maternus, with a traditional plea for the earth to be light upon her.

EDCS-05501937.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely the beginning of a name or dedication.

EDCS-05501938.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aulus Parraqui Avitius Maecianus, aged 65, is buried here.
EDCS-05501938.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Aulus Parraqui Avitius Maecianus, who died at the age of 65.

EDCS-05501939.translation: For Caelestis Augustae, of Paternus, who is also Constantii. They fulfilled their vows.
EDCS-05501939.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of vows made to Caelestis Augustae, possibly related to individuals named Paternus and Constantii.

EDCS-05501940.translation: Sulpicius Clemens fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus at his command, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-05501940.summary: This votive inscription records Sulpicius Clemens fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, acting under divine instruction.

EDCS-05501941.translation: Sacred to the August gods and the household Lares. Marcus Annius Verus and Verianus, centurions of the legions, father and son, [dedicated this] in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-05501941.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Marcus Annius Verus and his son Verianus, who were centurions in the legions, dedicated to the imperial gods and household spirits.

EDCS-05501944.translation: Virrore Viliaego Aitianus Paternus fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-05501944.summary: This votive inscription records Aitianus Paternus fulfilling a vow, likely to a local deity or spirit.

EDCS-05501945.translation: Rufus made me from a vision.
EDCS-05501945.summary: This is a short inscription potentially on an object or a statue, indicating it was made by Rufus due to a vision.

EDCS-05501946.translation: Paternus made this in fulfillment of a vow to Veroce.
EDCS-05501946.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Paternus, possibly dedicated to a deity named Veroce.

EDCS-05501948.translation: The son of Flavi made this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-05501948.summary: This is a votive inscription stating that the son of Flavi commissioned the object or structure.

EDCS-05501951.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Aurelius aris, a veteran of the Seventh Legion Gemina Pia Felix, erected this for his wife Aurelia Messia, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05501951.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Aurelia Messia, by decree of the decurions, for her deceased husband Lucius Aurelius aris, a veteran of the Seventh Legion Gemina Pia Felix.

EDCS-05501952.translation: Lucius Valerius Severus, a soldier of the Seventh Legion Gemina Felix, in the century of Carisius Rufus, aged 30 years, 6 years in service, lies here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05501952.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Valerius Severus, a soldier in the Seventh Legion Gemina Felix, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-05501953.translation: Vecius, son of Clutamus, from Coilerna, at the fort of Ielbris [---]...
EDCS-05501953.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely military, related to Vecius, son of Clutamus, and a fort named Ielbris.

EDCS-05501954.translation: Vecius, son of Veroblius, a decurion from the province of Hispania Citerior, from the fortress of Circini, aged 60 years, and Vecius, son of Vecius, a decurion of the troops, lie here; their heirs had them made.
EDCS-05501954.summary: This inscription is a tombstone for two decurions, Vecius son of Veroblius and Vecius son of Vecius, from Hispania Citerior, whose heirs arranged for their burial monuments.

EDCS-05501955.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Iulia Flacilla, aged 18 years, daughter of Lucius Augustus. Her most unhappy mother Flavia Paterna [erected this].
EDCS-05501955.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Flavia Paterna for her daughter Iulia Flacilla, who died at the age of 18.

EDCS-05501956.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Faustus [erected this] for his wife Aelia Faustus.
EDCS-05501956.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Faustus dedicated a monument to his wife Aelia Faustus.

EDCS-05501957.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aurelius Fronton had this made for his daughter Aurelia Nantia, aged 19 years.
EDCS-05501957.summary: This funerary inscription records that Aurelius Fronton commissioned a monument for his daughter Aurelia Nantia, who died at the age of 19.

EDCS-05501958.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Conia Puscina, aged 52.
EDCS-05501958.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Conia Puscina, aged 52.

EDCS-05501959.translation: For Julia Pompeiana, daughter of Pompeius Valentina.
EDCS-05501959.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Julia Pompeiana, daughter of Pompeius Valentina.

EDCS-05501960.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Quintus and Nantia.
EDCS-05501960.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Divine Shades for Quintus and Nantia.

EDCS-05501961.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Severius Fronton, aged 55. Carisia Alona erected this for her husband. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Carisia Alona, aged 90, was buried here.
EDCS-05501961.summary: This inscription records a funerary dedication by Carisia Alona for her husband Severius Fronton, and also states her own age and burial.

EDCS-05501962.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Severa, aged 20.
EDCS-05501962.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Severa, aged 20.

EDCS-05501966.translation: A mother for her most dutiful son.
EDCS-05501966.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a mother for her son.

EDCS-05501967.summary: This is likely a funerary dedication or an ownership mark by Lucius Vatinius.

EDCS-05501968.translation: To the Divine Shades. Marcus Alturi gladly dedicated this to Ceceais Tribalis.
EDCS-05501968.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Marcus Alturi for Ceceais Tribalis.

EDCS-05501969.translation: Marcus Ulpius Eutyches, freedman of Augustus, procurator of the Albocrarensis mine, gave this gift to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Anderonicus.
EDCS-05501969.summary: This inscription records a dedication of a gift to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Anderonicus by Marcus Ulpius Eutyches, a procurator of a mine.

EDCS-05501970.translation: Titus Caesius Rufus Saelenus made this in fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Candiedonus.
EDCS-05501970.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Titus Caesius Rufus Saelenus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Candiedonus.

EDCS-05501971.translation: To Mars the Father, Tiberius Iunius Quadratus, son of Tiberius, of the tribe Quirina, from Rome, commander of the cavalry regiment of the II Flavia Hispana, Roman citizens.
EDCS-05501971.summary: This is an inscription by a Roman commander of cavalry.

EDCS-05501972.translation: Ancetolus Auriensis from the fortress of the Semacae set up this votive offering to Nabia, fulfilling his vow.
EDCS-05501972.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Nabia by a man from the Semacae.

EDCS-05501973.translation: Annius set up this votive offering to Sesmacae Navia.
EDCS-05501973.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Navia Sesmacae by Annius.

EDCS-05501975.translation: To Aelius Sporus, from his heirs Iulius Flavinus and Atilius Astur, in accordance with his will.
EDCS-05501975.summary: This inscription records a bequest in a will to Aelius Sporus.

EDCS-05501976.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Atilia Anna, aged 21. Her father, Atilius Astur.
EDCS-05501976.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Atilia Anna, set up by her father.

EDCS-05501979.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Fa. Fortunatus set this up in fulfillment of a vow for the safety of Flavius Ter.
EDCS-05501979.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the safety of Flavius Ter.

EDCS-05501980.translation: To the Fountain, Capitonius Celer set this up willingly.
EDCS-05501980.summary: This is an inscription dedicating a fountain, set up by Capitonius Celer.

EDCS-05501981.translation: To Lucius Pompeius Reburrus, son of Lucius, of the tribe Pomptina, from Gigurrus in the Calubrigensian territory, who was approved in the 8th praetorian cohort as a non-commissioned officer of the tribune, a signaller, in the century, a standard-bearer, in the century, a treasurer, in the century, a military policeman, a summoned soldier of Augustus. His heir in accordance with the will, Lucius Flavius Flaccinus.
EDCS-05501981.summary: This inscription records the career of Lucius Pompeius Reburrus and is set up by his heir.

EDCS-05501982.translation: To Liber the Father, Gaius Vetius Felicio.
EDCS-05501982.summary: This is an inscription dedicating to Liber Pater, set up by Gaius Vetius Felicio.

EDCS-05501983.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a name on a monument or property.

EDCS-05501984.translation: To Iovionus, son of Elguist, aged 50.
EDCS-05501984.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Iovionus, son of Elguist, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-05501986.summary: This inscription records the death of Macana, daughter of Arconis, at the age of 60.

EDCS-05501990.translation: To Emuria, daughter of Tritus, aged 20.
EDCS-05501990.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Emuria, daughter of Tritus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-05501991.summary: This inscription records the death of Flavus Marcelus at the age of 4.

EDCS-05501992.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Germana, aged 50.

EDCS-05501994.translation: To Mentia, slave of Cestus, aged 20.
EDCS-05501994.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Mentia, a slave of Cestus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-05501996.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Menthina, erected by Flavus, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-05501997.translation: To Respectus, slave of Abucia, aged 18.
EDCS-05501997.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Respectus, a slave of Abucia, who died at the age of 18.

EDCS-05501999.translation: To the god Mentoviacus, Marcus Atilius Silo, son of Silo, of the Quirina tribe, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-05501999.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication to the god Mentoviacus by Marcus Atilius Silo.

EDCS-05502000.translation: Gaius Pelusius, son of Caius, of the Poplilia tribe, from Polentia.
EDCS-05502000.summary: This inscription records the name and tribal affiliation of Gaius Pelusius, son of Caius.

EDCS-05502001.translation: Marcus Valerius Secundus, son of Publius, of the Aniensis tribe, a veteran, is buried here.
EDCS-05502001.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Valerius Secundus, a veteran, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502002.translation: Marcus Volumnius Cremona, son of Caius, of the Aniensis tribe, a soldier of the Tenth Legion, is buried here.
EDCS-05502002.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Volumnius Cremona, a soldier of Legio X, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502003.translation: To the Spirits of the departed. Tyche S., to Severus, her dearest and most devoted husband, set up this memorial. He lived about twenty years.
EDCS-05502003.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a wife to her husband, Severus, indicating he died in his twenties.

EDCS-05502004.translation: During the consulship of Marcus Licinius Crassus and Lucius Calpurnius Piso, on the fourth day before the Kalends of May, the Desonci, belonging to the Zoelae tribe, and the Tridiavi, belonging to the same Zoelae tribe, renewed an ancient and long-standing relationship of hospitality with them. And they received each other into their faith and patronage, and that of their children and descendants. The Desonci, represented by Araus Ablecaeni and Turaius Clouti, and the Tridiavi, represented by Docius Elaesi and Magilo Clouti, Bodecius Burrali and Elaesus Clutami, conducted this business through Abienus Pentilius, magistrate of the Zoelae, during the consulship of Curunda Glabrio and Homullus, on the fifth day before the Ides of July. The same Desonci and Tridiavi also received into the same patronage and the same agreements Sempronius Perpetuus Orniacus from the Avolvigi tribe, and Antonius Arquius from the Visaligi tribe, and Flavius Fronto from the Cabruagenigi tribe, the Zoelae. Lucius Domitius Silo and Lucius Flavius Severus of Asturica conducted this.
EDCS-05502004.summary: This inscription records the renewal of ancient hospitality and patronage agreements between two tribes, the Desonci and Tridiavi, with other tribes, likely concerning trade or political alliances, dated to 45 BC.

EDCS-05502005.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, to Sol Invictus, to Liber Pater, and to the Genius of the praetorian prefecture. Quintus Mamilius Capitolinus, iuridicus for Flaminia, Umbria, and Picenum, legatus Augusti for Asturia and Callaecia, commander of Legio VII Gemina Pia Felix, prefect of the treasury of Saturn, for his own health and that of his family.
EDCS-05502005.summary: This is a dedication by Quintus Mamilius Capitolinus, a high-ranking imperial official with extensive military and administrative experience, to various deities for his well-being, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502008.translation: Sacred to the god Vagodonnego. The public council of Asturica Augusta, through the magistrates Gaius Pacatus and Flavius Proculus, from offerings, under the care of Iulius Nepos.
EDCS-05502008.summary: This is a dedication to the local deity Vagodonnego by the city council of Asturica Augusta, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502013.translation: Marcus Valerius Licinianus, from the municipality of Castulonensis, a soldier of Legio VII Geminae, lived for 86 years, is buried here.
EDCS-05502013.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Valerius Licinianus, a soldier of Legio VII Geminae, who lived to the age of 86.

EDCS-05502014.translation: ... to Iustina, his most holy wife. Calpurnius Quadratus, procurator Augusti.
EDCS-05502014.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Calpurnius Quadratus, a procurator Augusti (an imperial official), to his wife Iustina.

EDCS-05502016.translation: To the Spirits of the departed. To Aelia Myrsina, his dearest wife. Lupianus, Augusti dispensator.
EDCS-05502016.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Lupianus, an imperial freedman administrator, to his wife Aelia Myrsina.

EDCS-05502017.translation: To the Spirits of the departed. To Augustalis, his most faithful slave. Lupianus, Augusti dispensator.
EDCS-05502017.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Lupianus, an imperial freedman administrator, to his faithful slave Augustalis.

EDCS-05502019.translation: Lucius Cosconius Vallatensis, son of Lucius, augur, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. He lived
EDCS-05502019.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Lucius Cosconius Vallatensis, an augur, with a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-05502020.translation: To Licinia Procilla, daughter of Sparsus, of Lucius Lusius Asturica. [---]
EDCS-05502020.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Licinia Procilla, daughter of Sparsus and potentially related to Lucius Lusius Asturica.

EDCS-05502021.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Ammia Prisca, a Cilena, aged 50, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Aelius Proculus had this made for his most dutiful wife.
EDCS-05502021.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Ammia Prisca, aged 50, and was commissioned by her husband, Aelius Proculus.

EDCS-05502022.translation: Caius Julius Battalus, freedman of Caius, from Rome, according to his will, Caius Julius Solinus and Lucius Julius Atticus erected [this monument] for him, aged 20.
EDCS-05502022.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Caius Julius Solinus and Lucius Julius Atticus for Caius Julius Battalus, a freedman, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-05502023.translation: For Paedatura, daughter of Titus Iulius Valens, of Zoela.
EDCS-05502023.summary: This is a dedication to Paedatura, daughter of Titus Iulius Valens and from Zoela.

EDCS-05502025.translation: To Cornelia Materna, daughter of Marcus, for her most dutiful daughter, Antonia Ae(l)ianiana, aged 17.
EDCS-05502025.summary: This epitaph is a dedication from Cornelia Materna to her daughter Antonia Ae(l)ianiana, who died at the age of 17.

EDCS-05502026.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. For Domitia Postuma, aged 45, his wife who deserved excellently of him. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502026.summary: This epitaph honors Domitia Postuma, aged 45, and was likely erected by her husband, Valerius Fuscus.

EDCS-05502027.translation: Caius Licinius Felix, aged 60, Placidus, slave of Caius Licinius Himerus, aged 35, and Felicula, slave of Caius Licinius Himerus, aged 18, are buried here. Florus had this made for himself, his father, his brother, and his fellow-servant.
EDCS-05502027.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Caius Licinius Felix, his slaves Placidus and Felicula, and was commissioned by Florus for himself and his deceased relatives and fellow-servant.

EDCS-05502029.translation: To Lyde, slave of Quintus Lusius Saturninus, aged 28, is buried here. Thaumastus had this made for his fellow-servant and himself. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502029.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Lyde, slave of Quintus Lusius Saturninus, aged 28, and was commissioned by Thaumastus for himself and Lyde.

EDCS-05502030.translation: Domitius Senecio had this made for his sisters Caesaea Clouta, aged 25, and Pellias Visalis, aged 30, Visaliae, aged 25, and his nephews Coporinus, son of Copor, aged 12.
EDCS-05502030.summary: This funerary inscription was commissioned by Domitius Senecio for his sisters and nephews, listing their ages.

EDCS-05502032.translation: To Lucius Rufius Maternus, aged 15. Lucius Rufius, father, and Tryphaena, mother.
EDCS-05502032.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young boy named Lucius Rufius Maternus, set up by his parents.

EDCS-05502033.translation: To the divine shades. Sulpicius Paris, aged 60, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you, Sulpicia Pelagia, to her most devoted husband.
EDCS-05502033.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sulpicius Paris, dedicated by his wife Sulpicia Pelagia.

EDCS-05502050.translation: The Seventh Twin Legion, Victorious, [dedicated this] to Metius Caecilius' pottery workshop.
EDCS-05502050.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the Seventh Twin Legion to a pottery workshop belonging to Metius Caecilius.

EDCS-05502051.summary: This is a standard dedication or identification inscription for the Seventh Twin Legion.

EDCS-05502054.summary: This is a standard dedication or identification inscription for the Seventh Twin Legion.

EDCS-05502056.translation: Under Emperor Titus Caesar Vespasian Augustus, son of the divine Vespasian, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 9th time, Imperator for the 15th time, Father of the Fatherland, Consul for the 8th time; and Caesar Domitian, son of the divine Vespasian, Consul for the 7th time. The New Road was made from Asturica to Bracara by Gaius Calpetanus Rantius Quirinalis Valerius Festus, Imperial Legate with Praetorian power, a distance of 23 miles.
EDCS-05502056.summary: This inscription records the construction of a new road between Asturica and Bracara during the reigns of Emperors Vespasian and Domitian, commissioned by the legate Gaius Calpetanus Rantius Quirinalis Valerius Festus.

EDCS-05502057.translation: To the divine shades. Lucretius Proculus, keeper of arms, aged 35, and his wife Valeria Amma, aged 25, and their son Lucretius Proclus, aged 3. Valerius Marcellinus, father-in-law, father, and grandfather, set this up.
EDCS-05502057.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucretius Proclus, his wife Valeria Amma, and their young son, set up by the father-in-law, father, and grandfather Valerius Marcellinus.

EDCS-05502058.translation: To Aemilius Flavus, brother, soldier of the Seventh Twin Legion, aged 25. Aemilila Materna [set this up].
EDCS-05502058.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aemilius Flavus, a soldier of the Seventh Twin Legion, set up by his sister Aemilila Materna.

EDCS-05502059.translation: To Lucius Lollius Lollianus, son of Maternus, of Saldanum, aged 18. Lollius Maternus made this. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502059.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Lollius Lollianus, a young man from Saldanum, made by his father Lollius Maternus.

EDCS-05502061.translation: To the divine shades. Aebutia Atta, daughter of Aebutius, aged 25.
EDCS-05502061.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young woman named Aebutia Atta, daughter of Aebutius.

EDCS-05502062.translation: To Aebutia Attua, daughter of Aebutius, aged 40. Aplonius Primitivus [dedicated this].
EDCS-05502062.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Aebutia Attua by Aplonius Primitivus, likely a funerary monument from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502063.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Aemilia Itundia, daughter of Lucius, aged 60. Turennius Saturninus is here.
EDCS-05502063.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aemilia Itundia, set up by Turennius Saturninus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502064.translation: To Lucius Aemilius Valentis, son of Ammius, of the tribe Quirina, aged 28. Ammia Arocia, his heir, saw to its construction.
EDCS-05502064.summary: This funerary inscription records the age and tribe of Lucius Aemilius Valentis and the person responsible for its erection, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502065.translation: For Aemilia Ammia, daughter of Marcus, aged 17. Aemilius Pressus [dedicated this].
EDCS-05502065.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aemilia Ammia, set up by Aemilius Pressus, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502067.translation: Aplonia Accia saw to its construction for her father. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502067.summary: This funerary epitaph records Aplonia Accia's care for her father's monument, with a common wish for the deceased, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502068.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Attius Reburinus, dearest son, aged 25. His devoted parents set this up.
EDCS-05502068.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication to the son Attius Reburinus by his devoted parents, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502069.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Attia Maldua, daughter of Reburrinus, aged 58. Grania, her mother, [dedicated this].
EDCS-05502069.summary: This funerary epitaph for Attia Maldua, aged 58, was set up by her mother Grania, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502071.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Cabrilius Gracilis, dearest [son/husband], aged 31. [He] set [this] up.
EDCS-05502071.summary: This funerary inscription honors Cabrilius Gracilis, aged 31, likely set up by himself or a family member, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502072.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For his wife, Licinia Atta, aged 38. Gaius Aponius Maternus saw to its construction.
EDCS-05502072.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication to Licinia Atta by her husband Gaius Aponius Maternus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502073.translation: To all the spirits of the underworld. Sacred. For Minicia Atta, aged 31. [She] set [this] up.
