EDCS-05502073.summary: This funerary inscription dedicated to Minicia Atta, aged 31, was set up by herself, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502074.translation: To Gaius Oculatius Oculatianus, aged 23. Oculatius Caturis for his son.
EDCS-05502074.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Oculatius Oculatianus, erected by his father Oculatius Caturis.

EDCS-05502076.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Licinia Marcella for her most dutiful son Pompeius Paternus, aged 30.
EDCS-05502076.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pompeius Paternus, dedicated by his mother Licinia Marcella.

EDCS-05502077.summary: This inscription likely records the names of Valerius Marcellus and Aelia Marcia, possibly as dedicators or the deceased, but is too fragmentary to determine its precise nature.

EDCS-05502078.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. For Annetia Aroca, most sweet, most dear, aged 15. Her father Alles placed this.
EDCS-05502078.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Annetia Aroca, erected by her father Alles.

EDCS-05502079.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Here lies T. Blestus, aged 47. He arranged for this to be made for Hieron, his most dutiful husband, and for himself.
EDCS-05502079.summary: This is a funerary inscription for T. Blestus and his husband Hieron, commissioned by T. Blestus.

EDCS-05502081.translation: To Jupiter, in fulfillment of a vow. Publius Antonius.
EDCS-05502081.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter by Publius Antonius in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-05502086.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Arronidaeci and Coliacini placed this for their own health and that of their families.
EDCS-05502086.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Arronidaeci and Coliacini for the well-being of themselves and their families.

EDCS-05502087.translation: Vianeglus Segei, of the Abilicorum tribe. Tiogilus placed this for Caesar.
EDCS-05502087.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Tiogilus for Caesar, possibly honoring him or dedicating something in his name, mentioning his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-05502088.translation: Flavius Strategius Apion, the illustrious man, count of the most devoted household troops, and ordinary consul. Strategius Apion.
EDCS-05502088.summary: This is an honorific inscription for Flavius Strategius Apion, a high-ranking official, likely commemorating his consulship and role with the domestic troops.

EDCS-05502089.summary: This is a brief inscription dedicating something to Caesar, son of Tabalus, possibly a provincial official or a local figure bearing that name.

EDCS-05502090.translation: Titus Pompeius Peregrinianus dedicated this to Fortuna of the Baths for the health of himself and his family.
EDCS-05502090.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Fortuna Balneari by Titus Pompeius Peregrinianus for his family's well-being.

EDCS-05502095.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Arcaedunum, son of the Vandiniensis, aged 40, was placed here by Aelius. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502095.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Arcaedunum, son of the Vandiniensis, placed by Aelius, with a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-05502096.translation: This monument was erected to the spirits of the underworld for Bovecius, son of Boderi, a citizen of Orgenomescus from the Pembelorum tribe, aged 55, in the year 115.
EDCS-05502096.summary: This funerary monument was erected for Bovecius, a citizen of Orgenomescus from the Pembelorum tribe, who died in the year 115.

EDCS-05502097.translation: Monument for Cassius Corovescus, son of [---]ivi, of Vadiniensis, aged 9(?). His son [---] erected this.
EDCS-05502097.summary: This is a funerary monument for Cassius Corovescus, a young boy from Vadiniensis, erected by his son.

EDCS-05502099.translation: This monument was erected to the spirits of the underworld for Antonio Paternus, aged 60, of the Ablaidacorum tribe; his son erected it for his father.
EDCS-05502099.summary: This funerary monument was erected for Antonio Paternus, aged 60, by his son.

EDCS-05502100.translation: Nigrinianus, son of Nigrinianus, from the century of Ercoriobrigensis, aged 5 years. C L N T.
EDCS-05502100.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Nigrinianus, son of Nigrinianus, from the Ercoriobrigensis century, at the age of 5.

EDCS-05502104.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Blattia Caecilia, aged 9 years and 4 months, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502104.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Blattia Caecilia, who died at the age of 9 years and 4 months.

EDCS-05502105.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Ametyshus had this made for his most devoted wife Anna, aged 55. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502105.summary: This funerary inscription records that Ametyshus commissioned this monument for his wife Anna, aged 55.

EDCS-05502106.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Marcella had this made for her most devoted mother, Iulia Scauri filia, aged 55.
EDCS-05502106.summary: This funerary inscription records that Iulia Marcella commissioned this monument for her mother, Iulia Scauri filia, aged 55.

EDCS-05502108.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Licinia Festa set up this memorial for her most devoted son, Quintus Caianus, aged 22 years.
EDCS-05502108.summary: This funerary inscription records that Licinia Festa erected a memorial for her son, Quintus Caianus, aged 22.

EDCS-05502110.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Cornelia Zoe erected this for her most devoted husband, Gaius Pompeius Severus, who lived 41 years.
EDCS-05502110.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Pompeius Severus, erected by his wife Cornelia Zoe.

EDCS-05502111.translation: To the gods below, the Divine Spirits. Sempronia Anna erected this for her husband, Marcus Valerius, with her own money; he lived 51 years.
EDCS-05502111.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Valerius, erected by his wife Sempronia Anna.

EDCS-05502112.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Primus erected this for Tertius Crecetus, who lived 10 years.
EDCS-05502112.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tertius Crecetus, erected by Primus.

EDCS-05502113.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Licinia Anna erected this for her most devoted daughter, Valeria Rufina, who lived 12 years.
EDCS-05502113.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Rufina, erected by her mother Licinia Anna.

EDCS-05502114.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Lucius Varius Postumus, a slave, who lived 31 years.
EDCS-05502114.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Varius Postumus, a slave.

EDCS-05502115.translation: For Aelia Aminna, daughter of Titus Aelius. Saelia Elani, her mother.
EDCS-05502115.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aelia Aminna, daughter of Titus Aelius, erected by her mother.

EDCS-05502116.translation: Clodius Tertullus, son of Titus, took care to have this placed in honour and...
EDCS-05502116.summary: An inscription indicating a funerary monument was erected for Clodius Tertullus by an unnamed person.

EDCS-05502117.translation: Valerius Quintus, son of Narissus, made this in accordance with his mother Proclina's will, through his guardian Caelius Flavus Nannae.
EDCS-05502117.summary: A funerary monument established by Valerius Quintus, son of Narissus, as dictated by his mother's will.

EDCS-05502122.translation: To Gaius Baesus, son of Luaabus, from Uxamum, who lived 60 years. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502122.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Baesus, from Uxamum, wishing him peace in death.

EDCS-05502123.summary: A funerary epitaph for Acconius Matticus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-05502126.translation: To Atti Lerant. May the earth lie lightly upon you, Q. V. M., and your wife.
EDCS-05502126.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Atti Lerant, likely erected in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502127.translation: Aemilianus had this made for Camilia Aemiliana, aged 25 years. [---]
EDCS-05502127.summary: This tombstone records the death of Camilia Aemiliana, erected by Aemilianus, likely during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502128.translation: To Tancinus. [---] Anio. Aged 80 years. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502128.summary: This epitaph records Tancinus, who died at the age of 80, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502133.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription commemorating Sextus Licinius Mev[---], likely a tombstone from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502134.translation: Aemilia Flavina, mother, had this made for her son Licinius Titullus Oronicus, aged 18 years.
EDCS-05502134.summary: This tombstone records the death of Licinius Titullus Oronicus, erected by his mother Aemilia Flavina, likely during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502135.translation: To Maiuna, daughter of Fpaetis, granddaughter of Nq F. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502135.summary: This tombstone inscription honors Maiuna, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502136.translation: To Publicius Iuvenalis, freedman of Caius Iuvenalis.
EDCS-05502136.summary: This inscription records Publicius Iuvenalis, a freedman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502137.translation: To Quietus Maturus, aged 45 years, and to Licinia Protenis, while she was alive. [---]
EDCS-05502137.summary: This inscription likely records a couple, Quietus Maturus and Licinia Protenis, possibly a tombstone or dedication from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502140.translation: To Sulpicia Materna, daughter of Sulpicius, aged 29 years. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502140.summary: This tombstone inscription honors Sulpicia Materna, erected likely during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502141.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucius Fabius Sigerus erected this for his son Gaius Valerius Flavianus, aged 19 years, and for his daughter-in-law Herennia Paterna, aged 14 years.
EDCS-05502141.summary: This tombstone records the deaths of Gaius Valerius Flavianus and Herennia Paterna, erected by Lucius Fabius Sigerus, likely during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502142.translation: To Lucius Valerius Dexter, son of Lucius Valerius Fuscus, aged 50, for his most dutiful father; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502142.summary: A funerary epitaph from the 2nd century CE, dedicated by a son to his deceased father.

EDCS-05502143.summary: A very fragmentary epitaph, likely part of a larger inscription, mentioning a filiation.

EDCS-05502144.translation: Valeria Emiliana, mother, arranged for this to be made for her son, Valerius Maternus, aged 15, for his most dutiful son; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502144.summary: A funerary epitaph from the 2nd century CE, erected by a mother for her deceased son.

EDCS-05502146.translation: To Rufina Marcella, daughter of Marcus, aged 30. Cornelius Sexstus for his mother.
EDCS-05502146.summary: A funerary epitaph from the 2nd century CE, dedicated by a son to his deceased mother.

EDCS-05502151.translation: To I Tule, aged 70; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502151.summary: A funerary epitaph, likely from the 2nd century CE, commemorating an individual named I Tule.

EDCS-05502153.translation: Terentia Megiste willingly and deservedly paid her vow to the Matres.
EDCS-05502153.summary: A votive inscription from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, recording a vow paid to the Matres (mother goddesses).

EDCS-05502155.summary: A very fragmentary inscription, possibly related to a vow or dedication, with illegible numerical and alphabetical content.

EDCS-05502156.translation: To Gaius Sulpicius Valerianus, aged 23, and to his father Gaius Sulpicius Sempronianus, and to his mother Valeria Paternus. S Primitivus, his freedman, arranged for this to be made from his own resources for his most excellent patrons.
EDCS-05502156.summary: A funerary epitaph from the 2nd century CE, dedicated by a freedman to his deceased patrons (parents and son).

EDCS-05502157.summary: A fragmentary inscription, possibly a dedication or epitaph, mentioning a personal name.

EDCS-05502159.summary: A highly fragmented inscription with illegible content, likely part of a larger text.

EDCS-05502165.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Lucius Augurinus paid the expense for T.R.
EDCS-05502165.summary: A dedication to Jupiter, likely from the Republican or early Imperial period, recording a financial transaction.

EDCS-05502171.translation: To Caelius, son of Cloutus, IIIIvir...
EDCS-05502171.summary: A fragmentary inscription honoring Caelius, who held the office of IIIIvir, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502185.translation: Paternus Balatuscun, son of Amalmanus, lies here.
EDCS-05502185.summary: A funerary epitaph for Paternus Balatuscun, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502201.summary: A very fragmentary inscription, possibly funerary or dedicatory, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502203.summary: A funerary inscription commemorating Latro, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502204.translation: Lucius Tritalicum, son of Atton and Flavia, willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Hercules.
EDCS-05502204.summary: A votive inscription to Hercules, likely from the Roman Imperial period, by Lucius Tritalicum.

EDCS-05502205.translation: Valeria Severa, wife of Caius Densus, willingly and deservedly paid her vow to Hercules.
EDCS-05502205.summary: A votive inscription to Hercules, likely from the Roman Imperial period, by Valeria Severa.

EDCS-05502206.translation: Pompeius Docilicom willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Hercules.
EDCS-05502206.summary: A votive inscription to Hercules, likely from the Roman Imperial period, by Pompeius Docilicom.

EDCS-05502207.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Valerius Sangeni's son Calidus Abliqum willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-05502207.summary: A votive inscription to Jupiter Best and Greatest, likely from the Roman Imperial period, by Valerius Sangeni's son Calidus Abliqum.

EDCS-05502208.translation: Sacred to the Lugoves, Lucius Licinius Urcicom gave this gift to the guild of shoemakers.
EDCS-05502208.summary: A dedication to the Lugoves and a gift to the shoemakers' guild by Lucius Licinius Urcicom, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502209.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus. Pompeia Moderata, daughter of Lucius, ordered this to be set up in her will.
EDCS-05502209.summary: A funerary dedication to Mercury Augustus, set up by Pompeia Moderata in accordance with her will.

EDCS-05502210.translation: To Marcus Aemilius Lepidus, our patron, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05502210.summary: A dedication to Marcus Aemilius Lepidus, honoring him as patron, commissioned by the town council.

EDCS-05502212.translation: To Gaius Calvisius Sabinus, son of Aion, of the Galeria tribe, by the decurions from their own funds for his many services to the state.
EDCS-05502212.summary: A dedication by the town council to Gaius Calvisius Sabinus, recognizing his public service.

EDCS-05502213.translation: Nice, slave of Atilia Meduttio, aged 20, is buried here.
EDCS-05502213.summary: A tombstone inscription for Nice, a slave of Atilia Meduttio.

EDCS-05502214.translation: To the Divine Shades. Titus Cornelius [erected this] for his father Gaius Cornelius Paternus, aged 60, and his mother Valeria Via, aged 50, and his brother Gaius Cornelius Li, aged 30. [---]
EDCS-05502214.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Cornelius Paternus, Valeria Via, and Gaius Cornelius Li, erected by Titus Cornelius.

EDCS-05502215.translation: To Letondus, son of Crastuno, of Alnicum. Ranto, daughter of Magulio; Crastuno, son of Carbilius; Capito [erected this] for their son.
EDCS-05502215.summary: A funerary monument honoring Letondus, son of Crastuno, with inscriptions also for Ranto and sons Crastuno and Capito.

EDCS-05502216.translation: Fausta, aged 50, and Arraedo, son of [---], aged 16, are buried here. Freedmen.
EDCS-05502216.summary: A tombstone inscription for Fausta and Arraedo, identified as freedmen.

EDCS-05502217.translation: Lucius Licinius Seranus and Lucius Licinius Ladienus, son of Lucius Licinius Seranus, are buried here.
EDCS-05502217.summary: A tombstone inscription for Lucius Licinius Seranus and his son Lucius Licinius Ladienus.

EDCS-05502218.translation: Terentia Aucia had this made for her most devoted husband Lucius Terentius Paternus, son of Titus of Eburacum, aged 56, and for his daughter Terentia Paternus, aged 17.
EDCS-05502218.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Terentia Aucia for her husband Lucius Terentius Paternus and his daughter Terentia Paternus.

EDCS-05502219.translation: Valentilla. Her parents had this set up to be placed.
EDCS-05502219.summary: A funerary dedication for Valentilla, commissioned by her parents.

EDCS-05502220.translation: Lucius Arquius, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, Co[---]vanco, is buried here.
EDCS-05502220.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of Lucius Arquius.

EDCS-05502222.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Attius Vitalis erected this altar for his own health and that of his family.
EDCS-05502222.summary: This is a dedication of an altar to Jupiter by Attius Vitalis for the welfare of himself and his household.

EDCS-05502223.translation: To all the gods of the underworld. Father, Tterssia Reusia, and Memea in the year 251.
EDCS-05502223.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the gods of the underworld and names a father, Tterssia Reusia, and Memea, with a date in the consular era.

EDCS-05502224.translation: To Mars Augustus, Aulus Munerigius, son of Arqui, willingly and deservedly erected this altar.
EDCS-05502224.summary: This is a dedication of an altar to Mars Augustus by Aulus Munerigius.

EDCS-05502225.translation: Marcus Octavius Paternus, a soldier aged 20, died. His father Brateo arranged for this monument to be made.
EDCS-05502225.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Marcus Octavius Paternus, a soldier, and states his father commissioned the monument.

EDCS-05502226.translation: To the gods of the underworld. P.P. Ave, his wife D. Primiaee, aged 45, and A.E. Ofit, aged 18, and himself.
EDCS-05502226.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the gods of the underworld and names D. Primiaee and A.E. Ofit, with ages and presumably their husband/father P.P. Ave.

EDCS-05502228.translation: Nonius Quintilianus, son of Sextus, aged 33. His wife Fortunata, aged 30. They are buried here. Evasco, son of Titus, Barrus, arranged for the ossuary to be made.
EDCS-05502228.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Nonius Quintilianus and his wife Fortunata, and states that Evasco, son of Titus, Barrus, commissioned the ossuary.

EDCS-05502230.translation: Antestia Aunia paid her vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-05502230.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Antestia Aunia fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-05502231.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Cornelia Mansueta, buried here, aged 20. Her father Cornelius Saturninus erected this.
EDCS-05502231.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Cornelia Mansueta and states her father Cornelius Saturninus erected the monument.

EDCS-05502232.translation: Sofus Colimentis and Nasorvoitinus, aged 40, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-05502232.summary: This inscription records that Sofus Colimentis and Nasorvoitinus, both aged 40, commissioned a monument.

EDCS-05502233.translation: Lucius Terentius Rufinus, son of Rufi, aged 30, is buried here. His brother had this made.
EDCS-05502233.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Terentius Rufinus, erected by his brother.

EDCS-05502234.translation: To Lucio Valerio Seneca, aged 5 years and 5 months. Valerius Aristolaus and Maria Caesia, father and mother, set this up.
EDCS-05502234.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucio Valerio Seneca, set up by his parents.

EDCS-05502235.translation: To Valeria Titulla, aged 12, mother and sister. [She/He] had this made.
EDCS-05502235.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Titulla, likely erected by a family member.

EDCS-05502240.translation: To Lougestericus, an altar with a monument.
EDCS-05502240.summary: This is a dedication of an altar and monument to Lougestericus.

EDCS-05502243.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Silana Severina, Ivollerina, sacred, from a vow.
EDCS-05502243.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Silana Severina.

EDCS-05502259.translation: To Comemea, Sempronia, freedwoman of Paternus, [perhaps] freedwoman.
EDCS-05502259.summary: This is an incomplete dedication, possibly funerary, to Comemea.

EDCS-05502278.translation: Lucius Lucretius Densus, duovir, made this August road.
EDCS-05502278.summary: This is a public works inscription recording the construction of a road by Lucius Lucretius Densus.

EDCS-05502279.translation: To the divine spirits of Didius Marcellus, soldier of the VII Gemina Felix legion, of the first centuria of Germanus, aged 29. Secundinus M. took care of it.
EDCS-05502279.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Didius Marcellus, a soldier, likely erected by Secundinus.

EDCS-05502282.translation: To the divine spirits of Flaminus Aemilianus, aged 101, a veteran of the VII Gemina Pia Felix legion. His wife made this.
EDCS-05502282.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Flaminus Aemilianus, a veteran, erected by his wife.

EDCS-05502285.translation: To the divine spirits of Attilius Maxumus, for his most dutiful brother Attilius Paternus and his son Attilius Capito.
EDCS-05502285.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Attilius Paternus and Attilius Capito, set up by Attilius Maxumus.

EDCS-05502286.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Clarianus [erected this] for his dearest father, Clar, aged 70.
EDCS-05502286.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a man named Clar, erected by his son Clarianus.

EDCS-05502287.translation: Scribonius Epaprhas and Scribonia Callisste [erected this] for their most devoted son, Scribonius Maternus, aged 13.
EDCS-05502287.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young boy named Scribonius Maternus, erected by his parents.

EDCS-05502288.translation: Gaius Sempronius Gracchus Voconius Vitulus [dedicated this] to the state.
EDCS-05502288.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the state by Gaius Sempronius Gracchus Voconius Vitulus.

EDCS-05502289.translation: Ambata [erected this] for Matigeni, her mother, and for Gaius Flaccinus, her father, and for herself. Marcus [erected this] for Flavina, his wife, deserving and chaste and dutiful, who lived for her dear parents. She leaves this monument as a remembrance.
EDCS-05502289.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ambata and her parents, and also for Marcus' wife Flavina, erected by Ambata and Marcus.

EDCS-05502291.translation: from Cluniensis, from his own money.
EDCS-05502291.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription indicating a contribution from a person named Cluniensis.

EDCS-05502294.translation: Aurelia Lesuridantaris, daughter of ..., was buried here, aged 20 years, 5 days, under the care of her husband and children. Caericiocon(?).
EDCS-05502294.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelia Lesuridantaris, detailing her age, burial, and the care of her husband and children.

EDCS-05502295.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Aurelius Capito, soldier of the Seventh Gemina Felix Legion, of the centuria of Restitutus, aged 28, Luseia, his heir, saw to the construction.
EDCS-05502295.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a soldier named Aurelius Capito, erected by his heir Luseia.

EDCS-05502296.translation: Fusca, daughter of Coedius, Celtica Supertamarcus, from the fort of Blaniobriga. Secosilie, daughter of Coedius, her sister, placed [this].
EDCS-05502296.summary: This funerary inscription records Fusca and Secosilie, sisters, with Fusca identified by her tribal affiliation and origin from the fort of Blaniobriga.

EDCS-05502297.translation: For Laturo Tediuluanus, son of Calaetus, aged 50.
EDCS-05502297.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Laturo Tediuluanus, son of Calaetus.

EDCS-05502298.translation: Celticus Supertamarcus, aged 40, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502298.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Celticus Supertamarcus, wishing him a light earth upon burial.

EDCS-05502303.summary: This is a tombstone for Valeria Ambadae, a freedwoman who died at the age of 75.

EDCS-05502305.translation: Sacred to the Nymphs. Lucius C. S. [dedicated this to the] Nymphs. Lucius C. S.
EDCS-05502305.summary: A dedication to the Nymphs by Lucius C. S.

EDCS-05502307.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Antonius Pudens, son of Marcus, of the Galerian tribe, an eques duplicarius from Lugudunum, aged [---], is buried here.
EDCS-05502307.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Antonius Pudens, a duplicarius from Lugudunum.

EDCS-05502308.translation: Cornelianus, prefect of the first cohort of Callaeci, cavalry, Roman citizens.
EDCS-05502308.summary: An inscription mentioning Cornelianus, the prefect of a Roman auxiliary cohort.

EDCS-05502309.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Vettius Lubianus set this up for Terentia Nigella, aged 60, his most devoted mother, and for his son Vettius Felix, aged 65, his most devoted father.
EDCS-05502309.summary: A funerary monument set up by Vettius Lubianus for his parents.

EDCS-05502316.translation: Salus Umeritana, 3 pounds of Lucius P. Cornelianus.
EDCS-05502316.summary: An inscription recording 3 pounds of Salus Umeritana belonging to Lucius P. Cornelianus.

EDCS-05502317.translation: and set up for Sempronia, his wife, a memorial of his consulship.
EDCS-05502317.summary: A funerary monument commemorating Sempronia, the wife of the dedicator, and referencing his consulship.

EDCS-05502319.translation: Sandus Vimumburi restored the altar of Sandus with his own money.
EDCS-05502319.summary: An inscription recording the restoration of an altar by Sandus Vimumburi.

EDCS-05502320.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Aemilius Paternus set this up for Domitia Primitiva, his most devoted wife, and for himself while living.
EDCS-05502320.summary: A funerary inscription set up by Aemilius Paternus for himself and his wife, Domitia Primitiva.

EDCS-05502321.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Antestia Euterpe set this up for herself, aged 75.
EDCS-05502321.summary: A funerary inscription set up by Antestia Euterpe for herself.

EDCS-05502322.translation: Titus Sempronius Titullus, aged 50, and Aemilia Titulla, aged 30; Caius Sempronius Aemilianus and Pompeia Materna, daughter-in-law, and Sempronius Titullus, grandson, saw to its construction.
EDCS-05502322.summary: This funerary inscription records the construction of a monument by the surviving family members for Titus Sempronius Titullus and Aemilia Titulla.

EDCS-05502324.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Pederos, may the earth rest lightly on you. Receptus, his son, saw to the construction of this monument.
EDCS-05502324.summary: This epitaph is dedicated to Pederos by his son Receptus, asking for a light earth upon him.

EDCS-05502325.translation: Licinius, a cavalryman of the Baetasian ala, aged 10, is buried here.
EDCS-05502325.summary: This is a short epitaph recording the burial of Licinius, a young cavalryman of the Baetasian ala.

EDCS-05502326.translation: Lucius Vetius Vetus, son of Caius, aged 10, is buried here.
EDCS-05502326.summary: This brief inscription marks the burial place of Lucius Vetius Vetus, son of Caius, who died at the age of 10.

EDCS-05502327.translation: Marcus Octavius Gallicus, son of Sabinus, of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-05502327.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph recording the name, filiation, and tribal affiliation of Marcus Octavius Gallicus.

EDCS-05502328.translation: Marcus Porcius Tonius, son of Auscius, of the Quirina tribe, aged 75, is buried here; his heir had it made from the money bequeathed.
EDCS-05502328.summary: This funerary inscription states that Marcus Porcius Tonius, aged 75, is buried here and that his heir financed the monument with legacy funds.

EDCS-05502329.translation: Marcus Sempronius Flavus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, aged 70; his father Marcus Sempronius F.
EDCS-05502329.summary: This fragmentary epitaph records Marcus Sempronius Flavus, aged 70, and mentions his father, Marcus Sempronius.

EDCS-05502330.summary: A very brief funerary inscription stating that Aurelius Augustinus is buried here.

EDCS-05502331.translation: Egnatius Vitalis, son of Terentius, aged 65.
EDCS-05502331.summary: This inscription records the name, filiation, and age of Egnatius Vitalis, likely a funerary marker.

EDCS-05502332.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Gaius Fabricius Fuscus, aged 93. Secundinus, his paternal uncle, made this.
EDCS-05502332.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Secundinus for his nephew Gaius Fabricius Fuscus, who lived to be 93.

EDCS-05502333.translation: Licinius Serenus, son of Hispanius, aged 45.
EDCS-05502333.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Licinius Serenus, who died at the age of 45.

EDCS-05502334.translation: Munatius Fuscus, son of Ambaicus, aged 75, is buried here.
EDCS-05502334.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Munatius Fuscus, who died at the age of 75.

EDCS-05502335.translation: Rhodanus, slave of Atilius, aged 50. His wife Illuna, his mother-in-law Tychia, are here.
EDCS-05502335.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Rhodanus, a slave, and his mother-in-law Tychia.

EDCS-05502338.translation: Sempronius Severus gladly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-05502338.summary: This inscription records Sempronius Severus fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-05502339.translation: Ablonius, slave of Licira, aged 70, is buried here.
EDCS-05502339.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ablonius, a slave, who died at the age of 70.

EDCS-05502340.translation: Anicius Reburrus, son of Reburrinus, aged 75, is buried here.
EDCS-05502340.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Anicius Reburrus, who died at the age of 75.

EDCS-05502341.translation: Segontius, son of Iammari, aged 70, is buried here.
EDCS-05502341.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Segontius, son of Iammari, who died at the age of 70.

EDCS-05502342.translation: Marcus Sempronius Flavi, aged 25, is buried here.
EDCS-05502342.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Sempronius Flavi, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-05502344.translation: To the departed spirits of Quinta Fabia.
EDCS-05502344.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Quinta Fabia.

EDCS-05502345.translation: Segontius, son of Ablonius, aged 42, is buried here.
EDCS-05502345.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Segontius, son of Ablonius, who died at the age of 42.

EDCS-05502346.translation: Sempronius Silo, aged 50, is laid here.
EDCS-05502346.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sempronius Silo, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502347.translation: Ambatus, son of Plendus, aged 30, is laid here.
EDCS-05502347.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ambatus, son of Plendus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502348.translation: Ciras, son of Egoitus, aged 30, is laid here.
EDCS-05502348.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ciras, son of Egoitus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502349.translation: Ambata, daughter of Appa, aged 50, is laid here.
EDCS-05502349.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ambata, daughter of Appa, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502350.translation: Ambatus, son of Seranus, aged 80, is laid here.
EDCS-05502350.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ambatus, son of Seranus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502351.translation: Araica, daughter of Araius, aged 45, is laid here.
EDCS-05502351.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Araica, daughter of Araius, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502352.translation: Cantaber, son of Tritus, 9 months old, is laid here.
EDCS-05502352.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for the infant Cantaber, son of Tritus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502353.translation: Caricus, son of Carus, aged 40, is laid here. May the earth rest lightly on you.
EDCS-05502353.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caricus, son of Carus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502355.translation: Segontius, son of Ambatus Vectus, aged 30, is laid here.
EDCS-05502355.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Segontius, son of Ambatus Vectus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502357.translation: During the consulship of Nero Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus II and Lucius Cassius Martial, on the 8th day before the Ides of December, the community of Pompelon renewed the guest-friendship with Lucius Pompeius Primianus, son of Lucius Aniensis, and with his children and descendants. Sextus Pompeius Nepos and Sergius Crescens acted as legates.
EDCS-05502357.summary: This inscription records the renewal of a guest-friendship pact by the community of Pompelon, dated precisely to December 5, 54 AD.

EDCS-05502359.translation: Under the consulship of Materno and Bradua, on the Kalends of November, the public administration of Pompaelo, together with Publius Sempronius Taurinus Damasitanus, his children and descendants, established a guest-friendship with him and adopted him as a citizen and patron. Titus Antonius Paternus and Lucius Caecilius Aestivus managed this.
EDCS-05502359.summary: This is a decree from the public administration of Pompaelo (Pamplona) establishing a guest-friendship and adopting Publius Sempronius Taurinus as a citizen and patron, dated to the consulship of Materno and Bradua (likely late 2nd century CE).

EDCS-05502360.translation: Severa, his wife, made this for her husband, aged 20. She made it herself.
EDCS-05502360.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a wife for her deceased husband, specifying his age.

EDCS-05502361.translation: Porcius Felix from Karia, aged 70, lies here. He made this during his lifetime.
EDCS-05502361.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Porcius Felix for himself, specifying his age and origin, indicating it was a pre-arranged burial.

EDCS-05502362.translation: Sempronia Firmi, daughter of Firminus, from Andelonis, aged 30, lies here. Her husband Calpurnius Aestivos and her brother Sempronius Nepos arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-05502362.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Sempronia Firmi, specifying her age, origin, and the relatives who arranged her burial.

EDCS-05502363.translation: Quintus Antonius Certus ordered this to be made in his will for Quintus Antonius Flavus, freedman of his wife Domitia Marcellina, and for Antonia Aemilia, his daughter.
EDCS-05502363.summary: This inscription records a testamentary burial arrangement by Quintus Antonius Certus for his freedman and daughter.

EDCS-05502364.translation: Cornelia for herself and for Cornelius Cornelius Firmus, and their freedmen.
EDCS-05502364.summary: This is a tomb inscription indicating that Cornelia dedicated the burial plot for herself, Cornelius Cornelius Firmus, and their freedmen.

EDCS-05502365.translation: Lucius Aemilius Seranus for his son Lucius Aemilius Seranus.
EDCS-05502365.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Lucius Aemilius Seranus for his son, also named Lucius Aemilius Seranus.

EDCS-05502366.translation: Lucius Aemilius Seranus for his mother Calpurnia Urchatetelli.
EDCS-05502366.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Lucius Aemilius Seranus for his mother, Calpurnia Urchatetelli.

EDCS-05502367.translation: Calaetus, son of Eques, aged 20, killed by brigands. His mother Acnon made this from her own money.
EDCS-05502367.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a mother for her son who was killed by brigands, specifying his age.

EDCS-05502368.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Lucius Valerius Firmus, specifying his age.

EDCS-05502369.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Antonia Buturra, daughter of Viriatus, aged 30, is placed here.
EDCS-05502369.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonia Buturra, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-05502370.translation: Marcus Iunius Paternus, son of Cantaber, aged 30, is placed here.
EDCS-05502370.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Iunius Paternus, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-05502371.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sevius Scriba took care to have this made for Sevius Hermetio, father, aged 65, for Senpronia Dinedilla, mother, aged 60, for Sevius Ianuarius, uncle, aged 45, and for himself.
EDCS-05502371.summary: This is a funerary epitaph commissioned by Sevius Scriba for his family, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-05502374.translation: Sergia Praesentina perhaps made this for Marcus Caecilius Proclus.
EDCS-05502374.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, possibly indicating that Sergia Praesentina made it for Marcus Caecilius Proclus.

EDCS-05502376.translation: Fabia, aged 26, lies here, and also her mother Fabia.
EDCS-05502376.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fabia and her mother, also named Fabia, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-05502379.translation: Astinus, aged 6. Hastatus, father, made this.
EDCS-05502379.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Astinus, made by his father Hastatus, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-05502381.translation: Minicia Procula made this for her grandmother and for herself with her own money.
EDCS-05502381.summary: This is a funerary epitaph commissioned by Minicia Procula for her grandmother and herself, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-05502382.translation: This man is Caius Varius Domitianus, son of Caius, of the Lemonia tribe, soldier of the 6th Legion, in the 1st century of Prialfius, aged 24, is placed here.
EDCS-05502382.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Varius Domitianus, a soldier in the 6th Legion, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-05502387.translation: Antonius Lascivus, brother, took care to have this made.
EDCS-05502387.summary: This is a funerary epitaph commissioned by Antonius Lascivus for a deceased relative, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-05502389.translation: Statuius Arquius willingly fulfilled his vow to Mars the Unconquered, as was deserved.
EDCS-05502389.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Mars Invictus by Statuius Arquius, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-05502390.translation: To the Genius of the granaries, Aulus Annius Eucharistus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-05502390.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Genius of the granaries by Aulus Annius Eucharistus.

EDCS-05502391.translation: Artemas, slave of the colonists of the colony, [dedicated this] to Marcus Iulius Antonianus, aedile.
EDCS-05502391.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Artemas, a slave of the colonists, to Marcus Iulius Antonianus, an aedile.

EDCS-05502392.translation: To Quintus Vettius Amatus, son of Marcus, from Aquae Statiellae, his heirs, in accordance with his will.
EDCS-05502392.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring Quintus Vettius Amatus, whose heirs erected it according to his will.

EDCS-05502396.translation: Valeria Leolonina made [this] for her most deserving husband Valerius Liber and their most beloved son Liberius, from her own means.
EDCS-05502396.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Valeria Leolonina for her husband Valerius Liber and son Liberius.

EDCS-05502400.translation: I, the wife, saved the bedroom for the Genius of my sweetest wife; now a tomb is to be preserved for the bedroom, and the Manes [of the deceased], the pitiable wife was not allowed to desire other funeral rites.
EDCS-05502400.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, likely on a tomb, lamenting the death of a woman and expressing the grief of her surviving wife.

EDCS-05502401.translation: Lucius Cornelius Phoebus and Lucius Sergius Quintillus, seviri Augustales, saw to it that this was made at their own expense, in honor of Victoria Augusta.
EDCS-05502401.summary: This inscription records a public work commissioned by two seviri Augustales, Lucius Cornelius Phoebus and Lucius Sergius Quintillus, in honor of Victoria Augusta.

EDCS-05502402.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Atellius Sergia Paulinus, aged 75, is buried here; his most devoted children, Atelli Procula and Paulus, [dedicated this] to their father. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502402.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Attelius Sergia Paulinus, erected by his children, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05502403.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Iulius, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, Paetus, aged 70, is buried here, in accordance with his will. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502403.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulius Paetus, erected in accordance with his will, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05502404.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Manlia Psyche, daughter of Lucius, aged 16.
EDCS-05502404.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Manlia Psyche, daughter of Lucius, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05502405.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a funerary marker or a part of a larger monument, identifying Valerius Admetus and his son.

EDCS-05502408.translation: To Hercules the Pacifier of Peoples.
EDCS-05502408.summary: This is a dedication to the god Hercules, likely from a soldier or traveler seeking protection.

EDCS-05502412.translation: Melia Boria, daughter of Fulus, lived 57 years.
EDCS-05502412.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name, parentage, and age of Melia Boria.

EDCS-05502414.translation: Dedicated to the divine Diana. Lucius and Cornelia, resting in the sacred tenth part of the divine Diana's land, have dedicated a property; may the earth lie lightly upon them here.
EDCS-05502414.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Lucius and Cornelia dedicate land to the goddess Diana and are subsequently buried there.

EDCS-05502417.translation: To Lucius Fabius Gallus, Lucius Fabius Fabullus and Licinia Materna, for their son.
EDCS-05502417.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a tomb or plot to Lucius Fabius Gallus by his parents.

EDCS-05502418.translation: To Fabia Severa, daughter of Lucius, [died after] 30 days.
EDCS-05502418.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age (or duration of life) of Fabia Severa.

EDCS-05502419.translation: To Fabia Severa, daughter of Lucius, [died after] 25 days.
EDCS-05502419.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age (or duration of life) of Fabia Severa, likely a child.

EDCS-05502422.translation: Lucius Mansuetus to Vatinones. Petronia Helias [erected this].
EDCS-05502422.summary: This is a funerary inscription likely erected by Petronia Helias for Lucius Mansuetus.

EDCS-05502423.translation: Marcus Grumius [dedicated this] to the Goddesses.
EDCS-05502423.summary: This is a dedication to a group of goddesses, likely the local feminine deities, by Marcus Grumius.

EDCS-05502429.translation: Sacred to the All-Gods, Augustus. Lucius Julius Secundus, freedman of Lucius, [illegible text], a Sexvir Augustalis, had this made at his own expense and dedicated it.
EDCS-05502429.summary: This is a dedication to the Panthenon, commissioned and dedicated by Lucius Julius Secundus, an important local magistrate.

EDCS-05502430.translation: Flaccilla the freedwoman, willingly and deservedly, paid her vow to Tutela.
EDCS-05502430.summary: This is a votive inscription where Flaccilla, a freedwoman, thanks the goddess Tutela for fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-05502433.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Corellius Saturninus, aged 30. Corellius Limicus, to his most devoted brother, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502433.summary: A tombstone commemorating Corellius Saturninus, erected by his brother Corellius Limicus.

EDCS-05502434.translation: To C. M. Iu., who died in the city of Italy, aged [---]. Sulpicia Quinta, his devoted attendant, had this made.
EDCS-05502434.summary: A tombstone for C. M. Iu., erected by his devoted attendant Sulpicia Quinta.

EDCS-05502435.translation: Licinius Iulianus of Uxamensis, aged 20, lies here. His mother, Iulia, had this made. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502435.summary: A tombstone for Licinius Iulianus, erected by his mother Iulia.

EDCS-05502436.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Aemilius Severus, to his dearest friend Pusinca, had this made. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502436.summary: A tombstone for Pusinca, erected by her dearest friend Lucius Aemilius Severus.

EDCS-05502437.translation: Atilia Helpis, freedwoman of Lucius, aged 98, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Also Scopdra, daughter and heir, had this made in accordance with the will.
EDCS-05502437.summary: A tombstone for Atilia Helpis, erected by her daughter and heir Scopdra as per the will.

EDCS-05502439.translation: Flavio Mustaro, freedman of Flaviae Flavinae, lies here, aged 46. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502439.summary: A tombstone for Flavio Mustaro, freedman of Flaviae Flavinae.

EDCS-05502440.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Iulius Silvester, aged 66. Seranna, his wife, to her most dutiful husband. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502440.summary: A tombstone for Iulius Silvester, erected by his devoted wife Seranna.

EDCS-05502442.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Mutia Varilla.
EDCS-05502442.summary: A tombstone or dedication to the spirits of the departed for Mutia Varilla.

EDCS-05502443.translation: Domitia Fuscina, daughter of Fuscus Metturicus, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502443.summary: A tombstone for Domitia Fuscina, daughter of Fuscus Metturicus.

EDCS-05502444.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Pompeius Donus, son, aged 40. Pompeia Sparsillina, daughter. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502444.summary: A tombstone for Pompeius Donus, erected by his daughter Pompeia Sparsillina.

EDCS-05502445.translation: To the departed spirits. Pompeius Eclectus, aged 50. Pompeia Sparsillina, his daughter.
EDCS-05502445.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Pompeius Eclectus, set up by his daughter.

EDCS-05502447.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Bruttius Proculus, son of Lucius Bruttius, is buried here, aged 30. Fusia Paterna, aged 15. Tiberius Bruttius Fusius Paternus, aged 50, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Paterna, aged 15.
EDCS-05502447.summary: This is a funerary inscription for multiple individuals, including Lucius Bruttius Proculus and Tiberius Bruttius Fusius Paternus, with mentions of Fusia Paterna.

EDCS-05502450.translation: Longinus had this made for his father Priscus Calaeticus.
EDCS-05502450.summary: This is a funerary monument inscription where Longinus commissioned it for his father, Priscus Calaeticus.

EDCS-05502452.translation: To Lucius Albinus, son of P. LLCICIV S. His father.
EDCS-05502452.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring Lucius Albinus, set up by his father.

EDCS-05502453.translation: To Lucius Domitius Caucino, son of Lucius, and to Lucius Domitius Paterno, son of Caucino, her husband.
EDCS-05502453.summary: This inscription is likely funerary, commemorating Lucius Domitius Caucino and Lucius Domitius Paterno, possibly set up by a wife or mother.

EDCS-05502455.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Valerius Lucanus, aged 22. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05502455.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Valerius Lucanus, aged 22.

EDCS-05502456.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Valerius Latinus, set up by Caecilia.

EDCS-05502458.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Caia dedicated a monument to Gaius.

EDCS-05502461.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Coelia Melissa fulfilled her vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-05502461.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by Coelia Melissa.

EDCS-05502462.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Coecilia Meliasa Vareden fulfilled her vow willingly and wholeheartedly.
EDCS-05502462.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by Coecilia Meliasa Vareden.

EDCS-05502463.translation: His mother took care to have this made for Mucio Olympus.
EDCS-05502463.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a mother for her deceased son, Mucio Olympus.

EDCS-05502464.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld; his mother made this memorial for Mercurius, aged 22.
EDCS-05502464.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the underworld for Mercurius, erected by his mother.

EDCS-05502465.translation: Lucius Iulius Rufinus willingly fulfilled his vow to the Nymphs of Varcila.
EDCS-05502465.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius Iulius Rufinus dedicated to the Nymphs of Varcila.

EDCS-05502466.translation: Saturninus fulfilled his vow for the well-being of Caius Clodius Quintilianus.
EDCS-05502466.summary: This is a votive inscription by Saturninus made for the health of Caius Clodius Quintilianus.

EDCS-05502476.translation: From his freedman and heir under the will, Laetus, for Lucius Sempronius Fuscus.
EDCS-05502476.summary: This inscription records that Laetus, freedman and heir, established this monument for Lucius Sempronius Fuscus in accordance with his will.

EDCS-05502481.translation: Sacred to the household gods; Valerius Secundus [dedicated this].
EDCS-05502481.summary: This is a dedication to the household gods (Lares) by Valerius Secundus.

EDCS-05502482.translation: Aplondus, son of Dagenicus, aged 20, lies buried here.
EDCS-05502482.summary: This is a tombstone for Aplondus, son of Dagenicus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-05502483.translation: For Aemilia Festa, aged 45. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502483.summary: This is a tombstone for Aemilia Festa, aged 45, with a common funerary wish.

EDCS-05502486.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Tertius, a slave of Gaius Plotius Apronianus, aged 85, lies buried here. Quietus placed this for his father.
EDCS-05502486.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tertius, an 85-year-old slave of Gaius Plotius Apronianus, with an additional dedication from his son Quietus.

EDCS-05502515.translation: [...], of the VI Legion, Theop[---], sevir.
EDCS-05502515.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records a soldier from the VI Legion and a sevir, possibly a local magistrate.

EDCS-05502528.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Caius Porcius Marinianus of Dianium dedicated this to his wife, Voconia Materna of Uxam.
EDCS-05502528.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Voconia Materna, set up by her husband Caius Porcius Marinianus.

EDCS-05502534.translation: Caletyches, aged 53. Terentia Primitiva [set this up] for her most devoted sister.
EDCS-05502534.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caletyches, erected by her sister Terentia Primitiva.

EDCS-05502537.translation: Iulia Octa Titia, aged 10, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502537.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Iulia Octa Titia.

EDCS-05502539.translation: To our Mogontinus? [From] Caius Iulius Silvanus.
EDCS-05502539.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly a dedication or a dedication to a person named Mogontinus by Caius Iulius Silvanus.

EDCS-05502543.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph, mentioning Marcus Sempronius.

EDCS-05502580.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly containing personal names.

EDCS-05502581.translation: Titus Valerius Clementi, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, a Roman knight, ordered two statues to be set up for himself and a funerary banquet to be held, by his will.
EDCS-05502581.summary: This inscription records the posthumous funerary arrangements, including statues and a banquet, made by Titus Valerius Clementi in his will.

EDCS-05502585.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Diana by Anulius Martialis.

EDCS-05502621.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly relating to a position or location.

EDCS-05502626.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Themis, aged 18, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Pompeius Severinus arranged for this to be made for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-05502626.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Themis, erected by her husband Pompeius Severinus.

EDCS-05502630.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription identifying Lucius as the son of Nigrius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502641.translation: From Homonius' workshop. May you use it well. Vasconius, with Tiberianus acting as procurator in Christ, had the granary built for our lord Valentinian Augustus in his third consulship and for the most distinguished man Eutropius, with Elefantus as scribe and Vitalianus and Nebridius as magistrates.
EDCS-05502641.summary: This inscription records the construction of a granary, noting the workshop, supervisors, and consuls, placing it in the reign of Valentinian Augustus (likely Valentinian I, 4th century CE).

EDCS-05502645.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Tutela by Lucius Porcius Severinus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502650.translation: For Licinia Maxedonica, daughter of C. Licinius Speratus(?), perpetual priestess. C. Licinius Hedymeles dedicated his own funds for his excellent patroness. The place was granted by decree of the order.
EDCS-05502650.summary: This funerary epitaph honors Licinia Maxedonica, a perpetual priestess and patroness, with the monument erected by C. Licinius Hedymeles, who also provided the burial plot through a senatorial decree, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502669.translation: To Titus Caesar, son of Augustus, Emperor Titus Vespasian, Pontifex, VI-time Tribune of the Plebs, designated VI-time Consul, Censor, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05502669.summary: This honorific inscription, likely set up by the local curia, records acclamations for Emperor Titus, highlighting his titles and offices, probably dating to his reign (79-81 CE).

EDCS-05502670.translation: For Gaius Sempronius Celer, son of Celer. By decree of the decurions of the municipium Baesuccitanum, this municipality granted him praise, a burial place, funeral expenses, and statues; the municipium Flavium Laminitanum, by decree of its decurions, granted praise, a statue; the municipium Flavium Tugiense, by decree of its decurions, granted praise and a burial place, funeral expenses; the municipium Flavium Vivatiense, by decree of its decurions, granted praise, a burial place, and funeral expenses; the citizens and inhabitants of Baesuccitani granted statues. Gaius Sempronius Celer, the father, and Sempronia Auge, the mother, having received honor, remitted the expenses. The place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05502670.summary: This extensive funerary inscription details the numerous honors and provisions granted to Gaius Sempronius Celer by several municipalities, including burial plots and statues, with his parents ultimately covering the costs, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502672.summary: This is a simple funerary or dedicatory inscription identifying Gaius Sempronius Celer as a father, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502673.translation: Aprilis, slave of Marcus Fulvius Flaccus, aged 5 years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502673.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for a five-year-old slave named Aprilis, son of Marcus Fulvius Flaccus, and includes a common epitaph wish, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502675.translation: Publius Licinius Lucullus to his wife Licinia Maurilla.
EDCS-05502675.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Publius Licinius Lucullus to his wife Licinia Maurilla, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502677.translation: Sempronia Prisca for herself and for Gaius Sempronius Achoristus her husband, aged 60 years, and for Sempronia Romana her daughter, aged 18 years.
EDCS-05502677.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sempronia Prisca, her husband Gaius Sempronius Achoristus, and their daughter Sempronia Romana, detailing their ages and likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502678.translation: Claudius, while alive, had this made for Cornelia Corn
EDCS-05502678.summary: An inscription recording a funerary monument or dedication commissioned by Claudius for Cornelia Corn.

EDCS-05502679.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Modestus, aged 33, dutiful to his family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502679.summary: A funerary epitaph for Modestus, giving his age and stating his piety.

EDCS-05502680.translation: son of Valens, aged [---], and Valeriana, aged 25.
EDCS-05502680.summary: An epitaph, likely a continuation of another, for Valeriana, giving her age and parentage.

EDCS-05502682.translation: For Pietas Augusta, because Cornelia Marullina, daughter of Caius, had promised to erect an altar to the order of Castulonenses, in memory of her son Lucius Cornelius Marullus; her heir, Caius Cornelius Bellicus, made this offering from the funds published for the games.
EDCS-05502682.summary: A dedication to Pietas Augusta, commemorating a promise made by Cornelia Marullina to erect an altar, fulfilled by her heir Caius Cornelius Bellicus.

EDCS-05502683.translation: Cornelius Celsinius, fulfilling a vow, gave and dedicated this altar.
EDCS-05502683.summary: A dedication of an altar by Cornelius Celsinius, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-05502689.translation: To Quintus Torius Culleo, son of Quintus, procurator of the Emperor in the province of Baetica, who repaired the walls, collapsed by age, with his own money, gave land for building baths, fortified the road that leads through the Castulon meadow to Sisapon, damaged by constant rains, placed statues of Venus Genetrix and Cupid at the theatre; he remitted to the people the sum of ten million sesterces which was publicly owed to him, and moreover remitted a public feast for the people for two days; the townspeople of Castulo decreed this, and provided games for two days.
EDCS-05502689.summary: A public inscription honoring Quintus Torius Culleo for his extensive benefactions to the city of Castulo, including repairs, land donation, road construction, art installation, and financial remission, as decreed by the town council.

EDCS-05502691.translation: To Quintus Cornelius Valerianus, son of Marcus, prefect of the [---] wing [---], prefect of detachments in Thrace for 15 years, [from the legion ---] Macedonica, from legion VIII Augusta, from the tribunes of broad stripe and [junior officers], from the prefect of cohorts, with a statue crowned [---] to the Castulonenses and to the cohorts of Servia Juvenalis [---] and to Titia Optata, daughter of Lucius, wife of [---], this colony [---] Patricia [---].
EDCS-05502691.summary: A dedication, likely for a statue, to Quintus Cornelius Valerianus, honoring his military career and benefactions to the colony of Castulo and its military units, possibly also honoring his wife Titia Optata.

EDCS-05502692.translation: To Quintus Marcius, son of Caius, tribune of the 7th Legion Gemina Felix and of the 14th Legion Gemina Victrix, the legionaries set up [this monument] for the well-deserving soldier.
EDCS-05502692.summary: A funerary monument erected by the legionaries for Quintus Marcius, a tribune who served in two legions.

EDCS-05502695.translation: Cornelia Verecundina, daughter of Lucius, had this erected to her husband Caius Cornelius Valens, son of Caius, Galerian, twice chief magistrate and flamen of Rome and Augustus, in accordance with his will.
EDCS-05502695.summary: A funerary dedication commissioned by Cornelia Verecundina for her husband Caius Cornelius Valens, detailing his civic and religious offices.

EDCS-05502697.translation: To Valeria Paetina, daughter of Caius, Tuccitana, priestess of the colony Patricia of Corduba, priestess and flaminica of the imperial colony Gemella, and flaminica or priestess of the municipality of Castulo.
EDCS-05502697.summary: An honorific inscription for Valeria Paetina, detailing her various religious and priestly offices in several Roman cities.

EDCS-05502700.summary: This is a fragment of a dedication possibly related to mining interests of Titus Iuventus.

EDCS-05502701.translation: Lucius, son of Lucius Caelus Flavinius, from Segisame.
EDCS-05502701.summary: This inscription records the name and origin of Lucius, son of Lucius Caelus Flavinius.

EDCS-05502702.translation: Aelia Barna, daughter of Quintus Lucius, [property measuring] 32 feet in front from the road and 30 feet in the field.
EDCS-05502702.summary: This inscription likely marks property boundaries belonging to Aelia Barna, daughter of Quintus Lucius.

EDCS-05502703.translation: Lucius Argentarius Celsinus [and] Sabina, daughter of Marcus, of the Baebia tribe, Lucius Argentarius Valens, by his will, this monument shall not follow its heir.
EDCS-05502703.summary: This funerary inscription details the establishment of a monument by Lucius Argentarius Valens, which is not to be inherited.

EDCS-05502704.translation: Valerius Baeticus, aged 55, dutiful towards his own.
EDCS-05502704.summary: This epitaph records the death of Valerius Baeticus, aged 55, emphasizing his piety.

EDCS-05502705.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aurelius Beneficus, aged 75, dutiful towards his own. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502705.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Beneficus, who died at the age of 75, with a standard wish for the afterlife.

EDCS-05502710.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Clodius, aged 45, dutiful towards his own. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502710.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Clodius, who died at the age of 45, with a standard wish for the afterlife.

EDCS-05502712.translation: Clodia, daughter of Claudius, aged 33, dutiful towards her own, [with a plot measuring] 33 feet in front and [unknown measurement] in the field.
EDCS-05502712.summary: This epitaph records the death of Clodia, daughter of Claudius, aged 33, with partial measurements of her burial plot.

EDCS-05502713.translation: Gaius Cornelius Brocchus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe.
EDCS-05502713.summary: This inscription records the name and tribal affiliation of Gaius Cornelius Brocchus.

EDCS-05502714.translation: Publius Cornelius Diphilus, freedman of Publius, from Castulo.
EDCS-05502714.summary: This inscription identifies Publius Cornelius Diphilus, a freedman from Castulo.

EDCS-05502715.translation: Lucius Cornelius Sosimilos, may the earth lie lightly upon you, 12 feet in front, 7 feet in the field.
EDCS-05502715.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Cornelius Sosimilos, providing burial dimensions.

EDCS-05502716.translation: And to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, [who] suffers the wretched limbs of the cippus left behind. And also no daughter, Cornelia Firmilla, 18 years old, mother, Silvanus, father, 54 years old, [daughter, daughter] 26 years old. Stop, passerby, I beg you, as you pass by say 'May the earth lie lightly upon you.'
EDCS-05502716.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for multiple family members, including Cornelia Firmilla, her parents, and another daughter, requesting passersby to offer a blessing.

EDCS-05502717.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Cornelia Ursa made this for her incomparable and most devoted husband in his own family, who lived 40 years; her father by name Subitanus, four sons; Ursa, his grieving wife, with his sons.
EDCS-05502717.summary: This funerary inscription records Cornelia Ursa dedicating a monument to her deceased husband, mentioning her father and their four sons.

EDCS-05502718.translation: Fausta and her son Chalaetus are buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you both.
EDCS-05502718.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Fausta and her son Chalaetus, wishing them peace in the afterlife.

EDCS-05502719.translation: Flaccina, 42 years old, is buried here with her husband; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502719.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Flaccina, aged 42, with her husband.

EDCS-05502720.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Flavia Capratina, 43 years old, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Tiberius Flavius Chrisimio, patron of his most devoted freedwoman.
EDCS-05502720.summary: This funerary inscription honors Flavia Capratina, aged 43, and mentions her patron, Tiberius Flavius Chrisimio.

EDCS-05502721.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Flavia Liberina, 16 years old.
EDCS-05502721.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavia Liberina, who died at the age of 16.

EDCS-05502722.translation: Marcus Fulvius Garos [---] to the gods of the shades [---] from the province of [---] his freedman [---] placed [---]
EDCS-05502722.summary: This fragmentary inscription mentions Marcus Fulvius Garos and appears to be a dedication or funerary text, possibly involving a freedman.

EDCS-05502723.translation: Caius Gallus Valerius, freedman of Lucius Macius Lucius Saturninus, 45 years old, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502723.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Caius Gallus Valerius, freedman of Lucius Macius Lucius Saturninus, aged 45.

EDCS-05502725.translation: Cornelia Severa, daughter of Marcus Severus, placed this for her mother Severina, daughter of Marcus, with her own expenses.
EDCS-05502725.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Cornelia Severa for her mother Severina, funded by the daughter.

EDCS-05502726.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Licinius Euhodus placed this altar for his most beloved wife Postumia Vernacla, dutiful to her family; she lived 35 years. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05502726.summary: A funerary altar dedicated to Postumia Vernacla by her husband Licinius Euhodus.

EDCS-05502727.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Memmia Caretosa lived 90 years and 3 months. Her most dutiful sons made this.
EDCS-05502727.summary: A funerary inscription for Memmia Caretosa, dedicated by her sons.

EDCS-05502728.translation: Memmia Marciana, 26 years old, dutiful to her family, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05502728.summary: A tombstone for Memmia Marciana, recording her age and familial devotion.

EDCS-05502729.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Paridia, 23 years old. Her father Caius Valerius Paris and her mother Valeria Italica [dedicated this].
EDCS-05502729.summary: A funerary dedication for Paridia, made by her parents.

EDCS-05502730.translation: Porcia, mother of Luci, made this for her daughter Cornelia Siseia.
EDCS-05502730.summary: A funerary inscription from a mother, Porcia, to her daughter Cornelia Siseia.

EDCS-05502731.translation: Lucius Postumius Hilarus, freedman of Lucius Aulus, for his brother Philerotus and his daughter Marituma, 19 years old. [Measurements of] feet in front, feet in the field.
EDCS-05502731.summary: A funerary dedication by Lucius Postumius Hilarus for his brother and daughter, possibly indicating land boundaries.

EDCS-05502739.translation: Dutiful to her family, she lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Her daughters made this.
EDCS-05502739.summary: A funerary monument erected by daughters for a deceased female relative.

EDCS-05502740.translation: He made this for his most beloved daughter.
EDCS-05502740.summary: A funerary dedication for a beloved daughter.

EDCS-05502742.translation: Cornelia Publi Severa placed this for her mother-in-law Cornelia Caesian, daughter of Marcus, at her own expense.
EDCS-05502742.summary: A funerary monument erected by a daughter-in-law, Cornelia Severa, for her mother-in-law Cornelia Caesian.

EDCS-05502744.translation: GLA for himself, for a friend, as heir.
EDCS-05502744.summary: A very brief inscription possibly related to property or inheritance, mentioning GLA (likely a personal name or abbreviation) and a friend.

EDCS-05502746.translation: Sacred to the divine power and to Jupiter Best and Greatest. Acarius, a discharged soldier of the VII Gemina Maximiana Pia Felix legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05502746.summary: This is a votive inscription by a veteran soldier dedicated to Jupiter.

EDCS-05502747.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, in his second year of tribunician power, Caesar.
EDCS-05502747.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription honoring Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, likely on a public monument.

EDCS-05502749.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Antistia Pontiana, wife, aged 20.
EDCS-05502749.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Antistia Pontiana, likely set up by Publius.

EDCS-05502750.translation: To himself and to Gaius Clodius, his father, and to Clodia Antonia, his mother.
EDCS-05502750.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording dedications to the deceased parents and possibly the dedicator themselves.

EDCS-05502751.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Clodius Victorinus, aged 20. A friend is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05502751.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Clodius Victorinus, set up by a friend.

EDCS-05502752.translation: Thallus, aged 27, dutiful, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05502752.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Thallus, an individual remembered as dutiful.

EDCS-05502754.translation: Sacred to Jupiter. Caius Flavius Corydon, freedman of Caius Flavius Faustus, gave and dedicated this altar in recognition of his office.
EDCS-05502754.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by a freedman to Jupiter, related to his public office.

EDCS-05502755.translation: To Mars Augustus. Quintus Lucretius Silvanus, freedman of Quintus Lucretius, gave and dedicated this altar in recognition of his office.
EDCS-05502755.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by a freedman to Mars Augustus, likely related to his public office.

EDCS-05502756.translation: To holy Mars. Sulpicius Cilo, soldier of the VII Gemina Antoniniana Pia Felix legion, willingly placed this altar with a willing spirit, according to his vow.
EDCS-05502756.summary: This is a votive inscription by a soldier of the VII Gemina legion dedicated to Mars.

EDCS-05502757.translation: To Gaius Cornelius Nigellus, his son, his patron, and to Gaius Cornelius Senicio.
EDCS-05502757.summary: This is a funerary inscription possibly honoring a patron, Gaius Cornelius Nigellus, and another individual, Gaius Cornelius Senicio.

EDCS-05502758.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cornelia Avita, dutiful to her own, lived 10 years, 9 months. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502758.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelia Avita, who died at the age of 10 years and 9 months.

EDCS-05502760.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Porcia Iunia lived 18 years, for her incomparable husband. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502760.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Porcia Iunia, dedicated to her 'incomparable husband'.

EDCS-05502761.translation: For Sempronia Prisca, aged [---], for herself and for Gaius Sempronius Achoristus, her husband, aged 60, and for Sempronia Rombella, their daughter, aged 17.
EDCS-05502761.summary: This is a funerary dedication intended for Sempronia Prisca, her husband, and their daughter.

EDCS-05502762.translation: For the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 11th time, hailed as emperor for the 11th time, consul for the 2nd time, father of his country, the most excellent and bravest proconsul prince, the state of the Vivatenses, from the [---] in [---] dedicated.
EDCS-05502762.summary: This is a public dedication by the people of Vivatenses to Emperor Septimius Severus, likely commemorating a visit or acknowledging his authority.

EDCS-05502763.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Lucretius Silvinus, aged 40, and Quintus Lucretius Silvanus, dutiful to his own, are buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you both.
EDCS-05502763.summary: This is a joint funerary epitaph for Quintus Lucretius Silvinus and Quintus Lucretius Silvanus.

EDCS-05502764.translation: Phoebas, aged 27, dutiful to her own, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502764.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Phoebas, who died at the age of 27.

EDCS-05502765.translation: Grapte, servant of Aurelius Propinquus, aged 75, dutiful to her own, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly.
EDCS-05502765.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Grapte, a freedwoman of Aurelius Propinquus, who died at the age of 75.

EDCS-05502766.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Father and mother made this memorial for Solacius, who lived 23 years, 6 months.
EDCS-05502766.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by parents for their son, Solacius, who died at the age of 23 years and 6 months.

EDCS-05502769.translation: For Isis Puellari, by order of the god. Fabiana, daughter of Lucius Fabius, grandmother, in honor of her most pious granddaughter Avita, from 112 pounds of silver and a half ounce, five scruples of writings, also ornaments: in a brooch a single pearl and six pearls, two emeralds, two cylinders, seven gemstones: a carbuncle, a hyacinth, two ceraunian gemstones. In the ears, two emeralds, two pearls. On the neck, a four-part piece with 36 pearls and 18 emeralds. In the clasps, two emeralds, two cylinders, eight emeralds, eight pearls. On the little finger, two rings with diamonds. On the next finger, a polyhedral ring with emeralds and pearls. On the top finger, a ring with an emerald. In the sandals, eight cylinders, [---].
EDCS-05502769.summary: This inscription records a dedication of precious offerings to the goddess Isis Puellari by Fabiana in honor of her granddaughter Avita.

EDCS-05502770.translation: Livia Chalcedonica, devoted to the goddess Isis, is buried here, adorned as she could be. On her neck she has a jeweled necklace; on her fingers, 20 emeralds. On her right [hand].
EDCS-05502770.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Livia Chalcedonica, a devotee of the goddess Isis, describing her burial adornments.

EDCS-05502774.translation: To Faustina Augusta, daughter of Antoninus Pius Augustus; the colony Julia Gemella Accis.
EDCS-05502774.summary: A dedication by the colony of Acci to Faustina, daughter of Antoninus Pius, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05502775.translation: To Aurelius Ver Caesar, son of Augustus, twice consul; the colony Accis.
EDCS-05502775.summary: A dedication by the colony of Acci to Marcus Aurelius Verus (co-emperor with Marcus Aurelius), likely from the mid-2nd century CE.

EDCS-05502776.translation: To Julia Mammaea Augusta, mother of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus and of the camp; the colony Julia Gemella Accis, devoted to her divinity.
EDCS-05502776.summary: A dedication by the colony of Acci to Julia Mammaea, mother of Severus Alexander, likely from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05502777.translation: To Magnia Urbsica Augusta, mother of the camp, wife of our lord Carinus the Invincible Augustus; the colony Julia Gemella Accis, devoted to her divinity.
EDCS-05502777.summary: A dedication by the colony of Acci to Magnia Urbsica, wife of the emperor Carinus, likely from the late 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05502778.translation: Publius Octavius Priscus ordered this to be set up by his will to Publius Octavius Flavianus, flamen of the deified Augusti of the province of Baetica.
EDCS-05502778.summary: A funerary monument erected by a son to his father, a priest in the Roman province of Baetica, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05502780.translation: Publius Octavius Priscus ordered this to be set up by his will to Proculus, his brother.
EDCS-05502780.summary: A funerary monument erected by a brother to his deceased brother, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05502781.translation: To Plotia Firmana, daughter of Marcus, his paternal aunt.
EDCS-05502781.summary: An epitaph for Plotia Firmana, specifying her familial relationship, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05502782.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus, conqueror of Armenia, of Parthia, supreme in the Medes, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 5th time, consul for the 3rd time; Lucius Alfenus Avitianus, primus pilus, tribune of the 3rd cohort of vigiles, served 12 years in the urban cohort.
EDCS-05502782.summary: An inscription honouring the emperor Marcus Aurelius Verus, likely erected by a military officer, Lucius Alfenus Avitianus, and dated to his co-reign with Marcus Aurelius (c. 161-169 CE).

EDCS-05502785.translation: Aurelius Iulius, aged 40, served for 16 years.
EDCS-05502785.summary: A brief inscription recording the age and service length of a soldier named Aurelius Iulius, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-05502787.translation: Cornelius Maternus gave this gift in fulfilment of a vow to Mercury.
EDCS-05502787.summary: A votive dedication to the god Mercury by Cornelius Maternus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05502788.translation: Quintus Atellius Iucundus, aged 70, lies here. Atellia Felicia, freedwoman of Quintus, aged [---], lies here.
EDCS-05502788.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Quintus Atellius Iucundus and Atellia Felicia, dated to an indeterminate period by the style of the text.

EDCS-05502789.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, emperor, consul. The state of the Tuatugenses is devoted to his divine will and majesty.
EDCS-05502789.summary: This is a dedication from the state of the Tuatugenses to Emperor Gordian III, likely from his reign (238-244 AD).

EDCS-05502790.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. [---] IN, aged 31, lies here. Cordua Baetabia Pra LNA T T. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502790.summary: This is a tomb inscription for an individual identified as 'IN', aged 31, from Corduba in Baetica, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502803.translation: Whoever you are, if you admire the high points of the towers and the vestibule of the city, strengthened by a double gate, with twin porticoes and arcades on the right and left, upon which the vaulted chamber of the curia is placed, Comenciolus ordered these things, a patrician sent by Mauricius Augustus, master of soldiers in Hispania, against barbarian enemies, great in virtue. May Hispania always rejoice in such a ruler, as long as the poles revolve, as long as the sun circles the world. In the 8th year of Augustus, in the 8th indiction.
