EDCS-05502803.summary: This is a dedication commissioned by Comenciolus, a high-ranking military official under the emperor Mauricius, celebrating the fortifications of a city and likely dating to the late 6th or early 7th century AD.

EDCS-05502817.translation: Marcus Puupius, son of Marcus, freedman Sextus; Lucius, son of Sextus, freedman Gaep; Marcus Prosius, son of Marcus, freedman Numerius; Titius, son of Lucius, freedman Nu; Caius Vereius, son of Marcus, freedman Antiochus; Brutus, son of Lucius, slave Eleuterus; Terentius, son of Caius, slave Philemo; Aleidius, son of Lucius, slave Alexander; Titinius, son of Lucius, slave Acerdo; Sapo, son of Marcus, slave; took care to have three wrestling courts and foundations made of concrete.
EDCS-05502817.summary: This inscription records the commissioning of construction work, including wrestling courts and foundations, by a group of freedmen and slaves, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502818.translation: Prefect, duovir, quinquennial official, who officially served and fell in the cause of the state, as much as he was among his own people. Silia, daughter of Publius.
EDCS-05502818.summary: This fragmented inscription refers to a civic official who died in service and mentions Silia, daughter of Publius, possibly his wife or relative, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502822.translation: Marcus Publius, son of Rosciei, of the Maecia tribe.
EDCS-05502822.summary: This is a fragmentary tribal inscription identifying Marcus Publius and his tribe, likely from the Roman Republican or Imperial period.

EDCS-05502825.summary: This is a very brief inscription possibly naming an occupation ('sitularius' - a maker or seller of buckets) and a person named Sergia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502901.translation: To Gnaeus, son of [---], of the Galeria tribe.
EDCS-05502901.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription identifying a person named Gnaeus and his tribal affiliation, likely from the Roman Republican or Imperial period.

EDCS-05502904.translation: Lucius Petronius, son of Lucius, Celer.
EDCS-05502904.summary: This is a simple dedication or naming inscription for Lucius Petronius Celer, son of Lucius, likely from the Roman Republican or Imperial period.

EDCS-05502905.summary: This is a dedication to Pompeia Eleuterio.

EDCS-05502907.translation: Sacred to the Genius of the place of Ficariensis; Albanus, the steward.
EDCS-05502907.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Genius of the place of Ficariensis by Albanus, the steward.

EDCS-05502908.translation: Sacred to the Genius of the association of Monte Ficariensis; Albanus, the steward.
EDCS-05502908.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Genius of the association of Monte Ficariensis by Albanus, the steward.

EDCS-05502909.translation: Sacred to Mother Earth; Albanus, the steward.
EDCS-05502909.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Mother Earth by Albanus, the steward.

EDCS-05502910.translation: To the Divine Manes, Corentis, aged 24, is laid here; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05502910.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Corentis, who died at the age of 24.

EDCS-05502911.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes, Varia, aged 6. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05502911.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Varia, who died at the age of 6.

EDCS-05502912.translation: Lucius Rubellius Philostratus is laid here; Lucius Rubellius Philostratus, his son, took care to have it made at his own expense.
EDCS-05502912.summary: This is a tomb inscription recording that Lucius Rubellius Philostratus was buried, and his son arranged for the monument.

EDCS-05502913.translation: In honor of the divine house, Celer Lepidus, son of Celeris, son of Celeris.
EDCS-05502913.summary: This inscription is an honorific dedication made by Celer Lepidus to the imperial family.

EDCS-05502918.translation: Marcus Fulvius Flaccus, freedman of Marcus, is laid here.
EDCS-05502918.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Marcus Fulvius Flaccus, a freedman.

EDCS-05502919.translation: Pompeia Bileseton Proba, daughter of Marcus, is alive.
EDCS-05502919.summary: This inscription states that Pompeia Bileseton Proba, daughter of Marcus, is alive.

EDCS-05502922.translation: Greetings, Lucretia, freedwoman of her master.
EDCS-05502922.summary: A brief greeting to Lucretia, a freedwoman, likely on a tombstone.

EDCS-05502923.translation: Gaius Cornelius Carito and Lucius Heius Labeo, duovirs, saw to the repair of the aqueducts by decree of the decurions and approved them.
EDCS-05502923.summary: This inscription records the repair of aqueducts overseen by two duovirs, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05502924.translation: Lucius Turcilius Rufus, son of Publius, built the baths.
EDCS-05502924.summary: This is a funerary monument commemorating Lucius Turcilius Rufus, who built the baths.

EDCS-05502926.translation: Marcus Terentius, freedman of Marcus Terentius, lies here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05502926.summary: This epitaph records Marcus Terentius, a freedman, buried in this location, with a wish for a light earth.

EDCS-05502939.translation: Publius Fabricius Iustus and Publius Fabricio Respecto, duovirs, restored the temple of Juno at their own expense by order of the council.
EDCS-05502939.summary: This inscription records the restoration of the temple of Juno by two duovirs, funded by their private wealth, as ordered by the local council.

EDCS-05502941.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely the name of an individual, Quintus Voconius.

EDCS-05502954.translation: To the departed spirits. Valeria Diopane for Lucius Cornelius Carpus, aged 50, her father.
EDCS-05502954.summary: This epitaph is dedicated by Valeria Diopane to her father, Lucius Cornelius Carpus, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-05502966.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Valerius Hermes saw to the construction of this for Marcus Valerius Flavo, beneficiary of the Legio VII Gemina, aged 60.
EDCS-05502966.summary: This funerary inscription records Marcus Valerius Hermes commissioning a monument for Marcus Valerius Flavo, a soldier (beneficiarius) in the Legio VII Gemina, who died at 60.

EDCS-05502987.translation: The boundary of Caius, his most devoted wife, Valerius.
EDCS-05502987.summary: This inscription marks a boundary, possibly of property, and mentions Caius, his devoted wife, and a person named Valerius.

EDCS-05503046.translation: Tomb of Proba. Publius Incesuus, may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05503046.summary: This inscription records the tomb of Proba, with a wish for a light earth, commissioned by Publius Incesuus.

EDCS-05503048.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the burial of Quintus Agileius Donatus.

EDCS-05503050.translation: Caius Antestius Macrinus lived 20 years. Hail and farewell.
EDCS-05503050.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life and death of Caius Antestius Macrinus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-05503051.translation: To the memory of the deceased Antestia. Her husband lived 40 years, 3 months, and 5 days.
EDCS-05503051.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated to Antestia and records the lifespan of her husband.

EDCS-05503057.summary: This funerary epitaph records the age of Quintus Caecilius Samaius.

EDCS-05503059.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Clodia Cucuma.

EDCS-05503060.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or epitaph fragment mentioning Egnatuleia, daughter of Caius.

EDCS-05503063.translation: To the Divine Shades. Maro Talassa lived [---]. Her father Siliviviaanus set this up.
EDCS-05503063.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated to Maro Talassa and records that her father, Siliviviaanus, erected the monument.

EDCS-05503065.summary: This inscription seems to be part of a funerary epitaph or dedication for Caius, son of Sergius.

EDCS-05503066.translation: Sergius Ingenuus, son of Sergia Caesula. Died [---] 20. Farewell.
EDCS-05503066.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Sergius Ingenuus, son of Sergia Caesula, at the age of 20.

EDCS-05503070.summary: This funerary epitaph records the age of Sergia, who died at 6 years old.

EDCS-05503072.summary: This inscription records the name and age of Egna.

EDCS-05503084.translation: Marcus Badius Honoratus and Cornelius Silvanus built the temple of the Great Mother Attis with their own money.
EDCS-05503084.summary: This inscription records the construction of a temple to the Great Mother Attis by Marcus Badius Honoratus and Cornelius Silvanus.

EDCS-05503091.translation: Having held all the honors, Maecius Maecianus erected the statue.
EDCS-05503091.summary: This inscription records Maecius Maecianus erecting a statue after holding numerous public offices.

EDCS-05503103.translation: Lucius Minicius Apronianus, duovir quinquennalis, ordered by his will that a statue be erected from the silver funds of 15 pounds, with twelve ounces, to the Genius of the colony of Tarraco, the Triumphant City.
EDCS-05503103.summary: This inscription records the will of Lucius Minicius Apronianus, a quinquennial duovir, to erect a statue to the Genius of Tarraco.

EDCS-05503110.translation: Afrania Tertullina gladly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-05503110.summary: This inscription records Afrania Tertullina fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-05503112.translation: Sacred to Isis Augusta, in honor and memory of Julia Sabina, from her mother Clodia Orbiana.
EDCS-05503112.summary: This inscription records a sacred dedication to Isis Augusta by Clodia Orbiana in honor of her daughter Julia Sabina.

EDCS-05503113.translation: Sacred to Juno Augusta, in honor and memory of Caecilia Januaria, Lucius Caecilius Epitynchanus made [this] with his own money for his most excellent wife.
EDCS-05503113.summary: This inscription records Lucius Caecilius Epitynchanus dedicating a shrine to Juno Augusta for his wife Caecilia Januaria.

EDCS-05503114.translation: Telesphor and Plate gave a gift to our Lares and to the Genius of our Tutela.
EDCS-05503114.summary: This inscription records Telesphor and Plate making a gift to household gods and their protective Genius.

EDCS-05503115.translation: Sacred to Mars of the Campus. For the safety of the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Commodus Augustus and the equestrian guard. Titus Aurelius Decimus, centurion of the Legio VII Gemina Felix, prefect, and also instructor, dedicated [this] on the Kalends of March, in the consulship of Mamertinus and Rufus.
EDCS-05503115.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a sanctuary to Mars of the Campus by Titus Aurelius Decimus for the well-being of Emperor Commodus and his guards, dated to March 1st, 191 AD.

EDCS-05503117.translation: Quintus Attius Messor rebuilt the portico in front of the temple of Minerva Augusta, which had been damaged by age, through the work of plasterers and painters, at his own expense, and dedicated it to the worshippers.
EDCS-05503117.summary: This inscription records Quintus Attius Messor restoring a damaged portico of the temple of Minerva Augusta at his own expense.

EDCS-05503119.translation: Sacred to Neptune Augustus, made with his own money by Aemilia Nymphodote for her son and freedman, Aemilius Augustalis.
EDCS-05503119.summary: This is a dedication to Neptune Augustus made by Aemilia Nymphodote.

EDCS-05503121.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus, Atimetus, freedman and secretary of the province of Hispania Citerior, gave this gift for the welfare of the Emperor Caesar Hadrian Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the country, and his children.
EDCS-05503121.summary: This is a dedication to Silvanus Augustus made by Atimetus, a freedman and provincial secretary, for the welfare of Emperor Antoninus Pius and his family.

EDCS-05503122.translation: Publius Naevius Adiutor fulfilled his vow to the goddess Tutela.
EDCS-05503122.summary: This records Publius Naevius Adiutor fulfilling a vow to the goddess Tutela.

EDCS-05503124.translation: Aemilius Severianus, a mime actor, set this up to the god Tutela.
EDCS-05503124.summary: This records Aemilius Severianus, a mime actor, setting up a monument to the god Tutela.

EDCS-05503126.translation: Marcus Acilius Nymphodotus to the deified Augustus.
EDCS-05503126.summary: This is a dedication to the deified Augustus by Marcus Acilius Nymphodotus.

EDCS-05503128.summary: This is an inscription dedicated to the deified Faustina.

EDCS-05503129.translation: To Faustina, daughter of Emperor Antoninus.
EDCS-05503129.summary: This is a dedication to Faustina, daughter of Emperor Antoninus.

EDCS-05503130.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus.
EDCS-05503130.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus.

EDCS-05503131.translation: To Lucius Aelius, son of Emperor Antoninus, Aurelius Commodus.
EDCS-05503131.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Aelius Aurelius Commodus, son of Emperor Antoninus.

EDCS-05503132.translation: To Emperor Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus.
EDCS-05503132.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus.

EDCS-05503134.translation: To the most brave and most merciful Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carus Invictus Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power, consul for the second time, father of the fatherland, proconsul; Marcus Aurelius Valentinianus, most illustrious man, president of the province of Hispania Citerior, legate of the emperors with praetorian power, devoted to his divine majesty.
EDCS-05503134.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Carus by Marcus Aurelius Valentinianus, the governor of Hispania Citerior, likely dating to his reign (282-283 AD).

EDCS-05503135.translation: To the most victorious prince of the youth, Marcus Aurelius Carinus, most noble Caesar, consul, proconsul; Marcus Aurelius Valentinianus, most illustrious man, president of the province of Hispania Citerior, legate of the emperors with praetorian power, devoted to his divine majesty.
EDCS-05503135.summary: This is a dedication to Caesar Carinus by Marcus Aurelius Valentinianus, the governor of Hispania Citerior, likely dating to the period when Carinus was co-emperor (283-284 AD).

EDCS-05503136.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Aurelius Valerius Maximianius Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power, consul for the second time, proconsul; Postumius Lupercus, most perfect man, president of the province of Hispania Citerior, devoted to their divine majesty.
EDCS-05503136.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Maximian by Postumius Lupercus, the governor of Hispania Citerior, likely dating to the period of the Tetrarchy (286-305 AD).

EDCS-05503137.translation: To the conqueror of all barbarian peoples and above all previous rulers, the most provident Emperor Caesar Valerius Licinianus Licinius Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power, father of the fatherland, consul for the second time, proconsul; Valerius Iulianus, most perfect man, president of the province of Hispania Tarraconensis, always most devoted to his divine majesty.
EDCS-05503137.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Licinius by Valerius Iulianus, the governor of Hispania Tarraconensis, likely dating to the period of the Tetrarchy, around 308-313 AD.

EDCS-05503139.translation: To our most pious and renowned lord, Crispus, most noble, most brave, and most fortunate Caesar; Septimius Acindynus, most illustrious man, acting with vice-sacred authority in the Spains, including the province of Tingitana, always most devoted to his divine majesty.
EDCS-05503139.summary: This is a dedication to Caesar Crispus by Septimius Acindynus, acting as a high-ranking official in the Spains, likely dating to the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-05503141.translation: To the most blessed and most flourishing age of our lords Leo and Anthemius, Augusti.
EDCS-05503141.summary: This is a brief acclamation to the emperors Leo I and Anthemius, likely dating to their joint reign (467-474 AD).

EDCS-05503142.translation: To Publius Alfius Maximus Numerius Licinianus, son of Publius Alfius, of the Galeria tribe, a man of the board of X men judging disputes, a quaestor of the city, a tribune of the plebs-elect, a praetor, a Parthian governor; Publius Alfius Avitus Numerius Maternus, his son, to his dearest father.
EDCS-05503142.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a son to his father, Publius Alfius Maximus Numerius Licinianus, who held numerous important offices in Rome and the provinces, likely dating to the late 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05503143.translation: To Quintus Atrius Clunius, legate of the emperor with pro-praetorian power of the provinces of Thrace, Cappadocia, Greater Syria, and Hispania Citerior; Claudius Iustus, centurion of the Legio VII Gemina Pia Felix Severiana Alexandriana, to his most abstemious legate.
EDCS-05503143.summary: This is a dedication from a centurion, Claudius Iustus, to his legate, Quintus Atrius Clunius, who governed multiple eastern provinces and Hispania Citerior, likely dating to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-05503144.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Vincentius, most perfect man, president of the province of Hispania Tarraconensis, and above all other presidents, the most just restorer of the Montan Baths; Messius Marianus, curator of the public affairs of Tarraco.
EDCS-05503144.summary: This is a dedication by Messius Marianus, a local official in Tarraco, to the governor Marcus Aurelius Vincentius, praising his restoration of public baths, likely dating to the late 3rd or early 4th century AD.

EDCS-05503145.translation: To Marcus Caecilius Novatilianus, most illustrious man, legate-jurist, most abstemious, most just, most eloquent, patron of the most splendid city of Tarraco.
EDCS-05503145.summary: This is an honorary dedication by the city council of Tarraco to their patron, Marcus Caecilius Novatilianus, who served as a legal official, likely dating to the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05503146.translation: To Tib(erius) Cl(audius) Candidus, consul, member of the XV board of priests for sacred rites, governor pro praetore of the province of Further Spain and in it commander by land and sea against rebellious public enemies, likewise of Asia, likewise of Noricum, commander of the Illyrian army during the Asian, Parthian, and Gallic campaigns, administrator of the most splendid city of Nicomedea, likewise of the Ephesians, governor pro praetore of the province of Asia, curator of the city of Teos, promoted among the praetorian class, likewise among the tribunes, procurator of the XX for inheritances throughout Gaul, Lugdunensis and Belgica and both Germanies, commander of the forces of the second Germanic expedition, military tribune of the II Augustan Legion, prefect of the second cohort of Roman citizens. Silius Hospes, hastatus of the X Gemina Legion, his staff officer, erected this to their most excellent leader.
EDCS-05503146.summary: A funerary or commemorative inscription honoring Tib(erius) Cl(audius) Candidus with a lengthy list of his military and administrative achievements, erected by Silius Hospes.

EDCS-05503147.translation: To Lucius Domitius Gallicanus Papinianus, a most distinguished man, imperial legate pro praetore of Lower Germany, imperial legate pro praetore of Further Spain, imperial legate pro praetore of Dalmatia, most devoted and most upright. Aurelius Iulianus, to his incomparable patron.
EDCS-05503147.summary: An inscription dedicating a monument to Lucius Domitius Gallicanus Papinianus by Aurelius Iulianus, listing his high imperial appointments and praising his character.

EDCS-05503148.summary: A dedication to Fabius Cilianus, who held the office of quaestor.

EDCS-05503151.translation: To Fulvia Procula, a most distinguished lady. Celsia Flavina, her mother, to her dearest daughter.
EDCS-05503151.summary: A funerary dedication from a mother to her deceased daughter, Fulvia Procula.

EDCS-05503152.translation: To Quintus Gargilius Macrus, son of Quintus, of the Arnensian tribe, Aufidianus, moneyer of the capital, military tribune of the X Gemina Legion, urban quaestor, tribune of the plebs, praetor, proconsul of the province of Crete and Cyrene, imperial legate of Further Spain. Apronia Iusta, wife, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-05503152.summary: A funerary inscription for Quintus Gargilius Macrus, listing his career path from military tribune to proconsul and imperial legate, erected by his wife.

EDCS-05503153.translation: To Quintus Hedius Pollianus Lollianus Gentianus, son of Lucius, augur, consul, proconsul of Asia, censor of the province of Lugdunensis, likewise count of the cities of Lugudunum and the Veliters, candidate quaestor, candidate praetor, legate of the XXII Primigenia Legion, tribune of the VII Gemina Felix Legion, moneyer of gold, silver, and bronze. Fabius Marcellus.
EDCS-05503153.summary: A dedication to Quintus Hedius Lollianus Gentianus, detailing his extensive senatorial and administrative career, including consul and proconsul, by Fabius Marcellus.

EDCS-05503154.translation: To Quintus Hedius Lollianus Gentianus, son of Lucius, imperial legate pro praetore of Further Spain, to his most excellent commander. From the cornicularii, commentarienses, and speculatores of the VII Gemina Felix Legion: Pompeius Agrippa, Iulius Avitus (commentarienses); Caius Iulius Crassus, Titus Publienius Lupus (speculatores); Quintus Alfius Ianuarius, Gaius Valerius Marcianus, Lucius Maenaius Sementivus, Gaius Caecilius Restitutus, Caius Aurelius Octavius, Gaius Aufidius Hilarus, Lucius Aurelius Phileterus, Lucius Alfidius Urbanus, Caius Iulius Florentinus, Lucius Sempronius Firmanus.
EDCS-05503154.summary: A dedication to Quintus Hedius Lollianus Gentianus, imperial legate pro praetore of Further Spain, by members of his staff from the VII Gemina Felix Legion.

EDCS-05503155.translation: To Marcus Licinius Oviniianus Aemilianus, son of Galeria, a most distinguished man, of the convention of Bracara Augusta.
EDCS-05503155.summary: A dedication to Marcus Licinius Oviniianus Aemilianus, a distinguished man from the conventus of Bracara Augusta.

EDCS-05503156.translation: To the Divine Shades, of Marcus Maecius Probus, consular legate of the emperors pro praetore. To his most holy wife, Pomponia Arria, a most distinguished lady. And to their father, Marcus Pomponius Maecius Probus, a most distinguished boy.
EDCS-05503156.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Maecius Probus and his son, erected by his wife, Pomponia Arria.

EDCS-05503157.translation: Under the Emperor Caesar Publius Helvius Pertinax, prince of the Senate, father of the country, consul for the second time; with Quintus Sosius Falcone and Gaius Julius Erucius Clarus as consuls, on the third day before the Ides of February. The judgment which Lucius Novius Rufus, clarissimus vir, legate of the emperor pro praetore, delivered between the co-workers of the river Larensis and Valeria Faventina, having been inscribed and published the day before the Nones of November in the words written below. Rufus the legate, after conferring with the council, read the decree from the wooden tablet: 'My intention, with which I was approached most recently, is consistent with the arguments presented by both sides; the form of the case, which was acted upon before me, has been proven anew. Therefore, those who entered into Valeria Faventina’s private property should leave it immediately.'
EDCS-05503157.summary: A legal document recording a judgment delivered by the imperial legate Lucius Novius Rufus regarding a dispute between river workers and Valeria Faventina, dated to February under Emperors Pertinax and Falcone/Erucius Clarus.

EDCS-05503158.translation: To Lucius Ovinius Rusticus Cornelius, son of Lucius, of the tribe Quirina, consul-designate, praetor from the tribunes, curator of the Flaminian Way, legate of the legion of Lower Moesia, curator of the Tiburtine Way, curator of the republic of Ricinium; Rufria Ovina Cornelia, daughter of [him], to her most dutiful father.
EDCS-05503158.summary: A funerary dedication by Rufria Ovina Cornelia to her father, Lucius Ovinius Rusticus Cornelius, who held numerous high-ranking civic and military posts, likely dating to the late 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05503159.translation: To Lucius Septimius Mannus, most distinguished man, from the council of the province of Citerior Hispania.
EDCS-05503159.summary: An inscription honoring Lucius Septimius Mannus, likely a prominent local official, by the provincial council of Citerior Hispania, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05503160.translation: To the Divine Shades, from his heirs, of Quintus Umbricius Proculus, imperial legate of the province of Citerior Hispania, most distinguished man.
EDCS-05503160.summary: A funerary dedication by the heirs of Quintus Umbricius Proculus, an imperial legate in Citerior Hispania, indicating his high status, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05503167.translation: To Aurelius Ianuarius, most perfect man, procurator of the inherited estates, procurator of Osrhoena, Syria Coele, procurator of the Illyrian revenues, procurator of the province of Citerior Hispania Tarraconensis, governor of the province of Tingitana, governor of the province of Mauretania Caesariensis.
EDCS-05503167.summary: An inscription detailing the extensive career of Aurelius Ianuarius, who served as a procurator and governor in various Roman provinces, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05503168.translation: To the prefect of the I Breucorum cohort, tribune of soldiers, procurator of the deified Titus at Alexandria, agonothete of the penteteric festival twice, from his will; his heirs are Domitius, freedman, Caius Caecilius Fronto and Marcus Flavius Veranus.
EDCS-05503168.summary: A funerary dedication by the heirs of Domitius, a freedman who served in military and administrative posts and sponsored games, likely dating to the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05503169.translation: To the Divine Shades, from his will, of Lucius Alfidius Urbanus, tribune of soldiers of the VII Gemina Legion, Pious, Fortunate, Antoninian, a man in his life most excellent; by the arbitration of his father-in-law, Cornelius Rusticus. 'While you live, life is a small gift; soon it arises, then slowly it strengthens, then slowly it fades.'
EDCS-05503169.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Alfidius Urbanus, a military tribune, including a philosophical reflection on life from his will, likely from the Antonine period (mid-2nd to early 3rd century CE).

EDCS-05503170.translation: To Lucius Antonius Silon, son of Titus, of the tribe Galeria, prefect of the artificers, prefect of the IV Thracian cohort, prefect of the new cohort of recruits, prefect of the maritime coast of the convent of Tarraco.
EDCS-05503170.summary: An inscription honoring Lucius Antonius Silon, detailing his military and administrative career as a prefect in various capacities, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05503171.translation: To Quintus Caecilius Fronton, son of Lucius, of the tribe Galeria, quaestor, duovir, procurator of Augustus; from Gnaeus Cornelius Eupelastus and Gnaeus Cornelius Euhormus, for his outstanding merits as a friend.
EDCS-05503171.summary: A dedication by Gnaeus Cornelius Eupelastus and Gnaeus Cornelius Euhormus to their friend Quintus Caecilius Fronton, recognizing his merits and listing his public offices, likely from the late 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05503172.translation: To Caecilius Placidus, son of Placidus, of the tribe Quirina, prefect of the I cohort, tribune of soldiers of the legion.
EDCS-05503172.summary: An inscription honoring Caecilius Placidus, detailing his military career as a prefect and tribune, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05503173.translation: To Gaius Julius Speratianus, son of Gaius, of the Arnensis tribe, prefect of the I Celtiberian cohort; from his heirs.
EDCS-05503173.summary: A dedication by the heirs of Gaius Julius Speratianus, who served as prefect of the I Celtiberian cohort, likely dating to the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05503174.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Aelius Aquilus, soldier of the VII Gemina Pia Felix legion, who lived 23 years, served 2 years and 7 months, [---] OSA to his most dutiful brother.
EDCS-05503174.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Aelius Aquilus, a soldier in the VII Gemina legion, likely set up by his brother.

EDCS-05503175.translation: To Quintus Annius Aper, a scout of the VII Gemina Felix legion, who died intestate; his colleagues erected this at their own expense: Titus Flavius Reburrus, Lucius Valerius Festus, Lucius Valerius Maternus, Lucius Sempronius Maternus, Lucius Annius Vitalis, Marcus Memmius Celer, Publius Cornelius Grattianus.
EDCS-05503175.summary: This tombstone for Quintus Annius Aper, a scout in the VII Gemina legion, was erected by his colleagues.

EDCS-05503176.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Anteius Antiochus, freedman, made this for his excellent patron Lucius Anteius Flavius, a beneficiary of the consular governor, citizen of Asturica, of the VII Gemina Pia Felix legion, aged 45, with 23 years of service, according to his own will.
EDCS-05503176.summary: This tomb inscription records Anteius Antiochus honoring his patron Lucius Anteius Flavius, a soldier of the VII Gemina legion and beneficiary of a consular governor.

EDCS-05503177.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Lucius Aufidius Felix, beneficiary of the consular governor, a scout of the VII Gemina Felix legion, well-deserving.
EDCS-05503177.summary: This epitaph honors Lucius Aufidius Felix, a soldier and beneficiary of a consular governor, serving in the VII Gemina legion.

EDCS-05503178.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Avidia Nice, wife of the rarest example. Publicius Apronianus, hastatus of the XXII Primigenia legion, made this.
EDCS-05503178.summary: This tombstone commemorates Avidia Nice, wife of Publicius Apronianus, a soldier of the XXII Primigenia legion.

EDCS-05503179.translation: Ulpia Iuventina, wife and heir, saw to the erection of this for her most dutiful and indulgent husband, Marcus Aurelius Lucilius, son of Marcus, of the Papiria tribe, from Poetovio, unique soldier of the Emperor, centurion of the I Adiutrix legion, II Traiana, VIII Augusta, XIIII Gemina, VII Claudia, and VII Gemina, chief hastatus, aged 60, with 40 years of service.
EDCS-05503179.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Ulpia Iuventina for her husband Marcus Aurelius Lucilius, a highly decorated centurion who served in multiple legions and held the rank of chief hastatus.

EDCS-05503180.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Aurelius Severus, a veteran of the VII Gemina Felix legion, formerly a beneficiary of the consular governor. Plotia Trophime to her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-05503180.summary: This epitaph honors Aurelius Severus, a veteran of the VII Gemina legion and former beneficiary of a consular governor, erected by his wife Plotia Trophime.

EDCS-05503181.translation: Abiliana, wife, to her most indulgent husband, Antonius Saturninus, formerly a beneficiary of the consular governor.
EDCS-05503181.summary: This tombstone is dedicated by Abiliana to her husband Antonius Saturninus, who served as a beneficiary of a consular governor.

EDCS-05503182.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Caecilius Maturus, centurion of the frumentarii of the VII Gemina legion, who lived 37 years, 8 months, and 5 days. Aelia Cogitata, his mother, Caecilius Dexter and Artemas, his brothers, and Valeria Lupa, his wife, his heirs, erected this according to his will.
EDCS-05503182.summary: This funerary inscription for Caecilius Maturus, a centurion of the VII Gemina legion, was erected by his mother, brothers, and wife as his heirs.

EDCS-05503183.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Chrysampelus made this at his own expense for his excellent patron Titus Cassius Flavinus, centurion of the X Gemina legion.
EDCS-05503183.summary: Chrysampelus erected this tombstone for his patron, Titus Cassius Flavinus, a centurion in the X Gemina legion.

EDCS-05503184.translation: To the departed spirits. Allia Paralihenis made this for Titus Cornelius F, a beneficiarius of the Seventh Gemina Pius Felix legion, after 24 years of service; he lived 57 years. To a deserving host.
EDCS-05503184.summary: This is a tombstone for Titus Cornelius F, a beneficiarius in the Seventh Gemina legion, erected by Allia Paralihenis.

EDCS-05503185.translation: For the memory of Firmidius Caecilianus, a beneficiarius consularis of the Seventh Gemina Pius Felix legion. Valeria Primula, his wife, made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-05503185.summary: This is a tombstone for Firmidius Caecilianus, a beneficiarius consularis in the Seventh Gemina legion, erected by his wife.

EDCS-05503186.translation: To the departed spirits. Fulvius Cornelianus, freedman, made this for his deserving patron, Gnaeus Fulvius Capratinus, from the province of Baetica, approved for service in the Sixth Ferrata legion, transferred as a frumentarius to the Seventh Gemina Pia Felix legion, and appointed beneficiarius consularis; he served 21 years and lived 40 years.
EDCS-05503186.summary: This is a tombstone for Gnaeus Fulvius Capratinus, who served in multiple legions and ranks, erected by his freedman.

EDCS-05503187.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulius Hermadio made this for his most devoted patron, Gaius Julius Comatus, a cornicularius consularis, aged 48.
EDCS-05503187.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Julius Comatus, a cornicularius consularis, erected by his patron.

EDCS-05503188.translation: To the departed spirits. Herennius Rogatus [made this] for Julius Flavius, a commentariensis of the Seventh Gemina Felix legion, a quaestionarius, his dearest friend.
EDCS-05503188.summary: This is a tombstone for Julius Flavius, a commentariensis and quaestionarius in the Seventh Gemina legion, erected by Herennius Rogatus.

EDCS-05503189.translation: Gaius Julius Reburrus, a soldier of the Seventh Gemina Felix legion, from Segisama Brasaca, aged 52, with 24 years of service. Licinius Rufus, a soldier of the same legion, [made this for him].
EDCS-05503189.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Julius Reburrus, a soldier of the Seventh Gemina legion, erected by Licinius Rufus.

EDCS-05503190.translation: To the departed spirits. Caius Julius Ruschus, a centurion of the Twelfth Fulminata legion, made this for his deserving freedman, Gaius Julius Secundus, who lived 38 years, 3 months, and 10 days.
EDCS-05503190.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Julius Secundus, a freedman and soldier in the Twelfth Fulminata legion, erected by his centurion patron.

EDCS-05503191.translation: Lucius Junius Dionysius, a cornicularius consularis, [made this] for his most excellent cornicularius consularis.
EDCS-05503191.summary: This inscription likely commemorates Lucius Junius Dionysius, a cornicularius consularis, possibly honoring another holder of the same rank.

EDCS-05503193.translation: To the departed spirits. Aelia Valentina made this for her deserving husband, Quintus Moneius Verecundus, a veteran of the Seventh Gemina Felix legion, from Narbonne.
EDCS-05503193.summary: This is a tombstone for Quintus Moneius Verecundus, a veteran of the Seventh Gemina legion, erected by his wife.

EDCS-05503194.translation: Mamilia Prisca [made this] for her most excellent husband, Lucius Numerius Felix, son of Lucius, a centurion of the Seventh Gemina Felix legion, a centurion of the Twentieth Victrix legion, a centurion of the Third Cyrenaica legion, a centurion of the Twenty-second Primigenia legion, and a centurion of the Third Italica legion.
EDCS-05503194.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Numerius Felix, a highly decorated centurion who served in five different legions, erected by his wife.

EDCS-05503195.translation: To the Divine Manes. Porcius Paris and Quintius Urbicus, his colleagues, erected (this monument) to Quintio Arato of Tarraco, their deserving freedman.
EDCS-05503195.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by colleagues for a deserving freedman named Quintio Arato.

EDCS-05503196.translation: To Gnaeus Pompeius Fructus, a staff officer of the consular governor of Toletum, aged 42. Terentius Bassinus, the heir, made this in accordance with the will of Domitia Fortunata, his mother.
EDCS-05503196.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gnaeus Pompeius Fructus, a staff officer, erected by his mother's heir.

EDCS-05503197.translation: To the Divine Manes. Caius Tadius Lucanus, a centurion of the Legio VII Gemina Felix, set this up to his freedman Caius Tadius Ianuarius of Pisaurensis, who lived 34 years, (a freedman) who had deserved well of him.
EDCS-05503197.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a centurion for his well-deserving freedman, Caius Tadius Ianuarius.

EDCS-05503198.translation: To the Divine Manes. Tadia Honorata, his daughter, (erected this) to Lucius Tadius Simplex, a most distinguished man, former legate of the Emperor in the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503198.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a daughter for her father, Lucius Tadius Simplex, a former imperial legate.

EDCS-05503199.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aelia Parthenis (set this up) to Valerius Atticus, staff officer of the Legio VII Gemina Pia Felix, with 24 years of service, who lived 41 years, her deserving guest-friend.
EDCS-05503199.summary: A funerary epitaph erected by Aelia Parthenis for her deserving guest-friend, Valerius Atticus, a soldier with 24 years of service.

EDCS-05503200.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Caius Valerius Reburrus, a veteran. His wife Marcia Procula and his son Valerius Reburrinus (erected this) to their most dutiful father.
EDCS-05503200.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a wife and son for their dutiful father, Caius Valerius Reburrus, a veteran.

EDCS-05503202.translation: Lucius Valerius Secundus, son of Lucius, from Albin Milia, a soldier of the Legio VII Gemina Felix. Pamphilius Varus and Valius Velox, soldiers of the same legion, his heirs, erected (this monument).
EDCS-05503202.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by the heirs for Lucius Valerius Secundus, a soldier of the Legio VII Gemina Felix.

EDCS-05503203.translation: To Lucius Valerius Severus, a veteran of the Legio VII Gemina Felix. Caius Iulius Severus (erected this) to his most dutiful father.
EDCS-05503203.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Caius Iulius Severus for his father, Lucius Valerius Severus, a veteran of the Legio VII Gemina Felix.

EDCS-05503204.translation: Caius Valerius Avillius, of the Voltinia tribe, from Nemausus, a veteran of the Legio VII Gemina Felix, died aged 40 years, lies here buried.
EDCS-05503204.summary: A tombstone recording the death and burial of Caius Valerius Avillius, a veteran of the Legio VII Gemina Felix.

EDCS-05503205.translation: To the Divine Manes. May you fare well, traveler, who did not pass me by. Read this. Resting here I lie, born in Mylasa, I lived 5 years and months. If anyone comes later, this mausoleum owes nothing to anyone.
EDCS-05503205.summary: A funerary epitaph with a boastful epitaph for a young child from Mylasa, possibly indicating a family tomb.

EDCS-05503207.translation: A soldier of the 10th Legion Gemina rests here.
EDCS-05503207.summary: This is a tombstone for a soldier of the 10th Legion Gemina.

EDCS-05503210.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld: For Lucius Gargilius Rufus, record-keeper of civil affairs, an excellent and most honorable man.
EDCS-05503210.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Lucius Gargilius Rufus, a record-keeper, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05503211.translation: To Marcus Laelius Sabinianus, a decurial and aedilician official, from his dearest sister Laelia Coete, in Rome.
EDCS-05503211.summary: This epitaph commemorates Marcus Laelius Sabinianus, an official, dedicated by his sister, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05503212.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld: For Publius Aelius Aelianus Successus, freedman of Augustus, registrar of the province of Hispania Citerior, and his most dutiful son, from his parents.
EDCS-05503212.summary: This funerary inscription honors Publius Aelius Aelianus Successus, a registrar of Hispania Citerior, and is dedicated by his parents.

EDCS-05503213.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld: For Aurelius Macedo, freedman. From his freedman Marcianus, freedman of Augustus, and DaphIIne, his parents.
EDCS-05503213.summary: This is a tombstone for Aurelius Macedo, a freedman, set up by his freedman and another individual identified as his parents.

EDCS-05503215.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld: For Felix, freedman of Augustus, from the office of the XX hereditatium (inheritance tax collectors) of Hispania Citerior. From Hilarus, a fellow freedman and registrar of the XX hereditatium of the province of Lusitania.
EDCS-05503215.summary: This funerary dedication is for Felix, an imperial freedman and tax official, from a colleague in a similar position.

EDCS-05503216.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld: For Iulius Pamphilus, aged 13 years and 1 month. From Iulius Secundus, freedman of Augustus, and Iuliana, his mother.
EDCS-05503216.summary: This is a tombstone for Iulius Pamphilus, a child who died at age 13 years and 1 month, dedicated by his mother and another individual.

EDCS-05503217.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld: For Lucius Publicius, of the XX (inheritance tax), of the freedom of the Roman people, treasurer of the province of Hispania Citerior. From Bennia Venustina, his most deserving wife.
EDCS-05503217.summary: This funerary inscription honors Lucius Publicius, a treasurer of Hispania Citerior, dedicated by his wife.

EDCS-05503218.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld: For Victor, treasurer of the XX libertatis (inheritance tax) of the province of Hispania Citerior. From Quintilia Procula, his wife.
EDCS-05503218.summary: This tombstone is for Victor, a treasurer of the inheritance tax in Hispania Citerior, dedicated by his wife.

EDCS-05503219.translation: Gaius Aemilius Fraternos, son of Gaius, of the Galerian tribe, prefect of engineers, tribune of the soldiers of the V Alaudae Legion, flamen of the province of Hispania Citerior, conducted the census here in the province of Gallia Aquitanica and the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503219.summary: This inscription records the census conducted by Gaius Aemilius Fraternos, a high-ranking military and religious official, in Gaul and Hispania Citerior, likely in the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05503220.translation: To Lucius Aemilius Paulus, son of Lucius, priest of the province of Hispania Citerior, from the district of Carthago Nova (Attace), who held all offices in his state, military tribune of the III Cyrenaica legion, prefect of the I cohort in the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503220.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Aemilius Paulus, a Roman official who held various military and civic positions in Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503221.translation: To Aemilia Patera, daughter of Lucius, perpetual priestess of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503221.summary: This inscription honors Aemilia Patera, the perpetual priestess of the province of Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503222.translation: To Lucius Annius Cantabrus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, priest of Rome and of the deified Augusti of the province of Hispania Citerior, who held all offices in Segobriga; by decree of the local council, at public expense, from the people of Segobriga.
EDCS-05503222.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the people of Segobriga to Lucius Annius Cantabrus, a priest and official in Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503223.translation: To Gaius Annius Flavus Iuliobrigensis, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, from the Cantabrian people, in the province of Hispania Citerior, for defending public interests and benefits faithfully and steadfastly.
EDCS-05503223.summary: This inscription honors Gaius Annius Flavus for his faithful service and defense of public interests in Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503225.translation: To Lucius Antonius Saturninus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, twice aedile, duovir, priest of the province of Hispania Citerior; by Lucia Valentina.
EDCS-05503225.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Lucia Valentina to Lucius Antonius Saturninus, a priest and magistrate in Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503226.translation: To Gaius Atilius Crassus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, from Segontia, who held all offices in his state, priest of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503226.summary: This inscription honors Gaius Atilius Crassus, a distinguished citizen and priest from Segontia in Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503227.translation: To Lucius Aufidius Celer Masculinus, son of Masculus, of the Quirina Flaviaugustana tribe, designated priest of the province of Hispania Citerior; by his state, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05503227.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the local council to Lucius Aufidius Celer Masculinus, a designated priest of Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503230.translation: To Lucius Caecilius Caecilianus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, thrice duovir, priest of the deified emperors and of the Augusti of the province of Hispania Citerior; by Quintus Caecilius Campanus, to his most excellent father.
EDCS-05503230.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Quintus Caecilius Campanus to his father, Lucius Caecilius Caecilianus, a prominent priest and magistrate in Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503231.translation: To Lucius Caecilius Maecianus, son of Lucius Caecilius Charito, of the Galeria tribe, an equestrian from Alabanda, from the district of Carthago Nova, who held all offices in his state, priest of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503231.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Caecilius Maecianus, an equestrian and priest from the district of Carthago Nova, who served in Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503232.translation: To Quintus Caecilius Rufinus, son of Quintus Caecilius Valerianus, from Saguntum, for the embassy which he undertook gratuitously at Rome before the great emperor Hadrian Augustus; from the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503232.summary: This inscription honors Quintus Caecilius Rufinus of Saguntum for successfully completing a diplomatic mission to Emperor Hadrian in Rome on behalf of Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503233.translation: To Gaius Calpurnius Flaccus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, priest of the province of Hispania Citerior, curator of the temple, prefect of the walls of the colony of Tarraco, upon the decree of the decurions, Gaius Calpurnius Flaccus accepted the honor and remitted the immense [cost/obligation].
EDCS-05503233.summary: This inscription records the acceptance of an honor and remission of costs by Gaius Calpurnius Flaccus, a local magistrate in Hispania Citerior, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05503234.translation: To Marcus Calpurnius Lupus, priest of the province of Hispania Citerior, from the convention of Caesaraugusta and Ercavica, who held all the magistracies in his state, prefect of the first cohort of the Bituriges in the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503234.summary: This is an honorary inscription for Marcus Calpurnius Lupus, a military officer and local official in Hispania Citerior, dating likely to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05503237.translation: To Gaius Clodius Retus, son of Rectus, of the Aniensis tribe, procurator of the mint, prefect of the Fabri, priest of the province of Hispania Citerior, in the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503237.summary: This inscription honors Gaius Clodius Retus, who held financial and military administrative positions in Hispania Citerior during the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05503238.translation: In the province of Hispania Citerior, to Marcus Cornelius Marcius Severus Vergiliensis, son of Marcius, of the Quirina tribe, who held all the magistracies in his state, priest of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503238.summary: This is an honorary inscription for Marcus Cornelius Marcius Severus Vergiliensis, a local magistrate in Hispania Citerior, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05503239.translation: To Gaius Cornelius Valens of Pompaelo, for the successful and gratuitous performance of his census legation at Sirmium before the Emperor, in the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503239.summary: This inscription commemorates the successful completion of a financial mission by Gaius Cornelius Valens in Hispania Citerior, likely during the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05503240.translation: To Publius Cornelius Verecundus Castulonensis, son of Marcius, of the Galeria tribe, who held all the magistracies in his state, priest of the province of Hispania Citerior, in the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503240.summary: This inscription honors Publius Cornelius Verecundus Castulonensis, a local official in Hispania Citerior, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05503241.translation: To Cornelia Aciliana, mother of Florus Vegetus, priest of the province of Hispania Citerior, wife of Plautius Plautianus, equestrian prefect, upon the decree of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503241.summary: This honorary inscription is dedicated to Cornelia Aciliana, wife of an equestrian prefect and mother of a provincial priest in Hispania Citerior, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05503242.translation: In the province of Hispania Citerior, to Lucius Domitius Dentonensis, son of Marcius, of the Sergia tribe, judge of the decuriae, with the public horse, under Traianus IIvir, perpetual priest, tribune of soldiers of the fourth cohort of Astures of Gallaecia and Mauretania Tingitana, priest of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503242.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Domitius Dentonensis, a military officer and local magistrate in Hispania Citerior, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05503243.translation: To Gaius Egnatuleius Seneca, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, from Tarraco, aedile, quaestor, IIvir, honored with the public horse, prefect of the fourth Thracian cohort equipped with cavalry, priest of the province of Hispania Citerior; to Egnatuleia Sige, his most indulgent patroness.
EDCS-05503243.summary: This inscription honors Gaius Egnatuleius Seneca and his patroness Egnatuleia Sige, detailing his various civic and military roles in Tarraco and Hispania Citerior during the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05503245.translation: To Marcus Fabius Maximus Saguntinus, son of Marcius, of the Galeria tribe, priest of the province of Hispania Citerior, in the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503245.summary: This is an honorary inscription for Marcus Fabius Maximus Saguntinus, a local official in Hispania Citerior, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05503246.translation: To Marcus Flavius Sabinus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, an official, priest of the council of Bracara, and provincial priest of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503246.summary: This inscription honors Marcus Flavius Sabinus, likely a local magistrate and priest, with titles related to the province of Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503247.translation: To Lucius Fonteius Maternum Novatianus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, honored by the council with the rank of aedile, who was a duovir, quaestor, and provincial priest of Hispania Citerior, a judge of a decuria, and possessed the public horse; dedicated by Valeria Diana, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-05503247.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Fonteius Maternum Novatianus, a local official who received honors from the city council, possibly related to his service as aedile and other magistracies in Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503248.translation: To [---]rio, son of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe, Fuscus, duovir, priest of the deified Claudius, prefect of the maritime coast, and priest of the deified emperors of the province of Hispania Citerior. The council of Tarraco, within the province of Hispania Citerior, decreed these honors.
EDCS-05503248.summary: This inscription records honors decreed by the council of Tarraco to Fuscus, a local official who served as duovir and held several priesthoods and prefectures in Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503250.translation: To Lucius Gavius Romanus Tromentinus Secundus, tribune of the III Augusta legion, and provincial priest of Hispania Citerior. Lucius Aemilius Saturninus [dedicated this].
EDCS-05503250.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Gavius Romanus Tromentinus Secundus, a military tribune and provincial priest of Hispania Citerior, possibly by Lucius Aemilius Saturninus.

EDCS-05503251.translation: To Lucius Grattius Glaucus Segobrigensis, son of Caius, of the Galeria tribe, provincial priest of Hispania Citerior. [Dedicated by] Marcus Atilius Fraternus, his best friend.
EDCS-05503251.summary: This inscription is a dedication from Marcus Atilius Fraternus to his friend Lucius Grattius Glaucus, who served as a provincial priest of Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503253.translation: To Caius Julius Pila Segobrigensis, son of Caius, of the Galeria tribe, priest of the deified emperors in Rome and of the province of Hispania Citerior. The province of Hispania Citerior, the council of Tarraco [erected this].
EDCS-05503253.summary: This inscription honors Caius Julius Pila, a priest of the imperial cult and provincial priest of Hispania Citerior, with an erection by the province and the council of Tarraco.

EDCS-05503254.translation: To Lucius Iunius Aemilius Paterno Lanciensis, son of Blandus, of the Quirina tribe, who has held all offices in his state, twice duovir, priest of Rome and of the deified emperors of the council of Astura, admitted to the five decuriae of judges in Rome, and Augustal priest of the province of Hispania Citerior. The province of Hispania Citerior [erected this].
EDCS-05503254.summary: This inscription details the extensive public and religious career of Lucius Iunius Aemilius Paterno, highlighting his roles as duovir, various priesthoods including Augustal priest of Hispania Citerior, and judicial appointments.

EDCS-05503255.translation: To Publius Licinius Laevinus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, who was aedile, quaestor, priest of Rome and of the deified emperors, duovir, and prefect of the new cohort of recruits for the maritime coast. [Dedicated by] Iulia Ingenua, daughter of Quintus, his mother.
EDCS-05503255.summary: This is a dedication by Iulia Ingenua to her son Publius Licinius Laevinus, listing his various magistracies and religious offices, including a prefecture of naval recruits.

EDCS-05503256.translation: To Quintus Licinius Silvanus Granianus, provincial priest of Hispania Citerior, prefect of the maritime coast, and procurator of the emperor. The province of Hispania Citerior [erected this].
EDCS-05503256.summary: This inscription honors Quintus Licinius Silvanus Granianus, who served as a provincial priest of Hispania Citerior, prefect of the maritime coast, and imperial procurator.

EDCS-05503257.translation: To Quintus Licinius Silvanus Granianus, Augustal priest of the province of Hispania Citerior, prefect of the Laetana maritime coast, and procurator of the emperor. Gaius Terentius Philetus of Rome [erected this].
EDCS-05503257.summary: This inscription is dedicated by Gaius Terentius Philetus to Quintus Licinius Silvanus Granianus, recognizing his service as an Augustal priest of Hispania Citerior, prefect of the Laetana coast, and imperial procurator.

EDCS-05503258.translation: To Titus Mamilius Silo, son, of the Quirina tribe, Praesentis, from Tritiens, from Magallensis, who discharged all offices in his state, was appointed decurial, excused from the Italic census, and was flamen of the deified Pius for the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503258.summary: This inscription records the career of Titus Mamilius Silo, a Roman official in Hispania Citerior, honored for his public service and religious role, likely dating to the period after Antoninus Pius.

EDCS-05503259.translation: To Gaius Marius Niger, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, flamen of the deified emperors for the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503259.summary: This inscription honors Gaius Marius Niger, a flamen (priest) of the imperial cult in the province of Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503260.translation: To Gaius Marius Verus, son of Gaius, of the Palatina tribe, from Gerunda, who discharged all offices in his state, and was flamen for the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503260.summary: This inscription commemorates Gaius Marius Verus, who held various civic and religious offices, including flamen, in Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503261.translation: To Gnaeus Numisius Modestus, son of Gnaeus, of the Sergia tribe, from Carthago, who discharged all offices in his state, was elected by the provincial council for gilding the statues of the deified Hadrian, and was flamen for the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503261.summary: This inscription details the distinguished career of Gnaeus Numisius Modestus, including his election to a special artistic commission and his role as provincial flamen in Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503263.translation: To Lucius Numisius Ovinianus, son of Lucius, of the Palatina tribe, from Ovini, from Tarraco, who discharged all offices in his state, served as tribune of the I Macedonian Cohort, and was flamen for the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503263.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Numisius Ovinianus, detailing his military service and his high civic and religious positions in Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503264.translation: Lucius Antonius Modestus Intercatiensis, of the Vaccaei tribe, with the consent of his province of Hispania Citerior, made this at his own expense for his most devoted wife, Paetinia Paternus, daughter of Paternus, of the Amocenses, of the Clunienses, from the Cantabri tribe, priestess of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503264.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Lucius Antonius Modestus for his wife Paetinia Paternus, who served as priestess of the province of Hispania Citerior, likely in the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-05503265.translation: To Gnaeus Pompeius, son of Gnaeus, of the Galeria tribe, from Pompaelo, twice duumvir, flamen for the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503265.summary: This inscription honors Gnaeus Pompeius, a prominent citizen of Hispania Citerior who held both political (duumvir) and religious (flamen) offices.

EDCS-05503266.translation: For the province of Hispania Citerior: To Titus Pomponius Avitus, son of Titus, of the Anienses tribe, flamen of the deified emperors for the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503266.summary: This inscription is dedicated to Titus Pomponius Avitus, who served as flamen of the imperial cult for the province of Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503267.translation: For Pompeia Maxima, from the district of Bracara Augusta, priestess, wife of Ulpius Reburrus, flamen of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503267.summary: This inscription honors Pompeia Maxima, wife of the provincial flamen Ulpius Reburrus of Hispania Citerior, and identifies her as a priestess.

EDCS-05503268.translation: To Quintus Pontius Severus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, from Bracara Augusta, who discharged all offices in his state, and was flamen for the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503268.summary: This inscription honors Quintus Pontius Severus, a local dignitary of Bracara Augusta who also served as the provincial flamen in Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503269.translation: To Marcus Porcius Aper, son of Marcus, of the Aniensis tribe, twice *duovir*, *praefectus fabrum*, military tribune of the VI Ferrata legion, procurator of the Emperor for the alimenta, and priest of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503269.summary: This inscription records the career of Marcus Porcius Aper, listing his various civic and military offices in Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503270.translation: To Marcus Porcius Narbonensis, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, military tribune of the XXII legion, prefect of the Thracian Herculanean cavalry *ala*, prefect of the maritime coast, and priest of the deified Emperors of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503270.summary: This inscription details the career of Marcus Porcius Narbonensis, highlighting his military and religious roles in Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503271.translation: To Quintus Porcius Vetustinus Cantabrus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, from Iuliobriga, prefect of the I Cohort of the Latobici.
EDCS-05503271.summary: This inscription honors Quintus Porcius Vetustinus Cantabrus, recording his command of the I Cohort of the Latobici.

EDCS-05503272.translation: To Porcia Maternus, daughter of Marcus, of the Osicerdensis origin, priestess of the province of Hispania Citerior and later perpetual priestess of Osicerda and Caesaraugusta Tarraconensis, from Lucius Numisius Montanus, her husband.
EDCS-05503272.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Lucius Numisius Montanus to his wife, Porcia Maternus, who served as a priestess in Hispania Citerior and Caesaraugusta.

EDCS-05503273.translation: To Postumia Nepotiana or Marcellina, from the district of Caesaraugusta, of the Karensis origin, priestess and wife of Titus Porcius Verrinus, priest of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503273.summary: This inscription honors Postumia Nepotiana (or Marcellina), wife of Titus Porcius Verrinus, and herself a priestess in Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503275.translation: To Marcus Sempronius Capito, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, from Grallia, admitted to the order of Caesaraugusta, who held all the honors in both public offices, priest of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503275.summary: This inscription records the career of Marcus Sempronius Capito, a distinguished citizen and priest of Hispania Citerior who held various civic honors.

EDCS-05503276.translation: To Gaius Sempronius Fidus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, from Calagurris, military tribune of the IV Scythica legion, military tribune of the VI Ferrata legion, military tribune of the III Gallica legion, military tribune of the XX Valeria Victrix legion, and priest of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503276.summary: This inscription lists the extensive military career of Gaius Sempronius Fidus, culminating in his role as priest of Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503277.translation: To Sempronia Placidus, daughter of Fuscus, of Pompaelo, priestess, with the consent of the council of the province of Hispania Citerior; Caius Cornelius Valens, her husband, made this at his own expense.
EDCS-05503277.summary: This funerary inscription, funded by her husband Caius Cornelius Valens, honors Sempronia Placidus, a priestess of Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503278.translation: To Lucius Sulpicius Niger, son of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe, from Gibba, who held all honors in public office, priest of the deified Emperors of Rome and of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503278.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Sulpicius Niger, detailing his public career and his role as a priest of the imperial cult in Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503279.translation: Gaius Valerius Arabinus, son of Flavius Bergidus, formerly Flavius, who held all honors in public office, priest of Rome and of the Emperors. The entire council decreed that a statue be placed to adorn him among the *flamines*, on account of his faithfully administered care of the census registry.
EDCS-05503279.summary: This inscription records the honor bestowed upon Gaius Valerius Arabinus, a priest in Hispania Citerior, who was voted a statue for his diligent administration of the census.

EDCS-05503280.translation: To Manius Valerius Capellianus, son of Manius, of the Galeria tribe, from Aniés, admitted into the colony of Caesaraugusta through the favor of the deified Hadrian, who performed all public offices in both the republic, and was flamens of the deified Emperors of Rome for the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503280.summary: This inscription records the public career and offices of Manius Valerius Capellianus, who served as a flamens for the deified Emperors in the province of Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503281.translation: For Lucius Valerius Propinquus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, from Dianium, flamens of the deified Emperors of Rome for the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503281.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Valerius Propinquus, who held the office of flamens of the deified Emperors in the province of Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503282.translation: To Marcus Valerius Propinquus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, from Grattius Cerealis of Edeta, flamens of the province of Hispania Citerior, to whom the state granted the honors of his city, admitted into the equestrian order by Titus, Prefect of the Engineers twice, Prefect of the Second Cohort of Astures in Germany, Tribune of the V Macedonian Legion in Moesia, Prefect of the Wing of the Phrygians, and likewise Prefect of the III Thracian Wing in the province of Syria, Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503282.summary: This inscription details the extensive military and public career of Marcus Valerius Propinquus, culminating in his role as flamens of Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503283.translation: For Valeria Fida, daughter of Gaius Valerius Fidus, of Segobriga, from the convent of Carthago, wife of Lucius Caecilius Porcianus, flamens of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503283.summary: This is a dedication to Valeria Fida, wife of a flamens of Hispania Citerior, recording her family and origin.

EDCS-05503284.translation: To Gaius Vibius Latro, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, quaestor, duovir twice, and duovir quinquennial of the colony of Tarraco, flamens of the province of Hispania Citerior, from the will of Fulvia Celeres; the heirs are Fulvius Musaeus and Fulvius Moschus.
EDCS-05503284.summary: This inscription records the offices held by Gaius Vibius Latro, a flamens of Hispania Citerior, and indicates the inscription was set up by his heirs after his death.

EDCS-05503285.translation: To Gaius Vibius Porcianus Quintius Italicus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, from Libisosa, a citizen on horseback granted by the deified Hadrian, who performed all public offices in his republic, flamens of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503285.summary: This inscription honors Gaius Vibius Porcianus Quintius Italicus, a flamens of Hispania Citerior, highlighting his public service and a grant from Hadrian.

EDCS-05503286.translation: To Gaius Virius Frontonus, flamens of the province of Hispania Citerior, from the convent of Lucenses, by decree of the council of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503286.summary: This inscription honors Gaius Virius Frontonus, a flamens of Hispania Citerior, and notes it was erected by decree of the provincial council.

EDCS-05503287.translation: For Viria Flavia, daughter of Gaius Virius Frontonus, flamens of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503287.summary: This is a dedication to Viria Flavia, daughter of Gaius Virius Frontonus, a flamens of Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503288.translation: To Marcus Ulpius Reburrus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, from Bracaraugusta, who performed all public offices in his republic, flamens of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503288.summary: This inscription honors Marcus Ulpius Reburrus, a flamens of Hispania Citerior, detailing his public service and origin.

EDCS-05503289.translation: To Svlconia, tribune of the XXV Cohort of Volunteers, flamens of the deified Emperors of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05503289.summary: This inscription honors Svlconia, a tribune and a flamens of the deified Emperors in the province of Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-05503291.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Antonia Frontonia dedicated this to her very good husband, Aemilius Valerius Chorintus, a good man, who lived 40 years, and who died after holding the aedileship.
EDCS-05503291.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a wife to her deceased husband, aedile.

EDCS-05503292.translation: To Quintus Anthracius, son of Quintus, of the Velina tribe, who was enrolled in the order of Tarraco, twice chief magistrate, and aedile. Didia Amabilis dedicated this to her most excellent husband.
EDCS-05503292.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a wife to her deceased husband, who held civic offices in Tarraco.

EDCS-05503294.translation: Lucius Valerius Latinus of Barcino dedicated this to Lucius Caecina Severus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, twice chief magistrate, quaestor, priest of the imperial cult, prefect of the engineers, prefect of the first cohort, and of the maritime coast.
EDCS-05503294.summary: An inscription honouring Lucius Caecina Severus for his military and civic career, likely set up by a friend or associate.

EDCS-05503295.translation: Claudia Persina, a priestess, made this place, approved by the state, at her own expense.
EDCS-05503295.summary: An inscription recording a public works project by a priestess.

EDCS-05503296.translation: Pompeia Donace, his wife, dedicated this to Lucius Cornelius Celsus, son of Caius, of the Galeria tribe, twice chief magistrate, and prefect of the first and second cohorts and of the maritime coast.
EDCS-05503296.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a wife to her deceased husband, who held military and civic offices.

EDCS-05503297.translation: Aemilia Kara, his wife, dedicated this to Lucius Cornelius Romanus, son of Caius, of the Galeria tribe, priest of the imperial cult, twice chief magistrate of Osicerda, and twice chief magistrate of the colony of Tarraco.
EDCS-05503297.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a wife to her deceased husband, who held high civic and religious offices in two communities.

EDCS-05503298.translation: Publius Fabius Lepidus, son of Publius, of the Sergia tribe, and his son [ Publius Fabius Lepidus], son of Publius, of the Sergia tribe. This was set up by decree of the town councillors of Tarraco, after his death, with additional aedilician ornaments, and the expense remitted, which his mother Iulia Reburrina, daughter of Sextus, provided from her own funds.
EDCS-05503298.summary: A monument honouring Publius Fabius Lepidus, funded by his mother and approved by the town council, acknowledging his posthumous aedilician status.

EDCS-05503299.translation: The people of Ilerda, by decree of their town councillors, dedicated this to Marcus Fabius Paullinus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, who was granted public equestrian status by the Emperor Caesar Hadrianus Augustus, as a citizen of the highest merit, on account of his many generous acts towards their community, in a place obtained from the province.
EDCS-05503299.summary: An honourific inscription by the people of Ilerda for Marcus Fabius Paullinus, recognising his public service and generosity.

EDCS-05503300.translation: To Fulvia Celera, daughter of Marcus, perpetual priestess of Concordia Augusta. Fulvius Diadochus, her freedman, [dedicated this] to his patroness.
EDCS-05503300.summary: A dedication by a freedman to his patroness, who was a perpetual priestess.

EDCS-05503301.translation: To Lucius Furius Faventinus, son of Lucius, of the Suburani. Publicly.
EDCS-05503301.summary: A dedication to Lucius Furius Faventinus, whose public role or benefaction is implied but not specified.

EDCS-05503302.translation: To Marco Granio Probus, decurion and priest, who held the rank of aedilician honours, by his mother Caecilia Galla and his sister Herennia Aphrodite.
EDCS-05503302.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marco Granio Probus, honoring his civic and religious offices, set up by his mother and sister.

EDCS-05503303.translation: To Iunia Materna, daughter of Quintus, by decree of the decurions, from public funds.
EDCS-05503303.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument erected for Iunia Materna at public expense by the town council.

EDCS-05503305.translation: To Lucius Numisius Montanus, son of Lucius, of the Palatina tribe, aedile, quaestor, duovir, also quinquennial duovir, recipient of the public horse award by Emperor Hadrian Augustus, and judge of the first decury, his sister Numisia Victorina ordered this to be set up in the forum by her will.
EDCS-05503305.summary: This funerary inscription honors Lucius Numisius Montanus, detailing his various civic offices and an award from Emperor Hadrian, erected by his sister.

EDCS-05503306.translation: To Popilia Secunda, daughter of Marcus, flaminica of the colony of Tarraco, by Fulvia Celera, her most excellent mother.
EDCS-05503306.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Popilia Secunda, a priestess in the colony of Tarraco, dedicated by her mother.

EDCS-05503309.translation: To Marcus Vocontius, son of Marcus, aedile, flamen of the deified Augustus, quaestor, by his freedman Paganus.
EDCS-05503309.summary: This funerary inscription records Marcus Vocontius, who held several prominent civic and religious positions, commemorated by his freedman.

EDCS-05503317.translation: To the divine shades, to Marcus Aelius Hecataeus, VI vir Augustalis, Marcus Aelius Fabianus made this for his well-deserving father.
EDCS-05503317.summary: This funerary inscription is for Marcus Aelius Hecataeus, a member of the Augustales college, set up by his son.

EDCS-05503318.translation: To the divine shades, to Lucius Atilius Paezontius, VI vir Augustalis, by Atilia Alcyone, for her most beloved brother.
EDCS-05503318.summary: This funerary inscription honors Lucius Atilius Paezontius, a member of the Augustales college, dedicated by his sister.

EDCS-05503319.translation: To the divine shades, to Caecilius Eutyches, sevirus and magistrate, who lived 40 years, 5 months, and 7 days, Vergilia Gemina, his incomparable husband, and Caecilia Quintiana, his daughter, made this for their most devoted and well-deserving father.
EDCS-05503319.summary: This funerary inscription honors Caecilius Eutyches, detailing his age and offices, set up by his wife and daughter.

EDCS-05503320.translation: To the divine shades, farewell. To Caecilia Doris, Lucius Lucretius Martinus, VI vir Augustalis and magistrate of the Lares, for his incomparable wife, with whom I lived without complaint for 28 years; he was hostile to no one here, Caecilia, most dear to me.
EDCS-05503320.summary: This funerary inscription is for Caecilia Doris, dedicated by her husband, Lucius Lucretius Martinus, a member of the Augustales, emphasizing their happy 28-year marriage.

EDCS-05503321.translation: To the divine shades, to Clodius Musaeus, sevirus of Tarraco, Porcia Hieronis, his wife, made this for her most excellent husband.
EDCS-05503321.summary: This funerary inscription honors Clodius Musaeus, a sevirus in Tarraco, dedicated by his wife.

EDCS-05503322.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Cornelius Fuscus, *seviri Augustalis*, Cornelia Fortunata, his freedwoman, to her most excellent and deserving patron, and Seius Eutychus and Claudia Leuce, his heirs.
EDCS-05503322.summary: A tomb inscription for Cornelius Fuscus, a *seviri Augustalis*, set up by his freedwoman and heirs.

EDCS-05503323.translation: To Marcus Fabius Asiaticus, *seviri magister Larum Augustali*, Lucius Pompeius Severinus, to his excellent friend.
EDCS-05503323.summary: An epitaph for Marcus Fabius Asiaticus, *seviri magister Larum Augustali*, set up by his friend.

EDCS-05503324.translation: To Fabia Saturnina, his most excellent wife, by Caius Baebius Myrismus, *seviri Augustalis*.
EDCS-05503324.summary: A tomb inscription for Fabia Saturnina, wife of Caius Baebius Myrismus, a *seviri Augustalis*.

EDCS-05503325.translation: Titus Fisevius Eros, freedman of Titus, *seviri*. Iuventia Prima, freedwoman of mulieris, his wife. Caius Iuventius Quietus, freedman of mulieris. Iuventia Prisca, freedwoman of mulieris.
EDCS-05503325.summary: An inscription listing family members, including a *seviri*, possibly related to a tomb or monument.

EDCS-05503326.translation: Titus Fisevius Pamphilus, freedman of Titus, Titus Fisevius Eros, freedman of Titus. Iuventia Prima, freedwoman of mulieris. Caius Tarquitius Primulus, freedman of mulieris. This monument will not pass to the heir.
EDCS-05503326.summary: An inscription regarding property rights for a monument, set up by Titus Fisevius Pamphilus and Titus Fisevius Eros, likely brothers and freedmen.

EDCS-05503327.translation: To Lucius Flavius Chrysogonus, *seviri magister Larum Augustali*. Lucius Flavius Silvinus, his freedman, *seviri magister Larum Augustalis*, and Quintus Cornelius Silvicus, *seviri*. To their most indulgent patron.
EDCS-05503327.summary: An inscription honoring Lucius Flavius Chrysogonus, a *seviri magister Larum Augustali*, by his freedman and another *seviri*, and Quintus Cornelius Silvicus.

EDCS-05503329.translation: To the spirits of the departed. I, Marcus Herennius Mascellio, *seviri Tarraconensium*, made this memorial for myself while alive, and also for my beloved Herennia Faonicena, her deserving freedwoman and wife, most devoted.
EDCS-05503329.summary: A self-made tomb inscription by Marcus Herennius Mascellio, a *seviri Tarraconensium*, for himself and his wife/freedwoman.

EDCS-05503331.translation: To Lucius Perperna Numisianus, *IIIIII viro Augustali*. Tiberius Claudius Amiantus, to his excellent friend.
EDCS-05503331.summary: An epitaph for Lucius Perperna Numisianus, a *IIIIII viro Augustali*, dedicated by his friend.

EDCS-05503332.translation: To Lucius Perperna Numisianus. Gavius Felicianus, to his excellent friend.
EDCS-05503332.summary: An epitaph for Lucius Perperna Numisianus, dedicated by his friend Gavius Felicianus.

EDCS-05503333.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Porcius Felix, *VI vir Augustalis* and *magister*, made this for Cornelia Victoria, his wife.
EDCS-05503333.summary: A tomb inscription for Cornelia Victoria, wife of Porcius Felix, a *VI vir Augustalis* and *magister*.

EDCS-05503334.translation: To Marcus Raecius Montanus, one of the Augustales seviri and magistrate of the Lares, Caius Raecius Iulianus [erected this] to his most dutiful father.
EDCS-05503334.summary: A funerary dedication by a son to his father, who held local magistracies, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05503335.translation: Marcus Volumnius Primulus, freedman of Marcus, sevir; Marcus Volumnius Modestinus, son of Marcus; Septimiena Modesta, mother; Marcus Volumnius Celadus, freedman of Marcus; Volumnia Callais, freedwoman of Marcus; Marcus Volumnius Domesticus, son.
EDCS-05503335.summary: A tomb inscription listing family members, including freedmen and slaves, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05503336.translation: For the memory of my freedwomen and wife, aged 25. Ambirodacus, magistrate of the Lares of Uxamensis, made [this] while alive for himself, and for his freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants, and for the memory of his former wife who died in his presence.
