EDCS-05503336.summary: A self-funded tomb inscription for the dedicator, his freedmen, and his deceased wives, likely from the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-05503339.summary: A fragmented inscription indicating a freedman with a possible former role, lacking specific context or date.

EDCS-05503340.translation: To the Divine Shades, Gavidius Vitalis to his incomparably religious father Gavidius Primulus, priest of Caelestis, well-deserving.
EDCS-05503340.summary: A funerary dedication by a son to his father, a priest of the goddess Caelestis, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05503341.translation: To the Divine Shades, his most excellent wife Catcilia LII and Iunia to her husband Flavius Fulvianus, haruspex, [erected this].
EDCS-05503341.summary: A funerary dedication by a wife to her husband, a haruspex (diviner), likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05503342.translation: Marcus, son of Semp, aedile of Apollo.
EDCS-05503342.summary: An inscription recording an official (aedile) associated with the cult of Apollo, possibly from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05503343.translation: To the Divine Shades. Tiberius Claudius Apollinaris, freedman and heir of Tiberius Claudius Onitus, most learned in the art of medicine, is buried here. His wife Iulia Rhodine placed this monument to her well-deserving husband, and also to Claudia Iuliane Potentia, to her dutiful father.
EDCS-05503343.summary: A funerary inscription for a highly skilled physician, erected by his wife and daughter, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05503344.translation: To the Divine Shades. Flavius Rufinus and Sempronia Diofanis made [this] for their well-deserving charioteer Eutychetes, aged 22. The bones of the charioteer rest in this tomb, who, although young, was not ignorant of how to control the reins, daring to mount the four-horse chariot, and yet was not removed from the two-horse teams. My horses envied me. Cruel fate snatched me away, fate against which one cannot oppose one's hands. Nor was the glory of the circus granted to me, who was to die. May the pious crowd bestow tears upon me. Burning illnesses consumed my insides, which medical hands could not conquer. Sprinkle flowers upon my grave, traveler, perhaps you yourself favored me when I was alive.
EDCS-05503344.summary: A tombstone for a young charioteer, lamenting his early death from illness and celebrating his skill, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05503345.translation: To the Divine Shades. We, the undersigned, certain enthusiasts and fond admirers of the Green faction, have dedicated this altar to Fuscus. So that all may know this monument and pledge of love, you deserved praise for your untarnished reputation and your racing. You competed with many, feared no envy because you were poor, always endured bravely, lived nobly, and met a mortal fate. Whoever you are, traveler, if you remember or know who this man was, stop and read. All will say that Fortune is to be feared, yet Fuscus alone has titles [to fame]. Death has its tomb; a stone covers his bones. It is well, Fortune, you will be strong. We have poured out tears for the innocent; now we pray with wine that you may lie peacefully, and that no one may be like you. Your struggles will be proclaimed by eternity.
EDCS-05503345.summary: An epitaph for a charioteer named Fuscus of the Green faction, commemorating his skill, character, and popularity, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05503346.translation: Quintus Murrius Thales gave a sundial as a gift to the guild of craftsmen.
EDCS-05503346.summary: This is a dedication of a sundial to a craftsmen's guild by Quintus Murrius Thales.

EDCS-05503347.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Ovilius Successus made this for his most dutiful son, Quintus Ovilio Venustianus, a merchant.
EDCS-05503347.summary: This tombstone was erected by a father for his son, a merchant, in honor of his devotion.

EDCS-05503349.summary: This very brief inscription identifies Fulvia as a linen seller.

EDCS-05503350.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. Lucius Aemilius Euhodus set this up for Lucius Aemilius Hippolytus, his fellow freedman and educator, who deserved well, who lived for 97 years without suffering, and who was Greek by birth.
EDCS-05503350.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Lucius Aemilius Hippolytus, a Greek freedman and educator, by his fellow freedman and benefactor, Lucius Aemilius Euhodus.

EDCS-05503351.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. I, Aufidia Prima, from Cirta, am placed here, aged 34. Florentinus, her husband, set this up.
EDCS-05503351.summary: This tombstone records the death of Aufidia Prima from Cirta at age 34, erected by her husband Florentinus.

EDCS-05503352.translation: To the departed spirits. Caius Granius Sabinus Cascanto, while alive, made this for Grania Vitalis, his freedwoman, and for himself and his family.
EDCS-05503352.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Caius Granius Sabinus Cascanto for his freedwoman Grania Vitalis and himself and his household.

EDCS-05503353.translation: Tiberius Iulius Maternus, son of Tiberius, of the Fabia tribe, from Rome, aged 28, a centurion of the Legio VII Gemina.
EDCS-05503353.summary: This inscription identifies Tiberius Iulius Maternus, a 28-year-old centurion from Rome serving in the Legio VII Gemina.

EDCS-05503354.translation: Quartus, son of Satepillus, from the Voltinia tribe.
EDCS-05503354.summary: This inscription records the name and tribal affiliation of Quartus, son of Satepillus.

EDCS-05503356.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Oppius Maximus set this up for his son Lucius Sulpicius Maxentius, a homeborn slave from Tarraco, and for his wife Paedania Crescentina, who deserved well.
EDCS-05503356.summary: This epitaph was erected by Lucius Oppius Maximus for his son Lucius Sulpicius Maxentius, a servant from Tarraco, and his wife Paedania Crescentina.

EDCS-05503358.translation: To the departed spirits. To Castellana, the servant, who deserved well.
EDCS-05503358.summary: This funerary inscription honors a servant named Castellana.

EDCS-05503359.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Aemilius Peregrinus had this made for himself while alive.
EDCS-05503359.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Lucius Aemilius Peregrinus for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-05503360.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aemilia Prisca, recording her age at death.

EDCS-05503362.translation: To the Divine Spirits. The most unfortunate mother made this for her most dutiful and dearest son, Annio Leonas, who lived twenty-seven years, five months, and fifteen days.
EDCS-05503362.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a mother for her son, Annio Leonas, specifying his lifespan and her grief.

EDCS-05503363.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Publius Rufius Flavus made this for his wife Antonia Clementina and for himself while alive, and in perpetual memory, he bequeathed the adjoining gardens or suburban property to the freedmen and freedwomen from his wife's household, Marullus, Antroclus, and Tertullina, and stipulated that no one should sell them but that possession should pass through their descendants, or through agnates or freedmen.
EDCS-05503363.summary: This is a funerary epitaph and bequest by Publius Rufius Flavus for his wife Antonia Clementina and himself, detailing the inheritance of his property to freed slaves.

EDCS-05503364.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aemilius Donatus, husband, for his most rare wife Antonia Valentina.
EDCS-05503364.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a husband, Aemilius Donatus, for his wife Antonia Valentina.

EDCS-05503365.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Apuleia Antisforis made this for Publius Apuleius Crescentis, who deserved well.
EDCS-05503365.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Apuleia Antisforis for Publius Apuleius Crescentis, acknowledging his good deeds.

EDCS-05503366.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Atilius Calventianus arranged for this to be made for his most holy and deserving wife Pompeia Verana.
EDCS-05503366.summary: This is a funerary epitaph arranged by Atilius Calventianus for his wife Pompeia Verana, emphasizing her virtues.

EDCS-05503367.translation: For Lucius Rufidius, son of Lucius, of the Galerian tribe.
EDCS-05503367.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Rufidius, identifying his father and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-05503368.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Fabia Eucarpia set this up for her excellent husband Marcus Aurelius Nicephorus, with whom she lived for 26 years, for his good merit.
EDCS-05503368.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Fabia Eucarpia for her husband Marcus Aurelius Nicephorus, noting their duration of marriage and his worth.

EDCS-05503371.translation: For Baebia Quieta, daughter of Marcus. Maridia Quietilla, mother.
EDCS-05503371.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Baebia Quieta, set up by her mother Maridia Quietilla.

EDCS-05503372.translation: Brutius Demetrius made this for Herennius Primitivus, well deserving.
EDCS-05503372.summary: This is a dedication by Brutius Demetrius to Herennius Primitivus, likely a funerary monument, possibly from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-05503373.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Brutius Aper placed this for his freedman Bruttius Policarpo, well deserving.
EDCS-05503373.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Brutius Aper for his freedman Bruttius Policarpo, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-05503374.translation: To Quintus Caecilius Seranus, son of Quintus, of the Velina tribe.
EDCS-05503374.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Caecilius Seranus, recording his name and tribal affiliation, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-05503375.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Pyrrhacius placed this for his wife Caecilia Gemella, who lived 21 years, an incomparable wife.
EDCS-05503375.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Pyrrhacius for his wife Caecilia Gemella, recording her age at death, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-05503376.translation: To Marcus Aemilius Valerianus for Gaius Camilius Paterno, his best and most faithful friend.
EDCS-05503376.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Marcus Aemilius Valerianus for his friend Gaius Camilius Paterno, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-05503377.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Camilius Saturnalis made this for his most deserving patroness Camilia Natula.
EDCS-05503377.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Camilius Saturnalis for his patroness Camilia Natula, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-05503378.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Hermione, freedwoman and heir of Cassius Chrysampelus, arranged for this to be made according to the will.
EDCS-05503378.summary: This is a funerary epitaph arranged by Hermione, the freedwoman and heir of Cassius Chrysampelus, in accordance with his will, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-05503379.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ceionius Marcus and Iunia Eutychia, most unhappy parents, and Ceionia Apricla, sister, made this for their most devoted brother Lucius Ceionius Licinus, incomparable son, who lived 25 years, 10 months, 15 days, well deserving.
EDCS-05503379.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by the parents and sister for Lucius Ceionius Licinus, recording his age at death and their grief, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-05503380.translation: Charmosyne, slave of Marcus Titius Flavius, is buried here. Herma, fellow slave, and Charmosynus, brother, placed this.
EDCS-05503380.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Charmosyne, a slave, placed by her fellow slave Herma and brother Charmosynus, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-05503381.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Clearchus is buried here. Clearchus, who while alive deserved this name, renowned by a great Greek name, and was called by these letters, had a happy life in infancy. Grandson of Hostius.
EDCS-05503381.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Clearchus, celebrating his noble Greek name and good life despite an early death, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-05503382.translation: To Clodia Phyllis, his dearest and most obedient pupil, Clodius Fuscus [dedicated this].
EDCS-05503382.summary: This is a tomb inscription dedicated by Clodius Fuscus to his pupil Clodia Phyllis.

EDCS-05503383.translation: To Clodia Rufina, daughter of Quintus. Her most dutiful grandmother Iulia Helice and her mother Rufina [dedicated this].
EDCS-05503383.summary: This is a tomb inscription dedicated by the grandmother and mother of Clodia Rufina.

EDCS-05503384.translation: To the divine spirits of Claudius Philodespotus, for his most virtuous wife, Clodia Saturnina.
EDCS-05503384.summary: This is a tomb inscription dedicated by Claudius Philodespotus to his wife, Clodia Saturnina.

EDCS-05503385.translation: To Claudius Tertullina, his dearest wife, who deserved well of him, he made [this].
EDCS-05503385.summary: This is a tomb inscription dedicated by an unnamed individual to his wife, Clodia Tertullina.

EDCS-05503386.translation: To Marcus Cornelius Avitus, son of Marcus, from the Galerian tribe. Avita, daughter of Lucius, his mother, [dedicated this].
EDCS-05503386.summary: This is a tomb inscription dedicated by Avita to her son, Marcus Cornelius Avitus.

EDCS-05503387.translation: To Avita, daughter of Lucius. Cornelia Avita, daughter of Lucius, her mother, [dedicated this].
EDCS-05503387.summary: This is a tomb inscription dedicated by Cornelia Avita to her daughter, Avita.

EDCS-05503388.translation: To the divine spirits of Domitius Cato, for his wife Misia.
EDCS-05503388.summary: This is a tomb inscription dedicated by Misia to her husband, Domitius Cato.

EDCS-05503389.translation: To the divine spirits of Domitia Gemellina, for his wife who deserved well of him, Caecilius Priscianus [dedicated this].
EDCS-05503389.summary: This is a tomb inscription dedicated by Caecilius Priscianus to his wife, Domitia Gemellina.

EDCS-05503390.translation: To Domitia Quartilla, dearest daughter, from her parents Marcus Iulius Paris and Domitia Musa, and from her dearest son Lucius Domitius Theseus to his mother.
EDCS-05503390.summary: This is a tomb inscription dedicated by the parents and son to Domitia Quartilla.

EDCS-05503391.translation: To Sextus Erucius Athenodorus, who deserved well of him.
EDCS-05503391.summary: This is a tomb inscription dedicated to Sextus Erucius Athenodorus.

EDCS-05503392.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Fabia Chrysis, his wife, made this for Publius Fabius Ianuarius and for her son Chryserotus, aged 20.
EDCS-05503392.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to Publius Fabius Ianuarius by his wife.

EDCS-05503393.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Fabia Maurula, her mother, Fabius Parilis, her brother, and Lupus, her husband, made this for their most dutiful and well-deserving wife, who lived 34 years and 8 months.
EDCS-05503393.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to Fabia Maria by her mother, brother, and husband.

EDCS-05503394.translation: Lucius Faltonius, freedman of Lucius, for Paullae Fulviae, twice.
EDCS-05503394.summary: An inscription indicating ownership or dedication by Lucius Faltonius for Paullae Fulviae.

EDCS-05503395.summary: An inscription identifying Faustus as the slave of Crassus Frugi.

EDCS-05503396.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Firmidonius lies here; his daughter Firmidonia arranged this burial.
EDCS-05503396.summary: A funerary epitaph stating that Firmidonia arranged the burial for Firmidonius.

EDCS-05503397.translation: Manius Flavius Gemellus, son of Manius, aged 27, lies here. Statius Servilius Nymphodotus and Flavia Successa, Gemellus's mother.
EDCS-05503397.summary: A funerary epitaph for Manius Flavius Gemellus, mentioning his mother and Statius Servilius Nymphodotus.

EDCS-05503398.translation: Sextus Flavius Plutus, freedman of Sextus, is alive.
EDCS-05503398.summary: An inscription indicating that Sextus Flavius Plutus, a freedman, is alive.

EDCS-05503399.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Hostilius Campester made this for Flavia Alciste, his most excellent and loving wife.
EDCS-05503399.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to Flavia Alciste by her husband, Hostilius Campester.

EDCS-05503400.translation: Flavius Eugramus made this for Flavia Trophimes, his wife, during his lifetime, and for himself.
EDCS-05503400.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Flavius Eugramus for his wife Flavia Trophimes and himself.

EDCS-05503401.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Calarius made this for Fullonia Matrona, daughter of Lucius, who deserved well.
EDCS-05503401.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to Fullonia Matrona by Lucius Calarius.

EDCS-05503402.translation: Seleucus, freedman of Gnaeus Lucretius, son of Lucius, of the Scaptia tribe, arranged for this to be made at his own expense.
EDCS-05503402.summary: This is a dedication made by a freedman for his patron, Gnaeus Lucretius.

EDCS-05503403.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Salvius Bathillus, patron, placed this altar for his deserving freedwoman Helpidi, who lived for 30 years.
EDCS-05503403.summary: This is a funerary altar set up by a patron for his freedwoman.

EDCS-05503404.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Herennia Iucunda, daughter, who lived 6 years, 2 months, and 16 days. Her mother Manilia [placed this].
EDCS-05503404.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl, set up by her mother.

EDCS-05503405.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Hermeti, aged 23, and Harmonico, aged 18, brothers, his dearest slaves. Caius Iulius Merops [dedicated this].
EDCS-05503405.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a master for his two deceased slaves.

EDCS-05503406.translation: Hostilius, freedman of a woman; Hostilia, freedwoman of a woman; Hymnis.
EDCS-05503406.summary: This inscription lists three individuals, likely freedpersons and possibly a relative, associated with an unnamed woman.

EDCS-05503407.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Caius Iulius Aemilianus, his best friend.
EDCS-05503407.summary: This is a funerary dedication to a friend.

EDCS-05503408.translation: For Caius Iulius Fabianus, aged 19. His aunt Fabia Paula provided the last rite.
EDCS-05503408.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young man, set up by his aunt.

EDCS-05503409.translation: For Caius Iulius Lutatius Florus. Statilia Pulchra, his most loving and dutiful wife, [dedicated this].
EDCS-05503409.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a wife for her husband.

EDCS-05503410.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Caius Iulius Olympianus. Terentia Lucentina and her freedwoman Terentia Valentinaes, for herself and her husband, remain and renew the name; may it always be well for you.
EDCS-05503410.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a wife and her freedwoman for the husband, with a wish for remembrance and well-being.

EDCS-05503411.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Iulia Quintiana, [her] dearest daughter. An unhappy mother [dedicated this] for herself [and her daughter].
EDCS-05503411.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a grieving mother for her daughter and possibly herself.

EDCS-05503412.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Iulia Tertullina, daughter of Titus.
EDCS-05503412.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Iulia Tertullina, daughter of Titus.

EDCS-05503413.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Iunius Constantius. Aurelia Afrodite made this for her well-deserving husband, with whom she lived for 19 years and 6 months.
EDCS-05503413.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Aurelia Afrodite to her husband Iunius Constantius, noting their 19 years and 6 months of marriage.

EDCS-05503414.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Marcus Iuventilius Licinianus, who grew by 9 months, dearest son. Primus and Liciniana, his most unhappy parents.
EDCS-05503414.summary: This is a funerary dedication by the parents Primus and Liciniana to their son Marcus Iuventilius Licinianus, who died at 9 months of age.

EDCS-05503415.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Publius Lenius Fortunatus, my friend, who lived 27 years and 25 days. Aelius Faustinus made this from his own resources for the deserving one.
EDCS-05503415.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Aelius Faustinus to his friend Publius Lenius Fortunatus, noting his age at death and that it was self-funded.

EDCS-05503416.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Titus Licinius Severus. Didia Stratonice, mother, for her most dutiful son.
EDCS-05503416.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Didia Stratonice, mother, to her son Titus Licinius Severus.

EDCS-05503417.translation: Lucia, daughter of Porcius, lies here.
EDCS-05503417.summary: This is a simple tombstone marking the burial place of Lucia, daughter of Porcius.

EDCS-05503418.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Lucretia Atiliana. Atilia Auctilla, mother, for her most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-05503418.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Atilia Auctilla, mother, to her daughter Lucretia Atiliana.

EDCS-05503419.summary: This is a dedication, likely funerary, for Marcus Mamililius Atianus.

EDCS-05503420.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Manlia Quinte. Manlia Severa, patroness.
EDCS-05503420.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Manlia Severa, patroness, to Manlia Quinte.

EDCS-05503421.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Masclia Augusta. Masclia Glauce, freedwoman and heir, made this for her well-deserving patroness and for herself.
EDCS-05503421.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Masclia Glauce to her patroness Masclia Augusta, and also includes a space for the dedicator.

EDCS-05503422.summary: This is a simple funerary dedication to Lucius Minucius Philargurus.

EDCS-05503423.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, for Lucius Numisius Symphorus, a worthy patron, and for Caecilia Musa, his wife.
EDCS-05503423.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Numisia Philumene to her patron Lucius Numisius Symphorus and his wife Caecilia Musa.

EDCS-05503424.translation: To his excellent and most beloved wife, Perpernia Eunoidis, from her husband, Porcius Longinus.
EDCS-05503424.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Porcius Longinus to his wife Perpernia Eunoidis.

EDCS-05503425.summary: This inscription likely records a dedication or memorial by Lucius Severus for someone named Placidus.

EDCS-05503426.translation: To Lucius Pomponius Avitus, from Sempronia, daughter of Marcus, Materna, his wife, to her incomparable husband.
EDCS-05503426.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Sempronia Materna to her husband Lucius Pomponius Avitus.

EDCS-05503427.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, for Porcia Eucheria, aged 20, from Flavius Maternus, to his most beloved wife.
EDCS-05503427.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Flavius Maternus to his wife Porcia Eucheria, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-05503428.translation: To Mummius Marinus, for his most dutiful and indulgent mother, Procula.
EDCS-05503428.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Mummius Marinus to his mother Procula.

EDCS-05503429.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, for Aulus Postumius Agrestis, aged 30.
EDCS-05503429.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Aulus Postumius Agrestis, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-05503430.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, for Primitiva, his most dutiful wife, who lived 42 years and 5 months; her husband Ianuarius made this for her as a deserving woman.
EDCS-05503430.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Ianuarius to his wife Primitiva, who lived 42 years and 5 months.

EDCS-05503431.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, for Numerius Publilius Theopompus, from Publilia Nympherusa, his heir, to her excellent husband and for herself.
EDCS-05503431.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Publilia Nympherusa to her husband Numerius Publilius Theopompus and for herself.

EDCS-05503432.translation: To Raecia Irene, from Gaius Caecilius Augustalis, her most obedient wife.
EDCS-05503432.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Gaius Caecilius Augustalis to his wife, Raecia Irene.

EDCS-05503433.translation: Rubenia Sura, daughter of Aulus, is buried here.
EDCS-05503433.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Rubenia Sura, daughter of Aulus.

EDCS-05503434.translation: To Sempronia Materna, daughter of Marcus, his best, dearest, and most holy mother, from Gaius Caecilius Iubatus and Sempronius Maternus, her son, a woman of incomparable devotion and chastity.
EDCS-05503434.summary: This funerary inscription honors Sempronia Materna, dedicated by her son Gaius Caecilius Iubatus and Sempronius Maternus.

EDCS-05503435.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Sextilius Sulon, from Atiliana, his wife.
EDCS-05503435.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Sextilius Sulon by his wife, Atiliana.

EDCS-05503436.translation: To Sutoria Surilla, her most honorable and best patroness, from Sutorius Epitynchanus, who deserved well of her.
EDCS-05503436.summary: This inscription honors Sutoria Surilla, a patroness, dedicated by Sutorius Epitynchanus.

EDCS-05503437.translation: To the Divine Manes. Trocina Sirvando lived 25 years, 2 months, 25 days. Trocinia Afrodisia, her most unhappy mother, placed this in memory of her.
EDCS-05503437.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and death of Trocina Sirvando, dedicated by her mother.

EDCS-05503438.translation: To the Divine Manes. Caius Valerius Augustinus, aged 30 years. Atilia Quieta made this for her most indulgent husband.
EDCS-05503438.summary: This funerary inscription is for Caius Valerius Augustinus, dedicated by his wife Atilia Quieta.

EDCS-05503439.translation: To the Divine Manes. Sulpicia Ursa made this for Marcus Valerius Callistus and Licinia Satulla, who deserved well of her.
EDCS-05503439.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Sulpicia Ursa to Marcus Valerius Callistus and Licinia Satulla.

EDCS-05503440.translation: Aulus Valerius Iucundus [dedicated this] to Domitius Meliton, [and] Numerius.
EDCS-05503440.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication of a monument by Aulus Valerius Iucundus to Domitius Meliton and Numerius.

EDCS-05503441.translation: To Valeria Flavia, her dearest and most devoted mother, from Valerius Rogatus, her son, and Clodius Felicio.
EDCS-05503441.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Valeria Flavia by her son Valerius Rogatus and Clodius Felicio.

EDCS-05503443.translation: To the memory of Valeria Modestina, who lived 24 years, 3 months, and 27 days. Caecilius Polychronius and Aurelia Primitiva [made this] for their dearest daughter.
EDCS-05503443.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Modestina, set up by her parents, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503444.translation: To the departed spirits. For Varius Hippodamantes, whom lived 27 years, 5 months, and 7 days, Varius Ianuarius and Varia Saturnina made [this] for their most dutiful son, who deserved well.
EDCS-05503444.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Varius Hippodamantes, set up by his parents, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503445.translation: To the departed spirits. For Varia Iucunda, who died in the 18th year of her life, in the 7th month, on the 28th day. Varius Pollio and Varia Iucunda, her most unfortunate parents, made [this] for her, who deserved well.
EDCS-05503445.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Varia Iucunda, set up by her parents, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503446.translation: To Venus Latinilla. Spedius Maternianus, her husband.
EDCS-05503446.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Venus Latinilla, made by her husband, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503447.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Victor, father, who lived 90 years. Victrix, his daughter, made [this] for him, who deserved well.
EDCS-05503447.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Victor, set up by his daughter, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503448.translation: To the departed spirits. Titus Vinicius Sogalus is buried here. Vinicia Euploea [made this] for her well-deserving freedman.
EDCS-05503448.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Vinicius Sogalus, set up by his patroness, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503449.translation: To the memory of Ulpius Bonicius, son-in-law, and Octavia Calliste, dearest daughter. Octavia Graecula, mother.
EDCS-05503449.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ulpius Bonicius and Octavia Calliste, set up by their mother-in-law/mother, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503450.translation: To the departed spirits. Hail! For Volusia Paterna, most devoted wife. Terentius Primus, her husband.
EDCS-05503450.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Volusia Paterna, set up by her husband, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503451.translation: The freedman Urbanus, [belonging to] Maria Urbana, her freedwoman Aucta, Titus Quinctius, the freedman of the woman.
EDCS-05503451.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the status and relationships of several freedpersons, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503453.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary epitaph, recording a patronymic, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05503460.translation: To the most devoted veteran of the legion, 57 years, 4 months. If the spirits knew, they would take me, his miserable wife, away. I no longer wish to see the light of day, I have lost you, my sweet light. I, your wife, pour these tears for you, my sweetest husband. If tears do any good, show yourself to me. This house will always be yours eternally. Farewell, my dearest husband.
EDCS-05503460.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph for a legionary veteran, composed by his grieving wife.

EDCS-05503462.translation: On the Ides of December, to Aglaretus, by the will of Aelianus.
EDCS-05503462.summary: This is a dedication to a deity named Aglaretus, made by Aelianus.

EDCS-05503463.summary: This inscription is a dedication to a mother named Rusticus.

EDCS-05503464.translation: He lived 42 years, 2 months, [1?] day. Aemilia Lupa, his wife, and Melphone, his daughter, made this for their deserving father.
EDCS-05503464.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a man, likely deceased around the late 1st or early 2nd century CE, set up by his wife and daughter.

EDCS-05503468.summary: This inscription likely records a dedication or memorial for Flaminia, possibly by L. Famius.

EDCS-05503472.translation: Quintus Fadius, son of Publius, of the Voting Tribe Quirina.
EDCS-05503472.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a name and tribal affiliation, possibly for a citizen.

EDCS-05503475.translation: Licinius Calidromus willingly paid his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for my health.
EDCS-05503475.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Licinius Calidromus to Jupiter for his own well-being.

EDCS-05503481.translation: To Gaius Aufidius Victorinus, from Fabia Lyde, his most sincere and incomparable friend.
EDCS-05503481.summary: This inscription is a dedication from Fabia Lyde to her friend Gaius Aufidius Victorinus.

EDCS-05503482.translation: To Marcus Horatius Mercurialis, aged 58. Fabricia Serana, to her most indulgent husband.
EDCS-05503482.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Horatius Mercurialis, dedicated by his wife Fabricia Serana.

EDCS-05503486.translation: To Marcus Cornelius Faventinus. Cornelia Faventina, to her brother.
EDCS-05503486.summary: This inscription is a dedication from Cornelia Faventina to her brother, Marcus Cornelius Faventinus.

EDCS-05503487.translation: To Marcus Cornelius Phaedimus, a sevirus; Cornelia Faventina, for her brother.
EDCS-05503487.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Marcus Cornelius Phaedimus, identified as a sevirus, with Cornelia Faventina as the dedicator for her brother. It is likely a funerary or honorary epitaph.

EDCS-05503488.translation: To Marcus Fabius Auctus; Fabia Lyde, for her dearest brother.
EDCS-05503488.summary: This inscription is a dedication from Fabia Lyde to her brother, Marcus Fabius Auctus, described as 'dearest'. It is likely a funerary epitaph.

EDCS-05503490.translation: To Atilia Lucilla, daughter of Spurio; Marcus Antonius Inachus, for his wife.
EDCS-05503490.summary: This inscription is a dedication from Marcus Antonius Inachus to his wife, Atilia Lucilla, daughter of Spurio. It is likely a funerary epitaph.

EDCS-05503504.translation: Cornelius Vitellus is laid here; he lived [number] years.
EDCS-05503504.summary: This inscription records the burial of Cornelius Vitellus and the duration of his life, likely from a tombstone.

EDCS-05503549.translation: To Lucius Minicius Natalis Quadronius Verus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, consul, augur, proconsul of the province of Africa, legate of the deified Hadrian Augustus with praetorian power in the province of Lower Moesia, curator of public works and sacred buildings, curator of the Flaminian Way, prefect of the alimenta, legate of the VI Victrix Legion in Britain with Augustan authority, praetor, candidate for tribune of the plebs, candidate for quaestor, and at the same time legate of the province of Africa in the district of Carthage of his father, the proconsul, tribune of the soldiers of the I Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Legion, and of the XI Claudia Pia Fidelis Legion, and of the XIIII Gemina Martia Victrix Legion, moneyer triumvir for striking bronze, silver, and gold; Lucius Sempronius Carpio, his client, has granted this place by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05503549.summary: This lengthy honorary inscription records the extensive career of Lucius Minicius Natalis Quadronius Verus, detailing his various high-ranking official positions and military commands, with his client Lucius Sempronius Carpio dedicating the space. The inscription dates to the 2nd century CE based on the consulships and military appointments.

EDCS-05503551.translation: Because of the distinguished signs of his well-managed proconsulship, all of Asia granted a statue to Nummius Aemilianus Dexter, a most illustrious man, by principal benefit.
EDCS-05503551.summary: This inscription records that the province of Asia erected a statue to honor Nummius Aemilianus Dexter for his successful proconsulship, likely dating to the late Roman Empire.

EDCS-05503557.translation: To the departed spirits. Claudius Felicissimus Afer made this for Claudius Saturninus, who deserved well, as a gift of fate from stone.
EDCS-05503557.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Claudius Felicissimus Afer to Claudius Saturninus, likely a family member or friend, indicating it is a tombstone.

EDCS-05503558.translation: To the departed spirits. Freedmen of our Emperors, scribes of the Gallician and Spanish schools.
EDCS-05503558.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the departed spirits by freedmen who served as scribes for imperial administration, possibly related to a funerary context or a record of service.

EDCS-05503571.translation: To Maximus the aedile. APV [likely an abbreviation for a collegium or association].
EDCS-05503571.summary: This is a brief dedication to Maximus, who held the office of aedile, possibly indicating a public work or a collegial dedication from an association (APV).

EDCS-05503605.translation: Fabia Domitia, daughter of a woman, Secunda.
EDCS-05503605.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph identifying Fabia Domitia, also known as Secunda, daughter of a woman (indicating potentially an unusual or freedwoman parentage).

EDCS-05503632.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Umidius Agathonicus to his well-deserving father, Umidius Abrocomas.
EDCS-05503632.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from the Roman world, dedicated by a son to his father.

EDCS-05503650.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: For Caius Picarius, son of Caius. The council of the people of Baetulo gave him a burial place at public expense for his funeral and all honors. Caius Picarius will not be succeeded by an heir, nor will the burial place be [so succeeded].
EDCS-05503650.summary: This inscription records a burial plot granted by the local council of Baetulo to Caius Picarius, with specific conditions regarding inheritance.

EDCS-05503662.translation: To Democritus, son of Sostratos. Paulla Aemilia made this at her own expense.
EDCS-05503662.summary: This is a Greek inscription recording a dedication made by Paulla Aemilia, likely for a public building or monument, funded by her.

EDCS-05503665.translation: Porcia Severa, daughter of Marcus, from Gerunda, repaired [this], 6 units of gold, 9 of silver.
EDCS-05503665.summary: This inscription records a repair made by Porcia Severa from Gerunda, possibly to a public building or monument, detailing the cost in gold and silver.

EDCS-05600053.translation: Emperor Caesar Hadrianus Augustus, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 5th time, consul for the 3rd time, restored [this] 149 [years after the event].
EDCS-05600053.summary: This inscription records Emperor Hadrian's restoration of a structure, dating to his 5th tribunate and 3rd consulship.

EDCS-05600103.translation: To Emperor Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 18th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, 23 miles from Bracara Augusta.
EDCS-05600103.summary: This is a milestone inscription indicating the distance of 23 miles from Bracara Augusta, dedicated to Emperor Hadrian during his 18th tribunate and 3rd consulship.

EDCS-05600104.translation: To Emperor Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power, consul for the 3rd time, Imperator for the 5th time, 8 miles from Bracara Augusta.
EDCS-05600104.summary: This milestone inscription indicates the distance of 8 miles from Bracara Augusta, dedicated to Emperor Hadrian during his 3rd consulship.

EDCS-05600105.translation: To Emperor Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 18th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, 12 miles from Bracara.
EDCS-05600105.summary: This milestone inscription indicates the distance of 12 miles from Bracara, dedicated to Emperor Hadrian during his 18th tribunate and 3rd consulship.

EDCS-05600107.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus Germanicus Maximus, son of the deified Severus, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 17th time, Imperator for the 3rd time, consul for the 4th time, father of the fatherland, 14 miles from Bracara Augusta.
EDCS-05600107.summary: This milestone inscription indicates the distance of 14 miles from Bracara Augusta, dedicated to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus during his 17th tribunate and 4th consulship.

EDCS-05600109.translation: To our lord Emperor Caesar Flavius Iunior Constantinus Victor and Triumpher Augustus, grandson of the deified Valerius Maximianus, great-grandson of the deified Claudius, 21 miles from Bracara.
EDCS-05600109.summary: This milestone inscription indicates the distance of 21 miles from Bracara, dedicated to Emperor Constantine the Great during his reign.

EDCS-05600111.translation: To our lord Magnentius the Emperor, the Pious, Fortunate, born for the good of our republic, thirty-one [years old?].
EDCS-05600111.summary: This is a titulature of the emperor Magnentius, likely part of an honorific inscription, dating to the 4th century AD.

EDCS-05600112.translation: Son of Magnus, grandson of Theodosius.
EDCS-05600112.summary: This is a personal titulature, likely from a funerary or honorific inscription, dating to the Late Roman period.

EDCS-05600113.translation: To Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 18th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, [a milestone showing the distance] from Bracara Augusta to Asturica, 215 Roman miles.
EDCS-05600113.summary: This is a milestone inscription indicating distances from Bracara Augusta (modern Braga, Portugal) to Asturica (modern Astorga, Spain), likely dating to Hadrian's reign (117-138 AD).

EDCS-05600114.translation: To Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 19th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, [a milestone showing the distance] from Bracara Augusta to Calem, 35 Roman miles.
EDCS-05600114.summary: This is a milestone inscription indicating distances from Bracara Augusta to Calem (likely modern Valença, Portugal), likely dating to Hadrian's reign (117-138 AD).

EDCS-05600115.translation: Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of the deified Augustus, grandson of the deified Julius, chief priest, emperor for the 8th time, consul for the 5th time, with tribunician power for the 24th time, [a milestone showing the distance] from Bracara Augusta, 4 Roman miles.
EDCS-05600115.summary: This is a milestone inscription with the titulature of Emperor Tiberius, dating his reign (14-37 AD).

EDCS-05600116.translation: Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, chief priest, emperor for the 5th time, consul for the 3rd time, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, [a milestone showing the distance] from Bracara Augusta, 4 Roman miles.
EDCS-05600116.summary: This is a milestone inscription with the titulature of Emperor Claudius, dating his reign (41-54 AD).

EDCS-05600118.translation: To Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 18th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, [a milestone showing the distance] from Bracara Augusta, 13 Roman miles.
EDCS-05600118.summary: This is a milestone inscription indicating the distance from Bracara Augusta, likely dating to Hadrian's reign (117-138 AD).

EDCS-05600119.translation: To Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Severus Pius, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Germanicus Maximus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 17th time, emperor for the 3rd time, consul for the 4th time, father of the fatherland, proconsul.
EDCS-05600119.summary: This is the full imperial titulature of Emperor Caracalla, dating his reign (198-217 AD).

EDCS-05600120.translation: To Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Severus Pius, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-05600120.summary: This is a shortened imperial titulature of Emperor Caracalla, dating his reign (198-217 AD).

EDCS-05600121.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Severus Pius, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus.
EDCS-05600121.summary: This is a partial imperial titulature, likely of Emperor Caracalla, dating his reign (198-217 AD).

EDCS-05600122.translation: Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, conqueror of the Germans, the Dacians, the Sarmatians, chief priest, in his fifth year of tribunician power, seventh acclamation as emperor, father of the country, consul, proconsul, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, conqueror of the Germans, the Dacians, the Sarmatians, prince of the youth, son of our lord Emperor Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, rebuilt roads and bridges which had collapsed through age, under the supervision of Quintus Decius, legate of the emperors, proconsul, one mile from Bracara Augusta.
EDCS-05600122.summary: This inscription records the rebuilding of roads and bridges by Emperors Maximinus Thrax and his son Caesar Maximus, likely during the 310s AD, under the supervision of the legate Quintus Decius.

EDCS-05600123.translation: Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, conqueror of the Germans, the Dacians, the Sarmatians, chief priest, in his fifth year of tribunician power, seventh acclamation as emperor, father of the country, consul, proconsul, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, conqueror of the Germans, the Dacians, the Sarmatians, prince of the youth, son of our lord Emperor Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-05600123.summary: This inscription lists the titles of Emperors Maximinus Thrax and his son Caesar Maximus, likely from the 310s AD.

EDCS-05600124.translation: Chief priest, in his fifth year of tribunician power, seventh acclamation as emperor, father of the country, consul, proconsul, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, conqueror of the Germans, the Dacians, the Sarmatians.
EDCS-05600124.summary: This inscription lists titles associated with Emperors Maximinus Thrax and his son Caesar Maximus, possibly from an earlier or less complete monument, dating to the 310s AD.

EDCS-05600126.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, chief priest, father of the country, consul, proconsul.
EDCS-05600126.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Carus, likely erected during his reign (282-283 AD).

EDCS-05600127.translation: To the Emperor and most noble Caesar, prince of the youth, Marcus Aurelius Carinus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, chief priest, consul, proconsul, seven miles from Bracara.
EDCS-05600127.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Caesar Carinus, son of Carus, likely erected during their joint rule (282-284 AD).

EDCS-05600128.translation: To our lords Valentinian and Valens.
EDCS-05600128.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperors Valentinian I and Valens, likely erected during their co-rule (364-375 AD).

EDCS-05600129.translation: To our lord Flavius Valerius Constantius, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-05600129.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Caesar Constantius II, likely from the period when he was co-emperor (337-361 AD).

EDCS-05600130.translation: To our lord Flavius Julius Crispus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-05600130.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Caesar Flavius Julius Crispus, likely dating to his time as Caesar under Constantine I (c. 317-326 AD).

EDCS-05600131.translation: To Victor and triumphant, always Augustus, the Greatest, Magnentius, victor by land and sea, 16.
EDCS-05600131.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the usurper Magnentius, proclaiming his victories, likely during his revolt (350-353 AD).

EDCS-05600133.translation: To Emperor Caesar, grandson of the deified Septimius Severus, son of the deified Antoninus Pius the Great, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, in his second year of tribunician power, consul II, proconsul, father of the country, the most brave and most fortunate prince, one mile.
EDCS-05600133.summary: This inscription records titles of Emperor Elagabalus (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus), likely from the early part of his reign (c. 218-222 AD), indicating a road marker.

EDCS-05600137.translation: Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, chief priest, emperor V, consul III, with the tribunician power for the III time, father of the fatherland, Bracara Augusta XXXV.
EDCS-05600137.summary: This is an inscription recording the titles of Emperor Claudius, likely on a monument in or dedicated to Bracara Augusta, dated to his 3rd tribunician power and consulship, around 44-45 AD.

EDCS-05600144.translation: Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of the deified Augustus, grandson of the deified Julius, chief priest, emperor VIII, consul V, with the tribunician power for the XXXIIII time, Bracara Augusta LIX.
EDCS-05600144.summary: This inscription lists the titles of Emperor Tiberius, likely on a monument in or dedicated to Bracara Augusta, dated to his 32nd year of tribunician power, around 32 AD.

EDCS-05600145.translation: Emperor Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, chief priest, with the tribunician power for the XX time, repaired the roads at Aquae Flaviae for II miles.
EDCS-05600145.summary: This inscription records Emperor Hadrian's repair of roads near Aquae Flaviae, dated to his 20th year of tribunician power, around 136 AD.

EDCS-05600146.translation: Emperor Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, chief priest, with the tribunician power for the XX time, repaired the roads at Aquae Flaviae for V miles.
EDCS-05600146.summary: This inscription records Emperor Hadrian's repair of roads near Aquae Flaviae, dated to his 20th year of tribunician power, around 136 AD.

EDCS-05600147.translation: Emperor Caesar Traianus, son of the deified Nerva, Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, chief priest, with the tribunician power for the VII time, emperor IV, consul V, father of the fatherland, at Aquae Flaviae for V miles.
EDCS-05600147.summary: This inscription lists the titles of Emperor Trajan, likely on a monument related to roads near Aquae Flaviae, dated to his 7th tribunician power and 5th consulship, around 107 AD.

EDCS-05600149.translation: Emperor Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, chief priest, with the tribunician power for the XX time, repaired the roads at Aquae Flaviae for XLIII miles.
EDCS-05600149.summary: This inscription records Emperor Hadrian's repair of roads near Aquae Flaviae, dated to his 20th year of tribunician power, around 136 AD.

EDCS-05600150.translation: To our lord Constantinus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-05600150.summary: This is an inscription dedicating something to Constantine, a noble Caesar, likely from the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-05600151.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carus, the Invincible, P C, chief priest for the X time, with the tribunician power, VG, father of the fatherland for XV.
EDCS-05600151.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Carus, listing his titles and likely dating to his 10th period as chief priest and 15th year as father of the fatherland, late 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05600152.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, chief priest.
EDCS-05600152.summary: This is an inscription recording the titles of Emperor Carus, likely from the late 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05600161.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carinus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, with the tribunician power, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-05600161.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Carinus, listing his titles, likely from the late 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05600167.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Severus Pius, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Germanicus Maximus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 17th time, imperator for the 3rd time, consul for the 4th time, father of the country, proconsul, [from] Bracara, 17 miles.
EDCS-05600167.summary: This inscription records a milestone, marking 17 miles from Bracara, dedicated to Emperor Caracalla.

EDCS-05600170.translation: To Antoninus, Parthicus, Aurelius Antoninus, Sarmaticus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, imperator for the 3rd time, consul for the 4th time, proconsul, [from] Bracara, 19 miles.
EDCS-05600170.summary: This inscription records a milestone, marking 19 miles from Bracara, dedicated to Emperor Caracalla, with some abbreviations and damage.

EDCS-05600171.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, grandson of the deified Septimius Severus, son of the deified Antoninus Pius Magnus, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 2nd time, consul, proconsul, father of the country, the most brave and fortunate prince, [from] Bracara Augusta, 21 miles.
EDCS-05600171.summary: This inscription records a milestone, marking 21 miles from Bracara Augusta, dedicated to Emperor Elagabalus.

EDCS-05600173.translation: To our Lords Valerius Licinianus Licinius.
EDCS-05600173.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperors Licinius I and Licinius II.

EDCS-05600176.translation: To our Lord Valerius Licinianus Licinius Iunior, 26 miles.
EDCS-05600176.summary: This inscription records a milestone, marking 26 miles, dedicated to the junior emperor Licinius.

EDCS-05600181.translation: To the Emperor Caesar [---] from the aedes.
EDCS-05600181.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription possibly related to an imperial dedication or decree, with illegible parts.

EDCS-05600182.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus Germanicus Maximus, Dacicus Maximus, Sarmaticus Maximus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 5th time, father of the country, consul, proconsul, and to Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, Germanicus Maximus, Sarmaticus Maximus, princeps iuventutis, son of our lord the Emperor Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, under the administration of Quintus Decius Valerianus, legate of the Augusti, [from] Bracara Augusta, 29 miles.
EDCS-05600182.summary: This inscription records a milestone, marking 29 miles from Bracara Augusta, dedicated to Emperor Maximinus Thrax and his son Caesar Maximus, overseen by the legate Decius Valerianus.

EDCS-05600183.translation: To the deified Maximian, [from] Bracara Augusta, 29 miles.
EDCS-05600183.summary: This inscription records a milestone, marking 29 miles from Bracara Augusta, dedicated to the deified Emperor Maximian.

EDCS-05600185.translation: To our Lord Galerius Valerius Maximianus, unconquered, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-05600185.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Caesar Galerius Maximian.

EDCS-05600188.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Caro and Carinus Augustus, always most noble Caesars.
EDCS-05600188.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperors Carus and his son Carinus.

EDCS-05600189.translation: To Emperor Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Traianus Decius Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, consul for the second time, father of the fatherland, from Bracara Augusta, 31 miles.
EDCS-05600189.summary: This inscription marks a road distance of 31 miles from Bracara Augusta, dedicated to Emperor Decius.

EDCS-05600191.translation: To Emperor Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power for the 18th time, consul for the third time, father of the fatherland, from Bracara, 32 miles.
EDCS-05600191.summary: This inscription marks a road distance of 32 miles from Bracara, dedicated to Emperor Hadrian.

EDCS-05600192.translation: Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Augustus Germanicus maximus Dacicus maximus Sarmaticus maximus, chief priest, imperator for the sixth time, father of the fatherland, consul, proconsul, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, Germanicus maximus, Sarmaticus maximus, prince of the youth, son of our lord Emperor Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, restored the roads and bridges collapsed by time, under the supervision of Quintus Decius, legate of the Augusti with praetorian authority, from Bracara Augusta, 32 miles.
EDCS-05600192.summary: This inscription records the restoration of roads and bridges near Bracara Augusta, commissioned by Emperors Maximinus and his son Maximus, under the supervision of legate Quintus Decius.

EDCS-05600193.translation: To our lord Magnus Decentius, most noble, most flourishing Caesar, born for the good of the state, 32 miles.
EDCS-05600193.summary: This inscription marks a road distance of 32 miles, dedicated to Caesar Magnus Decentius.

EDCS-05600194.translation: To Valerius, clarissimus vir, legate of the Augusti with proconsular authority.
EDCS-05600194.summary: This inscription honors Valerius, a distinguished legate of the emperors.

EDCS-05600195.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carinus Pius Augustus, holder of tribunician power.
EDCS-05600195.summary: This inscription is dedicated to Emperor Carinus.

EDCS-05600196.translation: To Emperor Caesar Claudius Tacitus, the Unconquered Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, consul, father of the fatherland, proconsul, from Magno, 33 miles.
EDCS-05600196.summary: This inscription marks a road distance of 33 miles from Magno, dedicated to Emperor Tacitus.

EDCS-05600197.translation: Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, under the supervision of Quintus Decius Valerius.
EDCS-05600197.summary: This inscription, possibly a milestone, mentions Emperor Maximinus under the supervision of Quintus Decius Valerius.

EDCS-05600198.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carinus Pius Felix Invictus, prince of the youth, proconsul.
EDCS-05600198.summary: This inscription is dedicated to Emperor Carinus in his capacity as prince of the youth and proconsul.

EDCS-05600199.translation: To Emperor Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Traianus Decius Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, proconsul, consul for the third time, from Bracara Augusta, 33 miles.
EDCS-05600199.summary: This inscription marks a road distance of 33 miles from Bracara Augusta, dedicated to Emperor Decius.

EDCS-05600200.translation: To Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest conqueror of Germans, Dacians, and Sarmatians, chief priest, with the V power of a tribune, emperor for the VII time, father of the fatherland, consul, proconsul, and to Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, greatest conqueror of Germans, Dacians, and Sarmatians, prince of the youth, son of our lord Emperor Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus; carried out by Quintus Decius Valerianus, legate of the emperors, proconsul, most distinguished man.
EDCS-05600200.summary: An inscription honoring Emperors Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus Caesar, likely erected by their legate Decius Valerianus during their reign.

EDCS-05600203.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus, son of the deified Severus Pius, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Traianus Parthicus and the deified Nerva, chief priest, with the XVII power of a tribune, emperor for the III time, consul for the IIII time, father of the fatherland, proconsul, XXXIIII miles from Bracara Augusta.
EDCS-05600203.summary: An inscription honoring Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla), likely indicating a road distance from Bracara Augusta.

EDCS-05600208.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius III Felix Augustus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus Germanicus Maximus, son of the deified Severus Pius, grandson of the deified Antoninus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Traianus Parthicus and the deified Nerva, chief priest, with the XVII power of a tribune, emperor for the III time, consul for the IIII time, father of the fatherland, proconsul, XXXV miles.
EDCS-05600208.summary: An inscription honoring Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla), likely indicating a road distance, possibly related to military logistics or infrastructure.

EDCS-05600209.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus Germanicus Maximus, son of the deified Severus Pius, grandson of the deified Antoninus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Traianus, great-great-great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva, chief priest, with the XVII power of a tribune, emperor for the III time, consul for the IIII time, father of the fatherland, proconsul, XXXVI miles.
EDCS-05600209.summary: An inscription honoring Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla), likely indicating a road distance, similar to other inscriptions of this period.

EDCS-05600210.translation: To Emperor, our lord Flavius Constantius, Victorious and triumphant Augustus, son of the deified Constantine Maximus, grandson of the deified Flavius Constantius and Valerius Maximianus, great-grandson of the deified Claudius, XXXVII miles from Bracara.
EDCS-05600210.summary: An inscription honoring Emperor Constantius II, likely indicating a road distance from Bracara Augusta, commissioned during his reign.

EDCS-05600216.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus Germanicus Maximus, son of the deified Severus Pius, grandson of the deified Antoninus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Traianus Parthicus and the deified Nerva, chief priest, with the XVII power of a tribune, emperor for the III time, consul for the IIII time, father of the fatherland, proconsul, XXXVIII miles from Bracara Augusta.
EDCS-05600216.summary: An inscription honoring Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla), indicating a road distance from Bracara Augusta, likely for administrative or military purposes.

EDCS-05600220.translation: Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Augustus, greatest conqueror of Germans, Dacians, and Sarmatians, chief priest, with the VI power of a tribune, emperor for the VII time, father of the fatherland, consul, proconsul, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, greatest conqueror of Germans, Dacians, and Sarmatians, prince of the youth, son of our lord Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, ordered that roads and bridges, collapsed by age, be restored in those times; the work was overseen by Quintus Decius, legate of the emperors, most distinguished man.
EDCS-05600220.summary: An imperial rescript from Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus Caesar, preserved by their legate Decius, ordering the repair of roads and bridges.

EDCS-05600221.translation: To Emperor Nerva Caesar Augustus, chief priest, with the power of a tribune, father of the fatherland, consul for the III time.
EDCS-05600221.summary: An inscription honoring Emperor Nerva, likely a dedication or milestone marking his consulship and tribunician power.

EDCS-05600228.translation: To Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, chief priest, with the XVII power of a tribune, consul for the III time, father of the fatherland, LXXIIII miles from Bracara Augusta.
EDCS-05600228.summary: An inscription honoring Emperor Hadrian, likely indicating a road distance from Bracara Augusta, possibly related to imperial administration or travel.

EDCS-05600236.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of a god, Augustus, chief priest, emperor for the XV time, consul for the XIII time, with the XXXIV power of a tribune, father of the fatherland, IIII miles from Bracara.
EDCS-05600236.summary: An inscription honoring an unnamed emperor, likely marking a road distance from Bracara, with titles indicating a significant period of rule.

EDCS-05600238.translation: Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest conqueror of the Germans, greatest conqueror of the Dacians, greatest conqueror of the Sarmatians, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 5th time, emperor for the 7th time, father of his country, consul, proconsul, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, greatest conqueror of the Germans, greatest conqueror of the Dacians, greatest conqueror of the Sarmatians, prince of the youth, son of our lord Emperor Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, restored the roads and bridges collapsed by the decay of time; overseen by Quintus Decius, legate of the Augusti, propraetor, 18 miles from Bracara.
EDCS-05600238.summary: This inscription records the restoration of roads and bridges by Emperors Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus, overseen by their legate Quintus Decius, with a distance marker of 18 miles from Bracara Augusta, likely from the reign of Maximinus Thrax (235-238 AD).

EDCS-05600239.translation: To Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 18th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of his country, 20 miles from Bracara Augusta.
EDCS-05600239.summary: This is a milestone dedicating a section of road 20 miles from Bracara Augusta to Emperor Hadrian, dating to his 18th year of tribunician power (134 AD).

EDCS-05600240.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Germanicus Maximus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 17th time, emperor for the 4th time, consul for the 4th time, father of his country, proconsul, son of the deified Severus Pius, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva, 20 miles from Bracara Augusta.
EDCS-05600240.summary: This milestone honors Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla) and marks a distance of 20 miles from Bracara Augusta, dating to his 17th year of tribunician power (214 AD).

EDCS-05600241.translation: To the most victorious Emperor Constantius Magnus, the triumphant, always Augustus.
EDCS-05600241.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Constantius II, hailing him as victorious and triumphant, likely from the mid-4th century AD.

EDCS-05600243.translation: Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, chief priest, emperor for the 5th time, consul for the 3rd time, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, father of his country, 42 [miles] from Bracara.
EDCS-05600243.summary: This milestone indicates a distance of 42 miles from Bracara, honoring Emperor Claudius, likely from his 3rd tribunician power year (43 AD).

EDCS-05600279.translation: By order of our lord and prince, Magnus Maximus, the most victorious, always Augustus, I, Antonius Maximinus, first consular governor of the new province of Mauretania Caesariensis and Tingitana, and previously its president, have leveled the road, famous for its cliffs, which was being inundated by river waters, by flattening the road and diverting the river, with the land pacified and subdued.
EDCS-05600279.summary: This inscription records the work of Antonius Maximinus, governor of Mauretania, in repairing a road threatened by river flooding, under the authority of Magnus Maximus (likely the usurper Magnus Maximus, late 4th century AD).

EDCS-05600292.translation: Quintus Fabius, son of Quintus, Labeo, proconsul, 92 [miles].
EDCS-05600292.summary: This is a milestone indicating 92 miles from a starting point, attributed to Quintus Fabius Labeo in his proconsular capacity, likely from the late Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-05600293.translation: Quintus Fabius, son of Quintus, Labeo, proconsul, 94 [miles].
EDCS-05600293.summary: This is a milestone indicating 94 miles from a starting point, attributed to Quintus Fabius Labeo in his proconsular capacity, likely from the late Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-05600323.translation: Manius Sergius, son of Manius, proconsul, 21 [miles].
EDCS-05600323.summary: This is a milestone indicating 21 miles from a starting point, attributed to Manius Sergius in his proconsular capacity, likely from the late Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-05600331.translation: To Julia Helias, Caius Julius Faustus [erected this] for his deserving wife. She lived 24 years.
EDCS-05600331.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Julia Helias, erected by her husband Caius Julius Faustus, recording her lifespan of 24 years, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05600333.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Publicia Fortunata lived 19 years and 2 months. Pious in her own [household], she is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600333.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publicia Fortunata, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600335.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Ianuaria, 6 months old. May she be placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600335.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ianuaria, an infant, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600336.summary: An inscription recording a freedman of Marcus, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600337.translation: To Pedania Eutychia, Lucius Pedanius [dedicated this].
EDCS-05600337.summary: A dedication to Pedania Eutychia by Lucius Pedanius, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600397.translation: Frontonianus and Marica, children of Fronton and Rufi, gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-05600397.summary: A votive inscription by Frontonianus and Marica, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600398.translation: To Docio, son of Savigius, 60 years old.
EDCS-05600398.summary: An inscription recording the age of Docio, son of Savigius, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600399.translation: Coso Vegetianus Fuscus fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05600399.summary: A votive inscription by Coso Vegetianus Fuscus, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600400.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Iulianus and Rufina [dedicate this] to their most beloved and most missed mother Valeria, and to Postumus, their husband [and father]. [He lived] X years.
EDCS-05600400.summary: A funerary dedication by Iulianus and Rufina to their mother Valeria and their husband/father Postumus, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600401.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Silvana, aged 4 years and months. May the earth lie lightly upon her.
EDCS-05600401.summary: A funerary epitaph for Silvana, a child, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600402.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Postumiae Priscilla, daughter of Publius, most excellent, most dutiful, most sweet, who lived 25 years, 11 months, 20 days, and 8 hours. Postumius Priscus, her most unhappy father, had this made.
EDCS-05600402.summary: A funerary epitaph for Postumiae Priscilla, commissioned by her father Postumius Priscus, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05600403.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Postumia Sotira, his most excellent, most devout, and dearest wife, he placed [this].
EDCS-05600403.summary: A funerary dedication to a wife by her husband.

EDCS-05600404.translation: Gaius Pelgus Clemens, son of Lucius, of the Scaptia tribe, a veteran of the tenth legion Gemina, lived 56 years, is buried here. Gaius Pelgus Primus, his freedman, [erected this] by the will.
EDCS-05600404.summary: A funerary inscription for a veteran of the Tenth Legion Gemina, erected by his freedman.

EDCS-05600406.translation: Lucius Valerius Auctus, freedman of Lucius, a bird-watcher, Blaesus, 56 years old. May the earth lie lightly upon you, Felicio, my brother.
EDCS-05600406.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Valerius Auctus, mentioning his occupation and his brother.

EDCS-05600410.translation: To the Genius of the Seventh Legion [Gemina Felix?]. Lucius Attius Macro, imperial legate.
EDCS-05600410.summary: An inscription dedicating something to the Genius of the Seventh Legion by its legate, Lucius Attius Macro.

EDCS-05600412.translation: To Aemilia Antonia Placidia, his mother. Aemilia Materna.
EDCS-05600412.summary: A dedication to a mother by her daughter.

EDCS-05600413.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Flaccus, his most dutiful and most reverent grandson, most missed by me.
EDCS-05600413.summary: A funerary dedication to a grandson by his grandfather.

EDCS-05600415.translation: Flavianus paid his vow to Minerva, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-05600415.summary: A votive inscription to the goddess Minerva by Flavianus.

EDCS-05600416.translation: Quintus Manlius Bassus, son of Quintus, from Egelesta, 50 years old.
EDCS-05600416.summary: A tombstone recording the name, parentage, origin, and age of Quintus Manlius Bassus.

EDCS-05600417.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Primi, 4 years old, dutiful to his family, is buried here. Crescentianus and Prima, his guardians. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600417.summary: A funerary inscription for a young child, Primi, erected by his guardians.

EDCS-05600445.translation: To Gaius Julius Fidus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, from Asturica, priest of Rome and designated flamen of the Emperors of the province of Further Spain.
EDCS-05600445.summary: An inscription honoring Gaius Julius Fidus, a priest and flamen, likely erected posthumously or commemorating his achievements.

EDCS-05600450.translation: To his most dutiful sons, C. Alpinus Severus Albinius Severus, father.
EDCS-05600450.summary: This inscription is a dedication by a father to his sons.

EDCS-05600451.translation: Tiberius Claudius Rufus, for Jupiter Optimus Maximus, gladly paid his vow for 120 pounds of gold that was found.
EDCS-05600451.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Tiberius Claudius Rufus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the discovery of gold.

EDCS-05600729.translation: To his preserver, the people of Ilurcis, under the care of Lucius Aufidius Longus and Lucius Baebius Rusticianus.
EDCS-05600729.summary: This inscription is a dedication to a 'preserver' by the people of Ilurcis, supervised by two individuals.

EDCS-05600730.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Procilius Valerius Valerianus is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600730.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Lucius Procilius Valerius Valerianus.

EDCS-05600739.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. His most dutiful wife, Maxumina, saw to it that this was made for Flavius Albinus.
EDCS-05600739.summary: This is a tombstone inscription commissioned by Maxumina for her husband, Flavius Albinus.

EDCS-05600740.translation: To Iulia Avita, wife of Nigrus Sempronius.
EDCS-05600740.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Iulia Avita.

EDCS-05600742.translation: Trites Mebdius is buried here. Taurocutius, son of Apoltes, saw to it that this was made with his own money. Aucalus Hospites, Arcius, and Urtinus placed it.
EDCS-05600742.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of Trites Mebdii, commissioned by Taurocutius Apoltes, and placed by Aucalus Hospites, Arcius, and Urtinus.

EDCS-05600743.translation: Abricus Flavus, son of Apilius Valerius, of Abrica, gladly paid his vow as deserved.
EDCS-05600743.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Abricus Flavus.

EDCS-05600744.translation: Paternus Flavius placed the altar, as vowed, with a willing heart.
EDCS-05600744.summary: This inscription records the placement of an altar by Paternus Flavius in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-05600745.translation: Celea Clouti, to the god Durbedicus, with a willing heart, as vowed.
EDCS-05600745.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Celea Clouti to the god Durbedicus.

EDCS-05600751.translation: Urbanus dedicated this in fulfillment of a vow to the Nymphs for Crysede.
EDCS-05600751.summary: This is a dedication made by Urbanus to the Nymphs, likely on a shrine or altar, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-05600752.translation: Sacred to the Deities of the Underworld. Flavia Amena, aged 61.
EDCS-05600752.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Flavia Amena, recording her age and dedicating the tomb to the Deities of the Underworld.

EDCS-05600753.translation: Avitus, the slave of Gracilis, is buried here.
EDCS-05600753.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Avitus, a slave of Gracilis, indicating his burial place.

EDCS-05600754.translation: Adauc... [made] this to be done by his father. He is buried here.
EDCS-05600754.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, potentially for Adauc, stating that his father arranged for the monument and that he is buried there.

EDCS-05600755.translation: Sacred to the Deities of the Underworld. F. A. M. A., aged 92.
EDCS-05600755.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, likely for a woman with the initials F. A. M. A., recording her age and dedicating the tomb to the Deities of the Underworld.

EDCS-05600756.translation: Sacred to the Deities of the Underworld. Safa Ama, aged 75.
EDCS-05600756.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Safa Ama, recording her age and dedicating the tomb to the Deities of the Underworld.

EDCS-05600757.translation: Melia Toceta, aged 25, [dedicated this to] her mother C. T.
EDCS-05600757.summary: This is a dedication, likely funerary or votive, made by Melia Toceta to her mother C. T.

EDCS-05600758.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sunua Casabi, daughter of Casabus, recording her age.

EDCS-05600759.translation: [...] NN L. [Someone] arranged for this to be done by [their] father.
EDCS-05600759.summary: This inscription records that a father arranged for a monument to be erected, possibly for a child whose name is lost.

EDCS-05600760.translation: Sacred to the Deities of the Underworld. On[---], aged 20. Assu[---].
EDCS-05600760.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, likely for someone named On[---] and Assu[---], recording their ages and dedicating the tomb to the Deities of the Underworld.

EDCS-05600761.translation: Pissirus, son of Mebdus, aged 15, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. His father had this made.
EDCS-05600761.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young boy named Pissirus, stating his age and that his father commissioned the monument.

EDCS-05600763.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Pompeius Flavinus [set this up] for his sister, aged 20.
EDCS-05600763.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to a young woman, Pompeius Flavinus' sister, by her brother.

EDCS-05600771.translation: To Camalus, from Domitius. For Caberus, son of Camalus.
EDCS-05600771.summary: An inscription likely related to property ownership or a dedication, mentioning individuals named Camalus, Domitius, and Caberus.

EDCS-05600775.translation: To Coruberus, son of Caberus, from Medamus. From the family of Camalus.
EDCS-05600775.summary: An inscription mentioning individuals with Gaulish-sounding names, possibly related to family or property ownership within the Camalus group.

EDCS-05600776.translation: The House of Coronerus, son of Camalus.
EDCS-05600776.summary: An inscription likely marking a property, indicating ownership by Coronerus, son of Camalus.
