EDCS-05600797.translation: Munidus Fiduene willingly placed this altar here.
EDCS-05600797.summary: A dedication of an altar by Munidus Fiduene, likely to a deity.

EDCS-05600798.translation: Nemedecus consecrated this temple to the god Cosus.
EDCS-05600798.summary: A dedication of a temple by Nemedecus to the god Cosus.

EDCS-05600801.translation: Lucius Hispanius Fronto consecrated this sacred offering to Mars Cairiogibus, fulfilling a vow.
EDCS-05600801.summary: A votive dedication of a sacred offering to the god Mars by Lucius Hispanius Fronto.

EDCS-05600803.translation: Cananius Montanus and Fabia Marcella had this made according to the will of their most devoted master, Titus Cananius Marcellianus, aged 17.
EDCS-05600803.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by slaves or freedmen for their deceased master, Titus Cananius Marcellianus, who died at 17.

EDCS-05600804.translation: Marcus Aq Leda Peregrinor placed this according to a vow for Tutela.
EDCS-05600804.summary: A votive dedication by Marcus Aq Leda Peregrinor to the goddess Tutela.

EDCS-05600805.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Arro Clouti, dated by his age.

EDCS-05600807.translation: Iulia Praenia Mocion willingly and deservedly paid her vow to the altar of Sol.
EDCS-05600807.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Sol by Iulia Praenia Mocion.

EDCS-05600808.translation: Tiberius Lagius willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Nabiae.
EDCS-05600808.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Nabiae by Tiberius Lagius.

EDCS-05600809.translation: Cicero Manci willingly paid his vow to Nabiae.
EDCS-05600809.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Nabiae by Cicero Manci.

EDCS-05600811.translation: Acloviana arranged for the Nymphs of Silonis to be made according to the vow.
EDCS-05600811.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow concerning the Nymphs of Silonis.

EDCS-05600812.translation: Cambavius, son of Coralius, from Sena Foroiriensis, aged 50, is buried here; may the earth rest lightly on you.
EDCS-05600812.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Cambavius, providing his age and origin.

EDCS-05600813.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Iulia Valentina placed this for her devoted husband, Cornelio Chresimo, aged about 50.
EDCS-05600813.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Cornelio Chresimo, placed by his wife Iulia Valentina.

EDCS-05600814.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. A mother for her most devoted son, Naeviolo, aged 14, not willingly.
EDCS-05600814.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Naeviolo, placed by his grieving mother.

EDCS-05600815.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Secundianus, son of Secundus, aged 58, is buried here. Flaccinius Secundus [erected this?].
EDCS-05600815.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Secundianus, noting his age and possibly erected by Flaccinius Secundus.

EDCS-05600816.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Urbanilla, aged 25.
EDCS-05600816.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Urbanilla, providing her age.

EDCS-05600817.translation: Dentonius Verecundus gladly paid his vow to the Lares of the roadside.
EDCS-05600817.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the Lares of the roadside, made by Dentonius Verecundus.

EDCS-05600819.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Placidus, aged 50.
EDCS-05600819.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Placidus, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-05600820.translation: Placidus, son of Senecae, from Interamna.
EDCS-05600820.summary: This inscription likely identifies Placidus, son of Senecae, from Interamna, possibly as a tombstone or a marker.

EDCS-05600821.translation: Ursus and Faustinus set up this altar to Diana the Huntress by the instruction of Victor, for their own well-being.
EDCS-05600821.summary: This inscription records a votive altar dedicated to Diana Venatrix by Ursus and Faustinus, based on Victor's instructions, for their health.

EDCS-05600824.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Aurelianus, aged 36.
EDCS-05600824.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Aurelianus, who died at the age of 36.

EDCS-05600826.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Flavius Flavus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-05600826.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Flavius Flavus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-05600827.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valerius [dedicated this] to his dearest wife, Aemilia Homullina, aged 30.
EDCS-05600827.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aemilia Homullina, aged 30, dedicated by her husband Valerius.

EDCS-05600828.translation: Balaesina, daughter of Rufi, Severa, from Camp, aged 22, lies here.
EDCS-05600828.summary: This inscription records the burial of Balaesina Severa, daughter of Rufi, aged 22.

EDCS-05600829.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Publilia Florina, aged 8, lies here.
EDCS-05600829.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publilia Florina, who died at the age of 8.

EDCS-05600830.translation: Lucius Publilius Florus [dedicated this] to his wife Severiana.
EDCS-05600830.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Lucius Publilius Florus to his wife, Severiana, likely a funerary monument.

EDCS-05600831.translation: To Materna, daughter of Capito of Asturia. Lucius Lucretius [paid] the vow.
EDCS-05600831.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication by Lucius Lucretius to Materna, daughter of Capito of Asturia.

EDCS-05600832.translation: To the Divine Manes of Proculeius Gracilis, aged 55. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05600832.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Proculeius Gracilis, aged 55, with a standard 'sit tibi terra levis' wish.

EDCS-05600833.summary: This inscription is a dedication or memorial to Maecius Cornelius.

EDCS-05600834.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Bloena, aged 60.

EDCS-05600835.translation: To Magilo, son of Cilus, aged 25.
EDCS-05600835.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Magilo, son of Cilus, aged 25.

EDCS-05600838.translation: To Silvius Silvanus, aged 25. Silvius Calvus [erected this] for his brother.
EDCS-05600838.summary: This is a funerary epitaph erected by Silvius Calvus for his brother, Silvius Silvanus, aged 25.

EDCS-05600839.translation: To Silvia Calvina, aged 28, and to Gaius Silvius Annianus. Silvius Calvinus [erected this] for his daughter and grandson.
EDCS-05600839.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated by Silvius Calvinus for his daughter Silvia Calvina (aged 28) and his grandson Gaius Silvius Annianus.

EDCS-05600842.translation: Caraedudus, son of Fronto Reburrus, willingly fulfilled his vow as deserved.
EDCS-05600842.summary: This inscription records a voluntary fulfillment of a vow by Caraedudus, son of Fronto Reburrus.

EDCS-05600846.translation: To the NI [?]. Julius Florus [dedicated this].
EDCS-05600846.summary: This inscription is a dedication to an unknown deity or person, possibly by Julius Florus.

EDCS-05600848.translation: To the god Bodo. Veicius willingly fulfilled his vow as deserved.
EDCS-05600848.summary: This inscription records a voluntary fulfillment of a vow by Veicius to the god Bodo.

EDCS-05600849.translation: To the departed spirits, Fla, daughter of Fla, [son] of Fla.
EDCS-05600849.summary: A funerary inscription for Fla, daughter of Fla, son of Fla, with a dedication to the 'Dis Manibus' (departed spirits).

EDCS-05600850.translation: Flavia, wife of Flavius, dedicated this to the goddess Degantia in honor of the Argaeli, willingly.
EDCS-05600850.summary: A dedication to the goddess Degantia by Flavia, wife of Flavius, in honor of the Argaeli, likely a local community or group.

EDCS-05600851.summary: A funerary inscription listing the ages of two individuals, Flavus and Aspro.

EDCS-05600852.translation: To the Nymphs of the spring, Gnaeus Lucius Terentius Homullus Iunior, son of Lucius, legate of the Seventh Legion Gemina Felix.
EDCS-05600852.summary: A dedication to the Nymphs of a spring by Gnaeus Lucius Terentius Homullus Iunior, legate of the Legio VII Gemina Felix.

EDCS-05600854.translation: To the Nymphs, Titus Pomponius Proculus Vitrasius Pollio, consul, pontifex, proconsul of Asia, legate of the emperor with praetorian authority of the provinces of Lower Moesia and Citerior Spain, and Faustina his wife.
EDCS-05600854.summary: A dedication to the Nymphs by Titus Pomponius Proculus Vitrasius Pollio, a high-ranking imperial official, and his wife Faustina.

EDCS-05600856.translation: To Publius Atilius(?) Rufus, soldier of the Seventh Legion Gemina Felix, of the century of Aurelius Fronto, aged 36(?), 17 years in service.
EDCS-05600856.summary: A funerary inscription for Publius Atilius(?) Rufus, a soldier of the Legio VII Gemina Felix, including his age and years of service.

EDCS-05600857.translation: Lucius Campilius Paternus, a cavalryman of the Second Flavian Wing, an optio, had this prepared by Flavius Campilius Nofirus, his freedman.
EDCS-05600857.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by a freedman for Lucius Campilius Paternus, a cavalryman and optio in the Second Flavian Wing.

EDCS-05600858.translation: Sempronia Placida placed this for her most devoted father, Flavius Avitus Sup, aged 61, in consular year 363.
EDCS-05600858.summary: A funerary monument erected by Sempronia Placida for her father, Flavius Avitus Sup, with a dedication and the consular year.

EDCS-05600859.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Titus Montanius Maternus placed this tomb and monument for Titus Montanius Fronton, keeper of arms, citizen of Zoela, aged 53, with 26 years of service, his most excellent patron. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600859.summary: A funerary inscription and monument for Titus Montanius Fronton, a keeper of arms, placed by his patron Titus Montanius Maternus.

EDCS-05600860.translation: Uxamensis, aged 30, is buried here; his father made this.
EDCS-05600860.summary: A funerary inscription indicating that Uxamensis, aged 30, is buried here, and that his father erected the monument.

EDCS-05600861.translation: To Anna Chodina, daughter of Antonius Flavius Allainus, aged 35. Antonius Flavius Allainus erected this.
EDCS-05600861.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Anna Chodina, erected by her father.

EDCS-05600862.translation: To Candida, aged 31. Martialis, her husband, erected this for his dearest wife. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600862.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Candida, erected by her husband.

EDCS-05600863.translation: Domitia Pressilla made this bridge in a public place, with her freedmen Domitius Atticus and Aelius overseeing. Domitia Pressilla completed the bridge work in a public place; her freedmen Domitius Atticus and [---] oversaw.
EDCS-05600863.summary: This is a dedication by Domitia Pressilla for the completion of a public bridge, overseen by her freedmen.

EDCS-05600865.translation: To the divine spirits of Helicon, aged 20. Tautius Asclepiades erected this for his son. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600865.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Helicon, erected by his father.

EDCS-05600866.translation: To the divine spirits of Hygina, aged 17. Mercurius and Vitalis had this made for their daughter. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600866.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Hygina, for whom her parents had a monument made.

EDCS-05600867.translation: To Licinius Erbienicus, aged 42. Amma, his mother, [erected this].
EDCS-05600867.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Licinius Erbienicus, erected by his mother.

EDCS-05600869.translation: To Lucius Paccius, son of Paccius, aged 60. Sempronia Amma [erected this] for her husband.
EDCS-05600869.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Paccius, erected by his wife Sempronia Amma.

EDCS-05600870.translation: Philomelus, a household slave, aged 8, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600870.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young household slave named Philomelus.

EDCS-05600871.translation: Quoelia, his wife, aged 55, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600871.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quoelia, likely the wife of the person mentioned in the preceding inscription.

EDCS-05600872.translation: To the divine spirits of Gaius Terentius Chariton, aged 30. Restuta took care to have this made for her son.
EDCS-05600872.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Terentius Chariton, for whom his mother Restuta had a monument made.

EDCS-05600873.translation: To Lucius Terentius Reburrus, of the tribe Quirina, aged 55. Lucius Terentius [placed this monument].
EDCS-05600873.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Terentius Reburrus, likely set up by his relative Lucius Terentius.

EDCS-05600880.translation: Sacred to Mercury. Flaccus set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-05600880.summary: This is a votive dedication to the god Mercury by an individual named Flaccus.

EDCS-05600884.translation: Acarius set this up for Concordia, aged 30.
EDCS-05600884.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Acarius, possibly set up by Concordia, dating to his lifetime or shortly after his death at age 30.

EDCS-05600899.translation: Valeria Irene set this up for her most dutiful husband, Flavius Bocontius, to the Spirits of the Departed.
EDCS-05600899.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Valeria Irene to her husband Flavius Bocontius, honoring the spirits of the dead.

EDCS-05600900.translation: Lucius Corona Severus, a soldier of the Legio VII Gemina, from the centuria of Vettius, and Procula Octavia fulfilled their vow gladly and deservedly.
EDCS-05600900.summary: This is a votive inscription by a soldier Lucius Corona Severus and Procula Octavia, likely his wife, fulfilling a vow made to military deities or superiors.

EDCS-05600906.translation: Flavus, son of Auledus, from Cabarcus, of the centuria of Beriso, aged 15, lies here.
EDCS-05600906.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Flavus, a young man aged 15, likely a soldier or part of a military family given the mention of centuria.

EDCS-05600907.translation: Provesica, mother and father, set this up for their son Rubidius Maxsumus, aged 30, because of his merits.
EDCS-05600907.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by the parents of Rubidius Maxsumus, honoring his merits at the age of 30.

EDCS-05600908.translation: A monument for Cangilus, son of Oculatio, of the Viromenigo tribe, aged 30.
EDCS-05600908.summary: This is a funerary monument for Cangilus, a young man of 30 from the Viromenigo tribe.

EDCS-05600910.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Severilla, a young woman aged 20, likely set up by her parents.

EDCS-05600911.translation: Severa placed this monument for her mother Dovidena, aged 55, in the year 474 (of the Spanish Era, i.e., AD 436).
EDCS-05600911.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Severa for her mother Dovidena, dated to 474 SE (AD 436).

EDCS-05600912.translation: In holy memory, Norenus, who died at seven years of age, pitied his abandoned parents.
EDCS-05600912.summary: This epitaph records the death of a seven-year-old named Norenus and expresses the grief of his parents.

EDCS-05600914.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription identifying Quintus Vendiricus, son of Agedus.

EDCS-05600915.summary: This inscription records the age of Virius Fuscus, who died at the age of 18.

EDCS-05600916.translation: Po Dageni EKETI, 51 years old, from the Ratri tribe, for his family, by his will.
EDCS-05600916.summary: This funerary inscription states that Po Dageni, aged 51 and from the Ratri tribe, was remembered by his family according to his will.

EDCS-05600917.translation: To Talavia, daughter of Nei, his wife.
EDCS-05600917.summary: This is a dedication to Talavia, identified as the wife of the dedicator.

EDCS-05600918.translation: Tombstone for Ugio Ga, son of Doidero Ambatus, aged 25.
EDCS-05600918.summary: This inscription marks a tombstone for Ugio Ga, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-05600926.translation: Titus Calpurnius Fronto gladly and deservedly paid his vow to Victory Augustus.
EDCS-05600926.summary: This inscription records Titus Calpurnius Fronto fulfilling a vow to the goddess Victoria Augustus.

EDCS-05600928.translation: On the fourth day before the Nones of March, during the 14th consulship of Emperor Caesar, Acces Licirni Intercatiensis made a guest-friendship charter with the Palantine civic community for himself, his sons, and his descendants, through the magistrate Elaisicus, under the guest-friendship of Aneni Ammedi, on the second day before the Kalends.
EDCS-05600928.summary: This inscription records a guest-friendship agreement made in March, likely during the reign of Emperor Domitian, between Acces Licirni Intercatiensis and the Palantine community.

EDCS-05600930.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Lucius Pompeius Primus of Interamna, aged 75. Pompeia Maurina, daughter of Fufius Dinus, made this.
EDCS-05600930.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Lucius Pompeius Primus of Interamna, aged 75, and was erected by his daughter, Pompeia Maurina.

EDCS-05600931.translation: For Lucius Antonius Flavinus, aged 65. Lucius Antonius Ma...
EDCS-05600931.summary: This incomplete funerary inscription is for Lucius Antonius Flavinus, aged 65.

EDCS-05600932.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Coelia Rufa, most dutiful wife, erected this for Lucius Caelus Iason, aged 48.
EDCS-05600932.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Caelus Iason, set up by his wife, dating likely to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05600933.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Licinia Iulia had this made for her most dutiful brother Claudius Quartinus, who lived 25 years. Voconius had this made for his [son?], who lived 25 years.
EDCS-05600933.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudius Quartinus, commissioned by his sister Licinia Iulia and his father Voconius, dating likely to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05600935.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sempronius Hispanus for his most dutiful wife Iulia Chrysida, aged 40.
EDCS-05600935.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Chrysida, set up by her husband Sempronius Hispanus, dating likely to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05600936.translation: Vettius Semproninus and Vettia Sempronilla had this made for their mother Sempronia Acca Capito.
EDCS-05600936.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sempronia Acca Capito, commissioned by her children Vettius Semproninus and Vettia Sempronilla, dating likely to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05600939.translation: To Alla Couneido [?]. Cipolius for his wife. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05600939.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Alla Couneido, set up by her husband Cipolius, including a common wish for the deceased, dating likely to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05600941.translation: For Anna A[...], daughter of A[...], aged 60[...]. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05600941.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Anna A[...], daughter of A[...], with a wish for the deceased, dating likely to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05600942.translation: For Quintus Caelus Am M. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05600942.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Caelus Am M, with a wish for the deceased, dating likely to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05600943.translation: For Quintus Curio Pa q Abliqum.
EDCS-05600943.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Curio Pa q Abliqum, likely a tombstone inscription, dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05600944.translation: [...]liae P[at(ernae)] Acciq(um) / [...] aged 70 / [...] Paterna / May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05600944.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for [...]liae, daughter of P[at(ernus)], aged 70, with a wish for the deceased, dating likely to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05600945.translation: Tintioni, aged AV AM P [age not clear], had this made by his father.
EDCS-05600945.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tintioni, commissioned by his father, dating likely to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05600946.translation: To Gaius Valerius Tiro, son of Lucius, from the Tamanici tribe, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05600946.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Valerius Tiro, mentioning his tribal origin.

EDCS-05600947.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Magia, daughter of Sucarius, 19 years old, and her daughter, Magia, had this made for themselves.
EDCS-05600947.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Magia and her daughter, dedicated by Magia for herself and her daughter.

EDCS-05600953.translation: To Gaius Julius [---], former prefect of a cohort [---], military tribune of a legion [---].
EDCS-05600953.summary: This is a funerary epitaph or dedication fragment for Gaius Julius, who held military and prefectural posts.

EDCS-05600954.translation: To Lucius Pompeius Placidus, 19 years old. Pompeius Cantaber and Aemilia Nape had this made for their most devoted son and for themselves.
EDCS-05600954.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Pompeius Placidus, erected by his parents.

EDCS-05600955.translation: To Lucius Valerius Nepos, son of Nasonis, 45 years old, erected by the heir according to the will.
EDCS-05600955.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Valerius Nepos, whose heir commissioned the monument as per his will.

EDCS-05600962.summary: This inscription records a payment or fulfillment of a vow by Segontius.

EDCS-05600964.translation: Flavius Flavinus, a veteran, willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Mercurius Compitalis.
EDCS-05600964.summary: This is a votive inscription by Flavius Flavinus, a veteran, to Mercurius Compitalis.

EDCS-05600965.translation: The worshippers made an altar to Minerva at the shrine called Bruv for it to be set up.
EDCS-05600965.summary: This inscription records the erection of an altar to Minerva by worshippers at a shrine.

EDCS-05600968.translation: Sacred to Tutela. The heirs of Terentius Flaminius placed this.
EDCS-05600968.summary: This is a dedication of a sacred object or place to the goddess Tutela by the heirs of Terentius Flaminius.

EDCS-05600969.translation: Asinius Ausivos, son of Rutilus, 80 years old.
EDCS-05600969.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Asinius Ausivos, recording his name, parentage, and age.

EDCS-05600970.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Atea, a woman.

EDCS-05600971.translation: Elanus, son of Turaesamicius of the Ambati clan, aged 15.
EDCS-05600971.summary: This inscription records the name, parentage, and age of Elanus, a young male.

EDCS-05600973.translation: Vettius Segontius, son of Segontius, aged 35, rests here.
EDCS-05600973.summary: This funerary inscription records the name, parentage, and age of Vettius Segontius.

EDCS-05600979.translation: Iunia Ambata, daughter of Viron, aged 25, rests here.
EDCS-05600979.summary: This funerary inscription records the name, parentage, and age of Iunia Ambata.

EDCS-05600980.translation: Minicia Aunia, daughter of Segontius, aged 3, rests here.
EDCS-05600980.summary: This funerary inscription records the name, parentage, and age of Minicia Aunia, a young child.

EDCS-05600981.translation: To the departed spirits. Porcia, wife of Marci Batasii, aged 70, rests here.
EDCS-05600981.summary: This funerary inscription honors Porcia, wife of Marci Batasii, stating her age and dedication to the departed spirits.

EDCS-05600989.translation: The heir of Aemilia Placidia, for her grandmother Maria Cothidi, by will.
EDCS-05600989.summary: This inscription states that the heir of Aemilia Placidia erected this for her grandmother Maria Cothidi, according to her will.

EDCS-05600991.translation: The heir, by will, for Aemilia Placidia, wife of Materni.
EDCS-05600991.summary: This inscription indicates that the heir, following a will, erected this for Aemilia Placidia, the wife of Materni.

EDCS-05600992.translation: To Lucius Valerius Materno Boletanus, from Marcus Cornelius Pompeianus, his very good friend, for his merits.
EDCS-05600992.summary: This inscription is a dedication from Marcus Cornelius Pompeianus to his friend Lucius Valerius Materno Boletanus, honoring his merits.

EDCS-05600994.translation: The rightful heirs had this made by will for Gaius Turranius Albinus, son of Gaius.
EDCS-05600994.summary: This inscription records that the heirs had this monument made by will for Gaius Turranius Albinus.

EDCS-05600995.translation: To Tiberius Manlius Silvanus, son of Tiberius, of the Galeria tribe, to whom the office of aedile and the position of a magistrate for life were granted twice, Cornelia Faventina, his wife.
EDCS-05600995.summary: This is a dedication to Tiberius Manlius Silvanus, a magistrate, by his wife Cornelia Faventina.

EDCS-05600999.translation: Sacred to Hercules. Gaius Annius and Magia Atia, of Clun, fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-05600999.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Hercules by Gaius Annius and Magia Atia.

EDCS-05601000.translation: To the Divine Shades. Atilius Sosumus had this made for his wife Atilia Senarioni and freedwoman, aged 30. Mother, father, and brother are buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05601000.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Atilia Senarioni, set up by her husband, her mother, father, and brother.

EDCS-05601001.translation: To the Divine Shades. The brother had this made for Teia, his sister.
EDCS-05601001.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Teia, set up by her brother.

EDCS-05601002.summary: This is a dedication to Faustus Publicius Verecundus.

EDCS-05601003.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aurelius Gerontius had this made for Aurelia Euthenia, his mother, aged 55, in accordance with her will. She is buried here.
EDCS-05601003.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelia Euthenia, set up by her son Aurelius Gerontius in accordance with her will.

EDCS-05601008.summary: This is a dedication to Antonius Dangetus.

EDCS-05601010.translation: To Marcus Pison, son of T., aged about...
EDCS-05601010.summary: This is a dedication to Marcus Pison.

EDCS-05601014.translation: To the Divine Shades. Satur, wife of Marcuus Cornelius, aged [...]. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05601014.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Satur, wife of Marcuus Cornelius.

EDCS-05601019.summary: This is a dedication to Julius Aemilius.

EDCS-05601027.translation: Auctus Augustalis, veteran of the Praetorian Guard.
EDCS-05601027.summary: This inscription honors Auctus Augustalis, likely a soldier, and is a short dedication.

EDCS-05601029.summary: This inscription is a fragment with partially legible names or titles, likely from a tombstone.

EDCS-05601041.translation: To Aelius Ursulus, from Messenia Onesiphoris, his father.
EDCS-05601041.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a father to his son, Aelius Ursulus.

EDCS-05601047.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caecilia Iulia lived for [X] years.
EDCS-05601047.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Divine Shades for Caecilia Iulia.

EDCS-05601049.translation: Of [---] for 65 years. Porcia MFA OIA VI for [---] years. Here they are buried. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05601049.summary: This epitaph records the ages of two individuals, Porcia MFA OIA VI and another, buried together, with a standard wish for peace.

EDCS-05601050.translation: Of [---] for 90 years. A dutiful son in his own family. Here he is buried. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05601050.summary: This epitaph records the age of a dutiful son, buried in his family plot, with a standard wish for peace.

EDCS-05601051.translation: For 7 years. A dutiful daughter in her own family. Here she is buried. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05601051.summary: This epitaph records the age of a dutiful daughter, buried in her family plot, with a standard wish for peace.

EDCS-05601053.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Tatia Pia, in her own family, for 5 years.
EDCS-05601053.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Divine Shades for Tatia Pia, who died at a young age.

EDCS-05601056.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Fabricius Felicissimus.
EDCS-05601056.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Divine Shades for Fabricius Felicissimus.

EDCS-05601057.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Fulvius Chrestus, for 9 years. A dutiful son in his own family. Here he is buried. May the earth lie lightly on you. His father [dedicates this].
EDCS-05601057.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a father to his son, Marcus Fulvius Chrestus, who died at a young age, with a standard wish for peace.

EDCS-05601058.translation: For my deserts, Salutae, I give you this small offering; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05601058.summary: This epitaph, likely from a tombstone, is a dedication to Salutae, offering thanks for services rendered and expressing a wish for a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-05601059.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Baritto, aged 37, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05601059.summary: This epitaph records the death of Baritto, aged 37, and is dedicated to the Manes (Spirits of the Departed), a common practice for Roman funerary inscriptions.

EDCS-05601060.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Badia, aged 35, dutiful in her own family.
EDCS-05601060.summary: This epitaph honors Badia, who died at the age of 35, emphasizing her pietas (dutifulness) within her family.

EDCS-05601061.translation: Cornelia Prima, aged 65, is placed here.
EDCS-05601061.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription recording the death of Cornelia Prima at the age of 65.

EDCS-05601062.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Hermione, dutiful, aged 25, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05601062.summary: This epitaph is dedicated to Hermione, who died at 25, highlighting her piety and wishing for her peaceful rest in the afterlife.

EDCS-05601064.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Cornelius Optandus, aged 21, dutiful in his own family.
EDCS-05601064.summary: This inscription honors Cornelius Optandus, who died at the age of 21, emphasizing his dutifulness within his family.

EDCS-05601065.translation: Dutiful in his own family, he placed this for his most dutiful husband. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05601065.summary: This epitaph is a dedication from a wife to her husband, emphasizing their mutual piety and recording his burial.

EDCS-05601066.translation: G. Rufina erected this monument for her son Flaminio Rufino, aged 21. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05601066.summary: This epitaph records that G. Rufina dedicated this monument to her son Flaminio Rufino, who died at the age of 21.

EDCS-05601068.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Flaminia Acutia, aged 25, is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05601068.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Flaminia Acutia at the age of 25 and is dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-05601070.translation: Marcus Iunius Marci filius Hispanus is buried here.
EDCS-05601070.summary: This is a straightforward funerary inscription identifying the deceased as Marcus Iunius Marci filius Hispanus and stating his burial.

EDCS-05601071.translation: Aius, son of Quintus, [born in the tribe] Voltinia.
EDCS-05601071.summary: This inscription records the tribal affiliation of an individual named Aius, son of Quintus.

EDCS-05601080.translation: Aulus Lumnesius, son of Aulus, [belonging to the tribe] Maecia, and his sister Lumnesia, daughter of Aulus, [belonging to the tribe] Maxuma.
EDCS-05601080.summary: This inscription names two individuals, Aulus Lumnesius and his sister Lumnesia, noting their father and tribal affiliations.

EDCS-05601087.translation: To Numerius Vineius, son of Numerius, [belonging to the tribe] Galeria, from Alba.
EDCS-05601087.summary: This is an inscription dedicating something to Numerius Vineius, son of Numerius, from Alba, mentioning his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-05601088.translation: Lucius Aemilius, son of Marcus, grandson of Marcus, [belonging to the tribe] Quirina, named Rectus, from Rome, who is also called Carthaginian, Sicilian, Assotan, Lacedaemonian, and Bastetan, quaestorian clerk, aedilic clerk, honored with a public horse by the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, aedile of the colony of Carthage, patron of the state of Assos, by his will ordered that an annual banquet be established for the state of Assos.
EDCS-05601088.summary: This is a funerary or honorific inscription for Lucius Aemilius Rectus, detailing his extensive honors, offices held, and his posthumous endowment for the colony of Assos, likely dating to the Hadrianic period.

EDCS-05601089.summary: This inscription likely records the names and possibly the identities or affiliations of two individuals, Quirina Rectus and Sicellitanus.

EDCS-05601092.summary: This inscription records the priestly and magisterial offices of Marcus Postumius.

EDCS-05601093.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes, for Lucius Mallius Sempronianus Sempronius.
EDCS-05601093.summary: This is a dedication to the Divine Manes for Lucius Mallius Sempronianus Sempronius.

EDCS-05601094.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the second time, consul, father of the country, the state of Bari is devoted to his divine numen and majesty.
EDCS-05601094.summary: This inscription records the devotion of the state of Bari to Emperor Philip the Arab, likely during his second consulship.

EDCS-05601096.translation: Lucius C. M., aged ten years, is buried here.
EDCS-05601096.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription stating the name and age of the deceased, Lucius C. M.

EDCS-05601100.translation: To the Divine Manes. Ulpia Marciana lived for thirty years. Lucius Cassius Iunianus [dedicates this] to his dearest wife.
EDCS-05601100.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Lucius Cassius Iunianus to his wife Ulpia Marciana, noting her age at death.

EDCS-05601145.translation: Marcus S. Hermeros willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Tutela of Tarraco.
EDCS-05601145.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the goddess Tutela of Tarraco by Marcus S. Hermeros.

EDCS-05601153.translation: Here lies the light of Litorius, freedman of the Augusti, accountant of the Quadragesima of Gaul, also of the Tiber River basin, also of the province Baetica, also of the Cottian Alps. He lived 42 years, 41 days. Statia Felicissima to her incomparable wife, with whom she lived 21 years, 6 months, 32 days.
EDCS-05601153.summary: This epitaph commemorates Litorius, a freedman and accountant, and his wife Statia Felicissima, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE, given the imperial titles and mention of the Tiber.

EDCS-05601155.translation: To the departed spirits. Aurelius Marcellinus and Aurelia Sabina, heirs, made this for their brother Marcus Aurelius Victorinus, son of Marcus, enlisted from Iulia Emona, who lived 34 years, 6 months, 18 days, with 15 years of service. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05601155.summary: This tombstone honors Marcus Aurelius Victorinus, a soldier from Iulia Emona, erected by his siblings, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE based on military service and typical epitaph formulas.

EDCS-05601156.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Valeria Calliste, wretched mother, [made this] for her most dutiful son Lucius Valerius Reburrus, a grain official of the Legio VII Gemina Pia Felix, who lived 24 years, 4 months, 18 days, with 10 years of service.
EDCS-05601156.summary: This epitaph is dedicated by a mother to her son, Lucius Valerius Reburrus, a soldier and grain official of Legio VII Gemina, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05601157.translation: Placidinus, Lupus, Severus, Fixus, Modestus, Terentianus, Cornelianus, Sabinus, Geminus, Flavinus.
EDCS-05601157.summary: This inscription appears to be a list of names, possibly of officials or members of a collegium, without further context.

EDCS-05601159.translation: Haline, sister of Anthracus, freedman of Nedymus, deputy of Caesar's slaves, and Bassus, Quartio, Princeps, to their paternal uncle, aged 10.
EDCS-05601159.summary: This epitaph commemorates Anthracus and his sisters memorializing their young paternal uncle, suggesting a familial relationship and a tragic early death.

EDCS-05601160.translation: From the province of Hispania Citerior, to Marcus Aemilius Severus, son of Marcus.
EDCS-05601160.summary: This is a dedication or honorific inscription from Hispania Citerior to Marcus Aemilius Severus.

EDCS-05601161.translation: To Lucius Antonius Modestus, son of Paternus, of the Quirina tribe, from Intercatia of the Vaccaei tribe, who held all honors in his own state, priest in Rome and of the Augustan Altars, and flamen of the province of Hispania Citerior, from the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05601161.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Antonius Modestus, a Roman official from Hispania Citerior who held various priesthoods and civic offices, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05601163.translation: To Lucius Fonteius Materanus Novatianus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, granted by the order for aedilician honors, twice chief magistrate, judge of the third decuria, honored with the public horse by the Emperor Nerva Augustus, flamen of the province of Hispania Citerior, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05601163.summary: This inscription details the career of Lucius Fonteius Materanus Novatianus, highlighting his civic and religious offices in Hispania Citerior, including recognition from Emperor Nerva.

EDCS-05601164.translation: From the province of Hispania Citerior, to Quintus Herennius Aquila, son of Quintus, of the Aniensis tribe, who held all honors in his own state, admitted among the judges of the decuria in Rome, flamen of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-05601164.summary: This inscription honors Quintus Herennius Aquila, a Roman official from Hispania Citerior who achieved high status and served as flamen of the province.

EDCS-05601165.translation: To Nepos, son of Titus, tribune of soldiers of the Sixth Legion, and flamen of the Emperors in Rome, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05601165.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Nepos, a military tribune and priest, likely erected by local officials. The mention of the emperors suggests a date within the imperial period.

EDCS-05601168.translation: XXXV years old, his son XV years old, of the province of Hispania Citerior Tarraconensis. S.
EDCS-05601168.summary: This inscription records the ages of two individuals, likely father and son, possibly from the province of Hispania Citerior Tarraconensis. The abbreviated 'S' at the end is unclear without further context, but it might indicate a status or location.

EDCS-05601172.summary: This very short inscription simply records the name 'Primigenius' and his title 'sevir', likely a member of a group of six prominent men in a Roman municipality, possibly dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05601173.translation: Cornelius Florus and Licinius Candidus, his heirs, made this monument to their master of the Lares, Quintillianus, from their own funds.
EDCS-05601173.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintillianus, master of the Lares, dedicated by his heirs Cornelius Florus and Licinius Candidus, indicating a private religious role and familial relationship.

EDCS-05601174.translation: Agathocles, a home-born slave of Viennensis, aged nineteen, the goldsmith of Cornelia Cruseis, lies here.
EDCS-05601174.summary: This is a tombstone for Agathocles, a young goldsmith who was a slave of Cornelia Cruseis, likely dating to the Imperial period given the mention of slavery and a specific trade.

EDCS-05601175.translation: To the Divine Shades. Clodia Canilla made this for her well-deserving husband, Aemilius Assaracus, a plumber.
EDCS-05601175.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a wife, Clodia Canilla, to her husband Aemilius Assaracus, a plumber, indicating a private memorial to a tradesman.

EDCS-05601176.translation: Marinius Catinianus made this memorial for his good comrade Silicius Donatus, who lived 25 years and 9 days, and who was not permitted to enjoy his homeland and people, nor to see his parents, who died innocent, a Mauritanian, by fate; he himself was studious of the art.
EDCS-05601176.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Silicius Donatus, a Mauritanian who died young and possibly in exile, erected by his friend Marinius Catinianus, highlighting themes of loyalty, fate, and artistic inclination.

EDCS-05601177.translation: To the Divine Shades. Pompeius Fae, for trade purposes, and Ausone...
EDCS-05601177.summary: This inscription begins as a funerary dedication to Pompeius Fae, possibly mentioning his profession (trade) and another individual named Ausone, but it is incomplete and its full meaning is lost.

EDCS-05601178.translation: Ulpius Marcianus, assistant of the princeps, erected this.
EDCS-05601178.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by Ulpius Marcianus, who held the position of 'adiutor principis', likely an administrative or military role within an imperial context.

EDCS-05601179.translation: Firmus, steward of Marcius Aemilius Placidus, lies here.
EDCS-05601179.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death of Firmus, who served as a steward or manager for Marcius Aemilius Placidus, indicating a hierarchical relationship in a private household or estate.

EDCS-05601180.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Sulpicius Faventinus, heir, [erected this] for his excellent guardian Gaius Sulpicius Syntropus, who deserved well.
EDCS-05601180.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to a guardian by his heir.

EDCS-05601182.translation: Licinia Flaccilla erected this in memory for her most dutiful and incomparable sister, Pompeia Paternana, a native of Palentia and Vimina ciensis.
EDCS-05601182.summary: This is a funerary epitaph commemorating a sister by her sibling, with geographical references.

EDCS-05601183.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Pulicio Ziogas, a native of Leptis, a household slave, lived for 92 years. His daughter Pulicia Florina erected this for her most dutiful father.
EDCS-05601183.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a long-lived freedman, dedicated by his daughter.

EDCS-05601186.translation: Lucius Caesius Ampius, from his own resources.
EDCS-05601186.summary: This inscription records a private donation or expenditure by Lucius Caesius Ampius.

EDCS-05601187.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Cornelia Baruccia made [this] for her husband Publius Cornelius Secundinus, who deserved well.
EDCS-05601187.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to a husband by his wife.

EDCS-05601188.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Fabia Chreste arranged for this to be made for her brother Fabius Censor, most dutiful and deserving of good.
EDCS-05601188.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to a brother by his sister.

EDCS-05601189.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus Fabius Censor made [this] for his dearest wife, on the Sigerinian road.
EDCS-05601189.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to a wife by her husband, possibly located by a road.

EDCS-05601190.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Fulvia Domitia made [this] for her deserving slave Romulus.
EDCS-05601190.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to a slave by his owner.

EDCS-05601192.translation: For Gaius Licinius Miniciano. Licinia Crispula, his mother, [erected this] for her most dutiful and obedient son.
EDCS-05601192.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to a son by his mother.

EDCS-05601193.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcius Lucullus and Valeria Thais made [this] for their deserving female slave, who lived for more or less 25 years.
EDCS-05601193.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to a female slave by her owners, noting her lifespan.

EDCS-05601194.translation: To the divine spirits, Augustalis made this for Maritima, his most innocent and deserving wife, and for himself.
EDCS-05601194.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband for his wife and himself.

EDCS-05601195.translation: And Martialis, wife of Maria Quieta.
EDCS-05601195.summary: This appears to be a continuation or fragment of a funerary inscription, identifying a woman named Martialis.

EDCS-05601198.translation: To the divine spirits, Pompeia Clara and her husband Valerius Rogatianus made this for their wife [mother?] and well-deserving Pompeia Iuniana, who lived 28 years, 2 months, and 15 days.
EDCS-05601198.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband and daughter for a wife and mother, providing her age at death.

EDCS-05601199.translation: Publilius Cincianus and Nice to their dearest son Numerius Publilius Hilarius, aged 2 years, 3 months, and 9 days.
EDCS-05601199.summary: A funerary dedication by parents for their young son, specifying his age at death.

EDCS-05601200.summary: A funerary dedication for a person named Heraclid.

EDCS-05601201.translation: Lucius Statius Maximus arranged for this to be made for his most dutiful mother Gallica.
EDCS-05601201.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by a son for his mother.

EDCS-05601202.translation: Decimus Titurnius Diphilus, freedman of Decimus Titius, [and his] patron.
EDCS-05601202.summary: An inscription likely related to a freedman and his former master, possibly on a tomb or public building.

EDCS-05601205.translation: From the deceased, his sister and parents made this for their brother, aged [---] years.
EDCS-05601205.summary: A funerary inscription by siblings and parents for a deceased brother, with age indicated as missing.

EDCS-05601207.summary: A simple burial marker identifying the deceased as Flavianus.

EDCS-05601281.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrianus Augustus.
EDCS-05601281.summary: A dedication to Emperor Hadrian, likely on a public monument.

EDCS-05601282.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Claudius Tacitus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power, censor, six times consul, restorer of the world.
EDCS-05601282.summary: This is an honorific inscription for Emperor Tacitus, dating to his reign (275-276 AD), likely associated with a public monument celebrating his titles.

EDCS-05601283.translation: To our lord the Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Licinius Licinianus Augustus.
EDCS-05601283.summary: This is an honorific inscription for Emperor Licinius, dating to his reign (308-324 AD), likely part of a public monument.

EDCS-05601285.translation: The Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the deified [Severus], chief priest, fifteen times imperator, thirteen times consul, with tribunician power for the twenty-first time, father of the fatherland, 160 Roman miles.
EDCS-05601285.summary: This inscription, likely from a milestone, lists the titles of an emperor, possibly Caracalla or Elagabalus, given the high consular and tribunician counts.

EDCS-05601288.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Severus, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, descendant of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus Germanicus Maximus, with tribunician power for the seventeenth time, imperator for the third time, four times consul, father of the fatherland, proconsul, 30 Roman miles.
EDCS-05601288.summary: This is a detailed honorific inscription for Emperor Caracalla, likely from a milestone, listing his extensive imperial titles and lineage, dating to 212-213 AD.

EDCS-05601292.translation: To Quintus Junius Quartus Decimus, governor with praetorian power, from Bracara, 34 Roman miles.
EDCS-05601292.summary: This is likely a milestone inscription indicating a distance of 34 miles from Bracara, commissioned by Quintus Junius Quartus Decimus, a legate of praetorian rank.

EDCS-05601293.translation: To Quintus Junius Quartus Decimus, governor with praetorian power, from Bracara, 37 Roman miles.
EDCS-05601293.summary: This is likely a milestone inscription indicating a distance of 37 miles from Bracara, commissioned by Quintus Junius Quartus Decimus, a legate of praetorian rank.

EDCS-05601294.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius, chief priest, Germanicus Maximus, Parthicus Maximus, with tribunician power, father of the fatherland, from Asturica, 79 Roman miles.
EDCS-05601294.summary: This is likely a milestone inscription for Emperor Caracalla, dating to his reign (198-217 AD), indicating a distance of 79 miles from Asturica.

EDCS-05601296.translation: The Emperor Nerva Caesar Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power, father of the fatherland, three times consul, from Bracara, 36 Roman miles.
EDCS-05601296.summary: This is likely a milestone inscription for Emperor Nerva, dating to his reign (96-98 AD), indicating a distance of 36 miles from Bracara.

EDCS-05601298.translation: The Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus Germanicus Maximus Dacicus Maximus Sarmaticus Maximus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the fifth time, imperator for the seventh time, father of the fatherland, consul, proconsul, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, Germanicus Maximus, Dacicus Maximus, Sarmaticus Maximus, prince of the youth, son of our lord the Emperor.
EDCS-05601298.summary: This inscription records the titles of Emperors Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus Caesar, likely from a milestone or monument, dating to 236-238 AD.

EDCS-05601303.translation: Gaius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, son of Germanicus Caesar, grandson of Tiberius Caesar Augustus, great-grandson of the deified Augustus, father of the fatherland, chief priest, with tribunician power for the fourth time, three times consul, Roman miles.
EDCS-05601303.summary: This inscription lists the titles of Emperor Caligula, likely from a milestone, dating to 37-41 AD.

EDCS-05601306.translation: Nero Claudius, son of the deified Claudius Augustus, grandson of Germanicus Caesar, great-grandson of Tiberius Caesar Augustus, great-great-grandson of the deified Augustus, Caesar Augustus Germanicus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, imperator, consul, miles (posted).
EDCS-05601306.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription listing the titles of Emperor Nero, likely from a public monument.

EDCS-05601313.translation: Aquilia Martia, as magistra, dedicated this gift to Augusta Venus.
EDCS-05601313.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Venus by a local official, Aquilia Martia, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05601353.translation: Antonia Rufina willingly placed this as a vow to the Nymphs of Lupiae.
EDCS-05601353.summary: This is a votive dedication to the Nymphs by Antonia Rufina, likely from a shrine near Lupiae.

EDCS-05601354.summary: This is a simple epitaph for a woman named Coracia.

EDCS-05601355.summary: This is a simple epitaph for a man named Reburrus Ari Seurus, likely of local origin.

EDCS-05601356.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Sulpicius Rufus and Lucius Flavius Clemens, veterans, saw to the making of this according to the will of Lucius Alfius Reburrus, of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-05601356.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Alfius Reburrus, arranged by his heirs in accordance with his will.

EDCS-05601357.translation: Caius Sutorius made [this] for Decorus the shoemaker, aged 35, and Priscus the shoemaker, aged 20.
EDCS-05601357.summary: This is a funerary monument for two individuals, likely father and son, made by Caius Sutorius.

EDCS-05601358.summary: This is a simple epitaph for a man named Flavus.

EDCS-05601360.translation: Teida, wife of Tauricon Longani, is buried here.
EDCS-05601360.summary: This is a simple epitaph for a woman named Teida, wife of Tauricon Longani.

EDCS-05601361.translation: Urbicus willingly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-05601361.summary: This is a votive inscription by Urbicus to Jupiter, likely from a public or private shrine.

EDCS-05601363.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aia Origena, daughter of Vironus, took care to have this monument made for her most dutiful daughters, Aia Quemia, daughter of Boddi, a Celtigun, aged 31, and Aia Caravanca, daughter of Boddi, a Celtigun, aged 35.
EDCS-05601363.summary: A funerary monument set up by Aia Origena for her two daughters, Aia Quemia and Aia Caravanca, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period given the naming conventions and formula.

EDCS-05601364.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aninus set this up for Anna Calediga, his most pious aunt, who lived 80 years. Aninus set this up for Dovidena Calediga, his most dutiful mother, who lived 25 years.
EDCS-05601364.summary: A funerary monument erected by Aninus for his aunt Anna Calediga and his mother Dovidena Calediga, indicating family devotion and likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05601365.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Leonina, the mother, set up this monument for her son Sempronius, aged 20, and for herself, aged 40.
EDCS-05601365.summary: A funerary monument erected by a mother, Leonina, for her son Sempronius and presumably for herself as well, suggesting a shared burial plot and dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05601366.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Danuvius, son of Quintus, of the community of Orgnomescum, aged 22.
EDCS-05601366.summary: A funerary dedication for Danuvius, a young man from the community of Orgnomescum, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05601367.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Anna set up this memorial for her uncle Aelius Sextianus, aged 95.
EDCS-05601367.summary: A memorial monument erected by Anna for her uncle Aelius Sextianus, a long-lived individual, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05601368.translation: To the spirits of the departed. C. V. took care to have this made for his most dutiful son Miliocula, aged 37.
EDCS-05601368.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by C. V. for his son Miliocula, indicating a parent's grief and likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05601372.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Licinia Quieta took care to have this made from her own money for Licinius Festus, aged 50.
EDCS-05601372.summary: A funerary monument established by Licinia Quieta using her own funds for Licinius Festus, likely a husband or relative, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05601377.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Britto for Uloqum, son of Daticus, aged 70. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05601377.summary: A funerary inscription for Britto, dedicated to Uloqum, son of Daticus, with a standard wish for a light burial, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05601388.translation: Arbuscula, freedwoman of the woman Fulvia.
EDCS-05601388.summary: An inscription identifying Arbuscula as a freedwoman of a woman named Fulvia, likely a simple designation of status and ownership, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05601406.translation: [...itus / [...s(iti) t(ibi) / t(erra) l(evis) Api/nus Ci/[...] which translates to '[...]itus / [...may the earth / lie lightly Api/nus Ci/[...]
EDCS-05601406.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription that includes the wish 'sit tibi terra levis' (may the earth lie lightly upon you), indicating it is a grave marker, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05601413.translation: Saturninus, son of Caturonis, gladly fulfilled his vow to the Genius.
EDCS-05601413.summary: This is a dedication to the Genius by Saturninus, son of Caturonis.

EDCS-05601415.translation: Cadus Pedacianus erected this monument to his friend Pentovius, son of Aulgigunus, aged 30.
EDCS-05601415.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Cadus Pedacianus for his friend Pentovius, son of Aulgigunus.

EDCS-05601433.summary: This is a tombstone for Donatus, aged 10, set up by his mother Aletheia.

EDCS-05601434.translation: Atto, son of Manucius, aged 50. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05601434.summary: This is a tombstone for Atto, son of Manucius, aged 50, with a common epitaph.

EDCS-05601439.translation: To the spirits (of the dead), Baebia, daughter of [---].
EDCS-05601439.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Baebia, daughter of [---].

EDCS-05601456.summary: This is a dedication or ownership marker for Lucius Voconius Catini.

EDCS-05601461.summary: This is likely a property marker or dedication belonging to Gaius Julius Marianus.

EDCS-05601470.summary: This is likely a property marker or dedication belonging to Numerius C. Mucianus.

EDCS-05601499.summary: This inscription indicates the workshop or business of Pussinnicius.

EDCS-05601503.summary: This inscription indicates the workshop or business of Sabinus.

EDCS-05601504.summary: This inscription identifies a workshop belonging to Scottus.

EDCS-06100307.translation: To Acirsennius of Curnonium, a Hellene, aged 85, father-in-law and husband to Maia, daughter of Hellene, aged 35. Flaccilla, daughter of Fuscinius, had this made at her own expense.
EDCS-06100307.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Acirsennius, set up by his daughter Flaccilla, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-06100353.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Iemuria, daughter of Talavus.

EDCS-06100354.translation: To Pistira, daughter of Iir, aged 40.
EDCS-06100354.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Pistira, daughter of Iir, providing her age.

EDCS-06100357.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Domitia, described as a most beloved daughter.

EDCS-06100358.translation: To Domitius, father of the fatherland, son of Lucius.
EDCS-06100358.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Domitius, possibly a local dignitary.

EDCS-06100359.translation: To Quinta Tetia, aged 21. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-06100359.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quinta Tetia, aged 21, with a common Latin wish for the deceased.

EDCS-06100365.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quinto Vintidio, aged 50, and Flamilia, aged [---]. The living set this up for themselves. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-06100365.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by a couple, Quinto Vintidio and Flamilia, for themselves as a posthumous dedication.

EDCS-06100366.translation: Caius Valerius had this made for his brothers [RIOQU]M and [ANIQU]M.
EDCS-06100366.summary: This inscription records Caius Valerius commissioning a monument for his brothers.

EDCS-06100369.summary: This inscription records the death of Iulus Praesens in the region of Calaecia.

EDCS-06100371.translation: Sempronius Flavus, son of Flavius, aged 25, is buried here. His mother, Antistia Montana, and his parents saw to it that this monument was made.
EDCS-06100371.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sempronius Flavus, dated by the mention of his mother and parents as patrons.

EDCS-06100375.translation: Manilius Martialis fulfilled his vow to Larraha.
EDCS-06100375.summary: A dedication recording the fulfillment of a vow by Manilius Martialis.

EDCS-06100378.translation: Gnaeus Veius, son of Caius, Amba Maternus.
EDCS-06100378.summary: An incomplete inscription likely recording a name and parentage.

EDCS-06100382.translation: From the workshop of Mas(ter) R[---]ani. Hirinius paints. Use well, Materne, this bedroom.
EDCS-06100382.summary: A painter's signature and sales inscription for a bedroom, likely from a domestic context.

EDCS-06100383.translation: Under Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajanus Augustus.
EDCS-06100383.summary: An inscription marking a date during the reign of Emperor Trajan.

EDCS-06100384.translation: When Germanicus and Dacicus were consuls for the sixth time, completed in the Capitol.
EDCS-06100384.summary: A dating clause mentioning consular years and a location, possibly related to a public building.

EDCS-06100388.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Staberius Felix, soldier of the VII Gemina Pia Felix legion, a trainee of the armatura, with twelve years of service, who lived 45 years, [erected by] his citizens and comrades.
EDCS-06100388.summary: A funerary epitaph for Staberius Felix, a soldier of the VII Gemina legion, detailing his service and age.

EDCS-06400041.translation: Labicia Maura, freedwoman of a woman.
EDCS-06400041.summary: An inscription identifying Labicia Maura as a freedwoman.

EDCS-06400046.summary: A greeting inscription identifying Bacchis as a freedwoman.

EDCS-06900001.translation: In the name of the Lord, this temple was completed by Marispalla Dovota on the 14th day before the Kalends of April in the era 573, during the reign of the most serene king Veremundus.
EDCS-06900001.summary: An inscription recording the completion of a temple by Marispalla Dovota under King Veremundus in a specific era.

EDCS-06900005.translation: Apana, daughter of Ambollus, Celtic, Supertamarca, aged 25, is buried here. Her brother Apana saw to its construction.
EDCS-06900005.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Apana, who died at age 25, and mentions her brother as the one who commissioned the monument.

EDCS-06900012.translation: Madarssus, son of Blacaus and Burrilus of the Arniciorum tribe, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-06900012.summary: This votive inscription records Madarssus, son of Blacaus and Burrilus, fulfilling a vow made by his tribe, the Arniciorum.

EDCS-06900013.translation: Eburia, daughter of Calvenus, Celtic, Supertamarca, at the fort of Lubri, aged 26, is buried here.
EDCS-06900013.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Eburia, daughter of Calvenus, who died at age 26 at the fort of Lubri.

EDCS-06900015.translation: Decuminus, freedman of Decumus, a cavalryman of the Augustan wing, willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Obbelleginus.
EDCS-06900015.summary: This votive inscription records Decuminus, a freedman and cavalryman, paying a vow to Obbelleginus.

EDCS-06900018.summary: This inscription likely records the names and ages of two young individuals, possibly for funerary purposes.

EDCS-06900024.summary: This brief inscription likely marks an entrance or burial place associated with Arcontius.

EDCS-06900025.summary: This inscription indicates the presence or passage of Eustatius at this location.

EDCS-06900029.summary: This is a simple name inscription, possibly marking ownership or a tomb.

EDCS-06900030.translation: Cantienus paid his vow with intention.
EDCS-06900030.summary: This votive inscription records Cantienus fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-06900037.translation: Onnius Natalis, decurion of the Astures wing.
EDCS-06900037.summary: This inscription records the name and military rank of Onnius Natalis, a decurion in the Astures cavalry wing.

EDCS-06900038.translation: To Augustus Aiuncus, grandson of Nemedo, of the Medani tribe.
EDCS-06900038.summary: This is a dedication to the emperor Augustus Aiuncus, identifying him by tribal affiliation and family lineage.

EDCS-06900039.translation: Gallus paid 100 sesterces. Quintus Seranus... Doiderus Silvanus and Gaius Caninius...
EDCS-06900039.summary: This inscription appears to be a record of payment or a dedication, mentioning several individuals and a sum of money, possibly related to a construction or funerary monument.

EDCS-06900040.summary: This is a short votive inscription recording a vow made by Terentius.

EDCS-06900042.summary: This inscription simply records the names of two individuals, Gallus and Festus, possibly as owners or dedicators of a monument.

EDCS-06900043.summary: This inscription records the name of Terentius Levis, likely as the dedicator or subject of a monument.

EDCS-06900044.summary: This inscription records the name of Valerius Silvanus, likely as the dedicator or subject of a monument.

EDCS-06900045.summary: This inscription records that Rubenus visited a place or performed an action three times, the context of which is unclear.

EDCS-06900049.translation: Gaius Sempronius, on the third day before the Nones of May.
EDCS-06900049.summary: This inscription records a date, the third day before the Nones of May, associated with Gaius Sempronius.

EDCS-06900052.translation: Martialis and his sons Au and Avitus, and their slave Thurscando, took care to have this made at their own expense.
EDCS-06900052.summary: This funerary inscription records that Martialis and his sons, along with their slave, commissioned the construction of this monument for themselves.

EDCS-06900053.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For a distance of 17 feet, this monument was dedicated to our son, from his own property; to our daughter Cornelia Cornutina, aged 70; to our mother; to Lucius Petronius Cornutus, aged 19. Titus Petronius Carus and Lucretia Avita dedicate this to our son.
EDCS-06900053.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription dedicated by Titus Petronius Carus and Lucretia Avita to their son, their daughter Cornelia Cornutina, and their mother, specifying dimensions and ages.

EDCS-06900054.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Cae and Ursia set up this memorial for their daughter Thutis, aged five, who lived a most devoted life.
EDCS-06900054.summary: A funerary monument set up by Cae and Ursia for their five-year-old daughter Thutis.

EDCS-07000668.translation: Gaius Saturninus, son of Tricus, aged [---], father for his sons and himself.
EDCS-07000668.summary: An inscription by Gaius Saturninus, son of Tricus, establishing something for himself and his sons.

EDCS-07000679.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Calpurnia Mod set this up for her husband Sulpicius Rega, aged sixty. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-07000679.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sulpicius Rega, set up by his wife Calpurnia Mod.

EDCS-07000680.translation: Montana Urso set up this memorial, aged forty-five.
EDCS-07000680.summary: A memorial set up by Montana Urso.

EDCS-07000689.translation: From the century of Caelius Celer, standard-bearer, his mother Lapoena set this up for her devoted son Annon.
EDCS-07000689.summary: A monument set up by Lapoena for her son Annon, who was a standard-bearer in the century of Caelius Celer.

EDCS-07100008.translation: Lucius Didius Marinus, procurator of the Emperors, made this in fulfillment of a vow to Mars Gradivus.
EDCS-07100008.summary: A dedication to Mars Gradivus by Lucius Didius Marinus, an imperial procurator.

EDCS-07100012.translation: To Aesculapius, Salus, Serapis, and Isis. Lucius Cassius Paullus Augustanius Alpinus Bellicius Sollers and Marcus Cassius Agrippa Sanctus Paullinus Augustanius Alpinus [made this].
EDCS-07100012.summary: A dedication to multiple deities by Lucius Cassius Paullus and Marcus Cassius Agrippa.

EDCS-07100017.translation: To Jupiter, from the century of Queledinus.
EDCS-07100017.summary: A dedication to Jupiter from a specific century.

EDCS-07100019.translation: The detachment of the Seventh Double Legion, the Fortunate, fulfilled a vow willingly and deservedly on the birthday of Aquila, for the health of Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, under the supervision of Lucius Marcius, a centurion of the same legion, and Valerius Victor, a decurion.
EDCS-07100019.summary: A votive dedication by a legionary detachment for the emperor Antoninus Pius' health, on the birthday of Aquila.

EDCS-07100032.translation: Augo Propeddius Craro willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07100032.summary: This is a votive inscription by Augo Propeddius Craro, likely fulfilling a promise made for a favor received.

EDCS-07100034.translation: Flavinus, son of Cossue Nedoiedius, willingly with spirit.
EDCS-07100034.summary: This is a brief inscription by Flavinus, possibly a dedication or a personal statement of good will.

EDCS-07100041.translation: Under Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus.
EDCS-07100041.summary: This is an inscription indicating a context related to the reign of Emperor Hadrian.

EDCS-07100044.translation: Fuscus, son of Dorilsis Eptaecenti, a soldier from the cohort of Thracians, in the century of Iulius Martialis, from Sardinia, 25 years old, 9 years in service, is buried here.
EDCS-07100044.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Fuscus, a soldier of the Thracian cohort serving in the 1st century, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-07100045.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld: Granio Fortis, a veteran of the Legio VII Gemina Pia Felix, 84 years old, and Vettia Sabina, 50 years old. Aelia Priscilla placed this in memory of her parents.
EDCS-07100045.summary: This is a tombstone inscription set up by Aelia Priscilla for her parents, Granio Fortis, a veteran, and Vettia Sabina.

EDCS-07100048.translation: To Virillius, soldier of the Legio X Gemina, in the century of the first spear, 32 years old, 14 years in service, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon him.
EDCS-07100048.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Virillius, a soldier of the Legio X Gemina, who died at the age of 32.

EDCS-07100050.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Lucius Annio Abascantus, 60 years old, and Terentia, daughter of Annia, 18 years old. Terentia Apra for her husband and daughter.
EDCS-07100050.summary: This is a tombstone inscription set up by Terentia Apra for her husband, Lucius Annio Abascantus, and her daughter, Terentia Annia.

EDCS-07100051.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Aro Successa, 40 years old. To Aro Trita, her dearest sister. Copori.
EDCS-07100051.summary: This is a tombstone inscription set up by Copori for her sister, Aro Successa.

EDCS-07100052.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Aurelia Fortunata. Aelius ni Gr for his wife.
EDCS-07100052.summary: This is a tombstone inscription set up by Aelius ni Gr for his wife, Aurelia Fortunata.

EDCS-07100055.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Calpurnia Elanis. She placed this for her most devoted nephew, aged 15.
EDCS-07100055.summary: This is a tombstone inscription set up by Calpurnia Elanis for her nephew, who died at the age of 15.

EDCS-07100057.translation: Corinthia arranged for this to be made for her son and herself.
EDCS-07100057.summary: This epitaph records that Corinthia commissioned a monument for her son and herself.

EDCS-07100058.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Dativo, son of Iuliana, a most dutiful mother, who lived 3 years and 30 days.
EDCS-07100058.summary: This epitaph records the death of Dativo, son of Iuliana, who died at the age of 3 years and 30 days.

EDCS-07100059.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Flavia Leona, and Terentia Florilla, and Flavius Florus, Flavius Ambatio and Flavia Martilia, their deserving patrons.
EDCS-07100059.summary: This epitaph honors the patrons Flavia Leona, Terentia Florilla, and Flavius Florus, by Flavius Ambatio and Flavia Martilia.

EDCS-07100061.translation: Sabinus, freedman, to Tiberius Iulius Vegetus.
EDCS-07100061.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Sabinus, a freedman, to Tiberius Iulius Vegetus.

EDCS-07100064.translation: Marius Successus for his most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-07100064.summary: This epitaph records Marius Successus dedicating a monument to his daughter.

EDCS-07100065.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Proculus of Galeria, a beneficiarius of the procurator of Augustus. Marcus Terentius, heir.
EDCS-07100065.summary: This epitaph records the dedication to Proculus of Galeria, a beneficiarius of the Augustan procurator, by his heir Marcus Terentius.

EDCS-07100066.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Titus Septimius Martialis, who lived 85 years. Septimia Berulla placed this for her most dutiful husband. He is buried here.
EDCS-07100066.summary: This epitaph records that Septimia Berulla erected a monument for her husband Titus Septimius Martialis, who died at age 85.

EDCS-07100067.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Sestius Chrestus. Sestia Iulia arranged for this to be made for her most missed wife, who lived 25 years.
EDCS-07100067.summary: This epitaph records that Sestia Iulia commissioned a monument for her wife Sestius Chrestus, who died at age 25.

EDCS-07100068.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Similis, a slave of our emperors, who lived 10 years.
EDCS-07100068.summary: This epitaph records the death of Similis, a slave of the emperors, at the age of 10.

EDCS-07100069.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Sulpicius Placidinus, who lived 55 years. Sulpicius Messor arranged for this to be made for his excellent patron.
EDCS-07100069.summary: This epitaph records that Sulpicius Messor commissioned a monument for his patron Sulpicius Placidinus, who died at age 55.

EDCS-07100070.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Tyche, wife of the unnamed dedicator.

EDCS-07100071.translation: Quintus Varius Reburrus, son of Reburrus, from Transminium, aged 18, lies here.
EDCS-07100071.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Quintus Varius Reburrus, who died at the age of 18.

EDCS-07100073.summary: This is a dedication to Avenna by an unnamed individual from Cluniensis.

EDCS-07100075.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Postumia Marcella, wife, had this made for her most devoted husband, Aurelius Iulianus, soldier of the Legio VII Gemina, who lived 35 years.
EDCS-07100075.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelius Iulianus, a soldier in Legio VII Gemina, erected by his devoted wife.

EDCS-07100076.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Aurelius Victor, soldier of the Legio VII Gemina Pia Felix, of Thracian origin, aged 45, with 20 years of service, optio of the cavalry, 5 feet 9 inches tall.
EDCS-07100076.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Aurelius Victor, a Thracian soldier and cavalry optio in Legio VII Gemina, detailing his age, service, and stature.

EDCS-07100077.translation: Gaius Aemilius Talavus, son of Talavus, of the tribe Quirina.
EDCS-07100077.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying Gaius Aemilius Talavus, mentioning his father and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-07100079.translation: To the Divine Shades, to Gaius Aplonius.
EDCS-07100079.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Gaius Aplonius.

EDCS-07100080.translation: His mother Atta placed and dedicated [this]. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-07100080.summary: This is a funerary epitaph where the mother, Atta, placed the monument for her deceased child and expressed a wish for the afterlife.

EDCS-07100081.translation: Caecilia Materna, wife of Caibaliqum, from Uxamensis, aged 38.
EDCS-07100081.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caecilia Materna, identifying her as the wife of Caibaliqum and stating her age.

EDCS-07100082.translation: To the Divine Shades. Abascantus and Scintilla [place this] for their daughter Calityche, aged 15. Titus [son] of [father's name, omitted].
EDCS-07100082.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Calityche, aged 15, set up by her parents Abascantus and Scintilla.

EDCS-07100083.translation: Da Caranca, his wife, saw to its construction.
EDCS-07100083.summary: This funerary inscription records that Da Caranca, the wife of the deceased, commissioned the monument.

EDCS-07100084.translation: Sempronius to Coelius Patientis, son of Patientis, aged 35.
EDCS-07100084.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Sempronius to Coelius Patientis, who died at the age of 35.

EDCS-07100085.translation: Marcus Iulius Praesens to Fabia Alla, aged 40, his mother.
EDCS-07100085.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Marcus Iulius Praesens to his mother, Fabia Alla, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-07100086.translation: To the Divine Shades. Popilius Respectus, a lictor from the cohort of lictors, to his most sweet wife Flavia Candida, aged 23, saw to its construction.
EDCS-07100086.summary: This funerary inscription records that Popilius Respectus, a lictor, commissioned the monument for his wife Flavia Candida.

EDCS-07100087.translation: To the Divine Shades. [Someone] saw to the construction of this for Flavius Erastio, husband of Hiero, most beloved.
EDCS-07100087.summary: This funerary inscription records that Hiero commissioned the monument for her beloved husband, Flavius Erastio.

EDCS-07100088.translation: To the Divine Shades. Christe and Marcellus placed this for their most excellent father Flavius Pisto, freedman of Archelai, tribune of the Seventh Gemina Pius Felix Legion, who lived 48 years.
EDCS-07100088.summary: This funerary inscription records that Christe and Marcellus commissioned the monument for their father, Flavius Pisto, a freedman and former tribune.

EDCS-07100089.translation: To the Divine Shades. Flavius Festus saw to the construction of this for his most dutiful and most longed-for son Flavius Sabinus Lanciensis, who lived 32 years, 1 month, and 14 days.
EDCS-07100089.summary: This funerary inscription records that Flavius Festus commissioned the monument for his son, Flavius Sabinus Lanciensis, who died at the age of 32.

EDCS-07100090.translation: For his most dutiful husband Flavo. [Someone] saw to its construction. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-07100090.summary: This funerary inscription records the construction of a monument for Flavo, addressed as a dutiful husband, with a wish for his repose.

EDCS-07100091.translation: To the Divine Shades, sacred. Iunius Fuscus placed this tomb for the memory of Fuccsi Galionili, son of Saturnini, aged 7 years and 6 months.
EDCS-07100091.summary: This funerary inscription records that Iunius Fuscus placed this tomb for the memory of his son, Fuccsi Galionili, who died at the age of 7 years and 6 months.

EDCS-07100092.translation: To the Divine Shades, sacred. Diosume, his mother, placed this for Germana, her incompatible daughter, aged 1 year and 8 months.
EDCS-07100092.summary: This funerary inscription records that Diosume, the mother, placed this monument for her daughter Germana, who died at the age of 1 year and 8 months.

EDCS-07100093.translation: To the Divine Manes of Iulia Pressilla, aged 85.
EDCS-07100093.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Pressilla, who died at the age of 85.

EDCS-07100094.translation: To the Divine Manes. Antonia Flavia erected this monument for her most devoted son, Iulius Celer, aged 40.
EDCS-07100094.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Antonia Flavia for her son Iulius Celer, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-07100095.translation: Lollianus Dureta had this made for Saldanica, defender of youth.
EDCS-07100095.summary: This inscription records that Lollianus Dureta commissioned a monument for Saldanica.

EDCS-07100096.translation: To Lomieto Ancondo, son of Abruni. Claudia Aravica.
EDCS-07100096.summary: This is a funerary inscription mentioning Lomieto Ancondo, son of Abruni, and Claudia Aravica.

EDCS-07100098.translation: Rucretianus had this made for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-07100098.summary: This inscription records that Rucretianus commissioned a monument for his wife.

EDCS-07100099.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription mentioning Tiberina, identified as an aunt.

EDCS-07100100.translation: to his son V F Q Vegetus, aged 56. Sempronius.
