EDCS-07100100.summary: This is a funerary inscription mentioning Sempronius dedicating a monument to his son V F Q Vegetus, who died at 56.

EDCS-07100116.translation: To the Divine Manes of Aurelius Paterno Caesariano. He lived for...
EDCS-07100116.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Paterno Caesariano, whose lifespan is incomplete.

EDCS-07100117.translation: Caelius Ianuario erected this monument for her husband Morinis, whom she survived, aged 42. To the Divine Manes.
EDCS-07100117.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Caelius Ianuario for her husband Morinis, who died at the age of 42.

EDCS-07100118.translation: To the Divine Manes of Didius Hermodorus. To his most devoted husband, aged 34.
EDCS-07100118.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Didius Hermodorus, dedicated to her most devoted husband who died at the age of 34.

EDCS-07100121.translation: A monument to the Infernal Deities, to Quintus P. S. Flaccus, his most devoted husband.
EDCS-07100121.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to a devoted husband, Quintus P. S. Flaccus.

EDCS-07100122.translation: Rufinus Beibalus, aged 45, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you, Rufina.
EDCS-07100122.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Rufinus Beibalus, aged 45, with a wish for peace for Rufina.

EDCS-07100123.translation: To the Deities of the Underworld. Iulius Capito, a soldier of the 1st Gallic Cohort, aged 33, with 28 years of service in the century of Valerius Caelianus, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Laelius Decuminus, a fellow soldier and heir, had this made.
EDCS-07100123.summary: This funerary inscription honors Iulius Capito, a soldier with 28 years of service, and was erected by his fellow soldier and heir.

EDCS-07100124.translation: [...] of 28 years of service, son of Boutius, [is here].
EDCS-07100124.summary: This fragment likely records military service details, possibly for a soldier named Boutius.

EDCS-07100125.translation: To Lucius Calpurnius Flaccus. Calpurnius Paternus and Calpurnius Proculus [dedicated this] to Lemuria.
EDCS-07100125.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication to Lucius Calpurnius Flaccus by Calpurnius Paternus and Calpurnius Proculus, possibly in relation to a woman named Lemuria.

EDCS-07100126.translation: Albinus, son of Alburius Cilinus, aged 60, is buried here.
EDCS-07100126.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Albinus, son of Alburius Cilinus, who was 60 years old.

EDCS-07100128.translation: Lubaecus Cabruleicus, slave of Nomia, and Longinus Lusitanus, aged 10, are buried here.
EDCS-07100128.summary: This epitaph records the burial of a slave named Lubaecus Cabruleicus and a child named Longinus Lusitanus, aged 10.

EDCS-07100129.translation: Memmius Perpetuus, son of Montanus, aged 15, is buried here.
EDCS-07100129.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Memmius Perpetuus, son of Montanus, who died at the age of 15.

EDCS-07100134.summary: This is a fragment of an epitaph indicating a lifespan of 10 years.

EDCS-07100135.translation: wife of Montanus. May the earth lie lightly on you, dearest. If age had conquered the children... and it was easy... from the pure heart of conjugal love the wife herself feels... the bed... the husband...
EDCS-07100135.summary: This is a fragmentary epitaph for the wife of Montanus, expressing deep affection and grief, possibly with wishes for her peace.

EDCS-07100136.translation: Felix lived 60 years. [This monument is] in the field of Lupianus.
EDCS-07100136.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Felix, dated by the mention of the owner of the land where the monument was placed.

EDCS-07100147.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. A monument for Alla, wife of Viancius, aged 20 years. Her mother Amma erected it.
EDCS-07100147.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Alla, erected by her mother.

EDCS-07100148.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Amparamus, son of Brigetinus.

EDCS-07100150.translation: Us Doiderus, son of Canus, Canives, son of Caldaecus, and Anna, daughter of Madugenus, took care to have this made.
EDCS-07100150.summary: This is a funerary inscription mentioning three individuals who commissioned its construction.

EDCS-07100171.translation: Araus, son of Salius, erected this monument for his son M. Io. Vemenocis. This is his tomb.
EDCS-07100171.summary: This is a funerary inscription for M. Io. Vemenocis, erected by his father.

EDCS-07100183.translation: Susicus took care to provide the title for the construction of the Seurrorum road to Placidius Intercisus.
EDCS-07100183.summary: This inscription records the provision of funds or resources by Susicus for the construction of a road overseen by Placidius Intercisus.

EDCS-07100184.translation: Conso and Marcus Eutropius erected an altar in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-07100184.summary: This is a dedication of an altar by Conso and Marcus Eutropius in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07100186.translation: During the reign of Emperor Titus Caesar Vespasianus Augustus, son of the divine Vespasian, pontifex maximus, in his 9th year of tribunician power, imperator 15 times, father of the country, consul 8 times, and Caesar Domitianus, son of the divine Vespasian, consul 7 times, a new road was made from Asturica to Bracara by Gaius Calpetanus Rantius Quirinalis Festus, imperial legate with praetorian power, covering 23 miles.
EDCS-07100186.summary: This milestone inscription records the construction of a new road between Asturica and Bracara, commissioned by the legate Gaius Calpetanus Rantius Quirinalis Festus during the reigns of Vespasian and Domitian.

EDCS-07400376.translation: Titus Sulpicius Rufus took care to have this made for Jupiter Optimus Maximus Rurifebus.
EDCS-07400376.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, commissioned by Titus Sulpicius Rufus.

EDCS-07400379.translation: Marcus Aemilius Lepidinus Porolo willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-07400379.summary: This inscription records Marcus Aemilius Lepidinus Porolo fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07400381.translation: Ulacius Sousenus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the household gods of Varigis.
EDCS-07400381.summary: This is a votive inscription from an individual named Ulacius Sousenus to the Lares of Varigis, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-07400382.translation: Fuscinus, son of Fuscus, willingly and with a free spirit fulfilled his vow to Debaroni Muceaigaego.
EDCS-07400382.summary: This votive inscription records Fuscinus, son of Fuscus, fulfilling a vow to an otherwise unidentified deity or spirit named Debaroni Muceaigaego.

EDCS-07400384.translation: Goilius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the goddesses Useis for Odinus.
EDCS-07400384.summary: This votive inscription indicates that Goilius offered a vow to the goddesses Useis on behalf of Odinus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07400385.translation: Sulpicius Coraecus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the household gods of Ormonufis.
EDCS-07400385.summary: This votive inscription records Sulpicius Coraecus fulfilling a vow to the Lares of Ormonufis, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07400386.translation: Ledrius willingly and with a free spirit fulfilled his vow for Claudio Nusio Ulci Cal.
EDCS-07400386.summary: This votive inscription records Ledrius fulfilling a vow on behalf of Claudio Nusio Ulci Cal, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-07400387.summary: This is a brief votive inscription by Flavariorus, indicating a vow fulfilled, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07400388.translation: Melvius Flavinus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Palmueno.
EDCS-07400388.summary: This votive inscription records Melvius Flavinus fulfilling a vow to Palmueno, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07400390.summary: This inscription records the name Virono and his age of 2 years, likely a tombstone from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07400391.translation: To the divine shades, Quadratus, son of Proculus, aged 50 years.
EDCS-07400391.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the Manes of Quadratus, son of Proculus, who died at the age of 50, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07400392.translation: To the divine shades, Placidus, son of Silon, aged 80 years.
EDCS-07400392.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the Manes of Placidus, son of Silon, who died at the age of 80, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07400393.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Flaccus, son of Quintus, aged 40. To the Divine Spirits of Alia, daughter of Muntanus, aged 50.
EDCS-07400393.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph for two individuals, Flaccus and Alia, detailing their names and ages.

EDCS-07400394.translation: To Dovius, son of Talavus, aged 1.
EDCS-07400394.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph for Dovius, noting his paternity and age.

EDCS-07400395.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph for Aemilius Reburrinus, indicating his age.

EDCS-07400396.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph for Faustus, though the text is highly abbreviated and damaged.

EDCS-07400405.translation: To Atta Capito, daughter of Capito, aged 25. To the Divine Spirits of Fronto, son of Capito, aged 10.
EDCS-07400405.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph for two individuals, Atta Capito and Fronto, specifying their names, parentage, and ages.

EDCS-07400406.translation: To Vironica, daughter of Seccus, aged [---].
EDCS-07400406.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph for Vironica, mentioning her paternity and age, though the age is incomplete.

EDCS-07400407.translation: To Severus, son of Severus, aged 22.
EDCS-07400407.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph for Severus, son of Severus, detailing his age.

EDCS-07400408.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Niger, son of Maro, aged 85. To the Divine Spirits of Nigrinus, son of Maro, aged 55.
EDCS-07400408.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph for two individuals, Niger and Nigrinus, both sons of Maro, noting their ages.

EDCS-07400411.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Annua, daughter of Flavius, aged 40.
EDCS-07400411.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph for Annua, daughter of Flavius, detailing her age.

EDCS-07400412.translation: To Marcus, son of Licinius, aged 40.
EDCS-07400412.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph for Marcus, son of Licinius, indicating his age.

EDCS-07400413.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Irnius.
EDCS-07400413.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Irnius, likely a deceased individual.

EDCS-07400418.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits: to Cornelia Flavinia, my most faithful wife, aged 35.
EDCS-07400418.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a husband to his deceased wife, Cornelia Flavinia, who died at age 35.

EDCS-07400419.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Placidina, who died at age 35.

EDCS-07400420.translation: To the Divine Spirits: to Flavinus, son of Flavius, aged 40.
EDCS-07400420.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Flavinus, son of Flavius, who died at age 40.

EDCS-07400421.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Flavinus.

EDCS-07400426.translation: Aged 70. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-07400426.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an individual who died at age 70, with a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-07400431.translation: To the Divine Spirits: Terentia Reburrina set this up for her most dutiful daughter, Aelia Crispina, aged 20.
EDCS-07400431.summary: This funerary dedication was set up by Terentia Reburrina for her daughter Aelia Crispina, who died at age 20.

EDCS-07400433.translation: Gaius Domitius Bassus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-07400433.summary: This inscription records Gaius Domitius Bassus fulfilling a vow, possibly to a deity.

EDCS-07400439.translation: To Atilius Castus, aged 4, [set up by] Aponia Paterna for her freedman.
EDCS-07400439.summary: This funerary dedication was set up by Aponia Paterna for her freedman, Atilius Castus, who died at age 4.

EDCS-07400442.translation: Flavia Acca, aged 60. Aelius Proculus arranged for this to be made for his wife.
EDCS-07400442.summary: This funerary monument was arranged by Aelius Proculus for his wife, Flavia Acca, who died at age 60.

EDCS-07400447.translation: May Salva Rusticola be happy; [and] Oborius.
EDCS-07400447.summary: A short funerary or dedicatory inscription mentioning two personal names, Salva Rusticola and Oborius.

EDCS-07400450.translation: Publius F. Pontius, L. Materna, and Lucius P. Licinianus gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-07400450.summary: An inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by three individuals, Publius F. Pontius, L. Materna, and Lucius P. Licinianus.

EDCS-07400465.translation: Publius F. Pontius, L. Materna, and Lucius P. Licinianus gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-07400465.summary: An inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by three individuals, Publius F. Pontius, L. Materna, and Lucius P. Licinianus.

EDCS-07400475.translation: They established the cult images of the gods in lofty temples and high seats on the mountains of Phrygia; Lucius Oculatius Rusticus and Annius Crescens, priest of Aesculapius of Ebusus, also wrote this, on the sixth day before the Kalends of April.
EDCS-07400475.summary: A dedication of cult images to deities in Phrygia by Lucius Oculatius Rusticus and the priest Annius Crescens, dated to March 27th.

EDCS-07400531.translation: Gerontus, may you live for us.
EDCS-07400531.summary: A short personal inscription wishing Gerontus a long life.

EDCS-07400534.translation: Marcus Flavius Moschus gladly and deservedly paid his vow to Neptune.
EDCS-07400534.summary: An inscription recording a vow paid to Neptune by Marcus Flavius Moschus.

EDCS-07400539.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Lovesius Placidus, a soldier of the VII Gemina Felix legion, in the century of Antonius Marcellus, from the voting tribe Sergia, aged 36, with 19 years of service, lies here. Lucius Julius Victor, a soldier of the same legion, in the same century, his heir, saw to this being made.
EDCS-07400539.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Lovesius Placidus, a soldier of the VII Gemina Felix legion, who died at age 36 after 19 years of service, with his heir Lucius Julius Victor arranging the burial.

EDCS-07600247.translation: Gaius Antius Paternus gladly and deservedly paid his vow to the Matres Useis at Aviliocum.
EDCS-07600247.summary: An inscription recording a vow paid to the Matres Useis by Gaius Antius Paternus at Aviliocum.

EDCS-07600264.summary: A fragment of an inscription giving the age and parentage of an individual.

EDCS-07600265.summary: A fragment of an inscription giving the age and parentage of an individual.

EDCS-07600268.translation: Se Kara, daughter of Flavius, lies here.
EDCS-07600268.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Se Kara, daughter of Flavius, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07600273.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sam aieci, son, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07600277.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely the name of an individual, possibly Licinius Cordus, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07600283.translation: Gudiltia lived 18 years and rested here in peace 15 days before the Kalends of May in the fifth year of the most glorious reign of our lord Sisenandus.
EDCS-07600283.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan and date of death of Gudiltia, dating to the reign of King Sisenandus in the 5th century CE.

EDCS-07600285.translation: Primula, aged 10, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-07600285.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a 10-year-old girl named Primula, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100576.summary: This inscription records that the individuals were slaves belonging to Apalius Cornelius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100577.summary: This inscription records that the individuals were slaves belonging to Atalius Cornelius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100578.summary: This inscription identifies Placidus as a servant, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08100592.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly funerary or dedicatory, mentioning Anthima and Rome, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200046.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription that begins with the name Lucius Cornelius, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200051.translation: To Lougan... Longo(?) ... of Aiae ... mother(?) ... from his/her own funds.
EDCS-08200051.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary or dedicatory inscription, possibly from an altar or tomb, with a mother mentioned as contributing from her own funds.

EDCS-08200135.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication to a person named Flaccilla.

EDCS-08200163.summary: This inscription is too fragmentary to determine its purpose, but it appears to contain personal names or titles.

EDCS-08200173.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely refers to female names or titles.

EDCS-08200183.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to contain parts of personal names and possibly the word 'father'.

EDCS-08200186.summary: This inscription records Fabricius Fabricius, who held the office of IIvir (a type of magistrate) and was possibly honored or identified at this location.

EDCS-08200188.summary: This is a funerary or commemorative inscription recording that Bergius Seranus served as aedile.

EDCS-08200189.summary: This is a concise inscription identifying Bergius Seranus as an aedile, likely on a building or monument he was associated with.

EDCS-08200191.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely contains personal names or titles, possibly including a cognomen and parts of other names.

EDCS-08200193.translation: Astuan, up to 20, OC the painter.
EDCS-08200193.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary marker or epitaph for Astuan, who reached the age of 20, and identifies him as a painter.

EDCS-08200197.summary: This inscription likely refers to a tombstone inscription for Hermes Aionis, a freedman or slave, possibly from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-08200202.summary: This very brief inscription likely marks the arrival or presence of Cuspius Priscus, possibly on a milestone or boundary marker.

EDCS-08200204.translation: Aemilius Firmus Quirinus, a man of 30(?), forgot the clay.
EDCS-08200204.summary: This inscription, possibly a funerary epitaph, names Aemilius Firmus Quirinus and mentions forgetting clay, perhaps related to a craftsman or potter, possibly from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-08200205.translation: Full inscription: Copoltustra? Annius Paternus, the wrestler, R.
EDCS-08200205.summary: This inscription, possibly a tombstone, names Annius Paternus, a wrestler, and might include a place name or further designation, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08200206.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records the name Lucius Domitius, possibly identifying his father or a cognomen, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08200209.summary: This inscription simply records the name Glauca Martiallis, possibly a funerary epitaph or a dedication.

EDCS-08200217.summary: This inscription records the full name Titus Cornelius Cantaber, possibly a freedman or belonging to a family with connections to the gens Cornelia, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08200224.summary: This fragmentary inscription mentions Glauca and suggests a restoration or a posthumous commemoration, possibly a funerary context.

EDCS-08300426.translation: To the departed spirits. Placidius Flaccus of Interamna, aged 60, is buried here.
EDCS-08300426.summary: This is a standard funerary epitaph for Placidius Flaccus of Interamna, who died at the age of 60, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08300429.translation: Fla, freedwoman, arranged for Arquiocus. Aemilius made for his mother.
EDCS-08300429.summary: This inscription records a funerary dedication made by Aemilius for his mother, and also mentions a freedwoman named Fla and someone named Arquiocus, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-08300431.translation: I wish to erect this for my most dutiful mother, Pompeia Antila.
EDCS-08300431.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Pompeia Antila, erected by her child.

EDCS-08300432.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Menas, slave of Claudia Quieta, for Claudia Quieta.
EDCS-08300432.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Claudia Quieta, made by her slave Menas.

EDCS-08300433.translation: Her sons had this made for Aemilia Buttola. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08300433.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Aemilia Buttola, made by her sons.

EDCS-08300434.translation: For Calvus, slave of the Aemilii, aged 65. Aemilia Arbuscula had this made from her own money. May the earth lie lightly and softly on you.
EDCS-08300434.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Calvus, a slave of the Aemilii family, paid for by Aemilia Arbuscula.

EDCS-08300436.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Lucius Paccius Proculus.

EDCS-08300438.translation: To Diana. Cassia Margarite fulfilled her vow, gladly, happily, deservedly.
EDCS-08300438.summary: This is a votive inscription to Diana, dedicated by Cassia Margarite.

EDCS-08300440.translation: To Diana Frugifera. Publius Tullius Paris, freedman, fulfilled his vow, gladly, deservedly.
EDCS-08300440.summary: This is a votive inscription to Diana Frugifera, dedicated by Publius Tullius Paris, a freedman.

EDCS-08500382.translation: To Alaecae Caemurula. Corunis fulfilled her vow, gladly, deservedly.
EDCS-08500382.summary: This is a votive inscription to Alaecae, dedicated by Corunis.

EDCS-08500388.translation: To our lord, forever victor and triumphant Augustus.
EDCS-08500388.summary: This is a dedication or acclamation to Emperor Augustus.

EDCS-08900430.translation: To our lord Valentinianus, forever victor and triumphant, perpetual Augustus, six miles.
EDCS-08900430.summary: This inscription records a distance of six miles dedicated to Emperor Valentinianus.

EDCS-08900435.translation: To our lord the Emperor Claudius Julianus, victor and triumphant, perpetual ever Augustus, year 32.
EDCS-08900435.summary: This is an inscription dedicated to Emperor Claudius Julianus, likely from the 4th century CE, indicating his titles and a regnal year.

EDCS-08900438.translation: Capito, son of Capito, willingly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-08900438.summary: This is a votive inscription by Capito to Jupiter, likely from a private individual in the Roman Empire.

EDCS-08900439.translation: Pedanius Maximus fulfilled his vow to the great god Laraucus willingly.
EDCS-08900439.summary: This is a votive inscription by Pedanius Maximus to a deity named Laraucus, likely from the Roman Empire.

EDCS-08900440.translation: Rufinus Caurunius willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08900440.summary: This is a votive inscription by Rufinus Caurunius, likely from a private individual in the Roman Empire.

EDCS-08900441.translation: Quintus Annius Modestus, a soldier of the VII Gemina Pia Felix legion, vowed an altar to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-08900441.summary: This is a votive inscription by a soldier named Quintus Annius Modestus of the VII Gemina legion, dedicating an altar to Jupiter.

EDCS-08900444.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely an epitaph or dedication, to Camillius Macrinus.

EDCS-08900446.translation: Victorius, aged 31. His mother, ICI O.
EDCS-08900446.summary: This appears to be a funerary inscription for Victorius, aged 31, mentioning his mother.

EDCS-08900451.translation: Lucius Lucilius Secundus Termestinus gave tools to Lucius Calisdaus from Uxama.
EDCS-08900451.summary: This inscription records a gift of tools from Lucius Lucilius Secundus Termestinus to Lucius Calisdaus of Uxama.

EDCS-08901015.translation: Sulpicius Sulpicianus for what he vowed to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-08901015.summary: This is a votive inscription by Sulpicius Sulpicianus to Jupiter, possibly commemorating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-08901025.translation: Let the magistrates and judges present at the time be judges for the Senate of Contrebia regarding the land which the people of Sallvia bought from the people of Sosinesta for the purpose of making a channel or conducting water; and if it appears that the people of Sosinesta sold the land in question to the people of Sallvia according to their rights, let the judges judge that the people of Sosinesta sold it according to their rights; and if it does not appear so, let them judge that they did not sell it according to their rights. The same individuals shall be judges if the territory were of the Sosinestan state, and if the people of Sallvia recently publicly marked boundaries on the public land of the Sosinestans, and if it is permitted for the people of Sallvia to make a channel through the public land of Sosinesta according to their rights, or if it is necessary to make a channel through private land of Sosinesta, let them make a channel according to their rights, provided that the people of Sallvia pay the purchase price for the land through which the channel is to be made. And if it appears so, let the judges judge that the people of Sallvia are permitted to make a channel according to their rights; and if it does not appear so, let them judge that they are not permitted to make a channel according to their rights. If they judge that the people of Sallvia are permitted to make a channel, then the people of Sallvia shall publicly pay money for the private land through which the channel is to be made, according to the arbitration of those magistrates of Contrebia, and of any others from their Senate, whom they shall appoint. The judgment was added by Caius Valerius Flaccus, son of Caius, imperator. Our judgment is that we judge in favor of the people of Sallvia regarding this matter, since this case has been judged. The magistrates of Contrebia at this time were: Lubbus, son of Urdinocum, son of Letondonis, praetor; Lesso, son of Siriscum, son of Lubbus, magistrate; Babbus, son of Bolgondiscum, son of Ablo, magistrate; Segilus, son of Annicum, son of Lubbus, magistrate; Satuso, son of Ulovicum, son of Uxe ti, magistrate; Ablo, son of Tindilicus, son of Lubbus, magistrate. Assius, son of Eihar, of Sallvia, defended the case of the people of Sallvia. Turibas, son of Teitabas, of Allavona, defended the case of the people of Allavona. Acted at Contrebia Balaisca, on the Ides of May, in the consulship of Lucius Cornelius and Gnaeus Octavius.
EDCS-08901025.summary: This lengthy inscription is a legal judgment from Contrebia, dating to the consulship of Lucius Cornelius and Gnaeus Octavius (likely from the late Republic or early Empire), concerning a land dispute between the peoples of Sallvia and Sosinesta over water rights and the construction of a channel.

EDCS-08901027.summary: This inscription records the name of Gaius Publicius, possibly on a tombstone or dedicatory plaque.

EDCS-09001070.translation: Nectaris Galetes lies here in the place of the Pitermae.
EDCS-09001070.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Nectaris Galetes, indicating his burial place and connection to the Pitermae family or region.

EDCS-09001443.translation: An altar was placed for the Divine Manes by Flaviani Flavi.
EDCS-09001443.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar to the Divine Manes by Flaviani Flavi.

EDCS-09001444.translation: M placed an altar for the Household Gods.
EDCS-09001444.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar to the Household Gods by an individual identified only by the initial M.

EDCS-09001448.translation: Paulus Catueni placed this for Elanica, freedwoman, aged 10.
EDCS-09001448.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Elanica, a freedwoman aged 10, dedicated by Paulus Catueni.

EDCS-09001449.summary: This inscription is a dedication, likely funerary, for Cadarna, daughter of Elaesus.

EDCS-09001455.translation: Quintus Caecilius F made this for Marcus Popilius Celsinus, a decurion, his well-deserving friend, for the Divine Manes.
EDCS-09001455.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Marcus Popilius Celsinus, a decurion and friend, made by Quintus Caecilius F.

EDCS-09001459.translation: Porcianus, aged 8, is buried here; his father Firmillus and grandmother Maurilla had this made while alive.
EDCS-09001459.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Porcianus, aged 8, and notes that his parents commissioned the monument during their lifetime.

EDCS-09100018.translation: For Gaius Valens Hostilianus Messius Quintus, most noble Caesar, prince of youth, the Valentini veterans and the veterans (dedicated this).
EDCS-09100018.summary: This is a dedication to Gaius Valens Hostilianus Messius Quintus, who held the title 'prince of youth', made by veterans.

EDCS-09100049.summary: This inscription records the names Gnaeus Ba Herma, likely on a tombstone or dedicatory object.

EDCS-09100099.translation: Pius, outstanding, eager, eloquent Justinian, a celibate pontiff and priest, built new temples and restored and adorned old ones, preaching festivals with his words among the people, instructing virgins, and governing monks. He wrote many things that would benefit future generations. Here, with a marvelous fortification, he enclosed an island of the sea, where, with the surrounding sea waves breaking the rock, he found sweet water. Here he left the glorious martyr of Christ, Vincent, whom he served with a most pious devotion while alive, as his heir when dying. He is to be counted among the saints, having served as a bishop for eleven times five years and four years and four months.
EDCS-09100099.summary: This inscription describes the pious deeds and burial of Justinian, a pontiff and priest, who built and restored temples, instructed religious communities, and founded a monastery on an island, leaving the martyr Vincent as his heir.

EDCS-09100147.translation: Ambatus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-09100147.summary: This is a votive inscription by Ambatus, likely dedicating a monument or offering.

EDCS-09100168.translation: Lucius Valerius, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe.
EDCS-09100168.summary: This is a Roman citizen's funerary or dedication inscription, identifying Lucius Valerius by his name, filiation, and tribe.

EDCS-09100204.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary epitaph, listing names and an age.

EDCS-09100205.translation: The freedwoman of Caius, aged 16. Here lies the freedman of Marcus, aged 60.
EDCS-09100205.summary: This inscription is a double epitaph for a freedwoman and a freedman, identified by their former patrons and ages.

EDCS-09100220.translation: Marcus Valerius Poliantus, aged 90. Quintia, aged 70.
EDCS-09100220.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Valerius Poliantus and Quintia, providing their ages.

EDCS-09100221.translation: To Didia, daughter of Marcus, most dutiful daughter, aged 25. Crescens.
EDCS-09100221.summary: This inscription is an epitaph for Didia, daughter of Marcus, who died at 25, set up by Crescens.

EDCS-09100224.translation: Marcus Didius Crispinus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, for himself and his son.
EDCS-09100224.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Marcus Didius Crispinus, son of Lucius, for himself and his son.

EDCS-09100225.translation: To Lucius Valerius Tempstivus. Pompeia Paterna, for her husband and herself.
EDCS-09100225.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Pompeia Paterna to her husband, Lucius Valerius Tempestivus, and also for herself.

EDCS-09100228.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Atilia Celsilla, aged 6. Otacilia Celsilla, her most deserving mother.
EDCS-09100228.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph for the young Atilia Celsilla, aged 6, set up by her mother, Otacilia Celsilla.

EDCS-09100230.translation: Marcus Clodius, son of Caius, [erected this monument].
EDCS-09100230.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Marcus Clodius, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09100272.translation: Herennia Villana, daughter of Lucius, aged 50, is laid here.
EDCS-09100272.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Herennia Villana, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09100274.translation: Lucius Lucretius Paternus, son of Caius, aged 50, is laid here.
EDCS-09100274.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Lucretius Paternus, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09100332.translation: Fabius Isidorus, freedman of Marcus, took care of this.
EDCS-09100332.summary: This funerary inscription indicates that Fabius Isidorus, a freedman, arranged for the burial, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09100333.translation: Lucius Manlius Fabianus, son of Caius, duovir, [paid] from his own money.
EDCS-09100333.summary: This inscription records Lucius Manlius Fabianus, who held the office of duovir, funding a public work from his personal funds, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09100371.translation: Gaius, son of [---]ius, Cinnamus, Probus, Dionysius, January, Verus.
EDCS-09100371.summary: This inscription appears to be a list of names, possibly of individuals associated with a collegium or a public commission, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09100372.translation: Lucius Terentius Fraternus [erected this] for his relative Antonia Sergilla, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-09100372.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Lucius Terentius Fraternus for his relative Antonia Sergilla, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09100373.translation: Lucius Iulius Actius and Porcia Melete [set this up for] Sergia Peregrina, daughter of Marcus.
EDCS-09100373.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Lucius Iulius Actius and Porcia Melete for Sergia Peregrina, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09100374.translation: Marcus Sergius Vetto [set this up for] his aunt Sergia Peregrina, daughter of Marcus.
EDCS-09100374.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Marcus Sergius Vetto for his aunt Sergia Peregrina, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09100375.translation: Marcus Sergius T. and Sergia Sergilla [set this up for] Lucius Antonius, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, from Numidia, prefect of the engineers, tribune of the soldiers of the first Italian legion.
EDCS-09100375.summary: This funerary inscription honors Lucius Antonius, a military officer of high rank, and was erected by Marcus Sergius T. and Sergia Sergilla, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09100376.translation: To Antonia Sergilla, daughter of Lucius. Vegetus, freedman.
EDCS-09100376.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Antonia Sergilla by her freedman Vegetus.

EDCS-09100377.translation: To Antonia Sergilla, daughter of Lucius. Valeria Propinqua.
EDCS-09100377.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Antonia Sergilla by Valeria Propinqua.

EDCS-09100378.translation: To Perigrina, daughter of Marcus Sergia. Theomnestus, Lais, and Didyme, freedmen.
EDCS-09100378.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Peregrina, daughter of Marcus Sergia, by her freedmen Theomnestus, Lais, and Didyme.

EDCS-09100379.translation: To Perigrina, daughter of Marcus Sergia. Theomnestus, Lais, and Didyme, freedmen.
EDCS-09100379.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Peregrina, daughter of Marcus Sergia, by her freedmen Theomnestus, Lais, and Didyme.

EDCS-09100380.translation: To Lucius Antonius, son of Lucius, of the Galerian tribe, formerly prefect of the army engineers, tribune of the soldiers of the First Italian Legion. Lucius Rubrius Polybius, a friend.
EDCS-09100380.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Antonius, son of Lucius, a man who held various military and civil offices, by his friend Lucius Rubrius Polybius.

EDCS-09100384.translation: To Lucius Aemilius Veranus, son of Lucius, of the Galerian tribe, formerly aedile, twice a city official, flamen, and quaestor.
EDCS-09100384.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Aemilius Veranus, son of Lucius, who held multiple civic and religious offices.

EDCS-09100400.translation: To Voconius Romanus, his excellent father.
EDCS-09100400.summary: This is a dedication to Voconius Romanus by his son.

EDCS-09100401.translation: To Rectina, daughter of Lucius Popillia, aged 18. Caius Licinius Marinus, son of Caius, of the Galerian tribe, and Voconius Romanus, to his wife.
EDCS-09100401.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Caius Licinius Marinus and Voconius Romanus to his wife Rectina, daughter of Lucius Popillia, who died at age 18.

EDCS-09100412.translation: Publicia Sacerdos, freedwoman of the Saguntines. Publicia Acirtilla, freedwoman of the municipality of Saguntum, aged 30.
EDCS-09100412.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publicia Acirtilla, a freedwoman of Saguntum, aged 30, possibly honored by Publicia Sacerdos.

EDCS-09100436.translation: Lucius Annius Odephorus, Manlia Procula, daughter of Gnaeus. Gnaeus Manlius Auctus.
EDCS-09100436.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary dedication involving Lucius Annius Odephorus and Manlia Procula, daughter of Gnaeus, with Gnaeus Manlius Auctus also mentioned.

EDCS-09100456.summary: This funerary inscription records the age and name of Baebia, likely erected by Montanus.

EDCS-09100483.translation: Herus, her heir, set up [this monument] for Cornelia Symmache.
EDCS-09100483.summary: This funerary inscription records that Herus, the heir of Cornelia Symmache, erected this monument for her.

EDCS-09100491.summary: This inscription simply records the name Quintus Fabius Dionysius, likely a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-09100525.summary: This funerary inscription records the age and name of Quintus Licinius.

EDCS-09100526.translation: Quintus Licinius Primus, a centurion of the first cohort of the Praetorian Guard.
EDCS-09100526.summary: This inscription identifies Quintus Licinius Primus, likely a military dedication or tombstone, possibly from the early imperial period given the Praetorian Guard context.

EDCS-09100528.translation: To Lucius, freedman of O.R. [erected this].
EDCS-09100528.summary: This inscription records the name of Lucius, a freedman, and likely indicates a dedication or tombstone.

EDCS-09100530.summary: This inscription is a dedication or funerary marker for Lucretia of Illipens.

EDCS-09100561.translation: Iphitus Asiaticus, freedman of ThrHacius; Herma, Horus, Iuvenis, Secundus, Dorotice, Dynamis, Myrsine, Nardis, Astice, Nephele.
EDCS-09100561.summary: This inscription lists several names, including Iphitus Asiaticus who was a freedman, likely a funerary monument or a list of dedicants.

EDCS-09100569.translation: To Marcus Valerius Victorinus, son of Marcus, [erected by] Septidius Valerinus, his best friend.
EDCS-09100569.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication from Septidius Valerinus to his friend Marcus Valerius Victorinus.

EDCS-09100577.summary: This funerary inscription records the age and name of Quinti, son of Canus.

EDCS-09100631.summary: This is a funerary inscription that records the name of Lucius Valerius.

EDCS-09100638.translation: To the spirits of the Underworld. Publius Caecilius Rufus and his wife Valeria, while alive, purchased this place, which is vaulted with walls, baths, and gardens, and constructed a monument for themselves and their daughters, until they might be mingled with their ancestral ashes.
EDCS-09100638.summary: This is a funerary monument inscription from the 1st or 2nd century CE, detailing the purchase and construction of a family tomb by Publius Caecilius Rufus and his wife Valeria.

EDCS-09100656.summary: This is a funerary inscription that records the name of Gnaeus Cornelius Victor.

EDCS-09100665.translation: Lucius Valerius Muntanus Tarbellus, a Signanus from Narbonensis, served four times.
EDCS-09100665.summary: This is an inscription honoring Lucius Valerius Muntanus Tarbellus, a soldier from Narbonensis, possibly indicating military service or a rank held four times.

EDCS-09100686.translation: This monument will not pass to an heir of Publius Popillius Hebdomus, freedman of Publius, and Placida, freedwoman of Cornelia Decimus.
EDCS-09100686.summary: This funerary inscription specifies that the monument will not pass to an heir, indicating a desire for specific burial arrangements for Publius Popillius Hebdomus and Placida.

EDCS-09100791.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age of Aglao.

EDCS-09100794.translation: May Christ bless the life of Teudered and Deudates.
EDCS-09100794.summary: This is a funerary inscription from the 6th century CE, invoking a Christian blessing on the lives of Teudered and Deudates.

EDCS-09100837.translation: To Marcus Aelius, son of Marcus, Galeria Gracilis, quaestor, legate of the deified Augustus, patron of Dertosani.
EDCS-09100837.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated to Marcus Aelius, detailing his career as quaestor, legate, and patron.

EDCS-09100842.translation: Marcus Porcius Theopompus, Augustalis Seviri, holding the rank of aedile in perpetuity.
EDCS-09100842.summary: This is an inscription honoring Marcus Porcius Theopompus, who held the prestigious religious and civic office of Augustalis Seviri and also the perpetual jurisdiction of an aedile.

EDCS-09100853.translation: (In Aramaic) Peace upon Israel, this is the resting place of Meliosa daughter of Iudanti and Kyra Maria. Remembered for a blessing. Her rest is for life eternal. In the Book of Life. From this (comes) peace. (In Greek) In the name of the Lord, here is the memorial where rests the much-remembered Meliosa, daughter of Iudantius and Kyra Mares, who lived twenty-four years, in peace. Amen.
EDCS-09100853.summary: This is a bilingual funerary inscription in Aramaic and Greek, dating to the 6th century CE, commemorating Meliosa and her mother Kyra Maria, and invoking peace and eternal life.

EDCS-09200320.translation: To Arco Betunus, wife of Caburancus, aged 50.
EDCS-09200320.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a woman named Arco Betunus, identified by her age and relationship to Caburancus.

EDCS-09201495.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Senucondius Secundus, son of Senocondius, as father, and Primulus, brother, [erected this].
EDCS-09201495.summary: A funerary epitaph where Senucondius Secundus and his brother Primulus set up a monument to the departed spirits, likely for a deceased family member.

EDCS-09300272.translation: Clarinus Celticus Supertamaricus, son of Clarus, aged 6, is buried here; may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-09300272.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a child named Clarinus Celticus Supertamaricus, specifying his age and including a common funerary wish.

EDCS-09300275.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Luria Victorina had this made for her most dutiful husband, Flavius Tertullus, aged 45. Sacred to the spirits of the departed.
EDCS-09300275.summary: A funerary epitaph where Luria Victorina dedicated a monument to her husband Flavius Tertullus, noting his age and her devotion.

EDCS-09300276.translation: To the spirits of the departed and to Annia Montana, aged 30. Her mother Attia Caenia had this made; may the earth rest lightly upon you. To the spirits of the departed and to Annius Fronto, aged 31. His mother Attia Caemia had this made; may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-09300276.summary: This inscription records two separate funerary monuments erected by Attia Caenia and Attia Caemia for their respective children, Annia Montana and Annius Fronto, both aged 30 or 31.

EDCS-09300286.translation: Penius Festus, son of Doiderius, from Vadinia, aged 20, is buried here, [placed by] friends because of his merits.
EDCS-09300286.summary: A funerary inscription for Penius Festus, noting his age, origin, and that friends erected the monument due to his merits.

EDCS-09300287.translation: To Balaesus Fronto, his friend, aged [..], is in this place.
EDCS-09300287.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a friend to Balaesus Fronto, indicating the deceased's location and the friend's affection.

EDCS-09300291.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian, son of the deified Trajan the Parthian, grandson of the deified Nerva.
EDCS-09300291.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Hadrian, listing his imperial titles and lineage, likely from a public monument.

EDCS-09300299.translation: Sacred to the gods. Areus E. Flaviaugustanus, a cavalryman of the 6th Legion, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-09300299.summary: A votive inscription by Areus E. Flaviaugustanus, a cavalryman of the 6th Legion, dedicated to the gods.

EDCS-09300302.summary: A simple dedication where Bodeius Carubilus commissioned a work, the nature of which is not specified.

EDCS-09300308.translation: Sacred to Vaelicus. Atta, daughter of Boutus, joyfully fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-09300308.summary: This inscription records Atta, daughter of Boutus, fulfilling a vow to the deity Vaelicus.

EDCS-09300309.translation: Culantius Pintolancus joyfully placed an altar to Vaelicus in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-09300309.summary: This inscription records Culantius Pintolancus dedicating an altar to Vaelicus as a fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-09300310.translation: Marcia Helene placed this [offering] for the god Vaelicus and joyfully fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-09300310.summary: This inscription records Marcia Helene dedicating an offering to the god Vaelicus and fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-09300311.translation: Mirtuo fulfilled his vow to the god Vaelicus.
EDCS-09300311.summary: This inscription records Mirtuo fulfilling a vow to the god Vaelicus.

EDCS-09300312.summary: This inscription records Sentia dedicating an altar to Vaelicus.

EDCS-09300313.translation: Joyfully fulfilled vow, [as promised] by vow.
EDCS-09300313.summary: This inscription records the joyful fulfillment of a vow to Vaelicus.

EDCS-09300314.summary: This inscription records Celer fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-09301027.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Amma placed [this] for Felicioni, her incomparable spouse, aged 50.
EDCS-09301027.summary: This funerary inscription records Amma dedicating a monument to her deceased husband, Felicioni, who was 50 years old.

EDCS-09301029.translation: The Madequenses erected [this] for Ladronus, son of Camalus, and for Antonius Almus.
EDCS-09301029.summary: This inscription records the Madequenses erecting a monument for Ladronus, son of Camalus, and Antonius Almus.

EDCS-09301037.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcia Paterna for Pompeius Festus, aged 20, her dear and most dutiful son, and for herself while still alive, with her own funds, in a half-share.
EDCS-09301037.summary: This funerary inscription records Marcia Paterna dedicating a monument for her deceased son Pompeius Festus (aged 20) and for herself, purchased with her own money.

EDCS-09301048.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Gaius Quintius, identifying him as a freedman.

EDCS-09301052.summary: This inscription records the age of a person named Aurelius, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-09301053.translation: Reburus, aged 60, to Casiena Reburus's daughter, aged 40
EDCS-09301053.summary: This inscription records the ages of two individuals, Reburus (60) and Casiena (40), likely a family tombstone.

EDCS-09301055.summary: This inscription records the age and parentage of Caeno, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-09301056.translation: To Saelius, son of Elaesus, aged 50
EDCS-09301056.summary: This inscription records the age and parentage of Saelius, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-09401090.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Aurelius to his dearest wife Festia Licinia Coelerna, aged 30; may you, who read this, say 'May the earth be light upon you.'
EDCS-09401090.summary: This is a tombstone inscription from Marcus Aurelius to his wife Festia Licinia Coelerna, who died at age 30, including a common epitaph.

EDCS-09401092.translation: I lie here. Together with me is buried Quinticus, who was dutiful and obedient to his father, and fortunate in his friends. Now we are covered by one tomb. May you who read this say 'May the earth be light upon you both.' Catura, his wife.
EDCS-09401092.summary: This is a tombstone inscription from Catura for herself and her husband Quinticus, expressing their relationship and wishing peace for the deceased.

EDCS-09401093.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, the Repeller [of evil]. Domitius Peregrinus, a veteran of the VII Gemina Pia Felix legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09401093.summary: This is a votive inscription by Domitius Peregrinus, a veteran, to Jupiter.

EDCS-09401095.translation: To the household gods. Petronius, according to his vow.
EDCS-09401095.summary: This is a votive inscription by Petronius to the household gods (Lares Compitales).

EDCS-09401096.translation: Fabius to his mother Sempronia Flavina
EDCS-09401096.summary: This inscription records a dedication from Fabius to his mother Sempronia Flavina.

EDCS-09401097.translation: To the most noble Caesar Marcus Numerius Numerianus.
EDCS-09401097.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Caesar Marcus Numerius Numerianus, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09401435.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Epaphroditus, aged 32, dutiful to his own people. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09401435.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Epaphroditus, who died at the age of 32, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09401449.translation: Maternus placed this willingly for the household gods of the street.
EDCS-09401449.summary: This is a votive dedication to the Laribus Vialibus (household gods of the street) by Maternus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09401450.translation: Ulpius Flavus paid his vow to the sea gods.
EDCS-09401450.summary: This is a votive inscription by Ulpius Flavus to the marine gods, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09401451.translation: Cornelius Vitalis promised a vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-09401451.summary: This is a votive inscription where Cornelius Vitalis made a promise to Jupiter, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09401452.translation: Ama Pitili, daughter of Laro, willingly made her vow for her husband.
EDCS-09401452.summary: This is a votive inscription by Ama Pitili for her husband, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09401453.translation: Cornelianus, a freedman, willingly paid his vow to the Veianian Mothers and Ibanduiocolis, deservedly.
EDCS-09401453.summary: This is a votive inscription by Cornelianus, a freedman, to the Matres Veianiae and Ibanduiocolis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09401454.translation: For Quintus Licinius Vegetus, the public body of Interamna, having secured the accounts equal to the treasury through his tireless care and industry, on account of many other merits.
EDCS-09401454.summary: This inscription honors Quintus Licinius Vegetus for his diligent financial management benefiting the community of Interamna, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09401455.translation: Flavius Flavianus paid his vow to the Torian Gombicius willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09401455.summary: This is a votive inscription by Flavius Flavianus to the deity Torius Gombicius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09401456.translation: Ladronius, son of Avitis, paid his vow to the ancestral guardian spirit; I ask that you be favorable.
EDCS-09401456.summary: This is a votive inscription by Ladronius Avitis to his ancestral Lar (guardian spirit), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09401457.translation: I consecrate all vows to the storm-god Danigus, and to Nabiae Coronae a cow and a ram, to Jupiter a ram, to Borgo a fattened bullock, to Ida a garland, completed in the year and house of the consulship of Largo and Messallinus, on the 5th day before the Ides of April, under the curators Lucretius Vitullinus, Lucretius Sabinus, and Postumus Peregrinus.
EDCS-09401457.summary: This is a votive inscription from 22 AD, detailing a series of sacrifices and dedications to various deities, including Jupiter, and mentioning consuls and curators.

EDCS-09401458.translation: Bloena fulfilled her vow to the Lar of Buricus.
EDCS-09401458.summary: This is a short votive inscription recording Bloena fulfilling a vow to the household gods (Lares) associated with Buricus.

EDCS-09401459.translation: Medamus fulfilled his vow to Jupiter.
EDCS-09401459.summary: This is a brief votive inscription indicating that Medamus fulfilled a vow made to Jupiter.

EDCS-09500001.translation: To the most noble Caesar, Consul II, Father of the Fatherland, Proconsul, 22 miles.
EDCS-09500001.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or milestone marking a distance of 22 miles, honoring a Caesar with multiple titles.

EDCS-09500002.translation: To the most noble Caesar Gaius Galerius Maximianus, the Unconquered Augustus, Consul, born for the good of the state.
EDCS-09500002.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated to Emperor Galerius, styling him as Caesar, Augustus, and consul, emphasizing his virtues and suitability to rule.

EDCS-09700176.translation: Quintus Mineius Facundus willingly fulfilled his vow to Ilubeda.
EDCS-09700176.summary: This inscription records Quintus Mineius Facundus willingly dedicating something to the deity Ilubeda.

EDCS-09700618.translation: Sextus Si dedicated [this] to Jupiter Best and Greatest, according to his vow.
EDCS-09700618.summary: This is a votive inscription where Sextus Si dedicates something to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-09800180.translation: Lucius Aurelius Fr willingly and deservedly placed this altar to the god and lord Cossue Segidiaecus, according to his vow.
EDCS-09800180.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius Aurelius Fr dedicating an altar to the deity Cossue Segidiaecus.

EDCS-09800181.translation: Flavinus Flavi placed an altar for Cossue Nidoledius.
EDCS-09800181.summary: This inscription records Flavinus Flavi dedicating an altar to the deity Cossue Nidoledius.

EDCS-09800182.translation: Octavia Iulia, aged 70 years, and Anius Silv..., aged 18 years, are buried here. Octavia.
EDCS-09800182.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Octavia Iulia and Anius Silv... with their ages.

EDCS-09800185.translation: To the Pious and Fortunate Augustus, son of Severus Augustus, holder of tribunician power for the 5th time, consul for the 2nd time, proconsul; the Ala II Flavia Hispanorum, Roman citizens, devoted to his divine will, decreed that a statue, erected by a communal fund, should be made, and they ordered it to be constructed at their own expense by their Invictus Augustus.
EDCS-09800185.summary: This inscription records a dedication of a statue to Emperor Severus Alexander by the Ala II Flavia Hispanorum, likely dating to his early reign (c. 222-235 AD).

EDCS-09800186.translation: To the Spirits of the departed. Amantius, may your own (things) be spared. If you live, may you prosper.
EDCS-09800186.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Amantius, intended for his tomb.

EDCS-09800189.translation: To the August Springs of Camenae. Gaius Ranius Sabinus, legate of Augustus.
EDCS-09800189.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a spring or bath complex by Gaius Ranius Sabinus, a Roman legate, likely in the imperial period.

EDCS-10200001.translation: Aphrodisia, aged 55, is placed here. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-10200001.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aphrodisia, a woman of 55 years.

EDCS-10200002.translation: Antelus for himself and his wife and...
EDCS-10200002.summary: A funerary inscription begun by Antelus for himself and his wife, likely incomplete.

EDCS-10200005.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the departed. Titus Meduttius Philetus, aged 51, Marcus Meduttius Fuscus, Valeria Probata, and Granius Exochus made (this) for their father out of their indulgence. May the earth be light on you. He is buried here.
EDCS-10200005.summary: A funerary monument erected by children (Marcus Meduttius Fuscus, Valeria Probata, Granius Exochus) for their deceased father (Titus Meduttius Philetus) and possibly other family members, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10200006.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the departed. Marcus Meduttius Fuscus, aged 45, pious in his duties, is buried here. May the earth be light on you. Valeria Probata placed (this) in memory of one who did not deserve death. Farewell, hail, greetings.
EDCS-10200006.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Meduttius Fuscus, erected by Valeria Probata, likely his wife or relative, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10200007.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the departed. Gaius Sempronius Abascantus, aged 40, is buried here. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-10200007.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Sempronius Abascantus, a member of a sodality, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10200008.translation: Valeria Graeca, daughter of Serapeis, aged 30.
EDCS-10200008.summary: A brief funerary epitaph identifying Valeria Graeca, daughter of Serapeis, and her age.

EDCS-10200010.translation: Sacred to August Health. Marcus Ulpius Hermeros, freedman of Augustus, fulfilled his vow willingly with his heart.
EDCS-10200010.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to the goddess Salus Augusta (Health) by Marcus Ulpius Hermeros, a freedman of an emperor, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10200011.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Paternus, his brother Orgenomescus, erected this for him, aged 20.
EDCS-10200011.summary: A funerary dedication by a brother for Paternus, who died at age 20.

EDCS-10200012.translation: Titus Pompeius Fraternus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, from Clunia, aged 15, lies here. I beg you, passerby, as you go, to say: May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10200012.summary: A tombstone for Titus Pompeius Fraternus, aged 15, with a standard epitaph and plea to the passerby.

EDCS-10200019.translation: Flavia Sperata dedicated this gift from her own funds to Lucius Cornelius Agricola, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, a local magistrate (IIvir) of the municipality of Castulo, and priest of Rome and Augustus.
EDCS-10200019.summary: A dedication of a statue by Flavia Sperata to Lucius Cornelius Agricola, a local magistrate and priest, likely in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10200021.translation: Cornelia Marullina had decreed that a statue be erected to Lucius Cornelius Marullus and his son, because the council of Castulonenses honored him for the generosity of Cornelia Marullina, his mother, who had adorned the citizenship of Castulo with silver statues, a feast, and games; having accepted the honor, she ordered this gift to be set up from her own funds. Her heir, Gaius Cornelius Bellicus, dedicated and erected this monument after games had been held.
EDCS-10200021.summary: A dedication by Cornelia Marullina, through her heir Gaius Cornelius Bellicus, honoring her son Lucius Cornelius Marullus and grandson for civic benefactions, likely in the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10200022.translation: In honor of Publius Valerius Valerianus Flaccus, son of Publius, of the Galeria tribe; Publius Valerius Carbo, his father, having received the honor, remitted the expenses.
EDCS-10200022.summary: A posthumous honor granted to Publius Valerius Valerianus Flaccus by the town council, with his father bearing the cost.

EDCS-10200023.translation: For Lucius Annius Capella of Segobriga.
EDCS-10200023.summary: A tombstone dedication for Lucius Annius Capella of Segobriga.

EDCS-10200024.translation: Stop, I beg you, passerby, and recognize this cippus. Antisporus, aged 8, dear to all, lies here. And I beg you to say: May the earth lie lightly on Antisporus.
EDCS-10200024.summary: A tombstone for Antisporus, who died at age 8, with a plea to the passerby to wish him well.

EDCS-10200025.translation: For Marcus Atilius, son of Buco, from Estuniga.
EDCS-10200025.summary: A tombstone dedication for Marcus Atilius, son of Buco, from Estuniga.

EDCS-10200026.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Atrius Chrestinus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, from Castulo, aged 38, who held all public offices, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10200026.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Atrius Chrestinus, a local official from Castulo who died at age 38.

EDCS-10200027.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Titus Atilius Paulinus, son of Sergia, aged 75, lies here for his most dutiful father, Titus Atilius Proculus, and for Paulina, his daughter. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10200027.summary: A funerary dedication by Titus Atilius Paulinus for his father Titus Atilius Proculus and his daughter Paulina.

EDCS-10200028.translation: Baebius Decentius Uccoritanus arranged for this to be made; he is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10200028.summary: This funerary inscription records that Baebius Decentius Uccoritanus commissioned his own burial monument and states a wish for his peaceful repose.

EDCS-10200029.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Claudius Severianus, aged 22 years and 15 days, pious among his own, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10200029.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Lucius Claudius Severianus, who died at the age of 22 years and 15 days, and expresses a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-10200030.translation: Claudia Syra, pious among her own, aged 20, you will give hospitality to our grief.
EDCS-10200030.summary: This funerary inscription likely laments the death of Claudia Syra, aged 20, and expresses the sorrow of those who leave her behind.

EDCS-10200033.translation: Cornelia, daughter of Quintus, called Moria.
EDCS-10200033.summary: This very brief inscription simply records the name of Cornelia, daughter of Quintus, with her cognomen Moria.

EDCS-10200035.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Minicius, aged 16 years, pious among his own, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10200035.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Minicius, who died at the age of 16, and expresses a wish for his peaceful repose.

EDCS-10200036.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Oratius Capito, son of Blatius, from Eburensis, aged 64 years, is buried here.
EDCS-10200036.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Lucius Oratius Capito, son of Blatius, from Eburensis, who died at the age of 64.

EDCS-10200038.summary: This inscription simply records the name Socedaiaunin and her parentage, daughter of Istamiuris.

EDCS-10200039.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Quintianus lived 5 years, 2 months, and 23 days. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10200039.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Quintianus, who died at the age of 5 years, 2 months, and 23 days, and expresses a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-10200047.summary: This inscription indicates that the person was the freedman of Cnaeus Iminius, possibly with an abbreviation for his name or origin.

EDCS-10200052.translation: Escossus, aged 7 years. Firmus H(ic) M(anus) S(it?).
EDCS-10200052.summary: This inscription records the death of Escossus, aged 7, and likely that Firmus arranged for his burial, with a wish for peaceful rest.

EDCS-10200053.translation: To the spirits of the departed, from Lucius, freedman, for his son.
EDCS-10200053.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a freedman for his son.

EDCS-10200055.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed, from Aurelius.
EDCS-10200055.summary: A funerary dedication by Aurelius to the departed spirits.

EDCS-10200076.translation: Publius Cornelius Spanus, son of Publius, aged [---] years. Publius Cornelius Sabinus, son of Publius.
EDCS-10200076.summary: A funerary inscription naming two individuals, likely father and son, with ages mentioned.

EDCS-10200077.translation: In the name of the Lord, Abba, though unworthy, made this and constructed two choirs here, and sacred rites were performed for the saints of God's church on the day before the Ides of May, in the year 729 of the era, in the fourth year of the reign of our glorious lord Egican.
EDCS-10200077.summary: A dedication for a church construction, mentioning religious figures and a specific date in the era of 729 during the reign of Egican.

EDCS-10200078.translation: To Cas, son of Publius Aelius Sservandus. To Ces, son of Publius Cornelius C. To Sab, son of Marcus Cornelius Scantianus. To Baist, son of Caius Celsus Sab. Lucius Valerius, son of Ild,edile and priest of the city and of the Augusti. To S S Ild, son of Caius Licinius Belnibarb. To Ild, son of Aulus Cornelius Belesticus. Publius Cornelius Suranna, THISS BROCTT HIN.
EDCS-10200078.summary: A complex inscription listing multiple individuals and their familial or official connections, possibly related to civic or religious offices in Rome.

EDCS-10200080.translation: Here he lies. May the earth lie lightly on you. Valens and Augurinus took care to make this.
EDCS-10200080.summary: A funerary epitaph stating that Valens and Augurinus arranged for the burial.

EDCS-10200083.translation: Pyrra, daughter of Thetis; Cyrce; Deidamia; Moedia. For all these are virgins who are daughters of the sun. For Lysides, son of Priam.
EDCS-10200083.summary: A poetic or philosophical inscription referencing several female names and their supposed connection to the sun, with a concluding mention of Lysides.

EDCS-10200086.translation: Aged 21 years. She lies here, pious, in her own [property]. May the earth lie lightly on you. Quintus Cornelius Silvanus and Festus, sons of V L, made this.
EDCS-10200086.summary: A funerary epitaph for a 21-year-old woman, stating that Quintus Cornelius Silvanus and Festus made the monument.

EDCS-10200097.translation: Claudia Phrugia, freedwoman of Caius, lies here. Greetings, farewell.
EDCS-10200097.summary: A funerary epitaph for Claudia Phrugia, a freedwoman, with standard greetings.

EDCS-10200098.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Crillus, aged 25 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-10200098.summary: A funerary epitaph for Crillus, aged 25, with a common epitaph formula.

EDCS-10200100.translation: Gemina, slave of Decimus Publicius Subicus, aged 25, is buried here. She died in childbirth. Caius Aerarius, freedman, placed this tombstone. If the gods below have any sense, you would have taken me by force. If you loved me, make them take me away. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10200100.summary: A tombstone for Gemina, a slave who died in childbirth, erected by her freedman lover Caius Aerarius, likely from the early imperial period.

EDCS-10200101.translation: To the Divine Manes. Laelia Rhodope, freedwoman of Caius Laelius Sedatus, from Salaria, aged 50, pious, is buried here in her own property.
EDCS-10200101.summary: A funerary epitaph for Laelia Rhodope, a freedwoman, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-10200105.translation: Fulvius, son of Lucius, from the Galerian tribe.
EDCS-10200105.summary: A fragmentary inscription identifying Fulvius, son of Lucius, and his tribal affiliation, likely a tombstone or dedication fragment from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10200107.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Aemilius Crescens, pious, aged 75, is buried here in his own property. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His associates dedicated this.
EDCS-10200107.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Aemilius Crescens, erected by his associates, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10200108.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Aemilius Orestinus, aged 55, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His associates dedicated this.
EDCS-10200108.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Aemilius Orestinus, erected by his associates, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10200111.translation: [...], aged 3, pious, is buried here in her own property.
EDCS-10200111.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl, identified only by a partial name and age, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10200115.translation: Quintus Cornelius Fortunatus, son of Lucius, from Nisa, aged 50, pious, is buried here in his own property. May the earth lie lightly upon you. To the Divine Manes. Say, 'May the earth lie lightly upon you,' whoever reads this tombstone.
EDCS-10200115.summary: A tombstone for Quintus Cornelius Fortunatus, a man of 50, likely from the Roman imperial period, requesting that readers invoke a blessing for him.

EDCS-10200116.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Cornelius Vetelus, aged 20, is here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Yarona dedicated this.
EDCS-10200116.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Cornelius Vetelus, aged 20, dedicated by Yarona, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10200117.translation: To the Divine Manes. Flacchinus, slave of Propinquus, aged 3, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10200117.summary: A tombstone for Flacchinus, a young slave, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10200118.translation: To the Divine Manes. Flaminius Felicio, aged 40, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. The collegium compitalicium (association of crossroads shrines) gave and dedicated this.
EDCS-10200118.summary: A tombstone for Flaminius Felicio, aged 40, dedicated by the collegium compitalicium, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10200119.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Flaminia Robia, aged 50, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10200119.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Flaminia Robia, aged 50.

EDCS-10200120.translation: To Quintus Fulvius Caledonnis, son of Sergius. Caius Flaminius erected this. 'ESCON' is likely an abbreviation for 'erexit', 'consul', or a similar phrase.
EDCS-10200120.summary: A funerary dedication by Caius Flaminius to Quintus Fulvius Caledonnis.

EDCS-10200137.translation: Hermocrates is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10200137.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Hermocrates.

EDCS-10200138.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Maura, aged 21, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10200138.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Maura, aged 21.

EDCS-10200139.translation: Pruciussa, aged 21, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10200139.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Pruciussa, aged 21.

EDCS-10200140.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Flavianus erected this for his deserving wife Secundae, aged 35. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10200140.summary: A funerary epitaph erected by Flavianus for his wife Secundae, aged 35.

EDCS-10200156.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Onesime, a servant girl, aged 23, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10200156.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Onesime, a servant girl, aged 23.

EDCS-10200158.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Laelia Graphie Rhodopis, freedwoman, aged 49, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Her husband [erected this] for her devotion.
EDCS-10200158.summary: A funerary epitaph for Laelia Graphie Rhodopis, a freedwoman aged 49, erected by her husband.

EDCS-10301562.translation: [Erected] by his sons at the request of Murinus, who bore public office with illustrious honor and great glory, [he] conceded [this space] with his own means, presided over by Gaius Filius.
EDCS-10301562.summary: A public dedication of space, possibly for burial or a monument, with contributions and oversight by multiple individuals including Murinus and Gaius Filius.

EDCS-10301571.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Agathenes, aged 28, is buried here. Mystes made this for his well-deserving spouse.
EDCS-10301571.summary: A funerary epitaph created by Mystes for his wife Agathenes, aged 28.

EDCS-10301579.translation: In the name of the Lord, Athanasius the bishop erected this altar in the seventh year of his consecration. Amen.
EDCS-10301579.summary: This inscription records the erection of an altar by Bishop Athanasius in his seventh year of consecration, likely dating to the Late Antique period.

EDCS-10301581.summary: This is a simple inscription identifying an individual named Marcus Aquillius Ason, possibly on a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-10301628.translation: A tablet of hospitality with Publius Turullius, son of Publius, of the voting tribe Maecia.
EDCS-10301628.summary: This inscription records a tessera hospitalis, a token of guest-friendship, between individuals or families, indicating a formal bond.

EDCS-10301645.translation: The vows which I, Flavus the master, when I was proceeding to the high places of Rome, offered up as a supplicant with a trembling mind, I now declare to you, victor and happy: to our patroness of innocence, do you only, I pray, accept with a favorable mind the offerings, the palm, the victim, which we present to you.
EDCS-10301645.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Flavus, possibly a master or official, to a deity or personified concept named 'Appenninae nostrae fautor innocentiae', likely made upon achieving success or reaching Rome.

EDCS-10301646.translation: Sempronius Betunus Selatse willingly paid his vow, as was deserved.
EDCS-10301646.summary: This inscription records a votive offering fulfilled by Sempronius Betunus Selatse, likely a personal dedication.

EDCS-10301647.translation: Iunius Germanus Selatse willingly paid his vow, as was deserved.
EDCS-10301647.summary: This inscription records a votive offering fulfilled by Iunius Germanus Selatse, likely a personal dedication.

EDCS-10301648.translation: Asclepius Paternus Selatse willingly paid his vow, as was deserved.
EDCS-10301648.summary: This inscription records a votive offering fulfilled by Asclepius Paternus Selatse, likely a personal dedication.

EDCS-10301651.translation: Aemilia Paterna Losae willingly paid her vow, as was deserved.
EDCS-10301651.summary: This inscription records a votive offering fulfilled by Aemilia Paterna Losae, likely a personal dedication.

EDCS-10301656.translation: Neria Helpis paid her vow to the Great Mother for the health of Coema.
EDCS-10301656.summary: This inscription records Neria Helpis fulfilling a vow to the Magna Mater (Great Mother) for the well-being of Coema.

EDCS-10301657.translation: Neria Helpis willingly paid her vow with a willing spirit to the Unconquered Sun for the health of Coema.
EDCS-10301657.summary: This inscription records Neria Helpis fulfilling a vow to the Unconquered Sun for the health of Coema, likely dating to a period when Sol Invictus was prominent.

EDCS-10301659.translation: Coelius Tesphoros and Festa and Telesinus sacred to Jupiter.
EDCS-10301659.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter by Coelius Tesphoros, Festa, and Telesinus.

EDCS-10301660.translation: Coelius Tesphoros and Festa and Telesinus, according to a vow.
EDCS-10301660.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Coelius Tesphoros, Festa, and Telesinus.

EDCS-10301661.translation: Oppia Coemia, daughter of Fronto, aged 60, and Vendius, son of Boutia, aged 20, arranged for this to be made; her father and mother arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-10301661.summary: This funerary inscription records that Oppia Coemia (60) and Vendius (20) had a monument made, with Oppia Boutia and her husband arranging for it.

EDCS-10301662.translation: Sempronianus placed this for his son Apronius, aged 37, for himself and his wife.
EDCS-10301662.summary: This inscription records Sempronianus setting up a monument for his son Apronius, and also for himself and his wife.

EDCS-10301666.translation: To Piculla, dearest sister, aged 25. Her sister Apruncula placed this.
EDCS-10301666.summary: This is a tombstone set up by Apruncula for her sister Piculla, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-10301667.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely identifying Domitia Semproniana, daughter of Maternus.

EDCS-10301669.translation: To the departed spirits, Valerius, aged 25.
EDCS-10301669.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the gods of the underworld for Valerius, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-10301670.translation: For Sempronius Flaccinus, aged 3. Peregrinus, his father, and Ambata, his mother, placed this. He lies here.
EDCS-10301670.summary: This funerary inscription records the parents Peregrinus and Ambata setting up a tomb for their son Sempronius Flaccinus, who died at age 3.

EDCS-10301671.translation: The woman Citastelule placed this monument for her husband Leucadius, aged 75.
EDCS-10301671.summary: This inscription records Citastelule setting up a monument for her husband Leucadius, who died at age 75.

EDCS-10301672.translation: Umme Sahari, son of Narhunges, placed this memorial at his own expense for his son Abisunhari, aged 25.
EDCS-10301672.summary: This funerary inscription records Umme Sahari setting up a monument for his son Abisunhari, aged 25.

EDCS-10301673.translation: To the divine spirits of Fortunatus.
EDCS-10301673.summary: This is a tomb inscription for a person named Fortunatus.

EDCS-10301675.translation: Doitena, daughter of Ambatus Celtus, had this made for her father-in-law Marcus Caecilius Flavius, aged 60, and for her husband Marcus Caecilius Flavius, aged 35.
EDCS-10301675.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Doitena for her father-in-law and husband.

EDCS-10301681.translation: Valeria Corneliana placed this here for her sister Ioviana and her daughter Vistena.
EDCS-10301681.summary: This inscription records the placement of a monument by Valeria Corneliana for her sister and daughter.

EDCS-10301682.translation: She made this for her husband Cornelius Felicionius.
EDCS-10301682.summary: This funerary inscription was made by a wife for her husband.

EDCS-10301686.translation: He erected this monument for his husband Iunius Licinus, for his son Iunius Licinianus, aged 32, and for himself.
EDCS-10301686.summary: This monument was erected by an individual for himself, his husband, and his son.

EDCS-10301687.translation: Octavia, daughter of Pudens, aged 30, is buried here.
EDCS-10301687.summary: This inscription records the burial of Octavia, daughter of Pudens.

EDCS-10301688.translation: Antonia Chrysaeis made this for Athenio, a public administrator.
EDCS-10301688.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Antonia Chrysaeis for Athenio, a public administrator.

EDCS-10301689.translation: For Lucius Caecilius, son of Lucius, Flavius Ca[---]
EDCS-10301689.summary: This is a fragmented inscription likely related to Lucius Caecilius.

EDCS-10301696.summary: This is a fragmented inscription recording the erection of something by an unknown person for individuals identified as 'La' and 'Cl'.

EDCS-10701764.translation: Maria Materna [placed this] for her father Marcus Marius Nepos, son of Marcus, twice duovir, flamen.
EDCS-10701764.summary: This inscription records Maria Materna dedicating a monument to her father, Marcus Marius Nepos, who held public offices.
