EDCS-10701765.translation: To Lucius Planius Russinus, son of Lucius.
EDCS-10701765.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Planius Russinus, son of Lucius.

EDCS-10701775.translation: Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, Germanicus Maximus, Dacicus Maximus, Sarmaticus Maximus, Pontifex Maximus, holder of tribunician power for the 5th time, Imperator for the 7th time, Father of the Fatherland, Consul, Proconsul, and Gaius Julius Felix Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, son of our Lord Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus, Germanicus Maximus, Dacicus Maximus, Sarmaticus Maximus, Princeps of Youth, son of Pius Felix Augustus, ordered the roads and bridges, collapsed by age, to be restored, with Quintus Decius, legatus of the Augusti, pro praetore, a most distinguished man, in charge of the work.
EDCS-10701775.summary: This inscription records the restoration of roads and bridges ordered by Emperors Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus Caesar, likely during their joint rule in the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10900851.translation: Severa, wife of Atilius Santono, son of Toutonis.
EDCS-10900851.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Severa to her husband, Atilius Santono.

EDCS-10900869.translation: Here rests Magnus, a faithful boy, in peace, who lived for 3 years.
EDCS-10900869.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young boy named Magnus who died at the age of three.

EDCS-10900871.translation: To our most invincible, most pious, most victorious lord, Princeps Augustus Julianus Augustus.
EDCS-10900871.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Julianus Augustus, likely from a provincial official or group.

EDCS-10900876.translation: Manius Sergius, son of Manius, proconsul, year I.
EDCS-10900876.summary: This inscription likely records the name and rank of Manius Sergius, a proconsul, possibly indicating the first year of his tenure or a date reference.

EDCS-11600328.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Herennius Optatus.

EDCS-11600367.summary: This is a dedication to Turia Marcella.

EDCS-11600371.translation: M. T. Dido and Seianus, fathers.
EDCS-11600371.summary: This inscription names two individuals, M. T. Dido and Seianus, identified as 'fathers', possibly in a familial or civic context.

EDCS-11600400.translation: Aulus C. A. I. S. C. A. M. XXXVIII.
EDCS-11600400.summary: This inscription appears to be an abbreviation or a coded message, possibly related to a person named Aulus C. A. I. S. C. A. and a numerical value of 38.

EDCS-11600413.translation: Honoratus, an honest man, dwelling with Christ, lived 20 years.
EDCS-11600413.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Honoratus, a Christian.

EDCS-11600414.summary: This inscription is a short Christian acclamation.

EDCS-11600420.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial place of Saenia Abra.

EDCS-11600436.translation: Severina lived 40 years and died in peace on the third day before the Ides of February.
EDCS-11600436.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan and death date of Severina.

EDCS-11700872.translation: Labo Paramigo, Cornelia Rufina, willingly and deservedly, from a vision.
EDCS-11700872.summary: This votive inscription records a dedication by Cornelia Rufina, possibly related to a vow or vision.

EDCS-11700877.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Valeria Thais placed this for her incomparable and well-deserving husband, Valerius Meleager, who lived 40 years.
EDCS-11700877.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication of a tomb by Valeria Thais for her husband Valerius Meleager.

EDCS-11700908.translation: To the most noble Caesar Gaius Galerius Maximianus.
EDCS-11700908.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Roman Emperor Galerius Maximianus.

EDCS-11700909.translation: To our Lord Valerius Licinius Licinianus the Unconquered.
EDCS-11700909.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Roman Emperor Licinius.

EDCS-11700910.translation: To Constantius, born for the republic.
EDCS-11700910.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Constantius, likely celebrating his role or birth in relation to the state.

EDCS-11700917.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carinus, Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered, Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holder of tribunician power, Father of the Fatherland, Consul, Proconsul.
EDCS-11700917.summary: This inscription is a full titulature inscription for the Roman Emperor Carinus.

EDCS-11700923.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the dead; Miro's wife placed this monument to Miro, aged 51. May the earth rest lightly upon him.
EDCS-11700923.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a wife for her deceased husband, Miro, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11700930.summary: This is a very brief funerary epitaph identifying the person buried.

EDCS-11700931.translation: The body of Ermengon rests here; she departed to the gods in the Kalends of May, era 662. Whoever reads this and with reckless audacity wishes to deposit another body in this place shall be anathema.
EDCS-11700931.summary: This is a funerary epitaph with a curse against unauthorized burials, dating to era 662 (likely 662 AD in the Spanish era).

EDCS-11700936.translation: Workshop of Rufius; workshop of Marcatulus.
EDCS-11700936.summary: This inscription likely marks the ownership or location of two workshops, possibly for artisans or merchants.

EDCS-11700939.summary: This is a personal name, possibly indicating ownership of property or a simple inscription identifying an individual.

EDCS-11700941.translation: To Gaius Marcius Masclus, aedile, duovir, and flamen; Marcia Tempestiva, his mother.
EDCS-11700941.summary: This is an epitaph honoring Gaius Marcius Masclus, who held several local magistracies, erected by his mother.

EDCS-11700946.translation: To Marcus Cornelius Arrianus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, aedile, duovir, and flamen of the Augusti; Licinia Nigrina, daughter of Lucius, to her most excellent and dearest husband.
EDCS-11700946.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Cornelius Arrianus, who held high local offices, dedicated by his wife Licinia Nigrina.

EDCS-11700982.summary: This is a personal name, likely an epitaph or ownership mark.

EDCS-11701003.translation: Termus was not hired here, but rather summoned by his means; thus Novalis will sing: 'CAN IA CA II defended him, for one he makes six.'
EDCS-11701003.summary: This is a peculiar inscription that seems to record a legal or possibly a theatrical event involving individuals named Termus and Novalis.

EDCS-11701006.translation: To Marcus Cornelius Faventinus, a decurion; Cornelia Faventina to her brother.
EDCS-11701006.summary: This is an epitaph for Marcus Cornelius Faventinus, who served as a decurion, dedicated by his sister Cornelia Faventina.

EDCS-11701007.translation: To Marcus Fabius Auctus, from Fabia Eudye, his dearest brother.
EDCS-11701007.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Marcus Fabius Auctus, set up by his sister Fabia Eudye.

EDCS-11701008.translation: To Marcus Cornelius Phaedimus, from Cornelia Faventina, his fellow priestess.
EDCS-11701008.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Marcus Cornelius Phaedimus, set up by Cornelia Faventina, who shared a religious office with him.

EDCS-11701009.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Cornelia Faventina, set up by her son.

EDCS-11701011.translation: Tullius Patroclus, freedman of Felix Boytianus, and Fortunatus, freedman of Felix Liso, and Celia, his mother, saw to its construction; Iulia Ianuaria made this for her brother, who was 30 years old, most devoted.
EDCS-11701011.summary: This is a funerary monument constructed by the mother and freedmen of the deceased, with a personal dedication from his sister Iulia Ianuaria, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11701012.summary: This is a funerary dedication to E. Fabricius Dener.

EDCS-11701029.translation: East Wind, West Wind, North Wind, South Wind.
EDCS-11701029.summary: This inscription lists the names of the four cardinal winds, possibly decorative or symbolic.

EDCS-11701040.translation: The adultery of Jupiter and Leda.
EDCS-11701040.summary: This inscription likely refers to a mythological scene or artwork depicting the adulterous union of Jupiter and Leda.

EDCS-11701045.translation: Seleucus and Caelius, these magistrates, saw to the construction of the pavement.
EDCS-11701045.summary: This inscription records the completion of a pavement project overseen by the magistrates Seleucus and Caelius.

EDCS-11701061.translation: Bellerophon killed the Chimera on the horse Pegasus.
EDCS-11701061.summary: This inscription describes a scene from Greek mythology, recounting Bellerophon's slaying of the Chimera while riding Pegasus.

EDCS-11701063.summary: This inscription, possibly a graffito or a marker, repeats the abbreviated phrase 'L() C(aballus?)', the meaning of which is unclear.

EDCS-11701084.translation: Sacred to Neptune; Glaucus, a slave of our Caesar, set up this inscription in fulfillment of a vow for the safety of our Emperors.
EDCS-11701084.summary: This dedication to Neptune was made by a slave of Caesar in fulfillment of a vow for the safety of the emperors.

EDCS-11701085.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus; Reginus, a vernaculus and exactor of the Emperors, set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-11701085.summary: This dedication to Marcus Aurelius was made by Reginus, a slave and an exactor, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-11701086.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus; Reginus, a vernaculus and exactor of the Emperors, set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-11701086.summary: This dedication to Lucius Verus was made by Reginus, a slave and an exactor, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-11701087.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits; Maternus and Valentina have placed here their mother, Materna, aged 52.
EDCS-11701087.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Materna, aged 52, placed by her children Maternus and Valentina.

EDCS-11701088.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Aretis, aged 80.

EDCS-11701089.translation: To Coso Udaviniago; Quintus, a decurio, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-11701089.summary: This dedication to Coso Udaviniago was made by Quintus, a decurio, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-11701090.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits; the veteran Daci placed this with a willing heart.
EDCS-11701090.summary: This dedication to the Divine Spirits was made by a veteran named Daci.

EDCS-11701092.translation: Erbo Erbieco; Lucius set this up with a willing heart in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-11701092.summary: This dedication, possibly to a deity or local spirit named Erbo Erbieco, was made by Lucius in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-11701093.translation: To Coso the Lord; Aebura Ati Cilena set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-11701093.summary: This dedication to Coso the Lord was made by Aebura Ati Cilena in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-11701094.translation: Ma Carae, son, aged 14, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. C. Lucius made this.
EDCS-11701094.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Ma Carae, aged 14, and was made by C. Lucius.

EDCS-11701095.translation: Marcus Quintius Doquirus, aged 70, lies here; may the earth be light upon you. Caius Quintius [laid this here].
EDCS-11701095.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Quintius Doquirus, erected by Caius Quintius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11701098.translation: Valerius Lupus, a optio (officer), gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Fortune.
EDCS-11701098.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Fortuna by an officer named Valerius Lupus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11701101.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Placidia Lupa erected this memorial to her most beloved husband, Iulius Severianus, who died at 47 years of age, in the valley of Mini.
EDCS-11701101.summary: This is a tombstone for Iulius Severianus, erected by his wife Placidia Lupa, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11701102.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Aproniana arranged for this to be made for her most beloved husband, Valerius Anterotus, aged 25.
EDCS-11701102.summary: This is a tombstone for Valerius Anterotus, erected by his wife Iulia Aproniana, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11701104.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucila Ebura, the heir, erected this for Severa, wife of Valentinus, aged 60.
EDCS-11701104.summary: This is a tombstone for Severa, erected by her heir Lucila Ebura, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11701105.translation: Reburrius Tertius [fulfilled his vow] to the Nymphs.
EDCS-11701105.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Nymphs by Reburrius Tertius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11701108.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valerius Daduchus [erected this] for his most devoted wife, Domitia Pusinca, aged 30.
EDCS-11701108.summary: This is a tombstone for Domitia Pusinca, erected by her husband Valerius Daduchus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11701110.translation: Flaccinius M.I. [fulfilled his vow] to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-11701110.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Flaccinius M.I., likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11701111.translation: Iunius Flaccus, a veteran of the Legio VII Gemina, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Pietas (Duty/Piety).
EDCS-11701111.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Pietas by Iunius Flaccus, a veteran of Legio VII Gemina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11701112.translation: Caeleus, son of Cadroiolon, of Cilenus at the fort of Berisamus, aged 60, and Caesarus, son of Caeleo, aged 15, lie here.
EDCS-11701112.summary: This is a tombstone for Caeleus and Caesarus, possibly father and son, who were stationed at the fort of Berisamus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11701113.translation: The monument of Appius Gemellus, aged 60, is located here. S. Festa made it for her husband and her sons, Appius Flaccus and Appius Gemellus.
EDCS-11701113.summary: This epitaph records the monument erected by S. Festa for her deceased husband, Appius Gemellus, and their sons.

EDCS-11701114.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Antonius Modestus made this for his most dutiful parents, Marcius Faus, aged 67, and Cantia Karia, aged 55.
EDCS-11701114.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Antonius Modestus to his deceased parents, Marcius Faus and Cantia Karia.

EDCS-11701116.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Saturnina, aged 36.
EDCS-11701116.summary: A brief epitaph for Saturnina, aged 36.

EDCS-11701117.translation: To the household gods. Valerius Q.
EDCS-11701117.summary: This dedication is made by Valerius Q. to the household gods.

EDCS-11701118.translation: To the household gods. Arc placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-11701118.summary: Arc fulfilled a vow with this dedication to the household gods.

EDCS-11701119.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Didia Graphice, for her sweetest daughter Graphici Augusti.
EDCS-11701119.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Didia Graphice to her daughter, Graphici Augusti.

EDCS-11701121.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Apro, aged [---].
EDCS-11701121.summary: An incomplete epitaph for Apro, indicating the deceased was of a certain age.

EDCS-11701122.translation: R. Bassinus willingly paid his vow to Jupiter.
EDCS-11701122.summary: R. Bassinus willingly paid a vow to Jupiter.

EDCS-11701123.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Vitales made this dedication for his son Primiano, aged 75.
EDCS-11701123.summary: This funerary inscription records Vitales dedicating a monument to his son Primiano, aged 75.

EDCS-11701126.translation: To the genius of the fort. Sabini willingly paid her vow.
EDCS-11701126.summary: Sabini fulfilled a vow to the genius of the fort.

EDCS-11701127.translation: Gaius Iulius Nepos to Coso Oenaego, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-11701127.summary: This is a votive dedication to the deity Coso Oenaego by Gaius Iulius Nepos.

EDCS-11701130.translation: Sacred to the Mother of the Gods and to Val, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-11701130.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made to the deities Mater Deum and Val.

EDCS-11701132.translation: Victor erected an altar to Victory, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-11701132.summary: This is a votive altar dedicated to the deity Victory by an individual named Victor.

EDCS-11701133.translation: Faustina erected this tombstone to her father Victori, aged 65, to the gods of the dead.
EDCS-11701133.summary: This funerary inscription records Faustina dedicating a monument to her deceased father, Victori, who was 65 years old.

EDCS-11701134.translation: Florina erected this monument to her mother, to the gods of the dead.
EDCS-11701134.summary: This funerary inscription records Florina dedicating a monument to her mother.

EDCS-11701137.translation: A memorial stone erected by Outeidus to his wife Vodoira, aged 30, to the gods of the dead. Farewell.
EDCS-11701137.summary: This funerary inscription records Outeidus dedicating a memorial to his wife Vodoira, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-11701138.translation: Severinus to the gods of the dead, sacred, aged 95.
EDCS-11701138.summary: This funerary inscription records Severinus, aged 95, dedicating something sacred to the gods of the dead.

EDCS-11701140.translation: P. Ma erected an altar to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for his own health.
EDCS-11701140.summary: This is a dedication of an altar to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by P. Ma for his well-being.

EDCS-11701142.translation: Parvillius made this for Cosoe Meobrigo.
EDCS-11701142.summary: This inscription records that Parvillius created something for Cosoe Meobrigo.

EDCS-11701143.translation: The law of the boundaries, according to the sacred order of our lords, the most invincible princes Valentinian, Valens, and Gratian, at the command of Marius Artemius, vir clarissimus, acting as vicarius, under the care of Potamio and Quentianus, principal officials.
EDCS-11701143.summary: This inscription records the establishment of boundary laws under the authority of the emperors Valentinian, Valens, and Gratian, and administrative officials Marius Artemius, Potamio, and Quentianus.

EDCS-11701308.translation: To his well-deserving daughter, daughter of Publius.
EDCS-11701308.summary: This is a funerary dedication, likely for a woman identified by her father's name and patronymic, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11701318.translation: Severus, son of Lucius, aged 70, is buried here.
EDCS-11701318.summary: This is a simple tombstone inscription recording the name, parentage, and age of the deceased, Severus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11701327.translation: To the Divine Manes. For my sweetest son Sempronius, aged 12. His most dutiful mother Iulia.
EDCS-11701327.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by a mother for her deceased son, Sempronius, who died at the age of 12, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11701329.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Gaius Vibius Quaestus, aged 55. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11701329.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Vibius Quaestus, aged 55, wishing him a peaceful afterlife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11701336.translation: Cornelius Simplex, may the earth lie lightly upon you. Manlia Trita and Manlia Serana are buried here.
EDCS-11701336.summary: This is a tombstone inscription commemorating Cornelius Simplex and two women, Manlia Trita and Manlia Serana, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800033.translation: Aerno paid for this altar in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-11800033.summary: This is a votive inscription recording that Aerno fulfilled a vow by dedicating an altar, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800036.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription, likely part of a dedication to a son, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800037.translation: To the Divine Manes. In memory of Pusincae, aged 50.
EDCS-11800037.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Pusincae, aged 50, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800038.translation: To the Divine Manes, sacred. Flaccinius, their father, arranged for this to be made in recognition of the dutifulness of Flaccinia, aged 15, and Filo, aged 28.
EDCS-11800038.summary: This funerary monument was erected by a father to honor his deceased children, Flaccinia and Filo, due to their piety, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800039.translation: Quintus Arrius Gnaeus Mansuetus from Clunia, aged 40. Aurelia Materna for her husband.
EDCS-11800039.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Quintus Arrius Gnaeus Mansuetus from Clunia, aged 40, set up by his wife Aurelia Materna, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800040.translation: Attilia Ammia, daughter of Caius, from Clunium, aged 70, is placed here. May the earth be light upon you. Quintus A M took care to have this made for his mother.
EDCS-11800040.summary: A funerary epitaph for Attilia Ammia, aged 70, with her son Quintus commissioning the monument.

EDCS-11800041.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Licinius Florus for his son Gaius Licinius Rufus, aged 3.
EDCS-11800041.summary: A funerary dedication to a young child, Gaius Licinius Rufus, aged 3.

EDCS-11800042.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. His mother placed this for Maritimus, aged 20, while abroad.
EDCS-11800042.summary: A funerary monument for Maritimus, aged 20, dedicated by his mother, possibly commemorating him while she was away.

EDCS-11800043.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. Placidus and Pollio made this for their father Rebilius, aged 60, as a memorial.
EDCS-11800043.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Placidus and Pollio for their father Rebilius, aged 60.

EDCS-11800044.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. Publicius Ursus took care to have this made for his mother Publicia and for Pestera, aged 35.
EDCS-11800044.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publicia and Pestera, with Publicius Ursus commissioning the monument.

EDCS-11800045.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. Rufa commissioned this made for her most dutiful daughter Junia, aged 20.
EDCS-11800045.summary: A funerary monument for the very dutiful Junia, aged 20, commissioned by her mother Rufa.

EDCS-11800046.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. Severa placed this for her mother Rufina.
EDCS-11800046.summary: A funerary dedication by Severa for her mother Rufina.

EDCS-11800047.translation: Vegetus, son of Amandus, aged 20, is placed here. His mother took care to have this made. Atimeta, I wish the earth may be light upon you.
EDCS-11800047.summary: A funerary epitaph for Vegetus, aged 20, with his mother Atimeta commissioning the monument.

EDCS-11800048.translation: Valeria Alla, daughter of Titus, from Clunium, aged 21, is placed here. May the earth be light upon you. Quintus Arrius Mansuetus took care to have this made for his wife.
EDCS-11800048.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Alla, aged 21, with her husband Quintus Arrius Mansuetus commissioning the monument.

EDCS-11800050.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. P. V. O. A., aged 1.
EDCS-11800050.summary: A funerary dedication for a child aged 1, with the initials P. V. O. A.

EDCS-11800051.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Cilen RV V.
EDCS-11800051.summary: This is a dedication to the gods of the underworld, with an incomplete name or title in the surviving text.

EDCS-11800052.translation: Sacred to the gods of the underworld. Ursa Severa.
EDCS-11800052.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the gods of the underworld by Ursa Severa.

EDCS-11800068.translation: Sacred to the gods of the underworld. Rufinus, to his most dutiful son Rufio, aged 20.
EDCS-11800068.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the gods of the underworld by Rufinus for his son Rufio, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-11800069.translation: Sabinus, aged 20; Secundio, aged 22; Lentinus, aged 20; the slaves of Marcus Audasius Maximus, centurion of the X Gemina legion, are buried here.
EDCS-11800069.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of four slaves belonging to a centurion of the X Gemina legion, providing their ages.

EDCS-11800070.translation: Sacred to the gods of the underworld. Placidina.
EDCS-11800070.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the gods of the underworld for Placidina.

EDCS-11800073.translation: Sacred to the gods of the underworld. To S. Faustinus.
EDCS-11800073.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the gods of the underworld for S. Faustinus.

EDCS-11800074.translation: Titus Iuncundus, of the VII Gemina.
EDCS-11800074.summary: This inscription likely records Titus Iuncundus, possibly a soldier or veteran of the VII Gemina legion.

EDCS-11800075.translation: Severius Flavus fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-11800075.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by Severius Flavus.

EDCS-11800076.translation: Sacred to the gods of the underworld. Mansueta, aged 65.
EDCS-11800076.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the gods of the underworld for Mansueta, who died at the age of 65.

EDCS-11800077.translation: Sacred to the gods of the underworld. Cresopes, aged 55.
EDCS-11800077.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the gods of the underworld for Cresopes, who died at the age of 55.

EDCS-11800079.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucila Vitalis, aged 11, of Castello Olca.
EDCS-11800079.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Lucila Vitalis, who died at the age of 11 in Castello Olca.

EDCS-11800080.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Vitalis, aged 35.
EDCS-11800080.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Vitalis, who died at the age of 35.

EDCS-11800082.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Artius, who died at the age of 75.

EDCS-11800083.translation: Sacred to Diana, with this votive offering, willingly made.
EDCS-11800083.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Diana, recording a votive offering made willingly.

EDCS-11800085.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Severina, aged 16.
EDCS-11800085.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Severina, who died at the age of 16.

EDCS-11800090.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Marcella, who died at the age of 21.

EDCS-11800091.translation: Pa. Severus willingly fulfilled his vow with a ready mind.
EDCS-11800091.summary: This is a dedication recording Pa. Severus fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-11800094.translation: Titus Lircinius erected an altar to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-11800094.summary: This is a dedication recording Titus Lircinius erecting an altar to Jupiter.

EDCS-11800095.translation: S. Lupus willingly fulfilled his vow to the household gods.
EDCS-11800095.summary: This is a dedication recording S. Lupus fulfilling a vow to the Laribus Vialibus (household gods).

EDCS-11800100.translation: SANN OAVA Caius Faustus Festus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11800100.summary: This is a dedication recording Caius Faustus Festus fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-11800101.translation: Severa set up an altar to the god Vestius Alonius.
EDCS-11800101.summary: This is a dedication to the god Vestius Alonius by Severa, likely an altar erected during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800103.translation: To the household gods of the road, Marcus.
EDCS-11800103.summary: A dedication to the Lares Viales (gods of the road) by Marcus, probably a small offering from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800106.translation: Tiberius Claudius Civo, a pure god, freely paid his vow to the god Bandua.
EDCS-11800106.summary: This inscription records Tiberius Claudius Civo fulfilling a vow to the god Bandua, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800107.translation: To Fusculus, son of Nedimus, Consta.
EDCS-11800107.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Fusculus, son of Nedimus, by Consta, likely a funerary or memorial inscription from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800109.translation: Aulus (son of Lucius) set up an altar to the household gods of the road with his own money, having made a vow.
EDCS-11800109.summary: Aulus dedicated an altar to the Lares Viales (gods of the road) at his own expense, fulfilling a vow, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800110.translation: To the departed spirits below, to Coamaea, daughter of Dannus, with her will satisfied.
EDCS-11800110.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Coamaea, daughter of Dannus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800113.translation: Publius Optatius freely paid his vow to the household gods of the road, as was deserved.
EDCS-11800113.summary: Publius Optatius fulfilled a vow to the Lares Viales (gods of the road), an altar inscription from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800114.translation: Severus, in fulfillment of a vow, to Mercury.
EDCS-11800114.summary: Severus dedicated this to Mercury, fulfilling a vow, likely an altar from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800116.translation: Caius Antonius Florus to the Nymphs.
EDCS-11800116.summary: This inscription records Caius Antonius Florus dedicating something to the Nymphs, likely an altar or shrine from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800118.translation: Quintinus, son of Quintus, freely paid his vow, as was deserved.
EDCS-11800118.summary: Quintinus, son of Quintus, fulfilled a vow, likely an altar inscription from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800119.translation: Flavus, son of Victor, willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Reo Cosoesoaegoe.
EDCS-11800119.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to a deity, likely made by Flavus, who fulfilled a vow. The date is uncertain but likely Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11800125.translation: Fronto Loveius Seicuius, aged 60, is buried here; his two sons took care to construct this monument.
EDCS-11800125.summary: This is a funerary monument commemorating Fronto Loveius Seicuius, erected by his sons, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11800126.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Severus, recording his death and age, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11800127.translation: Festus made this altar for Mercury.
EDCS-11800127.summary: This is a dedication of an altar to the god Mercury by Festus, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11800129.translation: Fronto Capito placed this in fulfillment of a vow to the god Mercury.
EDCS-11800129.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Mercury by Fronto Capito, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11800130.translation: Medamus Corali, aged 25, is buried here.
EDCS-11800130.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Medamus Corali, recording his age, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11800140.translation: From the Argelliani, son of Cananus the Great.
EDCS-11800140.summary: This inscription appears to be a genitive statement of family affiliation, possibly part of a larger dedication or epitaph, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11800148.translation: Flavus placed this for Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-11800148.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Flavus, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11800149.translation: M. Florus erected this in memory of his wife.
EDCS-11800149.summary: This is a funerary memorial erected by M. Florus for his wife, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11800154.translation: Monument of Ambatus Pentoviecus, son of Ambatus Pentovi, aged 60; Ambatus and Doiderus, his sons, erected this monument.
EDCS-11800154.summary: This is a funerary monument for Ambatus Pentoviecus, erected by his sons Ambatus and Doiderus, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11800156.translation: To the Divine Manes. Calpurnia Doidena, aged 41, and Anna, aged 20, were placed here by Etinius Araus Lucius Calpurnius Epae.
EDCS-11800156.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Calpurnia Doidena and Anna, set up by Etinius Araus Lucius Calpurnius Epae.

EDCS-11800157.translation: To Aelius Albinus, aged 75, Turaennia Pat [placed this].
EDCS-11800157.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aelius Albinus, aged 75, likely set up by Turaennia Pat.

EDCS-11800158.translation: In the consulship of 358 [?], his father Flavinus set up this memorial to his most dutiful son, Aemilius Sempronius Elesigainus, aged 30.
EDCS-11800158.summary: This funerary monument commemorates Aemilius Sempronius Elesigainus, aged 30, set up by his father Flavinus.

EDCS-11800166.translation: Augustan Boundary: it divides the meadows of the Fourth Legion and the land of the Iuliobrigenses.
EDCS-11800166.summary: This is a boundary marker, likely dating to the Augustan period, separating land belonging to the Fourth Legion and the Iuliobrigenses.

EDCS-11800167.translation: Augustan Boundary: it divides the meadows of the Fourth Legion and the land of the Iuliobrigenses.
EDCS-11800167.summary: This is a boundary marker, likely dating to the Augustan period, separating land belonging to the Fourth Legion and the Iuliobrigenses.

EDCS-11800168.translation: Augustan Boundary: it divides the meadows of the Fourth Legion and the land of the Iuliobrigenses.
EDCS-11800168.summary: This is a boundary marker, likely dating to the Augustan period, separating land belonging to the Fourth Legion and the Iuliobrigenses.

EDCS-11800172.translation: Augustan Boundary: it divides the meadows of the Fourth Legion and the land of the Iuliobrigenses.
EDCS-11800172.summary: This is a boundary marker, likely dating to the Augustan period, separating land belonging to the Fourth Legion and the Iuliobrigenses.

EDCS-11800173.translation: Augustan Boundary: it divides the meadows of the Fourth Legion and the land of the Iuliobrigenses.
EDCS-11800173.summary: This is a boundary marker, likely dating to the Augustan period, separating land belonging to the Fourth Legion and the Iuliobrigenses.

EDCS-11800174.translation: Augustan Boundary: it divides the meadows of the Fourth Legion and the land of the Iuliobrigenses.
EDCS-11800174.summary: This is a boundary marker, likely dating to the Augustan period, separating land belonging to the Fourth Legion and the Iuliobrigenses.

EDCS-11800364.translation: Lucius Servius Secundus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Evedutonius Barciaecus.
EDCS-11800364.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Lucius Servius Secundus fulfilling a vow to Evedutonius Barciaecus.

EDCS-11800368.translation: Quadratus, freedman of Figenia, [sets this up] for Sestius Munigalicus, son of Progeneius, on account of his services.
EDCS-11800368.summary: This is a dedication by a freedman for his patron, likely honoring the patron's services.

EDCS-11800372.translation: Aravus, son of Oilaridus, lies buried in this place, aged 51.
EDCS-11800372.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Aravus, son of Oilaridus, recording his age.

EDCS-11800373.translation: To the departed spirits of Proclus, aged 45.
EDCS-11800373.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the deceased Proclus, stating his age.

EDCS-11800374.translation: A monument was set up to the departed spirits; Marcus Licinius lived 91 years.
EDCS-11800374.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Marcus Licinius, recording his age and the erection of a monument.

EDCS-11800376.translation: Magnentia departed this life, aged 25, from the master's household.
EDCS-11800376.summary: This inscription records the death of Magnentia, aged 25, noting she was from her master's household.

EDCS-11800409.translation: Salt from the estates of Titus Vernius.
EDCS-11800409.summary: This is a property marker indicating salt originating from the estates of Titus Vernius.

EDCS-11800425.translation: To Publius Marcius, son of Publius, of the Velina tribe, thrice Augustan.
EDCS-11800425.summary: This is a dedication to Publius Marcius, son of Publius, of the Velina tribe, possibly honoring his thrice-held Augustan association.

EDCS-11800428.summary: This is a dedication or tomb inscription for Caecilia, daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-11800453.translation: Here rests Bassus, priest of the holy Roman church, he was laid to rest in peace on the Ides of April in the first indiction.
EDCS-11800453.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Bassus, a priest of the Roman church, specifying the date and ecclesiastical indiction.

EDCS-11800454.translation: Valeria the faithful lived 60 years, she passed from this life on the second day before the Kalends of October.
EDCS-11800454.summary: This epitaph records the death of Valeria, specifying her age and the date of her passing.

EDCS-11800456.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription identifying the deceased as Marg.

EDCS-11800473.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age at death of Caecilius Montanus.

EDCS-11800475.translation: Nigresaetas was 20 years old. Calcilia A BADI.
EDCS-11800475.summary: This funerary inscription records the age at death of Nigresaetas and names Calcilia, likely her owner or relative.

EDCS-11800477.translation: Quintus Sergius ARTO was 8 years old. C.
EDCS-11800477.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription recording the name and age at death of Quintus Sergius ARTO.

EDCS-11800483.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription recording the name of Caius Antisus and his age at death, which is lost.

EDCS-11800484.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription containing names and standard farewell greetings.

EDCS-11800485.translation: IHNATV IVS IHIS DAVCI IF LSIO, farewell
EDCS-11800485.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription containing names and a farewell greeting.

EDCS-11800492.translation: Scribonius L pax G, to the Lares Augustales, with money
EDCS-11800492.summary: This inscription appears to record a dedication to the Lares Augustales by Scribonius, possibly related to a financial transaction or offering.

EDCS-11800522.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly mentioning a person's name and their relation to a prefect.

EDCS-11800524.translation: ... of Titus, daughter of Lucius, Aemilius ... son N
EDCS-11800524.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that mentions familial relationships, likely part of a funerary or commemorative text.

EDCS-11800528.translation: Gaius Cavius, son of Cavius, from the colony of [...], [built/dedicated] [...].
EDCS-11800528.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Gaius Cavius, likely a dedication or funerary marker from a colony.

EDCS-11800539.translation: Aemilius Callicus, [son of...], from the time of the Emperor Caesar, 83.
EDCS-11800539.summary: This inscription seems to record a person named Aemilius Callicus and possibly a date or count associated with an emperor.

EDCS-11800540.translation: Quinctus Cornutus, from the time of the Emperor Caesar.
EDCS-11800540.summary: This inscription mentions Quinctus Cornutus and an imperial reference, likely part of a larger dedication or marker.

EDCS-11800541.translation: Numerius Mevius Aper, from the time of the Emperor Caesar, 86.
EDCS-11800541.summary: This inscription records Numerius Mevius Aper and a count possibly related to an imperial period.

EDCS-11800546.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Maxillus lived for [...] years.
EDCS-11800546.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring the deceased Maxillus.

EDCS-11800615.translation: Sempronius Maternus, son of Maternus, of the tribe Quirina, aged 40, is buried here.
EDCS-11800615.summary: This is a standard funerary epitaph for Sempronius Maternus.

EDCS-11800618.translation: Saturninus placed [this] to the spirits of the departed.
EDCS-11800618.summary: This is a votive or funerary inscription dedicated by Saturninus to the Manes.

EDCS-11800629.translation: Salanus, son of Tritus, aged 55, and his freedman Marinus, had this made at his own expense.
EDCS-11800629.summary: This inscription records Salanus and his freedman Marinus, who funded the construction of a monument.

EDCS-11800630.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a freedman named Allivius Tulus.

EDCS-11800632.translation: Quintus gladly and joyfully dedicated this to Caro the Preserver.
EDCS-11800632.summary: This is a votive inscription by Quintus dedicating something to the deity Caro Conservator.

EDCS-11800633.translation: Dulovius and Tabalianus, sons of Luggo and Arganticanus, erected these monuments.
EDCS-11800633.summary: This funerary inscription records the erection of monuments by two individuals, likely relatives, for themselves or others. The names suggest a local or non-Roman origin.

EDCS-11800895.translation: To the gods Cossus and Calucelaecus, Delaesus and Sonelaeus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-11800895.summary: This votive inscription is dedicated to two deities, Cossus and Calucelaecus, by Delaesus and Sonelaeus, who are fulfilling a vow. The personal names suggest a local cult or deity.

EDCS-11800904.translation: Felix willingly paid his vow to the god Laraheus, as was deserved.
EDCS-11800904.summary: This votive inscription records Felix willingly fulfilling a vow to the god Laraheus, likely a local deity, acknowledging the deservedness of the offering.

EDCS-11800926.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription that likely records the name and possibly the office or status of Lucius Cornelius Pro. Its fragmentary nature prevents further interpretation.

EDCS-11800927.summary: This inscription fragment appears to record the names Quinctus Cornelius and Eros, possibly related or indicating ownership or a dedication. The full context and relationship are unclear.

EDCS-11901100.translation: ... at his own expense in the name of Severus.
EDCS-11901100.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely refers to an expenditure made at someone's own expense, possibly in the name of an individual named Severus, suggesting a private dedication or commission.

EDCS-11901106.translation: To Mercury, willingly erected with merit.
EDCS-11901106.summary: This votive inscription is dedicated to Mercury, with the dedicant willingly and deservedly setting up the monument.

EDCS-11901114.translation: Claudius Trophimus paid for the inscription from his own funds, as was deserved.
EDCS-11901114.summary: This inscription records Claudius Trophimus paying for the monument out of his own resources, willingly and deservedly. It indicates a private dedication or funerary monument.

EDCS-11901116.translation: Reburrus, his son Quintus, and his wife Boutia took care to have this made at their own expense.
EDCS-11901116.summary: This inscription records a family, Reburrus, his son Quintus, and his wife Boutia, jointly commissioning and paying for a monument. It suggests a funerary or commemorative purpose.

EDCS-11901117.translation: Tateca, farewell well in Christ, in peace.
EDCS-11901117.summary: This inscription is a farewell addressed to Tateca, expressing good wishes for well-being in Christ and peace, likely a Christian funerary or devotional text.

EDCS-11901119.translation: Anna placed this, willingly and in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-11901119.summary: This is an altar or votive monument erected by Anna, fulfilling a vow, likely in the Roman period.

EDCS-11901124.translation: Two Germans, from the workshop of Licinius Amoenus.
EDCS-11901124.summary: This inscription likely refers to two freedmen or workers from the workshop of Licinius Amoenus, possibly indicating a status or origin, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901133.translation: To the Divine Shades: Licinius Hospitalis arranged for this to be made for Licinius Verus, aged 43, Licinia Placidina, aged 27, and Licinius Ianuarius, aged 12.
EDCS-11901133.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Shades, detailing the ages of three individuals and commissioned by Licinius Hospitalis, suggesting a family burial plot from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901134.translation: Valerius Cre placed this altar for Jupiter Best and Greatest, willingly and deservedly fulfilling his vow.
EDCS-11901134.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Valerius Cre, who willingly fulfilled his vow, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901136.translation: Sempronius arranged for this to be made for his father Marcus Flavius Paesurus, aged 39, and placed it.
EDCS-11901136.summary: This is a funerary inscription commissioned by Sempronius for his father Marcus Flavius Paesurus, providing his age and the commissioner's relation, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901141.translation: Faenusa lived for 1 year; Cosconius Valens, aged 65, is buried here.
EDCS-11901141.summary: This inscription records the death of Faenusa at one year old and Cosconius Valens at 65, indicating burial, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901142.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary or commemorative, for Gaius Valerius Seranus, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901143.translation: Gaius Aemilius Fronto, aged 70, is buried here.
EDCS-11901143.summary: This is a funerary inscription stating that Gaius Aemilius Fronto, aged 70, is buried here, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901146.translation: To Antestius Viator, son of Antistius Seranus, aged 15.
EDCS-11901146.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Antestius Viator, son of Antistius Seranus, providing his age, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901148.translation: Tiberius Caesar, son of the deified Augustus, to the municipium of Gracchuris.
EDCS-11901148.summary: This inscription records a grant or dedication by Emperor Tiberius to the municipium of Gracchuris, likely from the early Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901152.summary: This inscription likely records the name of Lucius Sabinius, possibly as a dedicator or owner, on an unknown monument.

EDCS-11901154.summary: This inscription records the name of Gaius Valerius Verdulus, likely as a dedicator or owner on an unspecified monument.

EDCS-11901159.translation: From the workshop of Valerius Reburrus
EDCS-11901159.summary: This inscription indicates the origin of goods or services from the workshop of Valerius Reburrus.

EDCS-11901209.summary: This inscription records that A(?) Vettius was the maker of an unspecified object or monument.

EDCS-11901210.summary: This incomplete inscription mentions Sempronius Cantabrus, likely in a context of ownership or dedication.

EDCS-11901211.summary: This inscription identifies the workshop of Miccio, likely related to a product or service.

EDCS-11901214.summary: This inscription records the name of Publius Fabius Sabinus, likely as a dedicator or owner on an unspecified monument.

EDCS-11901680.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, protector, Marcus Aurelius Severus paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-11901680.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter by Marcus Aurelius Severus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11901681.translation: Caelius Rufinus, in fulfillment of a vow, to Laho Paraliomegos.
EDCS-11901681.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Caelius Rufinus to the deity Laho Paraliomegos, possibly from a provincial area.

EDCS-11901682.translation: Valerius Optanus paid his vow willingly and deservedly to Rhea.
EDCS-11901682.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to the goddess Rhea by Valerius Optanus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11901683.translation: Reo Paramaeco, Aidus and Prudens his son willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-11901683.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Reo Paramaeco by Aidus and his son Prudens, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901684.translation: Silonius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Tutela.
EDCS-11901684.summary: A votive inscription from Silonius to the goddess Tutela, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901688.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator and the Divine Powers of the Emperors, to Juno Regina, Venus Victrix, Africa Caelestis Frugifer, Augusta Emerita, and the Household Gods of the Callaeci, Saturninus, freedman of the emperor, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11901688.summary: A complex dedication to various deities and imperial powers by Saturninus, a freedman of the emperor, likely from Emerita Augusta during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901689.translation: To Veteranus from Narbonne and Cornelia.
EDCS-11901689.summary: An incomplete dedication, possibly funerary or commemorative, to Veteranus from Narbonne and Cornelia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901690.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Septimius Hermeros, freedman of the emperor, made this for his most chaste wife Cassia Antipatra, with whom he lived for 24 years, for her well-deserved memory.
EDCS-11901690.summary: A funerary dedication for Cassia Antipatra, made by her husband Lucius Septimius Hermeros, detailing their 24-year marriage, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901693.translation: Gemellinus, son of Florus, [dedicated this] to his sons Martialis, aged 10, Loveus, aged 9, and Paternus, aged 4, slaves from the household of Laedius.
EDCS-11901693.summary: A dedication, likely funerary, by Gemellinus for his young sons, who are identified as slaves from the household of Laedius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901695.translation: Marcus Valerius Fronto of Lusitania arranged for this to be made for his most holy daughter Valeria Frontilla, aged 22, his dearest student.
EDCS-11901695.summary: A dedication, likely funerary, for Valeria Frontilla by her father Marcus Valerius Fronto, highlighting her status as a student, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901696.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Fortunata placed this for her deserving wife Aquilius Euprepes.
EDCS-11901696.summary: A funerary dedication by Fortunata for her wife Aquilius Euprepes, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901697.translation: Of Bulenus, aged 25; of Bulenus, aged 20; of Bulenus, aged 12.
EDCS-11901697.summary: An inscription listing three individuals named Bulenus with their ages, likely a funerary record, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901698.translation: Cara, daughter of Vesuclus, and Vecius.
EDCS-11901698.summary: An incomplete inscription, possibly a dedication or a funerary marker, mentioning Cara, daughter of Vesuclus, and Vecius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901703.translation: Sulpicius Sincerus, centurion, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Bandue Bolecco.
EDCS-11901703.summary: This inscription records a centurion fulfilling a vow to a deity named Bandue Bolecco.

EDCS-11901705.translation: Flavius Valerianus, in fulfillment of a vow, to Cuhvetena Berralogecu.
EDCS-11901705.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Flavius Valerianus to Cuhvetena Berralogecu.

EDCS-11901710.translation: Claudius Gauce Ascrierus, son of Verenus, [dedicated this] to the household gods.
EDCS-11901710.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the household gods (Laribus Vialibus) by Claudius Gauce Ascrierus.

EDCS-11901711.translation: He set up an altar to the household gods, at his own expense, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-11901711.summary: This inscription records the erection of an altar to the household gods in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-11901712.translation: [Dedicated] to the household gods by Caesianus.
EDCS-11901712.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the household gods (Laribus Vialibus) by Caesianus.

EDCS-11901713.translation: Caius Iulius Hispanus, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Lugus Arquienus.
EDCS-11901713.summary: This inscription records a military man, Caius Iulius Hispanus, fulfilling a vow to Lugus Arquienus.

EDCS-11901714.translation: Silonius Silo, in fulfillment of a vow, sacred to Lucobus Arquienus.
EDCS-11901714.summary: This inscription records Silonius Silo dedicating something sacred to Lucobus Arquienus in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-11901716.translation: Maxumus Secundi gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Mercury.
EDCS-11901716.summary: This inscription records Maxumus Secundi fulfilling a vow to the god Mercury.

EDCS-11901717.translation: To the Nymphs, gladly, in fulfillment of a vow. APF FSC.
EDCS-11901717.summary: This inscription records a vow made to the Nymphs, with abbreviations likely indicating the dedicator.

EDCS-11901718.translation: Sulpicius Maximus, in fulfillment of a vow, to the Nymph Arconucnica.
EDCS-11901718.summary: This inscription records Sulpicius Maximus fulfilling a vow to a Nymph named Arconucnica.

EDCS-11901720.translation: Sacred to Ae [Minerva?]. Gaius Valerius Carus, a soldier of the Tenth Legion Gemina, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11901720.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by a soldier of the Tenth Legion Gemina.

EDCS-11901721.translation: To Calutia Severina, daughter of Seguia, aged 23.
EDCS-11901721.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Calutia Severina, daughter of Seguia, who died at the age of 23.

EDCS-11901722.summary: This inscription records the burial of Nigrinianus, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-11901723.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Placidius Paternus, aged 17.
EDCS-11901723.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Placidius Paternus at the age of 17.

EDCS-11901724.translation: To the Divine Manes. Serania Victorina, aged 60.
EDCS-11901724.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Serania Victorina at the age of 60.

EDCS-11901725.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Severina, aged 91.
EDCS-11901725.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Severina at the age of 91.

EDCS-11901726.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Venustus, aged [---].
EDCS-11901726.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Venustus, with his age partially illegible.

EDCS-11901728.translation: To [the memory of] Felix, a good parent, to Iulio and his son.
EDCS-11901728.summary: This inscription is a dedication honoring Felix as a good parent, addressed to Iulio and his son.

EDCS-11901730.summary: This inscription contains fragmented Latin text with unclear meaning.

EDCS-11901733.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Trajan, conqueror of Parthia, grandson of the deified Nerva, Trajan Hadrian Augustus.
EDCS-11901733.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Hadrian, listing his full imperial titulature.

EDCS-11901735.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Marcus Aurelius, likely a funerary or honorific dedication, dated to his reign.

EDCS-11901740.translation: Lucius Minicius Myron to August Equity, for the office of the VIvirate, a public duty for the colony of Barcino.
EDCS-11901740.summary: This inscription records a dedication to August Equity by Lucius Minicius Myron in honor of his magistracy in Barcino, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901749.translation: Sacred to August Diana. Lucius Aemilius Saturninus, for the office of the VIvirate, which the council of Barcino gratuitously decreed to him.
EDCS-11901749.summary: This is a dedication to August Diana by Lucius Aemilius Saturninus in honor of his magistracy in Barcino, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901758.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Trajan, likely dating to his reign (98-117 AD).

EDCS-11901759.translation: To Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Antoninus, grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus.
EDCS-11901759.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Lucius Aurelius Verus, listing his imperial lineage and dating to his reign (161-169 AD).

EDCS-11901768.translation: To Gaius Cilnius Ferox, son of Gaius, of the Voltinia tribe, priest, military tribune of the Fourth Flavian Legion.
EDCS-11901768.summary: This inscription honors Gaius Cilnius Ferox, detailing his priestly and military offices, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901771.translation: Lucius Sempronius Carpio, a client, granted a place by decree of the decurions, to Lucius Minicius Lucius son Natalis Quadronius Verus, junior consul, augur, tribune of the plebs, proconsul of the province of Africa, imperial legate with praetorian power of the province of Lower Moesia, curator of public works and sacred buildings, curator of the Flaminian Way, prefect of the grain supply, imperial legate of the Sixth Victory Legion in the province of Britain, candidate tribune of the plebs, candidate quaestor of the deified Hadrian Augustus, and at the same time legate of the province of Africa, the Carthaginian diocese, under the proconsulship of his father, military tribune of the First Adjutant Legion, Pious and Faithful, also of the Eleventh Claudian Legion, Pious and Faithful, also of the Fourteenth Gemina, Martia, Victrix, moneyer triumvir for striking gold, silver, and bronze.
EDCS-11901771.summary: This is a lengthy honorific inscription detailing the extensive career of Lucius Minicius Natalis Quadronius Verus, likely set up by his client Lucius Sempronius Carpio and dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901777.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription identifying Lucius Licinius, likely a dedication or funerary monument, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901780.translation: By decree of the decurions, to Gaius Julius Licinianus, son of Gaius, of the Aniensis tribe, known as Seneca, provincial flamen of Hispania Citerior, aedile, duovir, municipal flamen, prefect of the artificers, military tribune of the Sixth Victory Legion, Pious and Faithful, and military tribune of the Fifteenth Apollinaris.
EDCS-11901780.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Gaius Julius Licinianus by the decurions, listing his civic, religious, and military offices, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901782.translation: By decree of the decurions, to Gaius Marius Aemilianus, son of Lucius, of the Aniensis tribe, duovir, municipal flamen, flamen of Roma and the Augustan gods, judge from the selections of five, from the selected five. Vibia Liviana to her most excellent husband.
EDCS-11901782.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Vibia Liviana to her husband Gaius Marius Aemilianus, detailing his magistracies and judgeships, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901783.translation: To Publius Aufidius Exoratus, centurion of the Legio III Augusta, the Legio XXX Ulpia Victrix, and the Legio VII Gemina Felix, his heirs, for his well-deserved service.
EDCS-11901783.summary: A funerary dedication to a centurion who served in three legions, erected by his heirs.

EDCS-11901784.translation: Lucius Caecilius Optatus, son of Lucius, of the Papiria tribe, centurion of the Legio VII Gemina Felix, and centurion of the Legio XV Apollinaris, honorably discharged by the Emperors Marcus Aurelius Antoninus and Aurelius Verus Augusti, and appointed by the people of Barcino among the exempt officials, having attained the aedileship and the office of duovir twice, and priest of Rome's gods and emperors, who thus endowed the public of Barcino: I give, I bequeath, and I want to be given, 7000 denarii, from the interest of which I wish to be provided annually for a period of gladiatorial games on the 4th day before the Ides of June, up to 250 denarii, and on the same day, from 200 denarii, oil to be provided in the public baths for the people. I want these things to be carried out on this condition: that my freedmen, as well as the freedmen of my freedmen and my freedwomen, whose freedom from the office of sevirate may occur, shall be excused from all sevirate duties; but if any of them should be summoned to the duties, then I order that these 7000 denarii be transferred to the public treasury of Tarraco, under the same provision for the provision of games as written above, the place for their presentation in Tarraco having been granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-11901784.summary: A detailed endowment by Lucius Caecilius Optatus, a former centurion and local magistrate, for public games and provisions in Barcino, with specific conditions regarding freedmen and a redirection to Tarraco if conditions are not met.

EDCS-11901786.translation: To Marcus Aemilius Optatus, son of Lucius, fourteen years old, the order of Barcino decreed for him the offices of aedile and duovir without payment. Marcus Herennius Severus, guardian.
EDCS-11901786.summary: A posthumous honorary dedication by the city of Barcino to a young boy, Marcus Aemilius Optatus, for whom civic offices were decreed, supervised by his guardian.

EDCS-11901787.translation: To Antonia Silsona, daughter of Lucius, from Caius Aelius Cotta, who has held all offices in the state, for his excellent and most rare wife.
EDCS-11901787.summary: A dedication by Caius Aelius Cotta to his wife Antonia Silsona, praising her virtues and acknowledging his own civic career.

EDCS-11901788.translation: To Lucius Cornelius Secundus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, aedile and duovir, for his brother Quintus Cornelius Nigro, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe; for his father Gaius Aemilius Niger, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe; for his mother Cornelia; and for his grandmother Numitoria, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-11901788.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Lucius Cornelius Secundus, an aedile and duovir, to his brother, father, mother, and paternal grandmother.

EDCS-11901789.translation: To Quintus Cornelius Secundus, son of Spurius, from the colony of Carthago Nova, for himself, and for his mother Cornelia Quartus, his wife Geminia Quartus, his daughter Cornelia Tertulla, his son Quintus Cornelius Seranus (aedile and duovir), his daughter Cornelia Dubitata, his daughter Cornelia Spuri, his sister Tertulla, and his nephew Lucius Maevius Rogatus.
EDCS-11901789.summary: A family funerary monument erected by Quintus Cornelius Secundus of Carthago Nova, commemorating himself and multiple relatives, including a son who held civic office.

EDCS-11901791.translation: To Lucius Pedanius Aemilianus, son of Lucius, of the Palatina tribe, aedile, duovir, and priest of the gods and emperors, from his most dutiful mother, Aemilia Furiana.
EDCS-11901791.summary: A dedication by Aemilia Furiana to her son, Lucius Pedanius Aemilianus, who held multiple high civic and religious offices in Barcino.

EDCS-11901792.translation: To Asphalus, son of Lucius, of the Palatina tribe, formerly Clementius the elder, who held all offices in his state of Barcino, and was quinquennial magistrate of the colony of Iulia Urbs Triumphalis Tarraco, Pedania Clementiana had intended to set up this monument for her most dutiful father; his heirs, Pedanius Sacerdos and Ianuaria, erected this for their patron. The place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-11901792.summary: A posthumous dedication by the heirs of Pedania Clementiana to her father, Asphalus Clementius, a prominent official in both Barcino and Tarraco, with the place granted by the city council.

EDCS-11901793.translation: To Lucius Pedanius Urso, son of Lucius, of the Palatina tribe, decurion of the colony of Barcino, from his most dutiful son, Pedania Dionysia. The place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-11901793.summary: A dedication by Pedania Dionysia to her father, Lucius Pedanius Urso, a decurion in Barcino, with the place provided by the city council.

EDCS-11901794.translation: To Lucius Porcius Celer, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, seventeen years old, aedile of Barcino, from his most beloved mother, Domitia Lucilia. The place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-11901794.summary: A funerary dedication by Domitia Lucilia to her seventeen-year-old son, Lucius Porcius Celer, who served as aedile in Barcino, with the place granted by the city council.

EDCS-11901797.translation: Quintus Calpurnius Nymphius, freedman of Quintus, a municipal priest of the imperial cult, while alive, shall not pass on this monument to his heir for himself and his wife Marcia Urania.
EDCS-11901797.summary: A private funerary monument recording the burial arrangements of Quintus Calpurnius Nymphius and his wife, intended not to be inherited.

EDCS-11901798.translation: To the departed spirits. This monument and burial plot shall not pass to the heir of Gaius Helvius Natalis, a municipal priest of the imperial cult of the colony of Barcino, and his wife Aemilia Fidentina, and their freedmen Liberalis and Marcella.
EDCS-11901798.summary: A private funerary monument recording the burial arrangements for Gaius Helvius Natalis, his wife, and their freedmen, intended not to be inherited.

EDCS-11901799.translation: For Iulia Coene, freedwoman of Gaius. Gaius Julius Daduchus, freedman of Gaius, a municipal priest of the imperial cult, [dedicated this] for his wife and himself.
EDCS-11901799.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Gaius Julius Daduchus, a municipal priest, for himself and his wife Iulia Coene.

EDCS-11901801.translation: To Lucius Licinius Secundus, his patron, who served as scribe, first, second, and third consul, and as a municipal priest of the imperial cult of the colony Iulia Urbis Triumphalis Tarraco, and of the colony Faventia Iulia Augusta Patera Barcino; Quintia Severa, daughter of Gaius.
EDCS-11901801.summary: An epitaph honouring Lucius Licinius Secundus, a prominent local official and priest, likely erected by Quintia Severa.

EDCS-11901804.translation: To Lucius Licinius Secundus, his patron; Lucius Licinius Surae.
EDCS-11901804.summary: A dedication from Lucius Licinius Surae to his patron Lucius Licinius Secundus.

EDCS-11901807.translation: To Gaius Onesimus, freedman of Gaius Trocina, a municipal priest of the imperial cult. Gaius Trocina Philetus, freedman, ordered this to be set up in his will. Gaius Trocina Paramythius, heir, erected it. The plot was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-11901807.summary: A funerary monument erected by the heir of Gaius Trocina Paramythius, following the wishes of the freedman Gaius Onesimus who was a municipal priest, with the plot granted by local authorities.

EDCS-11901816.translation: To Acilia Arethusa, mother. Pedanius Clemens, son, and Clemens Minicianus, grandson. The plot was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-11901816.summary: A funerary plot granted by decree of the decurions for Acilia Arethusa, her son Pedanius Clemens, and grandson Clemens Minicianus.

EDCS-11901817.translation: To Lucius Fulvius Clementinus, dearest son. Lucius Fulvius Clemens, father. The plot was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-11901817.summary: A funerary plot granted by decree of the decurions for the deceased son Lucius Fulvius Clementinus, by his father Lucius Fulvius Clemens.

EDCS-11901818.translation: To Lucius Julius Achilleus. Lucius Julius Eutychianus, because of his services to him.
EDCS-11901818.summary: A dedication from Lucius Julius Eutychianus to Lucius Julius Achilleus, for services rendered.

EDCS-11901821.translation: To Lucius Pedanius Narcissianus, aged 15 years and 52 days. Lucius Pedanius Narcissus, for his dearest son. The plot was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-11901821.summary: A funerary plot granted by decree of the decurions for Lucius Pedanius Narcissianus, a boy who died at age 15, by his father Lucius Pedanius Narcissus.

EDCS-11901822.translation: A place was granted to Turpilia Pacata by decree of the decurions, by her brother Cnaeus Turpilius Dius, for his most devoted sister.
EDCS-11901822.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from an uncertain date recording the allocation of a burial plot by the local council.

EDCS-11901825.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Aemilia Lucretia made this for her well-deserving husband Numisius Silvanus, and for herself.
EDCS-11901825.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Numisius Silvanus, set up by his wife Aemilia Lucretia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901826.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Aemilius Zosimus made this for Aemilia Materna.
EDCS-11901826.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aemilia Materna, erected by Aemilius Zosimus, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901827.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Calpurnius Octavius made this for his well-deserving partner Quintina.
EDCS-11901827.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintina, erected by her partner Quintus Calpurnius Octavius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901829.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Tarquitius Agatho made this for his partner Clodia Lupa, who deserved well, and for himself.
EDCS-11901829.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Clodia Lupa, erected by her partner Quintus Tarquitius Agatho, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901830.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Cornelius Faustus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901832.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Father Fabius Crescens made this for his dearest, deserving son, Fabius Rufus, who lived 10 years, 2 months, 5 days. Fabia Fiale made this according to [his] will.
EDCS-11901832.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fabius Rufus, who died at a young age, erected by his father and his will, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901833.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Fabia Agathonicena, who deserved well.
EDCS-11901833.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fabia Agathonicena, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901834.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Fabia Ferriola made this for her well-deserving husband Pedanius Dionysius, and for herself.
EDCS-11901834.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pedanius Dionysius, erected by his wife Fabia Ferriola, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901835.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Fabia Tertulla, the nurse.
EDCS-11901835.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fabia Tertulla, the nurse, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901836.translation: Urbana, freedwoman of Gnaeus and his wife, established this tomb for herself and for Julia Primigenia, freedwoman of Caius, aged 31, who is buried here.
EDCS-11901836.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Urbana, who set up a tomb for herself and Julia Primigenia, likely her daughter or partner, possibly dating to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-11901838.translation: To the spirits of the departed: the heirs, in accordance with the will, (erected this).
EDCS-11901838.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Flavia Theodota, whose heirs erected the monument according to her will, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11901841.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Karpes, formerly of Gaius Helvius Geta, lived 30 years and 4 days.
EDCS-11901841.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Karpes, a slave or freedman of Gaius Helvius Geta, who lived 30 years and 4 days, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11901842.translation: Gaius Julius Carus is buried here; the heirs, in accordance with the will, shall not be bound by this monument.
EDCS-11901842.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Carus, with a stipulation that the monument does not obligate his heirs according to his will, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11901843.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Lucius Iulius Protogenes (erected this) for Salvianus, his well-deserving slave and foster-son, aged 19.
EDCS-11901843.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Salvianus, a slave and foster-son of Lucius Iulius Protogenes, who died at the age of 19, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11901847.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Gaius L. Myron and his sons Claudius Myrismus and Myrinus (erected this) for Nicia, aged 21, their most dutiful wife and mother.
EDCS-11901847.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Nicia, aged 21, by her husband Gaius L. Myron and their sons Claudius Myrismus and Myrinus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11901848.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Livius Hellenicus (erected this) for his mother, Livia Lupula.
EDCS-11901848.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Livia Lupula, by her son Livius Hellenicus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11901853.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Numisia Tyche (erected this) for her mother, Numisia Eutychia.
EDCS-11901853.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Numisia Eutychia, by her daughter Numisia Tyche, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11901855.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Marcus Porcius Primitivus (erected this) for his most beloved, faithful, and well-deserving tent-mate, Vireia Augustina.
EDCS-11901855.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Vireia Augustina, by her partner Marcus Porcius Primitivus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11901856.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Marcus Porcius Privatus erected this for Salviana.
EDCS-11901856.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Salviana, erected by Marcus Porcius Privatus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11901857.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Pedanius Iucundus for his well-deserving wife, Porcia Fileteni.
EDCS-11901857.summary: This is a funerary dedication (Dis Manibus) by Pedanius Iucundus for his wife Porcia Fileteni.

EDCS-11901860.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Flavia Chrysis for her most devoted mother, Terentia Fuscula.
EDCS-11901860.summary: This is a funerary dedication (Dis Manibus) by Flavia Chrysis for her mother Terentia Fuscula.

EDCS-11901861.translation: Eroticus for his well-deserving father Eroticus and his sister Erotica, he made this.
EDCS-11901861.summary: This funerary monument (Dis Manibus) was erected by Eroticus for his father and sister.

EDCS-11901863.translation: Lucius Valerius Fronto, son of Lucius, while alive, for himself and his family.
EDCS-11901863.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Lucius Valerius Fronto for himself and his household.

EDCS-11901866.translation: Quintus Valerius Secundus, Quintus Valerius Faventinus.
EDCS-11901866.summary: This inscription simply records the names of two individuals, Quintus Valerius Secundus and Quintus Valerius Faventinus, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-11901873.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Theodorus made this for a well-deserving person, aged 18 years.
EDCS-11901873.summary: This is a funerary dedication (Dis Manibus) made by Theodorus for an individual who died at 18 years old.

EDCS-11901878.translation: To the spirits of the departed: for his dear son, Buturatus Neo.
EDCS-11901878.summary: This is a funerary dedication (Dis Manibus) by a parent for their beloved son, Buturatus Neo.

EDCS-11901880.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the divinity of a person named Cynegīolus, likely a cult or shrine.

EDCS-11901886.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Primitivus for his well-deserving wife, Marciana.
EDCS-11901886.summary: This funerary dedication (Dis Manibus) was made by Primitivus for his wife Marciana.

EDCS-11901888.translation: To the spirits of the departed: they made this for the well-deserving Faustanus, Pertullna and Lertola.
EDCS-11901888.summary: This funerary dedication (Dis Manibus) was made by Pertullna and Lertola for Faustanus.

EDCS-12100673.translation: To Fortuna Bona Redux, Publius Ulpius Maximus, procurator of the emperor, with his wife and son.
EDCS-12100673.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Fortuna Bona Redux by Publius Ulpius Maximus, likely an imperial official, accompanied by his family.

EDCS-12100674.translation: Gaius Otacilius Octavius Saturninus, a distinguished man and procurator of the emperor, dedicated this to Fortuna Redux Sancta, with his daughter and grandson.
EDCS-12100674.summary: This is a dedication to Fortuna Redux Sancta by Gaius Otacilius Octavius Saturninus, an official, along with his descendants.

EDCS-12100677.translation: Julius Silvanus Melanio, procurator of the emperors for the province of Hispania Citerior, dedicated this to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Custos, Juno Regina, Minerva Sancta, and the other immortal gods and goddesses.
EDCS-12100677.summary: This is a dedication to a group of deities by Julius Silvanus Melanio, an imperial procurator in Hispania Citerior.

EDCS-12100680.translation: To the unconquered god Serapis and Isis, Claudius Zenobius, procurator of the emperor.
EDCS-12100680.summary: This is a dedication to the gods Serapis and Isis by Claudius Zenobius, an imperial procurator.

EDCS-12100681.translation: Julius Melanio, procurator of the emperors, fulfilled a vow to Serapis Sanctus, Isis Myrionymae, Cora Invicta, Apollo Grannus, and Mars Sagatus.
EDCS-12100681.summary: This records the fulfillment of a vow by Julius Melanio, an imperial procurator, to several deities including Serapis, Isis, Apollo, and Mars.

EDCS-12100682.translation: Gaius Coelius Valens of the Papiria tribe from Narbo, a soldier of the tenth legion Gemina, century of Castellanus, aged 35, buried here, his heir by will.
EDCS-12100682.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Gaius Coelius Valens, a soldier from Narbo serving in the tenth legion, who died at age 35 and was buried according to his will.

EDCS-12100706.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Septimia Berulla placed this for her most devoted husband, Titus Septimius Martialis, aged 85, buried here.
EDCS-12100706.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Septimia Berulla for her husband, Titus Septimius Martialis, who died at the age of 85.

EDCS-12100718.summary: This is a dedication, likely funerary, to Clar Abic, by his husband.

EDCS-12100727.summary: This is a dedication, likely funerary, to Luci Inquentes.

EDCS-12100728.summary: This inscription, with an unusual name and possibly a Greek origin, appears to be a dedication or record of a person named Pleusicles.

EDCS-12100737.translation: Caraedudus son of Reburrus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-12100737.summary: This is a votive inscription by Caraedudus, son of Reburrus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-12100739.summary: This is a dedication to the god Cosiovius Ascannus.

EDCS-12100751.translation: For the safety of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, on the birthday of the eagle, a detachment of the VII Gemina Felix legion fulfilled its vow willingly and deservedly, under the supervision of Aurelius Eutychis, freedman of the emperor, procurator, and Valerius Sempronianus, decurion of the II Flaviae cavalry wing, on the fourth day before the Ides of June, when Marullus and Aelianus were consuls.
EDCS-12100751.summary: This military dedication by a detachment of the VII Gemina Felix legion was made for the safety of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus on the 'birthday of the eagle', dating to the consulship of Marullus and Aelianus.

EDCS-12100752.translation: For the safety of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus.
