EDCS-12100752.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the safety of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus.

EDCS-12100754.translation: V. R. son of Boutius, 28 years of service.
EDCS-12100754.summary: This inscription records military service, identifying the individual as V. R., son of Boutius, with 28 years of service.

EDCS-12100756.translation: Albanus, a Gaul by nationality, 25 years old, and Endegus, a house-born slave of Marcius Valerius Pila, 12 years old, are buried here.
EDCS-12100756.summary: This is a funerary inscription for two individuals, Albanus (a Gaul) and Endegus (a slave of Marcius Valerius Pila), providing their ages and origins.

EDCS-12100766.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Valerianus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, consul for the second time, father of the country, proconsul, the unconquered; to the Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Valerianus Gallienus, the unconquered, pious, fortunate Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, consul for the second time, father of the country, proconsul.
EDCS-12100766.summary: This is a lengthy imperial dedication honouring both Emperors Valerian and his son Gallienus, listing their titles and epithets.

EDCS-12100768.translation: By authority of Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, Emperor, the boundary of the fields of the III Gallorum cohort between the IIII Gallorum cohort and the civitas of the Bedunienses.
EDCS-12100768.summary: This is a boundary marker, established by the authority of Emperor Claudius, defining the territory of the III Gallorum cohort in relation to the IIII Gallorum cohort and the civitas of the Bedunienses.

EDCS-12100769.translation: By authority of Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, Emperor, the boundary of the fields of the IIII Gallorum cohort between the IIII Gallorum cohort and the civitas of the Bedunienses, 10 paces.
EDCS-12100769.summary: This is a boundary marker, established by the authority of Emperor Claudius, defining the territory of the IIII Gallorum cohort in relation to itself and the civitas of the Bedunienses, measuring 10 paces.

EDCS-12100773.translation: By authority of Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, Emperor, the boundary of the fields of the IIII Gallorum cohort between the IIII Gallorum cohort and the civitas of the Lugones.
EDCS-12100773.summary: This is a boundary marker, established by the authority of Emperor Claudius, defining the territory of the IIII Gallorum cohort in relation to itself and the civitas of the Lugones.

EDCS-12100774.translation: As authorized by Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus Imperator, this is the boundary marker of the fields of the Fourth Cohort of Gauls, between the Fourth Cohort of Gauls and the civitas of the Luggonians.
EDCS-12100774.summary: This inscription is a boundary marker for fields, authorized by Emperor Claudius, dating to the mid-1st century AD.

EDCS-12100775.summary: This is a very short inscription, likely a name on a tomb or property, with an uncertain date.

EDCS-12800720.translation: Sacred to Hercules. Marcus Sellius Honoratus, son of Lucius, from the Arnensian tribe, from the province of Mauretania Caesariensis, prefect of cavalry of the II Flavian Ala of Spaniards, Roman citizens, having fulfilled his vows, built the temples of the god Alcides from the foundations.
EDCS-12800720.summary: This inscription records the dedication of temples to Hercules by Marcus Sellius Honoratus, a Roman cavalry prefect, likely in the late 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-13301273.translation: For the Mothers of the civitas, Avitus Tacius and Tapila Tapila Tacius gladly and deservedly paid their vow.
EDCS-13301273.summary: This inscription records a vow paid by Avitus Tacius and Tapila Tapila Tacius to the Mothers, likely a local deity, with an uncertain date.

EDCS-13301275.translation: To Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus, pontifex maximus, father of the country, consul for the third time.
EDCS-13301275.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Trajan, likely a titulature on a public building, dating to his third consulship (AD 100).

EDCS-13301276.translation: To Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 13th time, consul for the third time, father of the country.
EDCS-13301276.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Hadrian, likely a titulature on a public building, dating to his 13th tribunician power and third consulship (AD 129-130).

EDCS-13301282.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Caro Pius Felix Augustus Invictus, with tribunician power for the 2nd time, father of the country, proconsul.
EDCS-13301282.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Carus, likely a titulature on a public building, dating to his second tribunician power and proconsulship (AD 283).

EDCS-13301294.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carinus, 35 miles.
EDCS-13301294.summary: This inscription likely marks a distance of 35 miles for Emperor Carinus, possibly on a milestone, dating to AD 283-284.

EDCS-13301298.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the health of the Augusti Marcus Aurelius Antoninus and Aurelius Verus, on the Ides of June, in the consulship of Laelianus and Pastor.
EDCS-13301298.summary: This inscription records a prayer to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the health of Emperors Marcus Aurelius and Verus, dating to AD 167.

EDCS-13301300.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Titus Albinius gladly and deservedly brought back his vow.
EDCS-13301300.summary: This inscription records Titus Albinius fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, with an uncertain date.

EDCS-13301301.translation: Flavius, son of Flavius, willingly paid his vow to Jupiter.
EDCS-13301301.summary: This is a votive inscription by Flavius to Jupiter, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13301303.translation: Lucius Enicus Rufini willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-13301303.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius Enicus Rufini to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13301305.translation: Dedicated to Jupiter, the Best and Greatest, the Exacter, the Remover, the Dissolver; Marcus Philippus Maximus erected this.
EDCS-13301305.summary: This is a dedication by Marcus Philippus Maximus to Jupiter, personified with various epithets related to granting release, possibly from debt or obligation, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13301307.translation: Fabius Saturninus erected this in fulfillment of a vow to Diana.
EDCS-13301307.summary: This is a votive inscription by Fabius Saturninus to Diana, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13301308.translation: Aurelius Flavius, deprived of health, [dedicated this] to the Nymphs in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-13301308.summary: This is a votive inscription by Aurelius Flavius to the Nymphs, made when he was suffering from illness, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13301309.translation: Mariacca Asturicae Augustae willingly and deservedly paid her vow to the Nymphs.
EDCS-13301309.summary: This is a votive inscription by Mariacca Asturicae to the Nymphs, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13301310.translation: Montanus consecrated this to Bandua Verubrica in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-13301310.summary: This is a votive inscription by Montanus to the local deity Bandua Verubrica, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13301311.translation: Marcus Silonius Galeria Silanus, standard-bearer of the First Gallic Cohort of Roman Citizens, willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Bandua Veigebrea.
EDCS-13301311.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus Silonius Galeria Silanus, a soldier, to the local deity Bandua Veigebrea, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13301312.translation: Terentia Rufina willingly and deservedly paid her vow to Bandua Cadoga.
EDCS-13301312.summary: This is a votive inscription by Terentia Rufina to the local deity Bandua Cadoga, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13301313.translation: Faenirusa paid her vow at the altar of Bandua Alaniobrica.
EDCS-13301313.summary: This is a votive inscription by Faenirusa at an altar dedicated to the local deity Bandua Alaniobrica, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13301316.translation: To Reve Larauco, Vallius Aper fulfilled [this vow].
EDCS-13301316.summary: A private votive offering by Vallius Aper to the god Reve Larauco.

EDCS-13301319.summary: A private votive offering by Evs Maximus.

EDCS-13301323.translation: Luaecus Reburrus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-13301323.summary: A private votive offering by Luaecus Reburrus.

EDCS-13301324.translation: To Reve Augustus, Pastor from devotion [dedicated this].
EDCS-13301324.summary: A private votive offering by Pastor to the god Reve Augustus.

EDCS-13301330.translation: Cornelius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-13301330.summary: A private votive offering by Cornelius to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-13301331.translation: Apronia Rufa arranged for this to be made for her daughters Dovaecia Paterniana and Talavia Flavia.
EDCS-13301331.summary: A funerary dedication by Apronia Rufa for her two daughters.

EDCS-13301332.translation: Marcus Domitius [dedicated this] to his father Marcus Domitius Saturninus, and to his husband Valeria Mamilia.
EDCS-13301332.summary: A funerary dedication by Marcus Domitius to his father and husband.

EDCS-13301333.translation: Popillius Hirsutus, son of Flavia Vendiecus, from the village of Lancicum, of the Vacoeci tribe, aged 32, is buried here.
EDCS-13301333.summary: A funerary epitaph for Popillius Hirsutus, a 32-year-old man from Lancicum.

EDCS-13301334.translation: Aulus Flavius, son of Pucius, aged 5. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-13301334.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aulus Flavius, a 5-year-old child.

EDCS-13301336.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Coria, daughter of Albinus, aged 11. May the earth lie lightly upon her.
EDCS-13301336.summary: A funerary epitaph for Coria, an 11-year-old girl.

EDCS-13301337.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Rufinus, son of Rufus, aged 40, lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon him.
EDCS-13301337.summary: This is a tombstone for Rufinus, a man aged 40, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13301338.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Licinius Senus to his most devoted wife, Licinia Amanda.
EDCS-13301338.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Licinius Senus to his wife, Licinia Amanda, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13301347.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or a funerary marker, mentioning Titus Caesennius, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13301350.translation: To the genius of the camp.
EDCS-13301350.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the genius of a military camp, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13301522.translation: To Salvo Vitalis, from the workshop of Felix, greetings.
EDCS-13301522.summary: This inscription seems to be a dedication or a greeting to Salvo Vitalis from the workshop of Felix, possibly related to a trade or business, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13301526.translation: Mela gave this willingly to Libero.
EDCS-13301526.summary: This is a dedication by Mela to Libero, a votive or honorific inscription, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13301557.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary dedication or mention of a son, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13301560.summary: This inscription is a name, likely a funerary marker or a personal dedication, for Annius Nice Tertius, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13301565.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or funerary marker, mentioning Publius Cornelius, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13301581.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, mentioning Sergius twice, likely a dedication or funerary marker, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13301590.summary: This inscription records a freedman named Italicus.

EDCS-13301597.summary: This inscription records a patronymic, son of Quintus.

EDCS-13301663.summary: This is a Christian funerary inscription wishing life to Maximina.

EDCS-13301671.translation: Campanus Fidentinus, advisor of the legate Fulvius, in the service of the Emperor, from Olossita.
EDCS-13301671.summary: This inscription records Campanus Fidentinus, an advisor to the imperial legate Fulvius, likely from Olossita.

EDCS-13301672.translation: Titus Aurelius Fulvus Rufus, imperial legate, imperial legate of the Olossitani.
EDCS-13301672.summary: This inscription records Titus Aurelius Fulvus Rufus serving as an imperial legate for the people of Olossita.

EDCS-13301674.translation: Fulvius Rufus, imperial legate, Maturus, imperial procurator, the council of advocates of the Indicetani.
EDCS-13301674.summary: This inscription records the roles of Fulvius Rufus as imperial legate and Maturus as imperial procurator, along with the council of advocates of the Indicetani.

EDCS-13301679.summary: This inscription is a dedication to A. Minucius Felix Italicus.

EDCS-13301684.translation: To Rufo and his wife T. ae.
EDCS-13301684.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Rufo and his wife.

EDCS-13301685.translation: To the hero and to Annae Q., for themselves and...
EDCS-13301685.summary: This inscription records a dedication to a hero and Annae Q., for themselves and others.

EDCS-13302460.translation: Sunua, daughter of Tongi, fulfilled her vow to Victoria.
EDCS-13302460.summary: This inscription records Sunua, daughter of Tongi, fulfilling a vow to Victoria.

EDCS-13302624.translation: Gallus placed this for Jupiter Best and Greatest, willingly and with good spirit.
EDCS-13302624.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter, likely on an altar or shrine, placed by Gallus.

EDCS-13302634.translation: The pool of the oyster-fishery of Nero at the shore of Baiae.
EDCS-13302634.summary: This inscription seems to describe a location or feature related to Nero's oyster fisheries at Baiae.

EDCS-13400102.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Balaesus, son of Lucius, aged 30, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-13400102.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Balaesus, son of Lucius, indicating his age and expressing a wish for a light burial.

EDCS-13400103.translation: Rilburrinus, son of Rilburri, aged 11. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-13400103.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Rilburrinus, son of Rilburri, stating his age and a wish for a light burial.

EDCS-13400104.translation: Flavius Pri placed this for Jupiter Best and Greatest, fulfilling a vow willingly.
EDCS-13400104.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter, placed by Flavius Pri in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-13400139.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Flavius Sutorius placed this shell.
EDCS-13400139.summary: This is a dedication to the Spirits of the Departed by Flavius Sutorius, possibly related to a shell offering.

EDCS-13400175.translation: In the consulship of Appius Iunius Silanus and Publius Silius Nerva, Tillegus, son of Ambati, from Susarro, made a guest-friendship agreement at the fort of Aiobrigiaeco with the inhabitants of the fort of Toletum, Lougeis, for himself, his wife, his children, and their descendants, and the inhabitants of the fort of Toletum received him, and his wife, and his children, into their protection and patronage in perpetuity. This agreement was made by Tillegus, son of Ambati himself, before the local magistrates Latino Ari and Aio Temari.
EDCS-13400175.summary: This inscription records a formal guest-friendship and patronage agreement between Tillegus and the people of the fort of Toletum, likely during the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-13700419.translation: To the Nymphs. Caius Sulpicius Festus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-13700419.summary: This is a dedication to the Nymphs, made by Caius Sulpicius Festus in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-13700420.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed and the Nymphs. Simplicia willingly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-13700420.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled willingly by Simplicia for the Spirits of the Departed and the Nymphs.

EDCS-13700430.translation: To Nabiae. Rufina willingly fulfilled her vow, as deserved.
EDCS-13700430.summary: This is a dedication to Nabiae by Rufina, who willingly fulfilled a deserved vow.

EDCS-13800103.translation: The potter Maxuminis Caturo gave this gift.
EDCS-13800103.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a gift from a potter.

EDCS-13900142.translation: Aulus Cornelius Anteros, standard-bearer of the Seventh Twin and Fortunate Legion, with a detachment, willingly and deservedly paid his vow to the Nymphs.
EDCS-13900142.summary: This is a votive inscription by a standard-bearer of a Roman legion.

EDCS-13900383.translation: Camala, daughter of Arqui, from Talabriga, willingly and deservedly paid her vow to the Tiaurauceian Genius.
EDCS-13900383.summary: This is a votive inscription by a woman from Talabriga.

EDCS-13900409.translation: Gaius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, son of Germanicus Caesar, grandson of Tiberius Caesar Augustus, great-grandson of the deified Augustus, father of his country, chief priest, with the tribunician power for the fourth time, consul for the second time, two thousand...
EDCS-13900409.summary: This is a fragment of a titulature of Emperor Caligula, listing his honors and ancestry.

EDCS-13900614.translation: Albula Paterna paid her vow to the Mother of the Gods.
EDCS-13900614.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Mother of the Gods.

EDCS-13900616.translation: Gelasius Caesarianus to the Mother of the Gods.
EDCS-13900616.summary: This is a dedication to the Mother of the Gods by Gelasius Caesarianus.

EDCS-14700046.translation: A veteran of the Seventh Twin and Fortunate Legion for his parents, for himself, and for Te dia E...
EDCS-14700046.summary: This is a funerary inscription by a veteran of a Roman legion for his family and himself.

EDCS-14700048.translation: Marcus Titius Fronto of Turiasona made this guest charter for himself, his children, and descendants, with the same rights and under the same law as the people of Intercatia.
EDCS-14700048.summary: This is a guest-charter inscription establishing hospitality between an individual and a community.

EDCS-14700055.translation: V socae Caelius willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-14700055.summary: This is a votive inscription by Caelius, possibly indicating a dedication to a female deity.

EDCS-14700058.translation: Terentius Thussus, son of Aettus, aged 2, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-14700058.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a two-year-old child.

EDCS-14700075.translation: For myself and for mine, I give and hand over to those in the underworld by law: Neicia and Time and the others whom I justly cursed. For me, for mine, devoted and bound to the underworld, devoted and bound to the underworld. For Time and Neicia and the others whom I rightly devoted, above, for me, for mine, Time, Neicia, Neicia.
EDCS-14700075.summary: This bilingual inscription in Greek and Latin appears to be a magical text or curse tablet, dedicating individuals to the underworld for personal reasons.

EDCS-14700106.translation: Quintus Varius Latrianus willingly paid his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest, as was deserved.
EDCS-14700106.summary: This inscription records Quintus Varius Latrianus fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely on a personal altar or shrine.

EDCS-14700107.translation: Flavia Materna, pay your vows to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-14700107.summary: This inscription is an exhortation or command for Flavia Materna to pay her vows to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, possibly on a public monument.

EDCS-14700110.translation: Socilius Valerius the pious erected this sacred offering to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-14700110.summary: This inscription records the pious Socilius Valerius dedicating a sacred object or building to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-14700112.translation: Valerius Reburrus arranged for this sacred rite to be performed for Jupiter Best and Greatest; Pius paid a promised vow willingly and as deserved from his private funds.
EDCS-14700112.summary: This inscription details Valerius Reburrus overseeing a sacred rite and Pius fulfilling a private vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-14700115.translation: Ligarius Sabinus willingly paid his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest, as was deserved.
EDCS-14700115.summary: This inscription records Ligarius Sabinus fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely on a personal altar or shrine.

EDCS-14700120.translation: Valerius Uti Paulus, according to his vow, to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-14700120.summary: This inscription records Valerius Uti Paulus fulfilling a vow made to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-14700140.translation: Sulpicia Saturnina, according to her vow, to the Salutary Nymphs.
EDCS-14700140.summary: This inscription records Sulpicia Saturnina dedicating something to the Salutary Nymphs in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-14700143.translation: Silo, son of Silo, willingly paid his vow to the Tutela of Oberisi, as was deserved.
EDCS-14700143.summary: This inscription records Silo, son of Silo, fulfilling a vow to the local protective deity (Tutela) of Oberisi.

EDCS-14700144.translation: Marcus Ulpius Saturninus, according to his vow, to the Tutela of the municipium of Aquiflavia.
EDCS-14700144.summary: This inscription records Marcus Ulpius Saturninus dedicating something to the protective deity of the Roman municipality of Aquiflavia in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-14700149.translation: Capito Severus sets up this altar to holy Diana.
EDCS-14700149.summary: This inscription records a private dedication of an altar to the goddess Diana by Capito Severus.

EDCS-14700150.translation: To Concordia of the citizens of the municipium of Aquiflavia, Lucius Valerius Longinus from his own funds.
EDCS-14700150.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Concordia by Lucius Valerius Longinus, likely an official or a wealthy citizen, for the municipium of Aquiflavia.

EDCS-14700152.translation: Cornelia Saturnina to Isis, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-14700152.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the goddess Isis by Cornelia Saturnina.

EDCS-14700154.translation: To Isis and Serapis, with the sacred rites and mysteries, Gaius Calpurnius Rufinus, vir clarissimus.
EDCS-14700154.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Isis and Serapis by the distinguished official Gaius Calpurnius Rufinus, referencing participation in religious mysteries.

EDCS-14700159.translation: Flavius Flavianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Torolus Gombiciegus.
EDCS-14700159.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Flavius Flavianus, possibly to a local deity or heroic figure named Torolus Gombiciegus.

EDCS-14700164.translation: To Atilacus, Lucius Cornelius Placidus, centurion of the Seventh Legion Gemina Pia Felix, in fulfillment of a vow, deservedly.
EDCS-14700164.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to a deity named Atilacus by Lucius Cornelius Placidus, a centurion of the Seventh Legion Gemina.

EDCS-14700166.translation: Taurinus Laesus willingly fulfilled his vow to Elanicus.
EDCS-14700166.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Taurinus Laesus to a deity or spirit named Elanicus.

EDCS-14700168.translation: T. S. set up [this monument] to Vurebo.
EDCS-14700168.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by T. S. to someone named Vurebo.

EDCS-14700169.translation: Fronto, son of Vucanus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Revve Reumiraego.
EDCS-14700169.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Fronto, son of Vucanus, possibly to local deities or spirits.

EDCS-14700178.translation: Quintus Iulius Tiro willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Tilleno.
EDCS-14700178.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Quintus Iulius Tiro, possibly to a deity or spirit named Tilleno.

EDCS-14700179.translation: Nabiae Rufinus, son of Flavius Iunius, made this offering in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-14700179.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Nabiae Rufinus to the Nymphs (implied by Nabiae).

EDCS-14700181.translation: Silo, son of Silo, set this up for the household gods of the road.
EDCS-14700181.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Laribus Vialibus (gods of the road) by Silo.

EDCS-14700184.translation: Lucius Campanius Macer willingly and deservedly paid his vow to the household gods of Circeii.
EDCS-14700184.summary: This inscription records a votive offering willingly paid by Lucius Campanius Macer to the Laribus Circeiabaeco.

EDCS-14700185.translation: D. Ceraecus willingly and wholeheartedly paid his vow to the household gods of Belaecum.
EDCS-14700185.summary: This inscription records a votive offering willingly paid by D. Ceraecus to the Laribus Belaecis.

EDCS-14700186.translation: Maxumus, son of Trupeisi, Rufinus, willingly and deservedly paid his vow to the household gods of Gegeiquis.
EDCS-14700186.summary: This inscription records a votive offering willingly paid by Maxumus Trupeisi Rufinus to the Laribus Gegeiquis.

EDCS-14700188.translation: Marcus Licinius Veleiensis willingly and deservedly paid his vow to the household gods of Gumelaecus.
EDCS-14700188.summary: This inscription records a votive offering willingly paid by Marcus Licinius Veleiensis to the Laribus Gumelaecuis.

EDCS-14700189.translation: Albinus, son of Balesinus, willingly set this up for the household gods of Findeneticum.
EDCS-14700189.summary: This inscription records a dedication willingly made by Albinus Balesini to the Laribus Findeneticis.

EDCS-14700190.translation: Rufus made this offering in fulfillment of a vow to the household gods of Erredicum.
EDCS-14700190.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Rufus to the Laribus Erredicis.

EDCS-14700192.translation: Quintus Apanicus Capito willingly and deservedly paid his vow to the household god of Ocaela.
EDCS-14700192.summary: This inscription records a votive offering willingly paid by Quintus Apanicus Capito to the Lar Ocaelaego.

EDCS-14700193.translation: Flavia Sabina willingly and wholeheartedly made this dedication from her own devotion to the household gods of Coreaecum.
EDCS-14700193.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Flavia Sabina from her own devotion to the Laribus Coreaecis.

EDCS-14700229.translation: Laucius Quirina Rufinus [erected this] to Laucius Rufus, his father.
EDCS-14700229.summary: A funerary dedication by Laucius Quirina Rufinus to his father.

EDCS-14700230.summary: An ownership or familial inscription identifying Laucia Rufina.

EDCS-14700234.translation: Magius, son of Magius, from Clunium, aged 20, is buried here.
EDCS-14700234.summary: A funerary epitaph for Magius, a man aged 20 from Clunium.

EDCS-14700235.translation: Fuscus, son of Camalus, aged 70, is buried here. Fuscinus, son of Fuscus, [erected this] to his father.
EDCS-14700235.summary: A funerary epitaph for Fuscus and a dedication by his son Fuscinus.

EDCS-14700243.translation: Caius Couneancus, son of Fuscus, from Clunium, aged 40. Lucius Ascius, son of Ri, aged 30.
EDCS-14700243.summary: A funerary epitaph listing two individuals, Caius Couneancus and Lucius Ascius, with their ages and origins.

EDCS-14700245.translation: Reburrus, son of Viriatus, from Interamna.
EDCS-14700245.summary: An inscription identifying Reburrus, son of Viriatus, from Interamna.

EDCS-14700247.translation: To the departed spirits. Manlia Sutoria, while alive, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-14700247.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Manlia Sutoria for herself during her lifetime.

EDCS-14700249.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Senecianus, aged 25.
EDCS-14700249.summary: A funerary dedication to the departed spirits for Senecianus, aged 25.

EDCS-14700255.translation: To the departed spirits. To Marcus Gracilis, aged 20. May the earth rest lightly on you.
EDCS-14700255.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Gracilis, aged 20, with a common wish for the afterlife.

EDCS-14700256.translation: Iumuria, daughter of Turai, aged 55, is buried here.
EDCS-14700256.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iumuria, daughter of Turai, aged 55.

EDCS-14700260.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the heir of a deceased individual named Lucius Aullus.

EDCS-14700263.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Docia Reburri, who died at the age of 55.

EDCS-14700272.translation: Vinius, son of Cilvus, aged [ ]; Naria, daughter of Cloutus, aged 60.
EDCS-14700272.summary: This inscription likely records two individuals, Vinius and Naria, with their parentage and ages, possibly on a shared tomb.

EDCS-14700275.translation: To the divine Manes, for Flaus Festus, son of Festus, aged 31. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-14700275.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the Manes for Flaus Festus, son of Festus, aged 31.

EDCS-14700280.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name of Pintus Proculus.

EDCS-14700284.translation: Sacred to the divine Manes. Paterna, aged 35.
EDCS-14700284.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the Manes for Paterna, who died at the age of 35.

EDCS-14700289.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Reburrinus, son of Reburrus, who died at the age of 15.

EDCS-14700292.translation: Festus, son of Lovesius, from Interamna, from the fort of Louciocelo, is buried here, aged 3.
EDCS-14700292.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Festus, son of Lovesius, from Interamna and the fort of Louciocelo, who died at age 3.

EDCS-14700293.translation: To the divine Manes. For Lucius Pompeius Primus of Interamna, aged 75. Materna, daughter of Pompeia Ma[---], made this.
EDCS-14700293.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated to Lucius Pompeius Primus by his daughter Materna, specifying his age and origin.

EDCS-14700296.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sextus Equaesus, who died at the age of 45.

EDCS-14700297.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Pistis, son of Equaesus.

EDCS-14700303.translation: Anceitus, son of Vaccus, a Limicus from the curia of Talabriga, aged 30, is buried here. May the earth be light on you. His brother Flavus Aquilus and Talavius Cloutius, son of Cloutaus, and Urtienus, son of Turda, and his brothers had this made because of his merits.
EDCS-14700303.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Anceitus, a Limicus from Talabriga, detailing his age and the individuals who commissioned his tomb due to his merits.

EDCS-14700307.translation: Gaius Septimius, son of Cluniensis, aged 30, is buried here.
EDCS-14700307.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Gaius Septimius, son of Cluniensis, at the age of 30.

EDCS-14700308.translation: Gaius Licinius, son of Cluniensis, aged 25, is buried here.
EDCS-14700308.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Gaius Licinius, son of Cluniensis, at the age of 25.

EDCS-14700315.translation: Latius and Tiocta, on the fourth day before the Ides (of a month).
EDCS-14700315.summary: A short inscription possibly marking a date or an event involving Latius and Tiocta.

EDCS-14700326.summary: An incomplete funerary inscription indicating the death of Avitus at the age of 38.

EDCS-14700345.translation: Camalus, a Limicus from the curia of Livairum, is buried here. Julius...
EDCS-14700345.summary: An incomplete funerary inscription recording the burial of Camalus, a Limicus from Livairum, with a partial mention of Julius.

EDCS-14700352.translation: Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, pontifex maximus, imperator for the 5th time, consul for the 3rd time, with tribunician power.
EDCS-14700352.summary: This is a titulature inscription for Emperor Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, listing his honors and titles.

EDCS-14700353.translation: Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, pontifex maximus, imperator for the 5th time, consul for the 3rd time, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland. Bracara Augusta 38.
EDCS-14700353.summary: This titulature inscription for Emperor Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus also includes a reference to Bracara Augusta and the number 38, possibly related to distance or a count.

EDCS-14700354.translation: Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, pontifex maximus, imperator for the 5th time, consul for the 3rd time, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland. Bracara Augusta 58.
EDCS-14700354.summary: This titulature inscription for Emperor Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus also includes a reference to Bracara Augusta and the number 58, possibly related to distance or a count.

EDCS-14700358.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Germanicus Maximus, with tribunician power for the 17th time, imperator for the 2nd time, has restored the road from Bracara Augusta.
EDCS-14700358.summary: This is a dedication by the emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla) for the restoration of a road, likely from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-14700360.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Felix Invictus and Magnus Augustus, and to Marcus Opellius Antoninus Diadumenianus, most noble Caesar, prince of youth.
EDCS-14700360.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperors Macrinus and his son Diadumenianus, likely from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-14700362.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Trajanus Decius Invictus Augustus, with tribunician power for the 2nd time, father of the country, proconsul, has restored 6 miles of road by Herennius Etruscus Messius, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-14700362.summary: This is a dedication by Herennius Etruscus Messius, honoring Emperor Trajan Decius and his son for the restoration of a road, probably in the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-14700364.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Galerius Valerius Maximinuus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-14700364.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Galerius Valerius Maximinuus, likely from the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-14700365.translation: To our Lord, the Emperor Caesar Aurelius Marcus Valerius Maximianus.
EDCS-14700365.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Maximianus, likely from the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-14700366.translation: Our Lord Flavius Julius Constantius, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-14700366.summary: This is a dedication to Caesar Constantius (likely Constantius Chlorus), likely from the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-14700367.translation: To the Emperor Flavius Valerius Constantius, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-14700367.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Constantine I (Flavius Valerius Constantius), likely from the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-14700369.summary: This is a dedication to Flavius Dalmatian, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-14700371.translation: To our Lords, the Caesars Constantine and Flavius Claudius Constantine.
EDCS-14700371.summary: This is a dedication to the co-emperors Constantine I and Constantine II, likely from the first half of the 4th century CE.

EDCS-14700372.translation: To our Lord Magnus Magnentius, Pius Invictus, always Augustus, from Bracara.
EDCS-14700372.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Magnentius, likely from the mid-4th century CE.

EDCS-14700399.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the divine Severus Pius, grandson of the divine Marcus Antoninus, great-grandson of the divine Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus and the divine Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-14700399.summary: This is a titulary inscription for Emperor Caracalla, listing his full imperial name and lineage.

EDCS-14700402.translation: Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest German, greatest Dacian, greatest Sarmatian, chief priest, with the power of a tribune for the fifth time, imperator for the seventh time, father of the fatherland, consul, proconsul, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, greatest German, greatest Dacian, greatest Sarmatian, prince of the youth, son of our lord Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus, son, Pius Felix Augustus, have ordered the roads and bridges collapsed by age to be restored, with the work carried out by Quintus Decius, clarissimus vir, legate of the Augusti, pro praetore, 67 miles from Bracara.
EDCS-14700402.summary: This inscription records the restoration of roads and bridges ordered by Emperors Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus Caesar, with the work overseen by the legate Decius, and indicates the location relative to Bracara, likely from the period of their rule (235-238 AD).

EDCS-14700408.translation: To our lord Emperor Caesar Galerius Valerius Maximianus Pius Felix Invictus, proconsul.
EDCS-14700408.summary: This is an honorific inscription for Emperor Galerius, identifying him by his titles and role as proconsul, likely from the Tetrarchic period.

EDCS-14700410.translation: To Flavius Valerius Constantius, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-14700410.summary: This is an honorific inscription for Caesar Constantius Chlorus, identifying him by his name and title, likely from the Tetrarchic period.

EDCS-14700413.translation: To our lord Flavius Valerius Constantius, Invictus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-14700413.summary: This is an honorific inscription for Caesar Constantius Chlorus, emphasizing his invincibility, likely from the Tetrarchic period.

EDCS-14700414.translation: To our lord Flavius Valerius Constantinus, Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, for the 36th time.
EDCS-14700414.summary: This is an honorific inscription for Emperor Constantine the Great, listing his titles and indicating a regnal year (possibly related to his tribunician power or acclamations), likely from the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-14700416.translation: To Flavius Julius Constantius Augustus, most noble prince.
EDCS-14700416.summary: This is an honorific inscription for Emperor Constantius II, referring to him by his name, title, and status as a prince, likely from the mid-4th century AD.

EDCS-14700418.translation: To our lord Flavius Claudius Julianus, victor and triumphant, perpetually always Augustus, with Nevita and Mamertinus as consuls, 36 miles from Bracara.
EDCS-14700418.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Julian the Apostate, mentioning his victory and consular dating, and indicates a distance from Bracara, likely from the mid-4th century AD.

EDCS-14700435.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius, 30 miles.
EDCS-14700435.summary: This is a concise inscription dedicating a road marker or milestone to Emperor Marcus Aurelius, indicating a distance of 30 miles, likely from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14700445.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Claudius Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, chief priest, with the power of a tribune, consul, proconsul, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-14700445.summary: This is a titulary inscription for Emperor Claudius Gothicus, listing his full imperial name and high offices, likely from the late 3rd century AD.

EDCS-14700446.translation: To our lord Marcus Numerius Numerianus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-14700446.summary: This is an inscription honouring Marcus Numerius Numerianus, addressed as "most noble Caesar."

EDCS-14700447.translation: To Galerius Valerius Maximianus, Augustus, pious and fortunate.
EDCS-14700447.summary: This inscription honours Galerius Valerius Maximianus, referring to him as "Augustus, pious and fortunate."

EDCS-14700448.translation: To our lord Flavius Dalmatius, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-14700448.summary: This inscription honours Flavius Dalmatius, addressed as "most noble Caesar."

EDCS-14700460.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Valerius Maximianus, pious, fortunate, unconquered Augustus.
EDCS-14700460.summary: This inscription honours Emperor Caesar Gaius Valerius Maximianus, bestowing titles such as "pious, fortunate, unconquered Augustus."

EDCS-14700472.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Carinus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-14700472.summary: This inscription honours Marcus Aurelius Carinus, referring to him as "most noble Caesar."

EDCS-14700473.translation: To our lord Emperor Flavius Valerius Constantius, pious, fortunate, unconquered Augustus, chief priest.
EDCS-14700473.summary: This inscription honours Emperor Flavius Valerius Constantius, calling him "pious, fortunate, unconquered Augustus, chief priest."

EDCS-14700479.translation: To Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajanus Augustus Germanicus, chief priest, in his seventh year of tribunician power, acclaimed imperator four times, consul five times, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-14700479.summary: This inscription details the titles and honours of Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajanus Augustus Germanicus, including his tribunician power, consulships, and other prestigious designations.

EDCS-14700487.translation: To the Emperor Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus, chief priest, in his second year of tribunician power, consul, father of the fatherland, from the community of the Aquiflavians.
EDCS-14700487.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the community of Aquiflavians to Emperor Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus, listing his various titles and honours.

EDCS-14700493.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Aurelius Antoninus.

EDCS-14700508.translation: Terminus of Trebius. Terminus of Obilius.
EDCS-14700508.summary: This inscription appears to be a boundary marker, possibly indicating the property divisions of Trebius and Obilius.

EDCS-14700510.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sigenata Pacata.

EDCS-14700511.translation: The Senate House, belonging to the sons of the colony, is cold.
EDCS-14700511.summary: This inscription likely refers to the state of the curia (Senate House) of a colony, possibly indicating its lack of heating or a general feeling of coldness, with 'S F' possibly standing for 'Senatus Foederatus' or 'Senatus Coloniarum'.

EDCS-15000042.translation: Auge, daughter of Cilea Milnia, placed her vow.
EDCS-15000042.summary: This is a votive inscription by Auge, daughter of Cilea Milnia, dedicating something in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15300212.translation: Buried [in honor of] Jupiter the Thunderer.
EDCS-15300212.summary: This is a funerary inscription or dedication to Jupiter Fulgur, possibly indicating a burial site or a votive offering related to thunder.

EDCS-15300213.translation: Cornelius Celsus and Cassia Materna [dedicated this] to the Mothers.
EDCS-15300213.summary: This is a dedication by Cornelius Celsus and Cassia Materna to the Matres (Mothers), a group of deities often associated with fertility and local cults.

EDCS-15300340.translation: Maxumus had this monument made for Blendea, aged 2.
EDCS-15300340.summary: A monument was erected by Maxumus for a child named Blendea, dated by the style of the inscription to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15500071.translation: Lovesius, son of Pugi, arranged for this to be made for himself and his daughter Boutea, aged 11.
EDCS-15500071.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Lovesius for himself and his daughter, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15700047.summary: A dedication or epitaph for Proculus, son of Caeni, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15700048.translation: For Docio, son of Magilo, aged 65. He lies here; may the earth be light on you.
EDCS-15700048.summary: An epitaph for Docio, son of Magilo, who died at age 65, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15700049.translation: For Arquius, son of Talaius, aged 30.
EDCS-15700049.summary: An epitaph for Arquius, son of Talaius, who died at age 30, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15900062.translation: Arcadius rests here in peace, aged about 50, hoping for resurrection from God.
EDCS-15900062.summary: A Christian epitaph for Arcadius, who died around age 50, reflecting belief in resurrection, likely from the late Roman or early Byzantine period.

EDCS-15900063.translation: To the memory of Marcus Aurelius Successus, freedman of the Augusti, sub-procurator of the XX hereditatium, [erected by] Ulpia Eutychis, his most sweet wife.
EDCS-15900063.summary: A dedication to Marcus Aurelius Successus, a freedman and imperial official, by his wife, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15900370.translation: Lucius Versenus Aper, prefect of the cavalry wing, having begun and completed the bath works under his command, gladly paid his vow to Fortune.
EDCS-15900370.summary: Lucius Versenus Aper, a cavalry prefect, dedicated a bath complex and fulfilled a vow to Fortune during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16200657.translation: Claudius Capito placed this for Tutela Bolgensis for his own safety and that of his family, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-16200657.summary: Claudius Capito dedicated this to Tutela Bolgensis for the well-being of himself and his family, fulfilling a vow during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16200659.translation: Lucius Herennius Callicus, son of Lucius, from Ugia, a soldier of the tenth legion Gemina, in the century of Licinius Clemens, aged 29, paid 9 asses, lies here.
EDCS-16200659.summary: An epitaph for Lucius Herennius Callicus, a soldier of the Legio X Gemina, who died at age 29, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16200666.translation: Sacred to Apollo. Dumus dedicated it.
EDCS-16200666.summary: This is a dedication to the god Apollo by an individual named Dumus.

EDCS-16200667.translation: From the workshops of Lucius Severinus.
EDCS-16200667.summary: This inscription indicates the origin of a product or object from the workshops of Lucius Severinus.

EDCS-16200954.translation: The next one, Pollen, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-16200954.summary: This inscription records Pollen fulfilling a vow, likely to a deity.

EDCS-16201685.translation: The republic of the Tutugians (dedicated this) to the divine Julia Augusta, grandmother of our lord Emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-16201685.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the community of Tutugi to Empress Julia Augusta, grandmother of Emperor Severus Alexander.

EDCS-16300113.translation: Caucets, son of Coironicus, and Ita and Nuana, from Uxama, and Acces Cauca, had this set up by his own people.
EDCS-16300113.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a group of people, possibly family members, for Cauceus and others associated with Uxama.

EDCS-16300118.translation: Decumus had this set up for his mother Magna.
EDCS-16300118.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Decumus for his mother, Magna.

EDCS-16300119.translation: Monova, daughter of Calaeticus, (dedicated this) to Anna.
EDCS-16300119.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Monova to Anna.

EDCS-16300120.translation: Acca Lecira, daughter of Caburatus, made this for (her) mother.
EDCS-16300120.summary: This is a funerary or dedication inscription made by Acca Lecira for her mother.

EDCS-16300122.translation: Flaccus, son of Albinus, willingly fulfilled his vow to the Eletenses of Aquae.
EDCS-16300122.summary: This inscription records Flaccus, son of Albinus, fulfilling a vow to the people of Aquae Eletenses.

EDCS-16300365.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. The associates place Marco Magio Vitali of Laxcuta, aged 75, here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-16300365.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marco Magio Vitali of Laxcuta, set up by his associates, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-16400424.summary: A very short inscription, likely a Christian profession of faith or a reference to a divine judgment concerning Christ, possibly from late antiquity.

EDCS-16700132.translation: Anderca, daughter of Caturon, aged sixteen, is buried here.
EDCS-16700132.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of a young woman named Anderca, daughter of Caturon, who died at the age of sixteen.

EDCS-16900058.translation: Aninius Africanus, son of Caerrus Africanus, willingly fulfilled his vow as deserved.
EDCS-16900058.summary: A dedication recording that Aninius Africanus fulfilled a vow, likely to a deity, and mentioning his parentage.

EDCS-16900274.summary: A fragmentary inscription that appears to be the beginning of a dedication or a funerary monument belonging to Publius Valerius Euryalus.

EDCS-16900290.translation: The sacred place was consecrated to Saint Mary by Bishop Asterius, on the day before the Nones of May, in the sixteenth year of our glorious lord Reccared.
EDCS-16900290.summary: A dedication consecrating a place to Saint Mary by Bishop Asterius, dated to the reign of King Reccared, likely in the Visigothic Kingdom.

EDCS-16900318.translation: From Tingis, according to the vow made to Usos.
EDCS-16900318.summary: A short dedication indicating the origin (Tingis) and that a vow was fulfilled for the deity Usos.

EDCS-16900320.translation: Dedicated to Mercury. Aulus D O Vibius, in the consulship of A Probus and Pomponius Victorinus, made this.
EDCS-16900320.summary: A dedication to the god Mercury, made by Aulus D O Vibius and dated by the consuls A Probus and Pomponius Victorinus.

EDCS-16900340.summary: A very brief inscription that appears to be a name, likely part of a funerary monument or a dedication.

EDCS-16900342.summary: A very brief inscription that appears to be a name, likely part of a funerary monument or a dedication.

EDCS-16900343.translation: From Conso, son of Sensius. Publius Arquius Clemens Gigurrus.
EDCS-16900343.summary: An inscription listing two names, possibly indicating parentage or origin for Conso and identifying Publius Arquius Clemens Gigurrus.

EDCS-16900344.translation: From the workshop of Severus Bovatis.
EDCS-16900344.summary: This is a maker's mark or workshop inscription from Severus Bovatis.

EDCS-16900412.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely referring to a centurion named Sergius.

EDCS-16900421.translation: Tiberius Claudius Quintinus was here, warlike, teachable, and agile. The nymph, whom you all fear, calls me Martina; she made me. I died on the sixth day before the Kalends of April.
EDCS-16900421.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tiberius Claudius Quintinus, who died on March 27th, likely in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-16900425.translation: Hail Lucius Panius, hail Lucius Panius! Greetings, Iulius Vernius.
EDCS-16900425.summary: This is a greeting or salutation, possibly from a playful or informal context, mentioning Lucius Panius and Iulius Vernius.

EDCS-16900427.translation: Archdeacon Salonius will rest here in peace.
EDCS-16900427.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Archdeacon Salonius.

EDCS-16900428.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Aurelius erected this at his own expense.
EDCS-16900428.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Marcus Aurelius for Edsuri.

EDCS-18100003.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Lucius Cornelius Domesticus [dedicated this].
EDCS-18100003.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius Cornelius Domesticus.

EDCS-18100022.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest God. Quintus Maenius Asiaticus erected this, fulfilling his vow with a willing spirit.
EDCS-18100022.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Quintus Maenius Asiaticus.

EDCS-18100023.translation: To Aesculapius. Tertia Flacilla, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-18100023.summary: This is a votive dedication to Aesculapius by Tertia Flacilla.

EDCS-18100026.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest God. Quintus Vanilo Lagius [dedicated this].
EDCS-18100026.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Quintus Vanilo Lagius.

EDCS-18100030.translation: Marcus Melvius Flavius Pamudeno willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-18100030.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus Melvius Flavius Pamudeno, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-18100032.translation: Our inheritance, from Eufrax, son of Eusan, son of Quinedus, son of Aeacus, son of Flavius Ruve, year 614.
EDCS-18100032.summary: This is a Latin inscription recording an inheritance and a date in the era of the province, likely AD 557, from a Roman provincial context.

EDCS-18100033.translation: Reve Aradaego, Arruntius Silvanus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-18100033.summary: This is a votive inscription by Arruntius Silvanus on behalf of Reve Aradaego, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-18100034.translation: Villanus the pious erected this for Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-18100034.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Villanus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-18100036.translation: M. Tertiolus erected this for the Turolic Larvae in accordance with his vow.
EDCS-18100036.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Turolic Larvae by M. Tertiolus, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-18100038.translation: Lucius Bassus, in accordance with his vow, for Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-18100038.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius Bassus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-18100044.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Galerius Valerius Maximianus, pious, fortunate, the victorious Augustus.
EDCS-18100044.summary: This is a formal dedication or acclamation to the Roman Emperor Galerius Valerius Maximianus, dated to his reign (AD 305-311).

EDCS-18100050.translation: To our Lord Emperor Caesar Magnentius, the pious, fortunate, invincible, always Augustus, and Emperor Caesar Gaius Valerius Maximianus.
EDCS-18100050.summary: This inscription honors two Roman Emperors, Magnentius and Maximian, likely from the mid-4th century AD.

EDCS-18100051.translation: Sacred to Mars. Velinus of the II Flaviae cavalry regiment erected this at his own expense.
EDCS-18100051.summary: This is a military dedication to Mars by Velinus of the II Flaviae cavalry, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-18100052.summary: This is a short inscription, possibly a dedication or mention of a person named Reve Anabaraego, from the Roman provincial period.

EDCS-18100054.translation: To the preserving god Mars, Nakius willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-18100054.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Mars, dedicated by Nakius.

EDCS-18100055.translation: To Guleis Vamitugaicis, Flavius Flavus willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-18100055.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to the deities Guleis Vamitugaicis by Flavius Flavus.

EDCS-18100059.translation: Rufus [dedicated this] to Jupiter Best and Greatest from his own property.
EDCS-18100059.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Rufus, likely from his own resources.

EDCS-18100063.translation: Allecius, son of Bibalius, a villager of Nemetobriga, erected this; may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-18100063.summary: A funerary monument erected by Allecius for a deceased person, possibly himself, in Nemetobriga.

EDCS-18100064.translation: Sacred to the Dis Manes. Flavius Fuscus [dedicated this] to his kinsman Marco Claudio Eoivesuli Ciscon, and to my dearest kinsman, I, Cusnilo.
EDCS-18100064.summary: A funerary dedication to the spirits of the departed (Dis Manes) by Flavius Fuscus for his kinsman Marco Claudio Eoivesuli Ciscon and his own kinsman Cusnilo.

EDCS-18100069.translation: Cosconius Flavinus to Tutela, according to his father's vow.
EDCS-18100069.summary: A dedication to the goddess Tutela made by Cosconius Flavinus, fulfilling his father's vow.

EDCS-18100070.translation: Latius Tiocta, the fourth day before the Ides [of some month].
EDCS-18100070.summary: An inscription recording a name, Latius Tiocta, with a date.

EDCS-18100073.summary: An inscription likely recording ownership or a dedication related to Flavinius, a cavalryman.

EDCS-18100079.translation: Son of Caenonis, aged 57. Here lies [buried] your wife; may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-18100079.summary: A funerary inscription for a woman, wife of Caenonis, who died at 57, with a wish for the deceased.

EDCS-18100189.summary: An incomplete inscription mentioning the name Silvanus and his age.

EDCS-18100203.translation: Talaus, son of Nimius, from Arus, son of Titus Licinius.
EDCS-18100203.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying an individual and their parentage.

EDCS-18100215.translation: On the thirteenth day before the Kalends of [Month], Montanus dedicated this.
EDCS-18100215.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Montanus on a specific date.

EDCS-18100227.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying an individual and their parentage.

EDCS-18100246.translation: In memory of Antistius Augustalis, who lived 50 years.
EDCS-18100246.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Antistius Augustalis and his lifespan.

EDCS-18100250.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying an individual and their parentage.

EDCS-18100255.translation: Felicia, sister, had this made for Maurula, freedwoman of Gaius, a member of the Sodales, who lived 60 years.
EDCS-18100255.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Felicia for her sister Maurula, a freedwoman, indicating the familial relationship and the deceased's age.

EDCS-18100259.translation: Sacred to Mavo. Lucius Aemilius Lupercus [dedicated this].
EDCS-18100259.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Mavo by Lucius Aemilius Lupercus.

EDCS-18100270.summary: This inscription is too fragmented to be translated or summarized.

EDCS-18100272.translation: To Optata Sabina, slave, aged 27 years.
EDCS-18100272.summary: This funerary inscription records the name and age of a slave named Optata Sabina.

EDCS-18100274.translation: To the Divine Shades, Anua, daughter of Flavia, aged 12 years. To the Divine Shades, Flavius Caesarius, aged 3 years. To the Divine Shades, Flavius Avitus, aged 52 years.
EDCS-18100274.summary: This funerary inscription lists three deceased individuals from the same family, including their ages, dedicated to the 'Dis Manibus' (Divine Shades).

EDCS-18100277.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a person named Cloutus, who died at the age of seventy.

EDCS-18100279.translation: He gave and dedicated to Fortune.
EDCS-18100279.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Fortune, likely from a private individual.

EDCS-18100281.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavius Placidus, who died at the age of eighty.

EDCS-18100282.translation: To Elguenus, son of..., aged three years.
EDCS-18100282.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a child named Elguenus, who died at the age of three.

EDCS-18100283.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Amma, aged forty years.
EDCS-18100283.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Amma, who died at the age of forty.

EDCS-18100286.translation: To Caburus, son of Arrenus, aged thirty-something years.
EDCS-18100286.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caburus, son of Arrenus, whose exact age is partially lost.

EDCS-18100290.translation: To Lucius Ve[...], son of C[...]
EDCS-18100290.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, mentioning a person named Lucius.

EDCS-18100297.translation: To Terentinus, son of Terentius, aged fifty years.
EDCS-18100297.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Terentinus, son of Terentius, who died at the age of fifty.

EDCS-18100298.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. BA AP
EDCS-18100298.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely for a person whose name is abbreviated or lost.

EDCS-18100301.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Augustus, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-18100301.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated to Emperor Augustus, likely during his lifetime or shortly after.

EDCS-18100302.translation: To Lucius Sempronius Quintilianus Marius, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-18100302.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Lucius Sempronius Quintilianus Marius, son of Quintus.

EDCS-18100303.translation: To Aemilia Atta, daughter of Plendovius, mother.
EDCS-18100303.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Aemilia Atta, daughter of Plendovius, identified as a mother.

EDCS-18100304.translation: To Terentia Appia, daughter of Terentius, for herself.
EDCS-18100304.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Terentia Appia, daughter of Terentius, for her own burial.

EDCS-18100305.translation: To Tiberius Claudius Pressus, son of Aemilius, of the Quirina tribe, quaestor of the people of Araugustana, priest in Rome, and elector of the Augusti, by the Emperor Galba Augustus, by Aemilia Alla and Aemilla Auga, for their father.
EDCS-18100305.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Tiberius Claudius Pressus, a notable official, likely from the Flavian period given the mention of Galba, set up by two women.

EDCS-18100309.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Eucte, a freedwoman.

EDCS-18100330.translation: To Lucilla Aboena, wife of Cilurnus, aged 101.
EDCS-18100330.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucilla Aboena, wife of Cilurnus, who lived to be 101 years old.

EDCS-18100331.translation: The kinship group dedicated this to their kinsman Aemilius Balaesus, standard-bearer of the ala Sabiniania.
EDCS-18100331.summary: This inscription records a dedication by a kinship group to Aemilius Balaesus, a standard-bearer in a cavalry unit.

EDCS-18100345.translation: Rovinus placed this milestone, marking 29 miles, for our lord Constantius, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-18100345.summary: This is a milestone inscription from the Constantinian period, indicating distance and dedicated to Caesar Constantius.

EDCS-18100346.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Paterna, aged 35.
EDCS-18100346.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Paterna, aged 35.

EDCS-18100348.translation: Flavia Sabina willingly placed this for the Lares of Coreaecus out of her devotion.
EDCS-18100348.summary: This is a votive inscription by Flavia Sabina dedicated to the Lares of Coreaecus.

EDCS-18100350.translation: Sextus Antistius, son of Sextus, from Narbonne, rests here.
EDCS-18100350.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Sextus Antistius, who originated from Narbonne.

EDCS-18100353.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Daecus to Selene.

EDCS-18100355.translation: The burial and Flavius Maternianus lived.
EDCS-18100355.summary: This funerary inscription marks the burial and notes the life of Flavius Maternianus.

EDCS-18100356.summary: This inscription consists of the names Maximus Flavius, possibly a funerary marker or a dedication.

EDCS-18100363.translation: To the Tutela of the municipality of Aquiflavium.

EDCS-18100376.translation: Flavia Duerta, daughter of Fuscius, aged 40, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.

EDCS-20301637.summary: This inscription records the ownership or dedication of Caius Lutatius Primus.

EDCS-20301686.summary: This inscription records the ownership or dedication of Lucius Gelidius Quadratus.

EDCS-20301693.summary: This inscription identifies the workshop of Silvanus.

EDCS-20301713.translation: Belonging to Felix, son of Lucius Umbricius.
EDCS-20301713.summary: This inscription records the ownership or dedication of Felix, son of Lucius Umbricius.

EDCS-20301739.summary: This inscription identifies the workshop of Lucius Faenius.

EDCS-20301764.summary: This inscription records the ownership or dedication of Aurelius Nicephorus.

EDCS-20301795.summary: This inscription identifies the workshop of Lucius Celsus.

EDCS-20301798.translation: From the colony of Caesaraugusta, Paccius Gallus.
EDCS-20301798.summary: This inscription records Paccius Gallus from the colony of Caesaraugusta.

EDCS-20400015.translation: From the workshop of F. Melybus, daughter of Marcus Africanus, Maxima.
EDCS-20400015.summary: This inscription identifies the workshop of F. Melybus, daughter of Marcus Africanus, who was named Maxima.

EDCS-20400016.translation: From the workshop of F. Melybus for Marcia, daughter of Marcus, Africania Maxima.
EDCS-20400016.summary: This inscription seems to record a tombstone or funerary monument dedicated to Marcia Africania Maxima, indicating her patronymic and filiation.

EDCS-20400018.translation: From the workshop of F. Melybus for Marcia, daughter of Marcus, Africania Maxima.
EDCS-20400018.summary: This inscription seems to record a tombstone or funerary monument dedicated to Marcia Africania Maxima, indicating her patronymic and filiation.

EDCS-20400041.translation: Placidus Placidius, in fulfillment of a vow, for the goddess Tutela of the Paesici people.
EDCS-20400041.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Tutela by Placidus Placidius on behalf of the Paesici people.

EDCS-20400064.translation: To Publius Minucius, son of Publius, from Gaius Minucius, son of Gaius, his grandfather.
EDCS-20400064.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Publius Minucius, son of Publius, by his paternal grandfather, Gaius Minucius.

EDCS-20400119.translation: Tile from the estate of Paul (Paulus).
EDCS-20400119.summary: This is a property mark on a roof tile indicating ownership or origin from the estate of a person named Paulus.

EDCS-20400236.summary: This is a dedication to a local deity named Cosus Sucivus.

EDCS-20400240.translation: Lucius P. Valentinus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-20400240.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Lucius P. Valentinus fulfilling a vow willingly.

EDCS-20400244.summary: This is a simple dedication or maker's mark, likely on a monument or object, stating that Ianuarius created it.

EDCS-20400245.translation: Flaccus Avitus willingly paid his vow to Tutela of Calubrigum for his son.
EDCS-20400245.summary: This is a votive inscription by Flaccus Avitus to the local deity Tutela of Calubrigum, offered for the well-being of his son.

EDCS-20400278.translation: Ana placed this monument to the spirits of the departed, [for] Coelius, aged [---] years.
EDCS-20400278.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Coelius, with Ana as the dedicator, intended for the Manes (spirits of the dead).

EDCS-20400287.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tergidus Gallus, likely recording his lifespan.

EDCS-20400288.translation: To the departed spirits. Minucius is buried here.
EDCS-20400288.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits and recording the burial of Minucius.

EDCS-20400290.translation: Sevius and Res made this for Mercury, deservedly.
EDCS-20400290.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Sevius and Res to Mercury.

EDCS-20400317.translation: Marciana, may you live for many years, mistress.
EDCS-20400317.summary: This is a wish for a long life addressed to Marciana.

EDCS-20400318.translation: This monument, Anna Doidena, daughter of Annius, aged 18, had Aravo of Arauco arrange to be made. Pentovius of Pesaga, son of Ama, aged 17, erected this monument at his own expense.
EDCS-20400318.summary: This is a funerary monument for two individuals, Anna Doidena and Pentovius, detailing their ages and the arrangements for their burial.

EDCS-20400319.translation: Silius Eorinus, of the sacrifices, the lake.
EDCS-20400319.summary: This inscription appears to relate to sacrifices and possibly a location called 'the lake' associated with Silius Eorinus.

EDCS-20400334.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Saturninus of Aventicum, aged 45.
EDCS-20400334.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits for Saturninus of Aventicum, who was 45 years old.

EDCS-20400370.summary: This inscription is a dedication or memorial belonging to Antonia Philete.

EDCS-20400372.translation: To the departed spirits. Aemilius Onso is buried here, aged 80, with his son.
EDCS-20400372.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits, recording the burial of Aemilius Onso, aged 80, and mentioning his son.

EDCS-20400379.translation: For the safety of Pompeius Carinus, Pompeia Flacus's daughter Nementina Atemniae willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-20400379.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Nementina Atemniae for the well-being of Pompeius Carinus.

EDCS-20400383.translation: To the holy Tutelo, Julia Vern [dedicated it].
EDCS-20400383.summary: This is a dedication to a deity, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-20400409.translation: From the workshop of Quintus Sem[pronius?].
EDCS-20400409.summary: This inscription indicates the origin of a manufactured item, possibly pottery or metalwork, from the workshop of Quintus Sempronius, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-20400418.summary: This is an epigraphic record of a bridge construction by Archetes, likely from the Roman Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-20400440.summary: This inscription appears to be a graffito or personal marker, possibly of early Christian origin due to the term 'alleluia'.

EDCS-20400444.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Primo Aruntinius, recording his age, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400447.translation: Camalia, daughter of Boutis, aged 20, is buried here.
EDCS-20400447.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Camalia, daughter of Boutis, who died at the age of 20, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400452.translation: To Terentius Iustus, son of Flaccus, of the Quirina tribe, aged 45.
EDCS-20400452.summary: This is a dedication or epitaph for Terentius Iustus, son of Flaccus, a member of the Quirina tribe, aged 45, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400453.summary: This is likely a funerary epitaph for Ammo, son of Balaes, who died at the age of 50, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400454.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Chilo, son of Arcon, who died at the age of 13, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400458.translation: To Valeria Titula, who gave her vow, aged 10, is buried here.
EDCS-20400458.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Titula, who died at the age of 10 and is noted for having made a vow, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400459.translation: Lucius Plexsena Quartio, aged 40, is buried here; his son Lucius Plexsena and his wife Vibia Rufilla saw to its construction.
EDCS-20400459.summary: A funerary monument for Lucius Plexsena Quartio, constructed by his son and wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400460.translation: To Caburia, daughter of Caelenus, aged 50.
EDCS-20400460.summary: A funerary inscription for Caburia, daughter of Caelenus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400461.translation: To Cloutius, son of Arcon, aged 26.
EDCS-20400461.summary: A funerary inscription for Cloutius, son of Arcon, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400464.translation: To Totonus, son of Arcon, aged 50.
EDCS-20400464.summary: A funerary inscription for Totonus, son of Arcon, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400475.translation: IKAR, son of Arcobriga, son of Go, son of Gidosqum.
EDCS-20400475.summary: A patronymic inscription, likely from a provincial Roman context, possibly recording ancestry or tribal affiliation.

EDCS-20400476.summary: A funerary or commemorative inscription naming two individuals, Valerius Flavianus and Valerius Flavus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400496.translation: To Germanicus Maximus, chief priest, in his 17th year of tribunician power, thrice emperor, twice consul, father of the fatherland, proconsul; Bracara, 14 miles.
EDCS-20400496.summary: A milestone inscription dating to the reign of Germanicus Caesar (likely Germanicus Julius Caesar, adopted son of Tiberius), indicating a distance of 14 miles to the city of Bracara.

EDCS-20400499.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, our lord Magnentius, victorious and triumphant, forever, always Augustus, conqueror of the Alamanni, greatest, father of the fatherland, proconsul, emperor twice, born for the good of the state.
EDCS-20400499.summary: A panegyric inscription honoring Emperor Magnentius, likely erected during his reign (350-353 AD).

EDCS-20400514.translation: Meducea, daughter of Meduenus, Roman citizen. Pentamus Arcius made this.
EDCS-20400514.summary: A funerary monument for Meducea, daughter of Meduenus, constructed by Pentamus Arcius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400524.translation: Emperor Nerva Caesar Augustus, chief priest, 111.
EDCS-20400524.summary: An inscription mentioning Emperor Nerva Caesar Augustus, likely related to a building or public work, possibly indicating a date or a numerical designation associated with the reign.

EDCS-20400526.translation: Sacred to the Eternal God, Lucius Pomponius Fundanus, with all his family, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-20400526.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius Pomponius Fundanus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400531.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Augustus, Titus, son of Titus, [---]
EDCS-20400531.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely dedicated to an emperor, possibly Augustus, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401601.translation: To the Nymphs, Marcus Ulpius Longinianus.
EDCS-20401601.summary: This is a dedication to the Nymphs by Marcus Ulpius Longinianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401602.translation: To the Nymphs, Lucius Valerius M[---]
EDCS-20401602.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication to the Nymphs by Lucius Valerius M[---], likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401605.summary: This inscription simply records the name Marcus Hortensius Maximinianus, likely an owner or dedicant, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401606.translation: At the expense of a vow, with Germanus in charge.
EDCS-20401606.summary: This inscription indicates that an undertaking was completed fulfilling a vow, with Germanus supervising, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401609.summary: This inscription records the name Lucius Larcius Claudianus, likely a dedicator or owner, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401640.summary: This inscription records the name Quintus E Merula, likely a dedicator or owner, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401645.summary: This inscription appears to record a quantity or measurement related to 'Ihisus', possibly a place or unit, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20900130.summary: This inscription identifies the owner of an urn as Gnaeus Vesvianus, likely from a funerary context in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20900157.translation: I am Paulinus Paulinus, beware of harm.
EDCS-20900157.summary: This is a personal inscription, possibly a graffito, where the author identifies himself and warns against misfortune.

EDCS-20900164.summary: This inscription is a simple ownership or dedication formula, likely found on a tomb or object belonging to Lucius Porcius Epatrus.

EDCS-20900167.summary: This inscription likely denotes ownership or is part of a funerary context for Faustinus Rarus.

EDCS-20900178.translation: For Pericarpus, Spurius Alphius Firmanus [dedicated this].
EDCS-20900178.summary: This inscription records a dedication, likely funerary or votive, made by Spurius Alphius Firmanus for Pericarpus.

EDCS-20900199.summary: This inscription records a dedication to P. P. Peregrinus, potentially on a tombstone or public monument.

EDCS-20900206.summary: This inscription seems to indicate that Marcus Aestivus holds a position of authority or responsibility, possibly in a public or private context.

EDCS-20900212.summary: This inscription identifies Iunia Aditta and her profession as a weaver, likely in a funerary or property context.

EDCS-20900224.summary: This inscription is likely a funerary dedication or an indication of ownership for Lucius Licinius Chrestus.

EDCS-21000052.summary: This inscription is a dedication or ownership mark belonging to Quintus Valerius Admetus, possibly on a tomb or object.

EDCS-21000063.summary: This is a simple inscription likely identifying Marcus Cosconius, possibly on a tomb, property, or artifact.

EDCS-21000065.summary: This is a fragment of a dedication to Lucius Cornelius Pr...

EDCS-21000066.summary: This is a fragment of a dedication to Lucius Cornelius Pro...

EDCS-21000067.translation: Eros, freedman of Quintus Cornelius Niger...
EDCS-21000067.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription dedicated by Eros, a freedman of Quintus Cornelius Niger.

EDCS-21000068.summary: This is a fragment naming Quintus Cornelius Fuscus.

EDCS-21000070.summary: This is a fragment of a dedication to Quintus Valerius.

EDCS-21000071.translation: Abdas, freedman of Gaius Licinius V...
EDCS-21000071.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription mentioning Abdas, a freedman of Gaius Licinius V...

EDCS-21000079.summary: This is a fragment naming Marcus Porcius M...

EDCS-21000081.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription related to Sextus Domitius Saturio.

EDCS-21000082.summary: This is a fragment of a dedication to Sextus Domitius Tibisius.

EDCS-21000083.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription from Sextus Domitius Atius...

EDCS-21000084.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely referring to Gaius Mussidius Nepos, possibly as an owner or honoree.

EDCS-21000085.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Marcus Valerius Gal[---], likely a personal name.

EDCS-21000119.summary: This inscription identifies Philo as the slave of someone named Marcus.

EDCS-21000120.summary: This inscription identifies Sus as the slave of someone named Sextus.

EDCS-21000188.translation: May the god Ares preserve your life
EDCS-21000188.summary: This is a personal dedication or prayer to the god Ares, asking for the preservation of life.

EDCS-21200007.translation: May you live, Marturi; make use [of life]
EDCS-21200007.summary: This is a hopeful inscription wishing the person named Marturi a long and well-lived life.

EDCS-21200008.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name and age of Lucius Vettius Pothus.

EDCS-21200011.summary: This is a short inscription, likely a votive or good-wish, addressed to Primitiva.

EDCS-21200013.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or ownership mark belonging to Titus Quintus Antiquus Capito.

EDCS-21300884.summary: This inscription records a votive offering dedicated by Apronus.

EDCS-21500432.translation: I, daughter of Caius, [set this up] for Marcus Octavius, son of Marcus, Tiberius M[...].
EDCS-21500432.summary: This inscription seems to record a dedication or memorial set up by a woman for Marcus Octavius.

EDCS-21500445.summary: This inscription likely records the name of an individual, possibly as a dedication or ownership mark.
