EDCS-21500450.summary: This inscription records the name of Lucius Pompeius Saturio, possibly as a dedication or ownership mark.

EDCS-21500459.summary: This inscription is a dedication or refers to a monument associated with Saint Julian the martyr.

EDCS-21700114.translation: [...]cus, son of Ambatus, aged [...], lies here.
EDCS-21700114.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name and age of a deceased individual.

EDCS-21700152.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of an individual, likely for identification or dedication.

EDCS-21700153.translation: To Ambatus, father, and to his son Vaelcius, and to his wife Cabura.
EDCS-21700153.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Ambatus, his son Vaelcius, and his wife Cabura.

EDCS-21700167.translation: Gaius Munatius Modestus of the Papirian tribe, from Emerita, aged 38, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His brother Eponeilus saw to its construction.
EDCS-21700167.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Gaius Munatius Modestus, who died at age 38, and notes that his brother commissioned the monument.

EDCS-21700168.translation: Clutia Atta of the Termestina tribe, aged 60, is buried here.
EDCS-21700168.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Clutia Atta, who died at age 60.

EDCS-21700169.translation: To Domitius Cutarius, son of Cutati, of the Termestina tribe, aged 57. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-21700169.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Domitius Cutarius, who died at age 57.

EDCS-21700170.summary: This is a dedication or funerary marker for Lucius Aemilius Censorinus.

EDCS-21700174.translation: His son saw to its construction for Alionnus, freedman of Nepos.
EDCS-21700174.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned by a son for his father Alionnus, a freedman.

EDCS-21700177.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Parents had this made for their most devoted son, Annianus, aged 20.
EDCS-21700177.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication of a monument by parents to their son Annianus, aged 20.

EDCS-21700178.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Argatus.
EDCS-21700178.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Argatus.

EDCS-21700179.translation: His son saw to its construction for AR, wife of AL, and RI.
EDCS-21700179.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned by a son for his mother and sibling, identified as AR, wife of AL, and RI.

EDCS-21700181.translation: and to Boq... May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-21700181.summary: This funerary inscription contains a wish for the deceased named Boq... to rest in peace.

EDCS-21700182.summary: This is a list of personal names, likely indicating individuals associated with a particular place or event, possibly funerary or commemorative.

EDCS-21700183.translation: From his own resources, Sius Cornelius Sosumus Laetus.
EDCS-21700183.summary: This inscription records a dedication or benefaction made by Sius Cornelius Sosumus Laetus at his own expense, possibly for a public work or religious offering.

EDCS-21700185.translation: To Elcamo the son of Sancenia.
EDCS-21700185.summary: This is a dedication or memorial inscription for Elcamo, son of Sancenia.

EDCS-21700186.translation: Lucius, aged sixty years, is buried here.
EDCS-21700186.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the burial of Lucius, who was sixty years old.

EDCS-21700187.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or dedication to deities or individuals, with lacunae making precise interpretation difficult.

EDCS-21700190.translation: To Mustaro, freedman of Crispiana. He gave freely.
EDCS-21700190.summary: This inscription records a dedication or benefaction made by Mustaro, a freedman of Crispiana, possibly in gratitude or as a votive offering.

EDCS-21700192.translation: Surus arranged for this to be made for his wife Condisa and his daughter Qubalia.
EDCS-21700192.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by Surus for his wife Condisa and daughter Qubalia.

EDCS-21700193.translation: Ber arranged for this to be made for his dearest son Titalus and his mother Monea.
EDCS-21700193.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by Ber for his son Titalus and mother Monea.

EDCS-21700194.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes (spirits of the deceased), with the name Marcus partially preserved.

EDCS-21700202.translation: Anna, to the god Vaelicus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-21700202.summary: This is a votive inscription by Anna to the god Vaelicus, given in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-21700203.translation: Caecilia Vacaena, daughter of Reburrus, and Titus Sempronius Reburrus, her nephew, took care to have this made while they were alive.
EDCS-21700203.summary: This funerary inscription records a building commission by two individuals for themselves.

EDCS-21700244.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying a woman as a physician.

EDCS-21700245.translation: Aecianus, son of Aelates, and Aemilianius, son of Atisuvenatus.
EDCS-21700245.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or acknowledgment of two individuals, possibly related to property or a legal matter.

EDCS-21700307.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, Germanicus Maximus, Dacicus Maximus, Sarmaticus Maximus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power, Imperator VII times, Father of the Fatherland, Consul, Proconsul, and to Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus, the most noble Caesar, Dacicus Maximus, Sarmaticus Maximus, Prince of Youth, son of our Lord Gaius Julius Maximinus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus.
EDCS-21700307.summary: This is a lengthy titulary inscription honoring the emperor Maximinus Thrax and his son, Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus.

EDCS-21700308.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia, the mother, [dedicated this] to her most dutiful son, Naeviolus, not willingly, aged 14.
EDCS-21700308.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a mother to her deceased son.

EDCS-21700359.translation: Servilius Marcellus, aged 25, lies here; may the earth be light for you, comrades. They gave and dedicated this gift.
EDCS-21700359.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Servilius Marcellus and notes that his comrades dedicated a gift to him.

EDCS-21700360.translation: To Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus' son Britannicus. To Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus' son Britannicus.
EDCS-21700360.summary: This inscription is a repetition of a dedication to Tiberius Claudius Caesar Britannicus, son of the emperor Claudius.

EDCS-21700369.translation: Gaius Bal and Gaius Amb, the heirs, took care of this.
EDCS-21700369.summary: This inscription records that the heirs, Gaius Bal and Gaius Amb, commissioned the monument.

EDCS-21700376.summary: This fragmentary inscription records the lifespan of an individual whose name is partially lost.

EDCS-21700377.summary: This inscription is too fragmentary to provide a definitive translation or summary, but appears to be a personal name or dedication.

EDCS-21700380.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Saronis, identified as a furrier, with a terse epitaph.

EDCS-21700385.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Domitia Theodotes, who lived 22 years and 10 months. Domitius Pothinus for his foster-daughter. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-21700385.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Domitia Theodotes, erected by her foster-father Domitius Pothinus, with a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-21700386.summary: This inscription appears to be the beginning of a dedication or epitaph for Marcus Ulpius Porcius, but is mostly lost.

EDCS-21700387.translation: Aemilia Heutycia, aged [3]. May the earth lie lightly upon you. // [3]vilus, aged 7. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-21700387.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription potentially mentioning two individuals, Aemilia Heutycia and another whose name is lost, both with the standard epitaph wish.

EDCS-21700389.translation: Sacred to Mars. Gaius Aburius Lupus willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-21700389.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Gaius Aburius Lupus fulfilling a vow made to the god Mars.

EDCS-21700390.translation: For the spirits of the departed. Julius Lusa had this made for Caecilius Cusa, son of Erotis.
EDCS-21700390.summary: This is a funerary inscription commissioned by Julius Lusa for Caecilius Cusa, son of Erotis.

EDCS-21700398.translation: Aulus Publius for the Furii, freedman of Publius son of Lucius.
EDCS-21700398.summary: This inscription seems to record Aulus Publius acting on behalf of the Furii family, identifying himself as a freedman of Publius, son of Lucius.

EDCS-21700401.translation: Caius, son of Cn. [Al]bius, aged 70, lies here. Cassia Maxuma, daughter of Spurius, lies here.
EDCS-21700401.summary: This is a bilingual epitaph recording the burial of Caius Albius and Cassia Maxuma, with their ages provided for Caius.

EDCS-21700402.summary: This is a very brief inscription potentially commemorating Quintus Sertorius in his role as proconsul, with the abstract concept of 'Piety' as a descriptor or virtue.

EDCS-21700405.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Quadratus Cassius willingly and with a willing heart paid his vow.
EDCS-21700405.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Quadratus Cassius fulfilling a vow made to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-21700413.summary: This is a personal name, likely a tomb inscription.

EDCS-21700422.summary: This is a personal name, likely a tomb inscription.

EDCS-21700429.translation: Atta Amusdiae, her heir, set up this monument.
EDCS-21700429.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by an heir for Atta Amusdiae.

EDCS-21700431.translation: To the Divine Shades. Terentius Martialis set this up for his most dutiful wife, Terentia Verna, aged 33. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-21700431.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Terentius Martialis for his wife, Terentia Verna, dated by her age to the mid-Imperial period.

EDCS-21700434.translation: Valeria Ematia Puleco, aged 50, set this up for her husband.
EDCS-21700434.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Valeria Ematia Puleco for her husband, dated by her age.

EDCS-21700435.translation: To A. L. O. for Ucitericus, son of Magilo.
EDCS-21700435.summary: This is a dedication, possibly funerary, to Ucitericus, son of Magilo.

EDCS-21700439.translation: Cornelius Maternus made this for his most dutiful mother, Cornelia Carisia, aged 40, with his own money. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-21700439.summary: This is a funerary monument funded by Cornelius Maternus for his mother, Cornelia Carisia, dated by her age.

EDCS-21700441.translation: The heir Sulpicius, along with the guardians Cornelius Fuscus and Valerius Carus, and also the heir Annius Flavus, along with the guardians Coelius, saw to the construction of the monument according to the will of Sulpicius, for Sulpicia Martiola. To the Divine Shades.
EDCS-21700441.summary: This is a funerary monument erected according to a will, detailing the responsibilities of heirs and guardians for Sulpicia Martiola.

EDCS-21700442.translation: Valerius Vitulus set this up for his wife Natali, aged 65. May the earth be light upon you. For Valerius Vitulus, aged 75, Valerius Valerianus [set this up for his] father.
EDCS-21700442.summary: This is a funerary monument with two dedications: Valerius Vitulus for his wife Natali and Valerius Valerianus for his father Valerius Vitulus, dated by their ages.

EDCS-21700443.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription where a son saw to the construction of a monument.

EDCS-21700444.translation: Munnus, a mother, is placed here for her son.
EDCS-21700444.summary: This is a tombstone inscription from an unknown date, recording a mother's dedication to her son.

EDCS-21700445.translation: Divius Caius, freedman, saw to its construction.
EDCS-21700445.summary: This inscription, likely from the Roman Imperial period, records the construction of a monument by a freedman.

EDCS-21700450.translation: Publius Abianicus, aged 60, and Emal Abianicus, [and] Pro Abianicus for their father.
EDCS-21700450.summary: This funerary inscription, dating to the Roman Imperial period, records the memorialization of a father by his sons.

EDCS-21700451.translation: Materna Cilisqum placed [this] for Licinius Antilicus, aged 4.
EDCS-21700451.summary: This tombstone records the placement of a memorial by Materna for her young son, Licinius Antilicus.

EDCS-21700532.translation: To Mars, Gaius Camilius Firmus, according to his vow.
EDCS-21700532.summary: This dedication to the god Mars was made by Gaius Camilius Firmus, likely during the Roman Imperial period, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-21700535.summary: This inscription is a simple ownership or memorial marker for Quintus Granius Adiutor, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21700576.translation: Durio, son of Adurius, had this made for his son Durioni, aged 9, and for Cassia Callaeca, aged 50, and placed [it] for his own people.
EDCS-21700576.summary: This is a tombstone inscription from the Roman Imperial period, recording a father's dedication for his son and wife.

EDCS-21700586.translation: Of Maelius Lalius, [son] of Maelius Lalius.
EDCS-21700586.summary: This inscription is a simple marker or dedication, possibly funerary, for Maelius Lalius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21700589.summary: This fragment is likely part of a funerary inscription or dedication, mentioning Lucius' son and Lepida, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21700596.summary: This inscription is a simple ownership marker or dedication for Titus Julius Justus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21700598.translation: Sextus Aninius Lupus made this for the people of the Galli district and the Segardini.
EDCS-21700598.summary: This is a dedication by Sextus Aninius Lupus for the inhabitants of two districts, likely a public work.

EDCS-21700607.translation: To the Divine Shades. Ama, aged 20, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-21700607.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a 20-year-old named Ama.

EDCS-21700609.translation: Doidera, daughter of Magilo, aged 50, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-21700609.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Doidera, daughter of Magilo, aged 50.

EDCS-21700637.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young child named Pintamius, son of Bobdaenus.

EDCS-21700641.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Arcia, with her age missing.

EDCS-21700643.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Successus, son of Albucius, aged 20, may the earth lie lightly on you. To the Divine Shades. ATE.
EDCS-21700643.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Successus, son of Albucius, aged 20, with a possible abbreviation at the end.

EDCS-21700644.translation: To the Divine Shades. Cornelia, daughter of Pinton, aged 15.
EDCS-21700644.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cornelia, daughter of Pinton, aged 15.

EDCS-21700666.translation: P. Rufus Apilus made this for the people of Albocola.
EDCS-21700666.summary: This is a dedication by P. Rufus Apilus for the inhabitants of Albocola, likely a public work.

EDCS-21700668.translation: Sabinus paid his vow willingly and deservedly for Adaegina.
EDCS-21700668.summary: This is an inscription recording Sabinus fulfilling a vow for Adaegina.

EDCS-21700669.summary: This inscription identifies a hot spring belonging to Eutychus.

EDCS-21900010.translation: Baelibius has paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-21900010.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to the deity Baelibius, likely from a private individual.

EDCS-21900015.translation: Minicius Florus, aged seventy years, is placed here for the Manes. Anica Flori, his servant, aged fifteen years, is placed here for the Manes.
EDCS-21900015.summary: A tombstone for Minicius Florus and his servant Anica Flori, indicating their death and burial, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-21900019.translation: John, may Christ live in you.
EDCS-21900019.summary: A Christian blessing or inscription, possibly a tombstone or a marker, invoking Christ's presence.

EDCS-21900022.translation: Fortunatus, may you live in Christ. From the workshop of Iliodorus. Felix [sends this] to Fortunatus.
EDCS-21900022.summary: A message from Felix of Iliodorus's workshop to Fortunatus, wishing him to live in Christ, likely a personal inscription or a dedication.

EDCS-21900023.translation: Curundus, son of Aelcecus Cadani, and Burria, daughter of Arrena, his wife, his heirs, had this made according to his will. He is placed here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-21900023.summary: A tombstone inscription for Curundus, commissioned by his wife Burria and heirs, with a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-21900025.translation: Sacred to the Manes. Balarus had this made for his son Cadanus Caburateiqum.
EDCS-21900025.summary: A funerary dedication by Balarus to his son Cadanus Caburateiqum.

EDCS-21900026.summary: A fragment, possibly a dedication or ownership marker, referring to Burrus, son of Magilo.

EDCS-21900027.translation: Regarding the conditions of oaths, under which Lolus had to swear by the order of Eunandus Argeredus, vicars of Ra Uvidericus Argeredus, judges, at the petition of Basilius, Lolus had to swear concerning the horses which they exchanged. I swear by God the Father Almighty and Jesus Christ his Son and by the four Gospels placed before him on the most holy altar of God the Father, that, as all seeing may fear by the example made, the conditions in the third year of the reign of our most glorious lord Eunandus, were subscribed by me, Argeredus, to these conditions, Uvidericus, by his sign, subscribed to these conditions.
EDCS-21900027.summary: A legal document recording an oath sworn by Lolus concerning a horse transaction, referencing Christian religious elements and officials of the period, dated to the third regnal year of Eunandus.

EDCS-21900028.translation: To my honorable lord and brother Desiderius, Gregorios the seller. Because it was agreed between us that I sell to you and give possession of a portion of the land itself, and you will defend and protect it, for the price agreed upon by our good peace, that is, one gold solid, the remainder of which you have given me and I, the seller, have received from you in full, and I promise nothing at all further of the price, which may be in your right. Profession of servitude. I, Unigildus, from the place of Langa Tomanca, having come to the place to serve at the house of Froilanus, I brought myself, testified by Froilanus himself, to the house of Desiderius. While I was at the house of Desiderius, Froilanus came and said to me, 'Lift yourself, Levita, and let us go to the house of Busani and Fasteni.' We went to the house of Busani, and for Froilanus he told us to go to the vineyards beyond, and I will put you there in the vineyards, and I took myself from the house of Desiderius two axes, one for cutting, one for... Signed Gandarius, witness. L. Vararicus, requested by the aforementioned to write this charter of conditions. Witness Marius, requested by the aforementioned to write this charter of conditions. I wrote this charter of conditions on the day and year as above.
EDCS-21900028.summary: A legal document recording a land sale and a profession of servitude, detailing an agreement between Gregorios and Desiderius, and Unigildus's personal history involving Froilanus, Busani, and Fasteni, with witness attestations.

EDCS-21900029.translation: To the honorable lord brother Montanus, and Aria. And it has come to us by good will that you may freely have the power to do and that whatever we may attempt against our [rights], I will bring proceedings before the court. Taxed malum expationis under the eighth day of King Riccaridus. Sign. Manual VII VI VIIIIII I V I VII II I II V I IIII III I III V III II I II III II I II IIII IIII
EDCS-21900029.summary: A legal or administrative document concerning the rights and obligations between Montanus, Aria, and the grantor, mentioning King Riccaridus and a date, with a series of numerical notations possibly related to measurements or quantities.

EDCS-21900033.translation: One sextarius, one sextarius, one sextarius, two sextarii, one sextarius, one sextarius, one emina. Ericus: one sextarius, one sextarius, one sextarius, one sextarius, one sextarius. Egidius: one sextarius, one sextarius, one sextarius. Tirius and Merulus: one sextarius. Intianus: two sextarii. Elianus' son: one sextarius. Erugio: one sextarius. Murildus: one sextarius. Domnus the elder: one sextarius. Domnela, by the command of her lord: one sextarius and a half. Valeria: one sextarius. Serena, by the command of her lord: one sextarius. By the command of Teodadus: one sextarius. Adiutor and Fervodus, in another chest: one sextarius. Morulus: one sextarius. Firmina, in another chest: one sextarius. Gundarius: one sextarius. Procula: one sextarius. Julius: one sextarius and a half, and one emina. Deicicius: one sextarius. Morulus: one sextarius and one emina. Golulfus: one sextarius. Elias, in the third chest: one sextarius. Adiutor, in the other chest: two sextarii. Bogiro: one sextarius and one emina. Quarter sextarius. Mauro: one sextarius and three quarters. Sni: one sextarius. Lusius, in another chest: two sextarii. In another chest: one sextarius. Iacia: three sextarii. In the third chest: three sextarii.
EDCS-21900033.summary: This inscription appears to be a record of distributed quantities, likely grain or wine, with various individuals named and the amounts they received, possibly for a funerary or communal meal.

EDCS-21900035.translation: Agilo: one denarius in the fourth chest. Edevindus: in another chest. Otes: one emina. Agilo: in the third chest. Argefredus: two sextarii. One sextarius and one emina. Tefreda: in other chests. Resus: one sextarius and one emina. Regions of... Argefredus: one sextarius. Argefredus: one sextarius.
EDCS-21900035.summary: This inscription lists quantities of goods, possibly grain, distributed to various individuals, indicating a record of provisions or payments.

EDCS-21900040.translation: Aurilianus possessed one sextarius. Record of the granary: Leontius, six sextarii; three quarter-measures of spelt; two sextarii; one sextarius; none.
EDCS-21900040.summary: This inscription is a record of grain quantities, listing specific amounts assigned to Leontius and other unspecified items from a granary, likely an administrative or inventory document.

EDCS-21900042.translation: Record, by which we are ordered to assign to Simplicio: six sesquannas with his pig, one sow, one cow, hospitality for the Matratium family, which is to give two calves in his court, from Lord Valentinus; twenty-five modii of wheat.
EDCS-21900042.summary: This inscription details provisions to be delivered to Simplicio, including livestock and grain, on the order of Valentinus, likely related to a duty or obligation.

EDCS-21900043.translation: Two for Eugami, one for the children, two for Leonis, one for Vericus, four for the elder children, two for Ispinaveli, one for Avitus, one for Emir, one for Ate ri, one for Iciterio, one for Anullus, three for Megetus, one for Simplicitus, three for Martinus, one for Gari, one for Semprunus, for the child.
EDCS-21900043.summary: This inscription appears to be a list of distributions or allocations to various individuals, primarily children, possibly for sustenance or support, with some adults also named.

EDCS-21900047.translation: Hear, citizens, senators, prefects! Cindasvindus, lord, thirteen, twelve. Go forth from the usage, go forth! Domandus, my son, greetings to Domandus! And show your wisdom. Sibyls, hear your king's words. Do not covet what belongs to others. Do not bring evil knowledge and perdition, even if you see it. Lord, in glory, forget the other meal. Do not...
EDCS-21900047.summary: This inscription contains a public exhortation or moral instruction, possibly from a leader or father figure named Cindasvindus, addressing citizens, senators, and prefects, and giving advice on conduct and avoiding temptation.

EDCS-21900051.translation: In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit, your... and saying to my lord, hear... Ciricus...
EDCS-21900051.summary: This inscription is a fragment that begins with a religious invocation, likely from a Christian context, but is too incomplete to provide further context.

EDCS-21900063.translation: May my servant serve me well. May God defend me from enemies. ... And he ordered for a large blessing from the third year and from the year of the Gallican king, which is mine, although people think... you owe six cevaria modii from our... Germanus owes three tremisses and has... to Toledo... two sheep and a goat...
EDCS-21900063.summary: This inscription is a fragmented text that seems to record debts and obligations, mentioning specific quantities of goods like cevaria modii, tremisses, sheep, and a goat, and referencing individuals like Germanus and Domnellus, possibly related to a legal or financial settlement.

EDCS-21900064.translation: And by the received one solidus, one verbice for Letorius, one verbice for Valerius, for the ploughed land: six modii for Paerivus.
EDCS-21900064.summary: This inscription details a payment of one solidus and lists distributions of six modii of grain for ploughed land, with named recipients Letorius and Valerius.

EDCS-21900081.translation: Record of the granary: Beatus, three solidi. Thus, three solidi.
EDCS-21900081.summary: This is a short administrative record from a granary, noting that Beatus owes three solidi, followed by a repetition of 'three solidi'.

EDCS-21900082.translation: Note concerning the necklaces weighed out to the treasury: I gave one for Pedulus, which Stator weighed out, two necklaces; I gave one for Lauros. Trasemundus gave two sheep, two [---]. Regarding the treasury from Severianeus, they took up thirteen; we expended two, then four.
EDCS-21900082.summary: This is a fragmentary record of accounting for items, possibly precious metals or goods, brought to the treasury, with some items being accounted for on behalf of individuals.

EDCS-21900086.translation: Regarding [something] with the lord: Theodorus and Quintus one [measure] for Arcadius, three for Justinian. Terentius and Primus [measure] for Terentius Primus and Cu [---]. Arcadius [will receive] one modius. For Anastasius, one modius. For Terentius, one modius.
EDCS-21900086.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely recording distributions of grain or other commodities to various individuals, possibly in the context of a landlord-tenant or patronage relationship.

EDCS-21900168.translation: To Mercury. [The dedicator] willingly and deservedly fulfilled [this] vow to the good god.
EDCS-21900168.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Mercury, recording the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-21900169.translation: To Navis. Marcus Flavius [dedicated this] in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-21900169.summary: This is a short dedication to the deity Navis, by Marcus Flavius, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-21900171.translation: Sinerusa and her son Quintillus, aged twenty, are buried here.
EDCS-21900171.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Sinerusa and her son Quintillus.

EDCS-21900172.translation: Lucius Junius Epagathus, aged eleven, is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-21900172.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Junius Epagathus, a child aged eleven, with a common epitaph wishing him peace.

EDCS-21900174.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Sextilla, aged twenty-five.
EDCS-21900174.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the 'Dis Manibus' (Spirits of the Dead) for Sextilla, aged twenty-five.

EDCS-21900177.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely for a person named Galo, indicating their age is missing.

EDCS-21900178.translation: Salvius, son of Iunius Carus, aged [---], is buried here.
EDCS-21900178.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Salvius, son of Iunius Carus, with the age missing, stating he is buried here.

EDCS-21900181.translation: Domitia Materna ordered this to be set up for her children. Lucretia Crista, in her own judgment, fulfilled the vow.
EDCS-21900181.summary: This is a funerary monument inscription commissioned by Domitia Materna for her children, with the vow fulfillment recorded by Lucretia Crista.

EDCS-21900183.translation: To Cornelia Flava, Lucius Aemilius Seranus, [her husband], made [this monument].
EDCS-21900183.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument dedicated by Lucius Aemilius Seranus to his wife Cornelia Flava.

EDCS-21900184.summary: This inscription fragment likely refers to the age of a deceased person, possibly related to another inscription.

EDCS-21900186.translation: Titus Valerius Paternus, a veteran of the same legion, had this made in accordance with the will, for Aemilius Ordunets, a veteran of Legio II Augusta.
EDCS-21900186.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aemilius Ordunets, a veteran of Legio II Augusta, whose monument was erected by another veteran, Titus Valerius Paternus, according to Aemilius' will.

EDCS-21900189.translation: In the name of God, let us honor the altar of Analsus. In the name of God, let us honor Analsus.
EDCS-21900189.summary: This is a religious inscription invoking God and honoring 'Analsus', possibly a deity or a sacred place, likely from a Christian context due to the invocation of God.

EDCS-21900190.summary: This is a short inscription possibly dedicating something to Anodius by Marcus Sigir(us?), perhaps a dedication or a marking of ownership.

EDCS-21900194.summary: This is a very short inscription, likely identifying or commemorating Leovigildus, a figure of some authority designated as 'dux'.

EDCS-21900196.translation: Latronius, son of Celtiatus, is buried here.
EDCS-21900196.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription stating that Latronius, son of Celtiatus, is buried at this location.

EDCS-21900225.translation: from one head sixty and five years in the fourth year of our king Egicanus.
EDCS-21900225.summary: This inscription fragment likely records a date or duration, referencing the fourth regnal year of a king named Egicanus.

EDCS-21900229.translation: Rufinus Placidius Macarius Avinus Darcus, sicius eguisnus mistidinus inisus Cantinus inus Pius, sicius idus civus Lucius M, sicius Pascentius Exuperantius.
EDCS-21900229.summary: This inscription appears to be a list of names, possibly associated with a collegium or group, with some descriptive terms ('sicius', 'civus') and potentially multiple individuals.

EDCS-21900231.translation: Gratus made [this]; may he who wishes [ill] pay the toll in a good community.
EDCS-21900231.summary: This inscription records Gratus as the maker of something and includes a malediction or warning against those who might wish harm, relating to a community toll or tax.

EDCS-21900239.translation: To Cantabrecus, aged 75. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-21900239.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cantabrecus, a man of 75, with a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-21900240.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Domitius, a father, placed this for his most distinguished son, Domitia, his most excellent daughter.
EDCS-21900240.summary: A funerary inscription set up by a father for his daughter, Domitia, described as excellent.

EDCS-21900243.translation: To Domitius, son of Flavi, aged 70. His son placed this.
EDCS-21900243.summary: A funerary monument erected by a son for his father, Domitius, who was 70 years old.

EDCS-21900244.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. C. Novius Vannus, aged 50, son of Caius. While alive, his father arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-21900244.summary: A funerary dedication by a father for his son, C. Novius Vannus, who was 50 years old and paid for by the father during his lifetime.

EDCS-21900252.translation: Aemilius Maternus, deceased, is buried here, aged 35.
EDCS-21900252.summary: A burial marker for Aemilius Maternus, who died at the age of 35.

EDCS-21900253.translation: Cornelius Viator, son of Arancisis, aged 50, Cornelius Valens, son of (Viator?), aged 25, and Aemilius Severus, son of Agirsena, aged 35, arranged for this to be made. May the earth lie lightly upon you. They are buried here.
EDCS-21900253.summary: A funerary monument arranged by a mother for her deceased sons, Cornelius Viator (50), Cornelius Valens (25), and Aemilius Severus (35).

EDCS-21900254.translation: Antestia Serana, daughter of Eni, aged 1, is buried here.
EDCS-21900254.summary: A burial epitaph for a child, Antestia Serana, who died at the age of one.

EDCS-21900255.translation: Aemilius Maternus, aged 40, is buried here with his wife and son and mother, who died aged 65. May the earth lie lightly upon him.
EDCS-21900255.summary: A tomb inscription for Aemilius Maternus, his wife, son, and mother, with specific ages mentioned for Maternus and his mother.

EDCS-21900262.translation: Tancinus, the freedman, arranged for this to be erected for his patron Caco, son of Turi.
EDCS-21900262.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a freedman, Tancinus, to his patron, Caco.

EDCS-21900265.translation: Cloutina, daughter of Octavus, a slave, aged 20.
EDCS-21900265.summary: A funerary epitaph for a slave girl, Cloutina, aged 20.

EDCS-21900291.translation: Sacred to the Nymphs. Gaius Gallius Polycarpus willingly made this.
EDCS-21900291.summary: A dedication to the Nymphs by Gaius Gallius Polycarpus.

EDCS-21900292.translation: The cold well of Sulpicius Flavius.
EDCS-21900292.summary: An epitaph or dedication related to a well belonging to Sulpicius Flavius.

EDCS-22200025.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Ursicenus Aseyucius placed this memorial for his dearest son, who died at the age of 20.
EDCS-22200025.summary: A funerary monument set up by Ursicenus Aseyucius for his son, dating to antiquity.

EDCS-22200030.summary: An epitaph for Aelia Hellice, possibly indicating her origin or family.

EDCS-22200032.summary: A funerary inscription for a child named Ianuarius.

EDCS-22200038.translation: Sam, son of Aiecius, is buried here.
EDCS-22200038.summary: A funerary inscription for Sam, son of Aiecius.

EDCS-22200056.translation: Mure, son of Pece, from Paramecum.
EDCS-22200056.summary: An epitaph or dedication possibly indicating a person's name, parentage, and origin.

EDCS-22200057.translation: Sempronius Cassius, sacred offering in fulfillment of a vow to the household gods.
EDCS-22200057.summary: A religious dedication by Sempronius Cassius to the Lares who protect the household.

EDCS-22200058.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Vernaculus, son of Ambaticum, of Modestus.
EDCS-22200058.summary: A funerary inscription for Vernaculus, son of Ambaticum.

EDCS-22200078.summary: An epitaph or dedication for Aelius Publius Miacus.

EDCS-22200096.translation: T. Cornelius, son of Canius, from Burgundy.
EDCS-22200096.summary: This is a funerary inscription for T. Cornelius, likely a Roman citizen of Gallic origin.

EDCS-22200099.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, possibly a personal name, with uncertain restorations.

EDCS-22200172.translation: To Saturninus, slave of Caius Craticius, his companion.
EDCS-22200172.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Saturninus, a slave and companion of Caius Craticius.

EDCS-22200180.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription honoring C. Sempronius Campanus.

EDCS-22200193.translation: Lucius Porcius Liberalis to Porcius Fuscus.
EDCS-22200193.summary: This inscription records a dedication or memorial from Lucius Porcius Liberalis to Porcius Fuscus.

EDCS-22200194.translation: The Emperor Caesar, son of the deified [one].
EDCS-22200194.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription referring to an emperor, likely from the early imperial period.

EDCS-22200197.summary: This inscription identifies Quintus Sertorius, who held the office of proconsul.

EDCS-22200221.translation: To the divine shades, Vaceae placed [this] for my wife, aged 110.
EDCS-22200221.summary: This funerary inscription honors Vaceae's wife, who lived to an exceptional age.

EDCS-22200223.translation: To the divine shades. C. Allius, son of C., Victor, and Victor, sons of Allius, having overcome the Younger Hergetes, defeated by bitter fate, tribunes of the 19th Legion, lived 32 years and 2 months. C. Allius, son of C., his excellent father, [erected this] and [provided for it].
EDCS-22200223.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates two military tribunes, C. Allius Victor and Victor, who served in the 19th Legion and died young, with their father setting up the monument.

EDCS-22200227.translation: Grattius Balbinus and Grattius Amba[t]us, the heirs, saw to it.
EDCS-22200227.summary: This inscription records that the heirs, Grattius Balbinus and Grattius Ambatus, were responsible for the monument or its upkeep.

EDCS-22200231.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Cornelius Quietus, aged 60, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22200231.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Cornelius Quietus, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-22200234.translation: Of Marcus Aquinius, son of Caius.
EDCS-22200234.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying Marcus Aquinius, son of Caius.

EDCS-22200235.translation: Of Lucius Planus Russinus, son of Lucius.
EDCS-22200235.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying Lucius Planus Russinus, son of Lucius.

EDCS-22200237.translation: Of Caius Messius, son of Lucius.
EDCS-22200237.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying Caius Messius, son of Lucius.

EDCS-22200238.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying Gaius Utius, son of Gaius.

EDCS-22200246.summary: This inscription is an incomplete admonition or curse directed at any reader.

EDCS-22200252.translation: Cornelius Alcimus: for a sore throat and suppurations, Cornelius Alcimus: for clarity [of vision] and for cataracts.
EDCS-22200252.summary: This text records medical remedies for various ailments, attributed to Cornelius Alcimus.

EDCS-22200294.translation: Marcus Turelius, son of Titus, of Calca.
EDCS-22200294.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying Marcus Turelius, son of Titus, and noting his origin or association with Calca.

EDCS-22200317.summary: This inscription, likely from a votive context, records a dedication by Cugius Viscicus for good health.

EDCS-22200346.translation: To Jupiter, Agilis gladly and deservedly paid his vow with his own money.
EDCS-22200346.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Agilis fulfilling a vow to Jupiter at his own expense.

EDCS-22200354.translation: Quintus Sabinius Florus willingly and deservedly paid his vow to the Genius.
EDCS-22200354.summary: This is a votive inscription by Quintus Sabinius Florus to a Genius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-22200356.translation: Sacred to Augusta Nabis. Aulus Caecilius Paternus willingly and with good heart paid his vow.
EDCS-22200356.summary: This is a votive inscription by Aulus Caecilius Paternus dedicated to Augusta Nabis, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-22200357.summary: This inscription records the age of a person named Ambatus, likely a funerary marker, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22200359.summary: This inscription records the age of Flavius Flavianus, likely a funerary marker, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22400198.translation: Arrena set up this altar [for] Selicens[i]...
EDCS-22400198.summary: This is a dedication of an altar by Arrena to Selicensius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22400203.summary: This is a dedication by Ateia to Vironus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22400325.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Sextia Procula, who lived 29 years. Flavius Archippianus for his most pious and most devoted wife.
EDCS-22400325.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sextia Procula, set up by her husband Flavius Archippianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22400329.summary: This is likely a funerary epitaph or a name inscription, possibly dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22400370.summary: This is likely a funerary epitaph or a name inscription, possibly dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22400383.translation: Titus Lucius Flacacius Severus, the worker.
EDCS-22400383.summary: This inscription identifies Titus Lucius Flacacius Severus by his profession as a worker, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22400387.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a woman named Lanciensis, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-22400407.translation: He/She gladly fulfilled the vow to his/her daughter Losa as deserved.
EDCS-22400407.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow, likely funerary, to a daughter named Losa.

EDCS-22400408.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a girl named Flaccilla, who died at the age of 10.

EDCS-22400411.translation: Sixtus, son of Antonius Serenus, and his sister Stratonice, Festa and Rust, their heirs, had this made at their own expense.
EDCS-22400411.summary: This funerary inscription records that Sixtus, son of Antonius Serenus, and his sister Stratonice, along with their heirs Festa and Rust, commissioned the monument.

EDCS-22400416.translation: To the god AVI, according to the vow. Tertionus gladly fulfilled the vow as deserved.
EDCS-22400416.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god AVI by Tertionus, who fulfilled his vow.

EDCS-22400421.translation: The son of Avitus gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-22400421.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by the son of Avitus.

EDCS-22400422.translation: Lucius Flavius, son of Flavius Ubaicus Bovitus.
EDCS-22400422.summary: This inscription identifies Lucius Flavius as the son of Flavius Ubaicus Bovitus.

EDCS-22400425.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Galerius Valerius Maximianus, victorious and perpetual.
EDCS-22400425.summary: This is likely a dedication or honorific inscription for Emperor Galerius Maximianus, highlighting his victories.

EDCS-22400427.translation: Victorious and perpetual conqueror, miles (of the road).
EDCS-22400427.summary: This inscription likely refers to an emperor or general, celebrating victory and conquest, possibly related to road construction or maintenance.

EDCS-22400433.translation: To Neptune, Iulius Coutai happily fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-22400433.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Neptune by Iulius Coutai, who gladly fulfilled his vow.

EDCS-22400435.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aemilius Reburrinus set this up for his wife Segaïda.
EDCS-22400435.summary: This funerary inscription records a husband's dedication to his deceased wife.

EDCS-22400456.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Aurelius Salusis [dedicates this].
EDCS-22400456.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aurelius Salusis.

EDCS-22400469.translation: Veisa and Turosoilobus [honored] the true Ticinus.
EDCS-22400469.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or honorific mention of Ticinus by Veisa and Turosoilobus.

EDCS-22400470.translation: [Dedication] to Pantrus and Quequius by Turo.
EDCS-22400470.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely recording a dedication by Turo to deities or individuals named Pantrus and Quequius.

EDCS-22400473.summary: This is a short inscription recording Tullos honoring or dedicating something to Ierso.

EDCS-22400476.summary: This inscription records Turos and Cotiriquus honoring the Carani.

EDCS-22400477.translation: Turos [honored] the true men of the Carani.
EDCS-22400477.summary: This inscription records Turos honoring the true men of the Carani.

EDCS-22400478.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription recording Guandos and Cotiriquus, likely in a dedicatory context.

EDCS-22400515.summary: This inscription is simply the name of Marcus Ennius Terentianus, likely marking a tomb or property.

EDCS-22400516.summary: This inscription likely indicates ownership or a dedication related to Lucius Vivarius Prunicus.

EDCS-22400518.translation: Of Caius Caristanius Caesianus and Rufus.
EDCS-22400518.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely naming two individuals, Caius Caristanius Caesianus and Rufus.

EDCS-22400519.summary: This inscription records the name of Marcus Fabius Nymphodotus, possibly as a dedicant or owner.

EDCS-22400520.summary: This inscription records the name of Aulus Aelius Onesimus, likely a dedicator or property owner.

EDCS-22400521.translation: Marcus Ennius Elpidius, the steward of the usurer (or of usury).
EDCS-22400521.summary: This inscription names Marcus Ennius Elpidius as a vilicus (steward or manager), likely associated with financial matters given the term 'anatocismus' (usury or compound interest).

EDCS-22400524.summary: This inscription records the name Caius Faelus twice, possibly indicating ownership or a dedication.

EDCS-22400525.summary: This inscription records the name of Lucius Petronius Iustus, likely as a dedicator or property owner.

EDCS-22400527.summary: This inscription records the name of Tiberius Claudius Genetlhius, likely a dedicator or property owner.

EDCS-22400530.translation: Of the substitute Marcus Vettius Bolanus.
EDCS-22400530.summary: This inscription names a substitute, Marcus Vettius Bolanus, possibly in a military or official capacity.

EDCS-22400531.translation: Of Marcus V. C. Marcus V. C.
EDCS-22400531.summary: This inscription repeats the name and potentially titles of Marcus, suggesting ownership or a dedication.

EDCS-22400534.summary: This inscription simply records the name Gaius Julius Aelianus, likely as a dedicator or owner.

EDCS-22400535.summary: This is a dedication to Acutia Capitoina, likely on a tombstone or altar.

EDCS-22400536.summary: This inscription likely records the name of Quintus Ocatvenius Hymnus, possibly as a tombstone or ownership marker.

EDCS-22400537.summary: This inscription probably names Lucius Petronius Justus, possibly on a tombstone or monument.

EDCS-22400538.translation: Narcissus, freedman of Tiberius Claudius Proculus.
EDCS-22400538.summary: This inscription identifies Narcissus, a freedman of Tiberius Claudius Proculus, likely on a tombstone.

EDCS-22400540.summary: This inscription records the name Sextus Exsuperius Vara, possibly on a tombstone or dedicatory monument.

EDCS-22400542.summary: This inscription likely refers to Paulla Eumetri, possibly on a tombstone or personal marker.

EDCS-22400547.summary: This inscription likely refers to Gnaeus Magnus, who held the title of Emperor, possibly on a military or official monument.

EDCS-22400548.summary: This inscription records the name Publius Rapellinius Derma, likely on a tombstone or dedicatory monument.

EDCS-22400554.translation: Reve Marandigui, Albinia Albina, willingly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-22400554.summary: This dedication records Reve Marandigui and Albinia Albina fulfilling a vow, likely on an altar or shrine.

EDCS-22600053.translation: Publius Gabinius Firmanus, a student of law, lies here.
EDCS-22600053.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Publius Gabinius Firmanus, who was a student of law.

EDCS-22800013.translation: Cania Maxima, daughter of Sumitus, is laid here.
EDCS-22800013.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cania Maxima, likely from the late Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-22800014.translation: Publius Aufidius Piso is laid here.
EDCS-22800014.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Aufidius Piso, likely from the late Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-22800046.translation: On the front, nine feet wide. Quirina, Incitatus, 41 years, more or less.
EDCS-22800046.summary: This inscription appears to be a boundary marker or a grave inscription specifying dimensions and the age of the deceased, Incitatus, possibly indicating a person or animal buried here from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800063.summary: This is a dedication inscription to Publius Rutilius Lucius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800093.translation: Flavianus, to Johannes, one of the brothers.
EDCS-22800093.summary: This is a funerary or memorial inscription for Flavianus, likely from the late Roman Empire, possibly with Christian connotations.

EDCS-22800096.translation: Valerianus, alive and well, alive and well.
EDCS-22800096.summary: This inscription seems to be a personal affirmation or dedication by Valerianus, potentially from the Roman Imperial period, possibly relating to recovery or well-being.

EDCS-22800104.translation: M. E. R. P. Nicitiuus, dutiful.
EDCS-22800104.summary: This is a funerary or dedicatory inscription, possibly an abbreviation, for Nicitiuus, indicating piety or dutifulness, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800202.translation: Arta Nigra, aged fifteen, is laid here. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-22800202.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Arta Nigra, a young girl, including a common wish for the deceased, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800209.translation: Publius Pontius, son of Asidae, from the tribe Quirina, and Lucius Pontius Licinianus, have gladly paid their vow, as deserved.
EDCS-22800209.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by Publius Pontius and Lucius Pontius Licinianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800214.translation: If you are well, do not touch what belongs to another.
EDCS-22800214.summary: This is a warning inscription, possibly related to property or a tomb, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800228.translation: Maxuma, daughter of Emerita Augusta, aged 19, lies here.
EDCS-22800228.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young woman named Maxuma from Emerita Augusta.

EDCS-22800259.translation: To Mars, Martia Flacilla, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-22800259.summary: This dedication is made to the god Mars by Martia Flacilla.

EDCS-22800268.summary: This inscription records Quintus Vecius as a flamen, likely a priest.

EDCS-22800272.translation: The work of Rufus Coelernus of the territory.
EDCS-22800272.summary: This records a work of construction or art by Rufus Coelernus, possibly related to land ownership.

EDCS-22800273.translation: Marcus Licinius Veleiensis gladly and deservedly paid his vow to the household gods.
EDCS-22800273.summary: This is a dedication by Marcus Licinius Veleiensis to the Larēs (household gods) in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-22800275.summary: This is a simple dedication or memorial by Vitalina.

EDCS-22800277.translation: Quintus Apilicus Severus gladly and deservedly paid his vow to Proinetia.
EDCS-22800277.summary: This is a dedication by Quintus Apilicus Severus to Proinetia, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-22800279.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Decimus.
EDCS-22800279.summary: This is a partial dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by someone named Decimus.

EDCS-22800280.translation: Peregrinus, son of Aprius, of Reve Velsuto.
EDCS-22800280.summary: This inscription identifies Peregrinus, son of Aprius, and his origin from Reve Velsuto.

EDCS-22800282.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Septimius Reburrus gladly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-22800282.summary: This is a dedication by Septimius Reburrus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-22800283.summary: This inscription is a simple personal name, possibly a tombstone or a dedication by Lucius Albus Albinus.

EDCS-22800296.translation: To Flavius Dalmatius, most noble Caesar
EDCS-22800296.summary: This is an inscription dedicating a monument or object to Flavius Dalmatius, who held the title of Caesar, likely in the 4th century CE.

EDCS-22800298.translation: Lucius Tertius Capitonis gladly and deservedly paid his vow to Tutela.
EDCS-22800298.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius Tertius Capitonis to the goddess Tutela, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800299.translation: Titus Vaecus Reburrus gladly and deservedly paid his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-22800299.summary: This is a votive inscription by Titus Vaecus Reburrus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800301.summary: This inscription marks a boundary stone belonging to Pacata, possibly from a rural estate.

EDCS-22800302.translation: Vallius Maximus to the household gods of the crossroads, according to his vow.
EDCS-22800302.summary: This is a votive inscription by Vallius Maximus to the Lares Viales (household gods of the crossroads), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800303.translation: To our lord, the Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantius, the Unconquered, Pious, Father of the Fatherland.
EDCS-22800303.summary: This is a lengthy honorific inscription for Emperor Constantius, likely from the mid-4th century CE, proclaiming his titles.

EDCS-22800309.translation: Sulpicius Gibbianus set this up by vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-22800309.summary: This is a votive inscription by Sulpicius Gibbianus dedicating something to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800311.translation: Sulpicius the pious set this up by vision to the Lar Permanes.
EDCS-22800311.summary: This is a votive inscription by Sulpicius to the Lar Permanes (household god of the threshold), fulfilled due to a vision, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800314.translation: To Gaius Antonius Calaedius, son of Quirina Festus, aged 60, Antonius Aemilianus and Auca Aunia had it made for their father.
EDCS-22800314.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Antonius Calaedius, commissioned by his children Antonius Aemilianus and Auca Aunia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800317.translation: Atta Origena set up this memorial to the Divine Spirits for her most dutiful daughter Alioniga Acida Avana, who lived 20 years, buried here in the consulship of 364, on the 11th of March.
EDCS-22800317.summary: A funerary monument set up by Atta Origena for her daughter, dated to the consulship of 364.

EDCS-22800318.translation: For the most dutiful memory of Acida, 35 years old, era 432; a memory of 45 years more or less, era 100.
EDCS-22800318.summary: A funerary epitaph for Acida, providing her age and two era dates, possibly indicating different dating systems or periods.

EDCS-22800324.translation: To the Divine Spirits for Sempronia Reburrina, 25 years old.
EDCS-22800324.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to Sempronia Reburrina.

EDCS-22800342.translation: He lived 70 years; his sister had this made.
EDCS-22800342.summary: A funerary epitaph stating the deceased lived 70 years and was commemorated by his sister.

EDCS-22800344.translation: Allon Lumpi set up an altar.
EDCS-22800344.summary: An inscription recording the dedication of an altar by Allon Lumpi.

EDCS-22800345.translation: Une, daughter of Lovi, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Mars.
EDCS-22800345.summary: A votive inscription by Une, daughter of Lovi, to Mars.

EDCS-22800346.translation: Pontia Onse, daughter of Nasonis, buried here, 10 years old.
EDCS-22800346.summary: A funerary epitaph for Pontia Onse, daughter of Nasonis, who died at the age of 10.

EDCS-22800348.translation: Minicia Valentina, wife of Titinius, buried here, 31 years old.
EDCS-22800348.summary: A funerary epitaph for Minicia Valentina, wife of Titinius, who died at the age of 31.

EDCS-22900002.summary: An inscription recording that Pomp Nico made this object or monument.

EDCS-22900004.summary: An inscription indicating that Eurinus made this, followed by an abbreviation whose meaning is uncertain.

EDCS-22900005.translation: Of Marcus Iulius Antoninianus, aedile. // VV
EDCS-22900005.summary: This inscription appears to record the name and office of Marcus Iulius Antoninianus, likely an official in charge of public buildings or markets.

EDCS-22900019.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young boy named Pinto, son of Talavus, who died at the age of 10.

EDCS-22900020.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Decuminus, who died at the age of 70.

EDCS-22900021.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Docinia Trita, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-22900022.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Anna Iemuria, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-22900023.translation: Elaesus Capito erected this monument to his daughter Tritia Abini.
EDCS-22900023.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Elaesus Capito in memory of his daughter, Tritia Abini.

EDCS-22900024.translation: To Elaesus, son of Tritus, aged 50.
EDCS-22900024.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Elaesus, son of Tritus, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-22900049.translation: The pious Pius fulfilled his vow, set up privately, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-22900049.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a private vow by an individual named Pius.

EDCS-22900051.translation: To Doredius Douroegus, Numerius Rufus set this up according to his vow, willingly and with a good heart.
EDCS-22900051.summary: Numerius Rufus dedicated this to Doredius Douroegus, fulfilling a vow willingly.

EDCS-22900056.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, P. Rufus fulfilled his vow, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-22900056.summary: This inscription records P. Rufus fulfilling a vow made to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-22900057.translation: Arrua, daughter of Anelius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-22900057.summary: This is a votive inscription by Arrua, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22900063.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of the deceased, Lageo, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23001901.translation: Flavus, son of Andion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Baelibius.
EDCS-23001901.summary: This is a votive inscription by Flavus, son of Andion, dedicated to Baelibius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23001922.translation: I went out and seized the son of Aggravius.
EDCS-23001922.summary: This is a short, potentially military or personal inscription, possibly related to a conflict or claim, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23002150.summary: This is a simple inscription of a personal name, Gaius Volusenus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23002263.translation: Proculus, son of Touton, aged 40, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-23002263.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Proculus, son of Touton, including a common epitaph, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23002269.translation: To Touton, son of Abalis, of the Argantiqum tribe.
EDCS-23002269.summary: This is an inscription dedicating something to Touton, son of Abalis, specifying his tribal affiliation, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23002301.translation: To Ena, of the Durota tribe, and family.
EDCS-23002301.summary: This is an inscription dedicating something to Ena and her family, identifying her tribal affiliation, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23002303.summary: This is an inscription recording the names of Nonia Liguria and Titula Cum, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23002317.translation: Of Marcus Aquinius, son of Caius.
EDCS-23002317.summary: This is a genitive inscription, likely indicating ownership or parentage, for Marcus Aquinius, son of Caius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23002318.translation: Of Marcus Sextius Calvus, son of Marcus.
EDCS-23002318.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying an individual by his nomen and filiation.

EDCS-23002319.translation: Of Quintus Seius, son of Publius, of the Menenia tribe, Postumus.
EDCS-23002319.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying an individual by his nomen, filiation, tribe, and cognomen.

EDCS-23002329.translation: To the good soul of Mysticus, aged 35, he is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-23002329.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Mysticus, recording his age and a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-23002338.translation: Gaius Fabius Auda had this made for his brother Ablo, son of Fabius, aged 10.
EDCS-23002338.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Gaius Fabius Auda for his younger brother, recording the deceased's age.

EDCS-23002340.translation: To the Infernal Gods, to the Manes. Elaesus, in memory of his most dutiful wife, [---] years old.
EDCS-23002340.summary: This is a dedication to the underworld deities (Dis Inferis Manibus) by Elaesus for his wife, recording her age.

EDCS-23002343.translation: To the Manes. Amma, wife, daughter of Lascivus; Lascivus, son of Euanthes; Ianuarius, husband, most dutiful, aged 55. They set this up.
EDCS-23002343.summary: This funerary inscription records multiple family members and the age of the husband, Ianuarius.

EDCS-23002361.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription identifying an individual by his praenomen and nomen.

EDCS-23002372.summary: This is a painted inscription, possibly a warning or advertisement, attributed to Verdullus.

EDCS-23002386.translation: As vowed, Rufinus, son of [---], fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-23002386.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Rufinus, son of [---], likely on a religious monument.

EDCS-23002401.translation: Canus set this up for his mother Vadonnia Reburrina, aged 50.
EDCS-23002401.summary: This funerary inscription was set up by Canus for his mother, recording her age.

EDCS-23002413.translation: To Gaius Marius Nigrinus, son of Gaius, of the Galerian tribe, from Ausetanum, flamen of the province of Hispania Citerior, in accordance with the decree of the decurions of Tarraco, after receiving the honor, at the expense of his wife Lucretia Montana.
EDCS-23002413.summary: This is a funerary honorific inscription dedicated to a provincial flamen of Hispania Citerior by his wife, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23002414.translation: To Marcus Fulvius Gratus, son of Titus, of the Galerian tribe, former aedile, quaestor, flamen, and duovir of the colony of Tarraco, the triumphant city, and tribune of the soldiers in the XI Claudia Pia Fidelis legion; Quintus Granius Lemnus and his daughter Grania Procula set this up on account of his many merits.
EDCS-23002414.summary: This is an honorific inscription for a prominent local magistrate and military officer in the colony of Tarraco, likely set up by his family.

EDCS-23002415.translation: Marcus Coelius Durracus, son of Marcus, of the Aemilian tribe, aged 80, is buried here, and his son Lucius Coelius Bellicus, aged 25, is buried here. Marcus Coelius Sabinus, a soldier of the VI Legion, from the century of Verecundus, erected this monument to his father and brother. May the earth lie lightly upon him.
EDCS-23002415.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a father and son, with the monument erected by the surviving son who is also a soldier.

EDCS-23002424.translation: Marcus, son of Masimus Primus, of Turros.
EDCS-23002424.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph or dedication identifying an individual named Marcus.

EDCS-23002442.translation: During the consulship of Marcus Livius and Lucius Calpurnius Piso.
EDCS-23002442.summary: This is a dating formula, likely the beginning of a longer inscription or a reference point for a specific event.

EDCS-23002459.summary: This inscription identifies an individual named Martialis, possibly indicating origin or ethnicity.

EDCS-23002463.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Primigenius made this for his wife Helpidis, also for his freedwoman, who lived 36 years and 6 days, who deserved well.
EDCS-23002463.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by a freedman for his wife and freedwoman.

EDCS-23002471.translation: Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of the deified Augustus, grandson of the deified Julius, pontifex maximus, imperator for the 8th time, consul for the 5th time, with tribunician power for the 43rd year; in Bracaraugusta.
EDCS-23002471.summary: This is an imperial titulature inscription for Emperor Tiberius, likely erected in the city of Bracaraugusta.

EDCS-23002486.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Camalus Corunus, son of Corunus Medamus, of Entienus, is buried here. Camalus Corunus, his dutiful son, took care to have this made for his father.
EDCS-23002486.summary: This is a funerary inscription where a son dedicates a monument to his deceased father.

EDCS-23002487.translation: To the Divine Spirits, sacred. LANN IP PINT, aged 85 years, is buried here.
EDCS-23002487.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a burial plot to an individual named LANN IP PINT.

EDCS-23101956.translation: To Macedonius, my dearest presbyter and deserving husband, his wife Maria adorned this tomb.
EDCS-23101956.summary: A tomb inscription dedicated by a wife to her husband, Macedonius, a presbyter, likely dating to the early Christian period.

EDCS-23101957.translation: To Rufus, her dearest husband, Viventius adorned this tomb.
EDCS-23101957.summary: A tomb inscription from Viventius to her husband Rufus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23101959.translation: Eterius lived worthily in peace for forty years; Pimenius adorned this.
EDCS-23101959.summary: A tomb inscription recording the death of Eterius after forty years of life, likely from the late Roman or early Christian period.

EDCS-23101963.translation: Ursicinus departed in the Lord's peace, in the name of God, aged forty-seven years; his daughter, aged 8 years, arranged this.
EDCS-23101963.summary: A tomb inscription for Ursicinus, who died at 47, arranged by his 8-year-old daughter, likely from the early Christian period.

EDCS-23101964.translation: Adam, Eva, Iosephus, Ismailites, and Judah.
EDCS-23101964.summary: A brief inscription listing several names, possibly representing a family or group, with a possible religious or ethnic designation ('Ismailites').

EDCS-23101969.translation: May you rest in peace, Ampelius.
EDCS-23101969.summary: A simple epitaph wishing peace to Ampelius, likely from the early Christian period.

EDCS-23201449.summary: A dedication to Flavius Flavianus, purpose unknown but possibly funerary or honorific.

EDCS-23300229.translation: Lucius Caecilius Iustus' freedwoman's mother, Caecilia Cara; they arranged for this to be made. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-23300229.summary: A tombstone erected by Lucius Caecilius Iustus for his freedwoman's mother, Caecilia Cara, with the standard 'sit tibi terra levis' wish.

EDCS-23300230.translation: To Titus Valerius Syros, freedman of Valerius Crescens, aged 40, Valerius Crescens arranged for this to be made. For the Spirits of the Departed, to Arruntia Pusinca, aged 70, Valerius Crescens arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-23300230.summary: A double tombstone commissioned by Valerius Crescens for his freedman Titus Valerius Syros (aged 40) and for Arruntia Pusinca (aged 70).

EDCS-23300231.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Fortunatus.
EDCS-23300231.summary: A dedication to the 'Dis Manibus' (Spirits of the Departed) for Fortunatus, likely a funerary inscription.

EDCS-23300232.translation: To Publius Sempronius, son of Titus, a man...
EDCS-23300232.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription honoring Publius Sempronius, son of Titus, likely from the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-23300234.translation: To the Divine Shades. Caecilius Polychronus, freedman of Caecilius, made this for his father Caecilius Caecilianus.
EDCS-23300234.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Caecilius Caecilianus by his freedman, Caecilius Polychronus.

EDCS-23300239.translation: From the workshop of Ani: may you use it happily.
EDCS-23300239.summary: This is a maker's inscription, possibly from a piece of pottery or metalwork, indicating its origin and wishing good fortune to the user.

EDCS-23300241.translation: Kantonia Nonunua, wife of Quintus, (and) Karitonis Quintus V L LI Varaden A S E.
EDCS-23300241.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Kantonia Nonunua, wife of Quintus, with additional names that are difficult to interpret fully due to abbreviation and possible regional variation.

EDCS-23400551.summary: This is a short epitaph or commendation for Diocles, describing him as a man worthy of praise.

EDCS-23900079.translation: Vindilius Licinus, freedman of Lucius, and Publius Manilius Firmus, freedman of Caius, took care to have the altar, pavement, and ceiling of the shrine made.
EDCS-23900079.summary: This inscription records the completion of construction work on a shrine, including an altar, pavement, and ceiling, by two freedmen, Vindilius Licinus and Publius Manilius Firmus.

EDCS-23900080.translation: Quintus Coronius Barbatus, from Avella, aged 70, is buried here; may the earth be light for you. Quintus Coronius Vernus, son of Veranius, placed this according to the will.
EDCS-23900080.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Coronius Barbatus, specifying his age, origin, and burial location, with the monument erected by his son Quintus Coronius Vernus as per his will.

EDCS-23900098.translation: To the Genius of the conventus of Tarraco.
EDCS-23900098.summary: This is a dedication to the Genius, the protective spirit, of the conventus (administrative district) of Tarraco.

EDCS-23900099.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Fabia Dionysia, aged 17 years, 3 months, and 10 days, is laid here; may the earth be light for you. Dionysiodorus and Restituta made this for their most devoted daughter.
EDCS-23900099.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fabia Dionysia, detailing her age at death and stating that the monument was erected by Dionysiodorus and Restituta, likely her parents.

EDCS-23900100.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Helpis, the most devoted mother, took care to place this for her son Cornelius Iulianus, aged 15 years; he is buried here; may the earth be light for you.
EDCS-23900100.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelius Iulianus, specifying his age at death and noting that the monument was erected by his mother, Iulia Helpis.

EDCS-23900101.translation: Gaius Julius Communis willingly fulfilled his vow for a merit.
EDCS-23900101.summary: This is a votive inscription by Gaius Julius Communis.

EDCS-23900102.translation: Hermetius, freedman of Gaius Julius Euodus, son of Gaius Julius, set up this monument to the Genius of the camp of the Carduani and to this place.
EDCS-23900102.summary: This is a dedication by Hermetius to the Genius of the camp of the Carduani.

EDCS-23900261.translation: Marcus Julius, son of Balanus, aged 50, is buried here.
EDCS-23900261.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Marcus Julius.

EDCS-23900262.translation: Saturninus, slave of Antonius Ai, aged 40, is buried here.
EDCS-23900262.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Saturninus, a slave.

EDCS-23900264.translation: Sempronia, daughter of Attasis, aged 25, is buried here. Gaius Sempronius (erected this) for his mother.
EDCS-23900264.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Sempronia, erected by her son Gaius Sempronius.

EDCS-23900265.translation: Antestia, daughter of Onse, aged 25, is buried here. Aemilius Seranus, son of Flavius, aged [---], is buried here.
EDCS-23900265.summary: This is a tomb inscription recording the burial of Antestia and Aemilius Seranus.

EDCS-24400061.translation: Grindiricus with his own hand [contribution description]. Vadentinus [contribution description]. Mauneficia [contribution description]. Natovigius [contribution description]. Teudotis [contribution description]. Gugo, son of Guldurad [contribution description]. Ranila [contribution description]. Ustrildis with her own hand [contribution description]. Godulfus [contribution description]. Unildero [contribution description]. Euvodia [contribution description]. Favila [contribution description]. Gussrio, son of Dionisius [contribution description]. Teudulfius [contribution description]. Vitellu [contribution description]. Emiteri and Eladia [contribution description]. Rodericus [contribution description]. Fragola [contribution description]. Rigius [contribution description]. Aretius [contribution description]. Fredo [contribution description]. Argefredus in another chest [contribution description]. Teodulfus for his son [contribution description]. Natovigius in the third chest [contribution description].lius [contribution description]. quiul in another chest [contribution description]. Deucicius [contribution description]. Ranila in another chest [contribution description]. Aloricia [contribution description]. Ustrildina [contribution description]. Agutor [contribution description]. Lunaredus in another chest [contribution description]. Elisinus for his mother Decentinus [contribution description]. Aurosus [contribution description]. domnus [contribution description]. salior in another chest [contribution description]. Iermina [contribution description]. Ranila in the third chest [contribution description]. Trasemundus in Alba [contribution description]. Gogulfus in his third [contribution description]. Marcellus in Ciliarto [contribution description].
EDCS-24400061.summary: This is a complex inscription, likely recording contributions or distributions, possibly related to communal or funerary provisions, with many individuals named and quantities specified.

EDCS-24500002.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly a funerary dedication, mentioning a freedman.

EDCS-24500012.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a name from a tomb or dedication.

EDCS-24500029.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Cornelius Lucius, freedman, aged 30. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-24500029.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Cornelius Lucius, a freedman.

EDCS-24500051.translation: Sacred to the Infernal Gods and the Manes. Tertiola, daughter of Forentius, placed this in memory of her granddaughter.
EDCS-24500051.summary: This funerary inscription records the memorial set up by Tertiola for her granddaughter.

EDCS-24500056.summary: This inscription appears to be a brief, perhaps jocular, note about a dog named Alea.

EDCS-24500063.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely recording the name of Camarica, wife of Quintus Petronius.

EDCS-24500072.summary: This inscription simply records the name Gaius Valerius Verdullus.

EDCS-24500078.translation: To [my] brother, Paulus, you are called good, Froila, and they will be aware of what she has given to us and ours, and let us be mindful, and if she gave something, let us be mindful of her.
EDCS-24500078.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription addressed to a brother, likely concerning familial duties and remembrance.

EDCS-24500079.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription that begins with a personal statement of having heard something.

EDCS-24500082.translation: 4 measures of grain, 4 measures of grain, 2 measures of grain, 1 measure of grain, 4 measures of grain, 2, 2, 1 measure of grain, 1 measure of grain, 1 measure of grain. Paulus: 3 measures of grain. Rufinus: 1 measure of grain. Red: 4 measures of grain, 3.
EDCS-24500082.summary: This inscription appears to be a record of grain distribution or accounting, listing quantities and possibly recipients or units of measure.

EDCS-24500817.translation: Peius dedicated [this] to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-24500817.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a person named Peius.

EDCS-24500945.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription that likely records a filiation, indicating a son of someone named Q.

EDCS-24500990.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that begins with the praenomen Quintus.

EDCS-24600013.translation: Lucius Sestius Corocaudius, freedman of Lucius, and his comrade Tubinenses, had this made for Publius Clodameus, son of Corocaudus, of the Seaveoni.
EDCS-24600013.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument erected by friends or associates for Publius Clodameus, son of Corocaudus.

EDCS-24600560.translation: The pottery works of Lucius Bruttidius Augustalis.
EDCS-24600560.summary: This inscription likely marks a pottery workshop or a product from the workshop of Lucius Bruttidius Augustalis.

EDCS-24600562.translation: Here, the blessed virgin Thecla, a servant of Christ, rests in peace, for whom her homeland Egypt merited it; she lived seventy-seven years and rests in peace in the Lord.
EDCS-24600562.summary: This is a Christian epitaph for Thecla, a virgin servant of Christ, who lived seventy-seven years and died in peace.

EDCS-24600563.translation: Here rests in peace Lupulus the traveler.
EDCS-24600563.summary: This is a short epitaph for Lupulus, identified as a traveler, who rests in peace.

EDCS-24600566.summary: This inscription is a simple naming of Eutactus Murranus, possibly a tombstone or owner's mark.

EDCS-24600578.translation: Segimus fulfilled his vow willingly with his whole heart.
EDCS-24600578.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Segimus.

EDCS-24600583.summary: This inscription appears to be the name of Lucius Licinius Atticus, possibly on a tombstone or property marker.

EDCS-24600614.translation: Serpentius, slave of Rufina, if you pursue him with malice, within a year the sacred vindicta of the goddess will pursue you, [leading you] from this [place] where the hands of the gods do not lead.
EDCS-24600614.summary: This inscription is a curse against anyone who disturbs Serpentius, the slave of Rufina, threatening them with divine retribution within a year.

EDCS-24600623.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription beginning with 'Your brothers b...', the full context and purpose are unclear.

EDCS-24600626.translation: Notitia of the cheese-maker, Dominica: 10 measures, 5 measures. Rutilius: 7 measures, 7 measures, with 5 [units] of a solidus. Aemilianus: 12 measures. Afinius: 3 measures. Aurilianus: [unclear]. Testius Pti [unclear].
EDCS-24600626.summary: This inscription appears to be a record of quantities, possibly of cheese or grain, with names and amounts, likely related to trade or distribution.

EDCS-24600627.translation: Theodebigius and thirty-one men from the tenth century group, preserve.
EDCS-24600627.summary: This inscription likely records a group of men, possibly a work crew, and their contribution or duty.

EDCS-24600631.translation: Acpulsoio Segontius, son of Primi, erected this as a vow.
EDCS-24600631.summary: This is a votive inscription by Acpulsoio Segontius, son of Primi, dedicating a monument.

EDCS-24600636.translation: Hear, Borducib! Badens gave this bright ship for the needs of the prosperous and of the distant ones, that they may possess the inheritance for us, the sanctuary of God, to be offered to our most glorious lord King Recaredo, son of Ganafred. Your servant, T eredo, to our lord King Recaredo, son of Gumar. Beati, who walk in the law of the Lord. Offering to God. Remembering God, you are called. I, your servant Paulus.
EDCS-24600636.summary: This lengthy inscription is a dedication of a sanctuary to God, made by T eredo and Paulus, servants of King Recaredo, referencing biblical passages and a plea for remembrance.

EDCS-24600639.translation: Sa Lolos, Rofinos Theodosius, son of Pauli, genius, Acentius, son of Entius, and Ses [erected this].
