EDCS-24600639.summary: This inscription lists several individuals, likely dedicating a monument or honoring someone.

EDCS-24600641.translation: Four times IIII [erected this]. Rustici, Flabios Constanti, Severos, Ponpuni, and Pompei.
EDCS-24600641.summary: This inscription lists several names, possibly a group or family, and their contribution or dedication.

EDCS-24600652.translation: Giussius, Possidius, Severianus, Remus, Iucundus, Exsuperantius, [---], Frumentius, Acinus, Auriolus, Martius, Avinus, Bodo, Marcus, Ursus, Avitus, Fortunatus, Venerius, Mauricius, Paulus [dedicated this].
EDCS-24600652.summary: This is a list of names, presumably dedicators of a monument or members of a group.

EDCS-24600653.translation: Son of Rufinus, Sulurus, Lolus, son of Flainus, Principius, Marcillinus, [---], Actian, Nonnellus [dedicated this].
EDCS-24600653.summary: This inscription lists several individuals, likely patrons or dedicators of a monument.

EDCS-24600654.translation: Son of Maternus, Vitorius, Antonius, Iohannes [dedicated this].
EDCS-24600654.summary: This inscription lists several individuals, likely dedicators of a monument.

EDCS-25200772.translation: To the divine majesty of Germanicus Gothicus, by decree of the town council, the public resources of Castulo are devoted.
EDCS-25200772.summary: This inscription is a public dedication by the town council of Castulo to the divine majesty of Germanicus Gothicus, likely honoring him.

EDCS-25300372.translation: To Nero Claudius Caesar Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, for the province being purged of brigands and of those who inculcated a new superstition into the human race.
EDCS-25300372.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Nero for purging the province of brigands and a new superstition, likely a decree or a public monument commemorating his actions.

EDCS-25600525.summary: This inscription is a fragmentary personal name, likely part of a longer dedication or epitaph.

EDCS-25600531.summary: This inscription records the name and title of Gaius Antonius, who held the consulship.

EDCS-25600532.summary: This inscription refers to a freedman of a priest (flamen), possibly on a funerary monument.

EDCS-25601376.summary: This inscription is a dedication of something sacred to Cosovius Ascanius.

EDCS-25900459.translation: The tomb here holds a sad lament for the beautiful and lovely Muse Valeria, whose husband and father of her children, sweet Theseus, when she departed to fate, first pressing his lips to her lips, then received the gift of her soul from her breast.
EDCS-25900459.summary: This is a Greek epitaph lamenting the death of Valeria Muse and describing the grief of her husband, Theseus, who died shortly after her, possibly of sorrow.

EDCS-26400007.translation: Manius Sergius, son of Manius, proconsul, for the seventh time
EDCS-26400007.summary: This inscription records Manius Sergius, son of Manius, who served as proconsul, possibly indicating multiple terms or a significant tenure.

EDCS-26400617.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely containing a personal name (Charite) and potentially another name or title, possibly on a funerary context.

EDCS-26601050.summary: This inscription identifies a 'Tessera Cauriensis' as a magistrate of the town of Turi.

EDCS-26700200.translation: Hermes, slave of Quintus Sextus. Hermes, slave of Quintus Sextus.
EDCS-26700200.summary: This inscription repeats the name and ownership of a slave named Hermes, likely appearing on a tombstone.

EDCS-26700358.summary: This inscription is a personal name, likely part of a dedication or funerary monument.

EDCS-27000229.summary: This is a commercial inscription advertising lamps for sale.

EDCS-27000230.summary: This is a commercial inscription from a workshop, identifying the product as filtered lamps.

EDCS-27800025.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Titullus Pin took care to have this monument made.
EDCS-27800025.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned by Titullus Pin for the deceased.

EDCS-27800026.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caurunius, who died at the age of 26.

EDCS-27800027.translation: To the dearest Celti[us?/son?], son of Cicius.
EDCS-27800027.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Celtius, son of Cicius.

EDCS-27800031.translation: Reburrus willingly and deservedly paid his vow to the Household Gods of Vialbus and Ilurbedus.
EDCS-27800031.summary: This is a votive inscription where Reburrus paid a vow to household deities.

EDCS-27800032.translation: Atta Lugua, wife of Ebureinus of Caraecicum, willingly and deservedly paid her vow to the people of Avilensis.
EDCS-27800032.summary: This is a votive inscription where Atta Lugua paid a vow, possibly to a local community or deity.

EDCS-27800040.translation: Willingly paid a vow to the god Vaelicus.
EDCS-27800040.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating a vow to the god Vaelicus.

EDCS-27800041.translation: Sacred to the god Vaelicus. A...
EDCS-27800041.summary: This is a votive or dedicatory inscription to the god Vaelicus, though incomplete.

EDCS-27800054.translation: To Jupiter. Ata, daughter of Hirnus of the Ambatici, willingly and with a willing heart paid her vow.
EDCS-27800054.summary: This is a votive inscription where Ata, daughter of Hirnus, paid a vow to Jupiter.

EDCS-27800059.translation: To Caecilius Metellus, twice consul, the victor.
EDCS-27800059.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Caecilius Metellus, noted as a victorious consul, likely on a monument or honorific structure.

EDCS-27800061.translation: Lucius Portius arranged for this monument to be erected for his excellent administration of the province, by the people of the Bastetani.
EDCS-27800061.summary: This text records a public dedication by Lucius Portius, honoring his successful governance of a province, sponsored by the people of Bastetani.

EDCS-27800062.translation: The war of Caesar and the fatherland was largely concluded, with Sextus and Gnaeus, sons of Pompey the Great, defeated here in the territory of the Bastetani.
EDCS-27800062.summary: This inscription commemorates the decisive defeat of the sons of Pompey the Great in the territory of the Bastetani, marking a significant conclusion to a war involving Caesar.

EDCS-27800066.translation: To the departed spirits. To T. Flavius, son of T., of the Voting Tribe Voltinia, from Gentina, for his son, himself and his wife.
EDCS-27800066.summary: This is a funerary dedication (DMS) for T. Flavius and his family, mentioning his tribal affiliation (Voltinia) and place of origin (Gentina).

EDCS-27800070.translation: M. Cornelius Albus, duovir of the colony of Vegentium, with honorable recompense, for the colony of Vegentium.
EDCS-27800070.summary: This inscription likely records an official act or dedication by M. Cornelius Albus, a duovir of the Roman colony of Vegentium, possibly related to a public building or honor.

EDCS-27800071.translation: Allia, daughter of Cuniricus, Ungius, wife, aged 30. C. Flavinus, her brother, aged 15. L. Calvus, her son, arranged for this to be erected. (with her sister).
EDCS-27800071.summary: This is a funerary epitaph commemorating Allia, her brother, and her son, providing ages and indicating familial relationships and the person responsible for the monument.

EDCS-27800114.translation: Nigella, daughter of Tilrecus, from Serpaesis.
EDCS-27800114.summary: This is a simple epitaph or dedication for Nigella, identifying her father and place of origin.

EDCS-27800116.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Valerius Silius arranged for this to be made, with the burial of the deceased of Aulius, and the bones were in his possession for 50 years.
EDCS-27800116.summary: This funerary inscription records Valerius Silius's arrangement for a burial, mentioning the deceased of Aulius and a duration of possession of the remains.

EDCS-27800117.summary: This is a short funerary dedication (Dis Manibus) to Vareus.

EDCS-27800120.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication or epitaph for Cinicus, identified as both a freedman and a son.

EDCS-27800128.summary: This inscription is a dedication or memorial to Aelcius Quintus Aurelius.

EDCS-27800129.translation: Araus, son of Araviaqum, to Turanus.
EDCS-27800129.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Araus, son of Araviaqum, to the deity Turanus.

EDCS-27800130.summary: This inscription is a dedication or memorial to Atia, daughter of Caburon.

EDCS-27800131.translation: Attia set up this monument to Balaisa Areinicum, and Vbaso and A[...].
EDCS-27800131.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by Attia for Balaisa Areinicum and others, likely a funerary or commemorative monument.

EDCS-28400009.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Sempronia Aunia set up this monument for her daughter Lic [Licoiom] daughter of Lic[o]. She set up the monument.
EDCS-28400009.summary: This funerary inscription records Sempronia Aunia setting up a monument for her daughter.

EDCS-28400018.summary: This inscription likely records a measure of land or distance associated with Vindicus, son of Sequrius.

EDCS-28400060.summary: This inscription is likely a fragment recording the name of Rufi, son of Miccio.

EDCS-28400084.summary: This inscription appears to be a name inscription, possibly for a tomb or ownership mark.

EDCS-28400088.translation: He made [this] for Materna, his most dutiful wife, aged 35. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-28400088.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication by a husband to his deceased wife, Materna, including a standard epitaph wish.

EDCS-28400425.translation: Cloutus, son of Munuapius, from Iappia, aged 30; to his son Auscus, son of Boutinus, from Uxama.
EDCS-28400425.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Cloutus, son of Munuapius, and possibly sets up a monument for his son Auscus, son of Boutinus.

EDCS-28400426.translation: To the Tutela of Lirias, Pompeius Clitus Corinthus Calvinus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-28400426.summary: This is a votive dedication to a local deity named Tutela of Lirias, made by Pompeius Clitus Corinthus Calvinus.

EDCS-28400427.summary: This is a dedication to the household gods (Lares) by Quintus.

EDCS-28400432.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Arator, aged 50. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-28400432.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Arator, aged 50, with a standard wish for peace in the afterlife.

EDCS-28400433.translation: To Aravo, son of Aron, aged 70.
EDCS-28400433.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aravo, son of Aron, who died at the age of 70.

EDCS-28400434.translation: To Avelcus, son of Elaesus, aged 30.
EDCS-28400434.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Avelcus, son of Elaesus, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-28400435.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Aunias, daughter of Tritus, aged 11.
EDCS-28400435.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aunias, daughter of Tritus, who died at the age of 11.

EDCS-28400436.translation: To Aurelius Talavus, son of Talavus, aged 100.
EDCS-28400436.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelius Talavus, son of Talavus, who reportedly lived to be 100 years old.

EDCS-28400437.translation: To Blena, daughter of Salai, aged 40.
EDCS-28400437.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Blena, daughter of Salai, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-28400438.summary: This is a dedication or funerary epitaph for Cloutius, son of Boutus.

EDCS-28400439.translation: To Cornelius Mabucius, aged 25. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-28400439.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelius Mabucius, aged 25, with a standard wish for peace in the afterlife.

EDCS-28400440.translation: To Docio, son of Tritus, aged 80. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-28400440.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Docio, who died at the age of 80.

EDCS-28400441.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Iemuria Caenonis, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-28400442.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Pintovius Clouti, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-28400445.summary: This inscription, possibly a dedication or a title, mentions Quintus Sertorius, a proconsul, and the virtue of Pietas.

EDCS-28700003.translation: Primus, freedman of Cornelia Optata, lies here, whom his parents brought here after death.
EDCS-28700003.summary: This funerary inscription records that Primus, a freedman of Cornelia Optata, was buried here by his parents.

EDCS-28700009.translation: Two-year quinquennial duovir, military tribune of the March legion.
EDCS-28700009.summary: This inscription likely records the career of a Roman official, detailing his military and administrative positions.

EDCS-28700010.translation: Marcus Licinius Hilarus, freedman of Marcus.
EDCS-28700010.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying Marcus Licinius Hilarus, a freedman.

EDCS-28700036.translation: Whoever you are, look upon this sacred monument and the place where I lie, and see my resting place; I preferred to have a sacred place for myself, a minister to the gods, and completed sixty years of life. After being buried, I commend myself to be protected by the saints, so that when the voracious flame comes to burn the lands, I may justly be associated with the companies of the saints and rise again. Here, life's course finished, Crispinus the presbyter, a sinner, rests in Christ's peace. Era 731.
EDCS-28700036.summary: This is a funerary epitaph of Crispinus, a presbyter, who died in Era 731, expressing his hope for resurrection and eternal peace.

EDCS-28700465.translation: He placed a monument in memory of Pentovius, aged 8.
EDCS-28700465.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the placement of a monument for Pentovius, who died at the age of 8.

EDCS-28700491.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Sempronius Ma, aged 20, dutiful to his family, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-28700491.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Sempronius Ma, who died at the age of 20 and is described as dutiful to his family.

EDCS-28700499.summary: This inscription records a construction project undertaken by Avita.

EDCS-28700511.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication to a person named Sabinus.

EDCS-28700513.translation: Publius Octavius, slave of Quintus and Sextus Mar, made this at his own expense.
EDCS-28700513.summary: This inscription records a construction commissioned by Publius Octavius, a slave.

EDCS-28700515.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld: Terentia Postuma for her most deserving slave, Julia.
EDCS-28700515.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Terentia Postuma for her slave Julia.

EDCS-28700518.translation: P. Medo, aged three years, is buried here.
EDCS-28700518.summary: This inscription is a tombstone for a child named P. Medo.

EDCS-28700522.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Cudatus.

EDCS-28700594.translation: Quintus Licinius Fuscus, water-channel supervisor from Varaiensis, willingly fulfilled his vow to the Nymphs for their favor.
EDCS-28700594.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Quintus Licinius Fuscus, an official responsible for water channels.

EDCS-28700595.translation: For the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Severus Pius, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, descendant of the deified Hadrian, descendant of the deified Trajan Parthicus and deified Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, thrice Parthicus Maximus, thrice Britannicus Maximus, thrice Germanicus Maximus, with tribunician power for the 17th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, proconsul, 93 miles.
EDCS-28700595.summary: This inscription is a milestone marking 93 miles dedicated to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, listing his extensive titles and lineage, likely dating to his 17th year of tribunician power.

EDCS-28701461.summary: This inscription appears to be a fragmentary dedication or epitaph for Quintus Vibius.

EDCS-28701463.translation: Lucoaugustanus, son of Cericius Capito, aged 38 years, is buried here. Caius Felicius [did this] for himself.
EDCS-28701463.summary: This inscription is a tombstone for Lucoaugustanus, son of Cericius Capito, with Caius Felicius indicated as the dedicator.

EDCS-28701467.translation: Lucobo Arousabo willingly fulfilled her vow as deserved, Rutilia Antiania.
EDCS-28701467.summary: This is a votive inscription by Rutilia Antiania dedicating something to Lucobo Arousabo.

EDCS-28701468.translation: Valerius Clemens willingly fulfilled his vow to Lucobo Gudarovis.
EDCS-28701468.summary: This is a votive inscription by Valerius Clemens dedicating something to Lucobo Gudarovis.

EDCS-28701479.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating that Ambati's daughter set up her own tomb while still living.

EDCS-28701518.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, Attic [lived/is buried].
EDCS-28701518.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Deceased by Attic, likely referring to the deceased Attic or a dedicant named Attic.

EDCS-28800017.summary: This inscription appears to record a measurement or quantity, but is heavily damaged and its meaning is unclear.

EDCS-28800019.summary: This inscription possibly records a gift or dedication from Cai Materni's Sacynto.

EDCS-28800020.summary: This is a very short inscription, likely the name of an individual, possibly on a tombstone or property marker.

EDCS-28800021.translation: To Marcus Popillius, son of Publius.
EDCS-28800021.summary: This is a dedication or funerary inscription for Marcus Popillius, son of Publius.

EDCS-28800027.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Tranquillus, who was 35 years old.

EDCS-28800041.summary: This inscription names Hilari, identifying him in relation to Aulus Sestius, and likely includes a cognomen Dama.

EDCS-28800615.translation: Marcus Baebius Faustus, son of Gnaeus, of the Galeria tribe.
EDCS-28800615.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Marcus Baebius Faustus, identifying his father and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-28800617.summary: This inscription identifies Marcus Baebius and his father, Gnaeus.

EDCS-28800655.translation: The body of Zacharias, a Christian, lies buried here. He lived for three years and died on the fourth day before the Kalends of November. With Arvanus's permission, Ianuarius went to the stars. Nothing is for Maxima.
EDCS-28800655.summary: This is a Christian tombstone inscription for Zacharias, recording his age at death and the date of his passing, likely from the 4th century CE or later.

EDCS-28800658.summary: This inscription appears to record the names of two individuals, Felicitas Publica and Lucius Septimius Severus.

EDCS-28800660.translation: The wound lives hidden in the chest.
EDCS-28800660.summary: This is a poetic or funerary epigram expressing a hidden sorrow or wound.

EDCS-28800670.translation: Sacred to the Household Gods. Gaius Iulius Saturninus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28800670.summary: This is a dedication to the Lares (household gods) by Gaius Iulius Saturninus, who willingly paid a vow.

EDCS-28800695.translation: I entreat that you command what parts of me I commend to you, the spirit, the face, the belly of Eterion, son of Aulius. In all anger, give [them] to the gods below. He begs and asks that you care for the cattle, which Fulvilla thus begs: the mouth, hands, fingers, ears, voice, [---]
EDCS-28800695.summary: This is a magical inscription, likely a curse or a plea, where the deceased (or someone on their behalf) addresses the underworld gods, commending parts of themselves and requesting care for cattle, possibly related to Eterion Auliani and Fulvilla.

EDCS-28800696.summary: This inscription likely refers to monuments or achievements related to Argus and Sympherusa, possibly marking their burials or honors.

EDCS-28800709.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Annia Cada/ri, indicating her parentage and age at death.

EDCS-28800710.summary: This is a fragmentary tombstone inscription, indicating the approximate age at death of an individual identified as the son of Arronis.

EDCS-29100967.translation: Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Augustus, Germanicus Maximus, Dacicus Maximus, Sarmaticus Maximus, Pontifex Maximus, Imperator IV times, Father of the Fatherland, Consul, Proconsul, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, Prince of Youth, son of our lord Emperor Gaius Julius Verus, restored roads and bridges collapsed by the passage of time, under the care of Quintus Lucius Decius, Legate Pro Praetore, thirty-seven miles from Bracara.
EDCS-29100967.summary: This inscription records the restoration of roads and bridges by Emperors Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus, dated to their reign (AD 235-238).

EDCS-29400204.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to record a dedication to a person named Cornelius Liraipomber, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29500014.translation: In the name of the Lord, Gundesa.
EDCS-29500014.summary: This inscription invokes the Lord and names Gundesa, likely a personal dedication or memorial from the early Christian period.

EDCS-29500078.translation: To Valerius Cres, IIvir; Valerius St, IIvir; Valerius C [---].
EDCS-29500078.summary: This inscription records the names of three individuals, at least two of whom held the office of IIvir (a type of local magistrate), possibly related to a dedication or honorific monument in the Roman period.

EDCS-29500092.summary: This inscription simply lists two personal names, Coemea and Plandica, likely representing a tombstone or a dedication made by these individuals.

EDCS-29600387.translation: Nero Claudius, son of the deified Claudius Augustus, grandson of Germanicus Caesar Augustus, great-grandson of Tiberius Caesar Augustus, great-great-grandson of the deified Augustus, Caesar Augustus Germanicus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power V, Consul III, [in the year] 812 (Ab urbe condita).
EDCS-29600387.summary: This inscription is a titulature of Emperor Nero, listing his full imperial titles and dignities, likely from a public monument during his reign (AD 54-68).

EDCS-29601141.translation: Marcia Pietas had this made for the god Herotoragus out of her own means.
EDCS-29601141.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication made by Marcia Pietas to the god Herotoragus, likely from the Roman provincial period.

EDCS-29700002.translation: To our lord Valerius Maximinus, most noble Caesar, thirty-two miles.
EDCS-29700002.summary: This inscription is a milestone or road marker dedicated to Caesar Valerius Maximinus, indicating a distance of 32 miles, likely from the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-29700003.translation: To our lord Flavius Claudius Julianus, victor and triumphant forever, always Augustus, thirty-three [miles].
EDCS-29700003.summary: This inscription is a milestone or road marker dedicated to Emperor Julian, proclaiming his victories and titles, likely from the 4th century AD.

EDCS-29700012.translation: Emperor Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, Father of the Fatherland, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power XVIII, Consul III, from the Augustan grove, ninety-six miles.
EDCS-29700012.summary: This inscription is a milestone or road marker detailing the imperial titles of Emperor Hadrian and marking a distance of 96 miles, dated to his 18th tribunician power (AD 133-134).

EDCS-29700030.translation: To our lord the Emperor Valerius Licinius Licinianus the Invincible Augustus, chief priest, with the tribunician power for the 7th time, consul for the 3rd time, proconsul.
EDCS-29700030.summary: This is a titulus honouring the emperor Licinius, likely from the early 4th century CE, recording his titles and powers.

EDCS-29700039.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carus, the Pious, Fortunate, Invincible Augustus, chief priest, with the tribunician power, consul for the 2nd time.
EDCS-29700039.summary: This inscription honours the emperor Carus, dated to 282-283 CE, listing his imperial titles and dignities.

EDCS-29700041.translation: To the Emperor Julianus Caesar, the Victorious and Triumphant, always Augustus, 14 miles.
EDCS-29700041.summary: This inscription records a distance of 14 miles in honour of Emperor Julian, likely from the mid-4th century CE.

EDCS-29700044.translation: To the Pious, Fortunate, Invincible Augustus, chief priest, with the tribunician power, consul for the 2nd time, father of the fatherland, for the 15th time.
EDCS-29700044.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription honouring an emperor, likely Diocletian or Maximian, listing his titles and powers, possibly from the late 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29700045.translation: To Carinus, Pious Prince of the Youth, father of the fatherland, consul, proconsul.
EDCS-29700045.summary: This inscription honours Carinus, dating to the late 3rd century CE, listing his various titles and offices.

EDCS-29700048.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Severus Pius, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, descendant of the deified Trajan Parthicus and deified Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus, with the tribunician power for the 17th time, emperor for the 3rd time, consul for the 4th time, father of the fatherland, proconsul, 16 miles.
EDCS-29700048.summary: This lengthy inscription honours Emperor Caracalla, dating to 211-213 CE, detailing his extensive lineage and imperial honours over a distance of 16 miles.

EDCS-29700051.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, grandson of the deified Septimius Severus Pius, son of the deified Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Magnus, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, with the tribunician power for the 2nd time, consul for the 2nd time, proconsul, father of the fatherland, the most valiant and most fortunate prince, 17 miles from Bracara.
EDCS-29700051.summary: This inscription honours Emperor Elagabalus, dated to 214-215 CE, marking a distance of 17 miles from Bracara and listing his titles.

EDCS-29700053.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carus, the Pious, Fortunate, Invincible Augustus, chief priest, with the tribunician power, consul for the 2nd time, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-29700053.summary: This inscription honours Emperor Carus, dated to 282-283 CE, listing his imperial titles and dignities.

EDCS-29700054.translation: To our lord Flavius Julius Constantius Maximus Caesar, the Victorious and Triumphant Augustus.
EDCS-29700054.summary: This inscription honours Emperor Constantius II, likely from the mid-4th century CE, proclaiming him as victor and triumphant Augustus.

EDCS-29700058.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, chief priest, with the tribunician power for the 18th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, 20 miles from Bracara.
EDCS-29700058.summary: This inscription honours Emperor Hadrian, dating to 137-138 CE, recording a distance of 20 miles from Bracara and listing his extensive titles.

EDCS-29700059.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carinus, Pious, Fortunate.
EDCS-29700059.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperor Marcus Aurelius Carinus, likely on a public monument.

EDCS-29700066.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 18th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the country, from Bracara Augusta, 23 miles.
EDCS-29700066.summary: This mileage marker records a distance of 23 miles from Bracara Augusta to honor Emperor Hadrian.

EDCS-29700078.translation: To our lord Magnentius, the victorious, ever Augustus, pious, victorious by land and sea, proconsul, he dedicated 27 miles.
EDCS-29700078.summary: This mileage marker records a distance of 27 miles dedicated to the emperor Magnentius.

EDCS-29700080.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carus, the Invincible, Augustus, with tribunician power, proconsul.
EDCS-29700080.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Marcus Aurelius Carus, likely on a public monument during his reign.

EDCS-29700081.translation: To our lord Maximus Magnentius, the victorious and triumphant, ever Augustus, victorious by land and sea, proconsul, Quintus Morinus dedicated.
EDCS-29700081.summary: This inscription records Quintus Morinus dedicating a monument or marker to the emperor Magnentius.

EDCS-29700084.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Probus Augustus, To the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Carus.
EDCS-29700084.summary: This inscription seems to record honors for two emperors, Marcus Aurelius Probus and Marcus Aurelius Carus, possibly on the same monument.

EDCS-29700088.translation: To our lords Constantine, Constantius, and Constans, Augusti, 32.
EDCS-29700088.summary: This inscription, possibly a mileage marker, honors the Augusti Constantine, Constantius, and Constans.

EDCS-29700091.translation: To our lord Flavius Valerius Constantine, the Invincible, Augustus.
EDCS-29700091.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Constantine I (the Great).

EDCS-29700116.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Severus, son of the Pious, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Germanicus Maximus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 17th time, Imperator for the 3rd time, consul for the 4th time, father of the country, proconsul, from Bracara Augusta, 31 miles.
EDCS-29700116.summary: This extensive mileage marker records a distance of 31 miles from Bracara Augusta and details the extensive imperial titulature of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus (Commodus).

EDCS-29700118.translation: To our lord Galerius Valerius Maximianus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, the Invincible.
EDCS-29700118.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperor Galerius Valerius Maximianus.

EDCS-29700119.translation: To our lord the Emperor, Caesar, the victorious, triumphant Flavius Constantius, son of our lords Constantinus, grandson of our lords Flavius Constantius and Valerius Maximianus.
EDCS-29700119.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Constantius, son of Constantine, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-29700128.translation: To the deified Maximian, 39 miles from Bracara Augusta.
EDCS-29700128.summary: This milestone records a distance to the deified Emperor Maximian, likely from the late Roman period.

EDCS-29700134.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a name inscription, possibly referring to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29700135.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, Great Conqueror of Germans, Great Conqueror of Dacians, Great Conqueror of Sarmatians, High Priest, with the tribunician power for the 5th time, Imperator for the 7th time, Father of the Fatherland, Consul, Proconsul, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, Great Conqueror of Germans, Great Conqueror of Dacians, Great Conqueror of Sarmatians, Prince of Youth, son of our lord the Emperor Julius Verus Maximinus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, under the administration of Quintus Decius Valerianus, clarissimus vir, legate of the Augusti with praetorian power.
EDCS-29700135.summary: This is a lengthy titulary inscription for Emperor Maximinus Thrax and his son Caesar Maximinus, likely from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29700136.translation: To our lord Galerius Maximianus, unconquered, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-29700136.summary: This is a dedication to Caesar Galerius Maximianus, likely from the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-29700138.translation: To our lord, the victorious and triumphant always Augustus, Maximinus Magnentius, greatest, father of the fatherland by land and sea, Callaicus.
EDCS-29700138.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Magnentius, likely from the mid-4th century CE.

EDCS-29700154.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Caro, Caesar.
EDCS-29700154.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription to Emperor Carus, likely from the late 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29700156.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Severus Pius, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, and descendant of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest Conqueror of Parthians, greatest Conqueror of Britons, greatest Conqueror of Germans, High Priest, with the tribunician power for the 17th time, Imperator for the 3rd time, Consul for the 4th time, Father of the Fatherland, Proconsul, 20 miles.
EDCS-29700156.summary: This is an extensive titulary inscription for Emperor Caracalla, likely from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29700166.translation: The Emperor Nerva Caesar Augustus, High Priest, with the tribunician power.
EDCS-29700166.summary: This is a fragmentary titulary inscription for Emperor Nerva, likely from the late 1st century CE.

EDCS-29700168.translation: To our lords Valentinianus and Valens, the most valiant, perpetual, always Augusti. Tiberius Caesar, son of the deified Augustus, grandson of the deified Julius, Augustus, High Priest, Imperator for the 8th time, Consul for the 5th time, with the tribunician power for the 34th time, in Bracara Augusta, for the fourth time, dedicated by their own order.
EDCS-29700168.summary: This inscription honors Emperors Valentinian I and Valens, and also includes the titulary of Tiberius, likely from the mid-4th century CE.

EDCS-29700175.translation: Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, greatest victor in Germany, greatest victor in Dacia, greatest victor in Sarmatia, chief of youth.
EDCS-29700175.summary: This inscription is a titulature for Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, likely dating to his elevation to Caesar or Augustus.

EDCS-29700176.summary: This is a dedication to Flavius Dalmatius, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-29700181.summary: This is a dedication to Magnentius, likely from the mid-4th century CE.

EDCS-29700187.summary: This is a dedication to Magnentius, likely from the mid-4th century CE.

EDCS-29700188.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified [Augustus], chief priest, imperator 15 times, consul 13 times, with tribune power 34 times, father of the fatherland, Bracara 30 [times/years?].
EDCS-29700188.summary: This is an imperial titulature, likely for Diocletian, dating to the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-29700189.translation: To our lord Valentinian, the victorious and triumphant, forever perpetual Augustus.
EDCS-29700189.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Valentinian I, likely from the mid-to-late 4th century CE.

EDCS-29700201.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Carinus, likely dating to his period as Caesar (late 3rd century CE).

EDCS-29700356.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified [Augustus], chief priest, imperator 20 times, consul 13 times, with tribune power 34 times.
EDCS-29700356.summary: This is an imperial titulature, likely for Diocletian, dating to the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-29700357.translation: To the most fortunate era of the Emperors Marcus Aurelius Antoninus and Lucius Aurelius Commodus Augusti.
EDCS-29700357.summary: This inscription likely commemorates a happy period during the joint reign of Marcus Aurelius and Commodus, dating to the late 2nd century CE.

EDCS-29700358.translation: They restored roads and bridges collapsed through age, under the governorship of Quintus Decius.
EDCS-29700358.summary: This inscription records the restoration of roads and bridges by local officials, likely during the reign of the emperor Maximinus Thrax (mid-3rd century CE).

EDCS-29700359.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Claudius Invictus Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, father of the country, consul for the second time, proconsul: one mile.
EDCS-29700359.summary: This inscription likely marks the distance of one mile, dedicated to Emperor Claudius Gothicus.

EDCS-29700360.translation: To Emperor Caesar Gaius Valerius Maximianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, father of the country, from Bracara.
EDCS-29700360.summary: This is a dedication from Bracara to Emperor Maximian (Galerius).

EDCS-29700361.translation: To Emperor Caesar Gaius Aurelius Valerius Diocletianus, holder of tribunician power, Augustus.
EDCS-29700361.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Diocletian.

EDCS-29700362.translation: To Emperor Flavius Valerius Constantius, Pius Felix, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-29700362.summary: This is a dedication to Caesar Constantius Chlorus.

EDCS-29700363.translation: To Emperor Galerius Valerius Maximianus, Invictus, Pius Felix, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-29700363.summary: This is a dedication to Caesar Galerius Maximianus.

EDCS-29700364.translation: To Emperor Caesar Galerius Valerius Maximianus, Pius Felix Invictus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, most noble Caesar, To Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus, Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-29700364.summary: This inscription is a joint dedication to Emperors Maximian and Constantine.

EDCS-29700365.translation: To our lord, our eternal prince, the happy Constantine Augustus.
EDCS-29700365.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Constantine the Great.

EDCS-29700366.translation: To Valerius Licinianus Licinius, Pius Felix Invictus Augustus.
EDCS-29700366.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Licinius.

EDCS-29700367.summary: This is a dedication to Flavius Crispus, likely Caesar Crispus, son of Constantine.

EDCS-29700368.translation: To our lord Valerius Licinianus Licinius the Younger.
EDCS-29700368.summary: This is a dedication to Valerius Licinianus Licinius the Younger, son of Emperor Licinius.

EDCS-29700369.translation: To our lord Constantine, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-29700369.summary: This is a dedication to Constantine, addressed as 'most noble Caesar', likely from the early 4th century.

EDCS-29700370.translation: To our lord Flavius Julius Constantius, the Younger.
EDCS-29700370.summary: This inscription honors Flavius Julius Constantius, referred to as 'our lord', likely dating to the 4th century.

EDCS-29700371.translation: To the victorious and perpetually triumphant Emperor Augustus, to our lord Constantine, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-29700371.summary: This is a lengthy honorific inscription for Constantine, styling him as victorious and perpetual Augustus, likely from the early 4th century.

EDCS-29700372.translation: To our lord Flavius Julius Constantius, Everlasting Augustus.
EDCS-29700372.summary: This inscription dedicates to Flavius Julius Constantius, styled as 'Everlasting Augustus', probably from the 4th century.

EDCS-29700374.translation: To Bracara, a mile marker at one pace, grandson of the divine Flavius Julius and Valerius Maximianus, great-grandson of our lord the divine Claudius.
EDCS-29700374.summary: This is a mile marker inscription referencing imperial lineage and possibly indicating a road or boundary, dating to the period of Diocletian and his co-emperors (late 3rd/early 4th century).

EDCS-29700376.translation: To our lords Valentinian and Valens.
EDCS-29700376.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the co-emperors Valentinian and Valens, likely from the mid-to-late 4th century.

EDCS-29700377.translation: To our lords Valentinian and Valens, unconquered princes, ever Augusti.
EDCS-29700377.summary: This inscription honors the co-emperors Valentinian and Valens, styling them as unconquered and perpetual Augusti, likely from the mid-to-late 4th century.

EDCS-29700387.translation: Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of the divine Augustus, grandson of the divine Julius, pontifex maximus, imperator for the 8th time, consul for the 5th time, with tribunician power for the 34th year, at Bracaraugusta, one mile.
EDCS-29700387.summary: This is a formal inscription, likely on a milestone, for Tiberius Caesar, detailing his titles and regnal years, dating to his reign (14-37 AD).

EDCS-29700388.translation: To our lord Constantius, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-29700388.summary: This is a dedication to Constantius, addressed as 'most noble Caesar', likely from the early 4th century.

EDCS-29700389.translation: Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 20th year, repaired this at Aquae Flaviae, forty-three miles.
EDCS-29700389.summary: This inscription records a repair made by Emperor Hadrian at Aquae Flaviae, indicating a road maintenance or building project, dating to his reign (117-138 AD).

EDCS-29800007.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius, supreme pontiff, greatest German, greatest Parthian, greatest Arab, holder of tribunician power, father of the country, 12 miles from Asturica.
EDCS-29800007.summary: This inscription likely marks a road, indicating the distance from Asturica to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius.

EDCS-29800014.translation: To Emperor Caesar Galerius Valerius Maximianus Pius Felix Augustus, Licinius Quadratus.
EDCS-29800014.summary: This is a dedication by Licinius Quadratus to Emperor Galerius Valerius Maximianus.

EDCS-29800024.translation: To our lord, the victorious and triumphant, forever Augustus the Great, Magnentius.
EDCS-29800024.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperor Magnentius, praising his victories.

EDCS-29800025.translation: To Emperor Caesar, our lord, the Great Magnentius, victorious, triumphant, forever Augustus.
EDCS-29800025.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperor Magnentius, proclaiming his titles and victories.

EDCS-29800027.translation: To our lord Flavius Julius Constantius, the eternal Augustus.
EDCS-29800027.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Constantius, an eternal Augustus.

EDCS-29800029.summary: This is a dedication to the emperors Valentinian and Valens.

EDCS-29800031.translation: To Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Valerianus Gallienus, the German, unconquered, pious, fortunate Augustus, supreme pontiff, holder of tribunician power, consul, proconsul, father of the country.
EDCS-29800031.summary: This inscription proclaims the titles and honors of Emperor Gallienus.

EDCS-29800040.translation: Forever Augustus, father of the country, L. M. Hilarius, 13 miles.
EDCS-29800040.summary: This inscription likely marks a road, indicating the distance of 13 miles and mentioning a certain Hilarius in relation to the emperor.

EDCS-29800044.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Caro, pious, fortunate Augustus, supreme pontiff, holder of tribunician power, consul, proconsul.
EDCS-29800044.summary: This inscription lists the titles and honors of Emperor Caro.

EDCS-29800047.translation: M. set up an altar to the Lares Viales.
EDCS-29800047.summary: This is a dedication of an altar to the Lares Viales, the gods of the road, by an individual named M.

EDCS-29800052.translation: To our Lord Constantine, Pious, Unconquered Augustus.
EDCS-29800052.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Constantine I, emphasizing his divine favor and military victories.

EDCS-29800057.summary: This is a formulaic epithet praising an emperor, likely part of a longer inscription, highlighting his triumphant nature.

EDCS-29800064.translation: To our Lord Magnentius, Pious, Unconquered, always Augustus, born for the good of the republic.
EDCS-29800064.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Magnentius, praising his piety, invincibility, and his perceived beneficial role in the state.

EDCS-29800087.translation: To the Lares of the Crossroads.
EDCS-29800087.summary: This is a dedication to the household gods associated with travel and boundaries, likely from a public or private shrine.

EDCS-29800088.translation: To the Lares of the Crossroads, he placed [this] in fulfillment of a vow with his own money.
EDCS-29800088.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by an individual to the Lares of the Crossroads, funded by the donor.

EDCS-29800093.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantine, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus.
EDCS-29800093.summary: This is a formal imperial titulature for Emperor Constantine I, listing his adopted names and honorific titles.

EDCS-29800094.summary: This inscription is a dedication to a person named Licinius Licinianus, possibly an official or prominent individual.

EDCS-29800097.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carus, Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power.
EDCS-29800097.summary: This inscription provides the full imperial titles of Emperor Carus, including his religious and governmental powers.

EDCS-29800106.summary: This is a formulaic epithet praising an emperor, likely part of a longer inscription, highlighting his triumphant nature.

EDCS-29800108.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, our Lord Magnentius.
EDCS-29800108.summary: This is an invocation to Emperor Magnentius, acknowledging him as ruler and lord.

EDCS-29800130.translation: Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, Germanicus maximus, Dacicus maximus, Sarmaticus maximus, pontifex, with tribunician power for the 5th time, imperator for the 7th time, father of the fatherland, consul, proconsul, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, Germanicus maximus, Dacicus maximus, Sarmaticus maximus, princeps of youth, son of our lord Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, ordered the roads and bridges, collapsed from age, to be restored, with Quintus Decius, clarissimus vir, legate of the Augusti, pro praetor, overseeing the work.
EDCS-29800130.summary: This inscription records an order by Emperors Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus Caesar for the restoration of roads and bridges, overseen by the legate Quintus Decius, likely dating to their joint rule in the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-29800131.translation: Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, Germanicus maximus, Dacicus maximus, Sarmaticus maximus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 5th time, imperator for the 7th time, father of the fatherland, consul, proconsul, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, Germanicus maximus, Dacicus maximus, Sarmaticus maximus, princeps of youth, son of our lord Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, ordered the roads and bridges, collapsed from age, to be restored, with Quintus Decius, clarissimus vir, legate of the Augusti, pro praetor, overseeing the work, 67 miles from Bracara Augusta.
EDCS-29800131.summary: This inscription records an order by Emperors Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus Caesar for the restoration of roads and bridges, overseen by the legate Quintus Decius, indicating a distance marker of 67 miles from Bracara Augusta, likely dating to their joint rule in the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-29800138.translation: To our lord Magnentius, Pius, Felix, Invictus, always Augustus, and to Imperator Caesar Galerius Valerius Maximianus.
EDCS-29800138.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperors Magnentius and Maximianus, likely dating to the mid-4th century CE.

EDCS-29800142.translation: To Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, Parthicus maximus, Britannicus maximus, Germanicus maximus, son of the deified Severus Pius, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva, with tribunician power for the 17th time, imperator for the 3rd time, consul for the 4th time, father of the fatherland, proconsul, 93 miles.
EDCS-29800142.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla), emphasizing his extensive lineage and titles, and marking a distance of 93 miles, dating to his reign in the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29800153.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Carinus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex, with tribunician power, father of the fatherland, proconsul, consul.
EDCS-29800153.summary: This inscription lists the titles of Emperor Carinus, likely dating to his reign in the late 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29800154.translation: To our lord Flavius Claudius Julianus, always Augustus.
EDCS-29800154.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Julian, likely dating to the mid-4th century CE.

EDCS-29800155.translation: To Imperator Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantius, Invictus Augustus, pontifex maximus, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-29800155.summary: This inscription lists the titles of Emperor Constantius II, likely dating to the mid-4th century CE.

EDCS-29800160.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Magnentius, including a numerical designation, likely dating to the mid-4th century CE.

EDCS-29800168.translation: To Galerius Valerius Maximianus, most noble Caesar, 36 miles.
EDCS-29800168.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Caesar Galerius Valerius Maximianus, indicating a distance of 36 miles, likely dating to the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-29800169.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Magnentius, likely dating to the mid-4th century CE.

EDCS-29900421.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, conqueror of the Germans, conqueror of the Dacians, conqueror of the Sarmatians, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the 5th time, Imperator for the 7th time, Father of the Fatherland. To Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus, most noble Caesar, conqueror of the Germans, conqueror of the Dacians, conqueror of the Sarmatians, Prince of the Youth, son of our Lord Emperor Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus. [He] restored roads and bridges that had collapsed with age, under the supervision of Quintus Decius, clarissimos vir, legate of the Augusti.
EDCS-29900421.summary: This inscription records the restoration of roads and bridges by an imperial legate on behalf of Emperors Maximinus Thrax and his son Caesar Maximinus, likely dating to the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29900423.translation: The Emperor Caesar Hadrianus Traianus Augustus, descendant of the deified Nerva and of the deified Trajan, conqueror of the Dacians, conqueror of the Britons, conqueror of the Germans, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the 4th time, Imperator for the 4th time, Consul for the 4th time, Father of the Fatherland, restored 550 miles from Certima to here at his own expense, in addition to what he remitted to the provinces, nine hundred and ninety thousand denarii owed to himself.
EDCS-29900423.summary: This is a milestone inscription by Emperor Hadrian, detailing road repairs and financial remissions, likely from the second quarter of the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-29900424.translation: The Emperor Caesar Hadrianus Traianus Augustus, son of the deified Nerva, descendant of Nerva, conqueror of the Dacians, conqueror of the Britons, conqueror of the Germans, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the 2nd time, Consul for the 2nd time, Father of the Fatherland, at his own expense restored from Munda and the river Sigila to Certiman and 20 miles further, in addition to what he remitted to the provinces, nine hundred and ninety thousand denarii owed to himself.
EDCS-29900424.summary: This inscription, likely a milestone, records Emperor Hadrian's restoration of a road and his remission of debts, dating to his second consulship in the 120s CE.

EDCS-29900426.translation: The Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajanus Augustus, son of the deified Nerva, conqueror of the Germans, conqueror of the Dacians, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the 13th time, Imperator for the 6th time, Consul for the 5th time, Father of the Fatherland, for the 19th time, paved with stone at his own expense.
EDCS-29900426.summary: This inscription records Emperor Trajan paving a road at his own expense, dating to his 13th tribunician power, likely around 114 CE.

EDCS-30000014.translation: E. Cius, his freedman, had this made at his own expense.
EDCS-30000014.summary: This is a dedication of a monument funded by a freedman named E. Cius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30000108.translation: Us., son of Cio, placed [this monument]. Avitea, aged 57.
EDCS-30000108.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the placement of a monument by Us., son of Cio, for Avitea, who died at the age of 57, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30000109.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Monument for Rufina, daughter of Flao M. Flavius. May he [live long].
EDCS-30000109.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Rufina by her father, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30000111.translation: Cerecius Capito, freedman of Lucius Augustus, aged 38, is buried here. Cerecius [son of?] s...
EDCS-30000111.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Cerecius Capito, a freedman of Lucius Augustus, who died at the age of 38, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30000114.translation: A. B. C. D. E. F. G. H. I. K. L. M. N. O. P. Q. R. S. T. V. Asterios, abbas. Asterius Armentarius.
EDCS-30000114.summary: This inscription lists names and titles, possibly including an abbot and a person named Asterius Armentarius, likely from late antiquity or the early medieval period.

EDCS-30200488.translation: This hall lay buried in long-neglected ruins. Consecrated long ago to the name of blessed Martin, when Christ Jesus had completed 1300 and 20 and twice 2 years, and Karolus reigned in the 15th year, then Count Gauzbertus, son of Sunner, rejoicing, renewed this place. Ermengardis, born of mother Semtembris, died on the day before the Ides. May the Father and the Son, and the Almighty Spirit, forgive his transgressions and grant him blessed rest. Amen.
EDCS-30200488.summary: This is a dedication inscription for the renewal of a church, mentioning Saint Martin, Christ's 1322nd year (implying a date around 816 CE), King Charles (Karolus), Count Gauzbertus, and Ermengardis, likely from the Carolingian era.

EDCS-30200500.summary: This is a funerary or dedicatory inscription naming Fortunate, possibly a freedwoman or slave, with a number that might refer to her age or a group she belonged to.

EDCS-30200508.summary: This inscription likely refers to a person named Pauli and their possession or association with Ulysses, possibly a slave, animal, or even a literary reference.

EDCS-30200511.summary: This is a simple patronymic inscription identifying Hilaronis, son of Eron.

EDCS-30200512.summary: This inscription records the name of Gaius Minicius Lepidus, possibly a tombstone or a dedication.

EDCS-30200515.summary: This inscription identifies Vesvia as the wife of Gnaeus, likely a tombstone or a dedication.

EDCS-30200516.summary: This inscription records the name of Publius Capatius, possibly a tombstone or a dedication.

EDCS-30200518.summary: This fragment appears to be part of a funerary inscription for Martialis, son of Cn...

EDCS-30200533.summary: This inscription indicates ownership or a dedication by Lucius Herennius Optatus, likely on a tombstone or a public monument.

EDCS-30200587.summary: This inscription records the name Gnaeus Lentulus Ris, potentially a tombstone or a dedication.

EDCS-30200598.summary: This inscription records the name Publius Aredius Sextus Mancia, likely a tombstone or a dedication.

EDCS-30200626.summary: This is a funerary monument stating that Minna Ucili made it, likely for a relative or loved one.

EDCS-30200630.translation: Fulvia, freedwoman of a woman, Arbuscula.
EDCS-30200630.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying Fulvia Arbuscula as a freedwoman.

EDCS-30200636.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and filiation of the deceased.

EDCS-31100082.translation: In the time of Xenophon of Dali.
EDCS-31100082.summary: This inscription, in Greek, records a date according to a local magistrate or official.

EDCS-31300163.translation: Dedicated to Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the divine, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 21st time, on his birthday, by Paullus Fabius Maximus, his legate pro praetore, for the Bracaraugustani.
EDCS-31300163.summary: This is a dedication of a sacred object or building to Emperor Augustus, likely by a provincial governor, in Bracara Augusta.

EDCS-31300164.translation: Marcus Magius Antiquus, son of Marcus, of the Galerian tribe, prefect of the cohort of Cilicians, prefect of engineers, [and] Cerialis, praetor, former legate pro praetore, former quaestor, thrice a commissioner of capital crimes, military tribune of the IV Scythica legion, [erected this].
EDCS-31300164.summary: This is a funerary or honorary inscription for Marcus Magius Antiquus, detailing his numerous military and political offices in the Roman army and administration.

EDCS-31400276.translation: Celicus Fronto of Arcobrigenses, Ambimogidus, made this. Celicus Fronto made this for Tongoenabagus.
EDCS-31400276.summary: This inscription records the maker of an object or monument, possibly a tomb, and identifies the person for whom it was made.

EDCS-31600050.translation: Ab, son of Erus Cianus, placed this monument for [his father] after [---] years of age.
EDCS-31600050.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by a son for his father, providing the age of the deceased at death.

EDCS-31600056.translation: Of Aulus Amatus, son of Aulus, freedman.
EDCS-31600056.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying Aulus Amatus as a freedman and providing his filiation.

EDCS-31600062.translation: From the workshop of Caius Vilius.
EDCS-31600062.summary: This inscription indicates the origin or maker of an artifact or monument, from the workshop of Caius Vilius.

EDCS-31600079.translation: Domitius, son of Frontonis, arranged for this to be made for his wife Marulla, aged 46.
EDCS-31600079.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a woman named Marulla, commissioned by her husband Domitius.

EDCS-31600081.summary: This inscription appears to be a very brief dedication or ownership marker for a horse.

EDCS-31600087.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Titus, son of Iulius, and P.P., fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-31600087.summary: A votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by Titus.

EDCS-31600090.translation: Aemilius Hispanus, for his wife, daughter of S., aged 35.
EDCS-31600090.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a woman, wife of Aemilius Hispanus.

EDCS-31600092.translation: To Boutius, son of Matugenus, aged 25.
EDCS-31600092.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young man named Boutius.

EDCS-31600093.translation: To Tritius, son of Corogenus, aged...
EDCS-31600093.summary: This is an incomplete funerary inscription for Tritius.

EDCS-31600094.translation: To Alia Liberta, aged 61, may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-31600094.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Alia Liberta, with a common epitaph wishing her peace.

EDCS-31600095.translation: Sacred to the deified Augustus, Sestius dedicated this.
EDCS-31600095.summary: This is a dedication to the deified Augustus by Sestius.

EDCS-31600096.translation: To IO, son of Cloutus, aged 100.
EDCS-31600096.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a centenarian named IO.

EDCS-31600102.translation: To the Divine Shades, Cebala, granddaughter of Geminius Metellus, from her grandmother.
EDCS-31600102.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cebala, dedicated by her grandmother.

EDCS-31600106.translation: To the Divine Manes of Riburra A.
EDCS-31600106.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Riburra A., likely a tombstone or a dedication on a grave.

EDCS-31600107.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name, parentage, and age of Boutia.

EDCS-31600131.translation: Compedio, daughter of Iaeus, aged 100 years, and Compedio, son of Vacus, aged 50 years, are buried here. Pentus had this made for Compedio.
EDCS-31600131.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Compedio and Compedio, son of Vacus, and states that Pentus commissioned the monument.

EDCS-31600132.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes of Reburrinus REA A ANCORIV.
EDCS-31600132.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Reburrinus.

EDCS-31900360.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a name on a tombstone or marker.

EDCS-31900369.summary: This is a brief, optimistic inscription wishing well to Simplicius, likely on a tomb or memorial.

EDCS-32001017.translation: Here lies Saturnina, who lived for six years and returned in peace. Whoever attempts this monument shall have a share with Judas Iscariot.
EDCS-32001017.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Saturnina, which includes a curse against desecrators, invoking Judas Iscariot.

EDCS-32001018.translation: Precursor of the Lord, martyr, baptizing John. I, King Reccesvinthus, lover of your name, consecrated the seat constructed by my own right in eternal reward, the glorious county of the kingdom 661 years after the era.
EDCS-32001018.summary: This inscription is a dedication by King Reccesvinthus of a seat (likely a church or altar) in honor of John the Baptist, dated to 661 A.E. (around 623 AD).

EDCS-32500199.translation: Licinius Carisianus had this made for his daughter Mia Gracilis, aged 60 years.
EDCS-32500199.summary: This funerary inscription records that Licinius Carisianus commissioned this monument for his daughter Mia Gracilis.

EDCS-32500201.translation: Octavia Materna [had this made] for her most dutiful, deserving husband [filio toni...].
EDCS-32500201.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Octavia Materna commissioned a monument for her husband.

EDCS-32601878.translation: To Lucius Aurunculeius, son of Lucius.
EDCS-32601878.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Lucius Aurunculeius, son of Lucius.

EDCS-32601906.summary: This inscription records a dedication or ownership of a workshop belonging to Felix.

EDCS-32601908.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Lucius Valerius Insequens.

EDCS-32601914.translation: From the workshop of Flavia Demetriana. Flavia Demetriana, her freedmen, and their descendants.
EDCS-32601914.summary: This inscription likely records the ownership or dedication by Flavia Demetriana and her household.

EDCS-32601928.summary: This inscription records a dedication or ownership of a workshop belonging to Marcus.

EDCS-32601951.translation: From the workshop of Lucius Aurunculeius.
EDCS-32601951.summary: This inscription records a dedication or ownership of a workshop belonging to Lucius Aurunculeius.

EDCS-32602085.translation: Corinthus, from the workshop of Flavius Cornelius.
EDCS-32602085.summary: This inscription likely records a dedication by Corinthus, possibly a freedman or associate of Flavius Cornelius.

EDCS-32602086.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Licinius Euangelus.

EDCS-32602109.translation: To Crescens, son of Caius Annius.
EDCS-32602109.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Crescens, son of Caius Annius.

EDCS-32602113.translation: From the workshop of the Cantabri.
EDCS-32602113.summary: This inscription records a dedication or ownership related to a workshop associated with the Cantabri.

EDCS-32602114.summary: This inscription is a simple dedication or epitaph for Creservus, son of Caius Annius.

EDCS-32602118.summary: This inscription likely records the name of Lucius Rasinius, possibly a freedman or a local notable from Pisaurum (modern Pesaro).

EDCS-32602121.summary: This inscription records the name Lucius Er Secundus, potentially as a dedication or an epitaph.

EDCS-32602123.summary: This inscription indicates the workshop belonging to someone named Manlius Ven(tilius?), likely a business or artisan's mark.

EDCS-32602124.summary: This inscription indicates the workshop belonging to Verina, likely a business or artisan's mark.

EDCS-32602143.summary: This inscription records the full name of Quinctus Octavius Ursinus, likely as a dedication or epitaph.

EDCS-32602154.translation: The Seventh Legion Gemina Felix, of Metius Caecilius.
EDCS-32602154.summary: This inscription indicates a dedication or claim by Metius Caecilius on behalf of the Legio VII Gemina Felix.

EDCS-32602216.summary: This inscription appears to be a boundary marker or a record of property ownership related to the areas of Calatia and Formiae, with the abbreviation S. T. possibly indicating a specific designation or owner.

EDCS-32602217.summary: This inscription is likely an epitaph or dedication for Quintus Cornelius Lucius Placidus.

EDCS-32602218.summary: This inscription records the name Irim, son of Marcus, possibly as an epitaph or a dedication.

EDCS-32602222.translation: From the workshop of Donatus Nascoi as
EDCS-32602222.summary: This inscription likely records the origin or ownership of a workshop, possibly related to crafts or trade, with the name Donatus Nascoi as.

EDCS-32602226.summary: This inscription names the workshop of Felix, suggesting a business or craft establishment.

EDCS-32602230.summary: This inscription records the name Lucius Rasinius, identifying him as being from Pisaurum (modern Pesaro).

EDCS-32602231.summary: This inscription records the name Lucius Rasinius, identifying him as being from Pisaurum (modern Pesaro).

EDCS-32602236.summary: This inscription records the name Stephanus, possibly indicating his origin or association with Avillius.

EDCS-32602289.summary: This inscription simply records the name Sepullius Manius, likely an individual identified on a monument or object.

EDCS-32602302.summary: This inscription states that Marcus Antonius Verus created the object or monument, indicating the maker.

EDCS-32602304.summary: This inscription names the workshop of Sextus Arruntius, indicating a business or craft establishment.

EDCS-32602305.summary: This inscription names the workshop of Lucius Aurus, indicating a business or craft establishment.

EDCS-32602307.summary: This inscription states that Caius Carus created the object or monument, indicating the maker.

EDCS-32602308.summary: This inscription records the ownership of Caius Cavius Plocus, likely on a tomb or other private object.

EDCS-32602311.summary: This inscription records the name of Publius Cornelius Autus, likely on a tomb or other private object.

EDCS-32602312.summary: This inscription records the name of Publius Cornelius [---]ustus, likely on a tomb or other private object.

EDCS-32602314.summary: This inscription records the name Crestus, son of Gnaeus Ateius, likely on a tomb or other private object.

EDCS-32602315.summary: This inscription records the name Crestus, son of Caius Annius, likely on a tomb or other private object.

EDCS-32602317.summary: This inscription records the workshop of Lucius [---]us Cornelius, likely on a tomb or other private object.

EDCS-32602318.summary: This inscription records the workshop of Damon, likely on a tomb or other private object.

EDCS-32602321.summary: This inscription records the name Epaphra, son of Sextus Annius, likely on a tomb or other private object.

EDCS-32602323.summary: This inscription records the workshop of Felix, likely on a tomb or other private object.

EDCS-32602328.summary: This inscription records the workshop of Gnaeus Gelius, likely on a tomb or other private object.

EDCS-32602332.summary: This inscription records the name of a workshop belonging to Labio, likely a craftsman or business owner.

EDCS-32602333.summary: This inscription records the name of a workshop belonging to Labio, likely a craftsman or business owner.

EDCS-32602334.summary: This inscription records the name of a workshop belonging to Labio, likely a craftsman or business owner.

EDCS-32602335.summary: This inscription records the name of a workshop belonging to Labio, likely a craftsman or business owner.

EDCS-32602347.summary: This inscription records the name of Phileros, son of Caius Tettius, possibly indicating ownership or a dedication.

EDCS-32602351.summary: This inscription records the name of Plotius, son of Caius Sentius, possibly indicating ownership or a dedication.

EDCS-32602357.summary: This inscription records the name of Princeps, son of Lucius Gellius, possibly indicating ownership or a dedication.

EDCS-32602361.summary: This inscription records the name of a workshop belonging to Quarti, likely a craftsman or business owner.

EDCS-32602364.summary: This inscription records the full name of Lucius Rasinius Draucus, likely an individual of some standing.

EDCS-32602365.summary: This inscription records the name of Romanus, son of Lucius Vibius, possibly indicating ownership or a dedication.

EDCS-32602366.summary: This is a genitive inscription, likely naming the owner or dedicator of an object or monument.

EDCS-32602377.summary: This is a genitive inscription, likely naming the owner or dedicator of an object or monument.

EDCS-32602378.summary: This is a genitive inscription, likely naming the owner or dedicator of an object or monument.

EDCS-32602401.summary: This is a genitive inscription, likely naming the owner or dedicator of an object or monument.

EDCS-32602402.translation: Of Communis, son of Gaius Sentius
EDCS-32602402.summary: This is a genitive inscription, likely naming the owner or dedicator of an object or monument.

EDCS-32602407.summary: This inscription indicates ownership or the name of a workshop, likely related to trade or craft.

EDCS-32602409.translation: From the iron-working shop of Marcus Raius Rufus
EDCS-32602409.summary: This inscription names the owner of an iron-working shop, indicating a commercial establishment.

EDCS-32602410.translation: Of Lucius Notavius, freedman of Eunuchus
EDCS-32602410.summary: This inscription names a freedman and his former master, indicating social status and patronage.

EDCS-32602413.summary: This inscription indicates ownership or the name of a workshop, likely related to trade or craft.

EDCS-32602415.summary: This is a genitive inscription, likely naming the owner or dedicator of an object or monument.

EDCS-32602416.translation: To Gnaeus Ateius, son of Marcus.
EDCS-32602416.summary: This is a dedication by Gnaeus Ateius, son of Marcus.

EDCS-32602417.translation: To Chrestio, son of Aulus Titus.
EDCS-32602417.summary: This is a dedication to Chrestio, son of Aulus Titus.

EDCS-32602419.summary: This is a dedication to Epaphroditus, son of Tettius.

EDCS-32602422.translation: To Ephebus, son of Caius Annius.
EDCS-32602422.summary: This is a dedication to Ephebus, son of Caius Annius.

EDCS-32602427.summary: This inscription names Gaius Julius Priscus.

EDCS-32602428.summary: This inscription names Lucius Iullius Aulus Piso.

EDCS-32602433.summary: This inscription records the workshop of Maternus.

EDCS-32602435.summary: This inscription records the workshop of Modestus.

EDCS-32602443.summary: This inscription refers to Lucius Rasinius, who is from Pisaurensis.

EDCS-32602444.summary: This inscription refers to Lucius Rasinius, who is from Pisaurensis.

EDCS-32602453.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription naming Aulus Titus Ploutus, likely recording ownership or a dedication.

EDCS-32602454.summary: This inscription records that Gaius Vibius Femus was the maker of the object or monument.

EDCS-32602455.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription naming Gaius Vivienus Faustus, likely recording ownership or a dedication.

EDCS-32602456.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription naming Gnaeus Cornelius Victor, likely recording ownership or a dedication.

EDCS-32800172.translation: From the upper workshops of Marcus Julius Fortunatus.
EDCS-32800172.summary: This inscription indicates that the object comes from the workshops of Marcus Julius Fortunatus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-33000040.translation: Valeria, daughter of Gaius, Paetina, gave and dedicated [this].
EDCS-33000040.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Valeria Paetina, daughter of Gaius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-33000046.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Calpurnius Reburrinus, a cavalryman of the II Flaviae ala. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-33000046.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Calpurnius Reburrinus, a cavalryman of the II Flaviae ala, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-33100027.translation: To Diocletian Caesar Augustus, the adopted one, and to Galerius in the East, the superstition of Christ having been abolished everywhere and the worship of the gods propagated.
EDCS-33100027.summary: This inscription, likely from the late 3rd or early 4th century CE, commemorates the suppression of Christianity and the promotion of traditional Roman religion under Diocletian and Galerius.

EDCS-33700004.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, invested with tribunician power, hailed as emperor, consul. The republic of Tutugiensis is devoted to his divine majesty.
EDCS-33700004.summary: This inscription, dating to the reign of Gordian III (238-244 CE), records the devotion of the community of Tutugiensis to the emperor.

EDCS-33700070.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Camalus, the dutiful son of Corunis, had this made for his father, Corunis, son of Medamus, a Cantian.
EDCS-33700070.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Corunis by his son Camalus, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-33700076.translation: Lucius Pompeius Paternus willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Arcon.
EDCS-33700076.summary: A votive inscription recording Lucius Pompeius Paternus fulfilling a vow to the deity Arcon.

EDCS-33700086.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Aemilia Blandina of Arcobrigia. Aemilia Marcella [dedicated this] to her mother. Q.
EDCS-33700086.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Aemilia Blandina by her mother, Aemilia Marcella, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-33700087.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sergius Maternus had this made for his mother, Pet[---].
EDCS-33700087.summary: A funerary inscription stating that Sergius Maternus commissioned this monument for his mother.

EDCS-33800148.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Vespasianus of Illiberis, in honor of the war in Jerusalem, delight of the human race. May the earth rest lightly upon you, Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius.
EDCS-33800148.summary: A funerary inscription honoring Vespasianus, linking him to the Jewish War and addressing Emperor Marcus Aurelius.

EDCS-34200029.translation: To my mother, Acuia Navis, daughter of [---], and to Fuscus.
EDCS-34200029.summary: An inscription dedicating something to Acuia Navis's mother and Fuscus.

EDCS-34200030.translation: Mother had this made for her dearest sons, Vinius, son of Cassius, and Ambatus.
EDCS-34200030.summary: A funerary inscription where a mother commissioned a monument for her two sons, Vinius and Ambatus.

EDCS-34200038.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valerius Silius had this made. [---] of Aunus, a man of consular rank, was the deceased; his remains were at [---].
EDCS-34200038.summary: A funerary inscription for Valerius Silius, possibly mentioning a man of consular rank and concerning the placement of remains.

EDCS-34200044.summary: A very fragmentary inscription mentioning the name Manlius, likely personal or dedicatory.

EDCS-34200045.translation: To Mars the Lord. [---] M I willingly paid [his] vow.
EDCS-34200045.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to the god Mars.

EDCS-34200050.translation: To Ine Maine, a sister of Camdus.
EDCS-34200050.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Ine Maine, likely by her brother.

EDCS-34300008.translation: Monument for Mebdi, son of Vago, aged 60.
EDCS-34300008.summary: This is a funerary monument for Mebdi, son of Vago, specifying his age.

EDCS-34300009.translation: For Domestica Flaccila, daughter of Vironus, aged 4. For Vironus Flaccus, son of Vironus, aged...
EDCS-34300009.summary: This inscription records the ages of two individuals, Domestica Flaccila and Vironus Flaccus, likely within a funerary context.

EDCS-34300454.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Attius, mentioning his age.

EDCS-34300455.translation: Tertius set up this monument for his companion, aged 18.
EDCS-34300455.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Tertius for his companion, specifying the companion's age.

EDCS-34300457.translation: Tertius set up this monument for Melia, his companion, aged 35.
EDCS-34300457.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Tertius for his companion Melia, specifying her age.

EDCS-34300530.translation: Calpurnia Materna, a dutiful [daughter], set up an altar for Turenia, aged 50.
EDCS-34300530.summary: This is a dedication of an altar by Calpurnia Materna to Turenia, likely a family member, specifying Turenia's age.

EDCS-34300637.translation: P. Flavius, son of [---], dedicated [this] to the household gods in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-34300637.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Lares (household gods) by P. Flavius in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-34300638.translation: Vitulinus, son of Vitulus, aged 10, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-34300638.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vitulinus, son of Vitulus, stating his age and including a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-34300651.summary: This is a formulaic phrase indicating the completion of a dedication, often found on religious or public monuments.

EDCS-34300653.summary: This inscription records the name of Quintus Sertorius Pietas, likely a funerary or honorary dedication.

EDCS-34300658.translation: To our lord the Emperor Caesar, Maximinus Magnentius
EDCS-34300658.summary: This is an inscription dedicating something to the Emperor Magnentius, likely from the period of his rule (mid-4th century CE).

EDCS-34300672.summary: This inscription records the names Iunius, Officina, and Sem, possibly a funerary dedication.

EDCS-34300674.summary: This inscription contains the names Coely, CRESVS, and PVB, possibly part of a funerary or honorary dedication.

EDCS-34300703.translation: To our lord the Emperor Flavius Magnentius, most noble and strongest Caesar
EDCS-34300703.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Magnentius, praising him and dating to his reign (mid-4th century CE).

EDCS-34300740.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Protector, Gallio Senior, from a vow.
EDCS-34300740.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter by Gallio Senior, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34300743.summary: This inscription records Avilius Reburrus as a member of a collegium (guild), likely a funerary or honorary marker.

EDCS-34300787.translation: With you, goddess, guiding me, may my life be blessed so that I may deserve to dwell in your holy places. I take hope that what I have done will be accepted.
