This temple has been consecrated by seven bishops: Rudesind of Astorga, Nausti of Coimbra, Sisnando of Tuy, Ranulf of Oviedo, Argimiro of Lamecense, Recaredo of Lugo, and Ellecan of Zaragoza, in the year 930, on the 16th day before the Kalends of October.
EDCS-38700153.summary: This inscription describes the consecration of a temple by seven bishops in 930, invoking God's mercy and forgiveness.

EDCS-38700154.translation: Here are the relics of Saint Mary, of her milk, of Saint Paul, of Saint Vincent, and others, and there are 18 relics in honor of Saint Mary. Suarius made me.
EDCS-38700154.summary: This inscription lists several relics housed within a structure and attributes its creation to Suarius.

EDCS-38700156.translation: In the name of the Lord Jesus Christ, for whose love Queen Velasquita, daughter of Ramiro, will build the temple of the Lord Saint Savior, and relics are hidden here, so that she may receive worthy rewards in the bosom of holiness, and may live here happily and possess the kingdom of God, amen. This temple has been consecrated.
EDCS-38700156.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a temple to Saint Savior by Queen Velasquita, daughter of Ramiro, for the purpose of housing relics and ensuring her eternal reward.

EDCS-38700157.translation: Here lies the servant of God Bonellus, abbot, on the 15th day before the Kalends of December in the year 914.
EDCS-38700157.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Bonellus, abbot, in 914.

EDCS-38700158.translation: In the name of the Lord, this temple has been consecrated by Lord Gudesteus, bishop, by the order of Lord Veremund, prince, son of Ordoño, on the Nones of February, 31 years after a thousand. There are relics hidden there, that is, of L.
EDCS-38700158.summary: This inscription records the consecration of a temple by Bishop Gudesteus, under the authority of Prince Veremund, son of Ordoño, in the year 1031, and notes the presence of relics.

EDCS-38700160.translation: Didacus Ovetense, bishop of the see, consecrated this church in the name of the Lord in the year 1056 of the era, on the 9th day before the Ides of February. Here are preserved the relics of the wood of the Lord, of Saint Saturninus the bishop, and of Saint John the Baptist.
EDCS-38700160.summary: This inscription records the consecration of a church by Bishop Didacus in 1056 of the era, mentioning the relics of the Lord's wood and two saints.

EDCS-38700162.translation: For the honor of Saint Saviour, Sanctia Gundisalvi made me.
EDCS-38700162.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Saint Saviour made by Sanctia Gundisalvi.

EDCS-38700163.translation: In the year 999 of the era, the church of Saint Saviour, Saint James, and Saint John was consecrated by Pelagius the priest on the 24th day before the Kalends of October, in the year 1003 of the era.
EDCS-38700163.summary: This inscription records the consecration of a church dedicated to three saints in the year 999 of the era by Pelagius the priest.

EDCS-38700165.translation: In the year 996 of the era, the road was made, during the reign of Froila with his wife Urraca.
EDCS-38700165.summary: This inscription records the construction of a road in the year 996 of the era, during the reign of Froila and Urraca.

EDCS-38700166.translation: This place, long ago dedicated to the honor of Saint Martin, was instructed with brief work and remained for a long time, ruined, until Abbot Ihoannes from Corduba came and consecrated this church; he raised the ruin of the building from the foundations and carved stone slabs, not by imperial order but by the vigilance of the brothers; these works were completed in two or three months, with Ordinius performing the tasks, in the year 919 of the era.
EDCS-38700166.summary: This inscription details the rebuilding of a church dedicated to Saint Martin by Abbot Ihoannes in the year 919 of the era, highlighting the work of the brothers.

EDCS-38700167.translation: In good season, this church was founded here by me, Eldoncia, servant of God, after the death of her husband, 15 years after the era; she ordered it to be made for the remedy of my soul, with a cost of 6000 [units]. The architect was appointed; this house of the Lord Saint Michael the Archangel was made from my gifts bought here. I pray you, priests, if you [receive?] some favor for me, Eldoncia, remember me, and the Lord be with you. Amen.
EDCS-38700167.summary: This inscription records the founding of a church dedicated to Saint Michael the Archangel by Eldoncia in the year [10]15 of the era, as a remedy for her soul.

EDCS-38700168.translation: In the name of Momus, living in the flesh, he made it in the year 931 of the era. Here sleeps. Died Emil Cilbateria on the 10th day of peace, Augustus. Died Gegahem.
EDCS-38700168.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Momus in the year 931 of the era and notes the deaths of Emil Cilbateria and Gegahem.

EDCS-38700172.summary: This inscription likely refers to Bishop Dastlae and a numerical value, possibly a date or quantity, from an unknown era.

EDCS-38700174.translation: This sacred cross was made for the honor of Saint Stephen, the deacon and protomartyr, and it is in memory of Lord Garseanus the prince. I, Sancho, king, son [of Garsea?], together with my wife Urraca the queen, ordered it to be made. Therefore, we implore all of you who read this not to be reluctant to pray for his soul and ours, so that by your aided intercessions we may have the kingdom of heaven along with you, in the year 1006 of the era.
EDCS-38700174.summary: This inscription records the creation of a cross in honor of Saint Stephen and in memory of Prince Garseanus, commissioned by King Sancho and Queen Urraca in the year 1006 of the era.

EDCS-38700175.translation: In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit, this church was built in the year 907 of the era, and here are the relics of Saint John the Apostle, and Saint James, the brother of the Lord, and Saint Peter. And whoever comes to this place and venerates these relics or wishes to ask the Lord for the soul of Dominicus Belazanus, so that forgiveness may be granted to them and their ancestors Oferisia and Dixilana, the Lord [grants it]. Amen.
EDCS-38700175.summary: This inscription records the construction of a church in the year 907 of the era, containing relics of Saints John, James, and Peter, and seeking forgiveness for the soul of Dominicus Belazanus and his ancestors.

EDCS-38700176.translation: Here rests Uvitiza, son of Teodefred. May God forgive him. Amen. Era 944 since the Incarnation of the Lord, year 900, in the 2nd year of the reign of King Charles, on the 13th day before the Kalends of April. Thus he died.
EDCS-38700176.summary: This inscription is a tombstone for Uvitiza, son of Teodefred, providing his death date within the context of King Charles' reign and an era dating system.

EDCS-38700177.translation: Under this tomb rests the body of Wifred, formerly count, son of Wifred, likewise formerly count, of good memory. May the Lord grant him peace, Amen. Who died on the 26th of April, in the era 952, in the year 1114 of the Lord, in the 13th year of the reign of King Charles, after Odo.
EDCS-38700177.summary: This inscription is a tombstone for Wifred, a count, indicating his death date and reign periods in the early 12th century.

EDCS-38700317.translation: To God the Lord, Nemedecus, Severus, son of Saturninus, placed this in fulfillment of a vow; Homullus and Caturo [also dedicated it].
EDCS-38700317.summary: This is a dedication to the deity Nemedecus by Severus, son of Saturninus, with Homullus and Caturo also mentioned as dedicators.

EDCS-38800080.translation: From the workshop of Cantius. May you use it happily, Senti.
EDCS-38800080.summary: This inscription is likely a maker's mark or a sales tag from the workshop of Cantius, intended for the buyer Senti.

EDCS-38800081.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or memorial to parents named Dido and Seianus.

EDCS-38800083.summary: This is a simple ownership or attribution inscription for Marcus Flaccus.

EDCS-38800294.summary: This inscription likely indicates ownership or refers to Gaius Oppius Restitutus.

EDCS-38800310.translation: Of Gaius, son of Gaius, of the Menenia tribe.
EDCS-38800310.summary: This inscription records the name and tribal affiliation of Gaius, son of Gaius.

EDCS-38800311.translation: Of Gaius Pontilienus, son of Marcus.
EDCS-38800311.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of Gaius Pontilienus, son of Marcus.

EDCS-38800312.translation: Of Publius Turullius, son of Marcus.
EDCS-38800312.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of Publius Turullius, son of Marcus.

EDCS-38800314.summary: This is a dedication to Tito Callonius Vintus, likely on a tombstone, from the Roman period.

EDCS-38800317.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius T. Celer Faventinus, likely on a tombstone, from the Roman period.

EDCS-38800320.translation: Numerius Trebius Corus, son of Numerius Trebius Corus.
EDCS-38800320.summary: This inscription names Numerius Trebius Corus, possibly identifying a father and son or indicating lineage, from the Roman period.

EDCS-38800321.summary: This inscription identifies Gnaeus Carvicus Marius and his origin from Latium, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-38800336.translation: To Aemilius Reburrus, my father, aged 80 years. To Aemilius Bassus, aged 20 years. Aemilius Reburrus, aged 18 years. Aemilius Flaccus, to his father, for his merits.
EDCS-38800336.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary monument honoring multiple members of the Aemilius family, including a father and his sons, with their ages, from the Roman period.

EDCS-38800338.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Flavinus erected this altar to his son Flavus, aged more or less [---].
EDCS-38800338.summary: This is a funerary dedication of an altar by Flavinus to his son Flavus, indicating a burial from the Roman period.

EDCS-38900219.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely refers to a period of time or a ritual, possibly funerary, from the Roman period.

EDCS-38900221.translation: To the honorable lord, our brother Montanus, and [---] and with good will, freely from power, and other things which we know have been done against our action, he brings forth before the laws, with mention of the tax and expedition, on the eighth day of the king Riccarid, by sign of hand.
EDCS-38900221.summary: This is a complex legal or administrative document, possibly a grant or agreement, mentioning a king Riccarid and a specific date, from a late Roman or post-Roman period.

EDCS-38900233.summary: This is a short, optimistic inscription, possibly a dedication or a personal note, from the Roman period.

EDCS-38900234.translation: Here lies, fortunate, the son of Grycos, son of Sambatius.
EDCS-38900234.summary: This Greek inscription is a funerary epitaph identifying the deceased as the son of Grycos and grandson of Sambatius, from the Roman period.

EDCS-38900244.translation: May he who receives healing here rejoice, and may the inner atrium of the people not be yielded for use, nor may a public woman ever enter there unwillingly. Let no one here unwillingly settle business; where the tender bloom of the monastery departs. But I, Ermefredus, bishop in the city of Lucen, dispensing the laws of the priesthood to the populace, confirming such vows by edicts for ages, have restored what had fallen and completed what had been begun well, and you alone, Christ, protect the bounds of this flock, lest any harmful plague disturb the open sheepfold. Therefore, may the monastic dignity shine here through the ages; may the holy rule of the fathers conquer souls.
EDCS-38900244.summary: This inscription, likely from a monastic context and attributed to Bishop Ermefredus, concerns the dedication and protection of a monastic space, emphasizing its purity and the divine rule governing it.

EDCS-38900245.translation: Suniefredus, Daniel, Alexander, Mana, Sala, Teudisclus (deceased), Bernardus the Levite, Miro, Chintila, Bernardus, Wadamirus, Sabidius, Teudefred, Oriolus, Suniarius, Volballid, Auselius the Presbyter, Ranovigius, Placidius the Presbyter, Baro, Audevagrus the Presbyter, Daniel the layman, Salamirus the Presbyter, Vciricys, Fruila, Manius the Presbyter, Undila, Ramius, Ramiusaldus, Bernus, Teudulfus the Presbyter, Adaulfo, Salamon, Aruidrius, Dexter the Presbyter, Argemirus the Presbyter, Audlmiro, Ermemirus, Gontalus the Presbyter, Eldebon the senior, Trasovadus, Preciosa, Froila the Levite, EIdalecus the Presbyter, Suisaricus, Raminus, Aigo the Presbyter, Dexter the Presbyter, Gamio, Lautardus, Solmo, Wimara the Levite, Gaugo the Levite, Agila, Altemiro, Sallomon the Presbyter, Gerimundus, Arvibius, Vudisclus, Gamis. I have subscribed. Argimiro, Blandricius, Palumba, Guifre.
EDCS-38900245.summary: This is a list of names, likely witnesses or participants in an event, possibly related to a church or religious community, given the presence of priestly and levitical titles.

EDCS-38900249.translation: Protheus made this for Thuresmunda, his wife. She died on the ninth day before the Kalends of January in the year 672 of the era.
EDCS-38900249.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Thuresmunda, wife of Protheus, with a specific date in the era of 672.

EDCS-38900251.translation: In the name of the Lord. This place, Anduiraes, (was built by) a noble woman who, with her husband Anduir, made this noble church. They were participants for 35 years; they had raised children four times. The aforesaid matron departed in peace and in the utmost chastity, with certainty.
EDCS-38900251.summary: This inscription records the founding of a church by a noble couple, Anduiraes and Anduir, who were involved for 35 years and raised children.

EDCS-38900254.translation: This humble offering the humble lady Flammola offers as a vow to God, the Sun, the Moon.
EDCS-38900254.summary: This is a votive inscription by a lady named Flammola, offering a humble gift to God, the Sun, and the Moon.

EDCS-38900264.summary: This inscription simply records the names Fredenandus and Gutina, likely indicating joint ownership or commemoration.

EDCS-38901361.translation: Amantia departed in peace, who lived 10 years and 25 days.
EDCS-38901361.summary: This is a short epitaph for Amantia, stating her peaceful death and her age at death (10 years and 25 days).

EDCS-38901365.translation: Honoria, aged 3 years, lived in peace and was placed here on the eighth day before the Kalends of April, in the fourth indiction.
EDCS-38901365.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Honoria, who died at the age of 3, with a specific date and indiction.

EDCS-38901366.summary: This is a very brief epitaph marking the resting place of a person named Marc.

EDCS-39000004.translation: In this place rests Ordonius, confessor of Christ, in the era
EDCS-39000004.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Ordonius, a confessor of Christ, with a date indicated by 'aera'.

EDCS-39000006.translation: Sabbas, priest of the holy Roman church, rests here, he was placed in peace on the I Ides of April in the year 354 of the era, in the first indiction, after the consulship of the most distinguished man Anastasius.
EDCS-39000006.summary: This epitaph records the burial of the priest Sabbas in the Roman church, dated to the Ides of April in the year 354 of the era.

EDCS-39000007.translation: January and November: 1st hour and 11th hour, 28 feet; 2nd hour and 10th hour, 18 feet; 3rd hour and 9th hour, 14 feet; 4th hour and 8th hour, 11 feet; 5th hour and 7th hour, 9 feet; 6th hour and 6th hour, 8 feet. February and March.
EDCS-39000007.summary: This inscription seems to record a schedule or measurement related to hours and feet, potentially for astronomical or architectural purposes, possibly related to the months of January, February, November, and March.

EDCS-39200433.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Severus Pius, grandson of the deified Marcus Aurelius, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus, descendant of the deified Hadrian, descendant of the deified Trajan Parthicus, descendant of the deified Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus.
EDCS-39200433.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, listing his imperial lineage and honorary titles.

EDCS-39300111.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a name, possibly a funerary or ownership marker.

EDCS-39300128.summary: This inscription records the name and title 'proconsul' belonging to Quintus Sertorius.

EDCS-39300129.summary: This inscription records the name and title 'proconsul' belonging to Quintus Sertorius.

EDCS-39300130.summary: This inscription records the name and title 'proconsul' belonging to Quintus Sertorius.

EDCS-39300131.summary: This inscription records the name and title 'proconsul' belonging to Quintus Sertorius, possibly a dedication or a label.

EDCS-39300132.translation: The Piety of Quintus Sertorius, proconsul.
EDCS-39300132.summary: This inscription likely records an epithet or a dedication related to the proconsul Quintus Sertorius, highlighting his piety.

EDCS-39300133.translation: The Faith of Quintus Sertorius, proconsul.
EDCS-39300133.summary: This inscription likely records an epithet or a dedication related to the proconsul Quintus Sertorius, highlighting his faithfulness.

EDCS-39300134.translation: The Truth of Quintus Sertorius, proconsul.
EDCS-39300134.summary: This inscription likely records an epithet or a dedication related to the proconsul Quintus Sertorius, highlighting his truthfulness.

EDCS-39300181.translation: Of Gaius Aquinius, son of Marcus.
EDCS-39300181.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely recording the name of a Roman citizen, Gaius Aquinius, son of Marcus.

EDCS-39300183.translation: Of Lucius Carulus, son of Lucius, of Hispalis, of the Menenia tribe.
EDCS-39300183.summary: This inscription records the name and tribal affiliation of Lucius Carulus, son of Lucius, from Hispalis.

EDCS-39300184.summary: This inscription records the name of Marcus Dirius Malchio, possibly an owner or dedicator.

EDCS-39300186.translation: Of Gaius Pontilienus, son of Marcus, of the Fabia tribe.
EDCS-39300186.summary: This inscription records the name and tribal affiliation of Gaius Pontilienus, son of Marcus.

EDCS-39300187.summary: This inscription records the name of Quintus Varius Hiberus, possibly an owner or dedicator.

EDCS-39300188.translation: Of Gaius Inguleius, son of Gaius.
EDCS-39300188.summary: This inscription records the name of Gaius Inguleius, son of Gaius.

EDCS-39300189.translation: Of Publius Nonaeus, son of Publius, of Nuceria.
EDCS-39300189.summary: This inscription records the name and origin of Publius Nonaeus, son of Publius, from Nuceria.

EDCS-39300190.summary: This is likely an inscription dedicating something to Caius Noni Asprenatis, a prominent Roman figure, possibly from the 1st century CE.

EDCS-39300191.translation: To the Lucretii, of the Partnership.
EDCS-39300191.summary: This inscription refers to the Lucretii, likely a family or group associated with a business partnership, possibly dating to the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-39300192.translation: To Lucius Planius, son of Lucius.
EDCS-39300192.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Planius, son of Lucius, likely a private individual commemorated on a monument.

EDCS-39300193.translation: To Lucius Planius Russinus, son of Lucius.
EDCS-39300193.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Lucius Planius Russinus, son of Lucius, probably a citizen from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-39300194.translation: To Lucius Planius Russinus, son of Lucius.
EDCS-39300194.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Lucius Planius Russinus, son of Lucius, probably a citizen from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-39300195.translation: To Caius Fidus, son of Caius, and Spurius Lucretius, son of Spurius.
EDCS-39300195.summary: This inscription jointly commemorates Caius Fidus and Spurius Lucretius, likely individuals of some standing, possibly from the Republican era.

EDCS-39501471.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Lucius Porcius. [---] ICN RI.
EDCS-39501471.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Porcius, possibly from the 1st or 2nd century CE, with some illegible or abbreviated text.

EDCS-39700010.translation: Marcus Carbo, on the tenth day before the Kalends of January.
EDCS-39700010.summary: This inscription records the name Marcus Carbo and a specific date, December 23rd, likely indicating an event or a person's death.

EDCS-39700012.translation: On the second day before the Kalends of May, Cornutus Cordons, Gaius Atilius.
EDCS-39700012.summary: This inscription records names and a date, likely related to a personal or official record.

EDCS-39700033.summary: This is a simple ownership mark indicating 'Quartus' workshop'.

EDCS-39700037.translation: Flavinus Antiochus, Flavus Torgalinus, Lucius Antonius, Fullonius Quintin, Frontinus Fronto, Mussora, Quintus Quitinius, Lucianus, Septumus Pambanus, Pontius, Flavianus Duavus and his son set up this offering with their produce; Sempronius Ursinianus, Gemelus, Beduna, Maritumus, Maritumus, Fluvinus, Antonius, Capito, Calpurnius, Calpurnius, Sextus.
EDCS-39700037.summary: This lengthy inscription lists numerous individuals who jointly dedicated an offering, possibly related to a communal religious act or a cooperative enterprise.

EDCS-39700041.summary: This inscription simply records two personal names, Paternus and Curius.

EDCS-39700042.summary: This inscription simply records two personal names, Maternus and Capito.

EDCS-39700065.translation: Elugius, son of Horsi, of Arimiro.
EDCS-39700065.summary: This inscription provides a personal name, patronymic, and place of origin.

EDCS-39700066.translation: Marcus' freedwoman, Posilla, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-39700066.summary: This inscription records the name of a freedwoman, Posilla, and implies a dedication or acknowledgment.

EDCS-39700069.translation: To the god Silvanus, Senpronius gladly fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-39700069.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Silvanus by Senpronius.

EDCS-39700099.translation: The body rests, the spirit rejoices, which rejoices in Christ; lived for more or less 60 years.
EDCS-39700099.summary: This funerary inscription records the death and the duration of life of an individual, with a Christian context.

EDCS-39700106.translation: Of Quintus, son of C.C., from Tolosa.
EDCS-39700106.summary: This inscription indicates ownership or relation to Quintus, son of C.C., from Tolosa.

EDCS-39700112.translation: Pia Clodia rests here with her family.
EDCS-39700112.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Clodia, stating she is buried with her family, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-39700115.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a name, possibly on a tombstone or dedication, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-39700125.translation: Fortunata, his wife, had this made for [him]; [he was] 40 years old.
EDCS-39700125.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an unnamed man, commissioned by his wife Fortunata, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-39700136.translation: For Victor, son of Herm[es], Herm[es] made [this] by his own decree.
EDCS-39700136.summary: This is a dedication, likely to a deity or a deceased person, with the dedicant being the son of Hermes, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-39700137.translation: Lucius Valerius of Aobriga and Marcus Portius Marbasensis, twice supervisors of the waters, legally erected this building for the baths.
EDCS-39700137.summary: This is a building inscription by two officials, Lucius Valerius and Marcus Portius, concerning the construction of baths, likely from the late Roman Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-39700138.translation: For the Lares, Valerius Flaccus, son of Lucius, and Secundus, son of Lucius, erected this temple building.
EDCS-39700138.summary: This is a dedication of a temple building to the household gods (Lares) by two individuals, Valerius Flaccus and Secundus, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-39700139.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carus Augustus Invictus, Pontifex Maximus, Tribune of the Plebs for the [nth] time, Father of the Fatherland, to the province of Mucambetum.
EDCS-39700139.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Carus, listing his titles and dedicating something to the province of Mucambetum, dating to his reign (282-283 AD).

EDCS-39700140.translation: In Rome, Septimius Severus Rufius, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-39700140.summary: This is a votive dedication by Septimius Severus Rufius in Rome, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-39700141.translation: To Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Trajan, Trajan Hadrianus, Pontifex Maximus, Tribune of the Plebs for the 4th time, Consul for the 4th time, Father of the Fatherland, from M. Aobrigenis, by his own decree.
EDCS-39700141.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Hadrian, listing his titles and indicating it was made by M. Aobrigenis, dating to his reign (117-138 AD).

EDCS-39700144.translation: Torolo Combiciego fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-39700144.summary: This is a votive inscription by Torolo Combiciego, stating he fulfilled a vow, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-39700145.translation: To the Lares of Aldumia, Marbasens was a servant of Ilivs, son of Lucius, of the tribe Quirina. He gladly and deservedly set this up.
EDCS-39700145.summary: This is a dedication to household gods (Lares) by a freedman named Marbasens.

EDCS-39700146.translation: Valerius Samos Altiranus, son of Dnest, set this up willingly for his father, with the help of his freedmen.
EDCS-39700146.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Valerius Samos for his father.

EDCS-39700152.translation: Gustuna, wife of Oav, lived 72 years. Titus and Maternus, her sons, had this made.
EDCS-39700152.summary: This is a funerary monument for Gustuna, erected by her sons Titus and Maternus.

EDCS-39700153.translation: To our lord Flavius Valerius Constantinus, the most noble and brave Caesar, son of the divine Constantius the Pious.
EDCS-39700153.summary: This is an honorific inscription for Emperor Constantine.

EDCS-39700156.translation: Attetus Saicius, of the tribe Quirina, aged [---].
EDCS-39700156.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely records the name and possibly age of a person named Attetus Saicius.

EDCS-39800145.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely refers to a person named Lucius Porcius Valerianus.

EDCS-41300132.translation: The shipowners who do business in Cantabria and at the port of Juliobriga set this up, devoted to his divine numen and majesty, for the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, son of the divine Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius, grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-grandson of the divine Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus and the divine Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, holding tribunician power for the 7th time, consul for the 1st time, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-41300132.summary: This is a dedication set up by shipowners for Emperor Septimius Severus, likely during his first consulship and seventh tribunician power.

EDCS-41700135.translation: Minicia, daughter of Lucius. Satulla, freedwoman of Minicia. Lucius, freedman of Minicia. Cassia Rustica, daughter of Titus Cassius Niger, daughter of Lucius Minicius. Satula, freedwoman of Minicia.
EDCS-41700135.summary: This inscription lists several individuals, likely family members and freedpersons, associated with the Minicia family.

EDCS-41700172.translation: Born of the Roman people, of the Terentia family. Alas, he perished, struck down by a heinous contagion from heaven. His cousin, the most devoted P., son of M. mart., ordered him to be placed in this tomb. He lived 38 years, 4 months, and 10 days.
EDCS-41700172.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a man of the Terentia family who died young, likely during the Antonine Plague.

EDCS-41700173.translation: Camilius Aqileius ordered this to be inscribed while he was alive. 'Thus I was not; then I was; now I am not.'
EDCS-41700173.summary: This is a tomb inscription commissioned by Camilius Aqileius for himself during his lifetime, expressing a philosophical reflection on existence.

EDCS-41700175.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Tuscus, son of Castus, a centurion, aged 30, is buried here. Iulia Felis, his sister, had this set up.
EDCS-41700175.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tuscus, a centurion, set up by his sister, likely dated to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-41700219.translation: Iulia Paulina, daughter of Ortus, willingly and deservedly paid her vow to the Nymphs.
EDCS-41700219.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to the Nymphs by Iulia Paulina, likely dated to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-41700263.translation: Lucius Pompeius Quintus Saturinus is buried here, aged 50.
EDCS-41700263.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Pompeius Quintus Saturinus, dated to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-41700264.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sulpata's mother took care to have this made.
EDCS-41700264.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sulpata, commissioned by her mother, likely dated to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-42700026.translation: Valeria Threpta, freedwoman of Quintus Valerius Galeria Dativus, son of Lucius, is buried here.
EDCS-42700026.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Threpta, a freedwoman, likely dated to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-42700028.translation: Callistus is buried here. He lived 21 years. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-42700028.summary: A funerary epitaph for Callistus, including a common wish for the deceased, likely dated to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-43100005.summary: An incomplete inscription that appears to be a funerary epitaph mentioning Lucius Aemilius, likely dated to the Imperial period.

EDCS-43100006.translation: Cladus Mentonianus won. Claudius Mentonianus won.
EDCS-43100006.summary: A brief inscription celebrating victories in gladiatorial combat, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-43100014.summary: A fragment of an inscription possibly mentioning Titus Caecilius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-44100105.translation: Equales, son of Ladro, set up an altar to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-44100105.summary: A dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Equales, son of Ladro, likely dated to the Imperial period.

EDCS-44100106.translation: Quintus Iulius Aquinus, a soldier of the Tenth Legion Gemina, aged 20, and Tiberia Claudia Urbana, aged 35, are buried here. Titus Iulius Florus, a veteran, and Pat...
EDCS-44100106.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of two individuals, one a soldier of the Tenth Legion Gemina, and the other an older woman.

EDCS-44100113.translation: To the departed spirits. Terentia Antila, aged [...], may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-44100113.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Terentia Antila, invoking a common epitaph wishing the deceased a light burial.

EDCS-44500102.translation: [...] Sempro, son of Tiber, a decurion, a magistrate, for Bilbilis, decreed the expense of the funeral, the statue, and the burial place.
EDCS-44500102.summary: This inscription records a public decree by Sempro, a local magistrate of Bilbilis, to fund a funeral, statue, and burial plot.

EDCS-44500104.translation: The emperor Caesar Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, for the city of Bilbilis, magistrate [...], in the year [...] Sylla.
EDCS-44500104.summary: This inscription mentions Emperor Caesar Augustus and a magistrate of Bilbilis, dating to a period associated with Sylla.

EDCS-44500106.translation: Emperor Augustus Germanicus adorned Bilbilis with high, somewhat abbreviated, monuments; Hotinus made me.
EDCS-44500106.summary: This inscription states that Emperor Augustus Germanicus embellished Bilbilis, and credits Hotinus as the maker of the monument.

EDCS-44500107.translation: To the departed spirits. V. F. Licidius, son of Q. F., Galer Honoratus, a veteran of the Tenth Legion Augusta, and his wife Erani Sta, for himself and his children, in front [measure] 11, in the field [measure] 12.
EDCS-44500107.summary: This funerary inscription records the purchase of a burial plot by Licidius, a veteran of the Tenth Legion Augusta, for himself and his family.

EDCS-44500108.translation: To Licinia Ceres, the preserver. Licinius Licinianus, eques, prefect of the ala Valeriana, the Tenth Legion Augusta, dedicates, vows, grants, gives.
EDCS-44500108.summary: This dedication is made by Licinius Licinianus, a cavalry prefect of the ala Valeriana and the Tenth Legion Augusta, to the goddess Licinia Ceres.

EDCS-44800007.translation: To Lucius Terentius of the Fourth Macedonian Legion.
EDCS-44800007.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Terentius, identified as a member of the Fourth Macedonian Legion.

EDCS-44800008.translation: To Lucius Terentius of the Fourth Macedonian Legion.
EDCS-44800008.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Terentius, identified as a member of the Fourth Macedonian Legion.

EDCS-44800009.translation: To Lucius Terentius of the Fourth Macedonian Legion.
EDCS-44800009.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Terentius, identified as a member of the Fourth Macedonian Legion.

EDCS-44800010.translation: To Lucius Terentius of the Fourth Macedonian Legion.
EDCS-44800010.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Terentius, a soldier of the Fourth Macedonian Legion, likely from a military monument.

EDCS-44800011.translation: To Lucius Terentius of the Fourth Macedonian Legion.
EDCS-44800011.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Terentius, a soldier of the Fourth Macedonian Legion, likely from a military monument.

EDCS-44800012.translation: To Lucius Terentius of the Fourth Macedonian Legion.
EDCS-44800012.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Terentius, a soldier of the Fourth Macedonian Legion, likely from a military monument.

EDCS-44800013.translation: To Lucius Terentius of the Fourth Macedonian Legion.
EDCS-44800013.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Terentius, a soldier of the Fourth Macedonian Legion, likely from a military monument.

EDCS-44800014.translation: To Lucius Terentius of the Fourth Macedonian Legion.
EDCS-44800014.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Terentius, a soldier of the Fourth Macedonian Legion, likely from a military monument.

EDCS-44800015.translation: To Lucius Terentius of the Fourth Macedonian Legion.
EDCS-44800015.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Terentius, a soldier of the Fourth Macedonian Legion, likely from a military monument.

EDCS-44800016.translation: To Lucius Terentius of the Fourth Macedonian Legion.
EDCS-44800016.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Terentius, a soldier of the Fourth Macedonian Legion, likely from a military monument.

EDCS-44800019.translation: To the god Jupiter, Candamus of the Urrilici, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-44800019.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter, dedicated by Candamus of the Urrilici, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-44800024.translation: She took care that this was made for her slave girl; she is here.
EDCS-44800024.summary: This inscription records the provision made by an owner for her slave girl, possibly a funerary context.

EDCS-44800026.translation: Amma Aunia took care that this was made for her mother, Boutia Allonis, daughter of Latro, aged sixty years.
EDCS-44800026.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Boutia Allonis, daughter of Latro, erected by her daughter Amma Aunia, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-44800028.translation: Paternus set up this monument to the Divine Shades for his father.
EDCS-44800028.summary: A funerary monument set up by Paternus for his father, dedicated to the Divine Shades.

EDCS-44800056.translation: To the Divine Shades for Lucius Licinius Crasinus, aged...
EDCS-44800056.summary: A funerary dedication to the Divine Shades for Lucius Licinius Crasinus.

EDCS-44800057.translation: Atta, a man of rank, aged 35. Tuscus.
EDCS-44800057.summary: A funerary inscription for Atta, recording his age and possibly his origin or the name of the dedicator.

EDCS-44800068.translation: Caitta, the slave of Anna Maxumilla, aged 20, is laid here. Her mother Aia and Servilius Reginus arranged for her burial.
EDCS-44800068.summary: A funerary monument for Caitta, the slave of Anna Maxumilla, arranged by her mother and Servilius Reginus.

EDCS-44800069.translation: Flavus, son of Caerius, most dutiful, is laid here.
EDCS-44800069.summary: A funerary inscription for Flavus, son of Caerius, indicating he is buried at this location.

EDCS-44800070.translation: Flavia Acce, aged 60. Aelius Proculus had this made for his wife.
EDCS-44800070.summary: A funerary monument for Flavia Acce, commissioned by her husband Aelius Proculus.

EDCS-44800071.translation: For Gaius Julius, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, Paterno, aged 30. His mother Ata.
EDCS-44800071.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Julius Paterno, son of Gaius, indicating his age and tribe, and mentioning his mother Ata.

EDCS-44800072.summary: A dedication, likely funerary, for Sempronius Gibbianus.

EDCS-44800103.translation: Talania Paterna had this made for her son Lucius Talanus Reburrinus, most dutiful, to the Divine Shades.
EDCS-44800103.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Talania Paterna for her son Lucius Talanus Reburrinus, dedicated to the Divine Shades.

EDCS-44800106.translation: Ianuarius set this up for his wife Va...
EDCS-44800106.summary: A funerary monument set up by Ianuarius for his wife.

EDCS-44800107.translation: To the Divine Manes, Maropo placed this monument for his Allunus Severinus, aged 85 years.
EDCS-44800107.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Maropo for his relation Allunus Severinus, dated by the age of the deceased to likely the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-44800108.translation: A monument for Ivo, of Firmana, lived 67 years. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-44800108.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ivo, whose age is given as 67, likely dating to the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-44800109.translation: To the Infernal Divine Manes, Elesus (erected this) in memory of his wife Pinetisuma, (listing) the years.
EDCS-44800109.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Elesus to his wife Pinetisuma, mentioning her lifespan.

EDCS-44800110.translation: To the Divine Manes, a mother of 41 years. To the Divine Manes.
EDCS-44800110.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the Divine Manes for a mother who died at the age of 41.

EDCS-44800114.translation: To the Divine Manes, Rusillus (set this up) for his Aegatia, aged 80 years.
EDCS-44800114.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Rusillus for his Aegatia, who died at the age of 80.

EDCS-44800116.summary: This is a very brief funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Dorulius.

EDCS-44800117.translation: For Ispanilla, his most dutiful wife, aged 34 years.
EDCS-44800117.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Ispanilla, described as a most dutiful wife, who died at the age of 34.

EDCS-44800118.translation: Matria placed this monument for her most dutiful son Severinus, aged 20 years; Matria, aged 80 years. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-44800118.summary: This funerary inscription records Matria setting up a monument for her son Severinus (aged 20) and also states Matria's own age (80), likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-44800502.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription that likely refers to a bishop.

EDCS-45000051.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly naming individuals or a place, with uncertain meaning.

EDCS-45000099.summary: This is a very brief inscription indicating that Ianuarius was the maker of the object.

EDCS-45000106.translation: To the Divine Shades. Eutichya [dedicated this] to Iulius, who deserved well.
EDCS-45000106.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Eutichya to Iulius, honoring his merit.

EDCS-45000108.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of the deceased, Odo.

EDCS-45000110.translation: Lucretia, daughter of Annelia, aged 50. Lucretia Arganta [dedicated this] to her mother.
EDCS-45000110.summary: This funerary epitaph records the age of the deceased, Lucretia, and identifies the dedicator as her mother.

EDCS-45000111.summary: This is a simple inscription identifying an individual by name and parentage.

EDCS-45100095.translation: Nonius Gallus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for the health of Mucis Musa Magna.
EDCS-45100095.summary: This is a votive inscription where Nonius Gallus fulfilled a vow for the health of Mucis Musa Magna.

EDCS-45100099.summary: This is a simple inscription identifying an individual by name.

EDCS-45100139.translation: To Mercury, Lucius Silius Toloco [dedicated this].
EDCS-45100139.summary: This is a dedication to the god Mercury by Lucius Silius Toloco.

EDCS-45100392.translation: Primus for the Lugubus Arovieis [dedicated this].
EDCS-45100392.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or memorial by a person named Primus, possibly related to deities or spirits named Lugubus Arovieis.

EDCS-46000002.translation: Anius Coami, having received what he owed, willingly fulfilled his vow to his colleague.
EDCS-46000002.summary: Anius Coami fulfilled a vow to a colleague, likely a fellow worker or member of a professional association.

EDCS-46000005.translation: Areus, according to his vow, [dedicated this] for the health of Augustus Orpeton.
EDCS-46000005.summary: Areus fulfilled a vow for the well-being of Augustus Orpeton, possibly a local magistrate or patron.

EDCS-46000006.translation: Arquius, son of Iovius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Hercules.
EDCS-46000006.summary: Arquius, son of Iovius, made a vow fulfillment to the god Hercules.

EDCS-46000014.summary: Pompeius Urbanus fulfilled a vow, likely a dedication or offering.

EDCS-46100427.translation: Aemilius Maternus, son of Florus, aged 20, is buried here.
EDCS-46100427.summary: This is a tombstone for Aemilius Maternus, son of Florus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-46100428.translation: Ambato, aged 35, Andioni, aged 35, C [perhaps C(oncordia) or a name initial].
EDCS-46100428.summary: This inscription lists two individuals, Ambato and Andioni, both aged 35, possibly in a funerary context or noting a shared location.

EDCS-46100429.translation: Vibia Marcelina willingly and deservedly erected this for her brother Marcus Vibius Quintus Flavius, aged 23.
EDCS-46100429.summary: Vibia Marcelina dedicated this memorial for her brother Marcus Vibius Quintus Flavius, who died at the age of 23.

EDCS-46100430.translation: Flavus, son of Nis, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Baelisto.
EDCS-46100430.summary: Flavus, son of Nis, fulfilled a vow to Baelisto, likely a local deity or spirit.

EDCS-46100431.translation: Capito willingly and deservedly placed this altar for the Nymphs.
EDCS-46100431.summary: This is a dedication of an altar to the Nymphs by Capito.

EDCS-46100439.translation: For Quintus Verginius Scaevola, freedman of Libra.
EDCS-46100439.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Verginius Scaevola.

EDCS-46100440.translation: In the Lord, blessed be with you, Vifredus, alive.
EDCS-46100440.summary: This inscription is a religious blessing, possibly funerary or devotional.

EDCS-46100445.translation: He made the temple for the state, which he called Mercury's, Marcus Aurelius renovated it.
EDCS-46100445.summary: This records the construction of a temple dedicated to Mercury by an unnamed individual, later renovated by Marcus Aurelius.

EDCS-46200003.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Flavus.

EDCS-46200011.translation: Cus, son of Ambai, is buried here.
EDCS-46200011.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cus, son of Ambai.

EDCS-46200013.translation: Oppius Mundus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-46200013.summary: This records Oppius Mundus fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-46200014.translation: She placed [this] for a deserving husband.
EDCS-46200014.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a wife for her husband.

EDCS-46200017.translation: Maritus Anticus Esquilina Marcelinus, aged 20. Romulus to his son Maritus, placed this.
EDCS-46200017.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Maritus Anticus Esquilina Marcelinus, aged 20, set up by his father Romulus.

EDCS-46200023.translation: Luci Fesetina, aged 10. Octavia to her most dutiful daughter Anutisema, in memory.
EDCS-46200023.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucia Fesetina, aged 10, set up by her mother Octavia.

EDCS-46200024.translation: Segilus and his son Rusticus gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vow to the Lares of the Crossroads for their well-being.
EDCS-46200024.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by two individuals to the household gods for their health.

EDCS-46200025.translation: Pompeia Primitiva to the Useian Mothers.
EDCS-46200025.summary: This is a dedication by Pompeia Primitiva to a group of female deities.

EDCS-46200026.translation: To the Divine Manes, Aunnia Secundiana.
EDCS-46200026.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Aunnia Secundiana.

EDCS-46200027.translation: Lucilius Crispus, son of Ionia, aged 20.
EDCS-46200027.summary: This inscription records the name and age of Lucilius Crispus.

EDCS-46200038.translation: Plinthas, father, [dedicated this] to Marcus Lucretius Frater.
EDCS-46200038.summary: This is a dedication by a father, Plinthas, to his son, Marcus Lucretius Frater.

EDCS-46200044.translation: Aunia Ara, aged 12, is buried here.
EDCS-46200044.summary: This funerary inscription records the name and age of Aunia Ara.

EDCS-46200046.translation: Exuperius erected this monument for his dearest wife Materna, and for himself, and for his son Maternianus, aged 30, and for his dearest daughter.
EDCS-46200046.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Exuperius for his wife, son, and daughter.

EDCS-46200047.translation: M. Candidianus, freedman of a legion, a worthy man, aged 60.
EDCS-46200047.summary: This inscription records the name, status, and age of M. Candidianus.

EDCS-46200052.translation: Sacred to the Nymphs, the Good Ones, and the place.
EDCS-46200052.summary: This inscription records a dedication to local deities and the place itself.

EDCS-46400282.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Little Lupa, pitiable, 3 months old.
EDCS-46400282.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a very young child named Lupa.

EDCS-46400283.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Onna, husband, and Primulla, mother, set up this stone with pious feeling for Cermula, aged 23; she lies here; may the earth be light upon her.
EDCS-46400283.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Cermula, aged 23, and was set up by her husband and mother.

EDCS-46400856.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Aurelis, safe.
EDCS-46400856.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter, possibly recording a vow made by Aurelis.

EDCS-46400862.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Divine Spirits by Saturninus.

EDCS-46400866.translation: Drusuna, daughter of Cisa Diocum Suattani, freely and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-46400866.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Drusuna, daughter of Cisa Diocum Suattani.

EDCS-46400867.translation: Lucretius Albinus Italicus lies here, aged 35. Cam Gallaeca [sets this up] for my husband.
EDCS-46400867.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucretius Albinus Italicus, aged 35, set up by his wife Cam Gallaeca.

EDCS-46400868.translation: [Recording the death of] a man aged 60 and of Lubia, aged 50. Urbana E as cir
EDCS-46400868.summary: This fragmented inscription likely records the deaths of two individuals, one aged 60 and the other Lubia aged 50, with Urbana also mentioned.

EDCS-46400870.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Parents set up this monument for their dearest son S. Valerius and Valeria Nopri, aged 14.
EDCS-46400870.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of S. Valerius and Valeria Nopri, aged 14, and was set up by their parents.

EDCS-46400872.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Pullius Cani had this made for his father Gaius Pullius Quirinalis Mercurialis, son [of Dionisius], aged 32.
EDCS-46400872.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Gaius Pullius Quirinalis Mercurialis, aged 32, and was set up by his father Lucius Pullius Cani.

EDCS-46400889.translation: Set up by Ursus, with his own skill, for Rufinus Pintani, to the Divine Spirits.
EDCS-46400889.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Ursus to the Divine Spirits, made for Rufinus Pintani.

EDCS-46400959.translation: Gaius Julius Modestus set up an altar to Jupiter with his own money.
EDCS-46400959.summary: This inscription records the erection of an altar to Jupiter by Gaius Julius Modestus at his own expense.

EDCS-46600192.translation: To Braran, son of Acunelus Beotus, aged 51. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-46600192.summary: A funerary epitaph for Braran, who died at age 51, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46600207.translation: Marcus, son of Caius R S, dedicated this willingly.
EDCS-46600207.summary: A dedication by Marcus, son of Caius R S, possibly to a deity or in memory of someone, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46600209.translation: Amma Nova, with her one partner, raised two mistresses on milk; they themselves placed this inscription.
EDCS-46600209.summary: An epitaph set up by two women, Amma Nova and her partner, possibly commemorating the women they raised, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46600218.summary: A record of quantities, possibly for trade or inventory, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46600222.translation: Quintus Dimitius Varinus of Consabura is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-46600222.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Dimitius Varinus from Consabura, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46600223.translation: Lucius Publicius Daphnus, from Valentia, aged 50, is buried here. Plot 1.
EDCS-46600223.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Publicius Daphnus from Valentia, who died at age 50, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46600234.summary: A dedication or memorial inscription to Caius, son of Caius, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46600236.translation: Aulus, son of Aulus Co, freedman, I Qu
EDCS-46600236.summary: An inscription that begins to identify Aulus, a freedman, likely a funerary or dedication marker, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-48700125.translation: Aurelius Celsus and Cornelius are buried here.
EDCS-48700125.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelius Celsus and Cornelius, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-49300080.translation: To Marcus Sempronius Lepidus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, who fulfilled all the offices in his state.
EDCS-49300080.summary: This is an honorific inscription, likely from a monument, commemorating Marcus Sempronius Lepidus for his public service.

EDCS-49300084.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest. Sesta, wife of Caius Canus, willingly placed this altar, fulfilling her vow.
EDCS-49300084.summary: This is a dedication of an altar to Jupiter, set up by Sesta, wife of Caius Canus, to fulfill a vow.

EDCS-49300093.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Aulus Sempronius, who held the office of duovir, possibly on a public building.

EDCS-49300171.translation: Ursus, a freedman, placed this in honor of his former master Rufinus Pintani among the departed gods.
EDCS-49300171.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a freedman, Ursus, to his deceased master Rufinus Pintani.

EDCS-49300186.translation: From the workshop of Pat Surus.
EDCS-49300186.summary: This is a maker's mark, indicating the work came from the workshop of Pat Surus.

EDCS-49300360.summary: This is likely a tombstone inscription for Hilar, possibly a slave or freedman belonging to Lucius Tettius.

EDCS-49300430.translation: Sacred to the departed gods. Valeria, mother, [placed this] for Valerius Luppianus.
EDCS-49300430.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a mother, Valeria, for her son Valerius Luppianus.

EDCS-49300431.translation: Sacred to the departed gods. [This was placed] for Valerius Severinus.
EDCS-49300431.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Valerius Severinus.

EDCS-49300443.summary: This is likely an inscription on a tombstone or monument identifying Gaius Valerius Verdullus.

EDCS-49300456.translation: Cesia, a freedwoman, placed an altar for Jupiter.
EDCS-49300456.summary: This is a dedication of an altar to Jupiter by a freedwoman named Cesia.

EDCS-49300463.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Samuel Cosimi.

EDCS-49300466.translation: Dedicated by Lugoobos, son of Bai Cabaris.
EDCS-49300466.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Lugoobos, son of Bai Cabaris, likely for a religious or public monument.

EDCS-49300488.translation: Germinator, M. B. H. Fynyx, M. B. M. Aerasimis, L. B. S. Venator, who...
EDCS-49300488.summary: This inscription lists several individuals, possibly freedmen (indicated by 'M B', 'L B'), and mentions Venator, suggesting a dedication or a list of associated persons, perhaps related to a business or funerary context.

EDCS-49500007.translation: Herois, aged 41, of the Iptuccitan and Cordubensian tribes.
EDCS-49500007.summary: This funerary inscription records the age of Herois, who belonged to the Iptuccitan and Cordubensian tribes.

EDCS-49500008.translation: To the spirits of Caius Julius Severus, from Ebora.
EDCS-49500008.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Caius Julius Severus, who was from Ebora.

EDCS-49700002.translation: Hermaphilus the servant made this, aged 24.
EDCS-49700002.summary: This funerary inscription records that Hermaphilus, a servant, made it and died at the age of 24.

EDCS-49700003.summary: This is a fragment, likely part of a larger inscription, naming Clodius Martialis from Argen...

EDCS-49700005.summary: This inscription records the name of the workshop belonging to Caius Avidius, likely identifying a commercial establishment or artisan's space.

EDCS-49700017.translation: For Aufidianus Macro, legate of the Augusti in the province of Hispania Citerior; Apronia Iusta, his wife, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-49700017.summary: This is a dedication honouring Aufidianus Macro, a provincial governor, made by his wife Apronia Iusta.

EDCS-49700021.summary: This inscription records the name of the workshop belonging to Sergius Strato, likely identifying a commercial establishment or artisan's space.

EDCS-49700023.summary: This inscription is a name, likely a tombstone inscription for Levinius Genialis.

EDCS-49700060.translation: Son of Lucius, grandson of Marcus, Met[---].
EDCS-49700060.summary: This inscription contains a filiation and cognomen, likely part of a fuller name on a monument.

EDCS-49700090.summary: This inscription consists of a praenomen and cognomen, likely a tombstone inscription.

EDCS-49700142.summary: This inscription records a quantity of 24 sextarii, possibly related to a measure or capacity.

EDCS-49700143.translation: Marcus, son of Caius, Martial, son of Marcus, son of Caius, Faustinus.
EDCS-49700143.summary: This inscription appears to be a complex naming structure, possibly listing multiple individuals or a detailed genealogy.

EDCS-49700151.translation: Lucius, son of Caius, M. P., Lucius, son of Caius, M.
EDCS-49700151.summary: This inscription contains abbreviated names and filiations, likely part of a fuller inscription.

EDCS-49700159.translation: The freedman of Caius, son of Lucius, Marcus Valerius, made this.
EDCS-49700159.summary: This inscription records that a freedman made a dedication or monument.

EDCS-49900093.translation: To Mars the Holy, Iunius Scorpus and Geminia Festiva, according to their vow.
EDCS-49900093.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Mars by Iunius Scorpus and Geminia Festiva.

EDCS-49900094.translation: Germanus, freedman of Sextus, Alchibiades Priscus, freedman of Sextus, [Veicius] Publius, slave of Veicia, Sempronius Aeschines, freedman of Lucius, [---]damus, slave of Annius Publius, Magius, freedman of Marcus Caius, Cr[---], freedman of Sextus, Theog[---], freedman of Publius, approved the small shrine.
EDCS-49900094.summary: This inscription records the approval of a small shrine by several freedmen and slaves, likely related to a building project or religious dedication.

EDCS-49900099.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Attius Theacenis, Roman knight, lived 45 years, 6 months, 16 days. Attius Stefanus, his freedman, made this.
EDCS-49900099.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Attius Theacenis, a Roman knight, set up by his freedman.

EDCS-49900100.translation: To a most indulgent husband from his wife, Ria.
EDCS-49900100.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a wife to her husband, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-50000150.summary: This is a dedication to the deity Good Fortune by Valentinus Nubianus, possibly a votive offering.

EDCS-50000151.translation: Scipio Victor, having conquered Hannibal, captured Carthage, and increased the empire, erected this temple to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno his wife, on account of three greatest victories obtained over the Carthaginian generals; he has brought his ashes under marble cover, who was not obstructed by Europe nor Africa. Behold how brief is human life, which the urn presses.
EDCS-50000151.summary: This is a triumphal dedication by Scipio Victor commemorating his victories against Carthage and Hannibal, likely erected after his military achievements, possibly in the second century BCE.

EDCS-50000152.translation: Tiberius Caesar, son of the deified Augustus, from the municipality of Cascantum.
EDCS-50000152.summary: This inscription records Tiberius Caesar, son of Augustus, in relation to the municipality of Cascantum, likely indicating a grant or a building by the emperor to the town, dating to the early imperial period.

EDCS-50000153.translation: To Urgulanilla Augusta, because of Christians and other disturbances, after they were expelled from the city.
EDCS-50000153.summary: This inscription honors Urgulanilla Augusta, likely related to the expulsion of Christians and other disturbances, possibly dating to the early Christian period or a period of unrest.

EDCS-50000154.summary: This is a simple dedication or dedication by Emperor Vespasian, likely from the Flavian dynasty period (69-96 CE).

EDCS-50000155.translation: Emperor Titus Caesar Vespasian Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, Tribune of the Potestas for the second time, Consul for the seventh time, Father of the Fatherland.
EDCS-50000155.summary: This is an imperial titulature inscription for Emperor Titus, dating to his second tribunician power and seventh consulship, likely in the late first century CE.

EDCS-50000156.translation: Emperor Titus Caesar Vespasian Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, Tribune of the Potestas for the eighth time, Consul for the eighth time, Father of the Fatherland.
EDCS-50000156.summary: This is an imperial titulature inscription for Emperor Titus, dating to his eighth tribunician power and eighth consulship, likely in the late first century CE.

EDCS-50000157.translation: Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, Tribune of the Potestas for the second time, Consul for the second time, by senatorial decree, the grain supply having been increased.
EDCS-50000157.summary: This inscription records Emperor Hadrian's titles and an action to increase the grain supply, likely issued during his second consulship in the early second century CE.

EDCS-50000158.translation: To Hadrian, Emperor, Pontifex Maximus, triumphant warrior, for his singular benevolence, the municipality of Manresa dedicated this statue.
EDCS-50000158.summary: This is a dedication of a statue by the municipality of Manresa to Emperor Hadrian, honoring him for his benevolence and likely dating to his reign in the first half of the second century CE.

EDCS-50000159.translation: To Julianus, vir clarissimus, or perhaps to Julius Verus, the governor of Tarraconensis, Emperor Constantine.
EDCS-50000159.summary: This is an imperial dedication or commendation by Constantine to Julianus or Julius Verus, a governor of Tarraconensis, likely in the 4th century CE.

EDCS-50000160.translation: To Herod, King of the Jews, guest and exile, and to Herodias, his queen, his wife, a woman of rare example and singular faith, by decree of the citizens of Sequani, presented at their own expense and consecrated under the axe.
EDCS-50000160.summary: This inscription honors Herod the Great and his wife Herodias, likely set up by the Sequani citizens in the 1st century CE.

EDCS-50000161.translation: To my wife Plotonia Tricipitis, Proserpina, and Cerberus, I dedicate this gift. Carrying death with me, I consign my living soul and myself to this monument, so that my six sons, whom Scipio the father, having restored them safely from Libya to the Camertine lands, had left in desolate widowhood, may not perish with the collapse of homes. I lived miserably, 56 years, 1 month, 5 days. No one knows. Farewell, life.
EDCS-50000161.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Plotonia, set up by her husband who expresses his grief and despair, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-50000163.translation: To Gnaeus Pompeius, son of the General, as leader of the army, who subdued the faction of Sertorius, and pacified the entire province of Hispania. In recognition of his great benefactions and generous gifts, the people of Manresen dedicate this statue.
EDCS-50000163.summary: This is a dedication of a statue to Gnaeus Pompeius by the people of Manresen for his military achievements in Hispania, likely from the late Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-50000164.translation: To Marcus Tullius Cicero, son of Marcus, of the Roman tribe, prince of Roman eloquence, quaestor, aedile, consul, proconsul, emperor, father of the country, from Arpinum.
EDCS-50000164.summary: This is an honorific inscription celebrating the career and achievements of Marcus Tullius Cicero, likely dating to the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-50000165.translation: I decree that Marcus Tullius Cicero be safe throughout the whole world for his outstanding virtue and excellent gifts of mind, by Gaius Julius Caesar.
EDCS-50000165.summary: This is an imperial decree by Gaius Julius Caesar, wishing safety and well-being to Marcus Tullius Cicero, likely from the late Republic.

EDCS-50000166.translation: To Lucius Furius, son of Lucius, proconsul, praetorian prefect, governor of the provinces of Hispania and Gaul.
EDCS-50000166.summary: This is an honorific inscription for Lucius Furius, listing his high offices in the Roman administration, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-50000167.translation: May it be well with you, traveler, who have not passed me by. Yet I would have wished you to pass me by in reading if I were a soldier. May it be well with you. I was Flavius, son of Attilius, a soldier, and of the order of soldiers; a pious mouth owes reverence to the shades; they ought to bid soldiers farewell. I lived well for myself, my family, and my country, and no complaints remained concerning me. Planca, my mother, placed me here. I was born 90 years old, while she herself was in her 117th year. Happy if I had buried her before.
EDCS-50000167.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Flavius, a soldier, set up by his mother Planca, who outlived him significantly, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-50000168.translation: Where I am going, I do not know. I die unwillingly. Farewell. Postumus, husband of Postumia.
EDCS-50000168.summary: This is a funerary epitaph of Postumus, a cavalryman, who expresses his reluctance to die, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-50000169.translation: Here lies Orceus, of noble family, who served with distinction in the civil wars in Arca and Castra. He died at the age of 55.
EDCS-50000169.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Orceus, commemorating his military service in civil wars, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-50000170.translation: Lucius Aemilius Gallus, son of Lucius, flamen of Rome and the deified Augusti, had this bronze statue erected from public funds and the public money of Segobriga.
EDCS-50000170.summary: This is a public dedication of a bronze statue by a Roman flamen, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-50000171.translation: Quintus Licinius Gerund, a prominent citizen and flamen of Tarraco, dedicated this as a gift.
EDCS-50000171.summary: This is a votive dedication by a prominent citizen and flamen of Tarraco, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-50000172.translation: Tiberius Cerius Effundus, son of Chalybs, dedicated this as a gift to the deified Augustus.
EDCS-50000172.summary: This is a votive dedication by Tiberius Cerius Effundus to the deified Augustus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-50000175.translation: To the deified spirits. At eight days before the Kalends of September, Gaius Volturnus Marinus erected this for his father and grandfather, Silicernus and Suggrundus Moer.
EDCS-50000175.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Gaius Volturnus Marinus for his father and grandfather, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-50000176.translation: To the deified spirits. Driven by the envy of citizens, I, Clodius, came to this land of the Aragonese and ordered my ashes to be placed in this tomb as I was dying. My most devoted sister, Clodia, ensured the funeral rites with lamentation according to my will. Farewell, whoever you are.
EDCS-50000176.summary: This is a self-penned epitaph by Clodius expressing his persecution and desire for burial, with his sister Clodia fulfilling his wishes, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-50000177.translation: The mistress Farasca made this. Marascus lived more or less 100 years. May the earth lie lightly on him.
EDCS-50000177.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marascus, who lived to be about 100 years old, erected by his mistress Farasca, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-50000178.translation: To the deified spirits, for eternal sleep and perpetual memory for Flavia Melitina, who lived 31 years, 3 months, and 20 days.
EDCS-50000178.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Flavia Melitina, detailing her age at death, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-50000179.translation: To the deified Augustus, for Valerius Licinianus, son of Licinius Turius Loris, a notable.
EDCS-50000179.summary: This is a dedication to the deified Augustus for Valerius Licinianus, a notable person, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-50000183.translation: Gaius Annius, son of Lucius, of the Quirine tribe, dedicated this as a gift from his own property.
EDCS-50000183.summary: This is a votive dedication by Gaius Annius, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-50200239.summary: This is a simple ownership inscription for Sextus Murrius Festus, likely related to a tomb or property, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-50200240.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Cai Clodius Successus.

EDCS-50400002.translation: From the hand of Gallicanus, a Greek.
EDCS-50400002.summary: This inscription likely refers to the origin or skill of a person named Gallicanus.

EDCS-50600271.translation: To the god Cossua, Caelia Elaesus fulfilled her vow to Callaiqum.
EDCS-50600271.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Cossua by Caelia Elaesus.

EDCS-50600274.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Po La Fl. lived 35 years.
EDCS-50600274.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Po La Fl., recording her age at death.

EDCS-50600275.translation: M. O. Eluvpus paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-50600275.summary: This is a votive inscription where Eluvpus fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-50702316.translation: Cornelius Oculatus willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Bandua.
EDCS-50702316.summary: This is a votive inscription from Cornelius Oculatus to the deity Bandua.

EDCS-50702318.translation: To Arrena, daughter of Balaesus, aged 40. To Boutia, daughter of Tucius, aged 10.
EDCS-50702318.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the ages of two individuals, Arrena and Boutia.

EDCS-50801083.translation: To Edovius. Epagathus, a deputy servant of Deuteri Aprilis, the steward of Caesar, willingly and with a good heart paid his vow.
EDCS-50801083.summary: This is a votive inscription by Epagathus, a deputy servant of a steward, dedicated to Edovius.

EDCS-51400880.translation: To Father Dis. Lucius S M. willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-51400880.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius S M. to Father Dis.

EDCS-51400881.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cornelia Ameschusa, with incomplete text.

EDCS-51400911.translation: To Valerius Rufus Attianus, Procula and Rufinus [dedicate this to their] father, aged 60.
EDCS-51400911.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Procula and Rufinus to their father, Valerius Rufus Attianus, who died at age 60.

EDCS-51400912.translation: Rufinus of the Fourth Legion Macedonian...
EDCS-51400912.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Rufinus, a soldier of the Fourth Legion Macedonian.

EDCS-51900003.translation: The bodies of us, Marcus Calpurnius Celsus, son of Lucius, are placed here. Cruel fate snatched us away in our lifetime from our parents. We both lived happily until this point. Thus, however, we lived: to none of us [did] M....
EDCS-51900003.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Calpurnius Celsus, lamenting his premature death and the cruelty of fate.

EDCS-51900481.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sergia Caisula, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-51900510.translation: Valeria, daughter of Massius, [dedicates this] to Valerius Aquilus, her husband, aged 28, and to Aquilina, her daughter, aged 20.
EDCS-51900510.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Valeria for her husband Valerius Aquilus and daughter Aquilina.

EDCS-52100054.translation: Arkylos dedicated me to the glorious hero, with Arkylos dedicated me to the excellent hero, his companion.
EDCS-52100054.summary: This is a Greek inscription, likely a dedication of a tomb or offering to a 'hero' and his companion, possibly related to a soldier or notable figure.

EDCS-52100065.summary: This is a Greek inscription, likely a humorous or informal dedication to Heracles, possibly by a slave or freedman, referencing a characteristic action or epithet.

EDCS-52100079.summary: This is a Greek inscription, a simple ownership mark or dedication, possibly for a tomb or object belonging to Hyacinthius Monon.

EDCS-52100090.translation: Of Soterichus from the pottery of Caius Aninius.
EDCS-52100090.summary: This is a Greek inscription, indicating ownership or origin of an object (likely pottery) from the workshop of Caius Aninius, belonging to Soterichus.

EDCS-52100092.summary: This is a Greek inscription recording a dedication by Apollonius.

EDCS-52100120.translation: To the good fortune of the divine, venerable Smyrnaean Nemeses, Julius Silvanus Melanion, procurator of the Emperor, dedicated this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-52100120.summary: This Greek inscription records a vow dedicated to the Nemeses of Smyrna by Julius Silvanus Melanion.

EDCS-53000113.translation: To Iullius Euphoros, his associates, from 200 sesterces. To Iullius Euphoros, his freedmen, from 200 sesterces.
EDCS-53000113.summary: This Latin inscription records a monument erected by associates and freedmen for Iullius Euphoros, with a donation of 200 sesterces for each group.

EDCS-53000123.translation: Quintia Anthologis, freedwoman of Titus, [dedicated this].
EDCS-53000123.summary: This Latin inscription records a dedication by Quintia Anthologis, a freedwoman.

EDCS-53000318.summary: This is a fragmentary Latin inscription potentially recording a dedication.

EDCS-53000350.translation: To Minerva, [I dedicate this] according to my vow.
EDCS-53000350.summary: This short Latin inscription records a votive offering to Minerva.

EDCS-53200184.summary: This is a fragmentary Latin inscription mentioning the name Pompeius.

EDCS-53200189.translation: Lucius Fulvius Proclus willingly made this vow to Hercules.
EDCS-53200189.summary: This Latin inscription records a voluntary vow made to Hercules by Lucius Fulvius Proclus.

EDCS-53200190.summary: This is a very fragmentary Latin inscription that begins with a name.

EDCS-53200192.summary: This is a fragmentary Latin inscription that seems to record the payment of a debt or vow.

EDCS-53200193.translation: Urus [son of] Rio took care [of this]; he is buried here, aged 60 years. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-53200193.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Urus, son of Rio, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-53200196.translation: Liberalis Malebecum erected this altar to Jupiter Best and Greatest, fulfilling his vow willingly.
EDCS-53200196.summary: This inscription records Liberalis Malebecum dedicating an altar to Jupiter Optimus Maximus to fulfill a vow.

EDCS-53200210.translation: Antistia Serena arranged for this to be made according to the will of Octavia Cuntura, daughter of Tempestivus.
EDCS-53200210.summary: This funerary monument was set up by Antistia Serena in accordance with the will of Octavia Cuntura, daughter of Tempestivus.

EDCS-53200220.translation: The workshop of Iulius of the VL family.
EDCS-53200220.summary: This inscription identifies the workshop belonging to Iulius of the VL family.

EDCS-53200226.translation: Severus, aged 32 years, is buried here.
EDCS-53200226.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Severus, who died at the age of 32.

EDCS-53300309.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Reve Anabaraecus.

EDCS-54000024.translation: Capito Carai willingly and with a willing spirit fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-54000024.summary: This inscription records Capito Carai fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-55200062.translation: Urde Petronia Flavina willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-55200062.summary: This inscription records Urde Petronia Flavina fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-55401011.translation: Cornelius Flaccus, in accordance with a vow, to Tutela.
EDCS-55401011.summary: This inscription records Cornelius Flaccus making a dedication to the goddess Tutela in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-55701696.translation: Deius, son of Is, and Tauros, son of Pentus Bebius, dedicated [this] to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-55701696.summary: This inscription records the dedication of something to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Deius and Tauros.

EDCS-55701698.translation: To the goddess Reve Anabaraego, Quintius, freedman of the Domitii, gladly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-55701698.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Reve Anabaraego by a freedman, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-55701699.translation: To the goddess Reve Anabaraego, Ceaberus, son of Hia Metus, gladly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-55701699.summary: This votive inscription is dedicated to the goddess Reve Anabaraego, mentioning the dedicator's name and parentage, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-55701700.translation: To the goddess Reve Anabaraego, Titus Flavius Flavinus.
EDCS-55701700.summary: This inscription, likely a dedication to the goddess Reve Anabaraego, simply records the name of Titus Flavius Flavinus, possibly an official or benefactor.

EDCS-55701701.translation: To the god Madarsso, Soelagaumus, son of Avellcius, of the Abanician tribe, according to his vow.
EDCS-55701701.summary: This votive inscription honors the god Madarsso and identifies the dedicator as Soelagaumus, son of Avellcius, belonging to the Abanician tribe.

EDCS-55701703.translation: To the god Ambatia, [a woman] of forty years.
EDCS-55701703.summary: This inscription records the dedication to the god Ambatia by a woman who was forty years old.

EDCS-55701705.translation: To the goddess Nabiae, Proculinus, [fulfilled his] vow.
EDCS-55701705.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Nabiae by Proculinus.

EDCS-55701706.translation: Severus gladly and deservedly [fulfilled his] vow.
EDCS-55701706.summary: This votive inscription records Severus gladly fulfilling his vow.

EDCS-55701707.translation: Bodogenus, son of Abani Saibodaecus, twenty years old.
EDCS-55701707.summary: This inscription records the name and age of Bodogenus, son of Abani Saibodaecus.

EDCS-55701708.translation: During the consulship of Glabrio and Homullus, the pottery workshop of Titus Flavius Aticus at Recensitum.
EDCS-55701708.summary: This inscription dates to the consulship of Glabrio and Homullus and records the ownership of the Recensitum pottery workshop by Titus Flavius Aticus.

EDCS-55701714.translation: To Gaius Laetilius, son of Marcus, priest, aedile, duovir, and duovir quinquennalis, by the colonists and inhabitants after [his] death.
EDCS-55701714.summary: This posthumous inscription honors Gaius Laetilius, son of Marcus, for his various civic and religious offices, erected by the local community.

EDCS-55701727.summary: This is a dedication to Caius Licinius Donacis.

EDCS-55701735.translation: Publius Clodius Deuterus for himself and his wife Cornelia Cytheris, freedwoman of Aulus, and his wife Fabia Callistis, freedwoman of Quintus.
EDCS-55701735.summary: This funerary dedication records the provisions made by Publius Clodius Deuterus for himself and his wives.

EDCS-55900115.translation: I, Calenus, son of Calenus, from the territory of Suessula, turning my eyes from the west of Italy, came to Spain with many friends seeking tranquility, where, a most savage war having broken out, I exchanged death for life. You, my most dutiful sons, carrying my father's ashes from Spain to the living parent, bury me in a common tomb, so that I may rejoice mixed with ancestral ashes in the sacred anniversary celebration of being honored as a parent.
EDCS-55900115.summary: This epitaph records the request of Calenus to his sons to transport his remains from Spain for burial with his ancestors, likely dating to the late Republican or early Imperial period given the context of civil unrest in Spain.

EDCS-56500227.translation: Stephen departed in peace in the year 517, on the Kalends of January.
EDCS-56500227.summary: This epitaph records the death of Stephen on January 1, 517 (AD 460), indicating a Christian burial during the late Roman Empire or post-Roman period in the West.

EDCS-56800025.translation: Valeria Silvana and her mother-in-law Valeria Verana erected this for Lucius Valerius Tempstivus, father of Valerius Gallus, and for their husband. Quintus Flaccus, their uncle, and the heirs, having redeemed a portion of Valerius Avitus, whose price Valeria Silvana paid, placed this found in the house in the forum.
